# Thread: This breakfast gives crazy energy!

### Inger — 2012-10-25T17:48:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-60145>

So, that was my breakfast today, a breakfast that starts to get more and more often on my menu..

Small raw and fresh Baltic herring heads, a few had roe so I throw that in too, spaghetti algae, water. Made a big cup voll. Nose closed it "tastes" totally like baby porrige I used to love! Smooth, velvet like and a little salty, the saltiness I can taste strangely, from the seaweeds, and it makes it nice! After drinking the cup I rinse my mouth still nose closed and put a spoonful of wild lingonberries in my mouth and chew on them before breathing again. Easy as anything. No bad tastes or feelings at all.

...

After, I cooked the rest of the fish after removing the stomach and intestines and rinsing. I cook it just for a few minutes in water, add seaweeds, curcumin / turmeric, seasalt and then precooked and prefrozen mussels. I eat a big bowl of this too. Boah it is totally delish!! I never get tired of it. For dessert I eat wild berries as much as I want.

Now, I can tell you one feels just great after this meal..!

Even this groggy girl. Spontaneous pic from today soon after waking up,

with my moms old pullover on because here is coool.. and fuzzy hair.. sorry lol

And yeah.. do not forget to look at the sun rising.. This is from my balcony.

### Inger — 2012-10-25T19:46:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-60168>

Yes Janagram. Sunrises are so beautiful always! Now, after I have started to look at them, and into the sunset too, and avoiding fake lights as much as possible, I have gone so sensible about having to be in artificial lights after dark. It feels like my whole beeing is against it! I even use dark sunglasses when out and on my way home from work in the dark because of the streetlights. They are annoying to me too. People do stare.. what the heck it is so dark outside and just a few streetlamps on and she has sunglasses on...? lol

I cannot believe I just started to look at the sunrise and sunset now, because of Jack. I never did regularely before really! Now I want not to miss it! I don't know how to say it but it has almost something very spiritual to me...

That is how it is in always in my appartement in the evenings these days, with candels only, pic from the other day. I use sunglasses of course reading my computer.

### Inger — 2012-10-25T20:00:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-60171>

> **caroline;61759 wrote:** You look amazing Inger! That sounds like a big breakfast. When do you eat next? Do you have lunch and dinner? Are you still eating high meat? I would like to eat high meat sometimes but don't know how to start. Should I just get some grass fed beef at the grocery store?

Caroline.. I eat really much. That is a normal breakfast for me..

I eat 3 times a day. The next time around 1 pm or so. I never have very rigid eating schedules, it can vary.

Next meal was today 1,2 pund of raw salmon, made into tartar with onion, seaweeds, ginger, cilantro...

Then for dinner 1 pound of raw grassfed beef as carpaccio with raw beef fat slices, raw onion, raw dried wild mushrooms, olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Wild berries for dessert. I ate a lot! I craved it. I always eat as much as my heart desires. I counted my calories one day just out of curiosity and they were between 2500 and 3000. Yeah I know quite a lot because I do not exersice at all. But I am not gaining eather, ok I have maybe gained a tiny bit since summer but it is okay. I bet it is the huge amount of wild berries I eat..lol Bears gain on wild berries too so I figured I will allow myself the same. Ha.

I am going to make a batch of high meat these days, I just got some fresh grassfed beefheart! I had no high meat for months!

Yes, just get some good quality meat that is not prefrozen, and cut into pieces and put in a glass jar. Only around 50% full, there needs to be oxygen. Then close, and put in the fridge. Air it every other day or so for a minute. After a few weeks it is ready.

> women typically consumed between 3,000 and 3,500 calories per day while men consumed 4,000 - 5,000 calories,

[http://www.ifbb.org.uk/changing-diet](http://http://www.ifbb.org.uk/changing-diet)

It's really interesting. You're eating half a kilogram or more of fish or meat at a sitting sometimes. And the results speak for themselves: you feel great and look amazing.

Yet someone like Jimmy Moore says he has to eat no more than 12% of his calories as protein or he balloons out. You must be eating far more protein than he is, and yet he's a foot taller than you and perhaps twice your weight.

I dunno - maybe your metabolism is just normal and his is just totally screwed from the years of eating nothing but processed carbs.

You don't currently eat much in the way of vegetables, Inger? It's mainly fish/meat and the berries? And do you use olive oil, ghee, coconut oil, etc.? Or is your fat percentage probably lower than people might expect?

EDIT

Do you eat any dairy at all? Or is it all out for you even goat's/ewe's milk cheese, yoghurt etc.?

I sometimes feel weird eating so much, because you can read so much around about woman eating 1200-1500 kalories and counting and all that and I think OMG I eat way too much. Sure I am not skinny but I feel great. I am thinking as for now I am fine with beeing not skinny.
>

>

>

> You don't currently eat much in the way of vegetables, Inger? It's mainly fish/meat and the berries? And do you use olive oil, ghee, coconut oil, etc.? Or is your fat percentage probably lower than people might expect?

That is strange about Jimmy Moore for sure! I did not know..

I eat very littlevegetables. Once in a while one cucumber, I like their juicyness a lot. And once in a while a dirty carrot from the garden. But there can be days in between with no vegetables. I often eat a few wild herbs when outside, just a few leaves or so. Sometimes I eat a green salad but very seldom. I would say seaweeds are my vege of choise these days after reading Epi Paleo Rx.

When it comes to fat.. I want to quit coconut oil for the winter but I have done it a bit halfheartedly..lol. Because I use it for my coffee and sometimes I still make it. I use handwarm organic coffee with cardamom, 1 eggyolk and 1-2 large spoons of coconutoil and mix it into the most delish foamy drink.. I some days had only black coffee but when I feel like having something special in the morning I still sometimes do the bulletproof version.. And then one day I made bark from organic cocoa powder and coconut oil and a tiny bit raw wild honey and ate a lot. hmmm. But my goal is to quit it for the winter for sure!

Then, I use olive oil too. I use many spoons a day if eating carpaccio. And sometimes I do it on my cooked fish after cooking if the fish is not fatty. I love to eat slices of raw grassfed beef fat if I can get it. It is my favorit fat. I can eat a lot of it just plain!

Dairy.. I sometimes cheat at work and have some HWC in coffee. It does me no good and I should not. From that I gain for sure!

But I love raw organic goats milk if I can get it. Sometimes I buy a bottle or two and drink it plain, it is so delish. It does me no bad AFAIK. Only the regular HWC at work is real bad. I eat no dairy normally. I never buy cream, but I seldom have it when beeing invated for dinner and there is wild berries with HWC. Very seldom some good raw French goat or sheep cheese.

I eat nuts too at times. My favorit are Siberian Pine nuts, they are wild grown and so healthy! I just ordered some.. I would eat only these for nuts if I could. I feel great from them. Not so from the regular almonds I sometimes eat in my mothers kitchen..lol Sometimes I eat raw organic macadamias. But nuts I eat not regularely at all. Avocad also seldom, one here and there thats it.

In summer I like coconut! I eat the dried coconutflakes too.

Oh.. and the eggyolk foam.. it is my weakness..lol. I whisk up 4 organic eggyolks with a half teaspoon raw wild honey into thick foam, and eat with wild berries. Now.. I think it makes me a little swollen.. but it is my comfort food. I am going to quit it for the winter though, or eat it very seldom! Yes.. lots of improvement to do for me for sure!

### Inger — 2012-10-26T08:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-60273>

Michael, are you eating much of your food raw?

I am quite sure that eating a big part raw is a secret for my vibrant energy. I got a big AHA experience as I read Jacks BG series.. especially that about bacterias and viruses. OMG when we cook something we kill the bacterias and viruses too for sure! Our poor guts!

All of a sudden it was clear to me why the tiredness disappeared as I started to eat raw. And it never come back. I eat most of my food raw you see. It makes me feel like flying..lol I have nothing against some gently cooked seafood here and there though. But raw is certainly more healing for me at least. I could never eat cooked eggs, sausage, meatballs, fried bacon... as many do for breakfast, and feel great! I guess it would make me fat too. IDK. I always was fatter eating cooked. And less energy. Less happy.

If I ate like Jimmy Moore, I would feel terrible.

I need the high meat once in the while, I need the moose organs, raw, once in a while, the raw lambshearts, the raw seafoods.. wild berries.. wild herbs.. these all makes me feel jut GREAT! I can feel very well what my body appreciate, and my mind too. I cannot help it, but it is how it is. I am addicted to feel good so I follow my bodys needs. I have figured delish recipes too that are raw and please my palate if I feel like it. Recipes that makes me feel just great. Sometimes we need something very enjoyable not only fishheads..lol

> To be honest I think modern diets and one too many beers and late nights probably do things you don't bounce back from quickly.

You know what I think Michael? I think we could bounce back awfully fast if we want to! It depends what are we willing to do. If drinking raw fishheads, raw fish, seaweeds, high meat, raw hearts and meat and fat.. raw fresh oysters.. any powerfood out there from the wilderness, I bet one would feel amazing fast!

### Michael — 2012-10-27T16:51:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-60440>

> **x\_danny\_x;61999 wrote:** a question about eating raw ... what ... made you say "poor guts"?

I guess she's thinking that bacteria on and in the fish would include some that would be beneficial to the stomach, and that cooking the fish would destroy those. This would be similar to one of the reasons that some people prefer unpasteurized milk.

However, high heat can damage proteins and promote rancidity in fats, so there could be other reasons why the food makes her feel good when she doesn't cook it. When she does cook the herrings she does it gently and briefly in simmering water.

Depends on the food really. Some vegetable foods have to be cooked. Cassava or kidney beans could kill you if you ate them raw. "Animal foods" - as long as they're from a clean source - seem readily assimilable with little or no cooking. Few people would cook oysters. The French have steak tartare; the Japanese, sushi.

Richard Wrangham, as someone comments in another thread, has a whole book on the anthropology and prehistory of this.

### Inger — 2012-10-31T10:00:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-61046>

> **fitness@home;61928 wrote:** Ok, that was fun. I freaked out the contractor installing tile in our home with the smoothie. He watched as I put smelt, the juice of a lemon, and some seaweed into the Vitamix. By the way, the seaweed does add a slightly salty flavor to the concoction and is a nice addition.
>
>
>
> The look on his face as I gulped it down was priceless .

LOL... Fitness@home, I can tell you from personal experiences, one gets lots of additional funny moments into life without even trying, eating Epi Paleo.. which is adding to the positives for sure

Like this one.. It was in Germany, in the train and I was hungry on my way home from work. Some guys in their 20ies sat down close to me and started to chat with me. They asked if I'll come to a local party in a city close by. I said.. nooo I am tired I need to go home and eat my food I just buyed. They asked me what that was then. I said..hmm.. calf liver. They asked how would I prepair it. I said I eat it just plain raw. They could not believe it and said they want to see it. I said, no not here, this train is full of people! But they insisted. So I then grabbed my bloody liver from my bag and just munched it down..mmm it was so good! It was thin sliced and delish and tender. Now.. everyone around was STARING at me. A old lady said then.. hmm how does that taste? I said - great, little sweet like chocolate! She said, hmm that must be healthy for sure and smiled. But they stared in a nice way.

I then said to the guys, you want to taste the liver too? I have enough for you too! But they said no thanks..lol. I said, okay I have some raw organic carrots here, maybe you want them instead? They looked happy and said, yes! So I gave them each one organic carrot and they munched on it happily with a smile on their face. It was a totally funny trip! I will never forget it for sure.

### Inger — 2012-10-31T10:13:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-61047>

> **x\_danny\_x;61999 wrote:** a question about eating raw, i really dont have the time to read everything on the BG series. what was mentioned about the viruses and bacteria being killed that made you say "poor guts"?

Danny, you need to read BG 1 and 2 and all the others too. Now.. Jack does not said it directly you need to eat your food raw but that was what additionally got from the whole thing. I just thought it must be logic! He told the story about the centarians in Okinava and that they got the "long life" mutation from a virus from raw fish.. and then I checked that viruses and bakterias almost all get killed by heat and then I figured I need this mutations too.. lol.. and then.. when one gets lots of viruses and bakterias it could help my body in many ways! So this summer when eating strawberries from the garden I always looked out for the one already eaten by some birds or snails.. to hopefully get some more viruses in..lol They taste the best anyways, that I can tell for sure!

I do not recommend this to anyone else but this is how I do it.

Now, look. Our guts needs a lot of different, good bacterias! So I eat the carrots dirty too. And I let my meat rot in a glass jar(must be aired once in a while!), or just sit in the fridge, open on a wooden plate. It dries a little on the surface and get a nice taste. I then just cut nice thin slices when I want some, I eat it raw. It gets a taste almost like salami.

Danny.. sorry I have not time to look it all out for you and copy and paste. If I have more time one day I could do it but these days I am just so busy! You could maybe print the BG series out and read in the train or something?

### Inger — 2012-10-31T10:17:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-61048>

> **Marccus;62580 wrote:** it looks like very awesome breakfast to me! just want to ask as i am unable to understand the exact recipe of it can tyou give me a detailed procedure for preparing this fish i would be very thankful to you

1\. Put soft fishheads (mackerel, herring, sardines...) in a blender. Cut into smaller pieces with a scissor if bigger heads.

2\. Add water and seaweeds (I add soaked dried seaweeds and their water because I have only dried seaweeds here).

3\. Blend until smooth. Strain if still bony pieces in it, that nothing gets stuck in your throut. Add water if too thick.

4\. Enjoy (with nose closed if pussy like me..)

### Veg — 2015-01-21T12:11:48+0200

> So, that was my breakfast today, a breakfast that starts to get more and more often on my menu..
>
>
>
> Small raw and fresh Baltic herring heads, a few had roe so I throw that in too, spaghetti algae, water. Made a big cup voll. Nose closed it "tastes" totally like baby porrige I used to love! Smooth, velvet like and a little salty, the saltiness I can taste strangely, from the seaweeds, and it makes it nice! After drinking the cup I rinse my mouth still nose closed and put a spoonful of wild lingonberries in my mouth and chew on them before breathing again. Easy as anything. No bad tastes or feelings at all.

>
> ...
>
>
>
>

I cook it just for a few minutes in water, add seaweeds, curcumin / turmeric, seasalt and then precooked and prefrozen mussels. I eat a big bowl of this too. Boah it is totally delish!! I never get tired of it. For dessert I eat wild berries as much as I want.
>
> Now, I can tell you one feels just great after this meal..!

### Inger — 2015-01-23T17:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-153705>

I often do it like this too, Kovita - I first do the fish head smoothie with heads, roe and fish milk, then i rinse the mixer and make me a smoothie with seaweed only. And enjoy it  Because i do not like the taste of the fish head smoothie at all... but I love the taste of the seaweedsmoothie! So the seaweedsmoothie is my treat after the bad stuff is down

### Hope2Learn — 2015-01-23T22:01:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/this-breakfast-gives-crazy-energy.4497/post-153716>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=153704)
>
> They are bubbles.... LOL

Oh good, I feel much better now, LOL! Today at the grocery store my husband and I stopped and looked at some jarred herring for a moment and then I looked at my husband and said, "you know, there's a woman online who takes fish heads and blends them into smoothies and swears by them". He just turned his head slowly to look at me with this blank look on his face and his eyebrows slightly raised.

I took advantage of his silence and said "she also takes organic, grass-fed beef hearts and livers and eats them raw on occasion". He said "okay, that's enough" and laughed a little to himself while trying to wrap his head around it. I just happened to stroll past the hearts and livers at that moment and just turned and looked at him with one eyebrow up and he made a show of ignoring me and looking the other way.  It doesn't read as funny as it felt, trust me!

Hope you don't mind me talking about you like that, but as others have said - you're inspiring! I'm not to the point of making any of your recipes yet, but they certainly have made an impression anyway!

### Inger — 2015-01-24T11:58:13+0200

# Thread: What would eat while watching theatre in Shakespeare's time?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-eat-while-watching-theatre-in-shakespeares-time.4499/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-25T20:02:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-eat-while-watching-theatre-in-shakespeares-time.4499/post-60172>

Not popcorn, ice-cream, Coca-Cola, or the like, of course.

It's all real food - and it includes shellfish:

> There are a number of contemporary accounts saying that you could buy food and drink within these theatres. Amongst the groundlings standing in front of the stage, people would have sold their wares, rather like the old cinema usherettes with a tray on a strap around their neck. The theatres didnâ€™t have any room for a bar or foyer in a modern sense so things like nuts, apples, fruit, beer, or wine, or water could all be bought inside.
>
>
>
> **We know that people were eating shellfish; oysters, muscles, periwinkles, whelks, we even found a cuttlefish.** The groundlings would have just dropped the shells on the ground, although they would have been swept up at the end of the day â€“ most of the shells weâ€™ve found are in the dumps at the back of the building.

[http://blog.britishmuseum.org/2012/04/18/archaeological-findings-at-shakespearean-playhouses/](http://http://blog.britishmuseum.org/2012/04/18/archaeological-findings-at-shakespearean-playhouses/)

### kathylu — 2012-10-26T02:58:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-would-eat-while-watching-theatre-in-shakespeares-time.4499/post-60246>

Michael, I love your posts! Please keep finding obscure but fascinating information.

### ruby — 2012-10-27T08:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/people-forget-to-die.4508/post-60416>

from comments:

"I also notice that none of the media spoke about the fact that the island is radioactive. A village on the island is called Therma, it has thermal baths that allow people to bath in cooled down water that is still really hot. That water is leaking all over the island. No one drinks that water, but their food is growing in it. You walk everywhere. So, if I were to look at this one location, I notice terrain, fresh food, active life style, sleeping a lot and radiation."--- interesting

# Thread: Armour question for the Thyroid experts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-10-26T15:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60301>

So for those of you on Armour (I'm taking a compounded version) when you increased doses, did you just add to your am dose or did you split it across the day?

right now I'm taking it in the am, and again in the afternoon around 4pm. I'm increasing from 1/2grain (30mg) to 1 grain (60mg), when I get my new rx, it will be 60mg in one dose. Should I be splitting it or should it be taken all together..

how long will it take to see improvement... I'm still getting the HOT surges/flashes through out the day from my thyroid trying to turn on....

intuitively I think I need to be on a higher dose... but we'll see if I can talk pcp in to upping it to 90mg

Love to hear the experts thoughts

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-26T17:38:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60317>

I would split, and take a half grain am and noon. If (when) you get bumped up, add that to a pre-bed dose. A bedtime bump of t3 makes for much more refreshing sleep.

Once you know it won't cause any weirdness, you can combine them all into one if the t3 will last long enough. If you convert poorly and feel like you "run out", consider keeping a split schedule.

Have you tried taking it sublingually? That can yield more oomph while you wait for the doc to catch up.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-26T18:12:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60321>

The great thing about Armour is that you can pretty much do anything you want and your body will tell you whether it likes it or not. Personally, if it were me I would split the dose. I could not have gone all day with just one dose. If you get up to 90 mg you may want to split 45/45 or 30/60 or 60/30. The usual 2-3pm dip hypo people get can sometimes be relieved with a higher afternoon dose. I do a small evening dose with my T3 but never did it with Armour. Some people have trouble sleeping but I never do with the T3 or Armour. You'll know what to do once you start upping. When I first started upping I just used my intuition while taking symptoms into account to decide how much when.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-26T18:24:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60323>

In my kids, I notice a behavior difference even within 48 hours of increasing their dose. However, I think for full effect, it takes several weeks, as the cells see more T3 and think about doing stuff with it (making mitochondria, and expressing different genes). For me personally, I would start by splitting the dose for a more even level of T3 throughout the day. Of course symptoms are the ultimate navigating tool..

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-26T18:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60331>

That's what I'm inclined to do - but since Its compounded...and in capsules I'm not sure how to split the dose... right now I've got 1/2grain capsules...so I can split it... I see my doctor on Monday - and may ask her if she can adjust the Rx so that its split... 30mg in the morning and 30mg in the afternoon...

I definitely feel better w/my 2nd dose around 4pm. I guess we'll see.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-26T19:16:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60337>

I would think your doc will be fine with keeping the pills at 30mg and just taking two doses. It may cost a little more but if that's what works it's worth it!

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-26T19:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/armour-question-for-the-thyroid-experts.4509/post-60341>

> **Patty Cakes;61953 wrote:** I would think your doc will be fine with keeping the pills at 30mg and just taking two doses. It may cost a little more but if that's what works it's worth it!

That's what I'm hoping... guess we'll see if she's willing to do that...

# Thread: Q fever fatigue syndrome?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-10-26T15:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-60302>

Does anyone know anything about this? I am travelling with a lovely gentleman who has had this for ten years. It started out as Q fever with high temperatures (up to 43C) and horrible headaches and aching limbs and lethargy) and lasted for about seven months. It has now morphed into Q fever fatigue syndrome with terrible migraine headaches that are continual and extreme lethargy and hot sweats. There is a doctor -Sir Macfarlane Burnett who has researched and isolated the bacteria from which CSR labs developed a vaccine to inoculate vets and farmers and abattoir workers.

Q fever comes from urine spores in the dust.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-26T17:32:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-60316>

I am not familiar with this, at all, so please forgive if this is dumb question.

Is the Q Fever Fatigue syndrome substantially different from ME or Chronic Fatigue Syndrome? I wonder if a treatment protocol similar to what helps with CFS would be of any benefit.

### Jude — 2012-10-27T00:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-60378>

> **caroline;61918 wrote:** Does anyone know anything about this? I am travelling with a lovely gentleman who has had this for ten years. It started out as Q fever with high temperatures (up to 43C) and horrible headaches and aching limbs and lethargy) and lasted for about seven months. It has now morphed into Q fever fatigue syndrome with terrible migraine headaches that are continual and extreme lethargy and hot sweats. There is a doctor -Sir Macfarlane Burnett who has researched and isolated the bacteria from which CSR labs developed a vaccine to inoculate vets and farmers and abattoir workers.
>
> Q fever comes from urine spores in the dust.

All I know comes from my son contracting it at 12yrs. He was acutely ill with roaring temps, and eventually diagnosed clinically (before blood work proved it) . Had IV antibitics for a couple days and has had no reoccurring symptoms in 18yrs. Did your friend have ABX with his initial diagnosis?

### Remo — 2017-04-14T16:48:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-212321>

I need to get this tested myself as I live in the epi-centra of the exacty breakout + I ate a ton of goat cheese during the 2007-2010 era..

The only thing that has helped me regain some energy is sunlight.

### caroline — 2017-04-15T05:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-212338>

I am pretty sure this was an "ask Jack" question a few years ago.......

### JanSz — 2017-04-15T17:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-212352>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=212338)
>
> I am pretty sure this was an "ask Jack" question a few years ago.......

**Q fever**
Discussion in '[Ask Jack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/ask-jack.18/)' started by
[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/caroline.888/), [Oct 26, 2012](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/q-fever.4512/).
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/q-fever.4512/#post-60403](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/q-fever.4512/#post-60403)

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=60304)
>
> Dr Kruse - have you heard of Q fever? If so - could you point us to some research we could look at. Thanks for any help.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=60403)
>
> Sheep, goats and cattle are the main source of Q fever. It is spread from contact with excretions such as milk, urine, feces and the afterbirth of infected animals. Q fever is caused by a microbe called "Coxiella burnetii." This microbe can survive for months and even years in dust or soil. **The antibiotic tetra doxycycline is often used to treat Q fever.** Patients usually recover promptly when treatment is started without delay. **For chronic Q fever, both doxycycline and hydroxychloroquine may be used for several months.** Q fever is rarely fatal. Untreated chronic Q fever may be fatal and mainly in patients with existing heart disease.
>
> [http://www.cfsph.iastate.edu/FastFacts/pdfs/qfever\_F.pdf](http://http://www.cfsph.iastate.edu/FastFacts/pdfs/qfever_F.pdf)

### Remo — 2017-04-15T17:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/q-fever-fatigue-syndrome.4510/post-212355>

I would be very reluctant to do antibiotics even if I found out I had Q-fever.

# Thread: Cooked vs Raw in relation to brain growth study for big brains
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooked-vs-raw-in-relation-to-brain-growth-study-for-big-brains.4513/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2012-10-26T16:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooked-vs-raw-in-relation-to-brain-growth-study-for-big-brains.4513/post-60308>

[http://www.latimes.com/news/science/sciencenow/la-sci-sn-homo-erectus-cooked-raw-food-brain-size-20121022,0,2243513.story](http://http://www.latimes.com/news/science/sciencenow/la-sci-sn-homo-erectus-cooked-raw-food-brain-size-20121022,0,2243513.story)

### Michael — 2012-10-26T17:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooked-vs-raw-in-relation-to-brain-growth-study-for-big-brains.4513/post-60314>

I have, shamefacedly, to admit that I was given Wrangham's book last Christmas (by an anthropology student, as it happens) but have still not read it.

I think the _LA Times must be misunderstanding what he's saying by quite a long way. After all, gorillas don't eat the same things as humans, and Wrangham would hardly not be aware of that.

It's not as if Inger, who eats a high percentage of raw food has to spend all day chewing. On the Times_ writer's understanding, she would have to. The same goes for the Eskimos in the 1949 video I found and posted here recently.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-26T19:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooked-vs-raw-in-relation-to-brain-growth-study-for-big-brains.4513/post-60338>

No mention of seafood,shellfish, coastal dwelling and the fact that our brains are actually shrinking lately. Raw shellfish would provide enough nutrition without having to chew 8 hours a day. Hmmm.

### Michael — 2012-10-26T20:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooked-vs-raw-in-relation-to-brain-growth-study-for-big-brains.4513/post-60343>

Nail on the head, Dali.

Meat from land-animals or marine mammals isn't necessarily far behind. Stefansson remarks somewhere, when disputing the point that chewing is the reason for tooth-health among arctic peoples, that much of what they ate was soft and slipped down with a minimum of chewing. How much would you need to chew liver?

I think Wrangham probably has some interesting things to say, and the LA Times has misunderstood him.

I mean gorillas even eat twigs, don't they? I shan't be tucking into twigs tonight, raw or cooked.

Maybe he's talking about the energetic advantage of being able to make use of starchy tubers, which you HAVE to cook or something ...

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-27T23:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooked-vs-raw-in-relation-to-brain-growth-study-for-big-brains.4513/post-60473>

From my understanding, our bodies have no problems digesting raw meat. Some folks get a boost in eating things raw, such as eggs.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-27T00:43:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4519/post-60377>

Oh boy you do have a lot going on here. First you ASI is not OK. You should be in the top of the range in the morning when you need it the most. The second reading should still be near the top of its range. The third reading should be mid range and the last should be near the bottom. You have a reverse cortisol pattern. This actually is probably worse since you are on Cortef too. This could have been made worse by not taking the thyroid meds. Having a low FT3 constantly, stresses the adrenals because they have to pick up the slack. My guess is with the low amt of Naurethroid you are on is still not enough but you probably cannot handle more because of the poorly functioning adrenals. NDT will suppress the TSH way below 1.0. However, you want to pay attn to the FT3 and FT4 levels, not TSH so much. The FT4 is also low, should be near 3.0 or above. So you are looking metabolically inefficient to me.

DHEAs is low and should be in the top of the range. Pregnenolone is also low. No surprise as you probably have preg steal going on to help support the adrenals. Ferritin which is a storage iron hormone is also too low. Check your other iron labs to see what is going on there. Vitamin D could be better but in spite of all going on not bad.

I'm not a hormone expert but.....I think the estrogen should be a little higher which means the Pg should be way higher.

Good: hs-CRP and no Hashis apparent. You have some thigns to work on (like all of us!).

### hope2conceive — 2012-10-27T01:41:34+0300

So I was kind of glad that was getting better, a little...though the big thing is T3. I really need to find a different MD. Or hopefully with what Dr K says I can convince my MD to change course.

Yes, my ASI was much worse before - 6 in the morning only....but without Cortef my midnight cortisol was normal, the only one. I am not sure what I'd do without Cortef right now - without it I was miserable...though I only got on it after years of trying to do it with supplements.

The HS CRP is definitely confusing. I am totally leptin resistant, insulin issues...despite being on a VLC diet....

Thanks again, Patty!

### JanSz — 2012-10-27T02:57:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4519/post-60392>

> **hope2conceive;62003 wrote:** Thanks so much, Jan! I have some DHEA but not the Pregnenolone....would you take it in addition to Progesterone? That is what my home MD said to do...luteal phase. I would prefer the Pregnenolone though....

Make sure that you use the pregnenolone that I specified. Other types, pills or creams are rather useless.

Personally I use DHEA from lef.org but plan on switching to DHEA from Nutricology.

Nutricology & Allergy Research is same company, same products, different colors of labels.

That pregnenolone is expensive, and, you will need lots of it.

I buy mine from HerbsPro.com

When you buy dozen bottles they will give you break.

It is going to take a while to figure out proper dose.

It was simple for me (I am a man), you need to check at least two days within monthly cycle.

days 2-4 (minimums)

days 19-21 (maximums)

if you are rich ($$) add ovulation

It would be a good idea to add 100-200mg/day 7ketoDhea to this set,

but I have seen ladies complaining of excessive testosterone.

Usual thinking is that DHEA may increase testosterone

7ketoDHEA is thermogenic, thyroid should work better.

\=========

When using pregnenolone, I suggest that you do not use progesterone.

If your blood progesterone is low, increase pregnenolone pills.

but

hopefully

pregnenolone will increase your morning cortisol.

You may use little extra pregnenolone if you need to raise cortisol.

Do all dr Kruse's advices on circadian rhythm (light, sleep, BAB) that helps moving cortisol.

Note, when I first started pregnenolone and DHEA supplementation I had very low blood levels of them.

At first I was using 300mg/day DHEA

and

900mg/day pregnenolone

that lasted few month, then I filled up voids and my levels started to raise over top levels.

I use now 150mg/day DHEA

and

300mg/day pregnenolone

and

200mg/day 7ketoDHEA

I am a men, that is equivalent to women's needs on day 2-4

latter

women needs more progesterone

first

try to get some order on first half of your cycle.

Latter you will see if you will need to increase pregnenolone dose (or possibly for few days you may need 100mg/day Microgest or prometrium (natural, micronized progesterone), to give you the required higher progesterone levels in luteal phase.

///

When you use pregnenolone and all works as planned, you should get higher cortisol.

Do not use hydrocortisone, that may mess up proper flow.

Also,

watch out for interaction with thyroid hormones.

They (thyroid hormones) may start working,

you may get (temporary) hot flashes and higher heart rate.

Stop pregnenolone use for little bit, untill all settles.

Eventually, after the Steroid Hormones are at better level,

you will be able to work on your high TSH.

I use Thyroid-S

You can buy it now from amazon.com

for future use

When you will have good steroid hormone panel, max 3grains/day should be enough to keep

TSH(0.5-1.5)

Do not be preoccupied watching T3/RT3 ratio and forget about manipulating it with extra T3.

That is a dead end road.

RT3 and T3/RT3 are indicators, you watch them but do not manipulate them directly.

///

Hashi and other autoimmune

stop all grains

stop all nigtshades

have lots of selenium

have lots (excess) of iodine/iodide

make sure that all micronutrients that are checked by Spectracell (functionally) are at good levels

verify by analysis that you have good fatty acids

study and provide input on this board to better understand phospholipids and their interaction with fatty acids

possibly we will figure a way to identify phospholipid deficiencies and proper way to compensate.

............

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-visor-when-cloudy.4525/>

# Thread: ZRT  Sunday saliva test
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zrt-sunday-saliva-test.4541/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LinD — 2012-10-28T19:39:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zrt-sunday-saliva-test.4541/post-60605>

I hope I didn't take my collection too soon -- today. Sunday. UPS doesn't pick up until tomorrow. Should I freeze it and drop it off tomorrow?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-28T19:42:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zrt-sunday-saliva-test.4541/post-60607>

I would check online and through the paperwork they sent just to make sure freezing doesn't destroy anything in the saliva. I just refrigerated mine then dropped off the next day to be mailed.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-28T20:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zrt-sunday-saliva-test.4541/post-60613>

From their instructions:

> NOTE: Freezing your sample prior to mailing is recommended but not required. Saliva samples are stable for up to 30 days at normal room temperature.

### LinD — 2012-10-28T23:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zrt-sunday-saliva-test.4541/post-60638>

Awesome. Thanks. I found other info that says it's ok to freeze and that it's stable for 30 days at room temp. (ew!) But who knows what temp it's exposed to en route.

[http://www.pinestreetfoundation.org/avenues/avenues8/cortisolqa8.html](http://http://www.pinestreetfoundation.org/avenues/avenues8/cortisolqa8.html)

### Destiny — 2012-10-28T23:47:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zrt-sunday-saliva-test.4541/post-60643>

I did my test on Sunday as well (convenient) and had it in the freezer until Monday before shipping it.

# Thread: Optimal Canines
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2012-10-28T20:08:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60612>

Instead of hijacking the "ask Jack" thread, loved the suggestion of starting an Optimal Canine thread here! I've been thinking a lot about the dogs and optimal as I read the blogs. Sometimes I'm more interested in how it helps them lol, but am determined to help myself too.

I've been feeding my 2 dogs raw for almost 3 years (beef, turkey, chicken and some pork. Sardines a couple of times a week). In the summer, as I upped my seafood consumption, I also increased theirs. I noticed their body comp changed and they were more muscular. Their coat is so shiny, they've never looked better. I also noticed mood changes - they're happier (which could also be linked to the fact that I feel happier too lol).

But in conjunction with the seafood, I also started turning the lights out earlier, not watching as much TV. So these changes can be linked as well. Also, changing the order of eating and going on our walks too.

What I love about this is that I am in some ways using the dogs as markers - watching how the changes are impacting them, and validating that I'm doing the right thing. I definitely see changes in myself, sometimes it's easier to see them in others. I'm also watching them follow their instincts to learn from them - my older dog goes to bed when it gets dark, and now that it's fall, she will go at 7:30 or 8:00. They are eating dirt - which I'm sure is a natural instinct as well (but one I'm not likely to start lol. I hate dirt!).

I love that having dogs means I get up and into the air and sunshine first thing in the morning - every day. I have to get out and walk them, so we're out over an hour every day.

What I haven't cracked yet is the CT. I'm guessing that getting them into a lake to swim is probably the best way. Has anyone else done CT? My older dog has had 2 ACL surgeries in 2 years, so I'm interested in helping the healing process and keeping her joints healthy.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-28T21:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60629>

My Aussie is an outside dog. He gets his CT the old fashioned way

He chooses to lay on cement when it's below freezing, and if you coax him inside, he wants back out immediately "I said hi, now please open the door like a good human"

I no longer feed raw, as the truth is I just can't afford it. He was raised raw, and is far larger than he should be...he is a papered Mini Aussie, that tips the scales at 58 pounds in fighting trim. His cannon bones are thicker than my wrist. Both sire and dam were less than 30 pounds.

Im interested to see how the CT helps the ACL healing process for the old girl!

### diane — 2012-10-28T22:30:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60634>

Ha - that's awesome! Mine love the snow - but we didn't have much last year, and it's still been pretty warm here.

She's actually only 6 - so not that old. But once she did one leg, apparently it's high risk for the other. Which went about 8 months later. She probably was a bit heavy when she tore the first one, and I really worked to get her weight down so we weren't stressing the joints. But we still tore the other lol.

### JoeBranca — 2012-10-29T23:53:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60805>

I've brought our dogs into epi-paleo with me (minus the sea veggies except some dried sprinkled Dulse here and there). I get cheap eats like 5lb boxes of wild frozen whole sardines, frozen whole mackerels, shrimp, etc at the Asian markets.

Sometimes I'm running a little late or low on the raw stuff, so I still resort to canned salmon / sardines here and there, but I try to minimize that. Often their seafood comes with freshly heated bacon drippings.

For non seafood meals I still have lots of of bags of dry trimmings in the chest freezer from our side of grass fed beef purchase earlier this year. The problem is there's tons more fat to them than meat, which raises a question - what is a good rule of thumb in protein/fat ratios for canines?

### shah78 — 2012-10-30T00:22:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60817>

Here's a question. How much DHA does a dog or cat really need.?They may be offspring of cold water mammals like we are, but they did not undergo the huge DHA induced brain growth spurt that we humans did. Underdog and Top Cat are fictional, aren't they? They really don't speak and make sarcastic coments, do they?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-30T01:07:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60833>

> **shah78;62513 wrote:** Here's a question. How much DHA does a dog or cat really need.?They may be offspring of cold water mammals like we are, but they did not undergo the huge DHA induced brain growth spurt that we humans did. Underdog and Top Cat are fictional, aren't they? They really don't speak and make sarcastic coments, do they?

You've not spent much time around heelers then, I'll wager..I think they have the mentality of most six year old children, just without thumbs and/or the benefit of the english language.  They have an uncanny way of getting their point across, however, and most can certainly use sarcasm.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-30T05:59:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60871>

Our dog has eaten raw meat from birth. We buy most of his food from K9 Natural. They are solely free-range, grass-fed, without any grains or hormones/antibiotics, no preservatives or anything artificial (in other words, better than what many would buy in a US supermarket for human consumption!). And they are based literally up the road from where I live. I recently learned their business has become so successful that they are now throughout the USA and Canada, as well as Europe. For those of you with dogs and cats, it could be worth checking out... [http://www.k9natural.com/](http://http://www.k9natural.com/)

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-30T14:30:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60896>

our Lab started out on a grain fed diet when we got him from the breeder - we switched him to evo - however, after he was neutered at 2, he began to have problems digesting the high protein content... so we moved to pure vita salmonhttp://www.petflow.com/product/pure-vita/pure-vita-grain-free-salmon-formula-dry-dog-food . he loves it!

recently we've added a 1/4 of freeze dried raw to his afternoon meal - this is the brand we get [http://www.wyliewagg.com/index.cfm?action=store&sub=product&prod=1118&fromCat=177#.UI\_Hk2-HIRo](http://http://www.wyliewagg.com/index.cfm?action=store&sub=product&prod=1118&fromCat=177#.UI_Hk2-HIRo)

while not ideal - this is what works for us...

### diane — 2012-10-30T15:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60907>

> **JoeBranca;62501 wrote:** For non seafood meals I still have lots of of bags of dry trimmings in the chest freezer from our side of grass fed beef purchase earlier this year. The problem is there's tons more fat to them than meat, which raises a question - what is a good rule of thumb in protein/fat ratios for canines?

I think it's similar to humans - you have to find our own dog's n=1 to see what works in terms of fat ratios. Mine tend to gain weight with too much fat, but then again, could be the kind of fat that I was giving them. I feed my dogs the same thing, but one is leaner than the other. I also give them CO once a day which they love.

In terms of DHA, I give them as much as I can. Although I haven't switched to mainly seafood yet. And mine won't eat fish raw lol. But I do give them canned salmon, cooked fish and fish skin, canned mussels and oysters. Plus, i put dried kelp on their meals, about 1/4 tsp each day.

### Marie — 2012-10-30T17:05:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60936>

Here are some great sites for those wanting to feed raw:

[http://www.rawmeatybones.com/](http://http://www.rawmeatybones.com/) (Australian veterinarians Drs Tom Lonsdale)

[http://dogtorj.com/](http://http://dogtorj.com/) (Dr J is a member of this forum and just as wordy as Dr K. LOL)

[http://www.rawlearning.com/](http://http://www.rawlearning.com/)

What Really is in Pet Food? [http://www.bornfreeusa.org/facts.php?p=359&more=1](http://http://www.bornfreeusa.org/facts.php?p=359&more=1)

I watched this recently and loved it. [http://animal.discovery.com/video-topics/wild-animals/eating-giants.htm](http://http://animal.discovery.com/video-topics/wild-animals/eating-giants.htm)

(Now I know why Green Tripe has been called Doggie Crack. (Get your fresh frozen GT here: [http://www.greentripe.com/](http://http://www.greentripe.com/))

Anyway, it really gives you a good view of how a body is consumed by predators.

**

It is not for the squeamish.**

Oh, and for those who don't have a large freezer, yet, I just sourced some pastured beef for $0.08/lb. Purchasing a freezer on CL has paid for itself many times over and it costs me about $30/month to feed a 65lb dog all organic, pastured meats + Salmon oil + GT + Oysters + canned or fresh fish. Anyone who says Raw is expensive hasn't done their research. (Just don't put your ice too close to the GT. It doesn't make for a pleasant CT experience.)

### diane — 2012-10-30T18:06:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60950>

Thanks for the links Marie! I've seen some of them, but will definitely check out the discovery link. I switched after reading about what's in most kibble - although it took me some time to get my head around it. Now - no second thought!

My guys love tripe! And after a few years, I'm actually getting used to the smell. There is a canned version, which I will get if I can't find frozen, but I find it smells much worse! And yes, it really is doggie crack!

I too got a freezer from Kjijiji and we have a fairly small condo - yet found room for this darn freezer lol. Of course, it also came in handy when I got a 1/4 grass fed beef - that the dogs have to share lol.

Does anyone feed any veggies? I had done a "nutritional consult" and she recommended sweet potato (but also got me on the sardines for them, kelp - long before I found this blog lol), which I did for a few months. But then realized that if I don't need them, they probably don't either. And will occasionally include some pumpkin or squash. There are lots of people who feed their dogs raw who pile in the fruits and veggies.

### Marie — 2012-10-30T18:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-60963>

Sweet potato was the only veggie I feed, and I only feed that with tripe to firm up the poo. I use two back-to-back tripe meals as a "mini fast" due to the low protein and fat content. It gives his body about 36 hours of a break.

Regarding veggies...unless they are extremely cooked, very little is absorbed. I began feeding a BARF style plan and spent many, many, many hours cooking. This was when I was healing foster dogs' issues about 10 years ago. Now I feel Prey Model Raw is the way to go. _With pasture fed Green Tripe and Oysters, you're covered w/r/t veggie content.

{Look for a Raw Feeders co-op to get started, find out where they source their meats and start meeting and greeting in your neighborhood. This is how you get the phone calls saying "I need to unload 1,800 lb of pork loin at $0.03/lb can you call your friends?", or "Do you need any mackerel heads, I've got 10lbs that it is either you or the dump." This is also a great way to get REALLY good meat for yourself at 10% of the retail price.}

I suggest that you be HIGHLY suspicious of anyone suggesting supplementation is necessary, except at the most extreme of dog sports. Specific dogs may need some supplementation for their specific gene issues, but a broad diet plan should not need supplementation for the average healthy dog.

_Also, I would also recommend doing a lot of breed related research to pinpoint exactly what issues you may be facing._ I had a Boxer mix rescue who just died recently at the age of ~15. His diet was No Carb due to Boxer breed issues with cancer. His vet was shocked to discover how old he was as most boxers don't make it past 10. She is now feeding her dog a no carb diet (and is, despite 30 years as a DVM and dog sport competitor, struggling with the "lack of science" behind Raw diets. She has a lot of mythology to combat. I told her "Mother nature wrote the science, so just follow her direction." and she smiled, saying "Yes, it is as simple as that, isn't it?"

Yes, at the end of the day....it should_ be simple.

### diane — 2012-10-31T04:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-61032>

Thanks Marie. Great idea about the co-op. I have been feeding raw for 3 years, but have been going mostly to the grocery store near me, as it has a butcher in it and really great cuts (along with heart, tongue, etc). But I'm sure there's much better ways for me to get it!

In terms of supplementation - I would suspect that it's much the same as humans. The ideal is to get it from food, but some things are harder given the quality of the food now. This to me is more the issue for supplementation than those that perform in dog sports. After reading Jack's blog, I was actually pretty impressed that she recommended kelp - and I've started to use the dried kelp I bought for them lol. So they just have to share!  I also started making bone broth from their bones. So I've started to eat more like them!

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T16:51:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-61085>

Just read about a study that claims dogs fed ad libitum chose 63% fat, 30% protein and 7% carbs. Sounds like Paleo Pup to me!

[http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/releases/252177.php](http://http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/releases/252177.php)

### diane — 2012-10-31T18:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-61111>

Hey Suni - would love the link if you have it!

### maggie — 2012-11-17T21:58:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-canines.4543/post-64996>

Has anyone here had experience with treating a dog with a systemic yeast infection? I'm almost certain now that this is what is ailing my small mixed breed (possible beagle/chiahuaha mix). She's tested allergic to just about everything that grows and has a constantly recurring yeast-y ear infection, along with itchy skin that makes her bite, and runny stools.

In the past the vet has put her on prednisone when things got really bad, but I've just read that that causes even greater yeast overgrowth. Right now she's got an ear infection that isn't responding well to Zymox (usually that works).

I mentioned in the earlier thread that she has apparently recovered from a seizure disorder (almost three months off the phenobarb now, and no seizures) after going epi-paleo with me. Before that we were both eating paleo. And before paleo she was on one of those "ancestral diet" types of kibble. Only the fish has worked for the seizures. I imagine thirteen years of phenobarb has done a lot of damage, but I hope I can make it better.

Anyway, I'd really welcome hearing about any of your experiences or knowledge about handling systemic yeast.

Thanks.

# Thread: Courtesans  Fishcakes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/courtesans-fishcakes.4545/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-28T21:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/courtesans-fishcakes.4545/post-60619>

I've mentioned this book before - _Courtesans & Fishcakes: The Consuming Passions of Classical Athens.

It turns out that the _New York Times_ has a chapter online.

I don't know that the book tells us anything about fish, but it's certainly part of the interesting cultural history of fish-eating. In Classical Athens fish was the_ luxury food - much as it is Japan nowadays. (I recall Anthony Bourdain saying that the best meal he'd ever had in a life which must have included quite a few well-cooked meals was in Japan and mostly of fish and other seafood prepared with fanatical carefullness.)

Anyway, here's a taste:

> \`But if you go to the prosperous land of Ambracia and happen to see the boar-fish, buy it! Even if it costs its weight in gold, don't leave without it, lest the dread vengeance of the deathless ones breathe down on you; for this fish is the flower of nectar.' The Greeks were fond of fish. Fondness, on second thoughts, is rather too moderate a word for such a passion. What the literature of pleasure manifests, time and time again, is something rather more intense, a craving, a maddening addiction, an indecent obsession. ...
>
>
>
> What should be noted is not so much the extravagance of the language used to describe the fish, as the fact that in a work about the pleasures of eating in general, reference is made to almost nothing else. ...
>
>
>
> ... fish are sometimes found used as love-gifts in Attic vase-painting. One depicts a young man and his attendant approaching a hetaera \[courtesan\] spinning wool, with gifts of an octopus and two birds. Another vase, once in Leningrad, now lost, had a boy seated and wrapped in a cloak being offered a hoop and a large fish by a winged Cupid.
>
>
>
> It is not just their tastiness that connects fish to seduction, but also the way they look. The two sisters popularly known as the \`anchovies' mentioned in a speech of Hyperides were apparently so named because of their \`pale complexions, slender figures and large eyes'. ...
>
>
>
> ... the practice of comparing women to mouth-watering fish and fish to women seems to have been rather more general in Athenian society. Apart from the anchovy sisters mentioned above, we find flute-girls and hetaeras given nicknames like \`Sand-smelt', \`Red Mullet' and \`Cuttlefish', a practice exploited to full comic effect by the poet Antiphanes in his play She Goes Fishing, where he plays on this double-meaning of the names of fish, so that it is hard to know at any one time whether he is satirizing his victims for their love of fish or for their excessive devotion to hetaeras ...

[http://www.nytimes.com/books/first/d/davidson-courtesans.html](http://http://www.nytimes.com/books/first/d/davidson-courtesans.html)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Let's talk beef liver . . .
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-beef-liver.4546/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2

### BJK77 — 2012-10-28T21:21:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-beef-liver.4546/post-60622>

How do you know if it's 'bad?' I'm sure many would say it's always 'bad,' but I'm talking spoiled here  I absolutely love liver and generally eat it every day. I just got some ground liver from a new farm and the smell is just about killing me. It's stronger than any I've ever had before. I'm almost afraid to eat it.

I honestly have never had spoiled meat before so I don't know what it would smell like. This somewhat reminds me of dog food. It's just awful and soooo strong. I don't know if I can even stomach it if y'all say it's probably ok to eat. The infamous google seems to say that truly bad liver would have an ammonia smell and I definitely don't smell ammonia, but OMG!!

### Jude — 2012-10-29T03:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-beef-liver.4546/post-60666>

> **BJK77;62283 wrote:** How do you know if it's 'bad?' I'm sure many would say it's always 'bad,' but I'm talking spoiled here  I absolutely love liver and generally eat it every day. I just got some ground liver from a new farm and the smell is just about killing me. It's stronger than any I've ever had before. I'm almost afraid to eat it.
>
>
>
> I honestly have never had spoiled meat before so I don't know what it would smell like. This somewhat reminds me of dog food. It's just awful and soooo strong. I don't know if I can even stomach it if y'all say it's probably ok to eat. The infamous google seems to say that truly bad liver would have an ammonia smell and I definitely don't smell ammonia, but OMG!!

Perhaps it to do with what they have been fed? Turnips ? -they can give meat and milk an awful smell. We can't eat our sheep if they have been eating wild turnips--absolutely stinks- and what a waste of meat, of course the dogs think it's Christmas.....

Freshly killed sheep's liver doesn't smell. I can't stand the smell when it's being cooked -just don't like offal.

# Thread: Scientists: New GMO wheat may 'silence' vital human genes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientists-new-gmo-wheat-may-silence-vital-human-genes.4548/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jude — 2012-10-29T03:56:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientists-new-gmo-wheat-may-silence-vital-human-genes.4548/post-60667>

Australian scientists are expressing grave concerns over a new type of genetically engineered wheat that may cause major health problems for people that consume it.

[http://www.digitaljournal.com/article/332822#ixzz296GZsBOg](http://http://www.digitaljournal.com/article/332822#ixzz296GZsBOg)

"What we found is that the molecules created in this wheat, intended to silence wheat genes, can match human genes,

and through ingestion, these molecules can enter human beings and potentially silence our genes," Heinemann stated. "The findings are absolutely assured. There is no doubt that these matches exist."

Flinders University Professor Judy Carman and Safe Food Foundation Director Scott Kinnear concurred with Heinemann's analysis.

"If this silences the same gene in us that it silences in the wheat -- well, **children who are born with this enzyme not working tend to die by the age of about five,"** Carman said.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-29T15:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scientists-new-gmo-wheat-may-silence-vital-human-genes.4548/post-60693>

Fools rush in where angels fear to tread.

And then, when you factor in genetic drift...Once these genes are out there, eg RoundupReady everything, they cant be called back.

The human race will engineer their own demise.

# Thread: First round ever of HCG started today
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 71
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### natural_girl — 2012-10-29T17:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-60718>

So, I have been waiting on this stuff forever! First shipment got lost, second held in Customs and so I start today and I pray that I did this right! I am still in shock I stuck myself with a needle! LOL

So I followed the directions on the site Colleen moderates. I have 2,000 iu vials and added a total of 4 cc's bacteriostatic water to the powder in the mixing vial and did my first dose of .30 or 30 on the syringe. It just doesn't look like there is much left in that vial! I really hope I did it right! Plan on having me some Chipotle steak salad for lunch today & tomorrow since I won't be eating it for awhile.

A lot of people have commented they can't believe I am doing this through the Holiday's but what the hell. This is my busy time of year for work, crazy busy! I don't cook much because I am so busy so throwing some shrimp, chicken or steak on the George Foreman sounds perfect. I know exactly what I can eat don't have to think about it etc. I am not home much so the family can eat whatever they want. I have a big business trip in February to NYC and know that I will want some cocktails so I just figured why not. I am so tired of the weight. Yes, I want to be Optimal but to me that means a healthy weight also. I did not weigh this morning and I am loading today & tomorrow so I will weight tomorrow morning to see where I am at. Hoping to only have to do this one round but not sure cause I probably have anywhere from 30-40 pounds to lose. Although I care more about the size I am in than what the scale says. Today I am a size 12 and would love to be a 6 again so we will see!

Would love any suggestions on meals & sides from those of you who have done this before. I am making sauerkraut tonight so I can use that as a side often and I do plan on eating shrimp often. I have organic, pastured grass fed beef as well.

Will check in here often to keep me accountable & for support. Thanks Colleen & Cavemam for answering all my PM's really appreciate it!

### colleencoble — 2012-10-30T22:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-60990>

No. You need to eat the food as laid out. An extra 100 grams of protein is too much to sub in for those measly breadsticks. LOL Cavemam may have subbed a fruit out for that. I don't really recommend doing that long term though. It seems to cause problems late with thyroid suppression in some if the carbs fall too low.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T04:04:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-61029>

SO HCG Ladies,

Here are a couple of things I found on my round. If it conflicts with what Colleen says, go with her! She's seen more success and failure and will not guide you astray.

I had a 25 lbd net loss and so far have kept it below my LIW (last injection weight). I think my round ended mid august, so clearly I don't have much long term success to guide one.

I found:

1\. Fish just didn't cut it for me. I was way too hungry after meals and I never got that full feeling. When combined with strawberries it just wasn't enough food for me volume wise. I found I really needed the chicken/beef. I did mostly chicken with beef every couple of days and I kept TRYING fish, and would go back to chicken or beef. I would combine a chicken and beef day.

2\. I didn't have same veggie more than one time a day, and used fermented/pickled veggies quite a bit. It really helps the dry chicken! I did not mix veggies at a meal.

3\. I did not eat off protocol or fancy things up. I seasoned with salt and pepper and the allotted daily lemon and that was it. No recipes etc. I have a theory that its probably for the best if the food is bland and unappealing. Much less likely to take too many bites!

4\. I would weigh out several portions raw all at once.

5\. Dose issues aside, I found those times when hunger would creep in - a larger apple with my protein did the trick.

6\. I did not eat off protocol AT ALL.

7\. I did eat fruit every day. I'm for it. I mostly ate it with both meals. I did strawberries and apples.

8\. I cut melba and after adding up my calories for the day I made up the difference with veggie and protein. I did no more than 500 calories.

9\. Don't panic if you don't poop. PM me

10\. I did my round over the 4th of July family vacation. I pre cooked and took my food with me. It sucked so hard. It SUCKED MAJORLY. So, if my hungry fat arse can do that - you can make it 40 days through the holiday. I know you can! Go team!

### colleencoble — 2012-10-31T15:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-61073>

I could not eat beef on the protocol without major stalls. I ate almost exclusively fish and shrimp with some chicken. If you had hunger on fish, cavemam, your dose likely needed adjusted. On the right dose, it doesn't matter what you eat--you just aren't hungry.  But you did great! 25 pounds is super!

### natural_girl — 2012-10-31T16:56:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-61086>

Cavemam was the 25 pounds you lost including what you gained loading? I gained 6 pounds loading! Not surprising since I really never eat carbs and ate a ton of potatoes! I feel the water I gained funny thing is when I used to eat carbs I would bloat like crazy but lately that doesn't really happen maybe just a tiny bit but I feel like it's normal. I think that might mean my hormones are getting back on track.

Colleen when I read Pounds & Inches it said not to take thyroid meds while on protocol but you said you continued on yours right? It said people who have been on them long term lose less weight while on hcg? I have only been on my T3 since March so hoping that won't apply as much to me.

I plan on having shrimp as much as possible to stay Epi Paleo. I am not a fan of fish and love lobster & crab but not without ghee so shrimp is it! I will have pastured, organic chicken and occasionally beef but not often since I am low on beef and my cow won't be in & cut until end of November and most cuts I have left I think might be to fatty. I adore beef but I really want to lose as much as I can. I am hoping whatever I have left to lose after this I will be able to lose through IF or the Bulletproof RFL protocol

So today is my first restriction day. I brought 6 grilled shrimp, about a 1/4 head cabbage & apple to work for lunch. Did my 3rd injection of .30 cc and will continue with that dose. I am a bit hungry but thinking it might be my mind and I am sure the first few days are the worse? Can I chew a piece of sugarless gum? And I know we can have vinegar but any kind? What about balsamic?

Thanks girls! I am praying I lose a ton!

> Thanks girls! I am praying I lose a ton!

Watch the balsamic, they almost always have calories/carbs. Lemon is a nice thing, too. Look at is as a chance to cleanse out everything. If the hunger is sated - might as well get the benefits of resting the liver etc.

My 25 lbds was net. I gained a lot at load in. I was much bigger than you though, so you will most likely have smaller losses! I went up to 240 after load in, and ended at 208.8

I keep seeing more success in others with the bigger fatter gainier load ins. SO that was probably a helper.

Also, get out of your mind that you are "over" when the round is over. You really have to stay vigilant and do restrictions and slow introductions and steak days after the round is over during P3. I thought I was NEVER going to stabalize. . and was doing some back to back steak days to keep within the 2 lbd limit. Then boom, hit week 4 after and it all clicked and it was easy to maintain.

### colleencoble — 2012-10-31T17:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-61098>

Most of us stay on our thyroid meds. Some have stellar losses. I lost about 20 pounds per round but my metabolism was shot. And 20 pounds was fabulous as far as I was concerned. Most people count the load weight because it has to be lost too. Dr. Simeon's counted load weight. Your dose might be a bit high. If you're still hungry in the morning, do down to 25 units.

I think I did - with some exceptions. I would eat apple on most days, and then strawberries were usually my number two. I thought they helped me get down that damn chicken!

SO, weighing in week 1 is fun. Weighing in week 2 makes baby jesus cry!

I would use strawberries to increase the calories of my lunch when needed. Fruit is NOT just a carbs/fructose/glucose story. Just like with fish, there is a lot more to it than just the triad macro nutrients.

I would err on the side of working with your hunger + going as close to protocol as possible. When I said earlier I ate beef, I should have clarified - I only ate it maybe 1-2X a week and usually on a fish day as fish never seemed to work for me.

Your losses are so awesome!!! I know it's a lot of load weight, but still fun. That first 10 lbds is exciting. The rest a little less so.

### colleencoble — 2012-11-05T16:12:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-62202>

Your body will slow for a bit. It's normal to stall out at about 10 pounds. Now things will slow down to .5-.75 per day average. Some days you won't lose anything, then will lose a pound the next day. That kind of thing. It won't be consistent. The first week's losses are mostly water weight. Then as your body starts adding back in the water and continues to lose fat, the scale will be inconsistent.

### natural_girl — 2012-11-06T21:13:06+0200

VLCD #7 down a measely .2 pounds. Total 10 pounds.

I think my cycle might start interfering here some. First I messed up my progesterone dosage! I was supposed to go up and by up I mean double, since Dzugan just changed my P. So I was supposed to increase on Friday and for some reason thought it was Monday. I don't know why I had it wrote on the calendar! I guess I actually need to look at the calendar! So yesterday morning I woke up and my breast were very swollen & tender which doesn't happen much anymore unless I increase P and thought it was strange then I look at the calendar and realize I dosed wrong for 3 days. I am supposed to start my cycle in a week but it feels like it might come earlier, I guess I will see.

### Charlotte — 2012-11-10T16:52:43+0200

Next week I am starting my second round of hCG.First round, 5 weeks ago I lost 15 kg. Now I am hoping to lose the same. In between I did Epi-Paleo and lost another 2 kg. So I am happy. Today I got me a whirlpool for outdoors which I am putting up on my patio. I can keep this at 50-55Â°F and since ice in Germany is hard to come by, especially in the winter, I bought two (hot)water bottles from rubber which I will fill up, put in the freezer and put on my chest. Afterwards I can roll around in the snow. And if I feel like it I can put the temperature up and have a hot bath. So far, I did cold showers. Now temperature in Germany is down to 40Â°F already. Wonder, how this goes with hCG. Eating is not a problem, on hCG I did well on steak, fish and vegetable. I almost always skipped the apples and never did the bread stick due to gluten allergy. But I nevertheless had stalls, but after six weeks I was rid of the first 15 kg. So I think, stalls are normal. My diabetes vanished completely and I do not take any medication anymore. The body is amazing in knowing when to lose and when to stall. So I wish you that your weight loss continues to make progress and you do not lose motivation.

Colleen,

Â

Next day I was up .2 lbs, yesterday up .6 and today .8!!!! I don't know why it keeps increasing even though I have followed protocol since. It is sugar free though still. I usually chew one or two pieces at most a day but just in case I am giving it to my daughter. My dose is at .20 and I am not hungry. I did lower it some since I was starving on .25 and went down to .22 but was still hungry a lot. I feel the weight or bloat in my stomach. I so should have avoided that darn GF stuffing! I am so mad at myself but at the same time it just stinks that I can't have a few bites of what most people would consider healthy food without blowing up! I guess it will be like this forever! My body is so sensitive! Funny thing is though too I wonder if something had gluten or dairy or was because since then I have had a slightly scratchy sore throat which I usually get if I eat either one of those foods.Â  Now what should I do to get back to losing again? ### colleencoble — 2012-11-25T14:22:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-round-ever-of-hcg-started-today.4554/post-65568>

That is very normal. When you eat off protocol, the gains can go on for a couple of days. There's nothing you can do to avoid the consequences though. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Just stick to protocol and it will start falling off again. You can't have a few bites of ANYTHING on hcg. It's not a diet. It's a hormonal protocol and anything off the list can disrupt the hormonal response. That's the first thing people have to realize. And just because something is gluten free, doesn't mean it's healthy. Gluten free dressing still has grains and gluten free stuff is often higher in carbs than wheat products. So I avoid them the majority of the time.Â 

When you get to your ideal weight, if you stick to Doc's leptin timing, your insulin resistance will get better and better and you won't react to badly to the occasional ingestion of higher carbs than normal. Good luck!
