# Thread: Going raw
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T13:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66034>

OK I give up. Â I'm headed off to Sackett's today (local purveyor of fine local grass fed meats) and grabbing a steak. Â But the idea of chowing down on it ice cold straight from the fridge is more than kind of repulsive. Â To those of you who eat your meat raw do you let it sit out a bit to warm up to room temp? Â I know the big worry about doing that is bacterial growth.

Â 

Do you take tiny itty bitty little bits or do you just chomp? Â Do you add anything to it? Â Capers? Chopped onion? Â S&P?

Â 

And don't expect me to embrace either MHS or high meat any time soon. Â You know ... old dogs, new tricks, blah blah blah.

### kathylu — 2012-12-01T14:15:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66038>

Hi Darleen,

Â 

Why don't you try searing the steak in a hot pan, say 1 minute per side? Then you get the best of both worlds, that cooked flavor and raw most of the way through? It will still be raw and cool/cold in the center. Then I would salt and pepper, maybe melt a pat of herb butter on it...yum!

### endless — 2012-12-01T14:16:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66039>

I partially thaw it and slice it thin, or dice it up, and then add some flavor to it and let it marinate for a couple of hours. I prefer it closer to room temperature.
You could do a tartare-style seasoning with diced onion, dijon mustard and capers...even a raw egg yolk. If you google raw beef recipes you'll find a lot like that.
I like Thai flavors, so I use (for 1/2lb of meat)
2 T lemon juice
2 T fish sauce
sprinkle of red chile powder
2 green onions, sliced thin
some fresh cilantro, chopped

Mix it all together and let it sit covered for a few hours.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T14:56:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66046>

Those both sound yummy. I had, actually, thought about just searing the meat as a way to get past my social conditioning.

### Inger — 2012-12-01T15:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66052>

Darleen, you can finely take it out and let it warm up if you like it that way better. I have never had an issue with bacteria overgrowth..lol It is CW that is afraid of every bacteria.. as far as I have tested it, they do us good, the opposite actually! This is when the food is raw/raw animal food. Cooked foods can very well get posionous when long out in the warm. Because they have no more of the live bacterias and stuff.
I often make delicious dishes from raw meat and fish. I add capers, mustard, balsamic vinegar or vinegar, fresh herbs, onion, whatever you like! I actually like it much more to create nice dishes from raw ingredients.. it is so fresh and crispy, and fun! And I feel lighter and better after raw foods too..

This is my all time favorit that I make really often. Carpaccio, very thin sliced beef with onion, balsamic vinegar (brand without sugar, only grapes used), olive oil, fresh ground pepper, seasalt. I love crispy dried mushrooms on the top..

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Inger — 2012-12-01T15:30:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66053>

These too. Burgers. I eat them really often. They are air dried burgers, ground moose or any other meat, dried at body temp over night in my dehydrator. I eat them with coconutoil or raw butter on the top, and maybe raw onion-slices and slices of cucumber and some seasalt too. So yummy for breakfast and so fast to eat and nicely warm direct from the dehydrator!

This one is with raw butter and dill.

One could add spices to the ground meat before dehydrating, if one likes!

### caroline — 2012-12-01T15:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66054>

Those both sound yummy. I had, actually, thought about just searing the meat as a way to get past my social conditioning.

Darleen - do I hear the winds of change blowing thru?

### Inger — 2012-12-01T15:49:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66055>

I love tartar too. I got this recipe from a nice French restaurant in Hamburg where I used to eat tartar often! They made it exactly like this;

Finely ground, raw meat (they used beef but it works with any meat), maybe 2-300 grams
1 spoonful of french mustard (Dijon)
1-2 raw eggyolks (you can leave out these if you cannot have eggs)
2-3 spoonful of a nice olive oil
1 spoonful of fresh parsley, chopped
1 spoonful of fresh onion, chopped
1 smallish spoonful of capers
some drops tabasco, to taste.. more if you like it hot!
some drops Worchestershiresauce, to taste (I usually use balsamic vinegar instead)
salt and fresh ground pepper to taste

Mix everything well and taste.

You can add a touch of cinnamon, makes a nice flavor! And you can add a splash of balsamic vinegar too if you like..
You can vary the amounts of the ingredients depending on your taste.

This is very good when one is not used to raw. In Europe it is a very common and popular dish in restaurants.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T17:01:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66059>

### kathylu — 2012-12-01T22:22:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66090>

Just a suggestion if you are going to make beef tartare. Buy a good quality steak and have the butcher grind it for you, or take it home (preferred) and mince/grind it yourself. Don't use pre-ground meat...that's where folks get into trouble with bacterial contamination.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T19:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-raw.5016/post-66174>

> **endless saidÂ** 
>
> Holy crap....I give up. Seriously...what is the friggin trick for quoting?? When I push the button it puts both the quote and the reply in the shaded box.
>
> This forum is really not very user friendly...:-(

The trick is to make sure your cursor is outside the quoted area. Â It should appear all the way over to the left. Â If it's IN the quote, press return and then press the quote button up above your new post to turn it off. Â Took me a bit to figure it out.

I'm gonna jump in here and join the conversation if you all don't mind. Â I've been taking cytomel for about 6 weeks now. Â I had a couple of days where I just went WILD so cut way back to 1/4 tab dose. Â I've been trying to get the dose up to the full tab (25 mcg) and yesterday just decided to go for it. ### Destiny — 2012-12-02T18:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-with-cytomel.5018/post-66164>

Darlene,

I was extremely sensitive to T3 from the beginning. I started at the beginning of August with very small 3-4 doses during the day. I could not go over 12.5 mcg for almost 6 weeks. Otherwise, I felt a strange spasm in my abdomen, etc. It was very unpleasant. Eventually, I was up to 15 mcg, etc. I tried a few times to force it and it did not work. Today, I am up to 25-30 mcg a day.

Â 

It seems that you are very sensitive to it as I was. I would recommend baby steps.

### Destiny — 2012-12-02T19:21:27+0200

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-02T22:26:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-with-cytomel.5018/post-66190>

Destiny, IMHO I don't think you are quite there yet.Â  Your ratio still isn't above 20, nor is your T3 in the top of the range. Since you are getting better and feeling better you are on your way!Â  Also if the reason you have an rT3 problem is because of adrenals, iron, etc you also need to fix that issue first.Â  So you may need to test the iron again and see where you are.Â  Although I bet your adrenals are the culprit with all you have been through. They were the hardest for me to get fixed but I have to keep an eye on them.Â  Then there is the whole brain connection with teh adrenals that I need to readdress since I may only THINK I fixed the adrenals but not really.

Â

### Claudia — 2012-12-10T17:36:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-with-cytomel.5018/post-67041>

I think Destiny is making such great progress. Why does she have to increase her t3 so high? Maybe 29 or thereabouts is good for her. I have mixed feeling about the high doses of t3. My friend ended up in the ER because she got up to 75mcg and was having palpitations and felt like she was dying. She advised me not to follow this protocol because it could be dangerous.

I have similar labs to Destiny. my ratio is the same of rt3 to t3 is 8 (as Destiny's July labs). I'm probably still too low with the t3. My doc started me at 5mcg in the summer, around July, and I felt better right away, had more energy, felt more like myself, it was great. Then, it changed in September. I started feeling all my hypo symptoms again, fatigue, depression, no energy, working out hard, climbing stairs made me achy, brain fog, moody. So, I called the doc and he increased to 10 mcg.

I felt maybe a slight improvement but that didn't last more than a few weeks. I tried increasing it to 15mcg on my own, but got nervous and it didn't make any improvement anyway.

Â 

I'm' going back to the doc tomorrow. Any pieces of the puzzle that i"m missing? What labs should I get besides the ones I usually get: free t3, free t4, TSH, reverse t3. I always hear talk of iron and adrenals being the cause for these conversion t hyroid issues. What iron labs do I need, besides ferritin, which was on the higher side when I got it last? And for adrenals, do I do saliva? I still Â haven't done it.

And I think maybe I do need more t3 but I want to take baby steps with it. I feel awful lately

Any ideas? Thanks for the advice.

### caroline — 2012-12-10T18:03:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-with-cytomel.5018/post-67044>

I think you definitely need more labs Â - cortisol saliva - have you checked out ZRT labs and what you can get from them?

### caroline — 2012-12-10T18:23:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-with-cytomel.5018/post-67046>

I forgot to add - Destiny's situation is very different. Â All of us are very different even tho it might look like we have somewhat the same problems.

Dr. Kruse is always careful to tell us that - we can't always apply what someone else is doing to our situation and it could be quite dangerous. Â When we are reading about stuff - we can get great new ideas about things to look at that might apply - so then we can do some research.... but he always says we need a Doctor/partner to get us to where we need to go. Â Have you had an email lab consult with Dr.K? Â - then you could cut to the chase and plot a course for YOU. Â  just my 2cents - I am by no means an expert - just struggling along like everyone else here.

### Destiny — 2012-12-10T18:44:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progress-with-cytomel.5018/post-67048>

What Caroline suggests is very wise. If your have a cortisol problem, you will have a problem with T3. If you can get salivary cortisol done, it could shade some light on your particular situation. If your ferritin was on a higher side, you don't have to worry about that. I am not sure if you hear about the site stop the thyroid madness. It helped me a lot at the beginning.Â  Just in case here is the link.

Â 

http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com

# Thread: Cholesterol going in a wrong direction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Destiny — 2012-12-01T19:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66072>

**April 2012 - not horrible

Total cholesterol **219 <200 mg/dl

HDL **71 >50Â  mg/dl
Triglycerides **55 >150
LDL **137 <100 mg/dl
\-VLDL **11 7-32 mg/dl

Chol/HDL ratio **3.1 <5.8

LDL/HDL ratio **1.93 .56

**September 2012 - better

Total cholesterol **248 125-200 mg/dl

HDL **95 >50Â  mg/dl
Triglycerides **68 46 mg/dl
LDL 137** <100 mg/dl
Non HDL Cho 151** mg/dl

Chol/HDL ratio 2.6** <5.8

LDL/HDL ratio 1.4** .56

November 2012 - the worst in my life**
Went ketogenic at the beginning of October, lost 13 lbs

Total cholesterol 232** 125-200 mg/dl

HDL 54** >50Â  mg/dl
Triglycerides 141!!!!!** 46 mg/dl
LDL 150!!!!**<100 mg/dl
Non HDL Cho 178** mg/dl

Chol/HDL ratio 4.3** <5.8

LDL/HDL ratio 2.7** .56

I did not eat anything with sugar, fructose and had a minimum of greens. My triglycerides look like I am eating cakes all day long!

Not sure how to go about this .....

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-01T20:46:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66081>

Jack has said several times when you're loosing weight -your Cholesterol panels are worthless

you went keto and also started treatment for BC, had the stress of Sandy - its quite possible your body is just rebelling in general Â definitely worth a consult if you can swing it...Â

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-01T21:21:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66083>

I don't know if this applies to you, but there is suggestion that too much protein converts to glucose.Â  Jack and others usually suggest a high fat, moderate protein diet.Â  (I think people sometimes think they are in ketosis but are not necessarily.) I wonder if it's possible that being too protein heavy with insufficient fat causes trigs to potentially rise.Â  I also wonder if this could have to do with detox pathways.Â  Just thinking out loud.

### Destiny — 2012-12-01T22:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66092>

Those are all good points to think about. I also added many more supplements, maybe that is hard on the system as well.

I think I eat enough fat even though I don't feel easy about that since every second article concerning BC talks about eating low fat.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-01T23:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66098>

Destiny, I totally get why the high-fat thing is scary in the face of breast cancer! Â However, when I look more closely at the evidence, it seems ketogenic diets are the best option. Â And I found that it needs to be higher fat than I originally thought. Â Have a look at this articles. Â It is in a peer reviewed journal (with Dr Colin Champ, the ''Caveman Doctor' as the lead author)... he looked at all the evidence about what is associated with cancer. Â 

Â 

[http://www.hindawi.com/journals/ijbc/2012/506868/](http://http://www.hindawi.com/journals/ijbc/2012/506868/)Â

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-01T23:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66099>

And this blog post:

[http://www.lucastafur.com/2011/08/advan ... et\_10.html](http://http://www.lucastafur.com/2011/08/advanced-cancer-and-ketogenic-diet_10.html)

### Destiny — 2012-12-02T09:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66126>

Those sources are very reassuring in regards to high fats, Lauren, thank you. I have to figure out how to guess a proper ratio of protein and fat. Something like a small piece of meat (like my palm of my hand) and 2 tbs of fat to make it practical for a day to day life.

My glucose used to be in high 90-100's even with Metformin. Now, it is in 70-80's. According to the article, I should be in 55-65', so I have a long way to go in this regard.

Â 

Shijin might be right, that my body is in a chaos. I just could not believe that my HDL went down so much and my trigs went that high.Â 

I also noticed that my WBC went down from 5.2 to 4.4 during those two months. Â 

Â 

Anyhow, I think that I am on the right track even though these labs made me really nervous. Previously, it took me 10 months to lose 10 lbs. Now, when I completely cut out any glucose and ate only some low carb veggies for carbs portion of my diet, I went down 11 lbs in two months. I am at 131 lbs and think that I should get to 125 lbs at 5'4" and maintain that in order to limit any reoccurrence.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-02T09:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66127>

great info KiwiLauren...Â

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-02T15:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66158>

Destiny, I had another thought this morning. Â Are you trending your hsCRP? Â Given that trigs are associated with inflammation/oxidation, I think knowing how your hsCRP is doing would be critical info to have. Â If it is also rising, I'd be more concerned. Â But if it's falling and/or remaining stable, I think you could likely see it as a temporary cholesterol panel adjustment.

### Destiny — 2012-12-02T18:56:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66171>

That makes sense, Lauren. I did not ask for another test since my hsCRP was at 0.3 back in July.

I will have to ask for that when I go next month to check my cholesterol.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-02T22:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66193>

One more bit of info for you, Destiny, from Chris Kresser's interview with Chris Masterjohn. (he did a series of 3 interviews on cholesterol with him).

Â 

_Chris Masterjohn: Absolutely. So, the first thing that we need to understand is that there are good reasons and bad reasons for increases in cholesterol in the blood. So, one of the reasons that cholesterol can increase is if weâ€™re clearing lipids from the liver. Letâ€™s say, for example, that a person has nonalcoholic fatty liver disease and they start resolving it. Well, one of the key problems with fatty liver disease is that the lipids get stuck in the liver and theyâ€™re not being released into the bloodstream, so once you start clearing that, part of what may happen is you may get an increase in triglycerides, and you may get an increase in cholesterol in the blood. And that is a good thing because nonalcoholic fatty liver disease is not only very dangerous for the liver, but itâ€™s actually a much stronger predictor of cardiovascular disease risk. ...

_Chris Kresser: So, youâ€™ve written about this, Chris, what you were just talking about in terms of switching to a Primal/Paleo type of diet and the lipids going up because the fatty liver is sort of unpacking itself. And youâ€™ve written about this extensively that choline is one of the nutrients that makes that possible, so can you say a little bit more about that?_

Chris Masterjohn: Sure. So, the best sources of choline are liver and egg yolks. There are also a number of other nutrients such as folate, for example, that reduces the need for choline. So, it youâ€™re increasing your intake of liver, egg yolks, and leafy green vegetables â€” you know, a general increase in nutrient density in your diet â€” itâ€™s very likely that if you do have fatty liver you are going to contribute to its resolution, because choline is the key nutrient that is needed to package the fats in the liver and export them into the bloodstream so they can be metabolized by other tissues._

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-02T23:03:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cholesterol-going-in-a-wrong-direction.5019/post-66194>

> **KiwiLauren said**
> One more bit of info for you, Destiny, from Chris Kresser's interview with Chris Masterjohn. (he did a series of 3 interviews on cholesterol with him).
>
> Â 
>
> _Chris Masterjohn: Absolutely. So, the first thing that we need to understand is that there are good reasons and bad reasons for increases in cholesterol in the blood. So, one of the reasons that cholesterol can increase is if weâ€™re clearing lipids from the liver. Letâ€™s say, for example, that a person has nonalcoholic fatty liver disease and they start resolving it. Well, one of the key problems with fatty liver disease is that the lipids get stuck in the liver and theyâ€™re not being released into the bloodstream, so once you start clearing that, part of what may happen is you may get an increase in triglycerides, and you may get an increase in cholesterol in the blood. And that is a good thing because nonalcoholic fatty liver disease is not only very dangerous for the liver, but itâ€™s actually a much stronger predictor of cardiovascular disease risk. ...
>
> _Chris Kresser: So, youâ€™ve written about this, Chris, what you were just talking about in terms of switching to a Primal/Paleo type of diet and the lipids going up because the fatty liver is sort of unpacking itself. And youâ€™ve written about this extensively that choline is one of the nutrients that makes that possible, so can you say a little bit more about that?_

So, it youâ€™re increasing your intake of liver, egg yolks, and leafy green vegetables â€” you know, a general increase in nutrient density in your diet â€” itâ€™s very likely that if you do have fatty liver you are going to contribute to its resolution, because choline is the key nutrient that is needed to package the fats in the liver and export them into the bloodstream so they can be metabolized by other tissues._

My first 90 day labs after going epi-paleo with a concentration on choline rich foods ( I eat a lot of lobster roe, liver, and eggs) my cholesterol was up, my trigs were up,but still low and ldl was up and hsCRP was up. None of these increases was alarming and with T2D and Hashi's there are a lot of variables. Hopefully liver defatting has something to do with it.

### Destiny — 2012-12-03T07:58:33+0200

Â

# Thread: 3 Month Progress Report - Kicking Paleo Butt !
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-month-progress-report-kicking-paleo-butt.5022/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thor — 2012-12-01T20:25:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-month-progress-report-kicking-paleo-butt.5022/post-66080>

.

Just 5 months ago at the age of 38, I was about to be admitted into hospice care. After seeing more than 60+ doctors from around the world including alternative medicine - I thought nothing would work.Â  I've spent more than $300,000 on medical bills and no answers.Â  4 years ago I was involved in a bad auto accident.Â  Some teenager blonde cheerleader type forgot that brakes are used when cars in front of you are stopped.Â 

Â 

Having a degree in Bio-chemistry - I thought there has to be a solution to this problem.Â  But what?Â  After learning how serious genetic defects are - I got tested.Â  YIKES, I came back positive on an entire laundry list of genetic hickups.Â  With that much wrong there must be a "factory recall" on my model !Â  Thank God for Dr Kendal Stewart, MD for saving my life - he is the only doctor who knows anything about MTHFR, COMT, CBS ect...

Â 

Then by chance I ran a fasting insulin test - in four years of seeing doctors nobody thought to check it.Â  Sure enough my labs showed high fasting insulin, thyroid problems and all the other leptin resistant markers.Â Â  Looks like the final piece of the puzzle was found.Â  However, finding someone either MD, DO, ND, ect who could treat it?Â Â  Like finding a needle in a hay stack.Â  I'd ask a medical provider about Leptin & Insulin resistance - all I got back was a deer in the headlights look.Â  Ugh? Leptin who?Â Â  After 4 years of digging through mountains of research and finding doctors in all areas of the world - I thought I must be able to find someone.Â Â  One thing lead to another and I found Jack's site.Â Â  Now I've gotÂ the information and how to get started but still no Doctor in my local area that could further the process.Â  Yet again I found someone who had a slight clue about leptin and insulin resistance.Â  It's been hard to twist the docs ear but I'm making progress.Â  Progress not perfection.Â Â  One day at a time.

Â 

I've always had a kick ass and move forward attitude; my biceps are bigger than most peoples waistÂ Â Â  If I didn't have such a gurr attitudeÂ doctors 4 years ago would have had me on a morphine pump and kept me medicated til the end!Â  Once people get "sucked" into the medical world of treatment it's a downward spiral.Â  You'll never get better but only worse.Â Â  I know first hand - my dad was killed by a neurosurgeon on the operating table.Â  That neurosurgeon had 8 other patients die from his gross errors and is still in practice to this day.Â  I was not about to head down the road of pills and surgery.

Â 

Where your attention goes, your energy flows -Â  I never focus on what's wrong but what will be right.Â  Come high or hell I'm going to be healthy.Â  Failure is not a choice.Â  Pain is weakness leaving the body.Â  Pain is only temporary but failure is forever.

Â 

Hear me roar!Â Â  Who am I ?Â  I AM A CHAMPION !

Â 

I will conquer what has not been conquered.Â  Defeat will not be in my creed.

Â 

I WILL believe what others have douted.Â  I have trained my mind and my body WILL follow.

Â 

I AM A CHAMPION!

Â 

Weakness will never be in my heart.Â  No one will deny me - no one will define me.Â Â  No one will tell me who I am or what I can do.

Â 

Belief will change my world

Â 

Defeat - Retreat, those are not my words.Â  I understand victory!

Â 

No matter how bad things go - my heart and my mind will carry my body when my limbs are too weak.

Â 

I will not worry about what's going on right here, right now - I WILL define myself.Â  I will write my own praises. No one tell me otherwise.

Â 

Winning is a habit but so is losing.Â  Doing the right thing isn't a sometime thing - it's an ALL the time thing!Â Â  There is not room for 2nd place.

Â 

Don't talk about - be about it !!!!!Â Â  Go where you are afraid of going.

Â 

I'm down 60 pounds, getting insulin in check and feeling amazing.Â  This shit works.Â  I only have one speed - ON

Â 

I will only procede HARDCORE - go hard or go home.Â 

Â 

I'm using the cool fat burner vest, CT Bath and cryo-sauna to blast the piss out of my fat.Â Â  Fat will not succeed - I will win !!Â Â 

Â 

[http://youtu.be/hNbaf1Uoqk8?t=23s\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;hNbaf1Uoqk8?t=23s\]http://youtu.be/hNbaf1Uoqk8?t=23s\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;hNbaf1Uoqk8?t=23s)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Curcumin has been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antiviral, antibacterial, antifungal, and anticancer activities and thus has a potential against various malignant diseases, diabetes, allergies, arthritis, Alzheimer's disease, and other chronic illnesses. These effects are mediated through the regulation of various transcription factors, growth factors, inflammatory cytokines, protein kinases, and other enzymes. Curcumin exhibits activities similar to recently discovered tumor necrosis factor blockers (e.g., HUMIRA, REMICADE, and ENBREL), a vascular endothelial cell growth factor blocker (e.g., AVASTIN), human epidermal growth factor receptor blockers (e.g., ERBITUX, ERLOTINIB, and GEFTINIB), and a HER2 blocker (e.g., HERCEPTIN).
http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17569205

# Thread: Please hack my newest labs....thyroid related
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-my-newest-labs-thyroid-related.5032/>

### bennyha — 2012-12-03T12:25:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-my-newest-labs-thyroid-related.5032/post-66221>

I have been struggling with my health the past few years, and it's getting worse with each passing week. I am a 30 year old male, 5'11, 155 lbs. Had chronically elevated cortisol for a year or so period due to fasting, over-exercising, 1000 mg+ caffeine intake in form of pills during fast as well as other fat burners, and chronic stress due to gambling addiction. Developed serious binge eating disorder and destroyed leptin signally. Would eat 15,000 calories in one sitting! Eventually I crashed and my reproductive hormones have basically stopped. Now I have what feels like chronic low cortisol and have a very hard time making it through the day. Other symptoms:

\-constant, chronic brain fog-difficult to make it through an entire day/can't read or concentrate on anything
\-unable to sweat-haven't sweat for over a year now
\-cold hands and feet and cold overall-body temp avg. is 97.6
\-constant extreme fatigue-no longer able to exercise or work out/crash every day mid-morning and mid afternoon
\-constipation-poop only once or twice a week and when I do it's a 1 on the Bristol Stool Chart (just a few little pepples)
\-bad acne
\-no libido at all/very premature ejaculation
\-no facial/body hair growth at all
\-very depressed and horrible overall feeling of well being/things that use to matter no longer do and nothing seems to matter to me anymore except making it to the end of the day
\-poor sleep-wake up every A.M. b/t 3:30-4:30 to pee and rarely get back to sleep

I could go on, but I think you get the picture. Here are the two sets of labs I have taken so far. I plan on getting a full hormone panel in the coming weeks when I can somehow come up with the money. I am uninsured so these labs are costing me a ton of money I unfortunately just don't have. Also, been doing Leptin Reset since January and CT on and off since February. Stared EPCOTx protocol/Epi-paleo about a month ago. Binge eating desires have slowed but still have strongest temptations about once every week or two. Tells you how bad I damaged my leptin.

**July 2012-ZRT Labs
Estradiol (saliva)..........0.9........0.5-2.2
Testosterone (saliva).....73........72-148
DHEAS (saliva)...........15.2.......2-23
Cortisol AM (saliva).......6.4.......3.7-9.5
Cortisol noon (saliva).....3.1.......1.2-3.0
Cortisol evening.............0.8.......0.6-1.9
Cortisol night (saliva)......0.9.......0.4-1.0
PSA (blood)...................3.1.......0.5-2 (optimal)
Free T4 (blood)..............1.3........0.7-2.5
Free T3 (blood)..............2.5........2.5-6.5
TSH (blood)...................3.5........0.5-3.0
TPO (blood)..................25..........0-150

November 2012-LapCorp (all serum samples)**
Iron Bind.Cap.(TIBC)......257 ug/dL.....250 âˆ’ 450
UIBC............................141ug/dL......150 âˆ’ 375
Iron, Serum...................116 ug/dL......40 âˆ’ 155
Iron Saturation.................45 %...........15 âˆ’ 55
Reverse T3, Serum.........17.1 ng/dL......9.2 âˆ’ 24.1
Câˆ’Reactive Protein, HS....0.10 mg/L
Ferritin, Serum................290 ng/mL......30 âˆ’ 400
Thyroid Pero (TPO) Ab......5 IU/mL..........0 âˆ’ 34
Antithyroglobulin Ab.........20 IU/mL.........0 âˆ’ 40
Free T3 ,Serum................1.9 pg/mL.......2.0 âˆ’ 4.4
T3/Rev T3 ratio.................11.1................>20

Any help anyone can give me would be greatly appreciated. I know I need more info and labs and plan on doing so as I can continue to come up with the financial resources to do so. I know from constant reading and re-reading and taking notes of the current BG series on the blog I have a lot of underlying problems, not just thyroid. But I feel as if I need some kind of thyroid boost to get things going. Every day is such a struggle and it's getting worse. I am so appreciative for anyone who can offer some advice/suggestions as to what I should do next. Thank you from the bottom of my heart

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-03T16:54:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-my-newest-labs-thyroid-related.5032/post-66250>

Hi Bennyha!Â  I'm sure the carbs were helping feed the T3 but you can feel good without carbs.Â  Did you ever do the thyroid meds?Â  I'm guessing you haven't started those based on your labs.Â  That FT3 is so low I don't know how you get up in the morning.Â  Your adrenals aren't the best but also aren't totally trashed either, although I would guess without thyroid meds you are continuing to stress the adrenals as your body has to get energy from somewhere. Since you have such a low FT3/rT3 ratio I would use T3 as the med of choice. I would definitely eat AI since you are showing some thyroid ABs even though they are not out of range. But even with iodine the T3 is so low.

Also your iron looks a tiny bit high but Ferritin seems quite high which is probably due to inflammation.Â  But at least that won't be a problem with taking T3.Â  I think your adrenals are OK too for T3.Â  This is only my opinion but if you are able to conjure up some T3 and start slowly and get to feeling a little better you can tackle some of the other things going on.Â  I've been that low and know how hard it is to function.Â  I hope that helps.

# Thread: Thoughts on high Reverse T3 and how to get rid of it

Hello all. I am having some real difficulties as to what I should be doing to start feeling better. I have about 10 hypothyroid symptoms for the past year, and it seems to be getting worse with each passing month. The constant thick brain fog and fatigue, no libido, always being cold, constipation, crappy sleep, acne, no longer able to sweat no matter how hot I am and horrible overall sense of well-being is becoming more than I can take. Btw, I am a 30 year old male, 5'11, 155 lbs. Had severely elevated cortisol for over a year which trashed leptin signally and then battled a 2 year binge eating disorder. I have taken the following tests this past year:

**July 2012-ZRT Labs
Estradiol (saliva)â€¦â€¦â€¦.0.9â€¦â€¦..0.5-2.2

Cortisol night (saliva)â€¦â€¦0.9â€¦â€¦.0.4-1.0

Free T3 (blood)â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦..2.5â€¦â€¦..2.5-6.5

November 2012-LapCorp (all serum samples)**
Iron Bind.Cap.(TIBC)â€¦â€¦257 ug/dLâ€¦..250 âˆ’ 450
UIBCâ€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦.141ug/dLâ€¦â€¦150 âˆ’ 375
Iron, Serumâ€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦.116 ug/dLâ€¦â€¦40 âˆ’ 155
Iron Saturationâ€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦..45 %â€¦â€¦â€¦..15 âˆ’ 55
Reverse T3, Serumâ€¦â€¦â€¦17.1 ng/dLâ€¦â€¦9.2 âˆ’ 24.1
Câˆ’Reactive Protein, HSâ€¦.0.10 mg/L
Ferritin, Serumâ€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦.290 ng/mLâ€¦â€¦30 âˆ’ 400

T3/Rev T3 ratioâ€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦..11.1â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦â€¦.>20

Â 

My question for the board is, how does one go about fixing this? I tried taking Armour for a few months this summer, just 1 grain, but I felt worse than before. My face also got puffy, especially under the eyes. I have read that one shouldn't take anything with T4 in it if they have a bad Free T3/Rev T3 ratio because it can make symptoms worse, which I have found to be true. Should I go on T3 only for a while and try and get rid of the Rev T3? I have read in many blogs from Dr. K that CT is the best way to get rid of RT3, but for me this hasn't worked that well. I did it from Feb-May and again the past 8 weeks and still feel brutal. I can't even make it through an entire day of work anymore. I need some advice from people who have battled or are battling this as well. Thank you so much!

### vkiernan — 2012-12-03T14:12:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66235>

ThereÂ is some good infoÂ on the thread All Things T3.Â  I had this problem and finally went on t3 only to clear it out.Â  It took me 2 months and 4 days.Â  This just happened 1.5 weeks ago and now I am experimenting on how much t3 to take.Â  Everything seems to change when you finally get rid of the rt3.Â Â You can find good info on STTM including some good info on the Paul Robinson method.Â  Some people haveÂ cleared it on his method using NDT too.

Â 

Do you know why you have rt3 in the first place?Â  You would also want to know why so you don't end up back there once you do clear it.Â  I wish you luck.Â

### bennyha — 2012-12-03T14:43:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66240>

> **vkiernan said**
> ThereÂ is some good infoÂ on the thread All Things T3.Â  I had this problem and finally went on t3 only to clear it out.Â  It took me 2 months and 4 days.Â  This just happened 1.5 weeks ago and now I am experimenting on how much t3 to take.Â  Everything seems to change when you finally get rid of the rt3.Â Â You can find good info on STTM including some good info on the Paul Robinson method.Â  Some people haveÂ cleared it on his method using NDT too.
>
> Â 
>
> Do you know why you have rt3 in the first place?Â  You would also want to know why so you don't end up back there once you do clear it.Â  I wish you luck.Â 

Hey vkiernan thank you for your reply. I read that post from Jack you quoted in the All Things T3 thread which I had read before and really confuses me. He seems to think taking T3 only with high RT3 will make things worse from what I gather? I people have differing views on how to treat RT3. My ratio is really out of whack so at this point I think it's the only thing I can try because the CT and diet and iodine alone haven't made me feel better

As far as the Paul Robinson method, did you use his Circadian Method to help your adrenals? The one where you start early in the morning? I have his book but have yet to read it fully.

I think I know how I got to my situation. I used to fast/intermittent fast on a daily basis a few years back. I also over-exercised and took 1000 mg of caffeine pills every day during my fast in order to get by. I did this for over a year straight. Also had a gambling addiction. All these things chronically and severely raised my cortisol. I ended up completely trashing my leptin signals and developed a horrible binge eating disorder which lasted for 2 years. I would eat 15,000 calories in one sitting. Then I would fast for 2 or 3 days to try and "undo" the damage I had done. Then I would repeat the cycle again. It was the worst 2 year period of my life, and I've been through some pretty tramatic things in my life. In the meantime, I'm sure I completely ruined my digestive system. All of my hormones are either really low or no longer working. Like I said, I can't even sweat anymore, which is the most basic function of the human body.

I've been battling this on my own the past 3 years. No medical insurance so the tests add up. I lost my job a year or so back due to my fatigue (worked in the fitness industry) and now I work minimum wage job. It's extremely hard coming up with the money to fix myself so I do everything I can to the best of my abilities without a doctor. I have saved up for a few doctors in the past year and didn't get anywhere with them. It's so disheartening to save up money and work extra jobs to feel it being wasted on 1 doctor visit where you feel more informed than they are. So I'm doing this myself. Was thinking of just getting some Cytomel and trying to do this myself if this indeed is the correct route for me to take based on my situation.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-03T22:08:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66273>

I think Jack's point is you really need to know why you have a rt3 issue in the first place and then fix it and everything will fall into place.Â  So you are thinking you have adrenal fatigue?Â  Did you ever have an ASI done to really see where things are?Â  Sounds like you really may have done some damage.Â  I did start the t3 only protocol for the first month from STTMÂ and then others out here told me how successful they were with the PR CT3 method which is supposed toÂ help the adrenals and so after the first month I switched.Â  The book really is wishy washy but it does have a lot of good info in it.Â  Actually, the 2nd timeÂ I went thru it I got a lot more out of it.Â 

What are you currently doing to help yourself?Â  Are you doing the LRx?Â  Have you read Jack's blog on adrenal fatigue and the latest which talks about the PVN being the issue instead of the actual adrenals themselves or at least in most cases?Â

### Owl — 2012-12-03T23:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66278>

Your cortisol is not too bad according to your July test.Â  The AM could be a little higher, optimal is at the top of the range.Â  But overall, you've done a good job of getting your cortisol down, if it was high before as you said.Â  I would not say you have adrenal fatigue.Â 

Â 

I'm hypothyroid and had high RT3.Â  If I remember right, my ratio was 7.Â  My doc put me on NatureThroid, which made the RT3 go up even more.Â  Then I dropped way down on that, and added T3 to it.Â  I also started taking at least 200mg of milk thistle, twice a day, which helps with RT3.Â  I also took liposomal glutithione, but you could take NAC, the precursor to glutithione, which helps with inflammation.Â  After a few months, my RT3 went way down.Â  It is now below the range, and my ratio is 30.Â  Even after adding some NatureThroid back in, my RT3 has stayed low, below the range.Â  I still take milk thistle, but have stopped the glutithione.Â

### bennyha — 2012-12-04T10:37:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66290>

> **vkiernan said**
> I think Jack's point is you really need to know why you have a rt3 issue in the first place and then fix it and everything will fall into place.Â  So you are thinking you have adrenal fatigue?Â  Did you ever have an ASI done to really see where things are?Â  Sounds like you really may have done some damage.Â  I did start the t3 only protocol for the first month from STTMÂ and then others out here told me how successful they were with the PR CT3 method which is supposed toÂ help the adrenals and so after the first month I switched.Â  The book really is wishy washy but it does have a lot of good info in it.Â  Actually, the 2nd timeÂ I went thru it I got a lot more out of it.Â 
>
> What are you currently doing to help yourself?Â  Are you doing the LRx?Â  Have you read Jack's blog on adrenal fatigue and the latest which talks about the PVN being the issue instead of the actual adrenals themselves or at least in most cases?Â 

Hey vkiernan. I have been following Jack's blog since last February and have been doing all that he and this forum suggests in some form or another since. I have done CT last winter/spring and started again about 6 weeks ago. I eat epi-paleo/EPCOT. I eat fish at least twice a day and most days 3x a day. I did a cortisol test last summer and my cortisol was mid-range in morning, high at noon, and high but in range at night. I have almost every single symptom of low cortisol, so this is confusing. Around 2:00 each afternoon I completely crash and I feel so awful and tired and fatigued until about 5:00, where I wake back up. Only time I don't have brain fog is at night. I also tested testosterone which was low, and DHEA was normal. I am trying to come up with up with the money to get a better hormone panel done. The fact I can no longer sweat tells me that I have really messed up my hormones.

I plan on fixing the hormones further down the chain-progesterone, pregnenolone, and dhea, to fix the thyroid. But in the past 6 months making it through each day is becoming increasingly harder. I was looking to just use T3 as a boost to get the ball rolling and get ride of my RT3. I am always cold, constipated, can't sweat, have severe brain fog, etc etc. I have tried everything in regards to diet and light cycles and CT for the past 9 months and my labs have gotten worse. My free T3 is very low and my TSH is too high. My RT3/Free T3 ratio is way out of whack as well. So I am trying to get to the bottom of why I'm feeling so crappy, but am looking for some kind of boost in the meantime. All so confusing!!!

### bennyha — 2012-12-04T10:41:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66291>

> **Owl said**
> Your cortisol is not too bad according to your July test.Â  The AM could be a little higher, optimal is at the top of the range.Â  But overall, you've done a good job of getting your cortisol down, if it was high before as you said.Â  I would not say you have adrenal fatigue.Â

> I'm hypothyroid and had high RT3.Â  If I remember right, my ratio was 7.Â  My doc put me on NatureThroid, which made the RT3 go up even more.Â  Then I dropped way down on that, and added T3 to it.Â  I also started taking at least 200mg of milk thistle, twice a day, which helps with RT3.Â  I also took liposomal glutithione, but you could take NAC, the precursor to glutithione, which helps with inflammation.Â  After a few months, my RT3 went way down.Â  It is now below the range, and my ratio is 30.Â  Even after adding some NatureThroid back in, my RT3 has stayed low, below the range.Â  I still take milk thistle, but have stopped the glutithione.Â 

Hey Owl thank you so much for taking a minute to weigh in. Can I ask how you were feeling before compared to how you feel now? Do you feel significantly better since getting your ratio above 20? I feel so awful both physically and mentally and am hoping this can be a springboard into helping me feel better. Also, I took Armour for a few months this summer and felt absolutely brutal. Way worse than before. I'm guessing this just raised the RT3 in me and made symptoms worse?

Also, could you tell me what brand of milk thistle you took and the dosage?

Finally, my HS-CRP was 0.15. I was expecting this to be sky high based on how I feel and the things I've read. Can you or anyone else tell me what this number represents? Does it mean I don't have as much inflammation in the body as I feared? Or is it simply a number?

### Owl — 2012-12-04T11:39:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-high-reverse-t3-and-how-to-get-rid-of-it.5033/post-66294>

I do feel a lot better since my RT3 dropped.Â  I was really dragging before. I had no energy, didn't want to do anything active.Â  Yawning all day, brain fog too.Â 

If you're eating very low carb, that may be making your T3 level drop even more, making you feel worse.Â Â  I don't know that your afternoon crashes are cortisol, unless it has changed from July.Â  I guess it's possible that you have a dip between noon and 5.Â  But I suspect that if you get your thyroid levels up, that will go away.Â  You also need iron to make thyroid hormones, so test for that too.

I also started on all other hormones at the same time as the thyroid meds - progesterone, estrogen, testosterone, DHEA, pregnenolone.Â  So I needed everything like you do.Â 

I am also doing heavy metal chelation through my doc, because I still think my energy could be better.Â  I've got extremely high lead and other metals.Â 

Â 

I have pretty low HS-CRP too - .37, so I think my inflammation causing the high RT3 was localized, in my liver.Â  That's where milk thistle really helps.Â  I used the Jarrow brand.Â  Each capsule is 150mg, so I took 2 in the morning, and 2 in the evening.Â  Stop the Thyroid Madness recommends at least 200mg for each dose.

I am now taking this one http://www.amazon.com/Milk-Thistle-250-120-Caps/dp/B00068S5XU/ref=sr\_1\_2?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1354634083&sr=1-2&keywords=milk+thistle+swansonÂ  which has 250mg, so I only need to take one capsule for each dose.Â 

I do think you're going to need T3 supplementation.Â  There's no need to suffer for so long.Â  You're right about the Armour making things worse, the T4 in it probably just went to more RT3.Â  If you want to find a doc, try looking at this site for top thyroid doctors by state: http://thyroid.about.com/cs/doctors/a/topdocs.htmÂ  That's how I found mine.Â

Owl, I have not felt good since my rt3 cleared.Â  Can't figure this out either.Â  I upped my t3 again no change.Â  I am getting this fluttery feeling between the chest area too.Â  This did not happen prior to the rt3 clear.Â  I also thought I was sick the next day or so.Â  I was feeling happier, etc. right before and now I am tired, grumpy and plain ole' don't feel well.Â  Today I decided to drop down the t3 dose and dose closer together and see what happens.Â  I hate feeling like this and my poor dh takes the brunt of it.Â  I have gotten all the foods out of my diet and I have dropped weight.Â  I expected to feel really good now and it just isn't happening.Â  I have been taking oral prometrium and wonder if that is making me tired.Â  I was waking up early and could never go back to sleep; now I wake up tired and hate to crawl out of bed.Â  Any thoughts are most appreciated.

.

New Documentary shows eatting Epi-Paleo along with emotional support and proper medical care can CURE diabetes in 30 days or less....

Â 

**Simply Raw: Reversing Diabetes in 30 Days** is an independent documentary film that chronicles six Americans with 'incurable' diabetes switching their diet and getting off insulin

The film follows each participant's remarkable journey and captures the medical, physical, and emotional transformations brought on by this diet and lifestyle change. We witness moments of struggle, support, and hope as what is revealed, with startling clarity, is that diet can reverse diabetes\* and change lives.

Additional wisdom is provided by Morgan Spurlock, Woody Harrelson, Anthony Robbins, Rev. Michael Beckwith, and Doctors Fred Bisci, Joel Furman, and Gabriel Cousens.

Â

### shilohman — 2012-12-04T21:29:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cure-diabetes-in-30-days-documentary-film-epi-paleo-win.5044/post-66381>

> **Thor said
> .
>
> New Documentary shows eatting Epi-Paleo along with emotional support and proper medical care can CURE diabetes in 30 days or less....
>
> Â 
>
> Simply Raw: Reversing Diabetes in 30 Days** is an independent documentary film that chronicles six Americans with 'incurable' diabetes switching their diet and getting off insulin
>
> The film follows each participant's remarkable journey and captures the medical, physical, and emotional transformations brought on by this diet and lifestyle change. We witness moments of struggle, support, and hope as what is revealed, with startling clarity, is that diet can reverse diabetes\* and change lives.
>
> Additional wisdom is provided by Morgan Spurlock, Woody Harrelson, Anthony Robbins, Rev. Michael Beckwith, and Doctors Fred Bisci, Joel Furman, and Gabriel Cousens.
>
> Â 
>
> [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WhwwKl6t6Cw\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;WhwwKl6t6Cw\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WhwwKl6t6Cw\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;WhwwKl6t6Cw)

Lets check some facts here. 1) type 2 diabetics normally do not require insulin, they usually control their diabetics via diet and drugs (metformin, etc). Â Those that are unable to control their diabetes via diet or drugs have to restort to insulin injections. Â So, to do this documentary they had to search for six T2D who injected insulin, which is the most severe type of T2D. Â 2)Through a controlled diet for 30 days they were able to lower their blood glucose levels to where they were able to lower their injectable insulin levels by a third. Â 3) We know that at least one person lost weight during the 30 days. 4) We know that at least two people lowered their blood glucose levels. Â 

So from a test example of six individuals with T2D who inject insulin the doctor declared that because two of the people had lowered blood glucose levels, they all were cured of diabetes. Â SORRY, but there was no reversal of diabetes. Â At the end of the study all six people still had T2D. Â Don't know when this study was done, but it sure would interesting to followup with our six T2D after six months and 12 months to see if they are still having to inject insulin or not. Â The problem with this documentary is there is no science to it, its just nothing but 'raw' propaganda. Â  Like Dr. Kruse says, 'question everything'.

# Thread: Found a source for Grass-Fed Beef, Bison,  Lamb in Vancouver, B.C. area.

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### furface — 2012-12-04T22:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-source-for-grass-fed-beef-bison-lamb-in-vancouver-b-c-area.5053/post-66393>

Just to let anyone in the Vancouver, B.C./Lower Mainland area now, that there is a good source for Grass-Fed & Finished Beef, Bison, Lamb, as well as Wild Duck and other "Wild" game in Coquitlam. It's Hills Foods on Glacier St. They also have a website. http://www.hillsfoods.com/

Â 

They even have 3 different PrePackaged "Paleo" diet packs, which feature Grass-Fed Bison.

Â 

Just thought I'd pass it along in case there are others in the Lower Mainland trying to find Grass-Fed meats.

### endless — 2012-12-08T16:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-source-for-grass-fed-beef-bison-lamb-in-vancouver-b-c-area.5053/post-66837>

Thanks for the link! I'm in the Kootenays so hopefully they ship. I can at least stock up when I get down there for visits.

### furface — 2012-12-08T17:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-a-source-for-grass-fed-beef-bison-lamb-in-vancouver-b-c-area.5053/post-66848>

> **endless said**
> Thanks for the link! I'm in the Kootenays so hopefully they ship. I can at least stock up when I get down there for visits.

No prob. I've tried quite a few things from them...grass-fed bison steaks, lamb, bison, elk burgers, plus a few other things that I can't remember off hand lol. Just a note, everything they sell is frozen.

Â 

Also, if you're ever in Vancouver, Market Meats on West 4th Avenue, does sell fresh Grass-Fed/Finished Beef, lamb, bison, etc. Can't wait to go shopping there in a week and a half!

# Thread: Which cuts of Grass-Fed steak do you eat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-04T23:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/post-66397>

Just curious as to which cuts of Grass-Fed Beef/Bison do you eat?

Â 

I myself love Ribeye steak, even though it's higher in fat, but isn't beef fat supposed to be good anyway? (Grass fed that is)

Also found out that "regular" Grass-Fed ground beef is higher in Omega 3, and CLA than Lean, or Extra-Lean.

### diane — 2012-12-04T23:22:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/post-66404>

I get a side of beef, so all kinds of cuts. Sirloin is one of my favourites, and have found that the blade roast is amazing in the slow cooker. Plus I get bones and make bone broth. Oh, so many possibilities!Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### furface — 2012-12-04T23:25:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/post-66405>

Ooooh the blade roast in the slow cooker sounds delicious! As does the broth. Yeah a side, or even a hind/quarter sounds like the best idea. Hopefully one day I'll be able to afford one. (and get a bigger freezer LOL)

### diane — 2012-12-04T23:51:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/post-66406>

I got my freezer on kijiji for $50. One of the best buys I've made!

Â 

Overall the 1/4 beef saves me money - between what we eat, and my dogs eat raw, so overall it saves me money. Plus so much better quality!

### furface — 2012-12-05T00:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/post-66413>

That is a great deal on the freezer!Â  Too bad I don't have the room for one.

Â

### Rachelle — 2012-12-05T02:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-cuts-of-grass-fed-steak-do-you-eat.5054/post-66419>

As poor as I am, I buy, mostly, tenderloin from Wellness. I love it seared off or tartare. My only other choice is the ground beef for stews and such. Â Thank goodness I only eat beef twice a month, the rest of the month is seafood.

Hey ladies,

I have a personal blog and I've been recruiting friends and other bloggers I know who are breastfeeding advocates to collaborate with me to write a series on breastfeeding on my blog. Anyhow, I really want a post or two on great nutrition to support breastfeeding...but the problem is so many people are all about sticking to a low fat food pyramid type diet. I didn't know if any of you were bloggers or good writers and might want to consider contributing an article? I've never put together a big series like this with lots of authors, but I think it's gong to be fun.

# Thread: Can you fry oysters without breading them?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-fry-oysters-without-breading-them.5066/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-05T18:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-fry-oysters-without-breading-them.5066/post-66498>

Just curious as to whether or not you could fry oysters...either raw ones, or even smoked oysters from a can?

Â 

Figured it might be interesting to fry some oysters in a pan along with some coconut oil, a few strips of bacon, and some chopped up baby bok choy.Â  But I don't want it to turn out tasting so awful that I'll throw it out, and have wasted the money.

Â 

I tried searching on youtube for "fried oysters without breading" but had no luck.

### furface — 2012-12-05T19:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-fry-oysters-without-breading-them.5066/post-66502>

Don't know about frying the oysters without breading them, but eating smoked oysters along with bacon, coconut oil, and baby bok choy...not a good idea. LOL! They're tasty on their own, but IMHO, not in this particular combination. Live and learn. haha.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-05T20:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-fry-oysters-without-breading-them.5066/post-66512>

um....why would you want to cook them... they're 1000% better raw w/a little lemon juice and some horseradish. Â ohhh the taste of the ocean on your tongue...Â 

Â 

damn...now I want oysters....Â

### Thor — 2012-12-05T21:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-fry-oysters-without-breading-them.5066/post-66520>

.

Are you asking about **Rocky Mountain Oysters**? { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â  LoLÂ Â Â 

Â 

I"m blessed to live in the Pacific Northwest - home to some of the best seafood.Â  As an Executive Chef I'm a kid in a candy store....

Â 

You could fry the oysters with a non-bread safe paleo coating...Â Â  use raw organic almonds & place them in a food processor - try to get the nuts to almost flour like - still a bit crunchy.Â  Don't over do it.Â Â  Use the almond pieces like you would Panko and egg wash.Â  Extra yummy.Â Â 

Â 

The best way aside from raw is to steam the oysters.

Â 

Use a steamer kettle/pot

-

    1 Add about 2 cups of water or a wee bit wine, or enough to be 3/4 inch deep in theÂ  steamer. Boil on high heat for about 5 minutes or until the steam rolls out whenÂ  the lid is opened slightly.

-   2Â  Â AddÂ oystersÂ  and an additional cup of water and cover on medium heat.Â 
    3Â  Steam oysters about 5 to 10 minutes, or until the majority of the oystersÂ have opened. Do not overcook.
    4Â Â  Serve hot, with melted butter

### furface — 2012-12-05T22:12:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-fry-oysters-without-breading-them.5066/post-66523>

Oh, I like them raw as well...I live in Vancouver, B.C. so very familiar with all of the wonderful seafood we have here.

Â 

A spritz of lemon sounds yummy...but no to the horseradish sauce for me. LOL

Â 

Great idea Thor about the raw organic almonds. I'll have to try that. I might even try ground up macadamia nuts.

Â 

Thanks for the responses.

# Thread: Question about elevated SHBG and EOS even after Leptin Reset and Paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-elevated-shbg-and-eos-even-after-leptin-reset-and-paleo.5067/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-06T00:23:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-elevated-shbg-and-eos-even-after-leptin-reset-and-paleo.5067/post-66532>

Hi everyone,

> I have been lurking here for a while, but I am in need of some feedback following my recent lab results. I did a leptin reset a few months ago and have been paleo since and was hoping for some good news when I got my labs done recently-I did not. Â My doctor is stumped and I am concerned about our course of treatment. After my lab results showed an elevated SHBG (and consequently low free testosterone) I have started taking danazol 100mg x 2 daily. Today is day 2. Seems reasonable enough I suppose, but I think we need to find what is causing the elevation. The symptoms I am having are all the typical low-T symptoms. My numbers are listed below:
>
> Testosterone 496 Range 292-867
> SHBG 87 Range 16-94
> Calc Free Test 5.1 Range 4.8-25.0
> Estradiol 27 Range <=63
>
> Below are the elevated lab values
> Albumin 5.1 Range 2.9-5.0
> Calculated Globulin 2.0 Range 2.0-3.8
> Calculated A/G ratio 2.6 Range 0.9-2.5
> Eosinophils 9 Range 0-7
>
> Noteworthy is that my Eosinophils seem to always be elevated-as long as I have been having labs done. Sometimes my liver enzymes are also high. I have a history of heavy alcohol abuse for 20 years, but have not consumed alcohol for 4 years. I did have a fatty liver disease diagnosis during my drinking days. I am 6'3" and 200 lbs and approximately 16% BF. My body fat wont seem to budge. My diet is very clean, low carb, and mostly organic. I avoid wheat completely and avoid most grains 90% of the time. Diet has been like this for months. I was 30 lbs heavier in the past and had similar labs.
>
> I am looking for some feedback as to what might be the root cause of my elevated SHBG and other abnormal labs. When these labs were done last time my Test level was higher (732) and my SHBG was 62.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-06T01:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-elevated-shbg-and-eos-even-after-leptin-reset-and-paleo.5067/post-66533>

From Dr Kruse in Ask Jack back in September:

"SHBG tells us about Hypo or hyperthyroidism, estrogen collection., inflammation, osteopenia, or porosis, and LR. IT has to be placed in context but it gives us other things to look at that maybe going south."

### NittDan — 2012-12-06T07:36:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-elevated-shbg-and-eos-even-after-leptin-reset-and-paleo.5067/post-66539>

How are your lipids TRENDING?

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-06T22:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-elevated-shbg-and-eos-even-after-leptin-reset-and-paleo.5067/post-66631>

> **NittDan said**
> How are your lipids TRENDING?

The following are my flagged labs and the lipid section:

ALT (SGPT): 53 HIGH (0-44 Normal range)

Iron, Serum: 168 HIGH (40-155 Normal range) \*I love grass fed beef\*

Total Cholesterol: 250 HIGH (100-199 Normal range)
Triglycerides: 68 (0-149 Normal range)
HDL: 61 (>39Normal range)
VLDL Cholesterol Calc: 14 (5-40 Normal Range)
LDL Cholesterol Calc: 175 High (0-99 Normal Range)
T. Chol/HDL Ratio: 4.1 (0.0-5.0 Normal Range)
Estimated CHD Risk: 0.7 (0.0-1.0 Normal Range)

EOS: 8 HIGH (0-7 Normal Range)

# Thread: Question about how to consume the extra fat.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-06T14:45:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66569>

Just wondering how everyone consumes all of the extra fat that is in/on the Leptin RX, or Epi-Paleo eating plan?

Â 

What I mean is if you add a tablespoon or more of coconut oil to the pan while cooking whatever meal you are having, and if part of the meal already includes a few strips of bacon etc, or some beef tallow, etc. Even if you have some veggies with the meal, and they absorb some of the fat/grease that's in the pan, there's usually quite a bit left on the plate after you finish the meal.

Â 

So do most/all of you just "drink" the remaining fat/grease/"juice" that's left on the plate?

Â 

I know it seems so tempting to grab a nice, soft, warm, bun, and sop up all of that tasty liquid, but I know that's a big NO NO!!

Â 

Just curious.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-06T15:35:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66572>

> Just wondering how everyone consumes all of the extra fat that is in/on the Leptin RX, or Epi-Paleo eating plan?
>
> Â 
>
> So do most/all of you just "drink" the remaining fat/grease/"juice" that's left on the plate?
>
> Â 

Um. yeah. Â Lunch today was shrimp seared in bacon fat, garlic and salt. Â I ate all the shirmp and then drank the left over fat in the bowl ~ 1/4c. oh so yummie.

I have been known to spoon out ghee, coconut oil, bacon fat, and duck fat and just eat plain while Â making a meal. Â my kids eat copious amounts of ghee straight off the spoon...Â

>
> I have been known to spoon out ghee, coconut oil, bacon fat, and duck fat and just eat plain while Â making a meal. Â my kids eat copious amounts of ghee straight off the spoon...Â 

LOL what a coincidence...I'm just about to have shrimp, and bacon, along with some baby bok choy, and an extra tablespoon of Nutiva coconut oil, seasoned with onion powder, and some cajun spice.

Â 

I don't mind having a spoon of plain coconut oil when it's in the "solid" form, but not too find yet of it when it's liquified in the jar.

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-06T16:51:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66578>

Morning:Â  1-2 tbsp. of coconut oil in coffee.Â  I also add coconut milk to my coffee because I like the creaminess, plus it adds more coconut fat.Â  Make sure you buy the regular version, not the lowfat kind.Â  Also, 2 eggs with bacon, all cooked in butter.Â 

Â 

Lunchtime:Â  a couple of spoonfuls of coconut manna and a square of dark chocolate.

Â 

Dinner:Â  Shrimp salad with olive oil and avocado and bacon.Â 

Â 

Also, learn to eat your bacon not so crispy.Â  Cook it a little less, so there's more fat on it.Â

### furface — 2012-12-06T16:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66579>

Thanks, PrimalPam.

Â 

The coconut oil in coffee sounds like a great idea, and I'd try that if coffee didn't literally make me sick. I LOVE the aroma of it, but my stomach just can't handle it's consumption.

Â 

Lunch sounds very tasty...I've recently bought some 99% Lindt dark chocolate.

Â 

Dinner sounds very appetizing as well.

Â 

I actually prefer my bacon cooked not so crisp...takes too long to crispen it up, and I usually can't wait that long. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-06T17:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66580>

If you don't like coffee, then just take a spoonful of coconut oil by itself.Â  It's ok if you don't love the taste or texture; think of it as medicine.Â  Hey, it tastes better than cough syrup.Â

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-06T17:05:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66581>

You can also put the CO or the ghee in green tea and blend that up.Â  I personally eat a lot of bone broth this time of year.Â  Usually a cup w/breakfast that has a Tblsp each of CO and ghee.Â  Then that same concoction is often my lunch...keeps me full and is satisfying my fat requirement!

### furface — 2012-12-06T17:36:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66588>

> **PrimalPam said**
> If you don't like coffee, then just take a spoonful of coconut oil by itself.Â  It's ok if you don't love the taste or texture; think of it as medicine.Â  Hey, it tastes better than cough syrup.Â  /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_smile.gif

I actually don't mind consuming coconut oil by itself when it's in the solid form, but "drinking" it "neat" sort of upsets my stomach. I wish the coconut oil wouldn't keep "melting" in my cupboard.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-06T17:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66591>

figuring out how to get enough fat can be a challenge. I basically make everything with fat, reheat things with more fat and por fat over everything. CO in coffe and bone broth as mentioned above. Cook bacon in oven @ 260 deg or so use bacon fat to cook eggs and mushrooms eat bacon put away excess bacon fat to cook eggs in tomorow. I just made venison liverwurst from deer liver 50%and pork fat 50%. Mushroom will absorb lots of fat when sauteed. Butter in coffee. Real whipped cream you can eat tons if not AI. Sour cream. Drink the juice lick the plate. Fat party.Â  I eat like this everyday and my weight has been a solid 165 for many months on my 5' 9" frame. Body fat % somewhere around 10. This time of year I eat the highest fat.

### furface — 2012-12-06T18:01:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66592>

> Body fat % somewhere around 10

Â 

OMG that is fantastic!! I'd love to have a BF % that low one day. (My egotistical dream is to one day see my abs.)

Â 

Those all sound like delicious ideas/recipes. I'll have to try some.

Â 

Another question...is Type 2 Diabetes an AI? I tried searching on google, but they never EVER answer your direct question. All the results that they did give were basically a mixed message. (Why can't they ever just answer your direct question? LOL)

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-06T19:35:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66599>

I don't think T2D is definitely AI, but a lot of T2D's are AI.Â  You'd have to get tested for antibodies to see.Â  If you are hypothyroid, chances are upwards of 80% that you are AI, and since many T2D's are hypothyroid, it's a good chance.

Â 

Re:Â  Eating coconut oil in solid form.Â  Just refrigerate it, and then you can scoop it out.Â  I love watching the video Dr. K. made, walking through Whole Foods, where he shows a (16 oz?) jar of Coconut Oil, and says, "This is a weekend pack at my house".Â  I go through about 1/2 gallon a month by myself.

Â 

Another favorite dinner of mine (which I purchased today for dinner, thanks to your bringing up this issue, it reminded meÂ of it!Â  thanks!) is what I call my Fat Salad.Â  A huge bowl (I use a serving bowl!) of kale, spinach, arugula, etc.Â with mushrooms, cucumbers, onions, etc.Â  I add 1 whole avocado cut up, some shrimp or salmon, bacon, and drizzle olive oil all over it.Â  Tons of fat; I have no idea how much, but so yummy.

### furface — 2012-12-06T20:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66605>

Glad to help with your dinner.

Â 

Thanks for the info on the A.I. I'll try and find out about my situation.

Â 

Good suggestion about keeping the CO in the fridge.

Â 

Yeah, I love that whole video. I think we actually have a Whole Foods here in Vancouver.

### Chrysanthemama — 2012-12-06T22:07:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66624>

> **furface said**
>
> I actually prefer my bacon cooked not so crisp...takes too long to crispen it up, and I usually can't wait that long.

I have become quite adept at baking bacon in a large casserole dish in the oven.Â  Depending on how much you're cooking, and how closely packed in the dish it is, it might take 30 to 40 minutes, and you can control the crispiness of it pretty well.Â  I long ago tired of frying up bacon in pan.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-06T22:16:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-how-to-consume-the-extra-fat.5069/post-66628>
