# Thread: Does anyone make sauces out of heavy/whipping cream?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-06T17:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66589>

Just curious as to whether or not anyone makes sauces out of heavy/whipping cream, and then add some spices, to pour on top of fish, or veggies, or meat, just as another way to consume fat? Or just for a change of pace.

Â 

If so, do you use anything to thicken it up?

Â 

Perhaps even some sort of creamy clam, shrimp, or lobster chowder...without the spuds of course. Or maybe with a few portions of yams, or sweet potatoes.

Â 

Or would this all be taboo?

### Rachelle — 2012-12-06T19:41:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66601>

I try to stay away from cream because I am not all together sure my immune system is not compromised. My ANA tests say negative, but I still am not sure.

Â 

I make sauces out of coconut milk. Here is a really good fish recipe for Brazilian fish stew.Â http://www.simplyrecipes.com/recipes/moqueca\_-\_brazilian\_fish\_stew/

Â 

It is very yummy. You can make it with shrimp or anything fish really. You can add or subtract anything you like to this. I use nothing to thicken this up.

Â 

Bacon wrapped sweet potato bites are really wonderful too.

### furface — 2012-12-06T19:55:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66603>

Thanks, Rachelle...that all sounds so yummers!!

### kathylu — 2012-12-06T22:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66625>

If you can tolerate dairy, a really easy way to make a sauce is to pour heavy whipping cream into a saucepan and let it reduce by 50-70% over medium heat. Add spices, herbs, wine, etc and away you go! It thickens on its own, no need to add a thickener.

### furface — 2012-12-06T23:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66634>

> **kathylu said**
> If you can tolerate dairy, a really easy way to make a sauce is to pour heavy whipping cream into a saucepan and let it reduce by 50-70% over medium heat. Add spices, herbs, wine, etc and away you go! It thickens on its own, no need to add a thickener.

Thanks Kathy.Â  I'll try that.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-07T14:52:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66709>

I use Mascarpone to make sauces and desserts. Mascarpone is the thickest Italian cream you can get and is not liquid anymore it is so thick. Italians make Tiramisu with it. I just add it to meat, fish or anything else I can think of. Very delicious as a dessert is melting dark chocolate (90 - 99 %) in a pan over boiling water, add some almond milk and stir in the Mascarpone. Just add vanilla or any other spice you like and/or Stevia. Put it in the fridge and you have the best mousse au chocolat. Sometime I put in raspberries or blueberries, or I mix Mascarpone with strawberries to make a strawberry cream.

### furface — 2012-12-07T14:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66710>

> **[email redacted] said**
> I use Mascarpone to make sauces and desserts. Mascarpone is the thickest Italian cream you can get and is not liquid anymore it is so thick. Italians make Tiramisu with it. I just add it to meat, fish or anything else I can think of. Very delicious as a dessert is melting dark chocolate (90 - 99 %) in a pan over boiling water, add some almond milk and stir in the Mascarpone. Just add vanilla or any other spice you like and/or Stevia. Put it in the fridge and you have the best mousse au chocolat. Sometime I put in raspberries or blueberries, or I mix Mascarpone with strawberries to make a strawberry cream.

OMG that all sounds so delicious!!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-08T04:00:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66787>

yes, it is definitely delicious, and you know what is the best part? In all my life, I have never eaten so well and so good as now and a lot of it is as in my childhood, except for the carbs, but instead of gaining weight I am loosing weight. I am totally satisfied and I have no cravings what so ever for carb-foods.

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-08T10:52:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66802>

Oh my....mascarpone....that is such a great idea.Â  Never thought of it.Â  Now only to find it...

### Charlotte — 2012-12-08T14:33:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-make-sauces-out-of-heavy-whipping-cream.5070/post-66822>

Here, you can find it in any supermarket. I am sure in the US also. Or ask in an Italian restaurant where they make Tiramisu. It makes every sauce creamy and makes all kinds of delicious desserts, and it is great for baking.

Hi ~ This is my 2nd day...Was a non-meat eater before but was heavy into good fats and vegis, some carbs. Started yesterday with fish..Today BAB with bacon and eggs....salad for lunch...My head is pounding...pounding, pounding...Did not stop coffee. Past med history AF. Â  Help...am I doing it too fast? Maybe some neurons are connecting..

Could just be a coincidence...

### nonchalant — 2012-12-06T20:28:52+0200

Hi LavaLocks, welcome!Â  Seafood for your first meal?Â  Great choice!

No idea about the headache, sorry.Â 

I don't get them much anymore, but I've found that my headaches were caused by a tension in my neck that could be relieved by "lifting" my head a bit using my hands.Â  I didn't feel like I had done anything useful, but I would notice relief in about 10 minutes.Â  It didn't work on every headache, just most of them.Â  I'd use bromelain for the others, it's good for inflammation.Â 

Did you eat grains much before this?Â  I can't think of what else might cause them, since you are still drinking coffee.

# Thread: Nutrition resources for high schoolers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-06T22:03:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-66623>

I teach a high school Biology class for home schooled students. We only meet once a week for an hour for class (they have an hour with another teacher for lab), but we use moodle during the week to communicate and I give them assignments etc this way. Anyhow, weâ€™ve gotten to the â€œNutrition and Digestive Systemâ€ chapter. EEK! Itâ€™s making me crazy. Itâ€™s based on the food pyramid and extols the virtues of canola oil, corn oil, and a grain based diet. Because I have such a limited time, I really wonâ€™t be able to dispel all of this nonsense. Last week (while talking about the nervous system) we already got into a conversation about some of this as I was talking about how essential good fats were for proper nervous system functioning â€“ some of the girls were freaking out about the â€œsaturated fatsâ€ I was talking about. I was also explaining about how sugar can cause problems with the nervous system and one very athletic male student said heâ€™s prediabetic and has a very fast metabolism so has to eat sugar and carbs because of his metabolism. I tried to explain that he could avoid the prediabetes progressing to type2 diabetes with dietary changes. Anyhow, all of this to say I want these kids to learn more about nutrition than what I can pack into next weeks lecture, most of which needs to cover the digestive system.

Do you know of any resources, articles or online movies/clips, on good nutrition that would be appropriate for a high schooler? I'd like to have some resources to put up on moodle for them to be looking at over the next several weeks while we are on Christmas break. Any ideas?

### kathylu — 2012-12-06T22:14:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-66627>

I'd start with www.westonaprice.org. Lots of great articles about nutrition there. I don't really agree with their stance on grains (ok, if prepared in a "traditional" manner), but it's good place to start directing people gently away from the SAD. They are right on the money about a lot of topics.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-06T22:43:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-66632>

Fat head the movie. And the perfect health diet movie.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-07T10:14:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-66671>

Fat head is fun, I could see high schoolers enjoying it. Is the other movie, "Fresh"? I haven't seen that one, wish there was easier access to it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-14T13:41:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-67507>

Any other great articles that anyone could recommend for high school level? Blog posts?Â

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-14T16:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-67533>

Why not have them read a blog by Mark Sission and some other paleo bloggers, as well as JAck's Epi-Paleo Rx. Â And have them discuss the topics, then the next week have them do one on SAD, and the following week one on the Meditrarian diet. Â then the final week give them discussion topics Â in which they have to defend a particular way of eating.

HAve them watch Terri Whals TedX talk too!Â 

Have them answer - the following questions: Â What nutrients does the brain need most to run optimally? Â Which way of eating provides the most of the nutrient?Â 

What foods did humans evolve eating? Why are these foods critical to humans today?Â 

Â 

Ask them critical questions - that get them thinking, that make them what to learn more. Â  take this opportunity to build the foundation... ask them to question the status quo of the USDA and the food pyramid. Â Ask them to research who funds the research USDA does to come up w/the food pyramid/plate? Â Have them look into genetically modified foods - and defend why they're ok to eat, or not ok to eat. Â 

Â 

shine the flashlight... and let them discover their own ability to read, comprehend, and determine whats total BS vs truth.Â

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-16T12:26:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nutrition-resources-for-high-schoolers.5074/post-67635>

Thanks for the ideas, I had some similar ideas, but was looking for specific resources. I hadn't thought of Terri Whals TedX talk, but that would be a great one. I've already uploaded it and a few more blog posts/videos I've found onto moodle! I also gave them the opportunity to earn extra credit by writing a paper after watching Fat Head and checking out the other resources.Â 

Â 

It's fun to be able to expose the kids to some new (for them) ideas. I'll be interested to hear what they think after Christmas break!

Â 

Thanks again for your suggestions { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: Eczema and leaky gut
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eczema-and-leaky-gut.5080/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-07T06:48:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eczema-and-leaky-gut.5080/post-66650>

I have had eczema since I was 5 (now 42) and it has changed over the years. It currently manifests itself as extremely dry and flaky skin especially on the face (beard line is worse again). Itchiness is also associated with the dryness and for the better part of this year, my face has been red and blotchy. This varies on torso, legs and arms as well.

Had some bloods done in May and October and discussed with a naturopath this week. Highlights are:

May 2012:

ThyroglobulinÂ Â Â Â  9 IU/mLÂ Â  (range <4)

ImmunoglobulinÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  H 2019 kIU/LÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  (This was 5587 in Jul 2011)

Oct 2012:

hsCRPÂ Â Â  Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0.4 mg/L

HbA1c FractionÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  5.3%Â  (in SI units: 35 mmol/mol)

HomocysteineÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  10.0 umol/LÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â  (naturopath wants this down to 6-8 â€“ I donâ€™t really understand what this is yetâ€¦.)

25-hydroxy Vitamin D3Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  80 nmol/LÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  (naturopath wants this up in 100-120)

I have also done a leaky gut test this month (6 hour urine collection after taking Lactulose and Mannitol). Lactulose recovery was high at 0.46% (range is 0 to 0.3%) suggestive of GI mucosal hyperpermeability.

After discussions with naturopath, it would appear I have adrenal fatigue as well (no testing â€“ just from questionnaires).

I have been epi-paleo for about 3 weeks now.

Now to my questions:

When my October bloods were taken, I was on 50mg prednisone daily â€“ will this affect my hsCRP reading and should I get it retested once I am fully weaned off prednisone (about another week or so)?

My naturopath says it can take 2 years to heal a leaky gut. If I follow the leaky gut prescription in your blogs would this be correct? Is there any way I can speed up the healing eg cold thermogenesis? Will CT reduce my inflammation? Should I be following the GAPS diet you reference in your leaky gut blogs?

My naturopath wants me to do things similar to the leaky gut prescription, then do a detox for parasites, funghi etc followed by a liver cleanse. Does this make sense if I am following the leaky gut prescription and epi-paleo?

Should I get tested for my adrenals? What should I do to improve them?

### Destiny — 2012-12-07T08:01:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eczema-and-leaky-gut.5080/post-66652>

Neil,

To your question about a leaky gut. My husband had Crohn's most of his life. He has been eating right an doing CT twice a day since Feb 2012.

He is symptoms free. His labs are still wonky but he feels great.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-10T06:06:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eczema-and-leaky-gut.5080/post-66993>

Â

Neil

Was losing more weight. After CT, he lost his seasonal allergies and stopped using Claritin-D and Afrin. He was on those over 15 years.Â  Presently, he is trying to add more seafood. Today, he is 181 lbs 6'2", no Crohn's or allergy symptoms.
>
> Destiny, that is fantastic.Â  Kudos to your dh.

Â 

Thanks, V { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. # Thread: Fire breathing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2012-12-07T09:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66668>

I have been doing fire breathing while fully immersed in <40 deg . This is rapid deep belly breathing through the mouth. You have to try this . At colder temps when i breathe like this it fires up all my skin nerves with each breath. The sensation is amazing. It helps pass the time too.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-07T10:09:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66670>

do you have a good description of how to do this.... I'm interested.....Â

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-07T10:33:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66674>

this is a technique we use in Ashtanga yoga usually at the end of class in a lifted seated pose. Breath from the low abdomen rapidly and forcefully in and out like a steam train through the mouth. I do sets of like 100, Do as many sets as you like. I don't know what's going on with the sensation and it only happens in deep cold, but it's like with each breath my entire epidermal nerve web fires up. Way cool. I am fully immersed to my lips I am blowing out right across the water which is fun .Â  I am outdoors in a pond. I face the sun so there is great visual.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-07T10:56:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66676>

> **Dali Dula said**
> I don't know what's going on with the sensation and it only happens in deep cold, but it's like with each breath my entire epidermal nerve web fires up.Â  I am fully immersed to my lips I am blowing out right across the water which is fun .Â  I am outdoors in a pond. I face the sun so there is great visual.

Â 

Wow, Jack will give you an A+ for all that!Â  Sounds like you are doing it right!

Interesting effect.Â  Guess it isn't the vagus nerve.Â  I'd like to try it, but I haven't gone deep cold yet.

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-07T11:38:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66683>

That sounds really intense and wonderful!

The way I was instructed to do "breath of fire" was to only think about blowing breath out...you naturally take the in- breath...having the breath start from the bottom of your belly engages your core muscles rather than shallow upper chest breathing. I'm going to try this too. I haven't been able to get through the ice in our pond for the last week, but I'm going to try for my bath and shower CT.

### endless — 2012-12-07T23:16:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66775>

It's important to do it properly....the out breath is forceful and accompanied by a tightening of the diaphragm and core muscles. The in breath is kind of passive. It might be helpful to look up examples on YouTube.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-08T10:44:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66800>

> **endless said**
> It's important to do it properly....the out breath is forceful and accompanied by a tightening of the diaphragm and core muscles. The in breath is kind of passive. It might be helpful to look up examples on YouTube.

I don't know about important and properly. I breathe rapidly and forcefully in and out from the belly and my neural network explodes. I fool around with all types of breathing during CT it is all incredible. This would not be proper as it is properly done in Ashtanga through the nose while engaged in ujayi breathing. I have found any breathing you do mindfully has benefits. Play people play!

### kathylu — 2012-12-08T11:34:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66806>

"Breath of Fire" is also a great technique for dispelling pain and fatigue. Increases tissue oxygenation and helps detoxify the body.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-08T13:17:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fire-breathing.5081/post-66815>

I'm wondering if its similar to the breathing techniques I employed while in labor. Especially the breaths I used during the most challenging contractions. I focused on tucking my chin and breathing out and focusing on opening with each breath.

18 months paleo and on board with allÂ  of Jack's protocols for the last year I am now incredibly stink free. I wasn't trusting my own observations because you can't always smell yourself. Yesterday I wore a tee shirt for 14+ hours. I smell the pit area. Nothing. I shove the pit area of the shirt under my wife's nose this morning and ask "Do you smell anything?" "like what?" she says." Like BO?" "No" WTF The kicker is I haven't used soap for months. I shower everyday with just water and i have no stink. I don't use deodorant. WOW!

# Thread: Would this be an approved meal/snack for an ocassional "treat"?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-this-be-an-approved-meal-snack-for-an-ocassional-treat.5085/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-07T14:43:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-this-be-an-approved-meal-snack-for-an-ocassional-treat.5085/post-66708>

If a person can tolerate this, would it be an approved meal/snack for an occasional "treat"?...

Â 

1/2 cup heavy creamÂ  (or 1/2 cup cold water if you don't want the cream) or 1/2 cup coconut milk

1-2 Tbsp Coconut Manna

2 Squares 90-99% Dark Chocolate Melted

Stevia to taste if desired

1 scoop Kaizen Naturals Whey Protein (Made from milk from 100 % Grass-Fed New Zealand Cows...24 gr Protein, 2 gr Carbs, 1 gr fat)

Ice

Â 

Place in a blender, and blend until smooth.

Â 

As I said, just for an occasional protein shake "treat"?

Â 

Just curious.

### Glen PDQ — 2012-12-08T01:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-this-be-an-approved-meal-snack-for-an-ocassional-treat.5085/post-66782>

Are you having hunger pangs or food cravings? I don't expect there are any approved snacks/treats on epi-paleo rx.Â  Frequenting this website I've learned if one is still Leptin Resistant then snacks certainly aren't optimal since one should allow 4-5 hours between meals so digestive system can recover.

As for it being an acceptable meal replacement maybe someone else will chime in.Â  Doesn't protein powder cause a larger insulin spike than meat?

### furface — 2012-12-08T02:13:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-this-be-an-approved-meal-snack-for-an-ocassional-treat.5085/post-66783>

> **Glen PDQ said**
> Are you having hunger pangs or food cravings? I don't expect there are any approved snacks/treats on epi-paleo rx.Â  Frequenting this website I've learned if one is still Leptin Resistant then snacks certainly aren't optimal since one should allow 4-5 hours between meals so digestive system can recover.
>
> As for it being an acceptable meal replacement maybe someone else will chime in.Â  Doesn't protein powder cause a larger insulin spike than meat?

I didn't necessarily mean as a "snack" or "treat" between meals, I meant instead of having a "regular" seafood/beef/fish etc full meal. Yes I'm having the occasional hunger pang, but that's just something I'll have to get used to. Esp between dinner and the time I go to bed.

Â 

As far as this particular protein powder goes, I performed an experiment upon myself this afternoon...the data etc is in the Leptin Rx forum, and my blood glucose never rose higher than 9 points over a two hour period, and I tested it every 20 minutes during the first hour, and every 15 minutes during the 2nd hour. So the insulin spike for me wasn't too bad at all. I mixed one scoop in 6 ounces of water. Drank it on an empty stomach. Didn't consume anything during the testing.

I am also a type 2 diabetic.

# Thread: Just completed an experiment on myself.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-07T18:54:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66734>

Just to see how much my blood glucose would rise if I consumed 1 scoop of Kaizen Naturals Brand Vanilla Ice-Cream Flavor Natural Whey Protein Powder blended with 6 oz cold water. On an empty stomach. (My last meal was 5 hours prior...my BAB!!)

Â 

Some info on the Kaizen Naturals Natural Whey Protein Powders:

Â 

Natural Whey Protein by Kaizen Naturals, is made from milk from 100% New Zealand Grass-Fed cows. It is free of hormones (rBGH), and antibiotics, as well as being GMO free.

Â 

The ingredients are for the Whey Isolate & Concentrate blend protein:

Â 

New Zealand Protein Blend, natural flavours, Enzyme Blend (Prolease I, Prolease II, Lactase, Amylase) a very small amount of soy lecithin, and stevia leaf.

Â 

Protein per 1 scoop (29.4 gr): 24 gr,Â  Calories: 115,. Carbs: 2 gr., Fat: 1 gr Saturated Fat: 0.5 gr, Trans 0gr, Cholesterol: 30mg, Sodium: 40mg,

Potassium: 200mg, Sugars: 1 gr.

Â 

I am a type 2 diabetic.

Â 

I took blood glucose readings every 20 minutes for the first hour after I drank the shake, then every 15 minutes during the second hour after.

Â 

TIMEÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  READINGÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  CHANGE FROM START Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  CHANGE FROM PREVIOUS

Â 

Before DrinkingÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  79.3Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0

20 Minutes AfterÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  79.3Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0

40 Minutes AfterÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  82.9Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  +3.6 PointsÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  +3.6 Points

1 Hour AfterÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  77.5Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  - 1.8 PointsÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  - 5.4 Points

1 Hour, 15 Min AfterÂ Â Â Â  86.5Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  + 7.2 PointsÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  + 9 Points

1 Hour, 30 Min AfterÂ Â Â Â  86.5Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  + 7.2 PointsÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0 Points

1 Hour, 45 Min AfterÂ Â Â Â  79.3Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  0Â  PointsÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  - 7.2 Points

2 Hours AfterÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  88.3Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  + 9Â  PointsÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  + 9Â  Points

Â 

So in conclusion, according to Dr. Kruse, a "spike" would be a jump of 20 points, therefore it seems as if I could enjoy an occasional protein shake made with this particular brand of whey protein, since the highest increase in blood glucose levels I obtained was 9 points.

I could probably also add one scoop of the protein powder (Vanilla Ice cream Flavor is my preference) to some water, or coconut milk, or heavy cream, along with some Coconut Manna, Ice, Melted 90-99% Dark Chocolate, and have a nice, tasty, fairly healthy, and fairly quick shake for a meal.

Of course this wouldn't be a regular meal idea, but for an occasional treat, or "break" from cooking, I don't think it would be all that bad.

Â 

If I've made any errors in the "science" behind the data, please feel free to let me know.

Â 

As mentioned, I consumed nothing for 5 hours since my last meal, prior to drinking the shake. I consumed nothing during the 2 hours of testing my blood glucose. I mixed 1 scoop of the protein powder in 6 ounces of water, and blended with a magic bullet blender.

I apologize for the lengthy post, but just wanted to find out for myself if I was "safe" to have the occasional shake made with this particular protein powder.

It's actually the best tasting Vanilla protein powder I've ever tried...and I've tried A LOT!

### Jude — 2012-12-07T19:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66743>

Well done furface..most impressed with your n=1. Am going to see if I can get some of that in Aussie.

### caroline — 2012-12-07T19:55:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66752>

Well done furface..most impressed with your n=1. Am going to see if I can get some of that in Aussie.

I would be interested if you find any here Jude. I would love to have a protein shake every once in awhile

### furface — 2012-12-07T20:20:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66755>

> **caroline said**
> Well done furface..most impressed with your n=1. Am going to see if I can get some of that in Aussie.
>
> I would be interested if you find any here Jude. I would love to have a protein shake every once in awhile

Aww, thanks Jude, and Caroline.

Â 

Since it's made from milk from 100% grass-fed New Zealand cows, it probably is available in Australia...one would think. I hope you can find it.

It's also available as a 100 % whey isolate...the one I have is an Isolate/Concentrate blend. Which I prefer as it makes the shakes a bit thicker. It comes in Vanilla ice cream, chocolate, strawberry, orange creamsicle, and unflavored. I really like the vanilla, because if I want to make chocolate, I can just add some melted chocolate, or cocoa powder. Also at times I sprinkle on some cinnamon, and it tastes like cinnamon buns.

I was worried when Dr. Kruse mentioned that whey protein causes spikes in glucose levels of diabetics, and then he mentioned that a spike is 20 points, so I figured I'd experiment. Glad I did. As I also mentioned, I'm not going to drink protein shake after protein shake in lieu of fish, shellfish, grass-fed meats etc, but once in a while for a "treat" or for a break, at least I know I'm "safe" to enjoy one on occasion. I also like the fact that there aren't a lot of unnecessary ingredients in it.Â  Plus it's not too expensive. Here in Canada a 2 lb tub is around $30-$40, while in the U.S. it's around $25-$30.

Â 

Thanks again, and I hope you can find some in Australia.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-07T21:36:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66769>

It is great that you performed this experiment.Â  We are all so different.Â  Another person may have experienced a large spike with the exact same dose.Â  So now you know this is a safe drink for you, in terms of insulin spikes, at least for this time of year.Â 

I don't think I do well with foods that smell sweet.Â  I even try to avoid smelling the protein powder drink I make for DH each morning.

### furface — 2012-12-07T22:00:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66771>

> **Nonchalant said**
> It is great that you performed this experiment.Â  We are all so different.Â  Another person may have experienced a large spike with the exact same dose.Â  So now you know this is a safe drink for you, in terms of insulin spikes, at least for this time of year.Â 
>
> I don't think I do well with foods that smell sweet.Â  I even try to avoid smelling the protein powder drink I make for DH each morning.

So true about how we are all different. Like you said, at least I know I am safe having this on occasion at least for this time of year.

I hear you about the smell of foods that smell sweet. Not only that, but how about the smell when you walk past a bakery? They say that smell is the strongest sense of recall that we have, so if a person has ever enjoyed some fresh baked bread, or buns, or cookies, or a pizza hot out of the oven, or whatever, it definitely can play tricks, and test one's willpower.

Â 

At least now I know for myself, and in case I know I'll be on the road, or unable to prepare myself a full, healthy meal, I can always take my blender bottle shaker cup with me, a scoop of this protein powder in a ziplock bag, and with a half bottle of water, shake it up, and have a quick, not unhealthy meal. In a pinch.

### Jude — 2012-12-08T00:06:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66777>

I have done a few N=1's with various foods. Orange- gave me huge spike and took ages to come down. Blue berries cause me a 5-10 pt rise. 2 almond 85% choc cookies today( 7.8 within an hr-5.9 in 2hr) somehow eventually gives me a high FBG tomorrow !Â

### furface — 2012-12-08T00:23:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66780>

I think it's a good idea for people to experiment once in a while, just so they'll know how they'd react. All within reason of course.

Â 

During my experiment today, My BG levels ranged from 4.4 - 4.9. I just converted them to the more common units. Over the past month, my average BG reading before meals has been 5.3 and after meals (2 hours after) has been 7.4. Or converted, 95.5, and 133.3 respectively. That is over 80 readings combined.Â  So it seems as if lately at least, my blood glucose levels have been nicely within range.

For me the main issue has always seemed to be not so much WHAT I eat, well sure, I've eaten the "wrong" things a lot of the time, but moreso, HOW MUCH I eat. I'm sure if I ate 1/2 cup of watermelon, my BG would rise somewhat, but might still stay within range. However, who can only eat 1/2 cup of watermelon? Not me, that's for sure. lol

Â 

Anyway, it's good to test things out once in a while, which could give us a few more options of what we can eat. All within limits of course, and making sure it fits in the Leptin Rx eating plan.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-08T09:23:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66793>

Excellent job of testing on yourself to know if it is ok for YOU!Â  It is ok and Jack has mentioned somewhere that sometimes ifÂ a person can tolerate it, it is good forÂ IGF-1 iirc.Â  I would have to dig to find this info for you if you wanted more info on it.

### furface — 2012-12-08T11:17:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-completed-an-experiment-on-myself.5087/post-66805>

> **vkiernan said**
> Excellent job of testing on yourself to know if it is ok for YOU!Â  It is ok and Jack has mentioned somewhere that sometimes ifÂ a person can tolerate it, it is good forÂ IGF-1 iirc.Â  I would have to dig to find this info for you if you wanted more info on it.

Thanks, vkiernan, I appreciate that. Yeah I think I recall reading that Jack had mentioned that, or something about having the odd whey protein shake once in a while, if your body can tolerate it, and I was searching through the forums trying to find it, but I couldn't. But when I saw that he mentioned somewhere that a "spike" would be considered a jump of 20 points in your blood glucose level, I decided to use myself as a human guinea pig.

Naturally I wouldn't do that with something that would easily cause an enormous spike, such as half a watermelon, \*drools like Homer Simpson...mmmmmwatermelon...aaaaahhhhhhggggggghhh...\* but for something like this, I'm glad I did it.

Before the forum was moved, there was a great thread on shrimp recipes, but I can't seem to find it!

So please, post your favorite shrimp recipe!

### Glen PDQ — 2012-12-10T00:50:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shrimp-recipes.5094/post-66986>

I threw together something today with shrimp though it had losts of other seafood. I took a 1 pound bag of mixed seafood (mussels, shrimp, ocotopus, squid), rinsed in cold water, barely covered with water and brought to simmer just until done.Â  I diced 2 cooked tilapia filets and added them to the finished seafood mix and the cooking water.Â  Seasoned with salt and pepper to taste, drizzled in a liberal amount of olive oil, and then stirred in 4 ounces of guacamole just before serving. More veggies could have been added, but it was pretty good for something thrown together.

# Thread: Magic live music?

### Inger — 2012-12-08T16:17:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66832>

I am sitting here reading the forum before going to bed.. and then I hear music from downstairs. My mom plays piano and my brothers daughter that is visiting plays violin.. Christmas music. It is so beautiful.. something happends in my heart I can feel it fysically!

It is so sweet.. like sweet numbness but in a amazing way.. wow. What is this? I had some huge stress today and I guess my heart was still too tired but this music did wonder? Just very simple home made live music nothing fancy but how good it felt. I need to start playing and listening to more live music. I am now sure it must be very healing!

### prAna303 — 2012-12-08T16:40:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66838>

I think itÂ´s quite simple. When the filter of junk in our modern world begin to clear up we start to resonate with things. My thought is that everything in this world is about somekind of electricity. When we eat junk, drink junk, sleep junk, consume junk and everything is "dirty" we cannot se the special things in classic and live music, we think it is boring. We seek more and more volume and punchlines. When the fog clears up that junksciencestuff become very disturbing and the small things become more enjoyable. We really enjoy it because we se the details. This applies to everything. Even the smallest change in detail resonate with our ownÂ frequency.

### Inger — 2012-12-08T16:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66842>

Yes. May be live music played with instruments or voices have a special electricity.. for sure. That must be it...

One day at work I waitressed for a christmas party in the cabinet and all of the sudden as I was on my way upstairs to the room I heard them singing.. They were all members of a choir.. I did not know.. Oh man it was so beautiful I just stopped right there.. sat down at the stairs and enjoyed the music so immensely. I all of a sudden was in a totally different word.
Like heaven.

I get paralyzed... that is.

### prAna303 — 2012-12-08T17:15:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66847>

IÂ´m a bit into both music and movies... A little food for your brain because i think you are on the same road as myself...

"Solfeggio sound frequencies were apparently used in Ancient Gregorian Chants. The chants and their special tones were believed to impart tremendous spiritual blessings when sung in harmony during religious masses"

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solf%C3%A8ge

### caroline — 2012-12-08T17:17:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66849>

> **Inger said**
> Yes. May be live music played with instruments or voices have a special electricity.. for sure. That must be it...
>
> One day at work I waitressed for a christmas party in the cabint and all of the sudden as I was on my way upstairs to the room I heard them singing.. They were all members of a choir.. I did not know.. Oh man it was so beautiful I just stopped right there.. sat down at the stairs and enjoyed the music so immensely. I all of a sudden was in a totally different word.
> Like heaven.
>
> I get paralyzed... that is.

One day - when you come to Sydney to visit - we will spend a lot of time at the harbour and the Opera house. Â I never ever tire of being there - just sitting and watching and listening - absolutely pure magic.....like you said ...heaven. Â The Sydney Opera company does an opera on the harbour after Christmas Â ...I went last year but we got rained out - I was so dissapointed so I will go this year. Â The setting is so magical ...the harbour and the bridge in the background and the sky - it takes my breath away.

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-08T20:30:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magic-live-music.5096/post-66888>

Resonate is the word isn't it? Â Emotions and music are so closely linked through resonance. We feel the vibrations and they take us (or create, or remind us) of our feelings. And just like music, our emotions can reach out and effect others who are turned in. Being washed over with beautiful sounds is one of the best experiences in the world. We are all just vibrating along...and we can choose to feel this when we are healthy. It is so hard to do when our signalling is busted...just like prAna303 said...

Â 

Gregorian chants were always part of the Christmas music we listened to growing up. To hear them in a church performed live would be so special.Â 

I also love the Misa Creolla, by Ramirez. It's a Argentine folk mass written in 1964. The harmonies and rhythms are so much part of Christmas for me...take a listen if you care to...the first five minutes give you a good taste.

Â 

www.youtube.com/watch?v=HzoZNgXmMSU

### Inger — 2012-12-08T16:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-66836>

I recognized it is way too expensive for me to use candles all the evening as it gets dark already at 3 PM here now... so I started to look out for some other options. What I found was the small 15 to 18 watt lamps that are ment for the oven or the fridge. They work perfectly well in a small lamp covered with a lamp-shade! I am just happy to have found these because they are cheap so much cheaper then candles. I guess a red light bulb would be even better but they did not have those in the shop here, I will look after them in Helsinki city one day I am there.
Now.. perfect would be to have it totally dark..lol.. but I need to get something done still.. but maybe next winter. Step by step.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-11T13:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-67119>

I have been making my own oil lamps for a few weeks.Â  (I started while preparing for Sandy...)

I use a hempÂ "wick" through a cork (covered in tin foil- I didn't use all of it for hats!)

I float this in canola oil (mixed with a little bit of pepermint oil) in a martini glass.

It looks really nice ...and smells great.

### Inger — 2012-12-28T14:28:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-68599>

Here are my brothers (and I am taking the picture) making olive oil lamps as in the video above! It went great! ### SeaHorse — 2012-12-28T14:56:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/instead-of-candles.5098/post-68603>

I was wondering if regular oil lamps would burn with olive oil? I have a number of old lamps and I've been using store bought paraffin Â which doesn't smell at all but is a petroleum product..I'd like to use something a little less toxic. I'm going to experiment and hopefully not ruin a lamp!

I love reading by our woodstove with the lamp light..it doesn't happen every night and the past week or so with Christmas things were a little bright, but at least my family is accepting the dark this winter. I was the one who had the really bad light habits...Because I had S.A.D. I always just thought more light was better so I used my SAD light all the time, even at night. No wonder my sleep disappeared. Now I can count on a great sleep almost every night and I don't feel depressed in the darkness...I'm enjoying it for the first time in a very very long time.

I love your picture Ingar..I recently made some beeswax candles and ornaments. The beeswax smells so good and I was lucky to find a bucket of it in our barn that was left by the previous owners. I had some old teacups that I glued to their saucers and then melted beeswax in the teacup with a wick. It's a pretty candle and easy to refill the cup when the wax is gone.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T17:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66852>

I want to start LHT but have never done anything like this before. Â Kettlebells seem like fun and something I would like to do. Â From what I read I should start pretty light but will need a heavier one soon after - so I should buy a pair? Â I am not sure what weight to start at tho Â 4Kg or 6Kg or 8Kg?

I am a real novice \[and older\] with not much strength - still some muscle weakness but getting better Â  - but I have/want to start somewhere. Â 

Also I see there are pro ones and just regular ones. Â The regular ones have much bigger handles. It seems like the pro ones are better for a variety of reasons Â - is that correct?

thanks for any help.

BTW - my goal is to become an older version of Inger ...lol Â .......I know, I know ...good luck with that! Â  Â JK says we have to have big dreams! Â That is so true. Â I have always overstretched my limits - it was always intuitive .......to be more - I had to want more.

### diane — 2012-12-08T18:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66857>

There was a book recommended to me to learn how to kettle bell. It's called Swing, and the author is Tracy Reifkins. I got it on Amazon as a kindle download.

Â 

It's great because it shows you proper technique, but then gives beginner programs all the way to advanced. To start she recommends only 10-15 minutes a day. And start with a light weight and get heavier as you get better.Â 

Â 

I've done them before so I have a 20 lb to start. We have a store here that you can sell used sporting goods - if you have one like that, would be good to start light and then trade up. It's not like you can wear out kettlebells. LOL.

### caroline — 2012-12-08T18:38:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66863>

There was a book recommended to me to learn how to kettle bell. It's called Swing, and the author is Tracy Reifkins. I got it on Amazon as a kindle download.

Â 

It's great because it shows you proper technique, but then gives beginner programs all the way to advanced. To start she recommends only 10-15 minutes a day. And start with a light weight and get heavier as you get better.Â 

Â 

I've done them before so I have a 20 lb to start. We have a store here that you can sell used sporting goods - if you have one like that, would be good to start light and then trade up. It's not like you can wear out kettlebells. LOL.

so - Diane - you do use kettlebells? do you enjoy and are you seeing progress? I can't imagine you wouldn't see progress. 20Kg seems heavy - so what size do you think I should start with? I will look up that book.

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-09T20:24:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66963>

Most women start with an 8kg, but a 12 and a 16 are a good idea, as you will likely outgrow the 8kg fairly quickly.

Just ensure that you move from your hip joints (bottom of pelvis), and not from your back (top of pelvis) and you should be good to go.

Too many people blame kettlebells and not form when they hurt themselves.

I'd reccomend spending an hour or two with a certified trainer if you have any doubts.

### fitness@home — 2012-12-09T21:41:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-66967>

I liked the DVDs from Skogg for learning proper KB technique:

http://www.skoggsystem.com/

KBs are my favorite form of exercise. I like that it can marry weights, range of motion, and endurance type exercise together.

Need to get back to this soon. Anyone up for 200 swings per day challenge? Easy to work up to and doesn't take much time to complete. Can be done in short sets of 25-50 swings each.

### diane — 2012-12-10T19:25:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-67055>

I tried to find a certified trainer - it was so difficult. And I live in a large city! Hence the book, which really does explain form in great detail. The author is RKC certified I believe, so it was the best option for me.

### furface — 2012-12-10T20:29:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-67061>

I'd also suggest you try Craigslist or a similar place for some used kettlebells. They are quite expensive when purchasing new, and they don't really wear out, so perhaps you can find a good deal on some used ones from someone who doesn't want theirs any more.

I've used kettlebells myself at the gym, but at home I use adjustable dumbbells in place of kettlebells. I either use them with one hand around the grip, or with two hands around one of the plates. Works basically the same, and I can adjust the weight to however heavy I want. I have 3 sets of spin lock collar dumbbell handles with nearly 200 lbs. of plates from 1 1/4 lbs., 2 1/2 lbs, 5 lbs, 7 1/2 lbs, 10 lbs, and 25 lbs.

### caroline — 2014-02-11T23:31:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-109698>

I love my kettlebells. I think I have already said somewhere. They are great for core strength. I noticed huge improvements almost right away and only did about 35 swings a few times a week.
I started with 8kg. I credit them with my body appearing - that I didn't know I had ....

### sooperb — 2014-02-13T12:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-109966>

As an expert on buying then not using exercise equipment I also bought a kettle bell. I made a big mistake, it was 8kg and I could barely lift it so I had to buy a lighter one - 4 kg. even that was a challenge. I have made great use of them since, as bedroom doorstops  I bought a DVD from eBay which turned out to be a copy so was missing some of the instructions. Long story short, the rights owner ended up sending me a bona fide copy by which time my enthusiasm had waned again.

Funnily enough I was wondering if kbs would fit the LHT and was just reading about how even doing as little as the initial warm up swing followed by a lift is really good. I've also been practising squat sitting, it's a lot of fun lol.

### caroline — 2014-02-13T23:27:55+0200

It takes 5 minutes and I think it is so much fun ...... I think I am easily amused tho......

### Da-mo — 2018-02-04T11:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kettlebells.5099/post-234097>

Do some yard work. You could swing an axe and chop firewood, then stack it instead of kettlebells. I have 5 acres that needs lots of stuff done if anyone is looking for a place to train

But seriously, you can get enough exercise just doing stuff that needs doing without going out of your way to find a workout. I once lived on a hill so steep that I used to tie a rope to the lawnmower and lower it down the slope then pull it back up again. . . . . with a catcher on. Also, I would ride a bicycle to work instead of driving.

It cracks me up seeing people who drive to the gym then spend 20 minutes on the treadmill when they could have just walked there in the open air and sunshine.

# Thread: Is the fat/"juice" left in the pan after browning grass-fed ground beef good to consume?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-fat-juice-left-in-the-pan-after-browning-grass-fed-ground-beef-good-to-consume.5101/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-08T20:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-fat-juice-left-in-the-pan-after-browning-grass-fed-ground-beef-good-to-consume.5101/post-66883>

I realize that we're supposed to consume a lot of healthy fats, esp on Leptin Reset, which I am trying to do...coconut oil, bacon fat, fats from fish, etc, but I was just wondering is the fat/grease/juice that's left in the pan after you cook, or brown grass-fed ground beef, good to consume as well?

Â 

Maybe a better question would be, what are the fats that are NOT safe/good to consume...if any? Esp if trying to lose bodyfat.

Â 

Thanks.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-08T22:32:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-fat-juice-left-in-the-pan-after-browning-grass-fed-ground-beef-good-to-consume.5101/post-66904>

> **furface said**
> I realize that we're supposed to consume a lot of healthy fats, esp on Leptin Reset, which I am trying to do...coconut oil, bacon fat, fats from fish, etc, but I was just wondering is the fat/grease/juice that's left in the pan after you cook, or brown grass-fed ground beef, good to consume as well?
>
> Â 
>
> Maybe a better question would be, what are the fats that are NOT safe/good to consume...if any? Esp if trying to lose bodyfat.
>
> Â 
>
> Thanks.

Oh yeah that's good fat. Good veg fats are olive, avocado, coconut. All the animal and fish fats are good. bad fats are industrial seed oils like canola, peanut, soy, cottonseed, sunflower If the label says vegetable oil-pass. I render my own lard from pork fat and tallow from beef fat. CO is particularly good for weight loss as your body won't store it.

### furface — 2012-12-08T22:37:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-fat-juice-left-in-the-pan-after-browning-grass-fed-ground-beef-good-to-consume.5101/post-66905>

> **Dali Dula said
>
> > furface said
>
> **
>
> > I realize that we're supposed to consume a lot of healthy fats, esp on Leptin Reset, which I am trying to do...coconut oil, bacon fat, fats from fish, etc, but I was just wondering is the fat/grease/juice that's left in the pan after you cook, or brown grass-fed ground beef, good to consume as well?
> >
> > Â 
> >
> > Maybe a better question would be, what are the fats that are NOT safe/good to consume...if any? Esp if trying to lose bodyfat.
> >
> > Â 
> >
> > Thanks.
>
> Oh yeah that's good fat. Good veg fats are olive, avocado, coconut. All the animal and fish fats are good. bad fats are industrial seed oils like canola, peanut, soy, cottonseed, sunflower If the label says vegetable oil-pass. I render my own lard from pork fat and tallow from beef fat. CO is particularly good for weight loss as your body won't store it.

Awesome, thanks Dali.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-12-10T21:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-genes-interaction.5106/post-67073>

If rats can do it, I'm guessing we can too....

Fetal fuels. I. Utilization of ketones by isolated tissues at various stages of maturation and maternal nutrition during late gestation

Metabolism, Volume 26, Issue 6, June 1977

Abstract
The availability and utilization of B-hydroxybutyrate as an alternate oxidative fuel during fasting hypoglycemia has been examined in the rat conceptus at 18 and 20 days gestation. A 48-hr maternal fast between days 16 and 18 or 18 and 20 resulted in a 50% fall in fetal glucose levels and a marked rise in B-hydroxybutyrate, i.e., 30-fold at 18 and 60-fold at 20 days. Tissue concentrations of B-hydroxybutyrate or acetoacetate did not exceed extracellular levels. Placenta, fetal brain, carcass, and liver all oxidized 14C-labeled B-hydroxybutyrate to 14CO2 when incubated in vitro in the presence of B-hydroxybutyrate. Highest rates of oxidation were apparent in the placenta, followed by brain, liver, and carcass. The D isomer of B-hydroxybutyrate appeared to be oxidized preferentially by all tissues studied. Despite levels of 3-ketoacid CoA transferase and acetoacetyl CoA thiolase lower at 18 than at 20 days, rates of oxidation in individual tissues incubated under identical concentrations of substrate were similar at both times. In liver and brain, increasing rates of 14CO2 generation proportionate to graded concentrations of B-hydroxybutyrate in vitro indicated that such rates were probably determined by substrate availability. B-hydroxybutyrate oxidation in extrahepatic fetal tissues was unaffected by maternal fasting. By contrast, fetal liver derived from fasted mothers generated significantly less 14CO2 from B-hydroxybutyrate that livers from fed mothers. It has been suggested that capabilities for ketone utilization are widespread in tissues of the conceptus, and that such utilization may fulfill in part the oxidative demands for continued anabolic growth during fasting hypoglycemia in the mother.

### ruby — 2012-12-10T22:04:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-genes-interaction.5106/post-67079>

Thanks, KiwiLauren. I wonder if this is also one of mechanisms that plays a role here (this is an extreme clear cut example but the mechanism could be similar in the Western world in cases of less severe nutritional deficiencies):
"Programmed for Diabetes - Researchers are finding that underfed mothers produce small, undernourished babies with metabolisms equipped for deprivation and unable to cope with plenty. As a child growing up in an Indian village near the Arabian Sea, at least once a week Sonar plodded for two hours along a dirt path to collect water for her eight-member family, which existed on two meals a day of vegetables and rice. David Barker, a British doctor from the University of Southampton whose research has helped explain the origins of so-called lifestyle ailments says malnutrition during a babyâ€™s development affects how a personâ€™s body behaves for a lifetime. An undernourished fetus prioritizes sugar for its growing brain. To make more glucose available in the blood, the fetus stores less of the energy in its muscles by making the muscles resistant to the effects of insulin. What starts as a clever survival trick in the womb becomes a liability in later life. When food is freely available but the muscles canâ€™t store excess glucose, the blood floods with sugar and diabetes develops. Too much sugar in the blood damages the heart, small blood vessels and nerves, compounding the risk of heart attack, stroke and kidney failure. â€œThe biggest recipe for chronic disease is to have a very poor start in life and then to be in a situation where youâ€™re rapidly transitioning towards excess nutrition and inadequate activity,â€ says Caroline Fall, 56, a professor of international pediatric epidemiology at Southampton, who joined Barkerâ€™s group in 1990 and has studied diseases in India since 1991. â€œThatâ€™s absolutely characteristic of whatâ€™s happening in India.â€
Chittaranjan S. Yajnik observed that Indian newborns were typically small and thin -- like their mothers -- yet carried proportionally MORE insulin-repelling FAT than infants in England. He found low birth weight predicted insulin resistance in Indian children, identifying those already on the road to diabetes and cardiovascular disease, just as Barker had posited."

# Thread: DZLogic, XRMD or Local?  Any Differences in BHRT Treatment

### mafixit — 2012-12-09T13:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dzlogic-xrmd-or-local-any-differences-in-bhrt-treatment.5109/post-66941>

I am consulting with a local OBGYN practice that provides BHRT therapy.Â  My question is, what would DZlogic and/or XRMD do differently that would warrant me spending in excess of $2K out of pocket to not go local?Â  Or to get my new doc to administer the other treatment plan?

The new doc will order baseline labs and treat based on deficiencies.Â  I will know more after my consult.

Any idea of the treatment differences or benefits of paying for a consulting service such as DZ or XR?Â  I want to get well, and I think this is the path, but the $$$ is a big factor.Â  I am still trying to collect some out of pocket expenses for other labs and it's killing my and my credit card.

Any specific questions you think I should address at my visit?Â  I need to make an informed decision ASAP and optimal lab dates are going to happen soon.

Honest and thoughtful opinions are appreciated.

Â 

Karen from Arizona

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-09T18:01:25+0200

### mafixit — 2012-12-17T17:06:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dzlogic-xrmd-or-local-any-differences-in-bhrt-treatment.5109/post-67737>

Found a local doctor close by who specializes in women's health issues, believes in BHRT, and thinks Dr. Dzugan is quite intelligent.Â  She even recommended his book, The Migraine Cure.Â 

She was impressed with the labs I already had done (it was everything she was going to order, including the adrenal stress and salivary cortisol), and we have started a course of treatment for my migraines.Â  She thinks I may not be peri-menopausal, but actually in menopause already.Â  A few more labs are in the works for Ferritin, Homocysteine, Iron and Pregnenolone, along with RBC Zinc and RBC Magnesium.

Starting with Estrogen cream, Progesterone pill, a prescription dose of Vitamin D3 (50,000 IU once per week), a pro-biotic for my gut issues and a heavy duty mutli-vitamin.

AND she is in my insurance plan as a primary care physician!Â  Whooppeeeeeeee!

It took a lot of phone calls, a lot of research and some persistence.Â  Fingers crossed.

Supplements were expensive, but not as much as I have been spending on Mayo Clinic co-pays.

Please everyone think positive for me!

Karen from Arizona.

Â

Even if I can find GF bones, I still might go with non GF just for the price savings. For eating the meat, I'd stick with GF, but if it's just going to be for broth (well there might be some bits of meat left on the bones to add a "bonus" to the broth), I figure why not save some money if I can.

Â 

Also, is it okay after the broth is made, to take a smaller pot, add some diced onions, celery, carrots (not too many of course to increase the carb content, and make a "soup" or stew out of some of the broth, and have that as a meal?Â  Just for a change from a regular plate type meal.

.

I could be blind and missed itÂ so bare with meÂ - I haven'tÂ read anything on here about **Hemp Oil.Â  I'm shocked nobody has mentioned using it.Â  Hemp oil can actually fix bad DNAÂ  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â  It's a rogue repairman running lose inside you fixing damaged DNA.Â 

Â 

Research on the use of Hemp Oil found itÂ to be a major factor in DNA repair. Hemp has the perfect ratio of Omega fatty acids (Omega 3 and Omega 6) needed by the human body. One â€˜jobâ€™ of Omega 3 is cellular repair. Hemp is 65% protein, 35% of which is globulin edestin protein. It is the protein that is closest to human globulin, and is very easily digestible by the body.

Â 

The protein is a major factor in DNA repair, as the cells use that protein to correct the DNA damage.

Â 

Because hemp is the highest source of Edestin proteinÂ it'sÂ responsible for boosting the immune system among many of it's benefits. Hemp oil has been proven to lower bad cholesterol, raise HDL, prevent strokes, and more....Â  _see below (1)

Â 

Edestin is a globular protein (biologically active) as opposed to fibrous protein (structural). Edestin is a biologically active protein (most similar to globulins), and biologically active proteins are metabolized and used by the human body to manufacture antibodies (immunoglobulins), hormones, haemoglobin and enzymes.

Â 

Edestin has the unique ability to stimulate the manufacturing process of antibodies against invasive agents and contains a low aggravatory phosphor content (kidney ailments, etc.)

Â 

Globular proteins found in Edestin (and in Alpha 1 globulins, Alpha 2 globulins, Beta globulins and Gamma globulins) are long peptide-chains, precursors for biological proteins essential for life.

Â 

Purdue University; Hemp: A New Crop with New Uses for North America; Ernest Small, David Marcus; 2002_
Â 

**Hemp Oil: Benefits

-   **Hemp oil helps with hormonal balance. Hemp oil is the onlyÂ  edible seed that contains gamma-linolenic acid. G.L.A is the precursor for theÂ  production of the protective and calming prostaglandin PGE1 which helps regulateÂ  hormonal balance and support menopausal health.
    \[\*\]**Hemp oil helps regenerate skinâ€™s protective layer. ThanksÂ  to its content of Omega 3 and Omega 6 fatty acids, hemp oil has a compositionÂ  similar to skin lipids which makes it an excellent natural emollient andÂ  moisturizer. It is an ingredient in many skin and body care products. Hemp oilÂ  helps to calm irritated skin.
    \[\*\]**Hemp oil energizes the skin**. Moisturizing, regenerating andÂ  revitalizing, hemp oil is especially useful for dry, tired or dehydrated skin.Â  Skin regeneration and hydration of dry mature skin are enhanced through the useÂ  of hemp oil. It increases the skin elasticity and water retention capacity inÂ  tissues.
    Hemp oil has the ideal combination of fatty acids**. Hemp oilÂ  is the only vegetable oil that contains Omega 3 and Omega 6 fatty acids in theÂ  ideal ratio optimal for body needs.
    **

**
Â 

Hemp Oil: Remedies**

High Cholesterol Levels**. The essential fatty acids fromÂ  hemp seeds and oil can help lower cholesterol levels by accelerating metabolicÂ  processes. With a faster metabolism, fats burn faster and less are deposited onÂ  top of muscle mass or in artery walls.
Diabetes**. For diabetics, the blood sugar index of hemp oilÂ  is considered low because it has low carbohydrate content. It is also full ofÂ  nutrients that help moderate blood sugar levels.
\[\*\]**Atopic dermatitis. Research has discovered that psoriasisÂ  is caused by a deficiency of omega 6 fatty acids in the body. Therefore, hempÂ  oil is recommended as a complementary treatment. The fatty acids present in hempÂ  oil help improve skin oxygenation and hydration.
\[\*\]**Weakened Immune system. Research shows that the omega-3 andÂ  omega-6 fatty acids improve the immune system and regulate intestinal flora,Â  thus building a natural barrier against microbes and increasing the resilienceÂ  of the body.
Demyelination of Nervous system**. The essential fatty acidsÂ  are necessary for a healthy cell membrane structure, especially the nerve cellsÂ  of the myelin shield (which groups the nerve cells). Hemp oil is recommended forÂ  those who have already suffered from the process of demyelination where nerveÂ  membranes lose their myelin sheath.
Varicose veins**. Like other compounds high in omega-3s, hempÂ  oil can thin your blood, reducing blood clots and varicose veins.

### Thor — 2012-12-10T02:34:38+0200

.

As a Chef - I'm always thinking about food and science behind it.Â  Do I dear ask what's "_coco-crack_"??Â  You're making another batch?Â 

is it legal in most states?

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-10T12:57:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-wonders-of-hemp-oil.5112/post-67009>

DUDE ... you're a chef?! Â How'd you go from biology to cooking? Â Or am I remember that wrong?

Cococrack ... mmmm and perfectly legal, LOL. Â I have since improved on the general recipe (and not the first one either) ... take a jar of coconut butter/manna. Â If you're in a cold area you'll need to nuke it about 30 seconds to warm it up enough to move. Â Dump into a bowl with 40 grams -- more or less depending on your taste -- of dark chocolate (the less sugar the better). Â I like to add about 1/4 cup of semi-sweet chocolate chips because it seems to smooth it out a touch but if that's too much sugar you can just skip it. Â Add as much sweetener of your choice as you want. Â I have pure sucralose in my kitchen and it doesn't take much. Last time I used the equivalent of half a cup of sugar and it was just about perfect.

Nuke again for about 30 seconds and then stir stir stir. Â You may need to nuke again if you have a weeny microwave like we do.

Once it's all nice and melted and combined you can either leave it in the bowl and just eat with a spoon when the mood hits or stir in whatever. Â It does take a while to set back up though. Â I put it out on the floor of the porch (ceramic tiles get really cold in the winter so it's like having a walk-in cooler. Â 

Last time I added chopped walnuts and poured it out into a buttered pan (never can have enough butter, right?). Â I'm still working on it. Â I just hack off a piece whenever the mood hits. Â Next time I'm adding hemp seeds. And maybe a few chopped walnuts.

Oh and the original recipe called for pure coconut oil. Â People on another forum could NOT stop eating the stuff hence the name, cococrack. Â I could never get ANY sweetener to evenly distribute through the oil no matter what I tried. Â The butter/manna is a much better medium for that. Â You know how oil doesn't like to mix with anybody.

.

# Thread: mangled finger and wound care
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Priscilla — 2012-12-10T20:40:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67063>

OK, I need some advice. Â I just got home from the ER where I got 10 stitches in my finger, which got mangled with my immersion blender after making avocado oil mayo. Â Yes, very stupid.Â 

This was my first ER visit ever and I'm kind of paranoid about infections etc. Â And the waiting room was filled with people who were coughing and sneezing.

Any recommendations about supplements I should be making sure I take? Â I will only eat seafood and seaweed and bone broth these next few days. Â Anything else? Â  I can't get it wet for 36 hours.

It's interesting that I could feel my body responding to the shock of all of this. Â I don't know how to describe it, but I could feel my body react. Â And when I got there, my BP was 177 over something (I've had it checked twice within the last month and it was 120/80). Â But it went down 20 points when they checked it again.

I'd love to hear your advice.

Wow, it took me forever to type this!Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### nonchalant — 2012-12-10T21:10:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67069>

Oh Priscilla, I'm so sorry to hear that!Â  Ouch!

My first thought for prevention of infection was CT.

As for supplements, perhaps some vitamin C and/or zinc?Â  Topical iodine for the hand.

Take care of yourself.Â  Darn, I didn't know making mayo was a hazardous endeavor.Â  /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_wink.gif

### Thor — 2012-12-10T21:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67070>

.

First - follow all advice from the ER...Â Â  May need to see your Primary Care for follow up?Â Â  If you see any red "streaks" from the finger that run up your arm - call 911 !Â  Any signs of infection call your doctor.Â  Your condition gets worse - call your doctor.

Â 

Any good Antibiotic cream would be of help - keep it dry and change the bandge as instructions would reflect.Â  Supplements?Â  Hefty dose of garlic, grapefruit seed extract, Beta-Glugans, mega dose of probioticsÂ and perhaps toss in some EPICOR\[sup\]TMÂ Â Â  \[/sup\]

Â 

The old cowboys of yester year used good ol' whiskeyÂ 

Â 

Next - toss out blender and use a large hammer on it lol !Â Â  gurrrr bad blender...

### diane — 2012-12-10T21:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67077>

Wow - sorry to hear that Priscilla! Not sure what would help, but just wanted to say hope you heal quickly! You did much better than me in the ER - I usually pass out (or come close). LOL.Â 

Â 

Good luck!

### endless — 2012-12-10T23:10:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67089>

I did this two weeks ago...6 stitches in the pad of my thumb! I didn't change up my supplement regime at all, but I did use colloidal silver spray on the wound after the first 24 hours. Personally I wouldn't use a cream...too moist. I healed up quickly just with clean dry dressings, airing out daily and spraying with silver during my breaks from bandages.

Hope you heal up quickly!

### Jonathin — 2012-12-11T12:41:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67116>

A quality honey applied topically will keep the skin soft and stop or reverse any infection. This is cheap and really works with any skin injury including burns. Yes, you need to put some gauze over it and the honey will thin out over time. Too much is messy and not necessary. In wounds with dirt or particles, honey will clean the wound nicely which is better than the typical cleaning procedure. By the way the use of aloe on deep sutured wounds can heal the surface too quickly before the deeper flesh has knit together.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-11T16:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67144>

> **endless said**
> I did this two weeks ago...6 stitches in the pad of my thumb!
>
> Â 

Endless?Â  Another victim of mayo-revenge??Â

### Priscilla — 2012-12-11T17:47:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67150>

Thanks so much for all your suggestions. Â Hmmm, colloidal silver, honey, CT, whiskey, iodine, zinc and vit C. Sounds like a great regimen.Â 

I'm supposed to leave on their dressing until tomorrow, not getting it wet at all. I will air it out for a while when I take it off, I'll use gauze dressings as it all heals. Â I'm not feeling any pain, so that's good. I will keep a good eye on it because I don't want it to get infected.

Thor, I won't be using a hammer on the blender because it was user error/stupidity. I've beat myself up a lot over this already.Â 

Live and learn! Â Thanks, everyone!

### endless — 2012-12-11T20:46:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mangled-finger-and-wound-care.5118/post-67161>

> Endless? Another victim of mayo-revenge??

Lol! Not exactly...I was rushing while opening a can and the sharp edge got me.

# Thread: Just had a meal that raised my blood glucose level a WHOPPING 1.7 points!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-a-meal-that-raised-my-blood-glucose-level-a-whopping-1-7-points.5119/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-10T20:42:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-a-meal-that-raised-my-blood-glucose-level-a-whopping-1-7-points.5119/post-67064>

I was out doing some shopping, and came home and had some lunch.

I had a can of crab meat, 6 slices of a small, long, English, Cucumber, 3 mushrooms sliced up, 5 strips of bacon from a butcher shop (OMG so much better than "brand" name from the store), and 8 pistachios. (I know, I know, but hey I had a craving, and it was with my meal.)

Â 

Anyway, before I ate, my blood glucose reading was 4.2 or 75.7 in U.S. units.Â  Two hours after, my blood glucose went up a WHOPPING 1.7 points to 4.3 or 77.4 in U.S. units. That was without taking any diabetes medication.

Â 

Damn, looks like Epi-Paleo can even help lower, or keep blood sugar levels down!

### nonchalant — 2012-12-10T21:43:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-a-meal-that-raised-my-blood-glucose-level-a-whopping-1-7-points.5119/post-67074>

Furface, you're having a lot of fun with that glucose monitor.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### Destiny — 2012-12-12T07:01:51+0200

### kathylu — 2012-12-12T11:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-and-me-sale.5124/post-67210>

One of the reasons I did it was because there is no future cost or subscription. However, they already have your genome sequence so as new info becomes available on snp's, diseases, syndromes, etc. you will be able to see your results for those too! I think it's a great bargain. It's also fun...lots of ancestry info, etc. Jack will look at the results too. He often picks up things you might not notice.

Â 

It didn't tell me much that I hadn't figured out from my medical history (except that I have the hemochromatosis mutation), I have an increased risk for heart/kidney disease as compared to the general population. As I said, no surprise...everyone in my family seems to kick off from some heart related ailment. However, it's pretty motivating when you see risk profiles in black and white.

Â 

I'd go for it...no telling how long the price will be this low. I just ordered a kit for my husband and son!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23-and-me-sale.5124/post-67244>

thankx kathylu - I am going to order one.

(I won't have too much of that this Christmas though) It does have a lot of fat which is great to save for other cooking. My mother's copy of Joy of Cooking was always my standby for recipes. I love duck too, but goose seems more Christmassy. That's what I'm hunting around for this week.

# Thread: Not Sure If Jack Is Checking Blogs?  Solar Cycle  Embryo Loss Research
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-jack-is-checking-blogs-solar-cycle-embryo-loss-research.5134/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thor — 2012-12-12T12:22:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-jack-is-checking-blogs-solar-cycle-embryo-loss-research.5134/post-67215>

.

I keep beating my drum saying MTHFR 677 & 1298 -Â SNP is extremely serious - yet Jack's stance is it's the exception not the rule.Â  OH?Â  Here's some early proof that will knock your socks off!!

Â 

Solar Cycles & L-MTHF are related to embryo loss.Â  Circadian rhythm can affect an embryo - even before the fetus stage of life.Â  Even before a baby is exposed to light and dark outside of the womb.Â Â  And - the real kicker of this amazing research is a new field of disorders never seen before.Â  They are being called:Â  'solar activity/seasonality modulated developmental disorders' - WOW....Â Â  talk about evolution meets genetic science.Â Â Â  I could get into far more "geek" mode but I'll leave it short for now.

Â 

**solar cycle predicts folate-related human embryo loss:**

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22608858](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22608858)
