# Thread: What can I use to absorb, or sop up all the fat/juice left on my plate?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-12T13:51:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67227>

I'm trying to eat as much fat as I can with my meals as per the Leptin Rx instructions, and can tolerate "drinking" a tablespoon worth after my meal from what's left on my plate...or maybe even a little more. I've also managed to choke down a teaspoon or so of coconut oil from the jar, but at times I still have quite a bit remaining on my plate, and if I put it into a container for later, then I likely haven't consumed enough during my meal to reach the high percentage amount recommended for the meal.

Â 

So in lieu of a nice, soft, warm, bun, or roll, and leafy vegetables, or asparagus etc only absorb so much, what can I use to to sop it up?

Â 

Especially if I'm having fish which contains like 2 or 3 grams of fat only per the macronutrient breakdown.

Â 

Any advice or suggestions would be most appreciated.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67260>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67261>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-12T18:23:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67266>

### furface — 2012-12-12T18:44:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67268>

Oh most of the time I can "lap" it up...and don't care if any gets into/onto my beard, esp if it's full of spices. But at times it's just a lot of bacon grease for instance, and if I've just finished eating 8 slices or so of bacon as part of my BAB, then I'm kind of baconed out, but still want to consume the fat so I get the proper balance of macronutrients into each meal that I'm supposed to.

Â 

But maybe I'll just close my eyes, down the hatch with it, then run to the john.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-12T23:07:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67298>

### furface — 2012-12-12T23:43:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67301>

> **vkiernan said**
> If you are eating 8 strips of bacon in 1 sittingÂ I don't think you need to worry about eating more fat! LOL.Â 

Good point. LOL. Hey 2 strips is only between 5 & 7 grams of protein, so 8 strips only make up 20 - 28 grams of the 50-75 grams of protein required for the BAB. Add 3 eggs, and that still only gets you a maximum of 46 grams...still below the minimum requirement. haha

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-12-13T21:51:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67434>

### furface — 2012-12-13T22:39:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67451>

> The floret part of broccoli can sop up a good amount of fat....Â 

Another good idea! Thanks RiverNeighbor.

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-12-14T05:49:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-use-to-absorb-or-sop-up-all-the-fat-juice-left-on-my-plate.5136/post-67473>

I actually pour the grease of my bacon (microwaved) over the two eggs omelette with butter and blue cheese (microwaved) at breakfast.

Â 

I have to confess that I still have a little white potato (always smaller than 100g, I think) with my dinners (broiled/steamed seafood and vegetables) so I can smash it at the end and soak it with all of the olive oil I can. I know, I know

... I haven't went all the way to epi-paleo low carb on winter yet.

So I tested to wrap my cellphone into layers of aluminum foil - the one you use in the kitchen.

Made the signals much weaker, yay! So it means the foil will be great for a hat to protect my brain (and maybe face too?) from excess radiation as I need to sit quite a bit in front of the computer. I am going to make me a design hat and mask the Christmas days when peace is around and I will have time to do special things. Wow I am already exited!

I will try making the oil lamps then, too. My brother will be here for visit and sure we will have some fun. He is a huge epi paleo fan and wants to try out anything I suggest..lol

He recently called and said; Inger.. it is so weird but I have come so to peace lately. It is like I am totally happy with how things are in my life now, the restlessness is gone...

Guess how happy it made me. My dear brother. The most diligent human beeing I know for sure. He is always up to helping others.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-28T15:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-own-christmas-present-will-be-a-special-hat.5137/post-68605>

OMG Inger, I already started to laugh, when I read the first post, as my imagination was running wild. But when I saw your hat I totally cracked up. I almost fell off my couch, but lost my laptop and lost the threat, so I had to log in again. I am still laughing and you know what, I imagined you in a knights armour with a visir to put up and down and when I saw the picture, it was just as I had imagined. And the idea of you sweating under it. Gosh, you made my day.

Why don't you just convince your employer, to make his restaurant into a middle ages restaurant, where people can eat with their hands, get huge roasts with bone and all and you can wear a knights armour waiting on people?

This is so good. I needed a good laugh tonight and here it is. I am probably still laughing when I go to bed. You didn't just make my day, you made my dreams as well.

How to make candles? In my hippie days I used to make candles to make some money selling them on the flea markets. Its easy. Melt the wax and pour it into a pan, maybe 1/4 inch or more thick. Wait for it to cool down, so it is still manageable. Then take a wick, place it along one side and start rolling the wax into a tube. I used to use the baking pan as it has one side with a straight end and the other a 45Â° angle. Roll the straight side last. Then you have a nice candle. If you like you can sprinkle some color into the hot wax. It makes some nice effects. To buy parafin and stearin is cheap, then you can make your own candles in all sizes. Bees wax is nice too, but hard to get and expensive.

# Thread: Rx for a Narcoleptic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rx-for-a-narcoleptic.5145/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stoic — 2012-12-12T21:50:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rx-for-a-narcoleptic.5145/post-67290>

I can see two main issues when using the Leptin Rx for Narcolepsy:

\[list type=decimal\]
\[\*\]Appetite signalling can be completely broken
Wake times can be completely random
\[/list type=decimal\]
How would one address these when implementing the Leptin Rx. For example, it can take 300g-400g of protein for me to actually get the full feeling from a meal. Fat is mostly useless too, as I can easily finish 1/2 jar of coconut manna without feeling full. Even when my stomach is completely full and distended I can find I still have an appetite for more food.

Â 

My wake times can be very random, maybe 3am one morning, then 8am the next. I may even fall asleep again and wake up hours later.

Â 

So would I just implement the Leptin Rx with 75g lean protein every morning at the same time (e.g. 7am) regardless of when I actually wake. Then wait to see if that eventually helps?

### primat — 2013-01-29T00:04:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rx-for-a-narcoleptic.5145/post-73064>

Hey, I did the leptin rx a year ago, and I'm restarting it now with the intention of follow that eating pattern with epi-paleo ketogenic food.
I would eat 6-7am even if I woke early. Dinner finished no later than 6pm, and absolutely nothing but water outside the meals. It was the only time in my adult life I can remember having my body giving me the satisfied signal with food left on the plate. The low number and sensitivity of the hypocretin makes it really hard for us narcoleptics to actually get a correct "reading" of the leptin signaling. I remember feeling hungry for a few weeks last time, since I only made the food I knew I would need. Typically around 1500kcal at breakfast and 1000-1500 for dinner. I didn't loose much weight but I felt much better, and cataplexy attacks was a lot "lighter"

Your milage may vary but about the coconut manna: I had to lay off - I got \_wicked\_ cravings. Coconut oil is ok. And fish seemed to be a lot better that other protein sources. Also; I need to stay 100% clean from milk protein, even a tiny tiny bit will screw me up, no matter how organic.

I'd recommend you try eating at the same time every day, but you will have to keep at it longer before seeing results compared to Average Joe with normal hypocretin levels.

# Thread: Question about face dunking and showering in cold water for CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-face-dunking-and-showering-in-cold-water-for-ct.5159/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-13T19:04:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-face-dunking-and-showering-in-cold-water-for-ct.5159/post-67396>

Since I am unable to use my bathtub for "official" CT ice baths, and don't have the room, capacity, or area for a separate tub, and have to rely on taking cold water showers, and face dunking in ice water for my CT sessions, and currently don't have a skin thermometer, how can I tell if I'm "over doing" any of my sessions?

As I'm sure you all are aware, your face turns deep red after being dunked...at least I hope it's supposed to.

And for the first time last night after my 21 minute cold shower, my chest skin felt a bit..."frosty" for the lack of a better term. The kind of feeling where when something thaws out, and it's not frozen, but not quite completely thaw either. Sorry I can't describe it better. It felt sort of "tight" or "crunchy" even, but not painful at all. Just a bit difficult to move it...and I have a lot of skin to move.

So is there a way to tell when you've had "enough" time when first starting out? Not saying that I did of course, just curious.

Also how long should face dunking sessions last?

Thanks. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### endless — 2012-12-13T20:09:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-face-dunking-and-showering-in-cold-water-for-ct.5159/post-67411>

I don't do showers, but I've noticed that it takes water in the tub under 50F to make my skin turn red. The longest I've stayed in is 35 minutes. For face dunking, I just do it till IO get tired of doing it, really! I'd guess about 10 times?

Is your skin on your chest white after the shower? I think that as long as it is pink you are ok. Maybe someone else can give you a better answer...

### furface — 2012-12-13T22:44:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-face-dunking-and-showering-in-cold-water-for-ct.5159/post-67454>

> **endless said**
> I don't do showers, but I've noticed that it takes water in the tub under 50F to make my skin turn red. The longest I've stayed in is 35 minutes. For face dunking, I just do it till IO get tired of doing it, really! I'd guess about 10 times?
>
> Is your skin on your chest white after the shower? I think that as long as it is pink you are ok. Maybe someone else can give you a better answer...

Thanks endless.

Good point about the face dunking. I might eventually increase the amount of times I do it, once I'm able to hold my breath a bit longer than 32 seconds or so. But it really is so refreshing.

As far as my skin after the shower goes...it varies between white, and pinkish. Hasn't been red yet. And I do remember to rotate, or turn every few minutes. The first couple of times I was so bored just standing there, so last night, I decided to sing while standing there. haha. I kept singing the same songs so that both my front, and back would get the same amount of time. Hey whatever works, right?

Wow, 35 minutes is great! Good for you!

# Thread: PANDAS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandas.5162/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdBren — 2012-12-13T22:50:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandas.5162/post-67455>

Some of you may remember me trying to find answers for my daughter who developed motor and vocal tics.Â  After going to many specialists who told me it was anything from stress to tourette's syndrome, I finally (with a lot of prayer) decided I needed to test the theory of PANDAS on her.Â  She got really sick 2 weeks ago with a sore throat and I decided it was the best time to try to get my doc who didn't believe in PANDAS to give meÂ antibiotics for her.Â  Long story short,Â  her tics stopped overnight.Â  She is still on antibiotics and I found a good doc at UCLA who treats PANDAS, who has prescribed her azithromyacin for 6 months.Â  I was just coming to accept the idea that my daughter had tourettes, now the antibiotics have stopped the tics and the emotional lability/intrusive thoughts that came with it.Â  What does everyone think about all this?Â  It seems vaccine injury is the basis of this.Â  Her tics started the day she got her 5 year old shots.

### maggie — 2012-12-13T23:15:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandas.5162/post-67459>

Coldbren

That's awesome that your daughter is showing such great improvement with the antibiotics. What a huge relief for you.Â  I've been really interested in learning about PANDAS myself since I had rheumatic fever following strep as a child, and was diagnosed as an adult with ADHD after many years of problems.

I think you might be interested in this presentation by a doctor at Oregon Health Sciences University -- her son had serious ADHD which was helped enormously by iron supplementation and he then relapsed after a strep throat. She talks about tics, restless legs, and ADHD, and she has a bunch to say about PANDAS towards the end, but I think the whole presentation is fascinating.

http://www.ohsu.edu/edcomm/flash/flash\_player.php?params=2%60/hosp/ohsuclin/gr100510.flv%60vod&width=640&height=480&title=Restless%20Legs,%20Tics,%20%20and%20ADHD:%20Iron%20Deficiency%20to%20Blame

### freshveggies — 2012-12-14T11:54:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandas.5162/post-67497>

Most likely tied to Methylation genetics that are turned on.Â Â  [http://www.autismnti.com/conditionswetreat.html](http://http://www.autismnti.com/conditionswetreat.html)

Â 

Need to get the methylation cycle working to clear metals and virals that can be brought on by vaccines.Â  Just took the vaccines to tip the scale.

### ColdBren — 2012-12-14T20:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandas.5162/post-67553>

Fascinating, thank you!

### freshveggies — 2012-12-15T11:46:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandas.5162/post-67575>

> **ColdBren said**
> Fascinating, thank you!

You are Welcome!Â  Keep us posted.

# Thread: Is it alright to eat something if you're diabetic (type 2) and blood sugar level is extremely low (5
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-alright-to-eat-something-if-youre-diabetic-type-2-and-blood-sugar-level-is-extremely-low-5.5166/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-14T01:58:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-alright-to-eat-something-if-youre-diabetic-type-2-and-blood-sugar-level-is-extremely-low-5.5166/post-67465>

I am currently on Leptin Rx, and I know we're not supposed to eat anything between dinner, and the BAB, but I just checked my blood glucose level, and when fasting, the level is supposed to be between 4 - 7 or 72 - 126.1 in U.S. uniits, and my blood glucose level is 2.8 or 50.5! Which is extremely low, and far below the allowable range.

I know I should eat something...perhaps a couple of pieces of 85% Dark Chocolate (that's the only thing I have that is "approved" but still contains some sugar), but I don't want to ruin, or sabotage my LeptinRx. (Leptin Reset).

I'm doing my best to stay on course, and not stray from the path.

Maybe I should just go to bed, and take my chances that I don't pass out from having too low of a blood glucose level.

### Ray — 2012-12-14T10:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-it-alright-to-eat-something-if-youre-diabetic-type-2-and-blood-sugar-level-is-extremely-low-5.5166/post-67492>

If you are giving yourself insulin, you should consider reducing your dose rather than eating extra to compensate for administering too much.

# Thread: What do diabetics do (type 1 or type 2) if their blood sugar levels fall below 4 or 72 in U.S. units
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-14T02:17:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/post-67467>

Just wondering what any of you who are diabetic (I'm type 2) do if your blood sugar levels fall below 4 or 72 in U.S. units between dinner, and your BAB?

Mine is currently 2.8 or 50.5, and I don't want to sabotage my Leptin Reset, but I also don't want to pass out from having too low of a blood glucose level.

Do you just go to bed and take your chances that you won't pass out, or go into a diabetic coma, and wait until morning to eat?

I'm doing my best to follow the guidelines as best I can, and I don't want to sabotage myself. But I also don't want to sabotage my health either.

### furface — 2012-12-14T02:36:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/post-67468>

Nevermind. I just gave myself one of my three weekly Testosterone-Cypionate injections, then tested my blood glucose level again, and it's up to 3.6 mmol/L or 64.9 mg/dL so I think I'll just go to bed without eating anything.

Hope to see you all in the morning.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-14T08:20:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/post-67479>

Are you taking BG meds?

### furface — 2012-12-14T11:46:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/post-67495>

> **Dali Dula said**
> Are you taking BG meds?

Yeah, after breakfast, and after dinner. I realize that I could reduce them...and when I see my Diabetes Dr. early next month, hopefully he'll see that my A1c levels have dropped enough to lower my meds. But that doesn't solve my current issue.

Anyway, I checked it again before I went to bed, and it had risen to 3.6, so I went to bed. This morning it was 4.6 so everything is fine.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-14T15:03:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/post-67521>

Your current issue is due to your lowering your carb intake while maintaining your med dose, especially if you are doing insulin. I am not a doc, but I stopped all my BG meds when I went paleo and never looked back. Within three months a1c dropped from 7.5 to 6 and continued to drop.I now maintain a 5.4 without meds. You should contact your doc it,s not a good idea to continue those meds at your current dose while doing extreme dietary changes. Low BG is no joke.

### furface — 2012-12-14T16:35:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-diabetics-do-type-1-or-type-2-if-their-blood-sugar-levels-fall-below-4-or-72-in-u-s-units.5167/post-67532>

> **Dali Dula said**
> Your current issue is due to your lowering your carb intake while maintaining your med dose, especially if you are doing insulin. I am not a doc, but I stopped all my BG meds when I went paleo and never looked back. Within three months a1c dropped from 7.5 to 6 and continued to drop.I now maintain a 5.4 without meds. You should contact your doc it,s not a good idea to continue those meds at your current dose while doing extreme dietary changes. Low BG is no joke.

Thanks Dali. First off, congrats onÂ  your exceptional BG levels without meds! That is excellent!

I'm not on insulin, just metformin, glyburide, and some other weird sounding medication. (Too lazy to go check the bottle. LOL) I'm getting my a1c tested next week along with my creatinine, BioAvailable Testosterone, SHBG, and Estradiol. Hopefully my BG level will be low enough that My doc will either lower my meds, or take me off of them. But when I last got it tested, it was a bit high, but that was about 6 weeks ago, and I only began eating Paleo a week ago Monday. But since my last A1c test my before meals BG level has averaged 5.2, while after meals it's been averaging 7.3 of course that's been while taking my usual meds.

However since I began eating Paleo, while still taking my usual meds, my average BG level before meals has been 4.9 or 88.3 in U.S. units, and after meals, it's been 6.3 or 113.5 in U.S. units. Which is a bit lower than the 7-10 range they "allow", but not ultra low.

I will definitely talk to my Dr. about it though. Thanks very much for your input. And congrats again!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Low HDL (33) reading a result of pnemonia, or of leaky gut?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5177/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-15T00:32:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5177/post-67564>

This past July, I got a bad bronchitis infection, that turned into Pneumonia.Â  After 3 trips to the doctor, I finally got diagnosed correctly, as having pneumonia.Â  The following day, I got a metabolic panel done. Â 

Â 

HDL Cholesterol (flagged as Low) 33 \[40-59 MG/DL\]

CHO/HDL Ratio 3.4 \[0-4.4\]

Â \[Edit: 24 yrs. old, 135 lb. at the time. Female\]

None of this meant much to me.Â  I googled HDL, and found it it was the "good cholesterol" and needed to be higher, but that was it...

Â 

Until I found out about Jack Kruse a couple mo. ago, and read that he feels low HDL is a huge indicator of leaky gut syndrome.Â  Which was a huge ligh tbulb moment for me, b/c I have Hashimoto's thyroiditis, and have been wondering if I have leaky gut, and if that's the root cause of my Hashi's. Â 

Â 

So now I'm wondering if that low HDL reading was a direct result of my testing while I had pneumonia and hadn't eaten much in the two weeks prior to taking the blood work (no appetite due to pneumonia and virus), or if having pneumonia wouldn't have affected it at all, and if the low HDL reading is a sign of something bigger (like leaky gut).Â Â 

Â 

This is my first time posting in this forum, not sure if this was the right section to post in, might repost in one of the lab threads, as well.Â  Thanks. /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_smile.gif Â


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Low HDL (33) reading a result of pnemonia, or of leaky gut?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5178/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-12-15T00:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5178/post-67566>

This past July, I got a bad bronchitis infection, that turned into Pneumonia.Â  After 3 trips to the doctor, I finally got diagnosed correctly, as having pneumonia.Â  The following day, I got a metabolic panel done. Â 
Â 

HDL Cholesterol (flagged as Low) 33 \[40-59 MG/DL\]

CHO/HDL Ratio 3.4 \[0-4.4\]

Â \[Edit: Female. 24 yrs. 135 lbs. at the time\]

None of this meant much to me.Â  I googled HDL, and found it it was the "good cholesterol" and needed to be higher, but that was itâ€¦

Â 

Until I found out about Jack Kruse a couple mo. ago, and read that he feels low HDL is a huge indicator of leaky gut syndrome.Â  Which was a huge ligh tbulb moment for me, b/c I have Hashimoto's thyroiditis, and have been wondering if I have leaky gut, and if that's the root cause of my Hashi's. Â 

Â 

So now I'm wondering if that low HDL reading was a direct result of my testing while I had pneumonia and hadn't eaten much in the two weeks prior to taking the blood work (no appetite due to pneumonia and virus), or if having pneumonia wouldn't have affected it at all, and if the low HDL reading is a sign of something bigger (like leaky gut).Â Â 

Â 

This is my first time posting in this forum, not sure if this was the right section to post in, might repost in one of the lab threads, as well.Â  Thanks. /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_smile.gif Â

### nonchalant — 2012-12-16T19:21:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5178/post-67657>

Hi fearless, welcome.Â  Do you eat much animal fat?

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-16T19:30:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5178/post-67658>

> **Nonchalant said**
> Hi fearless, welcome.Â  Do you eat much animal fat?

Hi, thanks for the welcome!Â  At that point, when that lab was taken, I wasn't really going out of my way to, no.Â  Since then, though, I've been frying bacon every morning, ate a 1/2 quart jar of lard (in cooking over a month or so), eating pork cracklings, and eating tons of coconut oil.Â  When the lab was drawn, I hadn't eaten stable meals in the week or so leading up to it, as I was sick, and had no appetite.Â

### nonchalant — 2012-12-16T19:49:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-hdl-33-reading-a-result-of-pnemonia-or-of-leaky-gut.5178/post-67660>

Well, if you weren't eating much animal fat before you got sick, I don't know if I'd worry about the lab result.Â  Eating animal fats usually brings the HDL number up very quickly.Â  Sounds like you are eating a good amount now.Â  I had/have a leaky gut and my HDL zoomed upward when I realized animal fats weren't bad.Â 

If you get retested and the number is still low, then you might need to take extra measures.Â  But realize that everyone has a leaky gut to some extent.Â  I'm glad you have changed your eating habits; I'm sure you have already noticed some health benefits.

# Thread: Help with Thyroid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-15T13:17:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-67581>

I probably wouldn't skip the whole afternoon dose.Â  You may end up paying for it the next day.Â  If this was me I would eliminate one or both of the t3 doses and see how you feel.Â  Your FT3 lab is high but not that bad.Â  Plus it depends on when you took your last dose of either med compared to when you had your blood drawn. You want your FT3 in the top of the range and FT4 mid-high to top of range.Â  FT4 will go up with increasing the Armour but most likely so will your T3.

Â 

It could be the ABs reaction tot he gluten you said you may have ingested contributing as well. I'm wondering (out loud) what your adrenals are doing. Also another thought is that the light changes may be starting to affect you as well. There may be other things going on affecting the thyroid and causing it to react.

Â 

Is your rT3 ratio good?Â  http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-12-15T15:40:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-67588>

> **Patty Cakes said**
> I probably wouldn't skip the whole afternoon dose.Â  You may end up paying for it the next day.Â  If this was me I would eliminate one or both of the t3 doses and see how you feel.Â  Your FT3 lab is high but not that bad.Â  Plus it depends on when you took your last dose of either med compared to when you had your blood drawn. You want your FT3 in the top of the range and FT4 mid-high to top of range.Â  FT4 will go up with increasing the Armour but most likely so will your T3.
>
> Â 
>
> It could be the ABs reaction tot he gluten you said you may have ingested contributing as well. I'm wondering (out loud) what your adrenals are doing. Also another thought is that the light changes may be starting to affect you as well. There may be other things going on affecting the thyroid and causing it to react.
>
> Â 
>
> Is your rT3 ratio good?Â  http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/

Thanks for the reply. Â 

Â 

I take my blood/thyroid tests at the same time of day so I can accurately track them as much as possible. While T3/FT3 aren't the highest ever, they are really high for me. Â Esp considering that i wasnt able to get it up for so long. Â 

Â 

i'll probably just cut the aternoon dose of cytomel (wouldnt want to cut the armour as my T4 isnt really that great). this is very frustrating

Â 

your comment on the light is true. Â I have noticed i never feel as well during the winter and wonder if this is a connection. Â i do use a blue light though.Â 

Â 

according to that my ratio is good. Â sigh Â now i'm really confused

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-12-15T15:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-67589>

i would like to know what my adrenals are up to as well but as I am on a low dose of HC i believe that would mess with the cortisol testing i would normally do

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-03T20:10:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-69232>

Hello,

I reduced the afternoon cytomel...waited a week felt no difference (still had similar symptons etc) so upon Drs advice, took off the morning dose of Cytomel as well. Now I am just taking 1 grain of armour in the am and half grain at noon.

Its been a few weeks...how long should I wait before having labs drawn again? I'm curious what is going on, bc at night I've been extremely hot (my core especially). i am curious about my levels at this point.

How soon can I get my labs redone? (T3 would have been the only change I imagaine and as I understand it, cytomel has a shorter half life than T4 meds).

Thanks for any suggetions!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-03T21:46:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-69244>

It only has a 12 hour half life so should be out of your system within a day or two at most. Since you only eliminated the T3 I think your thyroid labs would be fairly true at this time. IMHO

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-04T18:10:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-69362>

Thanks! Thats what I'm hoping...

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-12T23:39:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-thyroid.5179/post-70465>

If you are doing what Jack suggests - leptin reset, CT Rx, epipaleo, etc then you can expect to have changes occur.

I've just taken myself completely off my Ecothyoid (supposed to be equivalent to Armour, but I found it MUCH better). Anyway I started getting lots of symptoms of what I thought were low thyoid and wondered why when I thought I was doing everything right. Anyway someone on this forum indicated that it was possible that these symptoms were also due to too much supplemental thyroid. So I oh so cautiously stopped taking my thyroid. And my goodness, a week later and the only symptom I had was thickened skin on my heels. I hadn't got terribly cold in the late afternoon. I still had energy. Its been more than three weeks now and the skin on my heels is starting to improve, not quite back to where it was when I was on the thyroid but not cracking any more. So I think that the move to epipaleo has meant that I don't need my thyroid supplement any more. I'm aiming to get some labs drawn soon so it will be interesting to find out what they say.

# Thread: Is  (DBP)=Gc-MAF  ??

### JanSz — 2012-12-15T18:18:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-dbp-gc-maf.5182/post-67600>

Is (DBP)=Gc-MAF ??

\=========================================
http://www.jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/
11\. Humans have a Vitamin D savings bank in our body. It is supported by a good protein diet and a leads to a better Vitamin D level. **An Epi-paleo Rx is an optimal choice for this bank account. It works by making a protein called Vitamin D binding protein (DBP)

\=============================
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gc-MAF
Gc-MAF or Gc protein-derived macrophage activating factor is a functional change in the Gc protein (Vitamin D-binding protein) caused by serial deglycosylation. Gc-MAF is extremely potent and will at very low concentrations activate, regulate and expand macrophages. A Macrophage Activating Factor (MAF) is a lymphokine that primes macrophages to become cytotoxic to tumors. It also controls the expression of Ia antigens on the macrophage cell surface.\[1\]
\=============================

Â 

http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1046/j.1525-1373.1999.d01-3.x/abstract;jsessionid=BA71647FB01CDB06D284F831B1A0A8EF.d04t04

Antitumor Effect of Vitamin D-Binding Protein-Derived Macrophage Activating Factor on Ehrlich Ascites Tumor-Bearing Mice

Â 

\==============================

http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?forums/gcmaf.10/

GcMAF**

A place to discuss treatment, post questions, provide information related to GcMAF.

I think I saw Dr Kruse mention Vit D binding protein is concentrated in fish skin? If it is, IÂ think it would need a biological conversion (via gut bacteria presumably?) into macrophage activating factor.
DBP is also present in breastmilk - and the required bacteria for the conversion also come as part of the package...offering an immune advantage (one of many)Â to a breastfed newborn.

Macrophage activating factor can also be bought as a probiotic starter culture for use with kefir or raw milk colostrum. The two types I'm familiar withÂ ato know their n=1re marketed as MAF 314 and MAF 838. They are more complicated to make than regular yogurt.

I took GcMAF for 30 weeks. I had been going to try the probioticÂ MAF after I'd finished my course of injectible GcMAF, but I'm confidentÂ for now that I'm continuing to make progressÂ using anÂ epi paleo diet though I'm unclear on eactly if/how this provides me with macrophage activating factor? ### caroline — 2012-12-17T01:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beautiful-thoughts.5192/post-67672>

I saw these lovely thoughts to live by today - they were in the window of a tiny flower shop in a small village close to where I live and I wanted to share them with everyone here.

LEARN from Yesterday
LIVE for today
HOPE for tomorrow
......................
ESSENTIALS FOR LIFE
Someone to love
Something to do
Something to look forward too
.......................
IF
you can imagine it

YOU
can create it

IF YOU
dream it

YOU .......CAN BECOME IT

Merry Christmas to all the Star Fish Here
and .... of course to
Dr. Kruse and his family

It has been an amazing year and a world class adventure!
x

My doctor mentioned trying to raise my test naturally then use medical grade test if unsuccessful. The worry I have with using test is shunting my bodies natural ability to produce. Due to DHEA being low, would you recommend possibly using DHEA? I only can get it through prescription here in Australia. Sorry, hcb=haemoglobulin? Hct=haematocrit? What is the purpose of a phlebotomy?

### [email redacted] — 2012-12-17T20:37:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-testosterone-elevated-igg-b6-through-the-roof-list-goes-on.5183/post-67758>

Would anyone happen to know functional level ranges (blood panel) for my deficiencies? Even better, would anyone happen to have functional levels for most/all blood panels? Thanks,

# Thread: Does anyone here eat swordfish on a regular basis?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 17

### furface — 2012-12-17T16:04:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67726>

Just curious as to whether or not anyone here eats swordfish on a regular basis as part of their Paleo/Epi-Paleo diet?

Â 

I'm planning on buying some this week, as I've always wanted to try it, and was interested in what those of you who have tried it think of it?

I'm also planning on buying some halibut, sablefish, salmon, tilapia, basa, clams, dungeness crab, lobster, prawns, snapper etc.

### Inger — 2012-12-17T16:21:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67728>

Swordfish? I eat it sometimes but very seldom. It is very expensive here and not often to get. It is not my favorit fish even if good.. kinda dry? I love halibut. My favorit. I guess halibut, mackerel and salmon are the ones I maybe like the best when talking fish. Halibut, raw, with some limejuice and fresh herbs on it is delicious! I had it often in Germany. Here I get it almost.. never. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. If you can get it, wow! Absolutely mild and fatty and super tasty!

### caroline — 2012-12-17T16:46:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67731>

I used to eat swordfish all the time in the Bahamas ....it was my very favorite .....but I am pretty sure it would have been caught on the day. Â It was pretty simply served Â ... Bahamian style with peas and rice. Â I have the most amazing memories of the Bahamas Â - brings tears to my eyes.Â

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-17T17:14:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67740>

Swordfish is very good. Great for the grill. I don't eat big fish very often due to mercury concerns. I figure when eating a mostly seafood diet it's best to eat the big stuff sparingly. The bigger the fish , the higher the mercury content. I try to limit swordfish, tuna, striped bass to once a month.

### furface — 2012-12-17T21:26:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67763>

Thanks everyone.

I just keep hearing a lot of bodybuilders eat swordfish, and have always wanted to try it. I found a place here in Vancouver, on Granville Island that sells frozen swordfish steaks @ 19.98 lb. So I may not make it a regular part of my diet, but to try it once, what the heck.

Â 

I love halibut! Living in Vancouver, we get a lot of fresh halibut since it comes from the Pacific Ocean up around northern B.C./Alaska. So deliicious.

Â 

That's awesome, Caroline. I'll bet it brings back a lot of wonderful memories of the Bahamas!

Question about tuna...does it taste ANYTHING like the canned stuff? I sure hope not! Not that I don't like the canned stuff, but I find it way too dry unless you add a whole bunch of mayonnaise. (I'm talking about eating it cooked, not raw. LOL)

Thanks.

### endless — 2012-12-17T21:45:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67766>

I suggest you eat it raw....cooked it IS pretty dry. Try it in a restaurant first...seared ahi is available in lots of places in Van. Its mostly raw, and very tasty!

My idea of an ideal dinner would be pigging out on oysters at Joe Fortes!

### furface — 2012-12-17T22:19:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67771>

Thanks Endless.

Not quite ready to eat fish or meat Raw. Never was a big fan of sushi. So maybe I'll hold off on the tuna for now. lol Lucky for us in B.C. that we have a wide variety of fish to choose from.

Have to get used to eating oysters. I've only had them twice...once breaded and fried, which is an Epi-Paleo no no, and the other time a few weeks ago when I had a can of smoked oysters, and IMHO they tasted like dirt. But I will give fresh, live ones another shot.

### caroline — 2012-12-18T03:25:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67789>

My Gosh - you are a stubborn man! Â I am going to try one more time!! Â When we speak of raw fish ... I think you kinda have the wrong idea. Â We don't just grab a whole fish and start munching ...no, no, no!

The lomi lomi salmon that you loved \[you said so\] is basically what we are talking about. Â Have you heard of scallop ceviche? Â same thing. Steak Tartare - same thing. Â  They are more or less cooked with lemon or lime juice or balsamic vinegar Â \[something acidic\]. Â If you took a piece of your fresh halibut and squeezed on some lime juice and added green onions, herbs etc. etc. and you watched it Â .. you would see it change to more opaque - or "cooked "as they say in the trade! Â  just like your Lomi Lomi salmon. Â  Â You have so many wonderful restaurants in Vancouver so - like endless said - try some of this stuff when it has been prepared for you. Â You are such a guy!!

Â  Â  Don't make e come over there and take you to dinner!

### furface — 2012-12-18T10:00:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67803>

Hey, I never said never lol. But I figure if I do things on my terms, at my speed, I'm more bound to eventually actually DO them...maybe, then if I'm basically forced to do it, or if someone TELLS me to do it, which is something I've always hated, haha, and in turn may make me say "screw it, I won't do it at all then!" lol. So as I said before, if I do 80-90% of every aspect, it's better than doing 50-60% or less, or none at all.

I've always been one who HATES being told WHAT TO DO, esp if it's told almost as if given an ORDER! I think I'm doing pretty good as I am, and this is just the start. But we'll see. I may change my mind down the road, but if not, I'm cool with that.

### NittDan — 2012-12-18T11:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67814>

### furface — 2012-12-18T13:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67817>

Great graph! Thanks NittDan!Â 

Looks like swordfish has equal amounts of selenium and mercury, so while it's not the best fish to eat, it won't kill me either. Not that I'm going to eat it 5 times a week. I might not even like it, but as I said, I've always wanted to try it, so I will, and take it from there. Hey whatever doesn't kill me can only make me stronger! Right?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-18T14:20:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-here-eat-swordfish-on-a-regular-basis.5196/post-67823>

He works at one of the very high end dining establishments down in So California and he said he will never buy it or serve it. It grossed me out and I just don't eat it any more.Â  Never had an affinity for it anyway.Â

### furface — 2012-12-18T14:57:04+0200

# Thread: Something a little sad
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2012-12-17T16:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67734>

I got an e-Christmas card the other day from my medical practice and then .....the next day.....
there is another email cancelling the Christmas wishes!

I guess it isn't politically correct to wish someone Merry Christmas any more. That is a big deal in the U.S. I think??

### diane — 2012-12-17T18:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67751>

It seems to be circling back a little bit here - at least the Facebook sentiment is LOL. Â Although most companies will use Happy Holidays to be politically correct.

I read a story a couple of years ago in the Toronto Star by a Muslim woman who supported Merry Christmas and her point was it didn't offend anyone she knew. No one else changes their holidays to be politically correct, so her point was why should Christians?Â 

Â 

I love helping other celebrate their holidays - I always learn something about other cultures!Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-17T19:11:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67754>

Not everyone celebrates Christmas, that's why. Â And it's not "political" correctness. Â Wishing someone happy holidays covers all the bases ... Christmas, Kwanza, Hannukah, Saturnalia. Â I don't know why people freak out about this so much. Â If it's a personal friend whom I know to be Christian I wish them Merry Christmas. Â Businesses who don't know their clients faiths (or lack thereof) play it safe by using Happy Holidays. Â WHY is this an issue?

Short answer it isn't. Â Unless you're looking to start a fight.

Having said all that:

Â 

"Happy whatever does not offend you."

### PrimalPam — 2012-12-17T22:16:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67770>

I think it actually is sad that we've become so politically correct we can't wish someone a Merry Christmas.Â  I happen to be Jewish, and I say Merry Christmas all the time.Â  And when someone says it to me, I say "Merry Christmas to you too".Â  I'm not sure I get what the big deal is.

Â 

When I was little, I remember saying to my dad, "But dad, we're Jewish, why did you say Merry Christmas back to that man?" and my dad would say, "Because he was saying something nice, and we'll say something nice back.Â  There's no reason to go any further when you are exchanging nice greetings".Â  So I learned from that, and all these years later, we've become so overly politically correct it's crazy.

Â 

If a Christian person was wandering in a heavily Jewish part of a city and someone wished them a Happy Hannukah, do you think they'd be offended?Â  Or pleased that someone took the time to wish a nice greeting?Â  I prefer to think the latter.

### diane — 2012-12-17T23:01:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67774>

Totally agree Pam. I think the issue is that people feel guilty or that there is something wrong saying "Merry Christmas". If they choose "happy holidays" no problem. It's just that "Merry Christmas" or "Happy Hanukkah" shouldn't be a problem, no matter the beliefs.

Â 

Heck, when I first moved to Toronto I learned how to say "kung hoy fat choy" (which I probably just butchered the spelling LOL). It doesn't matter that I may not celebrate it, but I wish that everyone be happy and merry. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Rachelle — 2012-12-18T16:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/something-a-little-sad.5198/post-67845>

> **PrimalPam said**
> I think it actually is sad that we've become so politically correct we can't wish someone a Merry Christmas.Â  I happen to be Jewish, and I say Merry Christmas all the time.Â  And when someone says it to me, I say "Merry Christmas to you too".Â  I'm not sure I get what the big deal is.
>
> Â 
>
> When I was little, I remember saying to my dad, "But dad, we're Jewish, why did you say Merry Christmas back to that man?" and my dad would say, "Because he was saying something nice, and we'll say something nice back.Â  There's no reason to go any further when you are exchanging nice greetings".Â  So I learned from that, and all these years later, we've become so overly politically correct it's crazy.
>
> Â 
>
> If a Christian person was wandering in a heavily Jewish part of a city and someone wished them a Happy Hannukah, do you think they'd be offended?Â  Or pleased that someone took the time to wish a nice greeting?Â  I prefer to think the latter.

Your father was a wise and considerate gentleman. I don't celebrate Christmas and still smile and aknowledge what they say with thank you and same to you. After all it's coming from a good place, so why not.

I have been struggling getting the appropriate labs done in Australia. It occurred to me yesterday - better late than never -
That it would be so much better to have all the necessary labs done at around the same time so you have a definite bench mark to go from - especially with a doc who is learning along with you. Now we have some crazy numbers and don't really know what is affecting what - it is such a hormonal dance.

# Thread: I think I'm getting adapted to eating Epi-Paleo after less than 3 weeks!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-17T21:51:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/post-67767>

I've been on the Leptin Rx since Dec. 3rd, and met my dad for lunch today at a local supermarket which has a built in cafe. So before I was eating Paleo, we'd have potato wedges, and chicken wings. Which is what my dad had. I could have "cheated" since it would have been the first time since I began Leptin Rx, but NO! I'm determined to stay on course.

So I had some fresh B.C. dungeness crab meat, and a couple of squares of 90 % Dark Chocolate. And a glass of ice water. And I was full! This was 6 hours after my BAB. I actually had no craving for the wedges or wings.

But damn was the crab expensive! $89.90 a KG!! Nearly $30.00 lb.

Had a crustaceous dinner as well...fresh steamed Atlantic Lobster (The whole thing) $8.98 lb. dipped in homemade garlic butter! That's it! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### caroline — 2012-12-18T03:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/post-67790>

just a thought .... Dr' K says if you fall off the wagon you set yourself back about 2 weeks or more and you should kick yourself in the ass! I think I am quoting that properly!

### Rachelle — 2012-12-18T16:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-getting-adapted-to-eating-epi-paleo-after-less-than-3-weeks.5201/post-67843>

Getting healthy is really enjoyable isn't it Furface?

Â 

And yeah, talk about expensive. I ordered just 1 lb of Alaskan Red King Crab for $40 a lb and the rest will be Alaskan Blue Crab for $29 a lb.Â 

**He sent us this warning**. "This is 100% U.S. Caught and Processed King Crab. It is not Russian King Crab which floods the market and generally costs much less (inÂ many cases, Russian crab is sold as Alaskan crab). The problem with Russian crab is that much of their population is in decline and theyÂ don't have the means to enforce fishing quotas. Purchasing Russian Crab here in the U.S. drives the poaching and over fishing in theÂ Western Bering Sea. For what it is worth, much of the Russian crab is processed in China. Alaska monitors crab populations very closelyÂ and sets tight regulations on the catches to prevent over fishing.There is also King Crab caught in the Barents Sea (not to be confused with the Bering Sea). The crab there is not native, and may beÂ considered a pest. However, there is nothing to say that threatened Russian Bering Sea King Crab is not being labeled as Barents Sea KingÂ Crab.

Â 

Sometimes it's better to be uninformed, maybe? However, since I am informed, he will be my only trust worthy source when it comes to purchasing some seafoods.

Â 

It's nice reading the progress you are making in such a short time. Same happened to me when I started to eat, mainly, fish.

# Thread: CT Success how long?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-12-18T02:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67782>

I'm having a hard time getting myself in the pool....its not necessarily the cold...its the time too...I'm worried I'll do all this and it won't do a thing.

Â 

I started Iodine with cofactors and Selenium etc.... and haven't noticed a big change.Â  I really thought my energy issue was a Thyroid one and still nothing.Â  Its driving me crazy thinking that this is another big letdown waiting to happen.Â  I suppose its possible something is improving with the added Iodine...but I'm certainly not noticing much on the positive side...

Â 

One of the possible "detox" effects was twitching eye....which I have sometimes...and it did seem to start happening more so than normal after I started the Iodine...but that's not a positive.Â  That is a negative...its not really annoying but its not a good thing either.

Â 

So tired of trying to figure this out...so much work and everything seems to be going no where.Â  Maybe its my failure to keep my diet going.Â  I keep falling off the wagon eating junk and probably haven't even been in ketosis or even in line with standard Paleo for two weeks in a row....I'm not the motivated sort.Â  This is hard.Â  So tired....

Â 

Thanks for any thoughts or ideas.Â  What exactly is eye twitching caused by?Â  Anyone know?Â  I think it had something to do with a possible detox side effect but it doesn't really hurt or anything...not sure if I'd call it a negative its more of a neutral.Â  Its a strange sensation.

### caroline — 2012-12-18T03:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67786>

Sorry Raiken that you are struggling. Â I don't know what help I can be but I will give it a shot. Â Have you had an email consult with Dr. Kruse?

You didn't say if you have all your labs and then if you can swing it Â - do a skype consult. Â You need to get a plan of action Â - chart a course to get to your destination.

You really need to do the leptin RX and stick with it Â - it really isn't going to work if you don't stick with it and give it 100% .... especially do epipaleo. Â That is so important. Â Paying attention to the light cycles is probably the most important Â - also C/T. Â  It all ties together perfectly and you just have to dive in and go for it if you want to see results. Â It really isn't that hard once you get going Â - you will start feeling tons better and there are plenty of really smart people here to help you. Â Maybe that seems a little simplistic but it really is if you want to reach your goals. Â Now - don't be fooled ......it could take time - but as Dr. K. has said many times - you didn't get broken overnite Â so you can't expect to get fixed overnite. Â You do have to put in the hard yards.Â 

Maybe re read the leptin R/X and the C/T blogs Â - there are easy start guides to help you. Â We have all been there and still trying to sort thru all of it and understand all the blogs. Â You really do have to give it your all Â - I promise - it will be worth it.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-12-18T10:52:32+0200

### furface — 2012-12-18T15:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67840>

As both Inger, and Caroline have said, Raiken...just take it one step at a time. Baby steps. Start slowly, and before you know it, you will build up so much momentum. Not to speed the results, but progressing along the road seems to follow naturally.

When I started my Leptin Rx on Dec 3rd, I weighed 360 lbs, had no energy, was always tired, and also loved junk food etc. I also had a freezer full of not totally Epi-Paleo approved food. Some Grass-Fed lean ground beef patties (Well that was E-PA), and some salmon and tilapia filets, but also quite a few ground turkey burgers. (I'd just gotten paid a week or so before and stocked up on what I thought was "healthy" meats.)

Anyway, I didn't want to throw that away since I'm on a fixed income, so I incorporated it into an semi Epi-Paleo diet I guess you could call it.

I did the BAB, bought plenty of bacon, some coconut oil, had some free run eggs...which some may like, some may not, but they help bring up the 50-75 grams of protein required for the BAB pretty fast. Bought some broccoli, asparagus, mushrooms, and my fave, baby bok choy.

I cut out all snacks between meals, started taking 5,000 i.u. of Vitamin D3 per day, 3 gr of Vitamin C, some B12, and B complex vitamins.

I also began doing face dunks in ice water a week ago today, and having Cold Water showers a week ago Sunday.

When I started the face dunks, I couldn't hold my breath longer than 28 seconds. This morning, only a week later I did two dunks of 1 minute each.

Same with the showers. My first one lasted 15 minutes. Last night it was 40.

It's surprising what the body can really do when motivated, and when you feel great!

I've also found that after only a week and a half of eating the BAB, my appetite has really decreased. I no longer crave snacks, and at times I don't even want to eat lunch.

The reason I'm saying all of this is because while the first week or so may be hard to adjust to, please do not give up, and give it another try, and stick to it. It truly does become easier, and all the baby steps put together actually make it easier to continue. You will feel so much better! You will sleep much better! CT is so refreshing, invigorating, and energizing, it's like you're "high" without the drugs (or so I assume), or crash.

Also once you start to see some results...either increased energy levels, more vigor, less appetite, better sleep, etc, it will motivate you to keep going, and increase your capacity and desire to add more to the whole LeptinRx/CT/Epi-Paleo recipe!

I weighed myself this morning, and currently weigh 236 lbs so in 15 days, I've lost 24 lbs. and it hasn't felt as if I've been deprived of anything!

Of course we are all different, but I'm just saying, as Caroline, and Inger have, please do not give up. Put forth the effort, and you will be thankful that you did!

Best of luck! We're all here to help!

I've learned so much from everyone here, and they have all been so helpful, and supportive!

### caroline — 2012-12-18T15:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67841>

I kind of think you are hanging too much on C/T Â - Â It is important but like Inger says baby steps! Â You have got to get your diet fixed pronto. Â You can get cans of salmon and sardines and liver and heart. Â These things are much cheaper.

The light cycles are very, very important. Â Are you on your computer a lot? Â TV a lot? Â You absolutely have to block out the blue lights.

You will notice a huge improvement just by tightening up your diet and face dunks and paying attention to light. Â Don't worry about testing right now.

This is your life ... your path to follow ... you need to really want change for yourself. Â You are only accountable for yourself Â - Â You need to help yourself first and not worry about the others in your household. Â You can set the example - be the benchmark for change.

So take one day at a time Â - have you thought of starting a journal? Â

### caroline — 2012-12-18T17:11:57+0200

see .... I think that is the thing ....I don't think of it as cheating anymore. I choose to do what I want for my health and well being. If I wanted ....I could have an ice cream cone. Maybe I will treat myself to one over the holidays but then again I might not. The other day I pigged out and do you know what it was on????? I had THREE APPLES.
We have the most gorgeous fruit this year. The cherries are incredible and the peaches and the mangos. I think that is what I am going to have - all these gorgeous fruits and some heavy whipping cream. I can hardly wait! what a change from half a cheesecake!

### furface — 2012-12-18T17:25:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-success-how-long.5202/post-67863>

That's awesome, Caroline! I wish fruits were in season here as well. But then again since I still have a lot of weight to lose, maybe it's a good thing that they're not. haha

You are so right about the "cheating". Once you get healthy, having an ice cream cone, or even a slice of pizza...if you even desire it, won't feel like "cheating" but rather maybe a "reward" or even just something different or "new" even though it's old, to try. But it most likely won't even be something we really desire. Not that a person still won't find enjoyment in it, because in all honesty they still taste great, but it's not like it's a MUST HAVE, and we'd feel deprived if we don't have it. It's a take it or leave it thing I think, and we most likely wouldn't even feel guilty if we did. (at least we shouldn't IMHO)

Hey, heavy whipping cream, a sprinkle of stevia, and you have an awesome, and sweet treat for desert, and a great topping for fruit. Or just straight out of the bowl. haha

Enjoy!

# Thread: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome  Inflammation IL-6  mAChR

### Thor — 2012-12-18T03:06:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-fatigue-syndrome-inflammation-il-6-machr.5203/post-67787>

.

I haven't seen anything on here talking about Chronic Fatigue Syndrome & it's role with inflammation.Â  The more I read - the more I'm floored how bad this gets.Â  My CFS is even more fuel to the "fire" /wp-includes/images/smilies/icon\_sad.gif Â Â  ugh...Â Â Â Â  Looks like the fall out of long term inflammation can be the fire that starts CFS.

Â 

This article contains lots of "_geek_" to show it's role:

Â 

Â [http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0051515](http://doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0051515")

Â 

**Reduction of \[\[sup\]11\[/sup\]C\](+)3-MPB Binding in Brain of Chronic Fatigue Syndrome with

Â Â  Serum Autoantibody against Muscarinic Cholinergic Receptor**

Â 

Chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) is a heterogeneous disorder characterized by persistent fatigue accompanied by rheumatologic, cognitive, and infectious-appearing symptoms. CFS research showed abnormal cytokine levels including tumour necrosis factor, interleukin-1, interleukin-6, increased markers of inflammation and stressful life events prior to CFS onset

.

Interesting look into WAT and how it plays a role into metabolic syndrome - looking at the Nrf2 activity....

Â 

Nuclear factor E2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) is a transcription factor that functions as a master regulator of the cellular adaptiveÂ Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â  response to oxidative stress.Â  Nrf2 plays a critical role in adipogenesis by regulating expression of CCAAT/enhancer-binding protein Î² and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor Î³.

Â 

[http://diabetes.diabetesjournals.org/content/early/2012/12/11/db12-0584.abstract](http://http://diabetes.diabetesjournals.org/content/early/2012/12/11/db12-0584.abstract)

I would do leptin Rx, the adrenal Rx (the latest) and work on the leaky gut.Â  A consult with Jack would be a great start.Â  I can offer you lots of advice but that is based on my own N=1.Â  Jack has made it very clear that everything is in context.Â  Even some of the things in the blogs might not be for you.Â  Do you have labs confirming what you think you are dealing with?Â  If so, great!Â  Otherwise, time to get those and then figure out why you are in the state you are in.Â  Sometimes we think "this" is the reason why and it ends up to be something completely different.Â  I am becomingÂ a lab queen but I am also finding a lot of things out.Â  I am dealing with everything you are and now more; I wouldn't have had a clue had I not done all of those tests.Â  It might cost you out of pocket but if you truly want to get to optimal, testing is theÂ best way to really get you there.

I would put a lot of effort into fixing the gut no matter what since everything stems from the gut!Â  You can do all 3 though pretty easily.Â  You can eat the BAB, get the morning sun in your eyes, wear blue blockers at night and eat a lot of bone broth.Â  This will work on all 3 areas and it is pretty simple to implement.Â  I kinda freak now in the evening with too much light.Â  Gotta wear them glasses. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â You can even just wear sunglasses instead of blue blockersÂ in the evening to get you going in the right direction.

I do eat ghee and I make it myself.Â  The president of our company just happens to be a chef and he taught me how.Â  He said to cook it until all the milk particles sink to the bottom.Â  I do this on very low heat and I do scrape off the top too in the mean time.Â  I have a dairy AI issue and can't eat it either.Â  I don't seem to have any problem with what I make.Â  There are also places to buy it and it is supposed to be virtually free.Â  Not saying you should, just that you might be able to.Â  You can also make your own and strain it thru cheesecloth or something.Â  Duck fat is very expensive, at least where I have bought it.Â 

If you have adrenal fatigue you are not really supposed to exercise, as well as until you are reset.Â  But I really don't know where you stand so it is hard to say.Â  You will use up your stem cells if you train too hard.Â  If it wears you out, then you are over training.Â I stillÂ have a little adrenal fatigue. Â You could walk or keep your training on theÂ mild side.Â Â Hard to say when I don't know howÂ hard you push.Â 

Jack talks a lot about sprints and lifting weights when seeing signs of LS.Â  No cardio.Â  I don't have weight to lose either and I have been doing this for 1.5 years.Â  I am hoping I can finally start exercising after I have my consult.Â  I know it use to make me feel better too.Â  You sound like you really need a consult.Â  Even a half hour to ask all your questions which are really good ones.

# Thread: Question for the Thyroid Experts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-12-18T10:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67804>

Ok wondering about a few things...

on Friday I got my updated thyroid meds - and added 30mg of compounded Armour in the afternoon. I'm taking 60mg in the am - around 530 am

I feel great in the afternoon now - no slump... but now in the AM I feel like I'm dragging...

wondering if adding the afternoon dose is causing the am fatigue? or if its just remnants from having the flu last week. I just feel like I'm not quite awake...just general sluggishness and I feel like I could take a nap, at least from my body's perspective - though my brain feels sharp.

any thoughts on this???

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-18T14:17:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67822>

I would venture a guess that it's leftover from the flu as your body is still trying to regenerate. I would continue on for at least a week or two and see how you feel then. I haven't heard of anyone having issues with adding that second dose.Â  You are kinda the exception though!!!!Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Also watch the times you take it.Â  If you don't start to feel better than maybe you need to change the dosing time in the afternoon.Â  Watch how tired you get in the evening as maybe you need to take it later.Â  I found that 2pm was good for me even though I got tired in the evening.Â  But I couldn't go until 3pm or I was wasted in the afternoon.Â  This could affect your morning feeling a little because of the T4 that's in there still converting to T3 (hopefully converting).

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-18T14:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67829>

Thanks Patty.

the 1st 2 times I took it... at 4pm or later... and discovered I had a hard time falling asleep - so I moved it fwd btwn 2pm - 3pm.

so I know I'm definitely sensitive to it...

going to grab lunch here in a few...then take my armour closer to 3pm and see how things go tonight...

I'm inclined to think left over flu - but not 100% sure...

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-19T09:25:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67938>

I'm interested in your experiment with timing too...I've been taking 60 mg Armour in the am and adding another dose in the afternoon an hour or so after lunch. To complicate things I'm also trying to replace the Armour with Thyro-Gold. (Long story, but basically I haven't been able to get the prescription I want) Â So far things are ok, I an now taking 30mgArmour and I empty the thryro-gold out of two capsules. I can really feel that working...in the afternoon I'm taking two more Thyro-Gold.

I am finding that I want to go to sleep earlier...but that's ok at this time of year...Solstice on Saturday! Â I'm curious to see how I'll react to the light changing....first time in many years that I've had any energy during the lead up to Christmas, I know supporting my thyroid is a big part of this difference.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-19T10:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-thyroid-experts.5208/post-67941>

I took my 30 mg of armour around 3pm yesterday. Â got home after work and hopped in to the Â CT Tank for 30mins... I was passed out asleep by 9pm (I lay down with the kids when they go to bed) Â  woke up at 1030... got ready for bed and was back out by 11. Â woke up refreshed when my alarm went off this am.Â 

Â 

going to do the same thing tonight and throw in a BRTV/Daily HIIT workout while grilling pork chops for dinner...Â 

Â 

We'll see how things go tonight....Â

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iodine-and-hyaluronic-acid.5215/post-67871>

I was reading that a substance called Haplex increases absorption of oral Hyaluronic acid.Â  Haplex is a sonicated combination of HA (hyaluronic acid) and phospholipids.Â  If iodine is absorbed via the selective power of leaky gut I wondered if combining iodine with liquid HA would increase bioavailability of HA.Â  Any thoughts?

Â 

I don't have a sonic machine so I was brainstorming alternate ways of absorbing HA.

Â 

Anyone else tingling too?

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-19T09:07:31+0200

I'm not in for very long but the water is in the high thirties/low forties, so I'm going for cold over time and an ongoing subtle tingling lasts long afterwards. I really like that feeling. Being outside in spring water really seems to help with the whole experience too.Â

# Thread: Has anyone ever thought to do some sort of pledge drive for charity doing CT during a "polar bear" s
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-ever-thought-to-do-some-sort-of-pledge-drive-for-charity-doing-ct-during-a-polar-bear-s.5219/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2012-12-19T05:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-ever-thought-to-do-some-sort-of-pledge-drive-for-charity-doing-ct-during-a-polar-bear-s.5219/post-67924>

I was thinking that it might be a great way to raise money for a charity, esp one that isn't doesn't already have people donating up the wazoo, like Cancer does. (I myself think that they do have a cure, but they'll never release it because they'd stop getting all the donations in, and the economy would just plummet worse than the depression.)

Something like Multiple Sclerosis, or Cystic Fibrosis, or even Diabetes Research etc.

What someone who is extremely good at lengthy CT sessions could do, is take pledges for each minute they stay in the water of a "polar bear" swim. If someone pledges $5 per minute, and the person, like for instance, Dr. Kruse stays in for one hour, that's $300 from that one pledge alone. If 100 people were to sign up for the same amount, that would be $30,000! etc.

I just thought it might be a novel idea since this is the time of year...esp New Year's Day, that a lot of places tend to have Polar Bear swims.

I think it would look so comical too...in a good way. You always see news footage of people running into the cold water, and quickly running back out, but then if we'd see someone who is adapted to CT, just non chalantly stroll out into the water as if it's 80Â°, and begin to frolic in the water, would be hilarious! And all for a good cause.

Just a thought.

That's how the polar plunges are organized in Vermont...the Special Olympics gets "plungers" to raise pledge money. I think they only expect a brief dip...I like your idea about endurance=more money! Â I'm going to do this in January for the Special Olympics...they have three events you can raise money for....

### Lyndra — 2012-12-19T13:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/has-anyone-ever-thought-to-do-some-sort-of-pledge-drive-for-charity-doing-ct-during-a-polar-bear-s.5219/post-67962>

I used to do that here on January 1 as part of the polar bear plunge. A few years ago, the event was taken over by a non-profit that does not allow anyone to use the event to raise funds for another cause.Â 

Â 

At the event here, they do not let people stay in for very long. First, there are 400+ people waiting to get their turn and only 5-10 jump at once so it takes quite a while to get through everybody. Second, they don't want the liability that someone could get hurt by staying in too long.

Â 

One benefit of the organized event is they have trained divers in the water ready to rescue anyone who has trouble. If you do it on your own, be aware of your limits and don't get trapped under the ice (that's my biggest fear). And have fun!Â

# Thread: Started BHRT Today....Here we go...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-bhrt-today-here-we-go.5224/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2012-12-19T11:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/started-bhrt-today-here-we-go.5224/post-67952>

Got my prescriptions and started BHRT last night with my new doc.Â 

Â 

Started with 50,000 IU of Vitamin D3 1x per week for the next 15 weeks.

Topical Estrodiol and Estriol - once in the morning and once at night

100mg Progesterone capsule 1x at bedtime

Â 

For the next few days I am overlapping my BC pills with this regimen per my docs recommendation.Â  Then I will stop BC pills completely.

Multi-Vitamin pack for 2x per day is ordered and on the way

Pro-biotic is ordered and on the way

Â 

Had a few more labs drawn yesterday, including Zinc and Magnesium.

Â 

I am on Day 3 of a constant migraine.Â  I am looking forward to some relief in the next few weeks.Â  The headache is starting in the back of my neck behind my ears at the trigger point - which actually hurts to the touch, as do the muscles in my shoulders.Â  Acupuncture session is scheduled for this afternoon to try to help with that.

Â 

Progesterone is at night for drowsiness, but I actually had insomnia last night - more likely than not it's cause is anxiety from starting a new treatment plan.

I post my progress.

Â 

Karen from Arizona
