# Thread: Grounding/Earthing discussion with Stephen Sinatra - video
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freesia — 2013-01-01T13:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-68912>

\[video=youtube\_share;11PC8AFrh5w\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=11PC8AFrh5w&\[/video\]

(I hope the link works.)

I think if you're following where Dr Kruse is going with Mitochondria...electrons...ATP you might enjoy seeing some overlap in thinking from another perspective. I personally found this VERY interesting. My husband is sceptical you can earth effectively on an airplane(!) but he's got plenty of time this week to ponder on it while he's building earthing plugs so we can try sleeping earthed. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Earthing is something that's been nibbling around the edges of my consciousness for a few years, since I've seen some people with my illness (which I'm beginning to believe has a lot to do with mitochondria) improve when they incorporate earthing.

I live in an older house (1911) and though the bathroom isn't quite \*that\* old, it's relatively low tech. By design or accident (?) the bath taps are earthed, so we've just clipped an earthing strap to the cold tap and I slip the strap around my ankle while I sit in the beautiful cold.
Interestingly, all three of the ice bathers in my household experienced an increase in 'detox type' symptoms or a more intense reaction to the cold after the first earthed icebath. Maybe a cooincidence?

PS Happy New Year Everyone! xx

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-01T14:56:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-68922>

Anne...this is a great video...thanks for posting it. Where I CT in my pond there is a little underwater spring which I can sit near. The bubbles tell me that the water is moving up from the ground. I'm sure this is the source of the electron/grounding benefits I'm feeling. Because I have heart issues, I'm particularly happy to be able to get a healing dose of CT/grounding.

### prAna303 — 2013-01-01T15:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-68926>

I often say to my friends that Gore-Tex killed us humans. Take notice that we started to use this stuff in the early eighties together with modern shoes...

"Gore-Tex materials are typically based on thermo-mechanically expanded polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) and other fluoropolymer products. They are used in a wide variety of applications such as high performance fabrics, medical implants, filter media, insulation for wires and cables, gaskets, and sealants. However, Gore-Tex fabric is best known for its use in protective, yet breathable, rainwear."

Notice the line insulation, we become a walking battery with constant charging.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-01-03T14:38:13+0200

### freesia — 2013-01-03T15:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-69189>

> **Raiken3712 said**
>
> It would be nice if someone sold footwear like Vibrams that work for earthing. Vibram shoes still have the same issue in that regard.

Pretty sure you'd be onto a winner there

You can get, from lessemf.com, some earthing straps that fit over regular synthetic sports shoes....probably too bulky for vibrams thoug

I mentioned earthing to my husband again, and sure enough - any excuse for a trip to the electronics store ha! - we're well and truly earthed now!
Yesterday he modified plugs to stay plugged in, earthed but switched off, beside our bed and we each have a one and a half metre spiral cord attached to an earthing band to sleep with. I feel tingling in my feet when my band is on, he feels no sensation but maybe a little more refreshed this morning. I'm intrigued to see how this works out longer term.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-03T19:42:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-69227>

I spent a few hours tying to find leather soled shoes and boots on the internet yesterday. I actually have a bunch of moccasins that a friend made for us in Canada, so I was all set to wear those...but then I saw she had lined the leather with a fuzzy acrylic material ARGGGGG. Despite this, I put on two pairs of socks and wore them to do barn chores in -16 degrees. My feet didn't get cold at all, so if I can find some plain leather slippers I'll just convert them to outdoor shoes. As long as it stays this cold, there is no moisture to worry about and it feels nice to feel the snow underfoot.

I was also looking at Frye boots and cowboy boots. Here's my new cultural theory of the day: ....maybe the western stereotypical taciturn, low key, unfussed cowboy reflects the fact that they are grounded in their leather boots!! Apart from people who can afford custom made Italian super expensive shoes, no group other than cowboys and cowgirls has maintained an attachment to leather soled shoes that i can think of. Amazing variety and artistry.

There are also some beautiful cobblers who make old fashioned footwear in England. I've drooled over hand made riding boots from the 18th century....now it may be a medical necessity!

Off to google moccasins now and see about grounding our bed.

### Dali Dula — 2013-01-04T09:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing-discussion-with-stephen-sinatra-video.5306/post-69278>

When I CT in the pond I spend time standing with water up to my chin and my feet firmly settled into the muck on the bottom. The muck and I have become friends. Once you get past the ick factor the pond muck becomes incredibly comfortable and I'm sure the grounding is good. The muck loves the cold, it grows big and fluffy as the temps drop in the fall.

# Thread: CT Damaging Toes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-01T16:25:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68931>

Have any of you found CT seems to damage your toes? After maybe 2 months of doing CT off an on with cold showers, or swims in the lake (with and without water shoes), I have permanently sore toes and toe nails. There are some with black spots looking in colour like a dark bruise (these have been there over a month). There are dead skin looking scales over some areas, especially the dark parts (I do have toenail fungus too, I think. Is it spreading?). Recently, my toes have started being swollen all the time, and the worst one, a baby toe, is sore with small bumps under the skin.
My feet are often hard to keep warm. They can very quickly become white and hard just walking around on my cold floor in the morning. At these times and during CT, my feet and toes are generally white, which I guess Jack warned against.
Am I damaging my feet? Anyone have anything like this? How do I adapt them or protect them?

### nonchalant — 2013-01-01T16:53:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68936>

Hi Mela, I haven't heard of this issue with CT. I used to keep my toes above water when taking baths.
What temperatures are you using for CT? Since I have been going below 50F, I've noticed white splotches on the soles of my feet that feel numb for a while after my baths. I may start keeping my entire foot above water. I wonder if wool socks would help?

I also wonder if your toes are showing symptoms of detox. I got a rash on my hand (with small blisters) about a month after starting CT.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-01T17:01:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68937>

I would be very careful Meta. There was a thread on Reynaud's syndrome a little while ago...you might want to read that. Two quotes from Dr. K that are in that thread.

"Well I think if you wear the socks and gloves and keep your hands and toes out you might not have any issues at all but you could test it out in your sink first. I would strongly consider getting an idea of what your Essential fatty acids are in your cell membranes because with Raynaud's people have some of the worse ratio's I have seen. This is why they have Raynaud's, along with a very permeable gut."

"Raynaud's in my opinion is a lack of bio-energenics in the vascular smooth muscles causing a lack of extracellular adenosine which cause reactive vasoconstriction to any stimulus. Anything tied to the MG ATPase function improves it. Mg, avoidance of calcium, D-Ribose, Phosphate, L-carnitine, DHA and Iodine help reverse it."

Hope this helps....

### Inger — 2013-01-02T02:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68966>

Meta, please protect your feets!
That is why we should always listen to our bodies. I do only a minute or two in close to freezing water, because I feel my toes cannot take more they start to hurt! and there is no way I can hold them out of water. I use crogs too now, because I realized my toes got a bit swollen too and I want to have beautiful toes always! Now they are beautiful again! You need to not let your toes freeze too much! Baby steps. Use sock or shoes or whatever you come up with, if you are going to stay longer in! Jack says in hes CT protocol to do cold baths.. keep the feets out.. AND use socks too!!! That is for a reason. Maybe go back and read the CT protocol and you will see with different eyes. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If you want more CT Meta, just go more often but short! I think that is way better.

And do CT where your feets and hands are kept warm. To keep your home cool is great! Just put wool socks on. I realize if I let my hands freeze too much they get not so pretty. Kind of swollen. Means to me, they are not ready for so much cold.

To help your toes heal, do some warm epsom salt foot bath, use a nice oil like coconut oil to keep them soft and moist, do some pedicure. I am sure your toes will look pretty soon!

### Inger — 2013-01-02T02:34:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68970>

That is how my feet look when they are healthy. Not swollen or read. If they do I know I need to change something..

### caroline — 2013-01-02T02:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-68971>

Once again Inger - your instincts are right on and so powerful. Beautiful feet! It is lovely for a woman to have beautiful feet and hands that are well taken care of......
so feminine and sensual I think. I wonder what the guys have to say about this????

### maggie — 2013-01-02T11:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-69002>

Mela, I got dark, bruised looking spots when I first started CT'ing and was using gel packs to spot CT. Those spots were tissue damage and they hurt and took a long time to heal. Occasionally I still make that mistake, but I'm much more careful now. Feet out of the water, in wool socks and wool hat makes CT easier to take. Takes a bit of maneuvering to get those socks on once I'm in the tub, but I think some people put them on wet feet.

Are you CT'ing in a tub or in a lake/ocean? If you're "wild" CT'ing, maybe neoprene booties would help.

Best of luck to you.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-02T13:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-69020>

I keep my feet and hands out also. I put socks on feet, mittens on hands and a hat to cover my head. All of that ultimately seems to help my toes not turn white.

I wonder if part of what is going on with you Mele maybe a detox symptom. I can't explain it as it was just a thought.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2013-01-03T10:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-damaging-toes.5311/post-69124>

> **Mela said**
>
> Have any of you found CT seems to damage your toes? After maybe 2 months of doing CT off an on with cold showers, or swims in the lake (with and without water shoes), I have permanently sore toes and toe nails. There are some with black spots looking in colour like a dark bruise (these have been there over a month). There are dead skin looking scales over some areas, especially the dark parts (I do have toenail fungus too, I think. Is it spreading?). Recently, my toes have started being swollen all the time, and the worst one, a baby toe, is sore with small bumps under the skin.
> My feet are often hard to keep warm. They can very quickly become white and hard just walking around on my cold floor in the morning. At these times and during CT, my feet and toes are generally white, which I guess Jack warned against.
> Am I damaging my feet? Anyone have anything like this? How do I adapt them or protect them?

Hi Mela,

From my reading about Raynaud's, I would be very concerned about the black spots showing up.
I would try to keep your feet warm while doing the CTs from now on.

Do your fingers or toes ever turn white or go numb during or after the CT?

# Thread: Epsom Salt in cold water can it work?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-01-02T15:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/post-69042>

So I've put it in cold water before..and was wondering...was that pointless? Will it get into my skin in cold water? Can I stir it to dissolve it...or would I have to actually make it warm for it to dissolve. I've heard people with thoughts that taking warm baths might slow down adaption to the cold so I don't want to take a separate warm bath for Epsom salt. If it doesn't work I'll just buy some more Magnesium Citrate supplement I was using.

Thanks

### nonchalant — 2013-01-02T16:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/post-69045>

I often use epsom salts in my cold bath, and I think they work fine. They dissolve easily in the cold tap water.
I use them to help detox bromine. I also use epsom salts, along with sea salt, in a bowl on the counter for my right hand, to keep the rash down. Again, I dissolve the salts in tap water, and dip my hand several times per day. Works like a charm.

### maggie — 2013-01-02T16:26:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epsom-salt-in-cold-water-can-it-work.5320/post-69046>

I dissolve 2 cups of epsom salts in a small pan of hot water and throw that into my cold bath. It's not enough to warm the tub water. Since Nonchalent's getting results from soaking her hand, I'm hoping that I'll be absorbing the mag this way.

# Thread: How to eat optimal with less money? Share your tips..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-01-03T13:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69156>

I am sure there are other that have also made some stupid mistakes in their life and therefor have a little less money.. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

and eating high quality foods is not the cheapest.

I have figured out if I am really short of money, the pet food store saves me. It sound weird but it is a great thing. I can get reindeer meat minced and frozen, elk bones with marrow.. minced game and organs and beef.. all for very cheap! One pound costs about 1,5-3 â‚¬. That means I can easily use only 4 â‚¬ / day for super healthy foods!
2 years ago in winter I was so out of money I often had a pet food mix with organs and game,
I ate it raw and hold my nose because it was not too delicious IMHO.

Before I did it - I called the number on the package (because it said on the package, for pets only) and explained to the women who answered my call, I have a dog I love so much I give her only the best foods, the same I eat too (I lied a little here because I have no dog.. but I figured these are situations it is allowed to lie..) and I wondered why it says not for human consumption on the package? What is dangerous with it? She said it was nothing, and perfectly safe, it just had to be labeled so because it is sold in the pet food store. It is very clean and handled just the same way as that what goes for human consumption.
I thought, fine, nothing to be scared of.. I felt great from it too. My brother once made meatballs from it and said it tasted fine..lol I preferred to eat it just raw as I feel better from raw meat. I did not liked the taste either and it is much easier to swallow raw for sure!

I buy the cheapest frozen wild caught fish, it costs about 2,60 â‚¬ for one pound. Then.. frozen cooked mussels. Very cheap too. Often they have a special offer, a local small fish, fresh and wild caught, for 4-6 â‚¬ kg, I eat he whole fish, make MHS from the heads. Sometimes they have really great offers on grass fed lamb, fatty and very delicious.

When spring comes.. I collect wild edibles, like nettles and such.They are great! All for free. Or mushrooms and in fall wild growing berries.

Organs are usually pretty cheap! Heart is much cheaper than muscle meat and I like it much better too.. it is amazing!
The fattiest meats are mostly the cheapest also. A win win!

If I lived near the coast I would collect seaweeds for sure! I am planning on taking a trip to Norway this spring and visit my siblings who are living there near the coast and then collect seaweeds and dry them there and take lots of them with me home!

So it is possible to live pretty cheap, and optimal too!

Then when I occasionally have a bit more money.. I use it on oysters and stuff...lol

### fitness@home — 2013-01-03T14:20:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69169>

Inger,

I have never seen any "unprocessed" meat at any pet stores here. I sometimes wonder about the additives they use in the refrigerated pet food mixtures.

The best investment I made was a chest freezer. It allows us to purchase meats and fish while on sale and store until needed. I also split a side of grass fed beef with a co-worker. Cheaper price per pound and you also get all the bones, organs, etc...

I also love growing my own veggies and herbs. Was much better at growing things to maturation in Michigan...especially tomatoes. Still learning how to grow them successfully in Texas

Asian and farmer's markets are two of my favorite places to food shop.

### diane — 2013-01-03T14:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69170>

Here it's mostly raw bones then processed "raw" - which means added veggies and not sure what else. And now the pet food is way more expensive than buying it on your own. Almost anything for pets is more expensive than for humans.

I too bought a freezer on kijiji for $50 - best investment! Then I get a 1/4 side of grass fed beef from a friend - which definitely saves. Lots of great cuts, and I know where it's coming from. Actually getting another 1/4 today - and got an extra 4 hearts too! I also ask for any extra bones for both the dogs and now my bone broth.

Agree - asian markets are great too. And lots of people join raw meat co-ops so they get cheaper meat. I haven't yet - but it's on my list! Befriending butchers is also a great idea!

Here mussels are very cheap. And there is fish on sale at various markets. But I'm lucky living in a big city in that I have lots of choice and places to go as well.

### Inger — 2013-01-03T14:41:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69173>

Too weird about your pet food! Here it is only plain, no additives at all. It has gotten very popular to barf dogs and pets so there are many good things to find. Funny how different it can be in different countries!
I have to go to an Asian market too, I never did yet as they are only in big city, not close to me and I have no car so it makes it a bit complicated.. I might find some treasures there though...

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-03T15:24:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69184>

+1 to a chest freezer being an essential and huge money-saver!!

I have relatives in the US. I see that US Wellness meats has something called 'pet burgers' that are a fraction of the price of the other meat and is all grass-fed with the added bonus of offal ground in. Sounds like something I'd be buying if I were there!

I also bought, for $20, a hand-operated meat grinder. This is another money-saver and means I can buy offal and other cheap cuts and make mince simply, while controlling the ingredients.

### diane — 2013-01-03T15:26:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69187>

Lauren - I've been looking at meat grinders lately. Good to know the cheap hand ones work - I was definitely favouring that over the pricey electric ones!

### endless — 2013-01-07T22:58:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69813>

I have a big upright freezer, and I order beef and lamb by the side or quarter in the fall. I am a fan of frozen cooked mussels too, they are pretty inexpensive. I buy prawns when they are on sale. I make it known that I am always up for extra fish if someone has a good day of fishing, and in the fall I ask that hunters save me the organs if they get a deer or elk.

### furface — 2013-01-08T12:12:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69860>

One of my best friends is in the process of buying a boat, and he does a lot of fishing in the coastal B.C. waters, so I should be in line to get a lot of salmon, dungeness, and snow crab, and possibly even some halibut, as well as any other types of fish or seafood he may catch. Even if I have to pay a small amount for some, or even go fishing with him. Should help to save a few shackles!

So once again the old adage, "It's WHO you know!" comes true.

Also I don't know if this would be feasible, but a lot of stores sell produce that is reaching it's "best before" date for extremely bargain prices. If it still looks good, and has no signs of deterioration etc, it might be a good way to save some money...esp if you cut out any "bad" parts, and make a big soup, or stew out of the products in question, and add some inexpensive GF meat, duck, game, or seafood, and make a "complete meal" out of it. That should last a few days, and could even be frozen.

### caroline — 2013-01-08T16:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-optimal-with-less-money-share-your-tips.5331/post-69907>

At my fish market I am able to buy 500gr. packages of Salmon trimmings - I think they call it salmon mince. It is the trimmings from all the beautiful salmon pieces in the display. It is really good stuff. I usually just lightly saute with garlic and ginger and onions and tumeric or whatever and it is great!

# Thread: German Chocolate Mocca Torte with loads of fat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T15:44:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/post-69195>

ok Bobbi, here goes, just took it out of the oven to cool down.
You need for the cake:
16 eggs
400 g dark chocolate (possibly with Stevia as it brings down Carbs)
550 g peeled almond flour
peel of one fresh lemon and orange (substituted this for candid lemon peel)
2 tablespoons of cinnamon (any other spice is great too such as pumpkin pie spice but I like the cinnamon)
Stevia

separate 11 eggs and add 5 whole eggs to the yolk. Beat until very fluffy and add Stevia, (as much as you see fit) lemon and orange peel and cinnamon. Before prepare chocolate by putting it through a shredder or grater. Then add the chocolate, keeping a little bit for decoration (3 tablespoons)
let it mix for a while and then add the almond flour, 500 g. Let it stir until the batter gets dark. Meanwhile prepare the pan (european style with the side to take away) by greasing it well with butter and take 50 g of almond flour to cover the floor and the sides.
Now beat the eggwhite and fold under the batter by hand, careful not to break the eggwhites.
Put in the pan and stick in the oven. Bake at very low temperatur (max. 150 Â°C) for app. 1,5 hrs.
Take out, take away the side of the pan after a few minutes, put it on a grid to cool down. After cooling down wrap it up and keep it cool over night.

I calculated the Nutrition facts for the cake: 622,65 g of fat, 346,15 g of protein, 63,2 g of carbohydrates

For the frosting you need:
700 g of butter (Kerrygold is best as is soft without salt)
300 g of dark chocolate (same recommendation as above)
500 g of Mascarpone
Stevia
2 tablespoons of freeze dried coffee

Beat the butter until real fluffy with Stevia. Melt the chocolate in a waterbatch and add to the butter, then add the Mascarpone and the coffee. (Instead of coffee you can use any aromas, that you like such as Nutaroma, vanilla, etc.)
Let the mixture cool off a little and meanwhile cut the cake into 3 layers.
Then spread the cream onto the layers, putting one layer on top of the other and keep some for the outside. Then spread the cream on and around the cake still keeping some for the decoration. Sprinkle the chocolate bits left over from the cake on top and on the sides of the cake. Now put the remaining cream into a Piping bag (am not sure if this is the right word) and decorate little stars or whatever you like on the cake. Decorate with Mocca beans or real coffee beans.

Nutrition information for the frosting:
Fat: 910,1 g, Protein: 31,58 g, Carbs: 47,05 g.

Total Nutrition information: Fat: 1.532,8 g, Protein: 377,73 g, Carbs: 110,25.

This cake makes easily 20 pieces as it is very rich. So per piece you have: 76,64 g fat, 18,9 g protein, 5,5 g of carbs. Ratio of fat to protein: 75% fat and 25% protein.

### diane — 2013-01-03T16:08:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/post-69199>

Sounds amazing Charlotte! Thanks for posting!

### Braden — 2013-01-26T08:43:15+0200

### suse1023 — 2013-04-12T18:40:39+0300

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-12T19:30:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/german-chocolate-mocca-torte-with-loads-of-fat.5334/post-83913>

Ingredients such as glycerin or honey attract moisture so its possible either might make a moister cake. Leaving the unfrosted cake overnight in a tight container with a wadded up damp paper towel might increase the moistness.

There's a cake called La Leche Cake (iirc) in which the cake is soaked with milk before serving. I wonder how that would turn out with coconut or almond flour.

# Thread: What is the best way to cook/prepare duck?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2013-01-04T14:17:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69317>

Just wondering what is the best way to prepare duck? I've never had it before, but one of our largest grocery store chains...Real Canadian SuperStore, has utility grade duck on sale this week for $2.38 lb. so I thought I might try it. I've read on here that the fat is very healthy for you, and since I haven't been eating any chicken or turkey while on the Leptin Rx, I thought some duck would be a nice change of pace from bacon, seafood/shellfish, and GF beef & lamb. Not saying I'll buy duck on a regular basis, but I wouldn't mind trying it out.

Thanks in advance. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-04T16:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69335>

I've de-skinned it and rendered the fat from the skin, and make cracklins for crispy goodness to sprinkle over top; you can roast it. or braise it. Joy of cooking has some great recipes that you can modify to meet Paleo standards.

I butchered mine up - and then braised it w/ mushrooms, and bockchoy in a beef broth seasoned w/ tumeric, corriander, cumin, garlic, black pepper and salt in a dutch oven. quite tasty - said cracklins were sprinkled over top for xtra crunchy goodness when served

### David Michael — 2013-01-04T16:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69336>

furface,
Here's how we cook it (assuming this is a whole duck):
\- We usually put it in to cook in the morning and let it roast all day.
\- Thaw in the fridge or a water bath.
\- Leave at room temperature for about an hour. Cut off the wing tips and save for stock.
\- Lightly score the skin in a criss-cross pattern; do not cut into the meat.
\- Pierce the fat layers along the back, thighs and wings.
\- Rub with olive oil, salt and pepper.
\- Place on rack and then into a roasting pan.
\- Roast at 400F for 20 minutes; turn oven down to 300F; roast an additional 40 minutes; turn oven down to 180F.
\- After a couple of hours, insert a meat thermometer probe between the thigh and body. Get it down close to where the leg joins the body.
\- Cook until the thermometer reads 170F. If it is getting close to the time you want to eat and the duck is not done, increase the oven temperature to hurry it along.
\- Let it rest for 15 minutes, then carve. Save all the fat as if it were liquid gold (it is). Save the bones and carcass for stock, it will make delicious soup.
Good Luck!
P.S. Except for scoring and piercing, this is the same procedure we use for a chicken or a turkey as well.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-04T16:34:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69341>

This is how I make it (Ã¤hm, used to make it) Now I do not do the pineapple anymore.
Put some fat (GHEE is perfect) into large meat pan and roast the chicken on all sides, piercing it carefully with a fork to release the fat. (my Mom used to juice a little pineapple, put the juice in a syringe and inject it all over the duck (makes the meat very tender from the enzymes)
Add a little water and keep frying it, until you have a nice dark gravy with lots of fat in it. That way it gets tender without getting dry.
Then after 1-2 hrs. put it in the oven and roast it on all sides until it is nicely roasted, still pinching with a fork occasionally to drain more fat into a pan underneath. Add the fat to the gravy and take out the duck when it is done. Sometimes I baste it also with a little bit of orange juice (fresh) and orange peel. Gives it a nice flavor.

### furface — 2013-01-04T19:01:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69367>

Thanks everyone for all of your suggestions and instructions. I may try a combination of methods, and see what happens. I'm planning on having it either tomorrow, or on Sunday, so if you don't see my replying the next few days you'll know that something went terribly wrong.

### furface — 2013-01-05T17:36:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69494>

Well I've just finished my first ever duck meal, and it was...SUCCULENT!! Extremely Delicious!

I took a little bit from each suggestion/recipe, as well as a few youtube recipes, and roasted it in my convection oven @ 350 degrees F, for about an hour and a half until it reached an internal temp of 175 degrees F at the thickest part of the thigh. It was a 4.3 lb. young duck.

I used some garlic powder, sea salt, pepper, thyme, orange zest, one whole orange cut into quarters stuffed into the cavity, along with half a sweet onion, and a couple of sprigs of fresh mint leaves. (Don't ask why I put them in, but I thought since it smells so good, it might help the duck taste good. lol...which it did).

Anyway, thanks everyone for all of your help, and I not only have a delicious few meals, but some great bones for stock, and some liquid gold duck fat/juice. (Some of which I smothered over some steamed broccoli to have along with the freshly roasted duck!)

### diane — 2013-01-05T17:39:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69495>

Jason - you've just convinced me to get a duck! I almost picked one up the last time I was at the grocery store as they were on sale, but I passed. Now I'm kicking myself! Thanks for asking the question!

### furface — 2013-01-05T17:52:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69496>

> **diane said**
>
> Jason - you've just convinced me to get a duck! I almost picked one up the last time I was at the grocery store as they were on sale, but I passed. Now I'm kicking myself! Thanks for asking the question!

You're most welcome, Diane! Actually that's why I got one...Real Canadian SuperStore here in the Vancouver area, have young ducks on sale this week for $2.38 or so per lb. And after reading on here how duck fat is considered "liquid gold", and that it's a good alternative to chicken or turkey, I thought I'd try it. Glad I did. lol

Why I tried the mint, I have no idea, but I love mint with lamb, and figured lamb is "dark meat", as is duck, so why not? Good move, added a nice little bit of "sweetness" along with the orange, to the meat.

Hope you try it, Diane, and enjoy it.

Later I'm going to make duck stock, and duck soup/stew with the leftovers.

### Destiny — 2013-01-05T18:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69503>

Yum, this sounds so good. I have to get myself a duck!

### furface — 2013-01-05T18:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69506>

Just an additional note...I poked a number of holes into the skin (not the flesh though) with a wooden skewer before roasting, and ended up with about 3/4 cup of duck fat/juice from the 4.3 lb. duck.

I began roasting it breast side down, then after 20 minutes, took it out, flipped it over, and cooked it the rest of the time, breast side up. I also kept the cavity closed after I stuffed it with the orange, onion, mint, and spices with the wooden skewer.

After the roasting time was over, I let it sit on my cutting board, covered in foil for about 15 minutes before carving.

### furface — 2013-01-06T02:13:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-best-way-to-cook-prepare-duck.5340/post-69555>

Just finished a 44 minute cold shower, but before I stepped into the shower, I started a duck stock in my slow cooker. I used the giblets, the leftover cooked bones and carcass, wings, drumstick bones, added some celery, baby carrots, onions, rock sea salt, and black peppercorns.

I'm going to let it cook on high for a few hours, then turn it on low and let it simmer overnight.

Hope it turns out okay.

# Thread: Research - Inflammation causes mental disease -
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/research-inflammation-causes-mental-disease.5345/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thor — 2013-01-04T17:54:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/research-inflammation-causes-mental-disease.5345/post-69359>

.
This was something I ran into today during my reading. Mental disorders like Depression are a result of chronic inflammation - it's a neolithic disease! Amazing how all roads lead back to inflammation?!?! Interesting to say for sure:

Maes and colleagues writing in Neuroendocrinology Newsletters, propose depression to be an inflammatory disorder:

There is now evidence that depression, as characterized by melancholic symptoms, anxiety, and fatigue and somatic (F&S) symptoms, is the clinical expression of peripheral cell-mediated activation, inflammation and induction of oxidative and nitrosative stress (IO&NS) pathways and of central microglial activation, decreased neurogenesis and increased apoptosis.

As listed in the abstract, this would help explain the frequent co-existence of depression in brain disorders such as â€œAlzheimerâ€™s, Parkinsonâ€™s and Huntingtonâ€™s disease, multiple sclerosis and strokeâ€, and medical disorders such as â€œcardiovascular disorder, chronic fatigue syndrome, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis, systemic lupus erythematosus, inflammatory bowel disease, irritable bowel syndrome, leaky gut, diabetes, obesity and the metabolic syndrome, and HIV infection.â€

The authors propose the â€œcommon denominatorâ€ in these disorders is activation of the microglial cells (the immune cells of the brain) and/or activation of the bodyâ€™s â€œinflammation and induction of oxidative and nitrosative stress pathwaysâ€ (IO&NS pathways). These â€œpathways function as a smoke sensor that detect threats in the peripheral and central parts of the body and signal these threats as melancholic, anxiety, and fatigue and somatic (F&S) symptoms.â€ (emphasis added)

The authorsâ€™ conclusion:

It is concluded that the activation of peripheral and / or central IO&NS pathways may explain the co-occurrence of depression with the above disorders. This shows that depression belongs to the spectrum of inflammatory disorders.

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-04T22:19:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/research-inflammation-causes-mental-disease.5345/post-69386>

That's very interesting, especially that they also grouped it together with metabolic syndrome and HIV infection.

# Thread: Water fasting and bloating from water

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-06T10:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-fasting-and-bloating-from-water.5359/post-69580>

Hi guys.

I dry fasted for 7 days then transitioned into a water fast, current total time 12 days. I am doing this fast to solve a bloating problem that occurs everytime I eat or drink anything.

During the dry fast all bloating was gone however as soon as I started drinking water all the previous problems came back.

I can drink as little as 200mls of spring water and still get the same problems.

I will drink the water as my mouth will be thirsty but soon after starting to drink my stomach will get uncomfortable and full even though my mouth is still thirsty. I will then experience an outward pressure feeling in my mid left abdomen and large amounts of bloating will occur in the same area (just to the left of belly button). I can also see my abdomen visibly pulsate which I assume is from increased blood flow.

I will feel slightly like the water could be vomited back up (but it never is) and this uncomfortable/sickly feeling will stay with me for up to 30hrs. In this time my mouth will feel thirsty but my stomach really doesn't want anything else in it.

What could be causing this. Your help is very appreciated!

Thanks, Sean.

# Thread: Just had an awesome lunch!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### furface — 2013-01-06T18:41:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69653>

After roasting my first ever duck yesterday, I decided to make a stock/soup from the carcass, wings, drumstick bones etc, and let it simmer overnight in my slow cooker. I also added some celery, baby carrots, and onions.

After stripping all the remaining meat off of the bones, I returned it to the broth, and let it simmer for a few more hours. (About 15 hours in total.)

So after reading that broth/stock is good for a leaky gut, I had a nice big bowl of the duck stock/broth (including a few of the baby carrots and meat), along with a couple of steamed snow crab legs. (OMFG soooo sweet, and succulent!!). Finished it off with a couple of pieces of 90% Dark chocolate covered with coconut manna.

I think I'm hooked on snow crab legs...this was also the first time I've ever had them.

I am learning so much while on the Leptin Rx!!

### endless — 2013-01-06T21:38:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69677>

You are inspiring me to try duck...I have a single breast in the freezer that I will try first but looking forward to getting a whole one if only for the fat! Thanks for the tips.

### furface — 2013-01-06T21:55:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69683>

Just glad to pass along any bits of info that I can. The thing I find about the duck fat, and the stock, and meat even, is that it doesn't really taste "fatty", or have that "gooey" sensation to it. Even the fat. It tastes "rich", and flavourful. At least to me. I also found the meat to be nice and moist...probably from the high fat content. haha.

Hope you enjoy the breast, and if you try it, the entire duck. The broth/stock/soup is very tasty.

### caroline — 2013-01-08T16:19:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69910>

Jason ... you are turning into a gourmet, epi/paleo Chef - very nice

### caroline — 2013-01-08T16:22:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69912>

Jason .. we are so at opposite ends of the spectrum ... I am eating everything as raw as possible - so I don't have to cook ...and you are making all this great sounding stuff

to each his own - right??

### furface — 2013-01-08T17:47:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69939>

Caroline -- Exactly! Whatever works for each individual as long as you follow the main guidelines of WHAT to eat. Sure RAW may be a bit more optimal, or maybe a lot more optimal, but if a person will ONLY eat something good "cooked" even if only seared for a minute or so on each side (Which is how I had my GF ribeye steak for lunch today along with some mushrooms sauteed in coconut oil and a small pat of kerrygold butter.), at least they're eating it, instead of shoving some other crap into their traps!

As far as becoming some sort of chef is concerned...I don't know about that, LOL, but I was cooking for my family since I was 15-16 when my mom began to suffer from Multiple Sclerosis. Hasn't hurt that I've had to "bache" for the past 30 or so years either.

Thing is, I hardly ever follow a "recipe". More often than not it's just trial and error. Sometimes it works out, i.e. the fresh mint sprigs with the roasted duck, sometimes it doesn't.

### caroline — 2013-01-08T21:11:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69979>

Good job ...and it sounds like you have been a very loving and caring son.....

### furface — 2013-01-08T21:45:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-had-an-awesome-lunch.5365/post-69997>

Thanks. I just do my best to do what I can to help.

Hi there!
Just wondering what were the main labs you used to begin your journey to optimal?!
I know there is a big list somewhere, however there are many in initials and i don't know what they stand for.
I realise I should post this in the labs forum, however my symptoms and history seem to be more suited to this area.

Symptoms include (some no longer have): multiple IgG food intolerances, fatigue hard to fall asleep (this is getting better), IBS (healed with grain/dairy free), asthma-healed, depression-healed, and full blown head to toe eczema!
Eczema has only been this severe since I removed removed all western meds and began a detox program (supps, colon cleanse). I have to used a mild cream (i know this is not good) now because it feels like i am being burned with acid, it itches so bad i bleed, the heat it releases make me feel like i am denaturing myself and results in insomnia and being unable to work.

So far I have done cortisol, hair mineral analysis.
Was thinking Thyroid, Omega 3, perhaps Liver Detoxification Panel?
Appreciate the help, so I can arrange a consult asap!!!

### ashryn — 2013-01-06T22:35:26+0200

I had eczema as a kid caused by vaccinations and the 'never well since' marker led the homoeopath to a treatment. I also cut out all dairy for a couple of years at the time, but have since added it back in with no issues so i credit the homoeopathy. Its one of those things that depends heavily on your own context though, so my telling you the remedy they used on me would be completely irrelevant. If you can find a classical homoeopath, (one remedy, one dose and wait) you might be able to kick the eczema in a hurry.

### lauren_r — 2013-01-06T23:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-journey-to-optimal-gut-adrenal-eczema.5368/post-69697>

Thanks Ashryn. I have had eczema on and off since childhood using cortisones on and off. But what began in Oct '11 when I cut all meds and began to "heal" everything, it became a whole new ball game. Face, arms, neck first then just arms for a few months and then doing the colon stuff sent my body into crazy overdrive detox holy crap state!!! That was March this year. I suffered through 3 months with nothing, which in hindsight was to the detriment of my physical health and mental health (read: it was beyond anything you could imagine or want your worst enemy to go through). So the cream it is for now and supps, and lots of omega 3, sunshine, etc etc. I just went one week without the cream (put it through the washing machine) and in a few days i am covered, acid burn, itchy, and not sleeping etc. So it is all there just being pushed back down { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### ashryn — 2013-01-07T00:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-journey-to-optimal-gut-adrenal-eczema.5368/post-69705>

On a macro level, eczema is all tied to the liver from what i recall, but of course there are so so so many things that upset the liver (such a fussy thing for sometthing so ugly) getting to the bottom of it all is a huge struggle, and if you have been using quartizone on and off likely your skins ability to expel toxins effectively (which is how your liver wants to get rid of them) anyway, i rant.. You already know this i'm sure since you have been dealing with it.. Go with at least the liver test imho.. And vitamin d.. I assume you're taking St Mary's Thistle?

Seriously though, have you tried an icy bath?

# Thread: What are you reading?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-06T22:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/post-69695>

I've seen a few book recommendations on various threads, and i thought it would be cool to collect them in the same place.. Until the last couple of weeks when i had to give my ipad back to work and lost all my books (not really lost, just stuck on my computer for now until i get around to updating the new ipad with them)

I use a booklovers site called goodreads (i'm ashryn there too if you want to find me) and love getting recommendations for new authors to read.
I'm going through a sci fi near future dystopian phase at the moment, and am re-reading a book called The Windup Girl by Paulo Bacigalupi. http://www.goodreads.com/book/show/6597651-the-windup-girl i'm really enjoing it, and i have 3 xmas presents to return (already own all of them) plus a gift voucher for my favourite bookshop, so i'm looking for recommendations..

What are you reading at the moment?
What is your all-time favourite book?

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-07T01:20:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/post-69707>

I'm reading "iodine, why you need it, why you can't live w/o it" by David Brownstein, MD
Heat Wave by "richard castle"
Game of thrones by George RR Martin

And yes, simultaneously. It drives DH nuts. (shrug)

Up next, Robert Crais's "The Two Minute Rule"
"The Shell Seekers" by Rosamond Pilcher (forced on me by a friend, not something I would normally read)
David Baldacci's "The Innocent"
and about 300 other books on my Kindle and about the same number in my bookcase. I don't dare go to the library!

My favorite book of all time? gee that covers way too much ground.

I would recommend Dan Simmons 4 book series:
Hyperion
Fall of Hyperion
Endymion
Rise of Endymion

And Olympus and Illium

Stephen King's The Dark Tower series ... his absolute BEST ever.

ANYthing by Pauline Gedge.

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T07:23:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/post-69726>

I love those Dan Simmons ones!
Never heard of Pauline Gedge.. Shall look her up now.
But Stephen king? Nope. Gonna disagree with you on that choice ;P

My favourite series is the Nights Dawn trilogy by Peter F Hamilton... After the hitchhikers guide to the galaxy, of course...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-07T13:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/post-69754>

But have you READ the Dark Tower series? It is based on the Song of Roland and is a real mind twister. It is completely different from anything he's ever written.

I loved his early stuff. IT being my all time favorite. I actually cried at the end because I didn't WANT it to end. He does the most amazing character development I have EVER EVER seen. Yeah the plots are pretty basic and sometimes downright simplistic. But his CHARACTERS! It's why his books have never translated well to film. Too much internal dialog and description in the books.

The Dark Tower started about 30 years ago as a short story in The Magazine of Fantasy & Science Fiction. then he wrote another bit and another and he finally had a book. then he continued. Took him about 25 years to finish the entire cycle but it was well worth the wait.

Pauline Gedge writes THE most amazing historical fiction (which I usually don't bother with). But she brings ancient Egypt to life. EVERY single one of her books puts me there in a way no other writer has ever been able to do. Her work is incredible. And I wish she'd get something new out there.

Peter Hamilton .... whoa there's another author who blows me away.

For some non-fiction, if you really want to warp your mind, try any of Zecharia Sitchin's books.

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T19:58:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/post-69793>

Hrm.. Well i have all of stephen kings books in epub somewhere, so i shall trust you and give dark towers a bash. I really like cx driven stories, but i have found SKs to be largely full of characters i hate!

Umm.. About Zecharia Sitchin... You meant fiction, or speculative fiction, right? { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-09T01:10:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-are-you-reading.5369/post-70019>

OOH OOOH! Bet you never heard of this one.

The Glass Books of the Dream Eaters and the sequel, The Chemickal Marriage by Gordon W. Dahlquist. There's only the two so don't get fooled by the volume I and volume II bs. # Thread: Developing written material for kids and older children
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-written-material-for-kids-and-older-children.5373/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-07T02:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-written-material-for-kids-and-older-children.5373/post-69713>

I was wondering if we have any gifted people on the forum who could start taking some of Jack's material and developing resources for older children about health? I have a grandson born with congenital hypothyroidism. He has his thyroid but at birth his TSH was off the scale so he's been on thyroid medication since day 2 of life. Now he is 11 and I see the damage he is doing due to living on white bread and pasta and a working mother out of the house from 7.30 am till 6pm. BUT he is very bright. He got very upset one day when I told him he was looking mentally retarded lolling around on the floor with his tongue stuck out and not doing anything during the holidays and refusing to eat anything I would call food. I told him he was losing his intelligence. This is the only way to get to him. BEcause he is a non-communicator and hates being told what to do I talked to him through his mother and suggested that one day he might like to maximise his intelligence by taking control over what he eats and then he might choose to eat foods which would make him more intelligent, not less, and also to start getting rid of the roll of fat that is starting over his torso. Much to my surprise I found him nodding his head.

So I feel I have a way in to helping him improve his health. But I need something with more authority than a Nana who others think have gone overboard with this health stuff.

I'm still feeling so new at this I'm not quite ready to write this myself but would be happy to participate. It would be good, too, if we could get Jack's imprimateur on it so we know we have the simplification right.

### ashryn — 2013-01-07T07:06:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-written-material-for-kids-and-older-children.5373/post-69724>

I have been simplifying it for myself and recording it in my journal, partly because when I do that i understand it better, and partly because i get distracted by a lot of the stuff in the blogs and for me it garbles the message a bit. The thing i haven't been doing however, is including the links to citations and research, so my notes might not convince a doctor. If you think any of my notes could be used as a starting point for explaining it all to teens, feel free to help yourself. Tough they would benefit from someone who has been doing this for longer than 6 weeks casting a practiced eye over them first to make sure i haven't missed the point.

I suspect if you could get him started on the leptin reset it would show him results fast and that would get him to seek out more info..

Kathylu is really good at making things clear and concise... And thor is great at researching interesting things.. (I'm sure there are many more people, but those two are just the ones that have really helped me to understand some things..)

### caroline — 2013-01-26T23:50:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-written-material-for-kids-and-older-children.5373/post-72770>

I am wondering as well about stuff for kids. My 9 year old grandson is really interested. We were talking a lot about electrons the other day ......

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-07T17:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-brain-gut-connection-breast-milk-sugars-give-infants-a-protective-coat.5378/post-69778>

Great link, Gretchen. My professional life sometimes intersects with breast milk researchers. One thing they tell me repeatedly is that science will never be able to come close to a 'formula' that replicates human breast milk (though they will use research like this to market their products as if they have). I love the description of mother's milk as an 'entity' - it is alive and responsive, interacting with mother, environment and infant. What an amazing thing.

# Thread: Cooking disasters and other tips for young players
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooking-disasters-and-other-tips-for-young-players.5384/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-08T10:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooking-disasters-and-other-tips-for-young-players.5384/post-69846>

\*\*\*Coconut flour is not an effective substitute for flour in a roux...

\*\*\*You can't make bulletproof iced mocha... Coconut oil solidifies when its cold... Who knew :/

\*\*\*If you make your bulletproof coffee with butter and coconut oil, dont blend it too long or the butter will turn into some strange gooey undrinkable mass.

\*\*\*Blending a shake made with heavy whipping cream too long will turn the cream into whipped cream.. Also not that yummy...

### endless — 2013-01-08T12:39:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooking-disasters-and-other-tips-for-young-players.5384/post-69863>

Coconut flour is also not very suitable for breading squid...at least not the way I did it. Gooey mess.

Whole frozen sardines have very tough clear scales that need to be removed before making MHS with them...or they clog up the straw! Yuck!

Unsalted sardines canned in spring water are NOT tasty.

### caroline — 2013-01-08T16:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cooking-disasters-and-other-tips-for-young-players.5384/post-69908>

# Thread: long-distance HELP please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-distance-help-please.5387/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-08T14:20:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-distance-help-please.5387/post-69879>

After 18 months of trying to find a doc in NZ to support me, I am still struggling (there are only a small handful functional/alternative docs in the entire country and I've already needed air travel to access them... each time I find one who I can educate enough to help me with one aspect, they turn out to be an idiot in some other aspect and I can't get what I need. At this point I have no funds for further airplane tickets to visit people who charge $250/hour and then tell me I must have my dental fillings removed before I can take BHRT!).

I have a GP who will support me with regular testing so here is where I need some help. Do you have online sources for high quality BHRT that you don't need a script to purchase?

I have current scripts for compounded whole thyroid, BHRT progesterone and BHRT DHEA. When they run out - if I haven't found another doc - I will need to replace them on my own and then do quarterly testing. I realise there is a lot out there and I have NO idea what is real/quality. I remember JanSz having a recommendation for thyroid meds you can purchase from Amazon. I have no clue about the others.

Please give any advice about products you trust (I would especially like sublingual P drops but would settle for cream; DHEA has been in pill form as has compounded thyroid).

Thank you!

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-08T14:36:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-distance-help-please.5387/post-69883>

Lauren

Dzugan is world wide - if your GP will work with you there's no reason he/she couldn't partner w/Dzugan. there's even a member here on the forum from Peru who uses him.
give Linda McHale a call Office 866.225.4877 x 302
or send her an email - [email redacted]
www.dzlogic.com

Let her know what you're looking for - and see if they can help, if your GP will sign the rx - you should be all set - they might even have a Compounding Pharmacy down in NZ that they already use....
as for DHEA I buy it all via Amazon - I buy the LEF brand - b/c I've had the best success with it.

Thanks, Gretchen! I have looked into Dzugan and would love to do this, but it isn't financial feasible. My husband lost his work after our earthquakes (he now travels M-F for work) and my income is less than $20k NZ per year. I have to find a way around this...I know I can, it will just take some good advice and persistance. Thanks for the heads-up on LEF DHEA. If I can find JanSz's thyroid rec, then all I need is a rec for Progesterone!! (And I'll keep a goal of working with Dzugan alive as well. Thanks).

### freesia — 2013-01-09T14:04:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-distance-help-please.5387/post-70056>

Hi again Lauren,
I'm not on BHRT and haven't tried to order anything along those lines from o'seas so can't offer any experience there. I understand your worry over customs etc though! I did have one thought but I'll PM you on it.
Ice guy

When the temperatures warmed up in November and I couldn't make enough ice for a bath in the 40's anymore, I just phoned an ice company whose truck I'd seen in the shopping street near me and asked the if they'd deliver to me.
I live centrally and I'm on their route between restaurants, so there's no delivery fee. (I think if there had been I would have had to re-think it.). So anyway they deliver an extra 10 kg twice a week when they're passing. I can make 30kg of ice every 48 hours at home so I'm doing that too.
8kg makes the 66 F tap water around 57 - 58 F for my kids (they're doing it for fun) and the rest goes in to get my bath 45 - 48 F.
HTH

Anne.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-09T02:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-where-do-you-hang-out-other-than-here.5396/post-70028>

at work

can't afford any other website, as this one sucks me in already. And beeing a newby there is still so much to read and besides that work is fun too,

actually it is this website that interrups my still delicate circadian balance as I only have the late evenings to read here. (or the early morning, like today when I woke at 5 and got up as I was rested)
I have all this energy lately and today I finally figure how to do all my supplements. I just put them in the blender with goat whey, d-ribose and MCT oil and raspberries and made a delicous shake which I am enjoying right now together with the tablets which do not do well in the blender.
Now off to work to hang out with a bunch of people who now also do either hCG or high fat diets. And now, the slim ones get jealous, because they think that now they are not the special ones anymore claiming they can eat anything and not get fat.

### Huck — 2013-01-09T08:54:13+0200

### Melinda — 2013-01-09T21:33:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-where-do-you-hang-out-other-than-here.5396/post-70115>

I miss a lot of the ppl that were here before the change in format. I wish I knew where they are. I may be reading some of them but everyone who uses a handle seems to use many so I can't sort them out. I like reading "It's the Woo"s blog. She does not think highly of Dr. K but she has some interesting insights as she works nights and works around her circadian mismatch. She also lost a ton of weight and has kept it off.

### Linz — 2013-01-10T10:32:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-where-do-you-hang-out-other-than-here.5396/post-70151>

Peter at Hyperlipid does a great job of breaking down (and tearing apart) scientific trials and interpreting them differently. It can scramble the brain a bit but he has some fascinating stuff.

### maggie — 2013-01-10T15:48:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-where-do-you-hang-out-other-than-here.5396/post-70196>

I rarely understand anything more than the general drift in Peter's blogs, but I always read them anyway. His type of British understatement and dry humor is always great and a lot of the comments are useful too. I go to Dr. Deans' blog pretty often--I use it as an index to other paleo/nutrition blogs that I like.

But I also get tired of reading just this stuff and want something different, so I also check out The Browser most days -- it aggregates interesting writing from lots of different publications.

I want to try to limit my online time so I can unplug my router for stretches of of time during the day. Also trying to remember to unplug before I go to bed at night. Not much traction on either goals yet, but I'm going to keep trying.

Hi guys,
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Every time I eat or drink anything I et the following symptoms:
\-Left mid abdomen visibly bloats
\-Feel hard lump in abdomen where bloating is
\-Heart rate increase and I feel strong pulsating in abdomen
\-Stomach feels full and sickly even if mouth is still hungry/thirsty
\-Feel uncomfortable outward pressure from abdomen.

The photos included are before and after drinking 300ml of spring water during day 12 of my fast. This means nothing else should be in my system. This happens every time I consume anything.

Any help would be very appreciated,

Thanks, Sean..
