I used to see them alot in Canada. They were meant as slippers as I recall but everyone wore them outside too. They looked great with jeans.

### Destiny — 2013-01-20T10:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-71807>

I found Juil sandals that look pretty good. They are using copper conductors. I am not sure if this sufficient or not.
https://www.juil.com/grounding

How does Juil help me make this connection?

Traditional footwear, made with rubber or plastic outsoles may serve a comfort need for us, but it certainly doesn't help us connect with the earth. On the contrary, it blocks the necessary flow of electrons into our body and prevents us from heeding the earth's natural resource. By placing Energy Flow Technologyâ„¢ (compound copper conductors) directly through the footbed and outsole of the sandals, Juil offers a direct conduit between our bodies and the earth's surface. You are able to go about your day, in comfort, while heeding the earth's reviving electrons necessary to sustain healthy, stable lives.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-20T11:14:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-71817>

Maybe we don't need shoes.
A few weeks ago I came into my office and there was a man standing there looking around. I asked if I can help and he said that he was just looking around as he was attracted by the company logo on the outside wall of the factory. It says: Natures Intelligence.
We were talking a few sentences and he seemed like an ordinary person. Then I looked down at his feet and... he wore neither shoes nor socks and it was freezing cold outside. He had his car in the shop and while waiting he walked around the neighbourhood and so came to our office.
His feet were quite red and I said: you are not wearing any shoes!!! (thinking by myself that he was some kind of a beggar or something along those lines, too astonished to really think anything much in this moment)
He told me that he had stopped wearing shoes a year ago and was going barefoot summer and winter and he explained that since he did that even his ganglions were receeding. He explained that since he did that he had the most amazing changes happen in his body. Especially also to his gut. As we talked it turned out that he was a proctologist and phlebologist from Augsburg. Thats when I took him seriously (here are my prejudices)
He had to leave so my curiosity was not stilled. So last week I called him to invite him to dinner, as I want to find out more about this. He said he would call me back. If he doesn't by end of this coming week, I call again.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-20T12:36:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-71837>

> **caroline said**
>
> These all seem like good ideas ... if anyone gets some can you report back on sizing? There used to be a German brand of clogs that were felt - Does anyone remember those? I used to see them alot in Canada. They were meant as slippers as I recall but everyone wore them outside too. They looked great with jeans.

I have some Stegmann boiled wool clogs I got many years ago. Unfortunately they have rubber soles so aren't going to be good for earthing. However, I also have a pair of Halflinger boiled wool slippers which are all wool, including the sole. They don't have enough durability for serious walking but I use them when I go stand outside in the morning to get some sunshine.

### laawindy1 — 2014-02-15T04:32:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-110358>

I have found shoes from earthrunners are extremely good. They have long experience in earthing shoes. They are promoting a more grounded way of living that is possible when connected to the Earth’s electric potential of zero. If you like you can try it.
[www.earthrunners.com](http://www.earthrunners.com/)

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-15T12:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-shoes.5530/post-110386>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=71837)
>
> I have some Stegmann boiled wool clogs I got many years ago. Unfortunately they have rubber soles so aren't going to be good for earthing. However, I also have a pair of Halflinger boiled wool slippers which are all wool, including the sole. They don't have enough durability for serious walking but I use them when I go stand outside in the morning to get some sunshine.

You reminded me of pattens. Not that pattens are clogs, but there's a kind of similarity.

People used to wear these in medieval times, strapping them on over their shoes, when they went outdoors, since even in town -- or possibly particularly in town -- it could be very muddy outside in streets and yards. There's a pair in the bottom corner of the van Eyck "Arnolfini Marriage" painting:

[http://www.nationalgallery.org.uk/paintings/jan-van-eyck-the-arnolfini-portrait](http://www.nationalgallery.org.uk/paintings/jan-van-eyck-the-arnolfini-portrait)

Doubtless Giovanni di Nicolao Arnolfini (the guy on the left who looks like Vladimir Putin) wouldn't want to get whatever costly shoes he wears indoors exposed to the mire!

I suppose reflection on clogs and pattens is interesting as showing that not everyone was grounded through their feet/footwear at all times, in all seasons of the year, and in all places up to the 1960s when rubber- or plastic-soled shoes became very common. Still, I expect bare feet or thin leather soled-shoes were closer to the norm.

### Andrea — 2014-02-15T13:38:25+0200

# Thread: Pregnenolone / DHEA Side Effects
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-01-18T14:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71421>

It's so good to be back here! I was avoiding the forum for a while due to being sick, traveling, the holidays, and then not being able to log in for weeks . . . not to mention the forum was super slooow when I could finally get on, but I'm so glad to hear the forum is going back to vBulletin { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Anyway, question for all you seasoned hormone experts! I had a consult last week with Jack and it was pretty obvious that I needed to increase my DHEA and Pregnenolone supplementation - my labs were very low. I had been doing 40 mg DHEA and only increased to 50 mg last Sunday. I added Preg in at 25 mg, so fairly low doses, BUT I'm an absolute wreck lately and am wondering if it could be from those slight increases.

I just binged on an entire head of cooked cabbage and now feel like I'm gonna vomit! I haven't had cravings (and still don't really) or binged on anything for years. I have no idea where this came from. I've been eating a bit more cabbage lately than I probably should, but not an entire head at a sitting! My emotions are all over the place too, but mostly just feeling super depressed and worried about everything. There was a stabbing at a school not far from me this morning and I nearly drove to both my kids' schools to pick them up. I've spent all morning (when I should be working) searching for houses at the beach and bawling my eyes out because I'm not sure DH will move . . . but I've got this irrational, urgent desire to uproot my entire family and get us near water, yet away from society. That thought alone then makes me hysterical because I don't want to leave the rest of my family! Poor DH . . . coming home to me today is not going to be fun . . .

So do you all think this is just a temporary meltdown as my body adjusts to the slight increase in DHEA/Preg? Is this a common side effect or what?!?!

### vkiernan — 2013-01-18T14:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71427>

Hugs to you. I know the feeling all too well and it is no fun. DH will deal with it, just be sure to let him know as soon as he comes home if not sooner. This should at least ward off an argument or otherwise cuz he will know it is the damn hormones speaking and not you. I can't really help you but I wouldn't think that small increase would make you react in such a way but that is only based on my own n=1. Where are you cycle wise? Maybe early peri-menapause or just a bump in the road? Are you taking any hormones?

### BJK77 — 2013-01-18T14:31:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71434>

Thanks Vicki! I just finished a cycle; otherwise I would have assumed it was PMS rearing it's ugly head. I do take Progesterone too - 100 mg for now until my Prometrium comes. I think my labs do show early peri-menopause . . .

I'm sure DH will be understanding. He usually is, which of course tends to make me even more emotional because I feel so guilty for acting like such a crybaby! Being a woman is tough

### vkiernan — 2013-01-18T14:44:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnenolone-dhea-side-effects.5531/post-71441>

> **BJK77 said**
>
> Thanks Vicki! I just finished a cycle; otherwise I would have assumed it was PMS rearing it's ugly head. I do take Progesterone too - 100 mg for now until my Prometrium comes. I think my labs do show early peri-menopause . . .
> I'm sure DH will be understanding. He usually is, which of course tends to make me even more emotional because I feel so guilty for acting like such a crybaby! Being a woman is tough

I can tell you that sometimes when adding progesterone, my pms is at the end of my cycle instead or prior. Don't know why this is but it started when the p was added to my routine. Ride it out a few more days and see if it gets better.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T01:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pegnenolone-help.5542/post-71548>

my preg was tested on Oct 2 and was high 21 nmo1/L range 0.3 - 6.3
I had been supplementing for 2 months daily with 50mg on the advice of Dr.K.
However I forgot to stop taking it before the test. Dr. K. and my Doc both thought it was supplement driven.

I had it retested 2 months later and it is still 21 - and I did stop taking it the day before this test

So do you think I should have stopped taking for longer before the test? or it is just high and I should stop taking it?
My progesterone was very low on my first saliva cortisol test at the end of August - it was 7 \[range 12-100\] and my DHEA was low 2 \[2 -23\]
That is why Dr. Kruse suggested DHEA and Pregnenolone and Progesterone

My last cortisol/saliva test - the progesterone has come up to 43 and DHEA to 2.5

Any thoughts?
Thanks in advance

# Thread: Lamb heart?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-01-19T03:05:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71564>

How are you preparing Lamb heart? I made some heart Tartare style tonite and I am really struggling with it. I think this is going to be a problem for me since I don't really like lamb ...I know that sounds un Australian .... I really dislike the smell - altho I do like the little tiny loin chops that cost a fortune. I just remembered that a lot of the smell is in the fat so I guess I should have trimmed all the fat off - but we want the fat - right?

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-19T03:38:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71567>

Caroline, I don't really like cooked lamb much either (I buy the mince and add lots of herbs & spices). But lamb heart I find easy to eat. I just buy it in the supermarket (which I'm assuming you can do in Aus, too), slice it into thin & small pieces, put it in a dish with a chopped raw onion, garlic, whatever herbs I have on hand, sea salt, pepper & either apple cider vinegar or lemon juice. Then I let it sit for a while (or often I do it at night and put it in the fridge and eat it for breakfast). It has very little taste - certainly not strong - and is really easy to eat. And, yes, keep the fat on it - it has a soft texture and is easy to eat. Lauren

### caroline — 2013-01-19T16:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71645>

thanks Lauren - that is pretty much what I did with it - I will see how I feel about it today.

### endless — 2013-01-19T17:06:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71652>

The lamb heart I had was whole, so I'm not sure if it is prepared in any way when you buy it, but I cut off the large, hard chunks of fat near the top of the heart before I used it. Softer fat I left on...but frankly I think that if the fat is a deal breaker you should trim it off and eat the rest. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I always toss with lemon juice and onion at the least...I like Thai flavors so I often add a bit of fish sauce and red chile paste too.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T18:02:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71666>

This is the first time I have bought Lamb heart - it seemed pretty well trimmed I think - there was no hard chunks of fat - Do you let it sit for a day? I just seem to be feeling really squeamish about everything the last couple of days. Maybe I have done too much earthing and my electrons are rebelling or I am over charged???

### diane — 2013-01-19T19:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71677>

I was at this amazing raw food pet store today - they had every kind of meat, organs, bones for dogs. I saw lamb heart on the "menu" - and asked to get some. And they were out! Darn it! I do want to find a source, but doesn't seem to be the easiest thing here. Beef or pork heart, no problem. But lamb I have to keep searching!

### Inger — 2013-01-20T13:18:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71847>

Caroline, I would say cut off the fat if it is what makes it yucky for you. At least most of it. You could fry it up a bit to get it moist and eat it or use it for cooking or eat plain and raw. I would cut lambs heart thin an make a carpaccio.. it is the most delicious thing IMO! Do some raw onion and dried crispy mushrooms on the top too! And of course a good natural balsamic vinegar and EVOO and fresh ground pepper and salt.

The other pic are lambs hearts made into rolls.. I filled them with minced elk meat that I made nicely spicy, and some raw fat inside too..

Caroline if you cannot get it dow raw, try to slice it in thin pieces and fry it very gentle into medium ++.. just let it warm up and fry a tiny bit and add salt and pepper and eat. I have done that some times too and it was good too!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T17:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lamb-heart.5543/post-71894>

Thanks Inger - that is what I did with it .... but I wasn't feeling the best and I forced myself to eat it and then the next day I think I had the flu so will give myself some time before trying again

# Thread: DIY'ish grounding straps.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diyish-grounding-straps.5544/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freesia — 2013-01-19T03:28:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diyish-grounding-straps.5544/post-71566>

A simple, low budget option, for those in the US, if to go visit www.lessemf.com and purchase a ready wired earth plug which can be attached to a wrist strap.
Total cost $9.95

On this page http://www.lessemf.com/ground.html 1/3 the way down is a PLUG TO GATOR
Alligator clip on one end, 3-prong molded plug on other end. Easiest way to ground fabrics, drapes, plastics. Six feet long. Only the round lug is conneted to the wire. The 2 flat prongs are not connect to anything. Grounding Cord, 3-prong/gator 6ft (Cat. #A295-3) ...... $6.00

...You attach the gator on that to another gator....such as this one 2/3 down the same page
VELCRO ANTI-STATIC WRIST STRAP
comfortable 2 cm wide, black nylon Velcro strap
interior surface is coated with conductive carbon
completely adjustable
coil cord with 1 Mohm resistor, connects to strap with snap, stretches to 60 inch
cord terminates with banana plug or alligator clip (included)
Velcro A-S Wrist Strap (Cat. #A469) â€¦â€¦ $3.95

This is essentially what we've made at home, except we have a banana socket & plug connection instead of the two gators.
They look very fetching plugged in and hanging up on the headboard...
They work.

We checked our wall sockets first with a "power point and earth leakage meter" (approx $20 here) to make sure the earth point is actually earthed, and that the power point is safe and wired correctly. They should be (!), but Dr Mercola mentioned on the Earthing video with Stephen Sinatra, that he takes a meter with him and a number of wall sockets are not actually earthed....maybe in older buildings??
There are detailed instructions for testing printed on the meter.

I had trouble uploading pics tonight...will try tomorrow. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

# Thread: Anyone know about ingrowing hairs?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ashryn — 2013-01-19T08:34:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71576>

I have a history of ingrown hairs... On my lower legs they are not so bad, but on my upper inside thighs they seem to grow very deep, and cause hard painful red lumps (big ones.. The size of a cherry) that either last for a few months if i don't touch them, or leave big purple scars if I try to squeeze them. (I tend not to do this unless I can see something in there trying to get out.)

I have never seen a doctor about them, (though a friend had one the same and did and the doc said to just jeave it alone and it would go away) and so I have no idea if there is a name for this problem. I don' get them all the time, and mostly don't think about it since upper thighs will always be hidden from view... Except, maybe now they won't be so I am wondering what causes it and how to heal it.

Has anyone else had experience with this kind of thing? I am interested to know if it too might be healed by epipaleo and CT.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-19T10:14:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71589>

not much help here I used to get them in my armpit when I shaved. when I went back to waxing every 3wks -they stopped... same for the bikini zone/upper thighs...

just my N=1.... you might consider seeing a dermatologist if its painful or gets infected regularly... I think there are certain types of cysts that require lancing to avoid infection.

### caroline — 2013-01-19T16:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71641>

not much help here either - I used to get them on my lower legs but haven't for a long time.....but not as you describe either....

### kzimm — 2013-01-19T16:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71646>

If they seem almost like boils, painful and taking a long time to heal maybe look into this condition. Hidradentis Suppurativa. I believe it's on the spectrum of AI. This blog has some informative links.
http://www.primalgirl.com/ If this isn't the case, so sorry for subjecting you.

### ashryn — 2013-01-19T18:24:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71670>

Hey, thanks colorado.. I have read about that, and since I had eczema, it isn't inconceivable that this is the right name for my condition, (though mine is only mild) I am leaning towards the idea that everything will turn into some kind of autoimmune disease if the cause is not removed.

Since I have changed the things I do, I expect to see this occur less often (or never again!) Has anyone noticed their leg hairs grow straight with paleo and CT and so don't grow inwards any more?

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-19T21:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71715>

I get ingrown hairs on my upper, inside thighs sometimes and have a few small purple scars to prove it. My esthetician tells me it's from waxing and the hair is starting to grow in weaker, or something like that. Not sure how true that is but that was her opinion. I have never had them the size of cherries but they do sometimes get red and a bit painful. If I know it's an ingrown hair I use a lancet ( this is what was recommended to me) to free the hair and then apply melaleuca oil and then I don't get a scar.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-20T08:43:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-about-ingrowing-hairs.5546/post-71778>

Lancets are the little plastic things that attach to the device used to prick your finger when testing your blood sugar. That's what was suggested to me because they're sharp, disposable, etc.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-help.5555/post-71637>

I was trying to access my webinars on the member dashboard. There is nowhere to log in.

This is what I see there:

Lost Your Webinar Login Emails?

No problem! If you need the download links to your webinars, please enter the email you are registered with below. Ensure you whitelist [email redacted] so you never miss an important email again.

When I entered my email, it really did not do anything. In addition, I did not lose my log in email, it is not simply possible to log in there.

Any advice? Thank you!

### endless — 2013-01-19T17:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-help.5555/post-71651>

Did you get an email recently about webinar access? I got one that contains links to all the webinars I am entitled to download with my membership level. Any others you would have to buy, and then you'd go to the purchase section. Check your email and click through to the webinar you want directly from there.

### Destiny — 2013-01-19T19:20:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-help.5555/post-71678>

Yes, I got the e-mail about the January webinar. I clicked on the link and got to the same place like when I clicked on the member dashboard.
I am silver so I should have an access to the latest 3 webinars. I'll try the e-mail link again. Thanks, endless!

### caroline — 2013-01-20T02:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-help.5555/post-71761>

I am finding I need to log in on the forum page ...the log in on the home page is gone...

### caroline — 2013-01-20T02:47:38+0200

Sorry Destiny - I see what you mean..... what does whitelist mean?

### Destiny — 2013-01-20T08:51:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-help.5555/post-71780>

Free access requires email registration at http://healthactivator.com/

# Thread: Fasting???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting.5590/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-01-22T11:40:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting.5590/post-72292>

Just a word of caution.

I just had my dr appt and doctor recommended I have a vitamin a lab as I hadn't had one for several years. The nurse came in and drew the blood. Today I was notified results were in and they were 97(38-98). This seemed high compared to previous labs, so I did a little research.

Here's what I learned on the LEF site:

> Fasting for a minimum of 8 hours is required. No foods containing vitamin A should be ingested in the previous 48 hours. Take all medications as prescribed. Do not take supplements the morning of the blood draw.

I wasn't fasting, had broccoli the previous night, and I had taken my supplements including one with vitamin c. Neither the doc or nurse asked if I was fasting.

So I guess the best thing would be to ask the doctor if fasting is required before they draw the blood.

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-26T21:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-tree-hugging-is-so-smart-after-all.5594/post-72751>

### ruby — 2013-01-27T03:37:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-tree-hugging-is-so-smart-after-all.5594/post-72821>

Hi Kahunamalaku,

Polish folk medicine is heavily focused on tree energy healing. The star of the lot is birch - it's famous for great conductivity and accelerated grounding effects. Birch juice, when drawn from the trunk first thing in the spring when the tree "awakes" is thought to have great nourisment value. They sell it bottled in supermarkets but fresh is best, obviously  I guess in the past when food was scarce, it was the earliest source of plant carbs in the spring before everything else started growing.
The info I cited comes from here: [http://www.uzytkowanielasu.zafriko.pl/kat/energie\_drzew](http://www.uzytkowanielasu.zafriko.pl/kat/energie_drzew) -- try google translate, maybe it will make some sense  Otherwise google "tree energy" or "birch healing" or "birch grounding". I am sure something will come up

# Thread: Missed almost 2 weeks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/missed-almost-2-weeks.5598/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cowboy — 2013-01-22T19:25:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/missed-almost-2-weeks.5598/post-72383>

Ended up very busy with work projects and buisness projects, and a bad cold and just could not find the afternoon time for CT, so I missed almost 10 days. Got back in today and after the first 30 seconds at 51 degrees I though I wasn't going to be able to stand it for more than a minute. Got some shivers and some shakes but slowly it started to feel ok, not good but ok. Started reading and the next thing I knew 20 minutes had passed. Guess I am back in the saddle again. We also had below zero temps (not just wind chill) during that missed period, and -15 really makes you wonder about CT. Also last Friday i ended up overstressing a should due to some ridiculous heavy akward lifting. Was out of town and away from my tank so used ice packs on it. Finally after three days I regained pretty good range of motion. After the 20 minutes in the tank, it feels really like I had no injury.

# Thread: Electrical Engineer need apply,  what conducts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-23T10:25:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72496>

1\. Do all "skins" conduct for the purpose of "earthing" for lack of a better word. Bearskins? unknown leather shoes with shearling lining? What else.

Lets figure out some McGyver stuff. I did a ton of research about this time last year, as I built an "optimal" backyard space. Despite all objections I did not vapor barrier or week barrier anything and made sure all was on actual earth. But there are areas that have a sand base, and areas that are concrete blocks and some large boulders we sit on.

Is there way to "check" this stuff?

Next outdoor project - which is on slate for this summer is getting the "soaking tub" built. Since metal is so dangerous at the low temps, I'm trying to decide on something that would also conduct through the saline water. . .it will be a salt water cold soak tub lowered in the dirt. . . any suggestions/ideas/warning?

### endless — 2013-01-23T12:29:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72515>

Clay? Not sure how you'd do that, but it should work.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-23T13:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72525>

I like the clay idea just from an aesthetic standpoint. Like the clay pots to cook in. I think that would look great in your backyard with all the natural rock you have! Now the question is does it make sense from a grounding standpoint as well as the financial outlay.

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-23T14:06:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72533>

Oh yea! Clay sounds awesome. I have something to googles on now. . .

### bigknitwit — 2013-01-23T14:47:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72545>

I think pretty much all skins will conduct because they are water permeable. However, when they are dry, I think they're pretty resistive. Basically the conductivity will vary with the degree to which they are wet.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-23T15:18:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72550>

have you ever handled wet clay? It's slippery as an eel. Wyoming, in addition to sitting on a lot of coal, has lots of clay. There are locations all around the area where the hillsides have gotten saturated from rains and then slid down on themselves taking trees and shrubs along with them. Everything stays pretty much intact but the land looks folded in on itself.

Years ago they redid Big Goose Road and one day I was coming back into town and it had been raining. There's a spot that curves as the road goes downhill. It's all clay. When I hit that spot my car just started sliding ... I could feel the traction disappear. A truck was trying to come up the hill (and it's really not that much a of grade) and the wheels were spinning like mad as the truck slid backwards.

So no, I don't think I'd be using clay unless you want a slip 'n slide.

I'm curious about our house though so I'm glad you brought this up. We're on a concrete block foundation with a 4' crawl space over good old dirt. I'm wondering if we're getting any kind of earthing effect or if that 4' air space just negates it.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-23T15:20:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72551>

Forgot to address the 2nd part of your question. Concrete topped with basalt tiles?

### ashryn — 2013-01-23T17:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72563>

Get a giant terracotta planter, plug the hole, seal it to stop the water soaking into it and making it moldy?

### Birdy — 2013-01-23T22:28:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrical-engineer-need-apply-what-conducts.5607/post-72602>

If you are concerned about conductivity you can use a metal tub and cover the bottom with a mat. This way, you will be in direct contact with the water, which will be in contact with the metal, which should give a good contact with soil underneath if it is moist. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electrical\_resistivity\_and\_conductivity

# Thread: Mitochondrial Rx - results?

### Lyndra — 2013-01-23T12:48:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-results.5609/post-72518>

Anybody else following the mito rx and seeing results?

I've been taking Q10 in the ubiquinone form for many years to try to stave off migraines (with some limited success). About 3 weeks ago I switched to ubiquinol (the reduced form) but cut back my dosage to 100 mg/day, thinking it was more bioavailable and that would be good enough. After learning more about mito health, just a week ago I decided to start ramping up. For the last two days, I've taken 600 mg/day. The last 2 nights I have had reasonably good sleep, staying asleep for 4-5 hours before waking and not waking with an urgent need to pee. Compared to my usual of waking every 2 hours, this is a huge improvement. I'm also taking d-ribose and r-lipoic acid but quit l-carnitine because it was giving me headaches.

Time to order a boatload of ubiquinol to see how much better I can get.

> Lyndra I wonder if you could add the l-carnitine back in once you replenished your ubiquinol. I'm thinking l-carnitine is not good for you by itself unless you have enough ubiquinol to balance it. Just a though because at this point I have no idea if I am right or not.

This idea is interesting to me! I started ribose, then carnitine 2 weeks later, then ubiquinol 2 weeks later again. In the first few days of carnitine (at high dose), I could start to feel a positive (almost calming/more mental resilience) difference. I was excited! By the time I was 10ish days into the carnitine, I was starting to get some rather dramatic set-backs thyroid-wise (feeling very hypoT). I took out the carnitine, and the symptoms resolved. How/why does ubiquinol need to be replenished before carnitine is added in?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-rx-results.5609/post-72559>

Whoa! ...Adding 5g of that in has made me sooooo tired, some digestive upset too...and (coincidence?) I've had burning hot feet.

It could just be that some bug has knocked me, from the same time I started to d ribose (Healthy Origins brand) 3 days ago.

Curious. Has anyone else not tolerated d - Ribose - then had success adding it in verrrry slowly?

### ruby — 2013-01-23T20:25:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-catching-on-among-the-middle-aged.5611/post-72582>

Theyâ€™ve been meeting every weekend morning like this for the past two years -- never less than two, sometimes as many as 15, and their numbers are growing.
Ranging in age from 44 to 57, they head off shortly after 9 a.m., jogging down the southern side of the hill.This is no ordinary running group. In their professional lives they work for banks, hedge funds and private-equity firms. The 25-minute run is just a preamble. Soon they strip to swimming shorts, gloves and swimming caps, not wetsuits -- and dive into a pond, which on this day is at barely 2 degrees Celsius, or 36 degrees Fahrenheit. It can dip well below that.
http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2013-01-21/bankers-fund-managers-strip-for-sub-zero-swims-in-london.html

### Inger — 2013-01-24T04:37:17+0200

### endless — 2013-01-31T18:06:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-73463>

I ordered an earthing rod and wrist strap from Earthing canada, and yesterday it arrived. Here is a quick review for anyone interested.

I have been experiencing acuter back and neck pain for the last several weeks. I have sciatic pain in both legs, nerve pain from my neck in both arms and hands, and wake up (frequently!) with numb fingers. I've been using chiropractic, massage, acupuncture, stretching, CT, etc.... Last night I slept with the earthing strap on, and slept all night with no pain or numbness. I woke up feeling far less inflammation. I can feel the actual locus of my problem, in my neck, instead of my entire spine throbbing.

I'm happy, and convinced. For $50 I just took a huge leap back towards wellness when I've been shelling out $$$ for treatments that haven't been working.

### Rachelle — 2013-01-31T19:49:07+0200

### fitness@home — 2013-01-31T20:32:04+0200

Thanks for the review. We need more reviews on this thread!

### nonchalant — 2013-02-12T22:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-75602>

I just thought about our doorbell. Ours went out a couple years ago, and instead of fixing the wiring in the attic, we just installed one of those wireless bells that plug into an outlet. Wonder how much that puts out. Darn conveniences!

### shilohman — 2013-02-13T02:42:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-75640>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=73504)
>
> Krista,
>
> Thanks for the review. We need more reviews on this thread!
>
> By now, many people here have purchased grounding items. I have not...waiting for more feedback

At first I was rather skeptical, but thought I would give it a try. Here is what I did:
I bought a grounding strap from the local electronic supply store ($8) and then I took a
an unused computer power cable. I cut the two flat prongs off the end that goes into the outlet
and left only the round ground prong. Next I cut the end off that goes to the computer. I peeled back
the insulation so that the green, white and black wires were exposed. I stripped the insulation off of the
green ground wire. I put the grounding strap on my wrist and used the alligator clip at the other end to
clamp onto the bare ground wire. So for less than $10 I figured I would give it a try.

My first night I plugged the ground into the wall outlet, clipped the alligator clip to the bare ground wire and put the grounding strap around my wrist. Personally, I expected nothing to happen, it just seemed too weird to be worth doing anything. But I was wrong, I woke up that morning having slept much deeper and restful than I had for many months. The second night I figured my first night might just be the placebo effect, so I did it again and now my sleep is better than it ever has been, much deep and better rested. I wouldn't dream of sleeping without being grounded. I also ground all day while working on the computer. So that's my experience with grounding and I would strongly recommend it to anyone to try.

### caroline — 2013-02-13T02:50:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-75643>

Great to be getting people's feedback - thanks

### nonchalant — 2013-02-13T03:33:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-75646>

I got a small grounding pad on clearance from lessEMF.com, along with a plug that is essentially like what shiloman did. I tested it with a regular AC/DC/current electrical meter, first detecting the AC voltage coming from my body, trying to get to ground. I was picking up this voltage just by sitting in the room. I saw that the voltage increased when I got closer to the computer, and skyrocketed if I put the laptop actually on my lap. Then I touched the grounding pad (with my hand or foot) and watched that voltage quickly drop to zero. I also tested various outlets in my home, and found that some of them are not properly grounded.

I don't have an EMF meter, but measuring AC voltage coming from my body while in the proximity of various devices seems to be an acceptable substitute. I have used the grounding mat, plugged into a grounded outlet, for a couple weeks now, on and off during the day, and at night. I actually don't feel different. But I don't like the idea of all that AC voltage without a route to ground.

One curious note, however, I've noticed that during the night DH, who is not grounded, seems to be touching me more, while he is asleep. He will have a forearm touching my shoulder, then he will move in his sleep, and then his foot touches me. Then he rolls over, and now it's just a finger, barely touching me as he snores away. Maybe it's my imagination, but I don't remember this constant connection previously.

I'd like to try grounding soon with a dedicated ground rod, at night.

### endless — 2013-02-13T03:50:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-75650>

> Endless, in order not to have this pain/numbness you would need to wear this earthing strap all the time? I am assuming no real curing effect takes place and that you would have to wear it all the time.

Well, I do not have the problem all the time. As I said, it is an acute problem that has lasted for several weeks and during the time I am grounded, I feel a reduction in pain and numbness. I think it is reducing the inflammation in my back and neck. I assume that reducing the inflammation is going to help resolve the issue so I think it's worth it.

I like the ground rod idea better than the outlet, personally.

### shilohman — 2013-02-13T04:12:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-links.5614/post-75653>

If you are putting in an individual grounding rod and you have multiple bedrooms that you want grounded you might consider paying an electrican to put in an isolated ground to your house wiring. You would just have him put the outlets for the bedrooms on the isolated ground. An electrican would install an earth ground and then run that to the house breaker box. Then the grounds for the bedroom outlets are moved to the isolated ground bar. Probably cost $300-400 to get done. Just a thought.

# Thread: Get your Gut Tested for a $99 donation! Only 10 days left to do so.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-your-gut-tested-for-a-99-donation-only-10-days-left-to-do-so.5616/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2013-01-24T09:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/get-your-gut-tested-for-a-99-donation-only-10-days-left-to-do-so.5616/post-72642>

http://www.indiegogo.com/americangut
American Gut - what's in your gut?

World's LARGEST open-source, community driven effort to characterize the microbial diversity of the American public. Does diet matter? Only one way to find out.

The Human Microbiome Project and other microbiome projects worldwide have laid an important foundation for understanding the trillions of microbes that inhabits each of our bodies. However, opportunities for the public to get involved in such research has been limited. Now, American Gut gives you an opportunity to participate and to compare the microbes in your gut to those in the guts of thousands of other people in the US and elsewhere. American Gut is a project built on open-source, open-access principles. Our data are for the good of understanding and will be shared both with participants and with other scientists. Our experience has been that our best ideas and work come when we involve people in as many steps of our work as possible, be they scientists, educators, roofers, ultra-marathon runners or corporate leaders. Everyone has something to offer, whether their sample, their hypotheses, their analyses or their dog (yes, their dog, we will get back to that). The more we can understand the complex microbial ecosystems on which we depend, the more everyone will benefit.

See how your microbiome compares to others and learn how your diet & lifestyle may shape your gut microbiome
Our team of 30 scientists has published over 800 papers
Our team has sequenced thousands of samples from all over the world
We start mailing out the PERKs (kits) in February (note that this is a "Flexible Funding" campaign and anyone who joins will be able to participate, even if we do not reach our funding goal. Giddy up folks!)

# Thread: How much is too much (oysters)?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-too-much-oysters.5617/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-01-24T10:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-too-much-oysters.5617/post-72648>

Is 1-2 pounds of oyster meat going to do any damage? I've been eating this for a week now and my libido has gone up something ridiculous, also my workouts have improved and I find my anxiety has gone down while concentration has gone up. When I find something good though I tend to overdo it sometimes. Is 1-2 pounds of oysters for a 6'3" 200 lb young male who's active too much? And by too much all I really care about is will I develop some sort of toxicity doing this long term?

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-24T10:44:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-too-much-oysters.5617/post-72653>

I'm guessing Jack would say no amount of oysters is too much. they're natures perfect food for your body and brain. eat up... If you can clip that much down go for it! the more the better...

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-24T11:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-too-much-oysters.5617/post-72654>

If that's true then I am very excited, lol, I don't want to stop eating these at this amount. Since introducing them I've noticed significant changes and I know that jack considers them the #1 food in bg6. They hook my bowels up, and though I don't think he says this anywhere, I seem to get sleepier sooner than I used to so I'm not sure if they're helping my circadian rhythm or what. But I think he's right because I've experimented with a lot of foods over the years and if I had to pick one single food item, to have for the rest of my life, oysters would be it, hands down.

### Inger — 2013-01-24T11:47:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-too-much-oysters.5617/post-72658>

Duces, I feel the same way about oysters you do.. if I had the money I would eat only these..huh! They are energizing in such a nice way and so juicy and tasty,,,
I get me 12-13 pieces every friday and I enjoy them immensely. I am sure Gretchen is right about that you can not eat too much. Very kind of you to share your experiences!

### [email redacted] — 2013-01-24T12:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-too-much-oysters.5617/post-72666>

Inger, definitely, I like many of Jack's ideas and, considering he is a neurosurgeon, if he writes a blog and says that the best food in the world is oysters. "1. The base of the Pyramid is shellfish (oysters) other than crustaceans: Provides the most nutrient density of any food source for optimal functioning of the human brain."-bg6 then I'm gonna take his advice, lol. I tried taking his advice a few months ago by getting fresh oysters from the market near me and eating them raw, but I got seriously I'll after eating a maybe questionable set of oysters. I recovered fully in under a week so maybe it wasn't that bad but then I remembered jack saying that the worst seafood is better than the best meat so I bought some bumblebee canned oysters for 2bucks per 8oz. And while this isn't as good as the fresh thing, it's more affordable, (I'm a college student so that's Important) and I don't have to worry about catching something if my supplies is questionable since they are actually cooked. But basically everything I've come across for oysters looks positive, they have dopamine, they probably increase sex hormones more than any other single food and they have o3s, selenium and b12 in huge amounts along with iron and iodine. My stools have improved, not that they were bad before and I feel sharper, though I concede that could be placebo, even though I don't think it is. So yea, I just figured that of anywhere I could post on e net about eating oysters, I'd probably get the most constructive feedback here, and that's true so far.

### Lava — 2013-01-26T17:52:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacific-ocean.5628/post-72730>

OK, when Jack said "if you live by the Pacific and don't get in it everyday, you are a damn fool." I took his advice and have been getting in almost everyday. I am fortunate to have found a beach that has cold fresh water springs from the underground volcano caves. The springs mix in with the ocean water and create the coldest water EVER with spurts of what feels like warmth. So along with CTing there are turtles lounging on the rocks and it's all just stunning. I wish I could share it with everyone here. Anyone visiting the Big Island let me know and I will direct you to this fabulous CT beach....

signed....never a damn fool again....

### Birdy — 2013-01-26T21:58:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pacific-ocean.5628/post-72752>

Volcanoes around here, too, and not sure if that is better. I am not sure if there is a beach here where it directly meets the volcano, since it hasn't blown for just about forever.

### caroline — 2013-01-26T23:38:39+0200

# Thread: Electrons & Health... EMF1&2 - Already Discussed In Book Titled "Forbidden Healing"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thor — 2013-01-27T03:21:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/post-72814>

.
The information on electrons and E=MC2 is identical to what T.C. Randall wrote about sometime ago in his book called "Forbidden Healing"
This information has been out there for sometime now. The medical community won't listen to it - there's no money it it.

See for yourself - here is a paragraph from the book:

"Life at its core is a phenomenon that requires energy in the form of electrons to operate and to keep its structures intact. Living cells are internal combustion engines that must have the right fuel and lots of oxygen to produce energy. Visualize lifeâ€™s molecules snatching/donating electrons, electrons powering cells, electrons traveling through nerves and across fascia. When electron energy is abundant, oxygen is abundant, immunity is strong and there is health, healing and celebration."

T.C. Randall goes into the same subject matter as EMF 1 & 2.... He's been researching this field for sometime now. Really amazing information on how we can heal ourselves. Interesting how EMF 1/2 and his book overlap with similar information. This isn't new information.

Here's another great paragraph from T.C. Randall's book:

Electrons and electro-chemistry are not quackery or spooky New-Age energy medicine (yet frequency of molecular vibration, biofields, biophotons and energy waves are involved on other levels). We are talking about the electron states of lifeâ€™s molecules themselves, + or â€“ , and the fact that like boys and girls, it is the gender and strength of charge that determines molecular relationships. Molecules with available electrons donate to or â€œmarryâ€ those with electron-hungry positive charges so energy in the form of electrons is constantly being transferred and exchanged; shared in bonds, taken by oxidants, given by antioxidantsâ€¦all to make your biochemistry work and cells function. The molecular world is locked in an endless game of â€œelectron grab-ass!â€

The charge of â€œsea-waterâ€ that lifeâ€™s molecules swim in constitutes the matrix of your cell-filled aquarium and by starting there the energy of life can be raised to maintain health or restore it. An aquarium could be acidic and exhibit weak properties of polarity and charge and gobble oxygenâ€¦ or be slightly alkaline and contain an oxygen-conducive electric potential (ORP, Oxidation Reduction Potential). Electric potential relies on optimum levels of electrolytes and pH buffers (such as bicarbonate, potassium, magnesium, sodium, chloride and calcium) that when in balance hold and enhance charge.

Charge and charge reversals activate or deactivate DNA function so various enzymes get transcripted or not. Even the building blocks of those enzymes assemble according to the charge terrain within the cell. Everything takes energy, electrons.

I've enjoyed the book as it's full of research based evidence into the inner-workings of humans.

### LinD — 2013-01-27T19:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/post-72898>

I'm a believer, Thor. Strategizing on making changes/improvements for the short and long term. Too bad I am unable to compress that time. Glad I know now at 50 than later because I don't have the luxury of time anymore.

### LinD — 2013-01-27T22:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/post-72906>

Thor,
Went to Lowes to get a voltometer, and besides the fact they were out of many, I wasn't sure after all which one would suit my purposes. I want to know my exposure pre-grounding and pst-grounding to ensure grounding is effective. Can you help?

### paleo-bunny — 2013-01-27T23:50:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/electrons-health-emf1-2-already-discussed-in-book-titled-forbidden-healing.5634/post-72921>

My personal experience is that EMFs increase release of histamine from my mast cells.

I have read that air-con in office environments that dehumidifies the air in combination with computing equipment, which releases positive ions, exacerbates electrosensitivity by helping keep common allergenic or irritant molecules airborne, as these are mostly negatively charged. This explains some of my symptoms.

I also have reason to believe that an extremely unnaturally altered electrical environment interferes with the central nervous system as well as the immune system. I have very thin skin and pick up electrical charge very easily. I blow lightbulbs and trip switches much more often than most people, and kill electrical watches on a regular basis.

I was extremely electrosensitive before I had my mercury amalgam fillings replaced. Just a few minutes on a USB soft-phone would set off chronic cattarh and lowering of blood pressure via a mercury/histamine release bomb.

These symptoms peak at around 3 pm when I am in an environment with lots of electrical equipment and/or air con (aeroplanes can be just as bad as offices).

Fortunately these symptoms are much reduced and rarely problematic, now that I eat a lowish histamine diet eating lots of quercetin and have radically reduced my exposure to mercury.

However, I am fascinated about EMF 1 and 2 - as am curious as to whether they might have a bearing on my experiences.

# Thread: Ultimate grounding experience
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-01-27T05:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/post-72839>

"One group in Russia has come up with an unusual method to fight stress and beat depression by burying people alive â€“ only temporarily, though.
The organizers of this extreme treatment claim twenty minutes in a fresh grave might change your mind on many things in life.
When someone is buried alive, below the surface they are trapped with their greatest fears.
â€œOh fantastic! The sun feels much better than under the ground,â€ admits volunteer Pavel Gordeev once he has been dug out after 20 minutes of being buried alive: not a form of torture, but extreme therapy.
After his first time under the ground, Pavel's anxiety got the better of him. Now he says he can handle the pressure.
â€œThe first thing you experience is panic. Once your face is covered with earth, you start tasting it and thinking what the hell am I doing down here? But once you calm down there is simply no other place like this,â€ Gordeyev said.
The grave-digging therapists are modeling the burials on an ancient form of self-enlightenment practiced by shamans.
They wanted to make the rites more accessible, believing everyone can benefit.
â€œThis is the most effective and powerful method for overcoming internal problems,â€ claims Konstantin Mukhin, trainer at Enlightenment Territory. â€œA person can neither see nor hear anything, nor even move underground. They have no other option but to delve deep inside their minds.â€
One burial costs you around $160, and attracts both men and women from students to 50-something professionals, all aiming to bury their own worries.
A full day's psychological training claims to prepare them for the fears they will face.
â€œPanic conditions can be subconscious and uncontrollable. There are different ways to overcome them: working with mental images, rhythm, breathing,â€ explains the director of Enlightenment Territory Andrey Gorbov. â€œThere is an array of means for a person to subdue their fear. We teach them that.â€
The maximum burial is 40 minutes; beyond that the mind struggles to cope with the lack of physical function. The depth of burial is a mere 30 centimeters. Any deeper and the pressure of soil would be too great to stand.
Once under the dirt, volunteers breathe through a tube.
The organizer is a ten-year veteran of burials, and says this shouldn't be tried at home.
â€œWe have to be able to get the person out very fast and also contact them. Earth has to be distributed in a certain way across the body so that the key joints aren't pressurized. You can't practice burials without knowing about these things,â€ Andrey Gorbov warns.
Even if you have never suffered from claustrophobia, that could easily change if you are going to try this ritual out for yourself. You will be given a thick plastic tube to breathe through, with which they monitor you at all times. If you want to stop then all you have to do is make a noise.
You are unable to move your limbs because of the weight of the earth, and despite being securely enveloped in waterproof fabric, it feels very cold and only adds to the tension.
Such burials might seem an extreme pastime, but they are positively tame when compared to other death-defying stunts like bungee jumping or white water rafting."

### LinD — 2013-01-28T01:09:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/post-72937>

Ruby, I don't like this, but if it works for someone else.....

### ruby — 2013-01-28T04:28:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/post-72958>

I like the idea but don't know if I would like the experience. They only way to find out is to try

### Raiken3712 — 2013-01-31T22:23:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/post-73524>

Would be an improvement if they set it up so you could breath through your nose which apparently has health benefits from what I've read lately.

### ruby — 2013-02-01T08:25:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultimate-grounding-experience.5637/post-73625>

### caroline — 2013-02-01T10:11:09+0200

such an interesting concept - I think I would give it a go.....

# Thread: bitter melon - glucose and fat buster
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitter-melon-glucose-and-fat-buster.5651/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-01-28T04:30:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bitter-melon-glucose-and-fat-buster.5651/post-72960>

Supplementation with bitter melon (BM) slows weight gain and improves insulin sensitivity in rats fed an HF diet. The present results extend our previous study to indicate that the chronic consumption of
BM leads to a general decrease in the TG content of the major organs. Elevated plasma catecholamines and serum FFA suggest an enhanced sympathetic activity and lipolytic process. These
data are consistent with the view that bioactive ingredients in BM possess anti-obesity properties that might be beneﬁcial to body weight and glycaemic control.
http://web4.uwindsor.ca/units/biochem/web/biochemi.nsf/18e8732806421826852569830050331b/7a371e9af805f74e85256a4f00538021/$FILE/Karela6.pdf

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=73127)

NO, NO, NO!! That must be the wrong answer! Anyone have an alternative opionion?

### ashryn — 2013-01-29T13:08:09+0200

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-29T17:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-73153>

It's a lower glycemic sugar but as stated above it is still a sugar. I have found that adding my powdered d-ribose to my coffee will have a slight sweetening effect so you might try that.

### Destiny — 2013-01-29T17:34:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-73156>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=73153)
>
> It's a lower glycemic sugar but as stated above it is still a sugar. I have found that adding my powdered d-ribose to my coffee will have a slight sweetening effect so you might try that.

Patty, I know that dr. Kruse said that d-ribose is not real sugar but it has a quite a few carbs. I am eating keto so it concerns me. Do you know anything about that?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-29T19:07:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-73173>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=73156)
>
> Patty, I know that dr. Kruse said that d-ribose is not real sugar but it has a quite a few carbs. I am eating keto so it concerns me. Do you know anything about that?

It's a different carbon sugar than regular sugar and is not metabolized the same. I did a test with this on myself yesterday, as a matter of fact. I ate my BAB and didn't get a chance to eat on my lunch (I chose to walk in the Pacific instead of eating). So nine hours after my BAB I was starved. I had some D-Ribose in my purse so noshed on 5mg and waited for the typical reaction in my body when almost any type of sugar is ingested (racing pounding heart, nervous feeling, etc). I got nothing! Plus as a side note, I have been taking it now for about a month 5mg once or twice a day and I'm on another slight loosing streak (2#). Took some labs today so we shall see what happens but I think it is different.

### Destiny — 2013-01-29T20:27:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-73188>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=73173)
>
> It's a different carbon sugar than regular sugar and is not metabolized the same. I did a test with this on myself yesterday, as a matter of fact. I ate my BAB and didn't get a chance to eat on my lunch (I chose to walk in the Pacific instead of eating). So nine hours after my BAB I was starved. I had some D-Ribose in my purse so noshed on 5mg and waited for the typical reaction in my body when almost any type of sugar is ingested (racing pounding heart, nervous feeling, etc). I got nothing! Plus as a side note, I have been taking it now for about a month 5mg once or twice a day and I'm on another slight loosing streak (2#). Took some labs today so we shall see what happens but I think it is different.

That is a great idea, Patty! I will test it on myself just before having a meal. I will also try to measure my BG too.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-29T20:42:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-73190>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=73188)
>
> That is a great idea, Patty! I will test it on myself just before having a meal. I will also try to measure my BG too.

Can't wait to hear your results so keep us posted! I also now remember that Dr K said something that usually he doesn't used d-ribose in winter but that this year he was. Can't remember if I saw that or heard it. But the great way it makes me feel is worth a few carbs in my opinion, especially since I am pretty much as low of carb as I can get right now!

### Kianders — 2013-04-25T15:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-84775>

As has already been said here, it's still a sugar. But I suppose that if you have to choose between a regular sugar and coconut one, coconut sugar is better. The number of calories in it is almost identical to the number in regular sugar but coconut palm sugar is notably higher in various micronutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium etc. (see this [article about coconut sugar health benefits](http://coconutwaterbenefits.us/healthy-properties/coconut-sugar-health-benefits-for-your-well-being-and-beauty/)) I also like the taste, it's not the same as white sugar, it tastes a little bit like caramel.

### Kianders — 2013-04-25T15:36:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-84777>

As has already been said here, it's still a sugar. But I suppose that if you have to choose between a regular sugar and coconut one, coconut sugar is better. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-sugar.5665/post-84778>

As has already been said here, it's still a sugar. But I suppose that if you have to choose between a regular sugar and coconut one, coconut sugar is better. ### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-31T03:13:40+0200

Thanks Kiwi and Destiny. I will try to look for both but of course if I can find nigari it would be the best value. I asked one place but I spelled it 'nagiri'...will try again and another place.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-31T03:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-oil.5668/post-73415>

Going a bit off track here - I founds mag chloride flakes and made "oil." Can anyone tell me how to use it? I'm sure I don't want to overdo it but have no idea how much, or little, that might be.

### ashryn — 2013-01-31T12:31:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-oil.5668/post-73445>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=73151)
>
> Would anybody know the best way to remove sprayed magnesium oil from a shower glass door?
>
> Whatever I do, some always gets there. It is impossible to get it sparkly clean!
>
> Any suggestions?

Not sure where you are Destiny, but we have a product here called orange power.. It is made with orange essential oil. It is the only thing I can get the hard water stains off my shower glass with. And I squeegee the glass now after every shower so I don't have to clean it so hard.

I bet you could make your own by mixing orange essential oil with vinegar (not water, it won't mix but vinegar is like alcohol and essential oils can be mixed with alcohol if I remember my aromatherapy classes...) if that doesn't work, I would have a go mixing the orange oil with coconut oil rub it on, leave a few mins give it a scrub with a brush and rinse with soap to get the oil off..

Dunno if it would work with mag oil.. Maybe need to do some chemistry to find out whether it needs a polar or non polar solvent.

### Destiny — 2013-01-31T13:54:10+0200

>
> I bet you could make your own by mixing orange essential oil with vinegar (not water, it won't mix but vinegar is like alcohol and essential oils can be mixed with alcohol if I remember my aromatherapy classes...) if that doesn't work, I would have a go mixing the orange oil with coconut oil rub it on, leave a few mins give it a scrub with a brush and rinse with soap to get the oil off..
>
> Dunno if it would work with mag oil.. Maybe need to do some chemistry to find out whether it needs a polar or non polar solvent.

Thanks, Ashryn! I am in the States. I will look into it and give a try! I can use cleaning stuff for hard water stains too.

### ashryn — 2013-01-31T15:59:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-oil.5668/post-73454>

Just reading into mag oil.. It is just. MAg and water so hot water should dissolve it...
