# Thread: I was in a car accident yesterday
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2013-01-30T02:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73268>

The story goes something like this (as written by my husband. I'm just copy/pasting because I'm too drained to re-write it):

_"Kirsten picks the kids up from school and starts driving them north. A fresh coating of snow is on the ground and she’ travelling through a very hilly section and very narrow lane (most sections of the road have guard-rails to prevents cars from falling down the hill). As she starts driving up a hill, she notices a pick-up truck at the top that is swerving and loosing control. She only has a second to yell to the kids to hold on as the truck over-compensates and heads straight into her lane for a direct impact. The truck twists around and impacts the side door and becomes briefly airborne, landing on its side, almost upside-down.

When Kirsten comes to, she hears all the kids screaming, a good indication that they’re alive. She checks herself and amazingly is all intact. The pick-up truck driver isn’t as lucky, and while his car starts smoking and risking an explosion, Kirsten collects the kids and moves them away from the accident. When she returns to the truck-driver, he is pinned under the truck and not very coherent. By then the car behind her (a friend from school who was also driving her kids home) calls 911 and some other by-standers come to help the guy from inside the car. They managed to free him and someone smashes a side window in order to turn the car off. When the ambulances come, they take him straight to Boston. At this time we still don’t know his condition and whether he’ll make it through, but there was a lot of blood at the site and the front window of his truck suggests a direct head impact. The EMT’s take our family to the local hospital and they are cleared having only minor cuts from the broken glass and bruises.

Everyone is fine today although some are very sore in the rib-cage, Kirsten also has a big goose-egg on her leg around the shin. The cops were a little emotional, maybe the fact that so many kids were involved, and threw every charge they could at the guy. It probably didn’t help his situation that he is roughly 19, Latino and has a record. The insurance adjuster just looked at the car and said although he has seen worse, this is in his top 10%. He was surprised to learn no one was really hurt."_

I'm having a difficult time getting past the event. I was incredulous that after impact I was so intact. I had to walk by the injured driver to help my kids get out of the car. This was a calculated move, and I would do the same thing again, however his hand frantically waving is on a never-ending loop in my head. I go tthe kids secured and went and just stayed with him - nothing I could do, and didn't want to since the risk of spinal involvement was huge. But then his truck started smoking and other people were finally on scene and we needed to get him out. Once he was out I was in such a state that I had to walk away and go check on my own kids again. I can't believe we all got out with no injuries. I can't seem to relax/sleep. I feel like I didn't do all I could have/should have for the other driver. I could have secured his head in a better grip when we were getting him out of the car... I don't know what happened to him, or how he's fared. This has nothing to do with grounding, carbs, leptin, or CT, but I felt the need to share. I am so grateful to the engineers of the Toyota Sienna. My mind is having a difficult time reconciling the events.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_7679-jpg.7/)
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_7671-jpg.8/)

### fitness@home — 2013-01-30T02:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73270>

I'm very glad you and the kids are okay. You should be proud of yourself for doing what you did to help the driver of the other vehicle.

How stiff are you today? Are the kids still shook up?

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-30T03:00:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73272>

glad you're ok... you did the right thing for you and yours - you are not an emergency responder - nor are you trained to be. Its hard to be in a situation where you want to help but can't. IMO you probably shouldn't have been walking around given the severity of the crash. you could have done serious damage to your self..

focus on getting back to taking care of you and your kids...

### diane — 2013-01-30T03:02:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73273>

Yes, very glad that you're ok. That was quite a collision from the pictures!

I was in a fender bender before Christmas, and was having a hard time deciding what to do in that minor situation. So please don't beat yourself up over what you could have done - you did the absolute best that you could. You looked after your kids first, which was your instinct, and then help came along for the other driver. And I admire that in that situation you could do anything to help!

Hope you and the kids recover soon. And remember that sometimes you feel it days afterwards. That's what happened to me, so I started to see the chiro.

### nonchalant — 2013-01-30T03:12:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73274>

Bigknitwit!!! So glad you and the kids are ok.
Sounds like you did everything right to me.

When something happens and I find myself going over and over it in my mind, I've found that writing it down helps considerably, as you have done here. As you think of more details, write them down too. Finally you are no longer afraid that you might forget something, and you can relax. At least you can start relaxing. Sort of.

### Destiny — 2013-01-30T03:17:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73276>

Oh no! That is horrible. I am grateful that you and the kids got out alright. You should be proud of yourself! You were there for your kids and the no good driver too.

### RiverNeighbor — 2013-01-30T03:47:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73280>

Wow. I'm saddened to hear about your accident but it is wonderful that you and your family came out with relatively minor injuries. I hope that you are all able to take as much time as needed to recouperate.

### caroline — 2013-01-30T04:08:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73284>

I am so very glad that you and the kids are okay and I would guess that you are all still in shock. You were so level headed at the time and did what needed to be done for your family first. That was necessary ... I hope you can get some sleep and maybe you will need a little help to get past this ....

### Jude — 2013-01-30T04:21:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73287>

Sounds terrible. So glad you all survived. You really did well and I wish you all the best.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-30T04:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73291>

What a frightening incident that was and one that will stay with you for a long time. I'm so glad you and your family are relatively unharmed and hope the other driver will come through it OK.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-01-30T05:08:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73297>

OMGosh!!! BKW, I'm glad to hear you and your family are OK. This looks really like a serious accident and I see why you got out and made sure you were intact. You handled that incident with grace and charm. Must be the BABs!!!

### KiwiLauren — 2013-01-30T08:54:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73314>

> [bigknitwit said:](/goto/post?id=73268)
>
> I'm having a difficult time getting past the event. I was incredulous that after impact I was so intact. I had to walk by the injured driver to help my kids get out of the car. This was a calculated move, and I would do the same thing again, however his hand frantically waving is on a never-ending loop in my head. I go tthe kids secured and went and just stayed with him - nothing I could do, and didn't want to since the risk of spinal involvement was huge. But then his truck started smoking and other people were finally on scene and we needed to get him out. Once he was out I was in such a state that I had to walk away and go check on my own kids again. I can't believe we all got out with no injuries. I can't seem to relax/sleep. I feel like I didn't do all I could have/should have for the other driver. I could have secured his head in a better grip when we were getting him out of the car... I don't know what happened to him, or how he's fared. This has nothing to do with grounding, carbs, leptin, or CT, but I felt the need to share. I am so grateful to the engineers of the Toyota Sienna. My mind is having a difficult time reconciling the events.
> [View attachment 7](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/7/)
> [View attachment 8](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/8/)

BKW, I think this is really big. It's trauma and everyone has a different trajectory for recovering from trauma. Often people who emerge 'without a scratch' can be just as traumatised as those who are injured... and they also have 'survivor guilt' to contend with. I know from my personal experience that dissociation kicks in when you need it. It is what gave you the ability to do what you needed to do. At the same time, part of you was having huge feelings and knew the deeper meaning of what was going on. Staying with the driver was definitely NOT doing nothing. Trauma recovery is shown to hinge most directly on the supportive presence of others. You staying by his side was a way to help him not feel the overwhelming stress of going through something so terrible alone. Sounds like you were able to be a fierce mother and a kind stranger simultaneously. Well done.

It will take a while for you to relax. Give yourself time and be gentle with yourself. You were also buffering the kids (likely still are) so they can feel safe again, which takes even more energy from you. The events you are trying to reconcile really can't be - logic doesn't dictate how these things happen or what the aftermath is. But it certainly gives a stark wake-up call about how precious life is.

As those 'loops' of images return, try to relax your body and let them be. Our brains expend a lot of energy trying to make sense of what our eyes have witnessed. You are not going crazy and you will get past this. Like all healing, these things take time and are particular to the person. Don't be afraid to reach out for listening ears and warm arms, too. It is very normal (and helpful) to want to tell the story over and over again. And it is good to end that story-telling with an affirmation that you survived, you are OK, and you will incorporate this into your life's experience to help make you wiser. But right now, allow the vulnerability and confusion if you can.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-01-30T10:23:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73318>

Nicely said, KiwiLauren.

### bigknitwit — 2013-01-30T16:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73341>

Thanks all. This is a difficult one for me, and then with the repeated loop of events comes the "what ifs." And I can rationalize it plenty, because the "what ifs" are present everyday of living. However, now, they seem so much more present, exposed.

I am SO STIFF! I feel like I did some kind of ultimate weightlifting workout that simultaneously hit all my upper body at once! Even my chest muscles are sore lol! I feel as if 2+2=5. The outcome of that kind of crash really shouldn't have added up to all of us walking away. I'm ever so glad it did, but it still doesn't make "sense" to me. I'm also annoyed with the other driver, because I don't think he was wearing his seatbelt. I don't even feel annoyed about him crashing us. he just over corrected as his wheels were heading him off the road. The tables could easily have been turned in my direction. But I blame him for making the outcome so much more gruesome for himself because he wasn't wearing the seatbelt. We have pictures of his truck, and there's a circular impact to his front glass, that really looks like the point of impact of his head.

Also, there's this terrible feeling I have that I think sums up how I felt immediately after impact/awareness. My mind re-awakened into emergency mode (I was a lifeguard as a teenager, so have some basic experience handling emergencies), but all the while, I was thinking to myself, "I've just been in a major car accident, and I can't believe I'm the one in the best position to go take care of everyone else!" It's a time when you want someone there to take care of you, but there's no one, and meanwhile there's my family and a severely injured driver who needs me more than anything. I just held his hand and monitored his his vitals and talked to him while we waited for emergency services to arrive. Then it became imperative to move him away from his smoking vehicle. That's when I realised my own limits. Another man came, broke his window and turned off his ignition (which stopped the smoking). I realized I would have been unable to do that kind of a task (approach a smoking car and atempt to turn it off. I'm still angry with him that he wasn't properly restrained. Now, if he survives his injuries, he's a twentyish year old kid, with a record of theft, who is has some major medical bills and serious traffic violations (the police are really throwing the book at him). Sigh... It really feels like 2+2 = 5. While I am ever so grateful for my family's outcome, it just doesn't all add up.

### ashryn — 2013-01-30T16:16:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73344>

Get some ice on your sore bits and get yourself to a chiro tomorrow, don't wait for it to get bad... You may not be as physically unscathed as you think.

I hope this helps.. It comes from my reading on the neuroscience of addiction and some study I did on OCD a while back (turns out they are quite similar)

With the looping, don't try to talk yourself out of it, because it makes you focus on it more, and 'practise' the neural pathways.. You need to do your best to let the loop happen as it needs to then let it go and try to get interested in something else.. Your mind needs to pack it away into a place that's just a scary memory, so let it happen, but try to help it by having something else to think about immediately after the loop is done. Learn a new language, do something you haven't done before.. Give your brain some other new pathways to burn whilst its in a pathway burning mood..

You were lucky, and lucky things happen sometimes, just like unlucky things do.. You can't do maths with it.. Luck (I believe anyway) is random.. I'm glad you were lucky by the way.  I wish you a speedy recovery too!

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-30T23:30:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73392>

Bigknitwit...so glad you and your family came through this! Another gentle suggestion re what ashryn is talking about, try to keep moving and walk a lot and swing your arms. Shaking can be very helpful right after trauma but even days later movement is good for your nervous system and can help regulate some of the loops in your brain. There are some great exercises in Brain Gym program that are very useful for integrating thoughts, feelings and sensations after shock. I would think lots of magnesium baths would be comforting too. Sending you best wishes, SeaHorse

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-31T00:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-73399>

I'm very sorry to hear about the accident but am glad to see you're out of it. Take all the advice from everyone here and ice and chiro it. I wish you and your family all the best for quick and swift recovery. You have the support of people on these forums too.

### bigknitwit — 2013-02-05T02:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-74205>

Well last Friday I finally decided that I deserved to hear how the person who hit me had fared. After all, I stayed with him, then pulled him from his heavily smoking truck to a safe location, and used my kids snowpants to support his head! I nervously called his house and talked with Grandma (he's only 19). Turns out he got through the crash with "just" a concussion. He was held overnight for observation, but was back at home recovering. I am SO GLAD I called. The flashbacks have significantly lessened now that I have the ending to the story. I feel like everyday I am reliving the incident less and less - such a relief. Now to replace our Sienna - not such an easy task it turns out...

### diane — 2013-02-05T02:51:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-74213>

Glad to hear he's ok. Good for you for following up - that's not easy to do, but sometimes knowing is all you need.

### nonchalant — 2013-02-05T03:03:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-74215>

I don't even feel annoyed about him crashing us. he just over corrected as his wheels were heading him off the road. The tables could easily have been turned in my direction. But I blame him for making the outcome so much more gruesome for himself because he wasn't wearing the seatbelt. We have pictures of his truck, and there's a circular impact to his front glass, that really looks like the point of impact of his head.

We all know that 19 year olds are immortal. I think that's Dr K's biggest problem, he's trying to reach young paleo's that aren't even slightly worried about growing old.

### Destiny — 2013-02-05T14:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-was-in-a-car-accident-yesterday.5673/post-74269>

I am glad, bigknitwit, that you are feeling a bit better. Good luck with your insurance company! That is a blood pressure raising experience most of the time!

### nonchalant — 2013-01-30T04:41:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oxidative-phosphorylation.5674/post-73293>

I recently found this video on the formation of ATP from ADP:
[ATP Synthase](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=H7P4xOUPYVw)

And this one has more on the entire cellular respiration cycle, which includes ubiquinone in action!
[Cellular Respiration](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xbJ0nbzt5Kw)

Finally, more detail on the electron gradients that power the production of ATP:
[Gradients](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3y1dO4nNaKY)

After watching these, I understand it a little better when Jack says something like "We leak more free radicals at Complex I".

### ruby — 2013-01-30T15:40:18+0200

### ashryn — 2013-01-30T15:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-some-aussie-here-will-be-interested.5675/post-73340>

### ruby — 2013-01-31T04:08:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/maybe-some-aussie-here-will-be-interested.5675/post-73416>

Yep, on the surface of it, it does.  But it's a good film: [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VkdnFYDbiBE](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VkdnFYDbiBE)
The number of Americans that say "yes, I am happy with my life" peaks in 1956 and goes slowly but steadily downhill ever since".
On the other end of the scale Bhutan faces huge challenges. It remains one of the poorest nations on the planet. A quarter of its 800,000 people survive on less than $1.25 a day, and 70% live without electricity. According to the Gross National Happiness (GNH) index for 2010, 10.4% of Bhutan people were ‘unhappy’; 47.8% were ‘narrowly happy’, 32.6% ‘extensively happy’; and 8.3% ‘deeply happy’.

I went to a screening of this film and a meeting with Ms. Norberg-Hodge - very interesting and thought provoking even though I do not fully agree with her on a number of things.

# Thread: Magnetic difference between North and closer to Equator...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-01-31T07:25:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73433>

My brother just come back from a trip to Europe, quite a bit south from here, he stayed in a big city there for 3 months. He told me, as soon as he planted he's feet at the airport in Helsinki (Finland) he felt so different. Like everything slowed down. When he came out here to the "countryside" (30 km west from Helsinki) he said it was even more remarkable.

Now, I totally can relate to what he says. I always feel the same comparing how it is to live in Germany, and then up here. I have moved back and forth 2 times. People are slower, everything goes slower here. It is so weird! Or.. it is not weird anymore now, when I think about the magnetic field of the earth. Up North it is quite a lot stronger than closer to Equator. It really feels like time slows down up here. I used to be irritated why stuff/people worked so slow and not so efficient and fast as in Germany.. because there really is a difference.. It is weird to think about, really.

Sometimes, I have this weird but nice feeling time does not exist.. or there is so much time I am just swimming in it and it feels just.. wow!

Makes me realize how bad modern life are for the ones living up North. Just look at the eskimos today. So sad. Scandinavia also have really high suicide rates and mental illness and other illnesses.

### caroline — 2013-01-31T08:26:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73435>

I love your observations Inger. That is how I feel at the beach - everything is much slower. I can stare at the ocean for hours it seems - I always take something to read but never do .....I am always just overwhelmed by all the simple beauty of everything......I can't imagine being anywhere else. We spent years and years as a family going to the Bahamas and I always felt the same peace - Bliss.....whereas lots of tourists would be insulting and rude to the Bahamians because of their slower way of life. Their love of the simple things in life - family,music, their faith - just life in general turned them on - I learned so much from them and I have never forgotten.....It is very sad if you have never experienced that ......To sit on a beach and see the sun setting is pure magic...

### ruby — 2013-01-31T08:28:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73436>

I think Germany is in a class of its own
City vs countryside - certainly true. Not so sure about fast and efficient close to the equator. Check out Thailand or the Phillippines. Yes, speed (as in chaos and madness) in Bangkok and Manila but without the efficiency. Then almost complete inertia in the countryside. Not that I dislike it
Also, people there certainly know some tricks about how to calm the mind and tune into nature.

### Inger — 2013-01-31T08:45:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73437>

Caroline, I do that too! When I bike to my favorit see close to here in summer, to stay for the whole day and swim and tan I always take something to read.. but I never read it!?
Lol.. so weird.. It is like one cannot possible get bored!

Ruby, it must have much to do with the city too.. to live in a city is totally stressing, also because the disturbed magnetic field there and other things too for sure.. I felt great when out in the woods and swimming in the river in Germany too.. alone and away from the city. So even if it is decreased magnetism on the countryside you will thrive in a different way everywhere. Still, I bet there is a diference... a very small one but it is.. Hard to describe in words though.. You should come visit. Maybe it has something to do with the cold and the dark too..lol I feel the effect diminishes when artificial light and heat are present somehow...

It is weird when you start to observe things more and consciously expand your awareness. It is like a new world opens up that was always there but unpossible to sense because you were running too fast?

### caroline — 2013-01-31T08:59:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73438>

I love your last paragraph - that is what is happening to me ...a new world has opened up that was always there but impossible to see because I was running too fast...

### ruby — 2013-01-31T09:07:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73439>

Hi Inger,

the furthest north I have ever lived was in Stockholm, which for me isn't really a city. More like a big village. And boy, people there are laid back and slooooow.
I love cold and dark -- in the environment.  Heat can be debilitating and mind-numbing.

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-31T18:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73464>

I used to be irritated why stuff/people worked so slow and not so efficient and fast as in Germany.. because there really is a difference.. It is weird to think about, really.
>
> Sometimes, I have this weird but nice feeling time does not exist.. or there is so much time I am just swimming in it and it feels just.. wow!
>
> Makes me realize how bad modern life are for the ones living up North. Just look at the eskimos today. So sad. Scandinavia also have really high suicide rates and mental illness and other illnesses.

what you're describing is how I feel when I go home to Idaho... its like I'm taking off the layers that don't belong to me. I feel it as soon as the plane touches down... I also felt this way when I first arrived in Guam... everyone complained about "island fever" I never got it... I never really wanted to leave the island. all those free electrons racing up the Marianas trench - There were also specific locations on Okinawa that had the same thing - in those places you'd also see the "Ghost Armies" from WW2 as well. in celtic tradition those locations would have been sacred b/c the veil btwn the two worlds was extremely thin...

Oh this makes me think of the Pixies song Wave of mutilation...

"cease to resist, giving my goodbye
drive my car into the ocean
you'll think i'm dead, but i sail away
on a wave of mutilation
a wave
wave

i've kissed mermaids, rode the el nino
walked the sand with the crustaceans
could find my way to mariana
on a wave of mutilation,
wave of mutilation
wave of mutilation
wave

wave of mutilation
wave"

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BihjWa47WuM](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BihjWa47WuM)

### Shijin13 — 2013-01-31T18:20:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73466>

I'm LOLing about the laid back and slow... b/c the word for good day, hello and good by in Chamorro (native language of Guam) is Hafa Adai - which also translates in half a day. the work day was from 10am -2pm. if you wanted to get anything done down town. and mondays and fridays we're days we regularly had bbqs, and events at the beach - b/c that's the only place we could get work done... Life was so much slower, even with all the Japanese, and Korean tourists...

### caroline — 2013-01-31T22:49:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73532>

Sounds like heaven Gretch - why did you leave?

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-31T23:20:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-difference-between-north-and-closer-to-equator.5683/post-73541>

When we first moved to Sheridan my biggest complaint was that everyone who lived here was so laid back they were practically horizontal! I finally learned to slow down. Or maybe it's just age.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-01T00:06:15+0200

I usually turn to oil of oregano too, so I plan on starting that this evening when I get home from work. Anyone have any great tips or tricks for kicking this early? I know I should try to get some CT in and I will if my schedule allows for it. I've just got an extremely busy schedule the next few days, which is why I need to get over this QUICKLY!

### diane — 2013-02-01T01:21:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/give-me-your-best-sore-throat-remedies.5688/post-73585>

The last sore throat I had, I found a salt gargle that worked so quickly. I could barely swallow, and after I gargled, my throat barely hurt at all. I did it a few times during the day and it was gone - which was my objective because I had to fly the next day!

warm water, salt, honey and cayenne pepper. As much as you can handle. Gargle and then spit.

Hope you're feeling better!

### kathylu — 2013-02-01T02:32:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/give-me-your-best-sore-throat-remedies.5688/post-73599>

# Thread: Labs in-worried!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Freddieboy — 2013-01-31T22:58:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/post-73535>

So Iâ€™ve just got back from my appt with a senior pathology specialist about my high cholesterol.

Here are my Jan 2013 numbers:

Total 10.26(396)
HDL 2.35(90.8)
LDL 7.59(293)
TRIGS 0.70(62)
APO A 2.28
APO B 2.14

Cpr <1
Vit D 59(148)

My numbers have been around these numbers since I went high fat, low carb a few years ago.
Previously my numbers were a lot lower, though trigs were slightly higher and HDL a bit lower.

This is the first time Iâ€™ve had the Apoliprotein A & B tested, the specialist said heâ€™s concerned about the high LDL, and the Apo B numbers.

Can anyone look at these numbers and determine if I should be worried or not.

Iâ€™m male, 46, 5â€™9â€, 138lbs, very active, run, cycle or weights most days.

I eat a fair amount of fish a week, salmon, mackerel, cod.
Lamb and Beef once a week.
A small amount of lambs liver twice a week.
8-10 eggs a week.
Little dairy,a bit of greek yoghurt and raw cream.
Lots of veg with lots of butter and EVOO.
Avocadoes, 1 piece of fruit a day
Coconut ouil and milk regularly.
Macadamia nuts(50g a day) and 1 or 2 brazil nuts a day.
1 small sweet potatoe a day.
A couple of glasses of red wine a week.
Small amount of dark chocolate.
No caffeine at all, just herbal teas and water.

Thatâ€™s about it for my diet, the doc said to have 1 egg a week, no liver, no lamb(too fatty!) a small amount of EVOO, no butter, no avocadoes.
He said to eat lots of fish, chicken, veg and fruit, and not a lot else!
Now, as I said, I work out most days, and generally aim for 2,500-3000 cals a day. Thatâ€™s an awful lot of fish, fruit and veg with no fats to get to 3000 cals!

All my other labs were excellent, he said the Vit D and B were very high, he couldnâ€™t believe I donâ€™t take Vit B supplements, guess itâ€™s the liver.
He checked me over for cholesterol deposits, none anywhere. He ruled out FH.
He listened to my arteries and heart, all excellent.
He was desperate to put me on statins, but has given me 4 months to sort via diet.
Heâ€™s sending me for a scan to check for any furring in the arteries.

Any feedback.advice would be greatly appreciated.

### fitness@home — 2013-01-31T23:40:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/post-73550>

When I plugged your numbers into the cholesterol calculator I got the following for the ratios:

Iranian Equation (your triglycerides were under 100)
LDL, Calc 245
Total/HDL 4.36 (ideal =5 or lower for men)
Trig/HDL 0.68 (ideal = 2 or below)
LDL/HDL 2.70 (ideal = 4.3 or below)

p.s. my doctor wanted to put me on statins too. I said NO!

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-01T00:34:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/post-73563>

numbers look good but only tell 1/2 the story.

1st you need a hsCRP, not a CRP. 2nd you need your hormone labs so we can see the rest of the picture... you've got plenty of HDL to remanufacture your LDL - but the real question is why is your LDL so high? do you have a hormone conversion problem? do you have an undiagnosed thyroid? you need vitamin a, and T3 to convert LDL to sex hormones... w/o them.. you're just telling your body to make more LDL b/c your not making sex hormones....

### Freddieboy — 2013-02-01T12:12:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/post-73638>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=73550)
>
> When I plugged your numbers into the cholesterol calculator I got the following for the ratios:
>
> Iranian Equation (your triglycerides were under 100)
> LDL, Calc 245
> Total/HDL 4.36 (ideal =5 or lower for men)

> LDL/HDL 2.70 (ideal = 4.3 or below)

Hi

Thanks for reply, yes I agree my ratios look good, but the ratio of the Apo A&B donâ€™t look good from what Iâ€™ve read.
The doc was desperate yesterday to write me a prescription for statins, he said â€˜I really shouldnâ€™t let you leave my office with LDL that high without a prescriptionâ€™!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/post-73639>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=73563)

I will push for a hsCRP test and full hormone panel.

This T3, sorry for being thick, what is that?!

Certainly my sex drive is not what is was, so would be interested to hear about how T3 converts LDL to sex hormones.

Thanks

### Jude — 2013-05-01T02:12:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-worried.5689/post-85225>

Freddieboy, Just noticed your labs and they are almost identical to mine....
in Aug last year mine were Chol 10. HDL 1.2 LDL 8 Trigs 3.4
My trigs were higher. After 8 mths on this epi paleo and implementing most of JK's protocols as I understood them managed much improved labs this April.
My Total Chol went down to 8 LDL to 5.5 HDL up to 2.5 and Trigs down to 0.6

# Thread: Floatation Tank
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-02-02T22:40:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73837>

I'm sure I already started a thread about this, but a search and check of all my recent posts is not showing it?? Weird . . . maybe it has something to do with switching the forum back to vBulletin??

Anyway, I finally had my first experience in a floatation tank and it was amazing! Just wanted to share in case anyone is considering it since Jack mentioned it a few times in the past couple of webinars. I wasn't sure what to expect and had to drive over an hour to get to the closest one I could find, but it was sooo worth it. Instead of an enclosed tank, this place actually has a small room built with a shower that leads directly into the floatation unit, which is basically just an oversized bathtub that has a wall separating it from the shower that rises to the ceiling. Hard to explain, but it totally eliminated any claustrophobic fears I had going into it.

I really wasn't sure what I would get out of floating. On the drive there I kept thinking there was no way I'd be able to lay in it for an hour. I'm "antsy" and don't do well just sitting around. I can't nap during the day - never have been able to no matter how dark a room is or how tired I am.

I became so relaxed floating that I didn't ever want to get out! It was unbelievable. They let me stay in for an hour and a half and it felt much too short! I'm sure I dozed off for a bit. What surprised me the most was that even though my mind was actively thinking about anything and everything, it was in such a calm way that I almost began to feel like I was dreaming things. I felt so positive and peaceful. I had strange thoughts almost like premonitions. I could "see" DH being given a job offer . . . he has been looking for awhile, but nothing has panned out yet. I saw it happen though . . . complete with details of the offer. I "saw" us at the beach with the kids playing and laughing in the ocean. It was so bizarre, yet wonderful all at the same time. I've never experienced like it and I can't wait to go back!

I'm really considering installing something like this in my house. I'm guessing if I can't move to the ocean, a grounded floatation tank would be the next best thing, right??

So if any of you are considering it or even if you haven't thought about it, DO IT NOW!!

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-02T23:00:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73841>

Thanks for the glowing report...I'm waiting to try the same. There is a guy starting a business about an hour away from us, so in a few months I'll be one of his first customers...sounds divine!!!

### ashryn — 2013-02-03T00:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73861>

Float tanks are awesome! Glad you had a go in one

### vkiernan — 2013-02-03T02:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73888>

That is on my list along with the HBOT. All good things for our mitochondria. Thanks for sharing. I know there is one 40 minutes from me so it is very doable.

### freesia — 2013-02-03T09:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73916>

I've done courses of HBOT (loved it) and I've been curious about flotation tanks -- so thank you for sharing your experience BJK77.
I don't know if there is one near me or not, but it sounds like a nice project to try and build at home anyway.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-03T14:44:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-73921>

Thanks for reminding me about HBOT. I've been wanting to try that too. There's actually one very close to me at an Amish home of all places! An Amish family purchased it to help heal their son's brain injuries from a fall and apparently they had great success with it.

Hmmm . . . wondering if one of these therapies should be prioritized over another or if it's great to do them in conjunction with each other . . .

### Lyndra — 2013-02-08T18:01:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-74876>

One thought about floatation tanks - if they use magnesium sulfate and you have methylation problems relating to sulfate, it might not be a good idea. I've been advised to avoid epsom salt baths along with supps like MSM and DMSO. I checked out a couple of local float places and they both use mag sulfate.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-08T20:22:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/floatation-tank.5712/post-74903>

> [Lyndra said:](/goto/post?id=74876)
>
> One thought about floatation tanks - if they use magnesium sulfate and you have methylation problems relating to sulfate, it might not be a good idea. I've been advised to avoid epsom salt baths along with supps like MSM and DMSO. I checked out a couple of local float places and they both use mag sulfate.

I was thinking about this too as I was reading about my CBS defect yesterday . . . good thing I didn't immediately jump in and buy a tank like I wanted to. The odd thing is I felt fantastic after floating and actually slept that night. Several nights before that and several nights after (still going on) I've had so much trouble waking in the middle of the night and being unable to go back to sleep for HOURS, but that night I slept so well. I just felt so light and carefree too. Although the week right after I was a complete emotional wreck - I would imagine that probably had more to do with TOTM though than the sulfate.

I'm not sure though. I guess if I can't figure out how to deal with this on my own I'll have to get a consultation with Dr. Tim or another methylation expert. I'm still hoping after I read all of Jack's EMF blogs I'll be able to make better sense of this and take more control.

### danrosz — 2013-02-03T07:24:24+0200

Nice forum here.... Going to try
and keep this as brief as possible... I'm 28 years and had my first
seizure when I was 18 years old. No focuses... Nothing irregular.
Played drug roulette last ten years ago. Did have seizure while under
24 hour EEG in hospital two years ago... Was told that seizure
appeared to originate in parietal lobe.

Anyway, I've tried exploring everything the last ten years to get a
hold of this. Felbatol and lorazepam when aura arises has actually
been pretty effective the last 20 months. With that being said, I
still have too many auras for my liking. The leptin connection is
this... Very thin as a teenager growing up. Started lifting weights
when I was 17. Typical stuff... Bench, Squats, etc... Put on a good
amount of good weight... Unfortunately, it was almost like I broke my
appetite... As the years went on, it would seem like the the 72 hours
after lifting something was wrong. It was this hunger that I couldn't
really satisfy. It wasn't your typical hunger... To this day, I don't
reallly exercise like I used to, but when I do, there is something
broken. These weird hunger feelings will turn into auras and then
sometimes seizures. These last ten years, I knew there was something
wrong with my body besides just my brain. This wasn't normal. The
best way I can describe the hunger is that it isn't a hunger in your
stomach... It's a hunger in your arms and legs and there is tingling.
Very ood thing.... I saw an endo a few years ago and he said my tests
were fine....

The reason leptin came up was because I was reading a published study
about how zinc supplementation seems to have a positive impact on
treatment of epilepsy. Was curious as to why and it's there that I
read about how some people think zinc helps control leptin levels. A
quick Google search lists numerous articles linking leptin and
epilepsy. I absolutely think there is a link in my case.

Out of curiosity, do my symptoms sound like my leptin are drop too low
or are they rising too high? Is there a way to correct this in a way
that I could fix this via diet, supplementation, etc.? Is there
anybody you would recommend in the Philadelphia area to see about
this? It would be nice to be able to exercise, live, and have a
healthy brain and body.... I honestly believe this may be the key.
Thanks for taking the time to listen.

### ashryn — 2013-02-03T08:10:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/post-73903>

This sounds like it will be an interesting n=1. I'm betting on the after exercise hunger thing being your body stealing ingredients to make ATP.. What's your exposure to emf like? How about sleep?

### danrosz — 2013-02-03T16:58:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-epilepsy.5716/post-73947>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=73903)

> This sounds like it will be an interesting n=1. I'm betting on the after exercise hunger thing being your body stealing ingredients to make ATP.. What's your exposure to emf like? How about sleep?

Not well-versed in emf's but this hunger can get worse when reading off of cell phone computer screens. Just figured that was unrelated to leptin and was part of parietal love issue. You google leptin and parietal lobe and there are some links. Have no idea how much of a role all this plays but I know it plays a big one.

Sleep is not great. Mostly due to felbatol.

### danrosz — 2013-02-03T17:02:03+0200

# Thread: Always look at the bright side of life :-)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-02-04T12:11:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/post-74054>

Benefits of inefficient healthcare: economic growth and young population -- Partly because of inefficient health care, the United States is aging much less quickly than countries like Japan and Germany, says Niall Ferguson in NYT. By 2050, according to the UN, more than 33% of Japanese, 31% of Germans and 25% Chinese will be 65 or over. For the US the figure will be just 21%.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-04T15:15:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/post-74061>

Ok.. I saw the title to this and could help thinking of monty python... LOL!

### nonchalant — 2013-02-04T16:09:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/post-74070>

Yeah, and a younger population doesn't remember 'the way things used to be done'. You lose a lot of cultural information.

### ruby — 2013-02-07T06:27:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/post-74590>

yes, like this
When, at a store checkout, the young cashier suggested to the older woman that she should bring her own shopping bags in future because plastic bags weren't good for the environment.
The woman apologised and explained, "We didn't have this green thing back in my earlier days”.
The cashier responded, "That's our problem today. Your generation did not care enough to save our environment for future generations”.

She was right, our generation didn't have the green thing in its day. Back then, we returned milk bottles, pop bottles and beer bottles to the shop. The shop sent them back to the plant to be washed and sterilized and refilled, so it could use the same bottles over and over. So they really were recycled. We refilled writing pens with ink instead of buying a new pen and we replaced the razor blades in a razor instead of throwing away the whole razor just because the blade got blunt. But we didn't have the green thing back in our day.

We walked up stairs, because we didn't have an escalator in every shop and office building. We walked to the shop and didn't climb into a 300-horsepower machine every time we had to go two streets. But she was right. We didn't have the green thing in our day.

Back then, we washed the baby's nappies because we didn't have the throw-away kind. We dried clothes on a line, not in an energy gobbling machine burning up 2200 watts. Wind and solar power really did dry our clothes back in our early days. Kids got hand-me-down clothes from their brothers or sisters, not always brand-new clothing. But that young lady is right. We didn't have the green thing back in our day.

Back then, we had one TV, or radio, in the house, not a TV in every room. The TV had a small screen the size of a handkerchief (remember them?), not a screen the size of the county of Yorkshire. In the kitchen we blended and stirred by hand because we didn't have electric machines to do everything for us. When we packaged a fragile item to send in the post, we used wadded up old newspapers to cushion it, not polystyrene or plastic bubble wrap. Back then, we didn't fire up an engine and burn petrol just to cut the lawn. We used a push mower that ran on human power. We exercised by working so we didn't need to go to a health club to run on treadmills that operate on electricity. But she's right. We didn't have the green thing back then.

We drank water from a fountain or a tap when we were thirsty instead of demanding a plastic bottle flown in from another country. We accepted that a lot of food was seasonal and didn’t expect to have out of season products flown thousands of air miles around the world. We actually cooked food that didn’t come out of a packet, tin or plastic wrapping and we could even wash our own vegetables and chop our own salad.
But we didn't have the green thing back then.

Back then, people caught a train or a bus and kids rode their bikes to school, or walked, instead of turning their mothers into a 24-hour taxi service. We had one electrical socket in a room, not an entire bank of sockets to power a dozen appliances. We didn't need a computerised gadget to receive a signal beamed from satellites 2,000 miles out in space in order to find the nearest pizza place.

But isn't it sad the current generation laments how wasteful we oldies were just because we didn't have the green thing back then?

Please forward this on to another, selfish old person who needs a lesson in conservation from a smart-ass young person.

### caroline — 2013-02-07T08:43:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/post-74598>

I love that Ruby - thanks for sharing. I am old enough to remember all that stuff ....

### ruby — 2013-02-07T10:03:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-look-at-the-bright-side-of-life.5730/post-74602>

### Destiny — 2013-02-04T14:07:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-we-go-again.5731/post-74056>

Vegetarians Have Low Heart Disease Risk

Going meatless gives vegetarians a 32 percent lower heart disease risk than non-vegetarians, a British study found, offering further proof that eating meat can be hazardous to health.

The study, published in The American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, involved 44,561 people enrolled in the European Prospective Investigation into Cancer and Nutrition (EPIC)-Oxford Study, which began in England and Scotland in 1993. Researchers sought to compare a range of diets and their impact on overall health, and 34 percent of all participants were vegetarians."It's a very good study," said Dr. William Abraham, who directs the division of cardiovascular medicine at Ohio State University, noting the large proportion of vegetarians. "It's further evidence that vegetarian diets are associated with a lesser risk of developing ischemic heart disease or coronary artery disease."

[http://gma.yahoo.com/blogs/abc-blog...k-study-finds-110046252--abc-news-health.html](http://gma.yahoo.com/blogs/abc-blogs/vegetarians-lower-heart-disease-risk-study-finds-110046252--abc-news-health.html)

### fitness@home — 2013-02-04T15:06:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forum-connectivty-issue-website-currently-unavailable-message.5734/post-74060>

Posting this question quickly due to issues getting on this forum. Figure I better ask while I can...

For the past week I have had intermittent connectivity issues for this forum. It is hit or miss. When I can't get on I will get a message the says "this website is currently unavailable".

Has this happened to anyone else? Could it possibly be a heavy traffic issue on this site?

We have no problem with any other website while surfing at home. I have also tried two other vbulletin forums and can always connect to them. Cleared cache, rebooted router, rebooted computer, laptop, and ipad. Logged out of and back into this forum when i had connectivity.

This has happened on our home Internet, my mobile hotspot, at library on their computer, and Starbucks wifi.

Have already started a ticket for this issue. Just wondering if other experienced the same issue and what you did to rectify it.

Thanks

# Thread: When you're reacting to something you've eaten...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-youre-reacting-to-something-youve-eaten.5742/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2013-02-04T21:08:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-youre-reacting-to-something-youve-eaten.5742/post-74115>

When you've eaten something and you're reacting to it, do you have any ways to speed through/help the reaction process? When I eat something that I react to, I usually notice within 3-4 hours. I start getting extra thirsty. Then the following morning my weight is up 1.5-2 pounds, and my eyes are swollen, sometimes my wrist hurts. This all seems to sit steady for 2 days, and then on the 3 day, the weight is off, and the symptoms pretty much resolve. When it's a bigger reaction (white rice), I get bloated within an hour or 2 of eating, stomach upset that lasts 24ish hours, and the weight gain/swelling holds for 6 days, and more joint pain. It would be nice to find a way to reduce this process.

There are so many foods that I react to, and I avoid them strictly once I confirm them, but there are hazy ones (the grass-fed roast beef was fine, but the grilled conventional steak wasn't. Is garlic really what set off that reaction? Sometimes I think high heat cooking of foods causes me problems (the sauteed onions added to the bone broth?). Sometimes it happens and I don't know what the culprit was (cross-contamination?).

Have you found something that helps the reaction resolve sooner, or helps lessen the reaction?

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-04T21:17:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-youre-reacting-to-something-youve-eaten.5742/post-74119>

CT has helped me when I've had swelling, histamine type reactions. Drinking lots of water and taking lots of enzymes have helped, but eating really simply has been the biggest relief. Not much help I'm afraid, but I would try the CT unless that triggers detox.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-04T22:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-youre-reacting-to-something-youve-eaten.5742/post-74143>

I second CT helping. If it's a histamine reaction, I'm also finding quercetin to work well. Activated charcoal or clay are great too depending on what the contaminant is - if I ever get glutened (God forbid), I'd be reaching for one of those STAT!

### caroline — 2013-02-04T23:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-youre-reacting-to-something-youve-eaten.5742/post-74154>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=74119)
>
> CT has helped me when I've had swelling, histamine type reactions. Drinking lots of water and taking lots of enzymes have helped, but eating really simply has been the biggest relief. Not much help I'm afraid, but I would try the CT unless that triggers detox.

for me eating simply has been so much better - and eating mostly raw. I feel so much better after eating raw steak but if I cook a steak - even lightly - I notice a huge difference in digestion and bloating and how I feel.

# Thread: Pg/E2 Ratio = 433
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-e2-ratio-433.5745/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-04T23:02:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pg-e2-ratio-433.5745/post-74149>

Can anyone believe that I have that great of a ratio. But.....before anyone goes to congratulate me I must explain. My estrogen last year was over 500! This was not where a 50 yr old should be. So I worked for a year on getting it down and just last Nov it was 61. Now it is so low (<6) I basically think I am no longer a female! But in relation to my Pg it looks good. LOL I started the whole hormone journey with a ratio of .87 so I've come a long way, baby!

This hormone balancing takes such play. First I have to get my doc to give me enough of the Pg. Not an easy task. Then I have to make sure I am detoxifying it the proper way. Apparently there is a methylation issue involved but I need to go back and re-listen to the webinar to figure out which gene that was. Not sure if I have the risk allele or not for that specific gene.

I just started to use small amounts of the Biest and will monitor that. My DHEA sucks as does my sleep lately. When my DHEA was around 350 I slept and felt great. Now it dropped to 97 and I wake 3-4 times a night and then get a hot flash. So I'm back on the full on DHEA plus a little 7-keto thrown in to get that result back up to 350ish. My problem is finding that optimal dose after I am at the optimal lab result. Does anyone have any ideas on how to figure that optimal dose? Say like drop the building dose down 25% or something like that?

# Thread: Choosing a lab for salivary tests and ordering tests
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/choosing-a-lab-for-salivary-tests-and-ordering-tests.5751/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-05T04:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/choosing-a-lab-for-salivary-tests-and-ordering-tests.5751/post-74228>

I have discovered that its possible to get salivary tests done here in Australia without a doctor's prescription.

Is there anything I should know about when choosing a lab?

Which salivary cortisol tests do I need? One, two, three or four in one day?

Are the estrogen and estradiol tests two different ones?

I'm post menopausal - does this make a difference to which tests I order?

At the moment I'm thinking of
An early morning and late afternoon cortisol
estradiol
testosterone
progesterone
DHEA

Do I need pregnenalone?
Anything else?

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-05T04:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/choosing-a-lab-for-salivary-tests-and-ordering-tests.5751/post-74229>

I have also read that salivary tests for sex hormones aren't as good as the serum tests according to peer reviewed literature. Has anyone got any thoughts on this?

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-05T05:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/choosing-a-lab-for-salivary-tests-and-ordering-tests.5751/post-74238>

> [Aussie Nana said:](/goto/post?id=74228)
>
> I have discovered that its possible to get salivary tests done here in Australia without a doctor's prescription.
>
> Is there anything I should know about when choosing a lab?
>
> Which salivary cortisol tests do I need? One, two, three or four in one day?
>
> Are the estrogen and estradiol tests two different ones?
>
> I'm post menopausal - does this make a difference to which tests I order?
>
> At the moment I'm thinking of
> An early morning and late afternoon cortisol
> estradiol
> testosterone
> progesterone
> DHEA
>
> Do I need pregnenalone?
> Anything else?

For salivary cortisol, get a 4-panel one (taken 4 times over the 24 hours) and a melatonin (which is taken at the same time as your midnight-ish cortisol).

I have used blood for the sex hormones, but also saliva (and I got wildly different results with test taken on the SAME day).

Estradiol is E2 (estrogen 2)... you can also test for E1 and E3 (estiol and estrone), especially if you are worried about E dominance and/or how you're metabolising E.

You might also want to get Testosterone and SHBG.

What lab did you find in Aussie?

# Thread: Raw Milk vs pasteruized milk for Seahorse

### Glamazon — 2013-02-05T15:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-milk-vs-pasteruized-milk-for-seahorse.5754/post-74281>

Dr. K asked that I post this article for you that I posted on Facebook.

This is why I drink goat's milk instead of cow's milk, because of the BCM 7 in most cow's milk: "Histidine (the mutated protein), on the other hand, only weakly holds on to BCM 7, so it is liberated in the GI tract of animals and humans who drink A1 cow milk. Now, BCM7 is a powerful opiate that can have a very detrimental impact on your body."[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/dr-mercola/dairy-free-avoid-this-pop\_b\_558447.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/dr-mercola/dairy-free-avoid-this-pop_b_558447.html)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-milk-vs-pasteruized-milk-for-seahorse.5754/post-74328>

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-06T01:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-milk-vs-pasteruized-milk-for-seahorse.5754/post-74353>

LOL, nice theory if the yogurt consumption hadn't been preceded by a week of strong cravings and 6 months of ever-increasing cravings. You may well be right about it being aimed for me, though.

I love dairy. Love love love love love. But she ain't meant for me.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-06T01:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-milk-vs-pasteruized-milk-for-seahorse.5754/post-74356>

There are things I 'question' and get a bad attitude about, but at least I know for CERTAIN that I don't do well with dairy (even I limit butter sometimes). It is at least ONE thing I get immediate feedback from which motivates me to not eat it. I don't eat it. I experimented in December with yoghurt and kefir (not even b/c of a craving, did not have a desire for these things) and I am not sure if I was going down hill or something anyways but I really think it wreaked extra havoc with me with GI sx and bloating and REALLY bad swelling in the joints that was painful to bend! I'm 27, lol.

There are other certain 'bad' foods that I 'tempt' fate with on occasion and I don't see/notice any bad benefits...so it is harder to 'play nice'. But I convince myself (obsessed actually) "I want to be 90 yrs old with NO diseases and be super active etc". I don't know, but after being vain about my current body composition the number 2 thought for me is thinking about my old age and how it better damn well be spent in perfect physical condition and perfect cognitive ability. lol So in other words I got the 'front end' (vain body appearance) balanced with the 'back end' (long term health into old age). lol

# Thread: Cool dream
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cool-dream.5769/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-06T16:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cool-dream.5769/post-74434>

I had a cool dream last night.

In it I knew I had to be ready for a concert for 3pm with a choir in which I was singing a duet with someone. lol

Then about 1:30 pm (in the dream of course) I decided to go to this potluck which had many folks from this forum LOL. Also it was at Jack's place (but in my area where I live). I didn't see Jack in the dream b/c he was in some other room but I believe I did see Misty and Sandy in addition to many others. So we were just hanging out with a potluck of good foods.

The time was going by quickly and it was already 2:30. I was like 'yikes' I got to get to my concert. Fortunately this potluck was 'down from my house'. So I ran home, got changed and I would have ran to the church (also at end of street \*and in real life it is at the end of my street') but I ended up getting a ride.

Dream fades and I didn't dream about the concert, just instead it was implied that I made it on time for the concert with no problems. LoL

Anyone else have dreams about Jack and/or people from this forum? Maybe I'm spending too much time here? lol

p.s. That is the second good dream in a few nights that I had and also was able to recall. So I am glad for this! I believe being able to dream and also if the dream is good and also if you can recall is a good sign of health (in some form or other). I hope this keeps up for me.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-06T16:20:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cool-dream.5769/post-74435>

It's kind of surprising that I haven't, b/c I have all kinds of bizarre dreams about everything in my life mashed together in a totally nonsensical way. Always have!

# Thread: An apology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-apology.5773/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-06T19:21:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-apology.5773/post-74453>

I feel it necessary to post a formal apology since perhaps what I have been writing lately could be very mis understood and be viewed as either inappropriate and or rude.

I do not want to be offensive or inappropriate and I am not in this to 'bash' anyone. I am very grateful for finding this site last January and I have benefited greatly from the protocols even if I made personal errors and screw ups based on lack of knowledge and/or lack of labs to help navigate.

I don't have any problem with Jack's concepts and I really do think he's out to help people. If I say anything that might be interpreted as bashing him it is not meant that way. There are certain idiosyncrasies and/or behaviours or remarks he may say that DO rub me the wrong way, but I am willing to bet that his intentions are only for the good.

I don't want to offend others and/or imply that I'm telling anyone that they 'can't' recover or anything like that. Again I was feeling frustrated with some en masse down hill turns in health which also included me so I was questioning things in a pissy mood.

So we are all human and we can move on and forgive.

I just ask that perhaps for JK if I or others ask a question which is not intended to compare our lives with yours that you don't respond with "If you think YOUR life is stressful well MINE is SOOOO much more stressful etc" When the question did not suggest or imply such comparison in the first place. I don't try to compare my life to anyone else's or see who has it better or worse. If I am having a rough time and it is minor compared to someone else, well the emotions are pretty much the same and I try all my best to overcome it but of course sometimes human mistakes make it harder and/or it gets expressed innappropriately (for which then has to be followed up in an apology such as this one lol).

So I hope that maybe some misunderstandings can be cleared and that I am cheering for everyone on here b/c we're all here for optimal health for our personal lives.

Thanks all!! And thanks for all those who have been supportive and encouraging to me lately (you know who you are)!!

Cox (Digital) Channel 135

# Thread: Website Changes--Please Note
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-changes-please-note.5798/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2013-02-08T04:27:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-changes-please-note.5798/post-74770>

Hi all,

Just thought I'd pop in to offer a heads-up on changes to the site. Most of you have already received this news via email, but I thought I'd pop in here to post an update.

Two major changes and a couple notes:

1) **No Member Dashboard**. In lieu of the Dashboard where members login and access webinars and sign up for consults, we have transitioned to an email-based system to deliver all your member content and benefits to you via email. Please make sure [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) is on your safe list and save the emails we send you, as they include links to download your content, sign up for consults, etc.

We are adding a "Member Portal" section to the site shortly that will include links for you to be able to sign up for consults and email lab consults if you are a member... That info is in the emails we sent you, however, so refer to them in the meantime for links, etc. to sign up.

Questions or issues? Email [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) or open a ticket: [http://jackkruse.zendesk.com](http://jackkruse.zendesk.com)

2) **Forums are back**! We've brought back the vBulletin forums, as you all requested, so you can access them with tapatalk. Glory be! We are going to add a banner here with links to the menu shortly. If you can't remember your password, go here and follow the prompts: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/login.php?do=lostpw](http://jackkruse.com/forum/login.php?do=lostpw).

If you still can't login, you know the drill: Email [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted]) or open a ticket: [http://jackkruse.zendesk.com](http://jackkruse.zendesk.com).

**The Optimal Reset!** We are several weeks in to the Optimal Reset now. I hope ya'll are enjoying it! this program isn't perfect. It's brand-spanking new and we are using this run to help iron out all the kinks. So please be patient with us. We are letting everyone in the community participate at no charge, and Dr Kruse is donating his time to do complimentary Q&As to support you guys as you're going through it. I hope ya'll are finding the structured program to be helpful and are enjoying supporting each other! Hop on over to the Optimal Reset forum for more help and encouragement.

We are going to be launching a newsletter soon... You guys have any ideas for a name? We'll use it to recap activity on the site, share upcoming news and events, and to do some special contests. Comment here if you have some ideas for us!

I know the last four months have seen a lot of changes, bumps and bobbles. If there's one thing we've learned, it's that you can't please everyone! I think we've about settled into "this is the way it's going to be," at least for a while. Never-ending change is hard on everyone. We've tried out so many of your suggestions and are grateful for each one of you who took the time to share your thoughts, ideas and experiences. Collectively, we've all tried hard to make the site better and to create more community. Jack has been on a roll with killer content this year, and we are about to start doing a push to spread some of this great information around the interwebs.

If you are experiencing egregious usability issues, I want to hear from you... Please open a ticket. If you wish the dashboard were back, or we would do some things like we used to, all I can say is we need to settle in and focus on some other things that I really feel like you're going to love! I hope you can make the site work for you. The most important thing is content and community, and we have that in spades.

Happy New Year (a little late).

Misty

# Thread: Tallow for the skin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rachelle — 2013-02-08T22:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-74925>

Thought this might be the place to post my favorite skin softener. No other oil makes my skin come alive.

I use grass-fed beef tallow, real olive oil, and essential oils. I noticed a difference in my skin the moment I used it on my rough dry hands. Honestly, if all else fails try it. Oh and it is cheap to make.

### Sharon — 2013-02-08T23:07:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-74928>

vintagetradition.com
Nice stuff but pricey.
Cheaper to make

### Rachelle — 2013-02-08T23:41:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-74935>

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-10T05:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75176>

> [Rachelle said:](/goto/post?id=74925)
>
> Thought this might be the place to post my favorite skin softener. No other oil makes my skin come alive.
>
> I use grass-fed beef tallow, real olive oil, and essential oils. I noticed a difference in my skin the moment I used it on my rough dry hands. Honestly, if all else fails try it. Oh and it is cheap to make.

Wonderful idea. Two questions: 1) can you use the fat that emerges on the top of home made bone broth after it's put in the fridge? 2) could you use duck fat? Thanks!

### Kiera2 — 2013-02-10T07:17:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75185>

I would Lauren, but first I'd render it. Once rendered, it will keep for a long time, and you don't need to refridgerate.
Scoop the fat off the broth (easier when it's cooled down), then slowly simmer the fat in a pan over a low flame. Just the same as making ghee; any solids will fall to the bottom, the water evaporates, and you're left with clear fat. Pour it into a jar, and it will become quite solid when cool.

You probably already know that, but I thought it might be useful info for someone else.

### KiwiLauren — 2013-02-10T07:47:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75188>

> [Coriander said:](/goto/post?id=75185)
>
> I would Lauren, but first I'd render it. Once rendered, it will keep for a long time, and you don't need to refridgerate.
> Scoop the fat off the broth (easier when it's cooled down), then slowly simmer the fat in a pan over a low flame. Just the same as making ghee; any solids will fall to the bottom, the water evaporates, and you're left with clear fat. Pour it into a jar, and it will become quite solid when cool.
>
> You probably already know that, but I thought it might be useful info for someone else.

Thank you. And, no, I did NOT already know that. I'm going to give it a try!

### Rachelle — 2013-02-10T17:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75222>

@KiwiLauren..Here is a good site I used when I rendered my beef fat. She gives very good advice. [http://www.theprairiehomestead.com/2012/02/how-to-render-beef-tallow.html](http://www.theprairiehomestead.com/2012/02/how-to-render-beef-tallow.html)

I wanted to say also that those who live in dry climates would need to spray water on body first before applying any moisturizers. Nothing happens when there is no humidity to absorb cream. I really dislike dry climates and winters that are dry. Have always felt better in humid climates.

Any other ideas?

### [email redacted] — 2013-02-10T18:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75226>

ok, so tallow is one oil I've not incorporated into my beauty stuff! R - did you filter it? One of my problems is the smell during processing - and it makes such a mess to cook with. I did a batch last summer - but haven't been inspired to go that route again, although for creams etc I might consider it.

### Inger — 2013-02-10T18:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75228>

I am going to ty this tonight as my expensive argan oil have to be day moisturizer only as it is quite expensive.. Seaweed water on skin, on the top tallow.. yay. I am curious if all my wrinkles are gone in the morning.
I will be reporting..

### [email redacted] — 2013-02-10T19:01:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75230>

also if any of you actually cook with the tallow, how do you clean it up?!? That was the biggie that I noticed - it seemed to fly all over and was super hard and I ended up spending way more time cleaning my kitchen that I was comfy with.

Of course, that IS the function of my grill/oven these days. I do almost all my "grease spatter" stuff in oven (self cleaning cycle to the rescue!) or grill. The only people the grill bothers are the neighbors. . .

### diane — 2013-02-10T21:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75260>

I found this while looking into making moisturizer - how to make tallow in the slow cooker. Sounds like it might be less messy. I'm really interested in trying this - will have to find some fat first!

[http://friskylemon.com/2013/02/07/tallow-a-long-lost-fat-how-to-render-tallow-in-the-slow-cooker/](http://friskylemon.com/2013/02/07/tallow-a-long-lost-fat-how-to-render-tallow-in-the-slow-cooker/)

### Lyndra — 2013-02-10T22:25:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75273>

I've done both the wet and dry methods of rendering. I found the wet method to be more foolproof in terms of avoiding burning. I also found grinding up the fat first to be far more effective than just dicing, as it melts at a more uniform pace when ground. One bit of advice - don't do this if you aren't feeling good as the smell may really get to you. I did it the day after being glutened when I was sick to my stomach and I now have unhappy memories associated with lard.

### [email redacted] — 2013-02-10T23:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75280>

I 2nd the bad smell. I did it with the crockpot on the back patio so only the neighbors had to smell it!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-11T04:41:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tallow-for-the-skin.5810/post-75355>

I always get some fat rendered off the bones when I roast them in prep for my broth. I took some tonight and rubbed it on my leg to see how it would feel after it was absorbed. I don't like a greasy feeling on my skin. It actually felt pretty good. So I am going to try and get a concoction pulled together this week with some nice smelling oils. I have practically every essential oil in my cupboard. Thanks for the post!
