# Thread: I still have my appendix and this is why it is a good thing to have.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-still-have-my-appendix-and-this-is-why-it-is-a-good-thing-to-have.5938/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2013-02-19T05:44:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-still-have-my-appendix-and-this-is-why-it-is-a-good-thing-to-have.5938/post-76418>

[Appendix good.](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/02/14/appendix-evolved-over-30-times_n_2685499.html?utm_hp_ref=fb&src=sp&comm_ref=false)

In 2007, Parker and his colleagues suggested that the appendix has an immunological role, acting as a "safe house" for beneficial gut bacteria. These bacteria help train the immune system and can prevent diseases by out competing dangerous pathogenic bacteriaâ€”but there are times when the dangerous microbes gain the upper hand and overrun the gut. The researchers reasoned that when this happens, the beneficial bacteria could retreat to the safety of the appendix, which remains unaffected. Once the immune system has beaten the infection, the beneficial bacteria emerge from the appendix to quickly recolonize the gut.

# Thread: Amazing survival?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-survival.5944/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-02-19T09:38:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-survival.5944/post-76431>

I just reread this blog
[http://http://www.jackkruse.com/do-food-electrons-impart-a-quantum-effect/](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/do-food-electrons-impart-a-quantum-effect/)

and found this quote.. and I got quite shocked..

> A human can live without food for 30 days. They can survive for without water for seven days. But the human brain cannot do without oxygen for 4 minutes or it dies.

Hubby had an accident about 11 years ago where he got shot and the bullet went through he's heart, the aorta, one lung.. he lost so much blood and the surgeon that operated on him for many hours told us later he's brain was 7 minutes without blood (??) and it was a amazing wonder he survived. They checked he's brain after, but all was good and no damage! Now I know, it was a real wonder! Wow!!!
It was in winter, and it was cold.. and it was 7 PM and dark.. it happened outside in the street. I wonder if all this had anything to say?

He even run about 20 meters after he got shot, he told me he wanted to run to a street lamp to check out where the bullet hit him.. The surgeon said after such a shot you fall down immidiately and do not run. The surgeon also told me the shock people get from such a experience helps the survival, and you can measure if this shock happened, he explained how but I do not remember.. but he told me the weird thing is, hubby didn't even had this shock reaction..?

It was an amazing suregon, a amazing personality for sure, a man with a huge heart.

I wonder if hubs is an animal..lol He sure survives the weirdest things.

I do remember how I visited him every single day and I told him to stay (he was in coma 2 weeks in between life and death connected to a breathing machine and other weird stuff) and not die and I crawled in he's bed and that was certainly not allowed as it was in the intensive care unit and I had to use hospital clothes and shoes all but the doctor never said a thing..lol Maybe I gave hubs electrons and the doctor knew that and that is why he let me do..

### caroline — 2013-02-19T09:42:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-survival.5944/post-76432>

That is an amazing story Inger ......... I think there are a lot of things that humans can't explain .....that defy all the odds.....

### HoneyChild — 2013-02-19T12:11:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/amazing-survival.5944/post-76442>

I don't think I can post links on this forum but do a search for "Surgeons use cold to suspend life" and you'll find a link to the BBC by Dr Kevin Fong. I watched his documentary when it was on TV a few years ago. I really wish I could watch it again as it was all about the effects of cold on the body. I'm pretty sure Wim Hoff was the guy featured on it too.

"Some 11 years ago, Anna Bagenholme, a 29-year-old skier, fell through ice covering a ravine in Norway.

Her heart stopped for more than three hours and her body temperature dropped to 13.7C (56.7F).

This is the longest and coldest cardiac arrest on record and that she survived at all is nothing short of miraculous."

# Thread: My n-1 with a grounding sheet
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-with-a-grounding-sheet.5962/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-02-20T15:20:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-with-a-grounding-sheet.5962/post-76648>

I've been somewhat testing out the use of a grounding sheet the last few weeks and I'm a little concerned with what I'm finding. I know I read on here (\*I think) that someone mentioned the grounding mats and sheets may just spread out dirty electricity if you're plugging them into a grounded outlet, which is what I'm doing.

I have a half sheet on my bed and we've been turning the breakers off for the bedrooms at night, so of course there's no electricity through the outlets and the sheet should be naturally grounded, right? I definitely can't say I'm sleeping any better or see any improvements, BUT when DH turns the breaker back on in the AM and I stay in bed for a little longer I am noticing a very uncomfortable, almost painful tingle in my feet and lower legs that touch the sheet.

Has anyone else read that using a grounding mat/sheet via an outlet can actually be more harmful than good? I'm going to get DH to attach it to a grounding rod outside as soon as the ground thaws, but who knows when that will be.

### freshveggies — 2013-02-20T16:24:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-with-a-grounding-sheet.5962/post-76653>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=76648)
>
> I've been somewhat testing out the use of a grounding sheet the last few weeks and I'm a little concerned with what I'm finding. I know I read on here (\*I think) that someone mentioned the grounding mats and sheets may just spread out dirty electricity if you're plugging them into a grounded outlet, which is what I'm doing.
>
> I have a half sheet on my bed and we've been turning the breakers off for the bedrooms at night, so of course there's no electricity through the outlets and the sheet should be naturally grounded, right? I definitely can't say I'm sleeping any better or see any improvements, BUT when DH turns the breaker back on in the AM and I stay in bed for a little longer I am noticing a very uncomfortable, almost painful tingle in my feet and lower legs that touch the sheet.
>
> Has anyone else read that using a grounding mat/sheet via an outlet can actually be more harmful than good? I'm going to get DH to attach it to a grounding rod outside as soon as the ground thaws, but who knows when that will be.

Dr. Klinghardt says to not plug into outlet. Need to ground outside.

### diane — 2013-02-20T16:42:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-with-a-grounding-sheet.5962/post-76655>

I can't ground outside - my entire complex sits over a parking garage, so no point, really. So I got an earthing mat - the cheapest alternative to see if it makes any difference. Got a strap as well. Used the strap at work yesterday - no tired feeling in the afternoon (which is pretty typical after a long weekend LOL), then used the mat when I got home. I ended up in bed at 8pm I was so tired! I slept on the mat, and I had a fantastic sleep. From one day, can't tell if it's the mat or not - but it didn't hurt.

For my own little test, I've been testing it with my dogs as they are way more in tune with the environment than I am. They have a huge bed that will fit both of them, but still gives space. I have the mat on the bed and they have 3 choices - lie on the bed not touching the mat, lie on the bed touching the mat, or lie somewhere else (which they often do).They aren't going out of their way to avoid the mat and one is lying on it now. I want to give them a choice, and see what they do.

### BJK77 — 2013-02-20T16:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-with-a-grounding-sheet.5962/post-76659>

> [freshveggies said:](/goto/post?id=76653)
>
> Dr. Klinghardt says to not plug into outlet. Need to ground outside.

That must be where I heard it. I've read/watched/listened to so much lately I'm having a hard time keeping track of it all.

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=76655)
>
> I can't ground outside - my entire complex sits over a parking garage, so no point, really. So I got an earthing mat - the cheapest alternative to see if it makes any difference. Got a strap as well. Used the strap at work yesterday - no tired feeling in the afternoon (which is pretty typical after a long weekend LOL), then used the mat when I got home. I ended up in bed at 8pm I was so tired! I slept on the mat, and I had a fantastic sleep. From one day, can't tell if it's the mat or not - but it didn't hurt.
>
> For my own little test, I've been testing it with my dogs as they are way more in tune with the environment than I am. They have a huge bed that will fit both of them, but still gives space. I have the mat on the bed and they have 3 choices - lie on the bed not touching the mat, lie on the bed touching the mat, or lie somewhere else (which they often do).They aren't going out of their way to avoid the mat and one is lying on it now. I want to give them a choice, and see what they do.

What a great idea Diane. I will have to try this with my dogs as well . . . although I'm afraid one of them has completely lost his intuition thanks to our efforts to domesticate him

I'm fortunate that at home I can ground the sheet outside, but I have also been using a mat off and on in the office and I worry about DH being in a car 4-6 hours each day. It isn't always practical or possible for one to ground outside constantly and I'm hoping we can find a working solution.

# Thread: 23andme results

### msclman — 2013-02-21T02:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76803>

so I got my 23andme results back. i thought it would list the snp's but it doesnt. here are my results, i would love for anyone to help me interpret these results:

[http://dl.dropbox.com/u/107715833/23andMe%20Printable%20Report.pdf](http://dl.dropbox.com/u/107715833/23andMe%20Printable%20Report.pdf)

### msclman — 2013-02-21T03:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76806>

actually found the raw data:

[http://dl.dropbox.com/u/107715833/genome\_mike\_zaf\_Full\_20130220170037.txt](http://dl.dropbox.com/u/107715833/genome_mike_zaf_Full_20130220170037.txt)

### fitness@home — 2013-02-21T03:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76809>

Download your raw data and save it on your computer. Then go to geneticgenie.org website

Upload your raw data file on that site and it will give you your methylation SNPs. Hope that helps...

### msclman — 2013-02-21T03:56:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76814>

it does thank you i will do it now

### fitness@home — 2013-02-21T04:48:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76821>

Hey, you may not want to leave your raw data on here...being a public forum and all. Just something to consider.

### msclman — 2013-02-21T05:25:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76830>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=76821)
>
> Hey, you may not want to leave your raw data on here...being a public forum and all. Just something to consider.

thank you sooo much, was exactly what i was looking for.

just out of curiosity why would i care if people see the raw data?

### freesia — 2013-02-21T05:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76832>

Someone could run your data through promethease or something and find out all your predispositions
Or they could figure out you're related to some outlaw or other and make bad jokes about it.

### msclman — 2013-02-21T05:39:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76833>

what do you mean by predispositions? diseases?

### freesia — 2013-02-21T06:17:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76838>

Genetic predispositions to conditions or disease. Which doesn't always mean much on it's own actually.
But theres probably other stuff I haven't considered.
I haven't seen anyone put all their data out there on a public forum before....but I guess that doesn't mean it's wrong!?
Your data - your call.

### msclman — 2013-02-21T06:29:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-results.5969/post-76840>

i guess better safe than sorry. thanks again

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-recent-labs.5981/post-76916>

Hey guys,
Here are my most recent labs, just wondering if you had any thoughts. I'd still like to see my FT3 come up some, but I finally am feeling like I'm part of the land of the living again. The last year and a half has been rough with my thyroid - low TSH and T3 and T4 levels that have been below normal range.
Any thoughts on my cholesterol?

Lipid Panel with Reflex to Direct LDL
Total Cholesterol 246 (125-200)
HDL 53 (>46)
Triglycerides 60 (<150)
LDL 181 (<130)
Chol/HDLC Ratio 4.6 (< or = 5)
Non HDL Cholesterol 193

Hemoglobin A1C with MPG
Hemoglobin A1c 5.0
Mean Plasma Glucose 101

Vigamin D, 25-OH, Total 104

TSH .01 (.4 - 4.5)
T4 Total 6.1 (4.5-12)
T4 Free .9 (.8-1.8)
T3 Free 3.2 (2.3-4.2)
T3 Total 119 (76-119)

# Thread: Earth Runners- minimalist earthing sandals [Feedback]
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-02-22T09:00:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77005>

After I read the Earthing book it seemed to describe my experiences while spending so much time barefoot out in nature. Ever since I transitioned to a more grounded lifestyle I have experienced remarkable results beyond belief. The timing of all this aligned so well with my graduation from college and passion to start a meaningful career. It was at this point a year ago I decided to combine all my skills and started a minimalist earthing sandal company called Earth Runners. I aimed to make conductive sandal that can be worn anywhere. Please check them out and let me know what you think.
[www.earthrunners.com](http://www.earthrunners.com)

I am constantly working to improve our design, please share any feedback you may have.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/both-models2-jpg.41/)
Cheers,
Mike

### Kiera2 — 2013-02-22T09:23:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77007>

Oh Mike, you beat me! My Earth Runners arrived this afternoon, and I put them on immediately.

I've tried a few different ways to lace them, and they are so incredibly comfortable! My dh has just put his on, and he says they feel like he's really barefoot. He has only ever worn 'normal' shoes, so he feels the lack of a heel, but I've worn minimalist barefoot shoes for years so the only challenge for me is the leather lace. I didn't think I would llke the feel of the long lace wound around my ankle, but it's fine; doesn't feel at all like I'm choking my foot, though I thought it would.

So, I highly recommend Mike's Earth Runners! My experience with him has been fantastic; great customer service. I ordered the sandals on the 12th, they were in the mail on the 16th, and on my feet today. I have received a very high quality product, and now I'm off for a walk!

### nonchalant — 2013-02-22T17:05:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77031>

I like the straps near the ankles. Much better than a flip-flop design. The foot can act naturally while walking.

### Rachelle — 2013-02-22T17:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77049>

Can't wait to order these. I might have to wait a few months because I just made a very expensive purchase for my home, but no doubt these are for me. I m flat footed so these type of shoes will never look good on my feet, but if they feel good is all that counts.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-23T02:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77182>

I am very excited to find this product. Certainly its time has come. I really would love to hear your story. Was grounding the only change you made at the time, and what were the health changes you experienced, and how dramatic?

If you have already posted this story elsewhere, such as on your website, feel free to point me there.

### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-02-23T08:54:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-77230>

> [MamaGrok said:](/goto/post?id=77182)
>
> I am very excited to find this product. Certainly its time has come. I really would love to hear your story. Was grounding the only change you made at the time, and what were the health changes you experienced, and how dramatic?
>
> If you have already posted this story elsewhere, such as on your website, feel free to point me there.

Since childhood, I have endured a persistent neuralgia issue and one of the most effective means of managing this condition has been through earthing. After reading the book on earthing, it explained the relief I found going barefoot outdoors and inspired me to help share this experience with others.

You can check out our bio's and how it all started here on the website [http://www.earthrunners.com/about](http://www.earthrunners.com/about)

We also made a video telling our story for our kickstarter campaign.\[video=youtube;GEDXR-kWtHo\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GEDXR-kWtHo\[/video\]

### Kiera2 — 2013-03-04T22:40:03+0200

### Rachelle — 2013-03-06T13:57:50+0200

When a person is born with flat feet its forever. Not sure if a rolfer could help a baby with this or not, but they cannot undo this as an adult.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-06T14:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79290>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=79203)
>
> Would love to get a pair, but my feet are completely flat i.e. no arch whatsoever, so I couldn't walk far in these things until pain sets in.

I am completely flat footed and experience no pain when I walk barefoot. It's a pain in the ass to not have ankle support along with no arch. Thinking I am probably better off allowing my feet to just do what is natural to them and not force them to hold my ankle up. That for me hurt me more than anything and it was only after reading and having rolfing done that I understood the ramifications of forcing my feet to hold my ankle up that hurt me the most. I never could wear the expensive orthotics that were made for me, especially after rolfing.

I was also born with my feet turned in and according to Ida Rolf the doctors did more damage in the way they forced my feet to turn straight. If only my mother would have known better and taken me to a rolfer maybe things would have been different.

Sure glad I am over 50 and not lquite as vain as I use to be. I can now allow myself to live with having my feet look a bit deformed. Well hell...cause they are.

### JoeBranca — 2013-03-06T22:12:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79342>

Good stuff, Mike. I've had my Ultralight (no bedding) with nylon laces for a couple of weeks now. They were actually on order when I was out "earthing" with Jack and Erwan in Santa Fe in January ; )

The shape and minimalist feel are great. I am still futzing with the nylon straps most days, I am not sure if this is a break in period still or I'm just doing it wrong (I did watch the videos). Seems the tightness I need to make on the straps to keep the heel from sliding back and off the edge, for example, ends up being so tight that some blood circulation to the front of the foot is partially impaired. So it's like I'm trying to find that "loose but not too loose" strapping a lot, at least if I want to do running or more off road stuff with them and not worry about the foot getting out of position.

I went for the model with the least support, but now I suppose the Quantums are the choice for better off road tread. Most of the time the Ultralight is fine, but when I'm going up natural terrain on an uphill, the grip is not as much as I need at times (for example climbing up on a layer of damp wood chips on a steep incline). Also I'm doing lots more MovNat this year... which I'm still thinking barefoot is best anyways, but at least this is good to get me to MovNat destinations, except for the occasional slippage.

Unless I successfully jerry rig a copper wiring in the soles of my KSO Treks or something like, maybe getting a pair of Quantums for more rugged terrains and activities is the ticket?

thanks

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-06T23:11:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79350>

Joe, are these the same sandals Erwan wore? I'm considering buying some, as well, but not sure if I'd better off trying to run in my moccasins.

### EarthRunner1987 — 2013-03-06T23:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79356>

> [JoeBranca said:](/goto/post?id=79342)
>
> Good stuff, Mike. I've had my Ultralight (no bedding) with nylon laces for a couple of weeks now. They were actually on order when I was out "earthing" with Jack and Erwan in Santa Fe in January ; )
>
> The shape and minimalist feel are great. I am still futzing with the nylon straps most days, I am not sure if this is a break in period still or I'm just doing it wrong (I did watch the videos). Seems the tightness I need to make on the straps to keep the heel from sliding back and off the edge, for example, ends up being so tight that some blood circulation to the front of the foot is partially impaired. So it's like I'm trying to find that "loose but not too loose" strapping a lot, at least if I want to do running or more off road stuff with them and not worry about the foot getting out of position.
>
> I went for the model with the least support, but now I suppose the Quantums are the choice for better off road tread. Most of the time the Ultralight is fine, but when I'm going up natural terrain on an uphill, the grip is not as much as I need at times (for example climbing up on a layer of damp wood chips on a steep incline). Also I'm doing lots more MovNat this year... which I'm still thinking barefoot is best anyways, but at least this is good to get me to MovNat destinations, except for the occasional slippage.
>
> Unless I successfully jerry rig a copper wiring in the soles of my KSO Treks or something like, maybe getting a pair of Quantums for more rugged terrains and activities is the ticket?
>
> thanks

Hey Joe, thanks for the feedback! Glad to here from a fellow movnatter.

Our [Ultralight model](http://www.earthrunners.com/products-page/product-category/ultralight-earth-runners) is definitely our most barefoot sandal and I mean this in terms of ground feel and traction. As the sandals continue to self mold it should help hold your foot in place for more demanding applications. The laces also need to be re-tightened regularly until the sandal has completely formed, due to the fact that the loops will come closer and closer to the foot. Over time the laces will settle in help making putting them on easier. The one thing you want to be sure of to ensure the security of the laces is that they criss-cross as they go through the loops on both sides of the ankle. This prevents the heel strap from loosening when going up hill.

Our [Quantum model](http://www.earthrunners.com/products-page/product-category/quantum-earth-runners) is great for movnat due to the traction is provides. You can keep the models rather minimal and lightweight with 2mm Vibram tread (better than Ultralight tr) or go for ultimate traction with the 4mm mm heavy tread. The one thing that also helps is the heel cup. When moving and climbing through nature the foot experiences a lot of shear force and the heel cup helps to hold the sandal in place for these applications.

Stay tuned as we plan to offer a flat sole sandal with heavy tread soon!

### Rachelle — 2013-03-07T16:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earth-runners-minimalist-earthing-sandals-feedback.5990/post-79466>

Well that's it, next month I want to buy a pair of Ultralight and see how they do. I am pretty sure they will work out, especially if they mold to your feet to hold them in place. Thanks much, Earthrunner.

### shilohman — 2013-03-07T19:49:32+0200

# Thread: One year old not gaining...any ideas?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-02-23T00:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77146>

Got this email from a friend.....any thoughts out there?

I was wondering if you know of a natural recipe for a pediasure (high calorie baby drink) alternative? Our little guy isn't gaining well and we finally need to bulk him up and the doctor of course recommended pediasure (full of sugar and additives).

Little guy is one year this month.

Would love to hear from some of you moms  One year olds are not my forte...

### ashryn — 2013-02-23T01:55:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77172>

Breast milk? Mums diet? Hard to make suggestions without context really. You gotta know why he isn't growing

### Hope — 2013-02-23T02:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77194>

Yeah...that's what I asked her too. She doesn't know why he isn't growing....she eats better than SAD but there are grains. Lots of her stuff is home grown, little processed foods, no wheat...definitely not paleo though I suggested it.

Thanks for responding!!!!

### ashryn — 2013-02-23T04:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77214>

More fat? Coconut milk for mum? More seafoods? Dare I raise the possibility... Is there a baby monitor or heavy EMF around? Someone had a cool story about a baby that looked to be going the way of a spectrum disorder that was sorted out by removing emf and adding sunlight, cold and time on the dirt..

It has been a long time since I had my own 1 year old, and she got put on formula at 6 months because I couldn't make enough milk for her to thrive. Oh how I would have done things differently if I had known then what I know now.. Unfortunately with a new baby and no support network, you trust what the child health nurses tell you, especially when there is the threat of _you will hurt your child if you don't...

_I hope someone more helpful than me responds to your question.. There are a few people around who know an awful lot about natural child rearing, but its probably going to be guess work unless we know all about mums lifestyle and diet and medical history.

### kathylu — 2013-02-23T16:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77260>

Tell her to visit the Weston A. Price Foundation. They have several terrific baby formula recipes. It was a life saver for my baby, since I was only able to breast feed for 3 months (long story) and I REFUSED to give him commercial formula!

[http://www.westonaprice.org/childre...ybXVsYWUiO2k6ODtzOjEyOiJiYWJ5IGZvcm11bGEiO30=](http://www.westonaprice.org/childrens-health/homemade-baby-formula-testimonials?qh=YTo5OntpOjA7czo0OiJiYWJ5IjtpOjE7czo2OiJiYWJ5J3MiO2k6MjtzOjY6ImJhYmllcyI7aTozO3M6NzoiYmFiaWVzJyI7aTo0O3M6NzoiZm9ybXVsYSI7aTo1O3M6ODoiZm9ybXVsYXMiO2k6NjtzOjk6ImZvcm11bGEncyI7aTo3O3M6ODoiZm9ybXVsYWUiO2k6ODtzOjEyOiJiYWJ5IGZvcm11bGEiO30%3D)

### RiverNeighbor — 2013-02-24T18:12:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77442>

How are baby's stools? Maybe something is going on there that is preventing him from digesting or absorbing properly.....

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-25T17:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-year-old-not-gaining-any-ideas.6006/post-77588>

has she gone to La leche league meeting? which growth charts is the doc using CDC charts are based off Formula fed babies - where as WHO charts are based off bf babies. bf babies growth slows after 6mths and just stays constant, they don't always gain weight like FF babies. additionally is baby still on his/her growth curve? how are diapers? how much solids are being used vs breastmilk - always offer breastmilk first, then solids - and as always solids before or at the age of 1 are just for fun, if nursing baby doesn't need solids...

my bet - nothing is wrong w/baby - just a doc noticing baby isn't in the bell curve and is an outlier...

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-23T02:14:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-truth-in-this.6008/post-77180>

I call hogwash. Weston Price (seriously, folks, you gotta read his book) documented that overbites & other orthodontic problems develop within one generation of introducing processed foods.

I see it around me still - nearly all first-generation African immigrants, most first-generation northern European immigrants, and most African-Americans I know who were still eating true southern soul food twenty years ago have great, wonderful, wide palates with perfect bites. Their kids have horrible teeth just like the rest of us.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-23T02:15:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-truth-in-this.6008/post-77181>

PS I didn't see any evidence in the article, just theory. Without evidence, I call complete and utter hogwash, like trying to explain the appendix as some useless leftover of evolution.

# Thread: Humans are semi-conductors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/humans-are-semi-conductors.6020/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2013-02-23T20:42:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/humans-are-semi-conductors.6020/post-77310>

After getting through EMF-7 this was one of the more interesting points to be made. The example given was about bone.

Semi-conductors are great because of their non-linear properties. A diode (an n-type (lots of available electrons) material attached to a p-type (lots of available "holes" = opposite of an electron = positive charge) has a junction potential, since holes diffuse into the ntype material and electrons into the ptype. The diode operates as a short circuit in the "on" position, and current readily flows through it. In the "off" position it is pretty much an open circuit. That's the basic principle, and it only exhibits this behavior when operated in the right voltage range. There's plenty of other non-linear effects to be had, and it only gets much more complicated when we talk about npn juntions, etc.

If bone is a piezo-electric material, then it would create an electric charge in response to mechanical stress, like other piezo-electric materials (crystals). And then what? I didn't understand much more in the explanation of the "circuit." Where is the diode (bone) in relation to the voltage source (what is the voltage source? How is the diode operated (when is it forward biased, when is it reversed), and for what reasons? A magnetic field (DC) doesn't have effect on a diode's electrical characteristics, so I'm still not putting that piece of the puzzle in with the rest of them.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Epi-Paleo for Cats
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-23T21:27:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77321>

I have a cat that came from a hoarder's house so she appears to have "issues". She doesn't mind other cats around her but has no use for them. She feels the same about dogs. However she pretty much dislikes all people and has all her 6 years except for DH and me. She runs and hides if the doorbell rings of hears the garage door open. She lives secluded in the bedroom 99.9% of the time.

I just recently switched my cat's food to a freeze dried fish based food. Since cats are carnivores she needs her daily dose of protein. I've read where cats cannot digest carbohydrates (ie kibble) very where if at all. It's only been two weeks but she has come out into the main part of the house about 30% of the time now and will sit with me peacefully or wonder around on her own. Her high alert mode is still there but in a much calmer way. We even felt a little earthquake last night and she just continue to lie next to me as the house shook! I expect more changes as time goes on.

Has anyone else had animal changes when switching up the diet?

### diane — 2013-02-23T22:03:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77324>

Absolutely. When I first switched my dogs from raw (and I was feeding them a good quality kibble) even the dog walker asked me what I did - they were just calmer. I've also had people switch their dogs from crappy kibble to good kibble and have noticed a change in hyperness.

Cats are usually much pickier eaters than dogs, so good to see changes in cats too!

### SeaJane — 2013-02-23T22:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77326>

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=77321)
>
> I just recently switched my cat's food to a freeze dried fish based food.

May I ask the name of the food? I have 2 cats who eat grain-free high protein kibble that seems to work pretty well for them and I keep looking for better alternatives. Our dog is eating a dehydrated raw food that works for her and me (easy to rehydrate and not smelly like canned food). Thanks.

### freesia — 2013-02-23T22:25:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77328>

My dog has fresh protein, plus kibble...but her kibble is freeze dried fish and herbs...very low carb....I think maybe 20% max? (No grains).

The dog trainer we used for puppy classes mentioned many dogs do dramatically better behaviourally just from switching to what she called "more biologically appropriate" kibble, but I imagine they'd do even better on a fresh raw diet.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-23T22:44:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77331>

My cat has been on grain-free, high protein, and as high of fat kibble as I could get since I got her at 6 months old. My last cat became diabetic at 10 yrs old so I thought I should start this one out grain-free. At about 3 yrs old I thought she still had allergies to something but couldn't figure out what. Then someone told me their dog was allergic to chicken. Yep, so was my cat. But she still had these socialization issues. So that brings me to this post on her food.

My favorite of the brands is the KiwiPeak. Seems to be the best ingredients. I have used this as a topping on top of her kibble for a couple of years so I know she really likes this one. The second one I use is the Primal Freeze-Dried Formula. This one has a few veggies and fruits but it is 53% protein and 30% fat and this is the one she chows down on the fastest. I did quite a bit of research and nothing seems really perfect unless I were to make up food for her every day. Since she is improving I'll keep doing this as I believe it is really helping her.

And the quantity of how much she eats has gone way down. There is so much in these nuggets that they don't need as much. It's more expensive but I think in the end the overall cost may actually be less.

We feed the dog high protein and high fat, 100% natural kibble. Both animals get RO remineralized water. Geeze, they will probably outlive us both!

### freesia — 2013-02-23T23:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77336>

Patty we made that observation too in our pup compared to the neighbours, she eats way less bulk.
Cheap nasty petfood is a false economy....hmm...same as for humans with all the health costs later on.

It's great your cat's improving!

### diane — 2013-02-24T01:42:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77356>

I notice a huge difference in water intake on raw. When I go to kibble (which I occasionally do a week or so of high quality kibble here and there) they drink a ton of water. When I go back to raw, they hardly drink any. There's also a huge difference in the poop with raw, even vs. a high quality kibble. It's like picking up rabbit pellets from my 85 lb dogs LOL.

### Hope — 2013-02-24T02:36:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77361>

not EP but....

[http://feline-nutrition.org/nutrition/proper-feeding-from-pottengers-cats](http://feline-nutrition.org/nutrition/proper-feeding-from-pottengers-cats)

### endless — 2013-02-24T03:12:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-for-cats.6022/post-77366>

My three cats and one dog are all on raw food. I used to feed a high quality kibble (grain free) but with cats especially it's asking for trouble eventually. I nursed one cat through kidney failure, and have another now that has kidney disease...the one I inherited 2 years ago after he'd been on kibble for 8 years!

All my animals smell better, have smaller, less stinky poops, and nicer coats since the transition. It's more expensive but I can't justify feeding them something that I know will reduce their quality of life in the long run. Kibble around here is reserved (for the cats) for when I am away and someone else is feeding them. It is a HUGE treat, they love it, but interestingly enough, they are eager for their raw lamb and chicken when I get home!

If frozen raw is too much of a hassle, the dried raw stuff is pretty good too, I've used that very successfully in the past.

.
Interesting article about parents in Finland & other Nordic Countries.... Parents know the health benefits of being cold! Parents take their children outside in sub-zero weather so they can take a nap. The parents are doing two important treatments in one shot: CT & Sleep... Can you imagine what Americans would think if someone one told them to put their baby out in sub-zero weather for a nap? lol

**Would you put your baby or toddler outside in the freezing cold for their lunchtime nap? Most Nordic parents wouldn't give it a second thought. For them it's part of their daily routine**

[http://campaigns.dailykos.com/p/dia/action/public/?action\_KEY=322](http://campaigns.dailykos.com/p/dia/action/public/?action_KEY=322)

The Federal Communications Commission is considering a proposal to open up a wider share of the high-quality wireless spectrum for public use. This would be an important first step toward making fast, free, public Wi-Fi available to everyone.

Predictably, the wireless industry doesn't like this idea, and would rather the FCC auction off more high-quality wireless spectrum to private carriers, and leave less open to public use. However, a lack of competitionâ€”from public or private sourcesâ€”is one of the reasons why broadband service in the United States has become too slow, too costly, and entirely unavailable to many people.

# Thread: If you had the chance............
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-02-24T08:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77395>

If you had the chance to build a new home for your family ...... where would be your top pick? location?

What kind of a house would you build? What materials would you use? What eco-systems would you put in place?

Would it be a big house? or would you be more content with a small home now?

Would a log house be better? or a straw bale house? or...???? or would you build a bunker in the side of a hill?

When we know better ..... we do better. So how much better can we do???

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-24T09:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77397>

First I would choose my community. People and family are important. I've lived close to family in a community which was mean spirited and nasty. Choose both the community and family that are accepting and congenial and with other values that are important to you. A place with people largely like myself. (I've lived with diversity... nuff said.) A place without crime, no war and where there is no religious, cultural or ethnic persecution of your group.
Secondly I would want it where there were good growing conditions for food (good water with fertile soils), a place that didn't flood or get regular droughts (over a 300 year period) and that is not known for bush fires. It should have trees.
Where the temperatures were largely between 0 and 30 C. 0 is cold enough for me in winter.
Where there are both education and work possibilities for the children/grandchildren.

Then I would like to have a high mass house that doesn't need much heating or cooling. Probably solar powered. Not sure about the whole electricity thing, but frankly on a high protein diet I need refrigeration and freezer space. I probably also need good internet access - hubby would never move anywhere without it. Nor would the grandchildren. Its too useful. Though what this means in terms of EMFs I'm not sure yet.

Probably living with or next door to family - not because we live in each other's pockets - its just easier when we don't have to do important things alone. Dealing with car crashes, hospital visits, children's illness while the parent has to work, etc are all so much easier with extra adults around.

### ashryn — 2013-02-24T10:53:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77398>

I think a hobbit house in the side of a cliff overlooking the ocean and backing onto a forest.. Go out the front door, you get ocean, go out the back door, you come up in a forest.. With a deep natural spring.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-02-24T19:25:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-had-the-chance.6032/post-77464>

> [ashryn said:](/goto/post?id=77398)
>
> I think a hobbit house in the side of a cliff overlooking the ocean and backing onto a forest.. Go out the front door, you get ocean, go out the back door, you come up in a forest.. With a deep natural spring.

Oh wow! I just got such visions of happiness from your idea. I love a green foresty setting with hills for hiking but can't seem to let go of my beach. Walking in the water, knee high right now, on the wet sand energizes me like nothing else. I feel at peace with so much in my life on the beach. Family to me are people you visit. I'm not tied to the norms of society of "doing" the family things unless I want to. So moving and being in a place that specifically works for me is so much more fulfilling. I have a great family too! But that is a part everyone's individual choices and I am fed more by nature than by family (blood family).

We had a well when I lived in the NW and I have yet to taste a water that tasted as good. I would love to have access to something like that again. Very nice addition to the perfect living spot!

Reading the chapter of the book on diabetes being an evolutionary survival mechanism where cell membranes are swapped for PUFAs in preparation for hibernation because saturated fats are to stiff to work with in cold temps got me thinking...

Those of us still eating a fair amount of saturated fats who are struggling to get cold adaptation might be having trouble because the fats are too stiff to work with.. Another reason to get fish adapted i suppose..

# Thread: Jared and Subway 15 years now ... Surprise: Keeping weight off is the hardest part
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jared-and-subway-15-years-now-surprise-keeping-weight-off-is-the-hardest-part.6044/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-02-25T16:15:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jared-and-subway-15-years-now-surprise-keeping-weight-off-is-the-hardest-part.6044/post-77571>

[http://www.usatoday.com/story/money/business/2013/02/23/jared-fogle-subway-diet/1928793/](http://www.usatoday.com/story/money/business/2013/02/23/jared-fogle-subway-diet/1928793/)

Fogle says the hardest part of all hasn't been losing the weight, but keeping it off. And when he puts on more than 20 pounds â€” as he did a few years ago â€” Subway comes knocking at his door and not-so-subtly reminds him to lose the poundage. Reason: Jared has been a gold mine for Subway.

"Subway generated the perception that it's a healthy place to eat through Jared, and it stuck," says restaurant researcher Malcolm Knapp. "That's a very powerful tool. You don't hear people saying that they can eat healthy at McDonald's." **_(wow ... CW at its best)_**

Jared Fogle's Six Diet Tips:

1) Watch portion sizes carefully.

2) Eat lots of fruit and veggies.

3) Drink lots of water. (He drinks six bottles daily.)

4) Exercise every day.

5) Ask for all sauces & dresses on the side â€” and put them on yourself, sparingly.

6) Don't let a bad day, or week, or month change your diet plans.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

I remember the days when I used to think Subway was healthy food. Only problem is that I was always hungry shortly after eating my Subway meal. Intuitively before discovering Paleo / Epi-paleo, I knew I needed more fat in my diet, and not less.

After 15 years of the Jared / Subway meme being promoted by w/ millions of dollars in ads, its not surprising many people believe it.

# Thread: No more appetite...except for fat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-02-27T02:46:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-77934>

So I have been on the LR Rx and doing EP within that since September. No problem not snacking for months, in fact I prefer it that way now, and the one time I sort of had a mid-morning snack it screwed up the rest of my meals that day so that won't happen again (which is exciting to me).

But the last couple weeks I have had no appetite, and have to force myself. I no longer do BAB but still do a good amount of protein, just not 50g. I don't even feel like eating veggies..all I want is fat. Even fish is harder (maybe bc I have been eating it so much for 5 months??).....not sure if I'm tired of it all, or what.

The only thing I really WANT appears to be fat....like butter, EVCO, duck fat, etc, my one kind of bad indulgence (almond and sometimes walnut butter), and a couple times in the last couple weeks, Brie from Normandy. (I don't have Hashi's but I know cheese is not great; I just really wanted some - plain or with a nice glob of GF butter. I still have my seafood etc and now love (!) oysters, mostly canned.....but it's not a pleasure anymore. I am forcing myself.

Anyone else ever experience this?? What do you do? I have thought about skipping lunch....

Thanks....

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-27T02:58:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-77939>

Yes I have. For a couple of months that was all I wanted. So that's pretty much what I ate. then I realized my hormones were bouncing all over the place, looked at my diet and added back in the protein. Things leveled back out. My takeaway from that was that you can follow your instincts but you absolutely MUST pay attention to how you are feeling. I waited a bit too long to catch it and paid for it big time.

### Hope — 2013-02-27T04:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-77941>

Hi Darleen,

thanks so much...and that makes sense. So appreciate it..

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-02-27T05:30:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-77947>

Beginning October 2012, I began eating mostly fat and protein and found that I was dropping off most vegetables. No snacking, no BAB. I was almost never hungry in the morning or at lunch, willing myself to eat one or the other. If I ate lunch, I had no interest in dinner. If I ate dinner, I wasn't hungry for breakfast. My hormones seemed to get a little funky towards the second half of November. My libido was low and cortisol was elevated in the am. During December, during an extended work break, I ate breakfast earlier, upped my CT, and ate dinner at an earlier hour. I found that my hormones seemed to modulate, and things have been fine ever since. I am still not hungry most of the time. When possible, I try to eat a fat and protein laden breakfast earlier. I still end up skipping lunch most days because I'm simply not hungry...or I'll snack on some seaweed, a couple macadamia nuts and some chocolate, but I'm not sure I'd call it "lunch." Even if I have a good amount of physical activity, I'm still rarely hungry. I have been trying to be very consistent with grounding outside regularly every day, and I've been doing CT at least 4 times a week. Actually, the only time I find myself with a rumbling tummy is when I've had wine without dinner the night before. Just my $.02.

### diane — 2013-02-27T06:30:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-77960>

Thanks for sharing that Chrysanthemama! That's really helpful.

### caroline — 2013-02-27T09:03:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-77975>

me too - I eat a smaller breakfast and then eat again around 3:00 - 4:30 and then not hungry until next day. I haven't been eating any vegetables to speak of.
I eat a lot of bacon and butter and sometimes indulge in Stilton or Goat cheese - but I probably shouldn't. Once I gave up fruit - things changed massively.

I am pretty sure somewhere JK mentioned that 4:30 is an ideal time for dinner and I can't remember why. That is normally the time I am hungry and so it works out perfectly. Altho if I am really hungry earlier - like 2:30 - I just eat whatever I am going to have but kind of space it over an hour while I am doing things and that works fine too and I am not hungry unill the next day

### diane — 2013-02-27T15:44:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-78003>

For the first time, I'm not all that hungry for lunch and can't always finish it. But I'm not yet to the point of skipping it.

### Hope — 2013-03-04T00:41:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-78835>

Ohhhh thank you all for sharing more. I love it and now I feel much better. I love the idea of having breakfast and then an earlier dinner....I will give that a shot for sure!

And Caroline - glad to hear you're doing less (or no?) veggies...I am leaning towards that as well.

Chrysanthemama, great blog! I will have to follow you. How much grounding do you do per day, and how?

### caroline — 2013-03-04T02:54:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-appetite-except-for-fat.6078/post-78856>

somebody asked me - somewhere for a recipe for lamb's liver pate - does anyone remember where that was???

# Thread: Sleeping on the ground
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2013-02-27T05:34:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-77948>

I want to try it, but my perfect flat spot is next to the smart meter. Soon, I'll be getting readings from my neighbor's smart meters, to minimize noise. That leaves either patio, gravel pathway or rock. Where should I sleep? Is it better to sleep directly on real rock in the ground, or is it no better than gravel? I am wondering if taking some of the gravel off will even help, because usually the ground is extremely dry. I also have a small brick area, but the ct tub is there. Would it be better to move the ct tub to a concrete patio and sleep on the brick?

### diane — 2013-02-27T06:25:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-77958>

Personally I would go for grass. But I've always been about comfort. LOL.

### Birdy — 2013-02-28T01:36:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-78170>

lol, there is a patch of grass in the front, but I am sure I would get arrested there, and there are too many street lights.

### diane — 2013-02-28T01:41:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-78173>

Yes, I tried laying in the snow in a park (I don't have a place to ground at home) and a man yelled out asking if I was ok. LOL.

I would go for the rock then. I believe that even cement is ok, as long as there's no vapour barrier below. But I would think the more natural the better.

### Huck — 2013-02-28T01:43:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-78175>

Maybe you could build a nest!

### Rachelle — 2013-02-28T23:19:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-78400>

I don't have any place to lay where I live so I plan on going to a different park as much as possible. I also plan on going camping often this summer. I, most certainly, will get out of this area during Labor day since they have a festival here in town that puts a huge tower up right next to this Co-op building. Ugh!! Last year, before the EMF series I was having a fit about it. I knew intuitively it just wasn't good. Oh (thinking to myself) why, oh why did I move here.

### Birdy — 2013-03-01T05:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-the-ground.6080/post-78460>

Oh, I would love a tree house, but being too high would probably put me in the range of my neighbors' smart meters.

# Thread: Five year old with stomachache and headache
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/five-year-old-with-stomachache-and-headache.6086/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cabiria — 2013-02-27T09:58:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/five-year-old-with-stomachache-and-headache.6086/post-77980>

My 5 year old daughter has been complaining of nonstop stomachache and headache for about 6 weeks now. The pains seem to be mild as she is continues to do her usual routine. Around the same time she developed a nervous tic of pulling her shirt away from the neck (in front) and shrug her shoulders at the same time. Also, she says she feels like " there is an earthquake all the time" -- she's never experienced one, but at school they've been doing some earthquake exercise drills. She's about 45" tall and 44 lbs. She is not yet eating a full epi paleo diet -- still eats rice, potatoes, yams, and has the occasional pizza at birthday parties. Her doctor said the tummy and head ache are not related, and very common in kids, as well as the nervous tic. For the headache he recommended to see an eye doctor.

### nonchalant — 2013-02-27T17:36:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/five-year-old-with-stomachache-and-headache.6086/post-78016>

I wonder if pulsed EMF might be an issue.

### diane — 2013-02-27T18:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/five-year-old-with-stomachache-and-headache.6086/post-78025>

Funny, when I was mentioning some of the EMF stuff to a coworker (got her to watch the movie Resonance), she mentioned that she has a friend who's daughter had migraines that they couldn't figure out. They had to get rid of cell phones, wifi, etc. and her headaches went away.

### SeaJane — 2013-02-27T23:33:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/five-year-old-with-stomachache-and-headache.6086/post-78128>

My daughter has had these symptoms (headaches, stomach aches and tics) at different periods over the last few of years. It started with stomach aches, then tics, and then a period from last June into November when she had a mild headache almost every day. We are free of all of them with the exception of minor episodes of tics when she is particularly stressed. We addressed them in a number of ways including increased seafood (she was already paleo), energy work, being strict with the light cycle (she has her own orange glasses), starting methylfolate and methyl B12 in the fall (she has methylation issues) and recently cutting back on EMFs, doing some grounding, and adding mitochrondrial Rx. Yesterday, in a consult with Dr. Tim Jackson, he confirmed my suspicion that her set of snps leads to low dopamine which is associated with tics, and he said that often kids with tics are more sensitive to EMFs. Based on what I've read here, what Dr. Tim said, and what has relieved her symptoms, I am sure my daughter was/is electron deficient and that is the source of her symptoms. Personally, I wouldn't buy what the doctor says as I think they are likely linked.

### cabiria — 2013-03-01T07:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/five-year-old-with-stomachache-and-headache.6086/post-78470>

Thank you so much for all the replies! This is more help than I got from my pediatrician. SeaJane, I believe the same way as you do, in that the symptoms are linked. We've seen the eye doctor, and no problems there, as I was expecting. What is puzzling to me is that she started having these symptoms after we started to be more strict about the paleo diet. I was wondering if she was getting too stressed about it, and that triggered the symptoms...She was eating quite a lot of seafood before the symptoms started (mostly smoked salmon, sushi, and shrimp), and I was considering cutting these down a bit at a friend's suggestion that have too much PUFAs ([http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsaturatedfats.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsaturatedfats.shtml)). I will be definitely looking into all the issues you raised, SeaJane -- thanks again!

# Thread: Road Trip for Spring Water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-02-27T21:58:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-78099>

Road trip to Mayberry (Mt Airy, NC) to get spring water. Filled up my wine jugs (about 10 gallons). The spring used to be a famous health spring back in the late 1800's.

You walk down about 7 steps into the round concrete foundation. The water comes out a pipe and there is drain directly below it. The brick is to use as a stop to hold your container (if is to big to fit like my jugs were). It took between 45 minutes and an hour to get 10 gallons. Right next to the spring are a couple of wooden rockers looking out on a small river. I got to practice mindfulness while I was waiting for a jug to fill.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The sulfur smell was really strong. The sign says if I leave them open overnight, it will go away.

**Mineral Content**​

-   Calcium 20.2 ppm
-   Sodium 7.9 ppm
-   Sulphate 7.8 ppm
-   Magnesium 2.3 ppm

And then I drove back with the top down - about 50 degrees and sunny.

FUN, I grew up in Hot Springs, Arkansas. Same thing. . .and I can buy overpriced bottled versions of it at the yuppie stores here (I used to get it free growing up there!). . .but I saw on an ask Jack thread that there was Floride and JK's advise was stay away.

### fitness@home — 2013-02-27T22:15:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-78107>

Nice way to spend an afternoon! I am envious. Have to locate a spring nearby...

Found one - the Humble Artisan Well. Humble is not far at all... Will look into this soon.

Diane, thanks for posting the link above.

### Vicster — 2013-02-27T22:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-78116>

Fitness@home the one in Humble is no longer operational. It is there and flows, but not from the original source. The city repiped it to run on treated water because they were concerned that run off of the spring water into a nearby lake would harm it. Go figure.....

### fitness@home — 2013-02-27T23:11:46+0200

### fitness@home — 2013-03-02T14:25:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-78585>

I took some pictures but am having technical difficulties trying to attach them...will have to do that from the computer at home.

Will definitely go back and swim when it is re-opened. Apparently the water temp is always very cool. We spend a weekend in Austin every 4-6 weeks.

### [email redacted] — 2013-03-04T01:25:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-78840>

Thanks so much for the link for the springs. I found one 30 minutes up the road from my house. We drove up there today and got about 7 gallons. The only thing is I wish I had something better than plastic to put it in. The water is fantastic. It takes so pure it's like to really doesn't have a taste (if that makes any sense!).

### diane — 2013-03-04T01:55:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-78845>

I found a spring near a place I go hiking. Haven't gone yet - just planning what the heck I will put the water in!

Glad you found one close to you kjgraffin!

### diane — 2013-03-17T22:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80897>

I finally got to the spring yesterday, and filled the meager water containers that I had. There were people there filling 5 or 6 of the huge water jugs that fit on the machine. I had one coleman water cooler (the small size), a mediumish jug that I bought spring water in, and a 1 litre water bottle. LOL.

I tried the water today. Oh my - you can just taste the freshness! It's wonderful! I realized that I've been using the tap water for my tea, which still has fluoride in it - guess I will have to use this water as well. Will definitely have to get more next time to make the most of the trip - and so I have enough that I don't have to go back every week.

### Huck — 2013-03-18T01:36:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80916>

I made my 2nd trip a couple of days ago and got 18 gallons. Drive time is about 3 hours round trip plus close to an hour to fill up the jugs. The 1st time I made coffee using spring water I was amazed at the difference in taste.

### caroline — 2013-03-18T07:35:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80941>

Thanks Huck - will have to do that for my morning coffee. That is quite a road trip for you ...what is the price of fuel these days in the U.S.?

### [email redacted] — 2013-03-18T13:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80952>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=80916)
>
> I made my 2nd trip a couple of days ago and got 18 gallons. Drive time is about 3 hours round trip plus close to an hour to fill up the jugs. The 1st time I made coffee using spring water I was amazed at the difference in taste.

What did you put your water in? We are using a 5 gallon plastic water dispenser that we usually take to the baseball field for the team, but after a week or so it starts to taste funny. I'm looking for something we can put in the fridge.

### Huck — 2013-03-18T14:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80953>

I use my PET wine jugs for storing it. One 5 gallon jug I keep in the kitchen and the others in a lower light room. I use wine airlocks and corks to keep oxygen out and keep a full pitcher in the refrigerator for drinking, coffee, etc. I think my 1st batch lasted about 2 weeks and still tasted fine.

### Huck — 2013-03-18T14:03:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80954>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=80941)
>
> Thanks Huck - will have to do that for my morning coffee. That is quite a road trip for you ...what is the price of fuel these days in the U.S.?

About $3.50 US a gallon. It cost about $25 in gas for the round trip. Compared to the cost of spring water in the store, it is a bargain.

Hi Diane,

As a fellow Torontonian, I'm curious as to where to find this spring. The empty 4L bottles are piling up from my new spring water habit. I was going to put them out in clear plastic bags rather than fill up the blue bin, but maybe I can reuse them by filling them up at a spring? How cool would that be!

### BJK77 — 2013-03-18T19:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/road-trip-for-spring-water.6095/post-80994>

Is anyone concerned about contaminants in the water or am I the only one? I found a spring just a few miles from my house(!) and was thrilled, but it's in a very dense agricultural area, so I'm wondering if I need to be worried about things like nitrates/nitrates, bacteria, etc.

### diane — 2013-03-18T20:00:46+0200

I was going to put them out in clear plastic bags rather than fill up the blue bin, but maybe I can reuse them by filling them up at a spring? How cool would that be!

It's north of Whitby - about an hour drive from downtown. It's right off Lakeridge Road - pretty easy to find (although I did turn right instead of left, but we got a little scenic detour).

[http://www.findaspring.com/chalk-lake-spring-uxbridge-ontario-canada/](http://www.findaspring.com/chalk-lake-spring-uxbridge-ontario-canada/)

# Thread: Simple Indian Style Shrimp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/simple-indian-style-shrimp.6106/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2013-02-28T12:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/simple-indian-style-shrimp.6106/post-78272>

I have been fortunate to have much ethnic diversity in my workplace and have collected many recipes over the years. This one is simple and came from a gentleman whose grandfather was a chef at a large hotel in India. It was more of an appetizer but I use it for dinner.

\-Raw shrimp, blot with paper towels to remove excess moisture
\- finely diced hot pepper (I use jalapeÃ±o), can control how hot the dish is by removing seeds
\- LIBERAL amounts of turmeric
\- ghee for sautÃ©ing

Sprinkle turmeric on shrimp and let sit for at least 30 minutes. SautÃ© finely diced pepper in ghee until softened and the add the shrimp to pan. The shrimp doesn't take long to cook.

I usually season the shrimp in the morning, so when I get home it's a very quick meal.

### diane — 2013-02-28T18:52:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/simple-indian-style-shrimp.6106/post-78336>

Thanks for sharing! I don't cook with turmeric enough - so thank you for the inspiration.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-28T20:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/simple-indian-style-shrimp.6106/post-78372>

# Thread: New Supplements for activating BAT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-supplements-for-activating-bat.6109/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jamezduffy — 2013-02-28T16:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-supplements-for-activating-bat.6109/post-78300>

Saw this new bit of research which was studied in humans and found that, "Grains of paradise," (Aframomum melegueta) activated BAT!

[http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract?fromPage=online&aid=8815293](http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract?fromPage=online&aid=8815293)

Checking the web, I found the Grains of Paradise for sale on eBay & some other websites. Couldn't find any alcohol extracts like used in the study though.

In another study, Conjugated Linoleic Acid (CLA)--which is easy to find for purchase--has been found to increase BAT in mice: [http://www.jlr.org/content/early/2013/02/11/jlr.M030924.short](http://www.jlr.org/content/early/2013/02/11/jlr.M030924.short)

The best results with CLA were found giving them 0.2% of their bodyweight in CLA. To get to that dosage, I'd have to take 9 of these: [http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-cla-1500](http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-cla-1500)

A bottle of CLA at that dosage would last me 10 days. Worth trying while doing ice baths? Worth trying the Grains of Paradise? Maybe Dr. Kruse could weigh in...

# Thread: What you have measured and learned about EMF radiation / protection..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-you-have-measured-and-learned-about-emf-radiation-protection.6113/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-02-28T18:40:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-you-have-measured-and-learned-about-emf-radiation-protection.6113/post-78328>

Okay I thought I will share what I have been measuring with my EMF meter and maybe other have stuff to add too, with different and better meters maybe. I have a Trifield Meter...

Here at home I was happy to see that my bedroom was practically zero.. yay! I always have the breaker off there and zero electricity too. I only sleep there so it is easy!
Then I measured my laptop.. and my old fashioned wired computer (all wired, mouse too). The laptop was horrendous..!!! Even if not plugged and only working with battery. Really bad! My other computer was bad only on the backside of the computer, there it was really bad but screen and mouse and dashboard were quite fine! So I just keep the computer as far away as it gets and it has zero radiation where I sit then.
Fridge was not bad but my parents fridge downstairs was way worse.. it is a big American fridge.. and mine is a Samsung.. so it looks there are big differences!
Hairdryer was horrendous.. but hair straightener was way less bad. Still bad though.
All dimmed lights were really bad, the more I dimmed the stronger the radiation.. crazy! Who would have thought that...? I was so happy to be able to dim the light in my bathroom when I needed to use it late after dark.. Now I use only a candle instead. Much more cosy. I shower with the candle lit and I love it.. after realizing water is always good I shower again (in addition to my dip in the river) and in candle light and wow it is magic..lol I have this beautiful "stony" corner in my bathroom and I put the candle there and it is all amazing! I use the light as little as possible even in day time.
Heaters were bad too, and it was huge difference on lamps! Old fashioned light bulbs had very little radiation but fluorescent were really bad.. As my parents saw it they immidiately replaced them all with old lamps and everything looks more cosy for sure! Cool..

I took it to work yesterday too and measured in the train and in the city too.
In the train the worst place was where you come in, the entrance and then close to the windows. The best place to sit was in the middle, it had almost zero radiation. If someone was sitting close and talking in their cellphone.. it was a different story.. help! In the city many places were so bad it was way way more than 100 milligauss.. no idea how much because that is the max on my meter..
My workplace was pretty bad too but not all places. Many places were a bit below 3 milligauss, which is where it starts to get really unhealthy. I showed my boss and my coworkers where to not spend too much time.. and my boss where pretty pleased when I tested he's desk to be less than 3 milligauss... ha It was really fun in fact. All the guys wanted me to test how strong they radiated... lol.. guys!

Some shops in the city was way over max.. I feel bad for the ones working there.. OMG. to be in that strong radiation 8 hours a day.. crazy!

Then my hubby, ex-hubby or best friend.. I dunno..lol but anyway, he was here and I measured he's cellphones.. he has two and one in each pocket. He got so scared that when he leaved for work next morning he said, Inger, check here! And he had placed both cellphones in a portfolio he showed me proudly.. yay! Now he is here again for visit and he is sleeping on the couch right now and had the cellphones at the floor by the couch.. and I took some thick tinfoil and wrapped them in...
He said he is going to create some business how to protect yourself better.. ha. He is a conservative, that must be why..lol
He spend pretty much time, days and night, in the big city, and always when he is here he sleeps so much! A crazy amount.. huh! I bet he can relax so well here because of the way less radiation.

I have to say I am really happy for the meter even if it was not too expensive, it helps huge to know what to change for sure!

### diane — 2013-02-28T19:23:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-you-have-measured-and-learned-about-emf-radiation-protection.6113/post-78360>

Ingrid - thanks for the info! If I had a meter, I know I would be doing what you are - pointing it at everything! LOL. Then probably making charts and graphs - oh the fun i would have.

### Meg — 2013-02-28T20:29:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-you-have-measured-and-learned-about-emf-radiation-protection.6113/post-78368>

### SeaHorse — 2013-02-28T22:03:54+0200

I have bought one of these too...very useful and really wakes you up doesn't it to see the the meter jumping up to the highest measurement? One thing it doesn't do is wifi...so I'm just trying to use my cell phone coverage to map that out in our house. (haven't used it for anything else for two weeks) The front of the house picks up signal, but not the back..which is where our bedroom is luckily. Our daughter is officially leaving her room. She never liked to sleep there and was always ending up bringing a blanket into our room in the middle of the night and sleeping on the floor. She's 14, so it's not about being alone...I think she's been getting the full brunt of the wifi and all the power lines which come into the house outside her window. So we're trying to work out another sleeping space for her...

We've made a number of other changes too, once we had a walkthrough with the meter...we all watched Resonance, the movie that Dr. K put in one of the first emf blogs. That helped take the pressure off me requesting more changes to our household...in this case my family was leading the charge.

Yes, that exactly is my meter! true, it does not measure wifi, we have wifi at work in every floor  but I tried to hold it onto it but nothing noticeable.. they are high on the roof though so I could not come close. I measured the smart meter at our house and it had crazy high pulsed EMF radiation! Way higher than the meter could measure... but only when I hold it very close.

### diane — 2013-03-04T00:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78836>

I love coconut water, and it seems to have good properties that could help with hydration.

From Wikipedia:

> Coconut water has a high potassium content and contains antioxidants.\[4\] It also contains cytokinins\[5\] which promote plant cell division and growth.

I just am careful and read the ingredients as many brands have added sugar. But there are some that don't.

Does anyone else drink coconut water?

### BJK77 — 2013-03-04T01:07:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78839>

Fellow coconut water lover here, but unfortunately I've found that even the higher natural sugar content doesn't suit me very well. I've tried fermenting it to reduce the sugars, but for some reason it doesn't always seem to work.

I do find if I'm feeling super dehydrated though that a couple of ounces helps without any adverse effects.

### diane — 2013-03-04T01:54:09+0200
