Interesting. What happens when you drink it?

### endless — 2013-03-04T03:30:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-water.6154/post-78865>

# Thread: Pentacyclic triterpenes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pentacyclic-triterpenes.6169/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-04T21:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pentacyclic-triterpenes.6169/post-78949>

I was searching for the effects of Pentacyclic Triterpenes on brown fat generation and found that ursolic acid, oleanolic acid, and betulinic acid have wide ranging health effects. Ursolic acid can increase brown fat, at least in mice. UA seems to convert between Oleanolic acid and UA in the body. Betulinic acid, UA, and OA are being studied for wide ranging health effects including obesity reduction and immunity. It is interesting that birch bark and chaga mushroom which grows on the birch bark in the far cold north have relatively high amounts of 1 or more of these PT's. Chaga is considered a medicinal or adaptogenic mushroom. Rosemary and apple peels are high in PT's, too.

Do PT's have a place in the overall Rx?

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-05T04:40:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79012>

Holy freakin moly. I just got hell in my esophagus. This happened a few days ago, I assumed I had maxed out on my HCl, took spoonful of baking soda in water, and it went away. It was BAD - I was listless b/c of the pain in my chest and throat.

As soon as it happened today, I knew why, b/c the only thing I've consumed in the last three hours is one cap of N-acetyl l-cysteine. Oh my gosh. Wow. Pain.

Just took the baking soda and now I'm burping. The burps just taste like I drank a bunch of carbonated water (probably a volcano in my gut, lol).

Anyone else experience this? What is NAC doing that is making this crazy heartburn? I just googled it and found people who have experienced this and they say to take it with lots of water. I don't know why baking soda seems to neutralize it if it's not an acid problem, but what the heck is happening?

I've been taking 1800mg a day for almost a year and never had this till this week. Today I definitely took it on an empty stomach (apparently a no-no), but the other day, it was \*not\* on an empty stomach. Both times were the final (third) dose for the day.

I welcome any input!

### TheKid — 2013-03-05T05:59:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79025>

It is a mucolytic. So you have unwittingly compromised the mucous layer that protects the esophagus and maybe the stomach.

I just did the same thing about a week ago, and I am still not entirely well.

[http://www.thoracic.org/clinical/co...-there-for-copd/what-are-mucolytic-agents.php](http://www.thoracic.org/clinical/copd-guidelines/for-patients/what-kind-of-medications-are-there-for-copd/what-are-mucolytic-agents.php)

### TheKid — 2013-03-05T09:37:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79046>

MamaGrok,

Because of the mucolytic effect, anything else that might irritate the esophagus and/or stomach may have an amplified impact. Coffee, for instance, is a well known irritant for those who's guts aren't optimal. So if you've been drinking Optimal/Bulletproof coffee, you may be throwing fuel on the fire. I am not sure what the best Rx is to allow the mucous to reestablish itself and for things to settle down.

My reflix has been so bad that it is now basically a GERD flare that is very resistant. I have actually succombed to taking a proton pump inhibitor under the advice of my doctor on a one off basis to tamp down on the erosive effects. I have a strong predisposition to esophageal cancer, so I don't want to play around. But I would imagine that from a dietary perspective, you could do a few days of "gentler" foods that might allow things to heal. If you come up with something, let me know.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-05T14:23:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79058>

What about aloe vera or slippery elm?

### ashryn — 2013-03-05T14:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79064>

Marshmallow root too I think.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-05T15:59:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79077>

Thanks, TheKid! I haven't had any pain in between episodes, other than a tiny bit this morning. I don't ever drink coffee, but I do have a relatively benign cold that is producing a lot of mucus across the board. I wonder if that is helping any.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-08T02:18:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nac-heartburn.6174/post-79568>

LOL, I have no idea what that means.

I'm so grateful for the responses on this thread, b/c I have not had one bit of heartburn ever since reading this. I make sure ever dose of NAC is accompanied by lots of food, lots of water, or both.

I have read this whole forum to see if i can work things out, but mine are in metric, so there's no easy comparison.. I have posted the ranges, most things look ok, but I know there are things you can tell with labs that I won't find on websites or in doctors offices..

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-07T00:26:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-hack-me.6177/post-79365>

Here are a few things I see (now that I have a moment to look at them):
1\. Thyroid dysfunction, although no current signs of Hashimotos (keep testing), but may be caused by something else since the TSH is OK
2\. AM Cortisol is low (brain disease) - sun in morning, Leptin Rx, no light at night, CT, EMF (bad for everything!)
3\. Glucose too high
4\. IGF appears to be good (in top of range)
5\. Iron saturation - whoa! That seems super high although ferritin is OK but serum iron seems a little high. Do you have hemachromotosis?
6\. Vit D is good!

You probably knew some of what I just said but I thought I would throw what I saw in the comments anyway! If you put it all together does it mean anything to you? Sounds like a consultation to me (before the big hibernation)!

> [Patty Cakes said:](/goto/post?id=79365)

> Here are a few things I see (now that I have a moment to look at them):

Thanks Patty,
the thyroid thing is shocking to me because I never considered it might be an issue, though it seems to explain the inability to lose weight past a certain point. I had a look to see what I could do to improve the ratio, and I am already doing all the things.. (Apart from meds, which I want to avoid if I can) i have a feeling that if I had tested 6 months ago we'd have seen something much uglier, because my sleep is good, and energy a million times better..
I will double my milk thistle dose, make sure I eat b 12 foods, and figure a way to get some iodine into me, and CT the thyroid.. Test again in 6 months and see what happens... Does this sound sensible enough? Or is thyroid a thing that can't fix itself?

About the AM cortisol.. What should it be? I thought it was ok. It might be something to do with having the test 5 hours after waking that day, so it was almost PM cortisol as far as my body is concerned (wake at 5am watch the sun rise) test was supposed to be at 8 am but they kept us waiting 2 hours.

i don't understand the glucose score either.. I had fasted for 19 hours (unintentionally) prior to the test, and I eat no sugar, and less than 10g carbs per day. How could it be too high? What am I making sugar out of? This is all so confusing!

There is some haemochromotosis in my family, but I have never had a diagnosis. I give blood a couple of times per year which might have kept it in check, but I haven't for about 12 months. I thought to recheck again after a few months and see if there is an improving trend..

Do you think it is worth testing again in 3 months instead? I don't really know how long these things take to change either.

Thanks so much for helping

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-the-canadians-rt3-testing.6178/post-79070>

To the Canadians on the forum, yes, I did get my RT3 results! Gamma Daynacare labs did the testing and I got the results yesterday. I know they're not in all provinces, but perhaps the lab you deal with will send to them?

Mine says 13.4 ng/dL (9.2 - 24.1). I have no clue what that means. LOL. But trying to look it up to get some info.

Just wanted to share because I know a lot of people are under the impression that you can't get RT3 tested here. Well, I found it!

### shilohman — 2013-03-06T01:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-will-be-1st-to-paint-your-bedroom-with-high-frequency-shielding-paint.6191/post-79205>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=79141)
>
> This is actually on our list of things to do - one thing you need to note - is once painted you need to ground the wall... DH is trying to determine if grounding the wall btwn 2 rooms -where the total sq footage is less than 1000 sqft is sufficient for both - or would we need to ground both rooms...

If the walls of both rooms are electrically connected, then you just need to ground at one point. If they are not, then you need to ground each room. Having an electrician install an isolated ground at the circuit breaker box might be your best way to ground the rooms.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-will-be-1st-to-paint-your-bedroom-with-high-frequency-shielding-paint.6191/post-79219>

I am not interested in the paint. It is permanent, and who knows if it works? If I was to go through all the bother of shielding, I think I would like copper sheeting. It is beautiful, and I could take it down if I moved.

### Optimalbound — 2013-03-06T11:19:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-will-be-1st-to-paint-your-bedroom-with-high-frequency-shielding-paint.6191/post-79276>

The Y-shield carbon paint looks like an excellent product but the documentation I've found so far does not say how well it works below 1GHz.

In order to make a proper faraday cage, I would think that one would have to paint the floor, too.

In any case, I'll wait for the EMF Rx before I start investing time and money on any given product or solution.

### HoneyChild — 2013-03-06T14:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/who-will-be-1st-to-paint-your-bedroom-with-high-frequency-shielding-paint.6191/post-79292>

Here's a cheaper alternative using "emergency blankets"...

[http://educate-yourself.org/cn/microwaveshielding09jan12.shtml](http://educate-yourself.org/cn/microwaveshielding09jan12.shtml)

I remember several years ago my dad installed "space blanket" insulation in the loft and then tried to get a TV signal using an indoor aerial but failed. That's when I realised that the aluminium coating was blocking the signal.

I also found this comment on a website from someone who's done the same thing...

> I recently relocated to a house that is 500 metres from a cellphone mast, and for the first time in my life I began to suffer horrible nightmares. Each night I would take half an hour to get to sleep, then have a nightmare and awaken within two hours and a half hours of going to bed. This two and a half hour cycle would repeat three times per night. Some mornings I would be nervous about driving because I was so shaken after the night before. After three weeks I realized the likely cause of the problem, and as an experiment I bought three highly reflective metallized mylar emergency (or "space" blankets) and fixed them around the two walls that face towards the mast with BluTac. This cost the princely sum of 11 euros. The blankets reach about 1.5 metres up the wall, I'm about half way that height above the floor in bed when I sleep. I took no further precautions - no earthing of the blankets, nothing covering the upper parts of the walls or the ceiling. It did the trick. The nightmares stopped that night and have not returned after a week, and although I do still awaken early, it happens only once a night, within half an hour of when I'd prefer to have awoken. When the camping shop has some more emergency blankets in stock I intend to cover the remaining parts of the walls and ceiling, but the worst is, I believe, over. There is only one other factor which I think might be connected to this, and I'd be very interested to see if it tallies with anyone else's experience. Back when we used mechanical, wind-up watches, I could never wear one. Even very expensive ones would keep terrible time and often stop altogether within a day of my putting them on. I've met several other people who had the same problem with watches over the years. I wonder if some of us might generate a more pronounced electrical field around our bodies than others, which interferes with the fine workings of mechanical watches and makes us more vulnerable to EM radiation by giving the transmitter something to act on?
>
> [http://www.electrosensitivity.org/cure.htm](http://www.electrosensitivity.org/cure.htm)

# Thread: Addressing Technology Addictions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2013-03-06T03:11:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79242>

Looking around today, I saw and heard so many kinds of devices. I heard pings from iPads in three different nearby offices and cell phones ringing. This is on top of the computer and/or laptop and phone already on each desk.

Speaking for myself, I know that my iPad has become an addiction of sorts. It is the original issue and was a gift. This tablet has wormed its way into my life deeper and deeper, until I was bringing it with me frequently. It has travelled to work every day and sat in its place of honor on my desk – next to my computer for three years.

This device made it easy to connect with my family/friends, send a quick message, check my personal email, look at posts on this forum, etc. I used to access the Internet through company wifi. When we upgraded phones I began to use my hot spot. \*_Bad, bad, bad…_

I took the day off on Monday. Spent some time reflecting on my addiction as I sat outside under the sunny sky, while lying down in the grass. The outcome is that I've made the decision to address the iPad addiction.

Step one: Admit I am addicted to the darn thing

Step two: iPad does not come to work with me any longer. I left it home today and survived (imagine that!) Nothing to distract me on my desk other than actual work… Well, my husband kept texting me on the phone. Finally had to tell him I had WORK to do. In retrospect, this might actually be harder on him

Step three: Cell phone will not be used for a hot spot. My surf time on the iPad will now be limited to a brief look in the early morning and some additional time in the evening.

It's a step in the right direction.

i had to google 'cell phone hot spot', my kids would be so ashamed LOL. Good for you; it sounds as though it wasn't as hard as you thought it would be.

On the days I allow myself to limit my computer to an hour I do feel a loss. I really need to address this feeling of loss and then I hope to move past it with regards to what it does for my health. Like what am I afraid I am going to miss out on? lol

I think you have much more of a life then me. I am going to want to work on changing that.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-06T21:33:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79328>

This is hard for me as well. I don't have an iPad just a MacBook Pro that I use for everything. It stays charged for about 5 hours so I charge it at night and keep it going during the day. I carry it from room to room as I work on it. I know this is a problem but I haven't figured out how to get around this for work just yet. I'd much rather be on the computer than the TV. A few years ago I took to reading at night before bed so that has saved me a bit in my addiction to my laptop. My challenge will be to cut my time down....an hour a day would be tough right now so I am going to try for three hours a day. Hey, gotta start somewhere!

I'm sporting an old school iPhone 3Gs, which is also bad but I am not addicted to that. I forget to take it with me during the day and no longer get as many calls as I used to. I really only use it as an emergency item. Thank goodness something is under control!

### Birdy — 2013-03-07T02:10:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79383>

I guess I am more old school, but I carve out some time for each of the activities, and try not to do everything all at the same time. I found that when I was working and trying to cram a "hello" in every once in awhile, I had no time for either. I was at work for more and more hours, but working less and less, and felt justified in adding other activities because I was working so hard. But the truth is, when you cram your daily life in between the spaces of your work life, it is like you really never get to have a real life.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T16:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79465>

This is something that until we started keeping the wifi off - except for specific things (taxes, order b-day presents, supplements, and depositing checks) it was a big problem. especially for me. you really don't realize how much you get sucked into the vacuum that the internet is, until you limit it. Its gotten easier at home. b/c its now a PITA to turn on the router, wait for it to boot up, and then turn on the wifi (I still need to buy that multiple hardline router) so it doesn't get turned on... Its different at work though - I spend all day schlepping around the web doing research for my client - and short breaks to hop in here or FB.

I've really focused on keeping my iphone in airplane mode. I keep it in my backpack when going to-from work most of the time. I definitely have become more aware of being in the moment - when the wifi and phone is off. If only we'd all get back to remembering the important things in our lives - isn't the technology

### shilohman — 2013-03-08T02:30:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79571>

The most important time for absolutely no technology is while we sleep, best way to do this is popping the circuit breaker to the bedroom. For the times during the day when we can not get away from technology we can at least mitigate the EMF effects by grounding. The best way to ground is with a earth ground, but if that is not possible then grounding to a un-interruptable power supply (UPS) is the next best solution. Why? Because most UPS systems (the really cheap ones don't) take the AC from the wall and convert it to DC to charge the battery, then they convert the DC back to a square wave AC. The result of this is that any dirty AC is cleaned up and the ground out of the UPS is cleaned up as a result. What most people don't realize is if you are grounded when near EMF the skin effect of electricity causes the EMF to be shunted to the ground and doesn't enter your body. If you want to observe the skin effect of electricity watch this video [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sm6iCl\_qI1w](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sm6iCl_qI1w) and this video [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t23iXhEiQUc](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t23iXhEiQUc)

Note that the high power worker can touch the inside of his suit and the professor is able to touch the inside of the faraday cage with no ill effect. This is the skin effect of electricity. Now read this link (thanks to OptimalBound)
[http://berkeley.intel-research.net/arahimi/helmet/](http://berkeley.intel-research.net/arahimi/helmet/)

In the last link, if the researchers had grounded their helmets, then they would seen that the results would have been zero voltage on the inside of the hat.

So if you have to use technology, get grounded the best way you can.

### SeaJane — 2013-03-08T05:02:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79600>

I just got a Trifield meter today and have been checking around the house with it so when the Hunter Fan question came up I checked ours out. Mine does not emit any electric field and the magnetic field is 1 (on the 0-3 scale) about 1 foot below the center of it. Interestingly, the emf at my desktop computer set-up is 1 or less (on the 0-3 scale). My kitchen is higher around 3-4 depending on what is on and my daughter's room is highest with nothing on (about 3-4) because the main electric feed to the house runs down the side of the house outside her room to the electric meter. Explains a lot of some of her struggles and why she is sleeping so much better the last year since she is now sleeping in our room. Surprisingly, our basement isn't lower. I'm thinking it is because the wiring is older--we had the main house rewired when we moved in.

Nonetheless, I have turned off the wireless except for 1/2 hour in the morning when my husband is using it, I unplug the TV, radio, computer when they are off and not being used. I have started turning the computer off for blocks of time during the day the last couple of days. That has been challenging and enlightening as I realize that it provided a false sense of connection with others. I've found that by having it off, I am connecting more directly with my daughter and my husband, I have called friends (on my corded phone) rather than send an email and have had brief and connected moments that are more rewarding than the sending and receiving of emails. I have a cell phone that I use for emergencies only so I don't have to break that habit. Overall, I am liking the shift in lifestyle and yet, still find it challenging to organize my life in blocks of time.

### shilohman — 2013-03-08T18:59:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/addressing-technology-addictions.6197/post-79677>

Grounding is very important and I know that many think it is difficult to do a work, but if your work place has orange colored outlets for computer equipment to plug into, then this is the perfect place to insert your grounding strap. The orange color means that the circuit is on an

> isolated ground

and things like space heaters are not allowed to plug into these receptacles.

Now fascinating new research is showing stem-cell programming may not be restricted to chemical signaling, that itâ€™s possible it also can be achieved by extremely low frequency signals in electromagnetic fields similar to the fields produced by the human heart and Earth.

This hourlong online presentation will be given by Dr. Carlo Ventura, M.D., a professor and noted researcher at the University of Bologna in Italy. It will be hosted by Institute of HeartMath Director of Research Dr. Rollin McCraty. The online event will be from 11 a.m. to 12 p.m. or 6 to 7 p.m. (PDT) Saturday, April 6. Register Now: 11 a.m. to 12 p.m. Register Now: 6 to 7 p.m.

Dr. Ventura will discuss strategies for programming stem cells that are orchestrated by extremely subtle information delivered via quantum-field signaling. Some experts believe this area of research may lead to revolutionary strategies in regenerative medicine.

Webinar participants will learn about:

Development of a process akin to a "time machine" that is capable of reprogramming adult cells into other types of cells.

A finding that cells can produce acoustic/sound vibrations and the ability of cells to express vibrational signatures, which change depending on the task the cells are performing and which reflect the cellsâ€™ health.

The possibility that sounds emitted by organs contain information that regulates cellular functions at molecular, submolecular, and quantum levels.

Nobel Laureate Luc Montagnierâ€™s revolutionary experiment in which he purports that a virtually identical copy of a fragment of DNA in one test tube was "teleported" via electromagnetic signals to a second test tube containing pure water.

New research being conducted to determine if the results of Montagnierâ€™s DNA teleport experiments can be recreated at the whole cell level in stem-cell cultures.

Featured speaker

Dr. Carlo Ventura, M.D., Ph.D., is a professor of molecular biology in the school of medicine at the University of Bologna, Italy. Ventura is chief of the Laboratory of Molecular Biology and Stem Cell Engineering at the National Institute of Biostructures and Biosystems in Bologna. He has conducted a variety of cardiology-related research at several institutions and among his noted achievements is the discovery of opioid receptors in myocardial cells. Ventura has published more than 120 full papers in prestigious journals of biochemistry, cellular/molecular biology, and cardiology.

Host and interviewer

Dr. Rollin McCraty, Ph.D, is director of the Institute of HeartMath Research Center, Global Coherence Initiative (GCI) Steering Committee member and project coordinator for GCIâ€™s Global Coherence Monitoring System. McCraty has authored many scientific articles, papers and e-books and conducted a wide range of laboratory and field studies. His research background and interests include the physiology of emotions and mechanisms by which emotions influence cognitive processes, behavior and health. He is an authority on heart intelligence, heart-rate variability and heart coherence.

DNA and Cell Reprogramming via Epigenetic Information Delivered by Magnetic Fields, Sound Vibrations and Coherent Water

Join us for a webinar on Saturday, April 6, 2013.

Space is limited, so reserve your webinar seat now by clicking the "Register Now" button below.

Title: DNA and Cell Reprogramming via Epigenetic Information Delivered by Magnetic Fields, Sound Vibrations and Coherent Water

Presented by: Dr. Carlo Ventura and Dr. Rollin McCraty

Date: Saturday, April 6, 2013.

Time: 11 a.m. â€“ 12 p.m. Noon PDT (GMT minus 8 hours)

Saturday, April 6, 2013. Time: 11:00 a.m. â€“ 12:00 p.m. PDT (GMT minus 8 hours)

Date: Saturday, April 6, 2013.

Time: 6 â€“ 7 p.m. PDT (GMT minus 8 hours)

Saturday, April 6, 2013. Time: 6 â€“ 7 p.m. PDT (GMT minus 8 hours)

System Requirements:

PC-based attendees
Required: WindowsÂ® 7, Vista, XP or 2003 Server

MacintoshÂ®-based attendees
Required: Mac OSÂ® X 10.4.11 (TigerÂ®) or newer

# Thread: OMG...love the pate' i just made!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-love-the-pate-i-just-made.6210/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2013-03-06T22:00:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-love-the-pate-i-just-made.6210/post-79339>

Lovin this!!!
Someone on this forum posted a recipe, I think, for chicken liver pate'! I remembered it was reall easy and I made it last night. It's so yum! Basically cooked livers(simmer about 3 minutes in water with onion slices and bay leaf) and butter in a 50/50 mix, whizzed until silky smooth. Oh, you drain the livers first...and add salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Now I can think of other yummy spices to add next time.....

### caroline — 2013-03-06T22:51:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-love-the-pate-i-just-made.6210/post-79345>

> [janagram said:](/goto/post?id=79339)
>
> Lovin this!!!
> Someone on this forum posted a recipe, I think, for chicken liver pate'! I remembered it was reall easy and I made it last night. It's so yum! Basically cooked livers(simmer about 3 minutes in water with onion slices and bay leaf) and butter in a 50/50 mix, whizzed until silky smooth. Oh, you drain the livers first...and add salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Now I can think of other yummy spices to add next time.....

That was me!!! It is so easy and so delicious! Did I actually post it? someone asked awhile ago and I didn't remember where it was ....anyway - I now make it with lamb's liver - It is stronger but still very good and lamb's liver is better for us. I just finished C/T and am shaking so bad I can't type lol!
I do still love it made with chicken livers and always take it with me if going to book club or friends so I have something to eat.

### caroline — 2013-03-06T22:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-love-the-pate-i-just-made.6210/post-79346>

The original recipe has cognac - did you use it? should I repost it?

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-07T00:17:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-love-the-pate-i-just-made.6210/post-79364>

Yes repost it since my palate is craving something like that! I hope chicken livers are not like chicken ...... foul. I want to make this pate!

### janagram — 2013-03-07T00:27:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omg-love-the-pate-i-just-made.6210/post-79367>

Oh yes, repost, Caroline! I used brandy.

# Thread: Lets talk about optimal beds
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### freshveggies — 2013-03-07T18:35:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79487>

I have been thinking about my bed that is very full of metal coils that act like an antenna. Here are a couple of articles that talk about beds:

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...gigantic-cancercausing-radiation-antenna.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2010/08/18/your-box-spring-mattress-could-be-acting-as-a-gigantic-cancercausing-radiation-antenna.aspx)
[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...Sleep-Matters-If-You-Want-a-Healthy-Baby.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2009/04/30/Why-Where-You-Sleep-Matters-If-You-Want-a-Healthy-Baby.aspx)

Now to find a bed that doesn't have coils and that is comfortable. The ones I have looked at so far are not around here and I would have to fly across the country to just try it out. Some do have a return policy. The other aspect that I was also concerned about is chemical free.

another option. What if I got a regular foam mattress--not memory foam as I do not do well with those - and encased it with this:
[http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html](http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html)

pore size is 2.9 microns. I wonder if that would stop the chemicals from leaching out?

Any thoughts?

### [email redacted] — 2013-03-07T18:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79488>

What about futons? I slept on one for years and found it comfortable. I still have one I use for a guest room bed and for taking naps.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T18:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79489>

WE've got a sleep number bed - which I love - though I now wonder if its a good thing? and if maybe going with a memory foam type mattress might be a better solution?

We've had the magnetico sleep pads now for close to 1 month and I love that thing... the kids are sleeping great - less night wakings etc...

as far as emfs - we've gotten very good at turning off both the wifi and router when we use it and most of the time its turned off (going to be adding the printer/computer/monitor to that as well. duh (face palm - why wasn't I thinking about that?)

I loved sleeping on tatami mats when I lived in Japan...very comfortable - there are times that I'd love to have a tatami room for sleeping in... awesomeness (now how do I talk DH into that????

### cantweight — 2013-03-07T19:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79490>

We are due for a new bed (13 yrs on this one!) and was planning on memory foam, but I would suspect they have mad VOC. Natural mattresses are way out of my price range. Any thoughts?

Christi

### shilohman — 2013-03-07T19:38:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79491>

Something you might try since you already have a sleep number mattress is attach a ground wire to the box springs and bed frame, this way any stray voltages that are picked up via the coils/frame can not act as an antenna and expose you. Turning off the circuit breaker to your bedroom would eliminate any EMF from you sleep number electronics. Add a grounding mat grounded to earth to your bed and there really isn't much more you can do.

### Hope — 2013-03-07T19:44:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79493>

I love our lifekind organic latex mattress which we got from lifekind.com...the best gift ever. No coils, no nothing. I had to fight for the non-fire-retardant cover of it with a note from my chiropractor (as I didn't' want to have to breathe in those chemicals), but we did it. It's six years old and still feels new to me, but a bear to move if you have to ever move it. I also love my pillow...though it's not organic  it has done wonders for my neck...and it has a gel cover that keeps my head a little cooler, especially in summer...I really believe in it. Maybe it's just placebo but I love it anyway. Check that out here if you like.....

[http://sleepcity.com/technogel-pillow-by-dr-scholls/](http://sleepcity.com/technogel-pillow-by-dr-scholls/)

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T19:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79495>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=79491)
>
> Something you might try since you already have a sleep number mattress is attach a ground wire to the box springs and bed frame, this way any stray voltages that are picked up via the coils/frame can not act as an antenna and expose you. Turning off the circuit breaker to your bedroom would eliminate any EMF from you sleep number electronics. Add a grounding mat grounded to earth to your bed and there really isn't much more you can do.

That's a great idea shiloman... the one great thing about the sleep number - is the base is plastic & the actual mattress is memory foam and air tanks. but grounding it would be a great idea....only just recently discovered that our bedroom & office outlets are not grounded... going to have to remedy that. I need to have DH help me put a grounding sheet on our bed and ground it outside....

one of the best places I've slept in - is at my aunts house that's about 1hr north of los cabos - she's off the gird, has a water cistern, 150m from the Pacific Ocean & limited cell service - you need a satellite capability in your cell phone to get it to work. Every single bed frame is made of adobe, with just a mattress on in... best sleep ever there... now I know why!

### freshveggies — 2013-03-07T21:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79509>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=79493)
>
> I love our lifekind organic latex mattress which we got from lifekind.com...the best gift ever. No coils, no nothing. I had to fight for the non-fire-retardant cover of it with a note from my chiropractor (as I didn't' want to have to breathe in those chemicals), but we did it. It's six years old and still feels new to me, but a bear to move if you have to ever move it. I also love my pillow...though it's not organic  it has done wonders for my neck...and it has a gel cover that keeps my head a little cooler, especially in summer...I really believe in it. Maybe it's just placebo but I love it anyway. Check that out here if you like.....
>
> [http://sleepcity.com/technogel-pillow-by-dr-scholls/](http://sleepcity.com/technogel-pillow-by-dr-scholls/)

what does your latex mattress feel like? What firmness level did you get? Does it indent a lot?

### prAna303 — 2013-03-07T21:28:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-79513>

My optimal bed as i said in an earlier post is simple a reindeer skin in the wild. Do sleep outside for about 100 nights a year but will start doing it even more soon. At "home" a simple bamboo mat with a sheet.

### Martha Ray — 2019-10-08T04:10:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-optimal-beds.6221/post-279805>

> [freshveggies said:](/goto/post?id=79487)
>
> I have been thinking about my bed that is very full of metal coils that act like an antenna. Here are a couple of articles that talk about beds:
>
> [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...gigantic-cancercausing-radiation-antenna.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2010/08/18/your-box-spring-mattress-could-be-acting-as-a-gigantic-cancercausing-radiation-antenna.aspx)
> [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...Sleep-Matters-If-You-Want-a-Healthy-Baby.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2009/04/30/Why-Where-You-Sleep-Matters-If-You-Want-a-Healthy-Baby.aspx)
>
> Now to find a bed that doesn't have coils and that is comfortable. The ones I have looked at so far are not around here and I would have to fly across the country to just try it out. Some do have a return policy. The other aspect that I was also concerned about is chemical free.
>
> another option. What if I got a regular foam mattress--not memory foam as I do not do well with those - and encased it with this:
> [http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html](http://www.natlallergy.com/prod/1740/zippered-mattress-covers-bedcare-elegance.html)
>
> pore size is 2.9 microns. I wonder if that would stop the chemicals from leaching out?
>
> Any thoughts?

**Bedding Thoughts abound....Cannot yet post at 'Raise head of bed 10-30 degrees or leave flat?' thread but if anyone is looking to Elevate their head at night.I found Avana Elevator Mattress a USA company out of Atlanta... they have 5", 7" and mentioned a 12" as well. Fits below current mattress and above box springs or slats. They sell on _Amazon_, also saw on _eBay_ and _Overstocks_ and at their own web site.**
**[https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html](https://www.avanacomfort.com/mattress-elevator.html)**
**I have lots of questions and have left word for _Avana Comfort_ to get back to me.

Just thought I'd post this if someone is looking for a wedge to elevate.

I spent years looking for a 'healthy' bed... settled on a very comfortable but very heavy king 100% natural latex with 2 twin slat foundation and now interested in elevating head as well as adding a Magnetico Sleep Pad. With Magnetico so many choices and I can't imagine how this will layer and if slat foundation will handle all the weight. Good thing I am losing weight on the Epi-Paleo Rx.**
**I am also 'princess and the pea' sensitive and hen pecking picky.
**....seems our bed is kinda like a complex puzzle and also similar to a boat as a bottomless expense.****

# Thread: Kicking it "Old School"  - what have you done this week w/o the help of technology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T21:12:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79506>

So, on monday I had to pick up my cousin at the airport - she flew in from Kenya. she lost her iphone sim card that allows it to work in the US. So what did we do - I went to pick her up, and instead of going to the cell phone waiting area - I just drove around waiting for her to come out, after clearing customs/immigrations. I waited to get to the airport 1hr after her flight landed and had to circle for 15-20min - but it was easy we connected.

As I was doing this - I really began to understand how "Dependent" we've become on technology - especially after Peter's post in the Technology addiction post. But the reality is we're not dependent - we're addicted and we really don't need this crap anyway.... yeah it helps make our lives easier but at what costs?

so in what ways have you been kicking it "Old School"?

### prAna303 — 2013-03-07T21:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79508>

Stopped using technology to "hook up", today i simply show up at friends and if the are not there, so what. If someone needs to ge in touch with me, go to my door and knock, find me at the local cafÃ© or send a letter. I never use a phone today, i simply stay present in the world and boy such nice meetings one can do there.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T21:27:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79512>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=79508)
>
> Stopped using technology to "hook up", today i simply show up at friends and if the are not there, so what. If someone needs to ge in touch with me, go to my door and knock, find me at the local cafÃ© or send a letter. I never use a phone today, i simply stay present in the world and boy such nice meetings one can do there.

Love this! I want to get to this point....

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-07T23:26:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79533>

I just don't answer my phone most of the time because I don't keep it on my body. This gets people to stop calling/texting and they will use email instead. This way I can get on the computer and do a check in with friends. But it's on my time and trying to cut the computer time down.

Although it is raining today the weather is getting nicer. I'm starting to get my DH to eat dinner outside and not in front of the TV. Once we are out there he will sit in front of the fire pit for a few hours. Cutting his EMF exposure even if he doesn't know there is a method to my madness!!!

### Destiny — 2013-03-08T02:57:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79576>

My cell phone -that is one habit I don't need to kick. Never was a big fan of it. I don't get that some people can talk and talk on their phones all the time. My family and friends always complain that I never answer my phone. I am famous for finding some messages a week or more later. Very often, my phone is just dead since I forget to charge it.

I don't know why, but I don't like it, it just bothers me.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-08T03:36:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79586>

Just for kicks this week, I used the paper bank statement and balanced my checkbook (without a calculator) to see if I still had that skill. Remember paper and pencils?

It's much easier to log into your account and keep up with it daily...but if technology ever takes a crap I can still manage the old way.

### Destiny — 2013-03-08T14:26:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kicking-it-old-school-what-have-you-done-this-week-w-o-the-help-of-technology.6222/post-79640>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=79586)
>
> Just for kicks this week, I used the paper bank statement and balanced my checkbook (without a calculator) to see if I still had that skill. Remember paper and pencils?
>
> It's much easier to log into your account and keep up with it daily...but if technology ever takes a crap I can still manage the old way.

It is also good to keep your brain sharp!

# Thread: Timing of DHEA / Pregnenolone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-03-07T21:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79511>

I always take my DHEA and Pregnenolone in the morning with my BAB. I had to start work extra early this morning and it just hit me that I forgot to take them! I can't recall if there was a reason behind my taking them first thing in the AM or if I just did it because it was (supposed to be!) easier to remember then.

So, the question is should I take them when I get home around 4 PM or is that too late and I should just skip them? When do you all take them? My gut says it should be fine since DHEA helps with sleep anyway, but I just wanted to make sure.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-07T21:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79515>

I think you should be ok... I take an afternoon/evening dose... and no such issues that impact sleep or anything else from my perspective...

### diane — 2013-03-07T21:43:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79517>

I would say try for a few days and find out if it works for you.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-07T22:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/timing-of-dhea-pregnenolone.6225/post-79521>

Thanks! I knew someone on here would have an answer. Y'all rock

# Thread: Wine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-08T04:19:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79592>

I threatened to start a thread on wine in someone's journal.....

Ode to wine.
Yes, I love it.

Similar to what other people have said, I think I just have to give it up. Although I love it so. I'm assuming that's related to my addiction to all things sugar too. I have definitely cut back, and most times, I have a glass or two, although occasionally more. I was hoping that the water in it was beneficial  but read Jack's post about only 1 glass.

Share your thoughts on wine! And maybe drink a glass while posting. LOL.

### endless — 2013-03-08T04:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79598>

Don't tempt me.....

I too love wine. I used to be fond of beer as well, but since going gluten and then grain free, I've switched almost entirely to wine. I am also fond of really good tequila. I have a hard time stopping at one glass....I usually like to have two, or sometimes even three. Unfortunately it makes my sleep SUCK terribly. I'm not sure if it's the dehydration that causes that or what but it's getting to the point that it's not worth it.

### Inger — 2013-03-08T09:07:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79614>

I love wine too.. as a waitress I know quite a lot about them too and I am picky... Bad quality wine makes me feel way worse.. so if I take a glass it must be high quality.

I need to be very careful with wine too. If I drink even one glass too often I have the feeling I get a tiny bit depressed somehow. I also can get pain in my hip bones from it when I drink 2-3 glasses. So to feel the best I need to drink very little. I am happy that wine is expensive here and I have too little money to buy it often. That is saving me!

I really feel I am way better of without it. At least now in winter. I really feel I tolerate cold way less when I do alcohol (I only drink wine, nothing else). I can feel it the days after too, I think so at least..

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T11:23:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79623>

No more than three glasses and I still bounce out of bed in the morning.. Sleep all the way through, feel rested and good all day... Only since turning off the lights half arsed CT, and grounding a lot though. Before than I took codeine to make sure I slept ok.. I have been thinking I need to ease up though. If I want to kick off the rest of the weight.. I know it messes with hormones

### Destiny — 2013-03-08T13:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79632>

Yes, I love it too but my max has always been 1 glass or even less. I know I don't tolerate much of alcohol.

Lately, I am not drinking any. I had 1/2 glass during Christmas and got very dehydrated during the night (I was thirsty like a camel). Then, I felt like having a sort of hangover in the morning.
It was very unpleasant so that really spoiled my appetite for wine.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-08T14:31:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79642>

Wine is one of the few things I find I really miss . . . . that and chocolate chip cookies! I always found it strange in the past that red and white wine affected me differently. Of course this was always drinking more than one glass, but white wine seemed to give me a headache whereas red wine messed with my sleep . . . or maybe it was the other way around! It's been so long since I've had more than one glass that I can't remember now

I think sulfites are a problem for me now too and I know there are a few wines without it still seems like too much of a bother to go looking for them, so for the time being I'm wine-less

### janagram — 2013-03-08T18:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79675>

I adore wine and my DH makes his own (we have about 75 vines in yard, but he buys grapes from vintners too). His wine is awesome......but I drink far less of it than I used to or than I am inclined....leaving me at about 7 glasses a week. Oh, and I like many other spirits too, but partake vey rarely. I had a "skinny" margarita yesterday, tequila, limes, soda water. Loved it.
Oh, I was hoping that a good islay (pronounced "eye-la") scotch would be full of iodine and be "prescribed"!!! (research!)

### Rachelle — 2013-03-08T21:19:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79694>

I gave up wine and only drink a good vodka if and when I drink.  I get a hangover with wine and do not with vodka. Have no clue why

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T21:26:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wine.6232/post-79695>

Only the good stuff, only a glass, and only every couple of months. I savor it when I drink it now and really taste it. If I drink two glasses or more my sleep is disrupted and I get the dehydrated thirsty thing going on in the middle of the night as well. It used to be a form of entertainment but now it is pure pleasure since it's so rare.

# Thread: Raw farmed fish bad idea?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-08T19:07:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79678>

So anyone I just wanted to see what would happen...I ate a bit of raw farmed Tilapia which had been frozen in my deep freeze. Are the advantages to raw food changed by freezing? Also I'm wondering if the temperature they were kept at is low enough to get rid of parasites.

I just ate a little bit...not much and than tried to feed the rest to my dog...he wouldn't do it. I did put some Bubbies pickle juice on it...maybe he didn't like that..or maybe its just cause he isn't used to raw food?

Note he does eat small pieces of Bubbies pickles I give him...maybe he just doesn't like the juice.

I'd like to have him on a raw food diet sometime if I can but he isn't on one now so maybe its just an unfamiliarity factor at play. I put some cilantro and parsley on it..perhaps that was part of it?

Any comments on the experiment and issues of raw food in general would be greatly appreciated. I read some articles recently on it..and while I know that wild caught would be preferred...I can't afford wild caught fish much at all...not with my money.

Anyone have any experience on eating raw food? Are there any foods that you can eat raw that aren't wild caught, pastured etc...I guess what I'm asking is can some non ideal food be more of a positive than a negative if prepared properly. Maybe not...maybe using farmed Tilapia was a bad idea. Tilapia is the right spelling isn't it?...I guess its not in the browser dictionary...it says its misspelled but no correction available...lame

### Hope — 2013-03-08T20:48:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79690>

Many people here eat some raw fish etc. Dr Kruse has said farmed fish is still better than most meats etc. FWIW. YMMV

And yes you spelled Tilapia right

I have fed some salmon (raw) to a friend's dog. Eat it right up. Was plain though, nothing more. Smoked too (Just very little). Again, all eaten right up. Maybe try without the sauce and herbs? Raw is GREAT for dogs for sure. Cats too. Have you heard of Pottenger's cats?

### diane — 2013-03-08T22:25:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79705>

My dogs eat a raw diet, but for some reason won't eat raw fish! Sometimes the skin, but not the meaty part. So I cook it a little and they will eat it right up.

### kathylu — 2013-03-08T23:31:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79721>

My local asian market has fresh farmed salmon (origin Canada) for $1.99 per pound on sale. Can't pass that up! I eat a lot of it raw, they clean and filet the fish for me (I keep the bones, skin and heads for other uses). I have sashimi and make ceviche the first night, maybe grill the other filet the next day. It's delicious. I guess wild is better, but this is good too.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T23:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79723>

We have eaten the farmed Scottish salmon raw sashimi style for a couple of years now. It's really fresh tasting and we have never gotten sick. That's got to be a good sign, right?

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-09T00:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79731>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=79690)
>
> Many people here eat some raw fish etc. Dr Kruse has said farmed fish is still better than most meats etc. FWIW. YMMV
>
> And yes you spelled Tilapia right
>
> I have fed some salmon (raw) to a friend's dog. Eat it right up. Was plain though, nothing more. Smoked too (Just very little). Again, all eaten right up. Maybe try without the sauce and herbs? Raw is GREAT for dogs for sure. Cats too. Have you heard of Pottenger's cats?

I know raw is good for dogs its actually ideal from what I've read which makes sense was just not sure why he seemed to avoid it. It being something he doesn't get normally and the things I had on it...were perhaps the issue. I'll try later with fish that has nothing on it to see if he goes after it.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-09T00:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79733>

Thanks for the comments all. Its good to know farmed isn't horrible. Getting used to eating raw fish...isn't going to be easy for me though. Part of its the coldness of it and part of it is the texture.

### diane — 2013-03-09T00:51:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79743>

I eat mine raw with pickled vinegar, or as Kathy said, as ceviche.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-09T01:40:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79751>

We buy sashimi grade salmon that is farmed and eat it raw, but I've never eaten 'regular' farmed fish raw.

### marthabob2u — 2013-03-09T04:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79773>

My Japanese neighbour told me that any fish that starts out in fresh water, then migrates to sea water, may pick up parasites and should be frozen before eating it raw just to be on the safe side. I LOVE raw fish. If anyone cooks my tuna I will get upset!

### Inger — 2013-03-09T09:06:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-farmed-fish-bad-idea.6238/post-79788>

Raiken,
I have been eating all kinds of raw animal food for many years already. I would not eat the tilapia if there was some other option. I always try to check how are they being farmed and feed. It makes a difference, you see. I am very cautious about farmed fish from Asia and never buy those. I can get pollock here very cheap, Pollock from Alaska, wildcaught. It is prefrozen and I do not love the taste raw, but it makes me feel more awesome eaten raw so I usually do it in my mixer with spices, raw onion, raw ginger etc.. roll small balls or "burgers" and it turns out so delish!

Raiken, try to always look for wild caught first. Pre-frozen is okay. And look for where it comes from. Wild and farmed. Pick the cheapest and the best. You will have to spend some time searching, but I bet you will find something better than tilapia. Do not care if it is not your favorite tasting fish, you can spice it and make it tasty, I promise! Always go after how healthy something is and not if you like the taste.

I eat farmed Norwegian Salmon at times, I have been looking into their farming methodes and they are quite ok. Sure they get vaccinated and stuff and vegetable oils but still they get lots of shrimp scales and fish-chowder to feed too. So I sometimes eat it. But I try to really limit it and eat wild instead. Even if my taste buds like the salmon better...

If your intestines are not healthy it might be good to be cautious about parasites, IDK. I never had an issues, but my gut was not that damaged either. For sure a bit but not seriously. Then, if you eat some raw risky fish and then cheat all the time with crap, I guess that is a really bad Idea. I think when you have a very strict and healthy diet you can get away with some parasites too, but you have to forget about cheating. That is how I do, and it works so well!

# Thread: Optimal Spring Planting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T22:49:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79708>

So those of you out there who are planning to do a garden, what are you going to plant? I'm looking for ideas on heirloom organic seeds. We have a rather large space to plant this year and it's time to get going on this project (at least it is here in So Cal). Also any good online resources anyone has to share for info or places to buy the seeds please let me know. Below is my starting list of what I may plant (not in any order). Most of these seeds are from Seeds of Change.

Beets - Golden, Candy stripe (haven't found this one yet)
Satsuki Lemon Cucumber
Lettuce - Butter Crunch Bibb, Lambs Breath (mache), red romaine, rouse de hiver
Rhubarb -
Onions - Yellow cippollini, Suskiora Sweet
Radish - French Breakfast
Swiss Chard
Okra - Star of David
Fennel - Zefa Fino
Melon - Sugar baby watermelon
Tomato - Yellow pear, pineapple, San Marzano (I think), plum and maybe brandywine and Arkansas or some others.

Of course the herb garden of thyme, cilantro, lemon basil, Korean licorice mint, chives, rosemary and parsely.

Let's share our ideas for which is the best company to buy from, the best gardening tips, and even what varieties you have been able to grow in your area.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T23:38:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79722>

Just added the spinach, kale, zucchini, and garlic to the list. I can't believe I forgot the spinach and kale!!! In my defense the list came from DH who is great at researching but I am the cook and I know food better than he does. I knew you all could help!

Thanks also for the resources. I will check those out and probably end up adding more items.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-09T01:42:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79753>

Love that you started this thread Patty! I can't wait to start my spring planting this year, but I've been a little depressed too thinking that I have no reason to grow half the fruits/veggies we enjoyed in the past. I decided to focus on salad veggies (lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, etc) and brassicas with some zucchini and berries. I'll probably skip the melons and starchy veggies . . .

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-09T01:57:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79755>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=79753)
>
> Love that you started this thread Patty! I can't wait to start my spring planting this year, but I've been a little depressed too thinking that I have no reason to grow half the fruits/veggies we enjoyed in the past. I decided to focus on salad veggies (lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, etc) and brassicas with some zucchini and berries. I'll probably skip the melons and starchy veggies . . .

The melons for me was kind of a last minute throw in. During the season I think they will be a nice treat. Oh yes and we just planted two blueberry bushes but honestly I don't have a lot of hope for them since So Cal is not exactly blueberry territory (but the garden shop said these varieties will do well here). Also we were thinking of adding some strawberries since they grow like crazy here. Already seeing the first signs of strawberries at the farmers markets already!

### Huck — 2013-03-09T02:35:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79759>

Broccoli, cabbage, cauliflower.
Peppers??

### nonchalant — 2013-03-09T03:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79766>

What about flowers? Food for the soul (and the bees).

### ashryn — 2013-03-09T03:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79771>

Blueberries grow well in Western Australia.. I think we have a similar climate to soCal

### marthabob2u — 2013-03-09T03:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-spring-planting.6242/post-79772>

Well whenever the snow leaves the garden at home in northern BC (which was June last year) I plan to plant spinach, swiss chard, lettuce, peas, carrots, kohlrabi, pole beans, radishes, a few cylindra beets, green and regular onions, garlic, and whatever else will fit. Homegrown produce is SO much better than store-bought! Success of crops in the north is weather dependent- sometimes we get lucky. And, lucky me, I get to trade excess veggies for fresh wild caught salmon and prawns.

In Mexico this winter I managed to grow tomatoes from seed- cherry tomatoes worked the best. Here i don't fight snow but there are a multitude of bugs, land crabs, leaf-cutter ants, iguanas, and the occasional chicken to fight off.

# Thread: Any suggestions what I need to do ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liv — 2013-03-08T23:07:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79715>

31 Oct 2012 Vit D25 40,4 ng/mL (ref 30-100) I have since then supplemented with 5000 IU every day. Today I got the results taken 5 March 2013 and it was Vit D25 31,3ng/nL(ref 30-100). How can I have so low D-vitamin level even if I have been supplementing with it ?

Other labs Taken 31 Oct 2012
Cholesterol 209mg/dl
Triglyceride 58mg/dl
HDL 61mg/dl
LDL 136 mg/dl
BUN 12mg/dl
Calcium 8.8mg/dl
Creat 0.7mg/dl
BUN/Creat 17.1
Glucose random 86mg/dl
Sodium 142mMol/L
Potassium 4.3mMol/L
Chloride 105mMol/L
CO2 26.6mMol/L
AGAP 14,7mMol/L
HgbA1c 5.5%

Labs taken 7 Dec 2012
Estradiol saliva <0.5 pg/ml low ,(postmenopausal optimal1,3-1.7)
Progestrone saliva 39 pg/ml (12-100 postmenopausal)
Ratiog/E2 78 low (optimal 100-500 )
Testorone saliva 52 pg/ml (16-55 age dependent)
DHEAS saliva 15.7ng/ml (2-23 age dependent)
Cortisol morning saliva 15.7 ng/ml (3.7-9.5)
TSH 1.280

Labs taken 21 Jan 2013
cardio CRP 0.35 mg/l (0.10-3.00)
DHEAS 210mcg/dl (15-170)

I am 52 years and have had big problems with fibroids since age 47. I have had several surgeries to take them away and it finally ended with a hysterectomy in Feb 2012 . Still have my ovaries but started to get menopausal problem in Oct 2012. My gyn MD put me on bio identical hormones now in Jan 2013. I take progestorone 100mg and have a weekly patch with estrogen. I am eating a LCHF low carb high fat diet since fall 2011 but now since a couple of months trying to adjust to epi paleo. I had an Oral Glucose test done in may 2012 it showed 166 mg/dl one hour and 169mg/dl two hours. I had pregnancy diabetes with my third child (have four children born 88,89,98 ,00)so I understand I am on my way to get diabetes if I dont try to be optimal. I have maybe 5-6 pounds extra weight and carries it around my belly. My waist has gone from 36 Inches to 32 inches the last year, but not so much change in my weight. I do understand CT should be my next step but it is so hard to do !
Any suggestions how to get optimal ?

### nonchalant — 2013-03-08T23:18:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79718>

Hi Liv, welcome!
Are you supplementing with D3 in gel capsules? Have you tried liquid vitamin D? I believe others have had success with vaginal application, where oral vitamin D wasn't helping because of leaky gut or other issues.

Your morning cortisol looks high. What is the reference range? You might want to reduce your exposure to light at night. Your cortisol may be stealing pregnenolone from your other steroid hormones, including vitamin D.

Please post ranges with your lab values so others can figure them out.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T23:49:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79724>

Agree w/all Nonchalant said. Also check your thyroid levels as those folks with low thyroid function have a tendency to have trouble in the CT tub due to cold intolerance. Just having your TSH tested in not enough. You need FT4 and FT3 w/Reverse T3 (if you can get it where you live).

### diane — 2013-03-09T00:48:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79741>

For Vitamin D - I was also supplementing and very low. I had to push way more than 5000 IU's to get mine moving up. I do between 10,000 and 20,000 per day, especially in the winter.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T01:32:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79746>

D ... get out in the sun. Barring that get in a tanning bed. That's what I'm doing since supplements didn't raise my levels at all.

I should just put this in my sig line ... my recommendation is to get TS Wiley's book, "Sex, lies & menopause" ... I've been on that protocol since July of 2011 and I feel GREAT. They can have my BHRT when they can pry it from my cold, dead fingers. I had a total hyst at age 51 and went rapidly downhill because of it. Oh ... a kilo's worth of fibroids to boot. I was anemic from the damned things bleeding me dry, literally.

That's all I've got. HTH.

### Liv — 2013-03-09T02:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79756>

Thanks . Cortisol saliva morning 7.6 ng/ml range 3.7-9.5. My supplementation of D3 is in liquid capsules. I just ordered glasses to block blue light from Amazone (the ones Misty has)looking forward to get them. Only other lab I have of thyroid is TSH 3rd gen 2.001mcIU/ml (0.550-4.780) but its quite old taken 5 Oct 2011. Its difficult to get labs because the MD sees in his opinion a healthy woman in front of him but I know my health is not optimal it can be better. I edited and put in the ranges of my lab in my first post.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-09T03:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-79767>

> [Liv said:](/goto/post?id=79756)
>
> . Its difficult to get labs because the MD sees in his opinion a healthy woman in front of him but I know my health is not optimal it can be better.

Good for you, Liv. Don't settle.

You said you have been on a LCHF diet for quite a while. Were the fats animal fats? If so, you should have no skin issues with getting lots of sun. Especially if it is Winter where you are.

### Liv — 2013-03-11T03:42:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-80016>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79767)
>
> Good for you, Liv. Don't settle.
>
> You said you have been on a LCHF diet for quite a while. Were the fats animal fats? If so, you should have no skin issues with getting lots of sun. Especially if it is Winter where you are.

I am using mostly butter(kerrygold from grasfed cows),some coconut oil and some olive oil. I have stopped with all other kinds of vegetable oils. I try to get grass fed meat and also to eat all lot of seafood. Looking forward to Spring Break next week when we will go to a warm sunny place and I will walk bare feet in the ocean as much as possible

### Justfourme — 2013-03-11T04:37:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-80017>

Liv - I just got my vit D test back and my level was 30. My dr is having me take 50,000 of vit D3 every other day for 15 doses. She said that would get levels up where they need to be. I had been taking 4000 in capsule soft gels per day. She also hinted that d3 supplement works better than a D2 rx.

### MsYo — 2013-03-12T04:37:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-suggestions-what-i-need-to-do.6243/post-80147>

My vitamin D3 was 20 almost 18 months ago. It is now up to 72. I take liquid vitamin D3 (CountryLife/vitacost) daily and have had positive results. I also take Lugols for fibroids as well as selenium.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-12T04:41:46+0200

# Thread: Pickup Line
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pickup-line.6246/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7

### Inger — 2013-03-09T09:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pickup-line.6246/post-79789>

Huck.. I am coming too....!!!  (can I take my rotten meat too..?)

Caroline, my suggestions;
Be yourself 100% and enjoy the moment 100%.... be present and enjoy everything! IDK for sure because I am not a guy but I have the feeling guys like when a women do that... And you will like it too..
Never complain. Make sure you fly.... Stay open, embrace the word. Eat lots of raw. When I eat all raw I get a different glow! Do not ask me why... but once a guy come to me at the cafe bar and said.. wow.. you are so alive! And he looked at me so amazed. When I start to eat more cooked I loose that a little bit.
Always look into the eyes, but never stare. Always be polite. Eyes are amazing...

Do not complain to me if you cannot save yourself from guys running after you now.. you asked for it.. ha

### caroline — 2013-03-10T02:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pickup-line.6246/post-79894>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=79789)
>
> Huck.. I am coming too....!!!  (can I take my rotten meat too..?)
>
> Caroline, my suggestions;
> Be yourself 100% and enjoy the moment 100%.... be present and enjoy everything! IDK for sure because I am not a guy but I have the feeling guys like when a women do that... And you will like it too..
> Never complain. Make sure you fly.... Stay open, embrace the word. Eat lots of raw. When I eat all raw I get a different glow! Do not ask me why... but once a guy come to me at the cafe bar and said.. wow.. you are so alive! And he looked at me so amazed. When I start to eat more cooked I loose that a little bit.
> Always look into the eyes, but never stare. Always be polite. Eyes are amazing...
>
> Do not complain to me if you cannot save yourself from guys running after you now.. you asked for it.. ha

I completely and totally agree with what you are saying Inger! That is my life now - just naturally ...I am in awe of everything I see.

But I do think - I don't know how to say this properly - that it is scary or off putting to many people \[not in a bad way\] but they don't get it. It is like it is too strong or something.

I find now that I only seem to connect to like minded people who are inspired by life. There is a peacefullness and calmness that takes place ...whereas others seem to have no interest or life or joy about themselves.

I think it has to bubble up from the inside out and it just escapes naturally and some people don't know what to make of that....

I think it takes an extraordinary person to appreciate all of that.....

Don't know if I really said what I wanted too say......

# Thread: Shocked at Trifield Meter readings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-03-09T20:21:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79829>

I just got my Trifield meter yesterday and have been playing since!! I am really surprised by some of the readings though . . . the kids' ipods barely register (unless charging) and my cell phone is the same, even DH's iphone. Most of the appliances register much, much higher than the handheld electronics. I never would have expected that.

Someone mentioned their ceiling fan had a high reading, but ours shows nothing. CFL bulbs are horrible, but halogen and LED give nothing. This is so interesting. I can't stop playing! Anyone else find something that really shocked them?? Problem is now that the kids saw that their ipods aren't as bad as I've been telling them I'm going to have trouble convincing them to give them up. I was hoping for the opposite to happen.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T20:54:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79833>

Check the iPods when they're connected to wifi and d/l something. Same with the cell phones. It's when they're connecting that I think the real issues begin. I could be wrong. Someone will correct me if I am.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-09T20:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79834>

The Trifield meter is a good starting point. However, no one meter will measure all EMF.

Your answer for the kids is that Wifi on iPads, etc is RF (radio frequency), and the Trifield meter does only a marginal job of detecting RF.

Radio frequency or “RF” refers to high frequency fields and microwaves emitted by microwave ovens, cell towers, cell phones, cordless phones, TV and radio broadcast towers, Wi-Fi, wireless computer devices, and other electronics. The electric and magnetic parts of RF fields are not separated, so you only need one meter to detect the RF field. RF is usually measured in units of microwatts per centimeter squared (μW/cmÂ²).

[http://emfcenter.com/metrsale.htm](http://emfcenter.com/metrsale.htm)

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-09T21:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79837>

Ditto....unfortunately the wifi isn't going to be measured well with the Trifield. We just got our taken out of the house all together. It makes me feel like I'm going in the right direction.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-09T21:18:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79838>

What type of energy are you measuring? Motorized devices and CRT monitors put out huge magnetic fields. Wireless routers, cell phones and cordless house phones definitely put out microwaves between 900MHZ - 2.4+ GHZ. Turn on the phones and they put out more. I have been in home offices that have large amounts of RF. I would take the [Concerto](http://coherentresources.com/emf_protector.php) with me to minimize adverse health effects when working on computers there. The owner's body was in constant pain and her joints were disintegrating literally. This was over 5 yrs ago when I knew less. My measuring device is not a tri-field meter but only detects microwaves. I don't remember the name at this time. Microwaves are arguably the worst emf unless you include the terahertz airport scanners!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-09T21:28:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79843>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=79838)
>
> Microwaves are arguably the worst emf unless you include the terahertz airport scanners!

### kathylu — 2013-03-09T21:31:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79845>

Me too. I was using an electric blanket to pre-warm my bed, then I turned it off before getting in. Thought that solved the problem. No! The meter literally showed more voltage when the blanket was off than when it was on! Now I am unplugging it...problem solved and I still have a warm bed.

### Martin — 2013-03-10T00:09:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79863>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79843)
