I doubt it, it's a measuring device rather than a transmitting device.

### Inger — 2013-03-10T07:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-79905>

Read the instructions that came with the meter to more effective use of it, it will help.
The iPods and cellphones gives out pulsed EMFs, you need to hold the phone to the left side of the meter, very close, and wait. Then you will see how it send the pulsed EMF all the time. Tell your kids the pulsed EMFs are really bad for us as they do not exist in nature at all. Ipod has a place I think on the upper right side where it has way stronger radiation, you must move the meter over it to detect it. You need to touch the screen and have it on, too.

The TriField meter is not too sensitive meter but good enough to get a picture.
Put to cellphones close, maybe a feet away.. and call the other, with the meter in between. You will get shocked at how strong and far it reacts!

### Hope — 2013-03-15T17:39:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80679>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79843)
>
> I've been wondering about exposure to the old fashioned metal detectors. Any harm in that? Probably?

I am sure they are not great. But still better than the new stuff. I refuse them every single time. I Just hope they don't phase out the old kind some day..

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-15T18:09:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80682>

I've turned the wifi off on my phone I won't turn it back on...

### Rachelle — 2013-03-15T18:25:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80685>

I'm so screwed. I live by a transformer grid yard and there is nothing in between my apartment and it. I took out my wifi and I am not watching TV, bought a corded phone and a Himalayan salt lamp, but I know in my heart I am not safe. I have lived here now for a 1 1/2 years and didn't realize there was a transformer grid yard right across from me. What is up with that? I was oblivious to it. I was so worried about everything else going on I missed it.

I really just want to pack up very little, move to an ocean spot and pitch a tent. That is the bohemian in me but there is also the conservative person I grew up to be gnawing in my head.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-15T18:56:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80688>

So my homeopath said it's actually the electricity in my home that is actually guarding me from the strong fields from the grid yard. And to stay off the street--never hang around here. That until I can move.

### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-15T22:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80700>

Sniffed out the house with the Tri-Field meter and, thank goodness, we seem to be doing very well. The bedrooms were clear, but now I can prove to my husband that I am not a "Nervous Nellie" for having wanted nothing plugged in near the beds and having all audio/video equipment plugged into grounded power strips that are turned off when not in use. I am also very glad that I have always made people sit FAR away from the TV, computers, and EdenPure heaters in my home. I did find a mysterious spot in the kitchen wall near where my daughter sits at the kitchen table. I bet it is some sort of fault in the wiring. We will be moving her spot at the table while awaiting an electrician. Our old desktop computer is horrible and so is our multi-function printer. I am glad we barely ever go in the office. I will be keeping them off unless in use. The laptop does not seem to be much of a problem at the distance I sit from it when it is unplugged from its AC adapter. Plugged into the AC adapter seems to make it just a bit worse. The signal from the cell tower 1380 meters away is not perceptible through the windows on the side of the house facing it--I know this is not the correct meter accurately measuring RF, but I am glad that whatever that tower is putting out is imperceptible to the Tri-Field. Working up the courage to go stand by the smart meter and see what it registers on the Tri-Field (again, I realize it is not an accurate meter for RF).

### kathylu — 2013-03-16T04:28:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80721>

I felt better almost immediately when I had the SmartMeter removed from my house. See my journal for the story.

I took the TriField meter out to my car today. I have an Acura 3.2 TL ...oh my god...it's bad! Electric and magnetic measurements literally off the meter, a little better in the back seat. I don't know what to do about it...gotta get reading about mitigation in cars! And I really don't want to think about what I'll find when I measure the Toyota Prius tomorrow (electric hybrid car)...maybe get a bicycle?

### nonchalant — 2013-03-18T03:00:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocked-at-trifield-meter-readings.6255/post-80928>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=80782)
>
> I'd love some input about how to ground this set-up...my poor snake has been well insulated for years and I'd love to get him grounded!

Endless, how about attaching a wire to a flat piece of smooth metal and just set it in the aquarium? If he likes it, he will lay on it.
Otherwise, you could attach a grounding wire to a piece of metal screen or hardware cloth in the bottom, and put rock and such on top.
Either way, you might want to have some wood in there so he can get away from the effect. I think at first the grounding can be a strong sensation, and he might want a break.

# Thread: What is going on with my body? Sick of feeling this way for so long.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MysteryInAZ — 2013-03-09T23:43:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79856>

I was just doing some research on Pregnenolone Steal, which led me to this site.

I'm 34 years old. I stand 5'11 and weigh 186lbs. I work out 4 times a week. I generally eat pretty clean/lean (except for an occasional cheat meal on the weekends), getting about 2600-2700 calories a day (5-6 meals total), 140-160 grams of protein. Most of my diet consists of turkey beef, chicken, green vegetables, oatmeal, eggs, greek yogurt, nuts.

At any rate, I really haven't felt right since 2005. That year I spent much of my time traveling around the country for my job. The trips would last about 4 weeks at a time, up on my feet moving around working 13 hour days 6 days a week. I basically worked myself to the bone, started abusing energy drinks and coffee to keep going. On one of the trips I ended up geting really sick. Had an awful infection in my mouth, 100+ degree temps, body aches. I felt like death. I tried to work through it when finally I checked myself into an emergency room. The doc didn't really have answers for me. He prescribed me antibiotics for the infection and then told me I needed to fly back home and rest up.

After a few weeks the infection cleared up, but I noticed a decline in my overall health. As the months progressed I started experiencing a laundry list of mysterious symptoms, enough of them to feel like a crazy person. How could I feel this way at such a young age? The major culprits were depression, anxiety, body aches, intense fatigue, massive brain fog, poor sleep, loss of muscle definition, poor overall body recovery from workouts, constipation, shallow breathing, strange sugar/salt cravings, nausea, etc, etc. Lack of sex drive also became a problem, which was far cry from my former self. It got so bad, it wreaked havoc on my relationship with a long time girlfriend. I felt like crap and because I didn't want to sleep with her it made her feel like crap. Just a total nightmare situation that ended nightmare-ishly.

I went to various doctors from 2005-2008 and got the run around from all of them. Typical "let's put you on some anti-depressant" crap. None of them ever wanted to take the time to get to the heart of my complaints, just wanted to mask them. I gave up going to them. They ran panels and the common theme among them was always T4 at the bottom of the range, TSH at the bottom of the range, high LDL cholesterol, testosterone at the very bottom of the range. I mean my testosterone when I look back at all the panels was always between 280-320, which would be normal for a near 60 year old man.

I can't stand how I feel. Always having to monitor how much energy I have to expend. Not taking delight in the things I used to. I can't even cry anymore. I always feel like I'm stuck in 2nd gear. Family thinks I've just not tried hard enough all these years. The lack of sex drive is depressing as is the hair loss, which has really gotten aggressive in the past year or so (receding, diffused thinning, scalp itch). Bright lights and wind have become my sworn enemy. Ironically as the hair loss has increased on my head, my body hair growth has picked up. I'm worn out from all the self-grooming I have to do, not to mention so self-conscious about it.

I wish I knew what was going on. My quality of life the last 8-9 years has been so poor and it's no way to live. I need to get panels done, but I'm currently without health insurance. I have serious concerns about my hormones and Thyroid/Adrenal axis. Lots of reading suggests something possibly going on between my testosterone, DHT, and estrogen. Even though my energy levels have sucked, I still force myself to work out. I know this will sound ironic, but I'd say that my workout has gotten more complex and more dedicated in the last 4 years than at any point before that. I've refused to let my condition take that away from me. If you just glanced at me you could tell I worked out, but again I don't feel like I look. I'm concerned though with what role my workout has played in all of this. Is it stressing my body out too much given that it's barely functioning at 50% capacity? What is it doing to my hormones?

Thank you for your time and looking forward to your response.

### diane — 2013-03-09T23:55:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79860>

Hi there. Here's a great success story to read - somewhat similar situation to yours. [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5914-As-Requested-A-Success-Story](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5914-As-Requested-A-Success-Story)

How's your sleep? That can be a clue as well.

If your hormones are issues, then working out is only going to make them worse.

### MysteryInAZ — 2013-03-09T23:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79861>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=79860)
>
> Hi there. Here's a great success story to read - somewhat similar situation to yours. [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5914-As-Requested-A-Success-Story](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5914-As-Requested-A-Success-Story)
>
> How's your sleep? That can be a clue as well.
>
> If your hormones are issues, then working out is only going to make them worse.

Sleep is pitiful for me. I can't even tell you the last time I woke up from a night's rest feeling refreshed. I typically go to bed around 9:30pm-10:00pm and get up at 6:30am .... but I'm always waking up periodically throughout the night. On the worst occasions, my sleep will get interrupted about 6-7 times.

The working out thing does concern me a bit, especially if my adrenals aren't working properly and if my body was already struggling to produce enough testostosterone. I've worked so hard just to get my body into a shape I feel confident about though. It would be hard for me to give up.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-10T00:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79864>

Mystery, pregnenolone steal sounds like a good diagnosis for you. Stress of any sort causes the body to shunt resources to cortisol. Not just emotional stress, but dietary stress, nutrient deficiencies, etc. Sleep, as diane mentioned, is a huge issue. Do you stay up late watching TV or on the computer? Another stress for the body is a lack of proper circadian timing. Another stress would be taking additional resources from the brain, which is what exercise can do while building up muscle, when there are not enough resources to go around.

The antibiotics you were given in 2005 may have contributed to a leaky gut, which could definitely have caused those early symptoms.

Do you like seafood?

### MysteryInAZ — 2013-03-10T00:27:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79869>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79864)
>
> Mystery, pregnenolone steal sounds like a good diagnosis for you. Stress of any sort causes the body to shunt resources to cortisol. Not just emotional stress, but dietary stress, nutrient deficiencies, etc. Sleep, as diane mentioned, is a huge issue. Do you stay up late watching TV or on the computer? Another stress for the body is a lack of proper circadian timing. Another stress would be taking additional resources from the brain, which is what exercise can do while building up muscle, when there are not enough resources to go around.

Within the past year I have eliminated all late night TV watching. However, I am on my laptop quite a bit. I'm on it for work from 7:30am to 2:00pm and it's sort've replaced any TV watching I used to do, so I'm always on it reading, participating in online forums, or researching stuff for work. I could stand to use it less in all honesty. There are many nights where I'm on it right before bed.

I gave up all cardio from my routine a couple years ago because my body couldn't handle it along with the weight lifting. Seemed like my muscles would get cannibalized.

> The antibiotics you were given in 2005 may have contributed to a leaky gut, which could definitely have caused those early symptoms.
>
> Do you like seafood?

The doctor prescribed me Penicillin 500V for that infection. I'm not quite sure how strong that is compared to other antibiotics.

I do like seafood very much. I just don't often get to eat it because of my strict budget.

### MysteryInAZ — 2013-03-10T00:28:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79870>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=79860)

> Hi there. Here's a great success story to read - somewhat similar situation to yours. [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5914-As-Requested-A-Success-Story](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5914-As-Requested-A-Success-Story)
>
> How's your sleep? That can be a clue as well.
>
> If your hormones are issues, then working out is only going to make them worse.

My sleeping is terrible. I can't remember the last time I felt refreshed from sleep. I typically go to bed at 9:30-10:00pm and wake at 6:30am. I can never get thru a night without random sleep disruption (at it's worst it happens 6-7 times before I actually have to get up).

As far as my workout goes, I'm concerned about it. But at the same time I've spent so long getting into shape that I could feel confident about, it would be hard to give up. I wonder though what it's doing to my adrenals and how it affects my testosterone since it's pretty clear it's on the low end.

### Meg — 2013-03-10T00:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79875>

Mystery- Wow I was struck by how many similarities there are between our stories. One big exception, I am a 47 year old woman. I have taught fitness classes for 23 years and when I hit rock bottom a little over a year ago I finally quit. I had worn myself out, I was totally depleted! I spent years giving my electrons away to a room full of students. It was so hard to quit - fear of gaining weight, and losing what I had worked so long and hard for but I can tell you it was the best thing I could have done. YOU do have limited energy, and you DO need to choose wherre you are going to spend it. Hopefully that will change in the future, but until it does you are killing yourself faster by exercising.

It sounds like you need to start with an ASI- cortisol test, probably a melatonin test as well. And then dig into your hormones. I see that you don't have insurance but don't let that discourage you. There is still so much you can do. There is so much great information on this site along with alot of creative ideas from people who give freely of their knowledge. I have come SUCH a long way in one year because of what I have learned on the forum and from JK's blogs. Dig in and don't settle for less than optimal!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-10T01:17:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79877>

Perhaps you should start your research on this website with the Leptin prescription, [http://www.jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/](http://www.jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/)
Your adrenals aren't really the problem in adrenal fatigue, as Dr. Kruse has taught us. The problem is in the brain. See this post:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)

The leptin prescription is a protocol designed to correct your circadian rhythm, which fixes the timing issues your various organs and cells have, to allow you to sleep and reduce the stress your brain and body feel. Sleep is the most important thing, because that is when the body performs essential repairs and also recycles your ATP to provide the energy you are lacking. There are other protocols and methods to improve sleep, but without your clocks working correctly, your efforts will not yield very good results. Another good blogpost is [http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/) where Dr. K outlines how the various hormones (insulin, ghrelin, leptin, GH, prolactin, melatonin) come into play at different times of the night, when cortisol is low.

### Meg — 2013-03-10T01:59:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79889>

Yes, knowing where to start can be confusing but I can promise you the answer to your issues can be found on this forum and in Jack Kuse's blogs. You will have to dedicate time and energy to reading and researching. I think NONCHALANT has perfect advice below and I would start by hitting the links she recommended.

_Perhaps you should start your research on this website with the Leptin prescription, [http://www.jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/](http://www.jackkruse.com/my-leptin-prescription/)
Your adrenals aren't really the problem in adrenal fatigue, as Dr. Kruse has taught us. The problem is in the brain. See this post:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-1...al-fatigue-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-1...al-fatigue-rx/)

The leptin prescription is a protocol designed to correct your circadian rhythm, which fixes the timing issues your various organs and cells have, to allow you to sleep and reduce the stress your brain and body feel. Sleep is the most important thing, because that is when the body performs essential repairs and also recycles your ATP to provide the energy you are lacking. There are other protocols and methods to improve sleep, but without your clocks working correctly, your efforts will not yield very good results. Another good blogpost is [http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/) where Dr. K outlines how the various hormones (insulin, ghrelin, leptin, GH, prolactin, melatonin) come into play at different times of the night, when cortisol is low_.

I know what you mean about exercising being your outlet and even your addiction. All I can say is it is so freeing to let go off something you've been hanging onto out of "fear". You (we all have) bought into alot of lies about what brings us health and well being. I do believe there is a time and place for DHEA and Pregnenelone. DHEA has helped me alot. There is a man named Janz who i believe has helped some other men with labsa and hormones. Perhaps you could look him up and ask him some questions.

### ashryn — 2013-03-10T03:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79898>

If you can sort out your sleep, your stress will reduce a lot just from that.. Just get on the leptin reset, do it for 2 months to start with then reassess how you feel. You will probably start adding other things in as you start to feel better, but start with one thing and get it right, then add the next and so on.

Its all a bit overwhelming at first, but worth the journey, and worth getting started straight away even if you're not fully across all the science behind it. Read through all the leptin blogs, but whilst you are reading them, you can start on the easiest bit... here's a step by step to get started..

1\. When you wake in the morning, go outside and get sun on your face and body for a few minutes. Get your bare feet on the earth.
2\. Eat within 30 minutes of rising, 50-70grams of protein, plus some good fat. If you must eat carbs keep them under 10g
3\. Leave 3-4 hours between meals. NEVER snack. Eat to fullness for lunch and dinner. Skip lunch if you are not hungry, and finish eating dinner by 7.30 at the latest. Follow the paleo eating template of no grains or beans. Keep total carb intake under 50g per day. Less if you want it to go faster. No snacking includes anything with caloric value.. So coffee and tea should be black unless it is a meal time.
4\. At sunset, turn off your lights, TV computer, any artificial light source. Turn off your things at the power to get rid of the blue status lights, and the microwave clock.
5\. Let 3-4 hours elapse between eating and going to bed. Ensure you are in bed by 11.30 at the latest, though earlier is better.
6\. If you wake in the night, do not turn on a light or look at a clock. Do not get up unless you must pee, use the awake time to practice some mindfulness. (Laying in bed in the dark is almost as good as sleep as far as resting your body goes, so it is better than getting up.)

NOTE if you mess any of this up, you start your reset over. You should be sleeping well within a week if you mind the light and the eating timing. Breakfast quantity is important because you are retraining your brain via the vagus nerve in your stomach..you should feel stuffed after breakfast.. (This gets easier)

**preparation**
You will need to do some preparation to make this easier...
1\. Make your sleeping room as dark as possible. Use block out curtains and remove all electrical things from your room including your phone.
2\. Arrange things in your house so you don't trip over things walking around in the dark. Get red or amber light globes or candles to use for light, and blue blocker glasses to wear after sunset.
3\. Clean out all carbs and sugar things and any snack ish things in your cupboards
4\. Go shopping and buy all of the seafood you can afford, plus grass fed meats, free range eggs, butter, ghee, coconut oil, kale, spinach, turmeric, and the top 10 paleo supplements from the blog index. Buy spring water and only drink it icy cold.
Plan your breakfasts... Work out how much and what you will eat the day before so you're not trying to make good decisions when you're half asleep.

once you have the first steps under control, you should look at cold thermogenesis, epi paleo and the EMF blogs. There will be a lot of stuff relating to your particular brand of broken, and you will need to tweak and change, add and subtract stuff as you learn more.

This site is about getting to optimal health, and if you follow everything you will get there, but the steps I have outlined above will get you to functional.. From there you will be much better equipped to make decisions that will get you to optimal.

Hope this helps!

### Inger — 2013-03-10T08:20:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79911>

> [MysteryInAZ said:](/goto/post?id=79856)

> I generally eat pretty clean/lean (except for an occasional cheat meal on the weekends), getting about 2600-2700 calories a day (5-6 meals total), 140-160 grams of protein. Most of my diet consists of turkey beef, chicken, green vegetables, oatmeal, eggs, greek yogurt, nuts.

To many nuts are very bad too if you re not healthy. I speak from experience. I would limit those really, or cut out 100% is better. Dairy too.

### Hope — 2013-03-10T18:51:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79965>

Wow, Mystery, you got a lot going on and you got some rockstar advice on here that many doctors couldn't even give you!!!!

One small idea I might add....if you haven't already, install flux on your computer (f dot lux, my PC won't let me spell it without autocorrect)...that will help too if you also take out the keyboard lights if you have those. Though of course, the less laptop/tech time, the better because of EMF etc.

All the best to you!!

### Hope — 2013-03-10T18:56:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79966>

> [MysteryInAZ said:](/goto/post?id=79869)
>
> The doctor prescribed me Penicillin 500V for that infection. I'm not quite sure how strong that is compared to other antibiotics.
>
> I do like seafood very much. I just don't often get to eat it because of my strict budget.

PS No matter what, the ABX, even Penicillin, could have caused gut issues, along with all the other stuff, light, circadian mismatches, etc etc.

Do you have Asian markets near you? You can find cheaper seafood there. It will be a key in your healing and if you follow Dr Kruse's advice you won't spend that money on the other foods you are going to cut out. It is literally a prescription for healing......far better than any kind of drug or test you can spend money on. Also, canned fish might not be bad though fresh is best. Sardines, oysters in olive oil could be an option. I do that when traveling. If you know someone with a Costco membership, they have frozen fish, even wild, that is cheaper. And, you can make seafood broth with fish BONES you could get free or very little money and also do seaweeds....just ask at the grocery store or fish market.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-10T20:58:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-going-on-with-my-body-sick-of-feeling-this-way-for-so-long.6259/post-79983>

> [MysteryInAZ said:](/goto/post?id=79881)
>
> I have very limited energy. I spend the bulk of it on my workout. The weird thing though is, what else would I do when I need to release stress ya know? It's been my go-to for a while now. My temple if you will.

I have a long and endearing relationship with many activities, and I lived with the false assumption that working out was critical to my health, my weight, my strength, my ability to de-stress, and my emotional well-being. 14 months ago I found this website and started reading like a fiend. My mind was blown, I was skeptical, but intrigued. I wasn't a big, hot mess, but I was headed in that direction. I thought I ate healthily as we ate lots of whole, organic foods and have always kept processed foods to a minimum.

When I started eating a paleo, ketogenic diet 11 months ago, for whatever reason, I just naturally dropped off my workout load. I maintained my martial arts training, swimming, and lifting, but dropped running and cycling for the most part. Lunch time has always been my workout slot, but I kept finding myself drawn outside where I would just sit, observe, think, meditate, and write. I maintained that habit at least twice a week, and discovered all sorts of things about myself and my newfound interests.

I continued to feel better and better and lost a substantial amount of weight without my normal routine. That was significant for me because I hadn't been able to drop a pound to save my life despite increased workouts and activity.

You will find a lot of really excellent information here on this site, and already you've been provided some great info to start. Good luck on your journey!

# Thread: Earthing Sheets = Atennas?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-03-10T17:51:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-79953>

Hope someone has some expertise to share. I'm making some assumptions I need confirmed or explained to be wrong.

I made an earthing sheet yesterday. I used aluminum tape fastened to a half sheet. I didn't have enough time to run a ground, so I slept on it ungrounded. I had the worst sleep in many years. My arthritis kicked in big time and I couldn't get comfortable. Finally, I decided to sleep on top of the cover sheet and that helped some. I checked voltage this morning and when I touched the probe to the foil, voltage ac went from .02 v to .7 volts.

So it seems to me that an ungrounded earthing sheet may act like an antenna and pick up voltages. If that's the case, if you are ever unable to ground it (lighting storm, travel, etc), you might want to put something thick on top of it or take it off.

Again, I don't have a strong background in electricity, so please correct me if I have made an incorrect assumption.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-10T18:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-79963>

I think you are right, Huck. Also if you use aluminum screen to block radiation from a smart meter, it has to be grounded or else you get reflections. I hope your joints feel better soon. Thanks for the warning.

### shilohman — 2013-03-10T19:58:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-79971>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=79953)
>
> Hope someone has some expertise to share. I'm making some assumptions I need confirmed or explained to be wrong.
>
> I made an earthing sheet yesterday. I used aluminum tape fastened to a half sheet. I didn't have enough time to run a ground, so I slept on it ungrounded. I had the worst sleep in many years. My arthritis kicked in big time and I couldn't get comfortable. Finally, I decided to sleep on top of the cover sheet and that helped some. I checked voltage this morning and when I touched the probe to the foil, voltage ac went from .02 v to .7 volts.
>
> So it seems to me that an ungrounded earthing sheet may act like an antenna and pick up voltages. If that's the case, if you are ever unable to ground it (lighting storm, travel, etc), you might want to put something thick on top of it or take it off.
>
> Again, I don't have a strong background in electricity, so please correct me if I have made an incorrect assumption.

You got it exactly, without a ground your earthing sheet is nothing more than an antenna. The electrical characteristics of the earthing sheet will determine what frequencies/wave lengths it will absorb/radiate. I have posted this URL on another threat, but it demonstrates this effect
[http://berkeley.intel-research.net/arahimi/helmet/](http://berkeley.intel-research.net/arahimi/helmet/)
Note that they excluded grounding their helmets, because I am sure this wouldn't have supported their point.

### Huck — 2013-03-10T20:10:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-79974>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=79971)
>
> You got it exactly, without a ground your earthing sheet is nothing more than an antenna. The electrical characteristics of the earthing sheet will determine what frequencies/wave lengths it will absorb/radiate. I have posted this URL on another threat, but it demonstrates this effect
> [http://berkeley.intel-research.net/arahimi/helmet/](http://berkeley.intel-research.net/arahimi/helmet/)
> Note that they excluded grounding their helmets, because I am sure this wouldn't have supported their point.

Thanks for confirming. I recently made a TV antenna, so I was pretty sure it was the same principle.

I have a question. I tried using a coat hanger pushed about 1 foot into the ground as my ground. When I attached a wire from the coat hanger to my earthing sheet, the voltage went up! I then went around the yard with the voltmeter, held one lead in my hand and pushed the other into the ground. The voltage ranged from .009 v to .9 v. One of the lowest readings I got was when I grounded to my car. Can you explain what is going on and why I am getting such a high reading?

### shilohman — 2013-03-11T03:07:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80011>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=79974)
>
> Thanks for confirming. I recently made a TV antenna, so I was pretty sure it was the same principle.
>
> I have a question. I tried using a coat hanger pushed about 1 foot into the ground as my ground. When I attached a wire from the coat hanger to my earthing sheet, the voltage went up! I then went around the yard with the voltmeter, held one lead in my hand and pushed the other into the ground. The voltage ranged from .009 v to .9 v. One of the lowest readings I got was when I grounded to my car. Can you explain what is going on and why I am getting such a high reading?

Your earthing sheet is acting as a antenna that is grounded, so you were reading the potential (voltage) of your antenna. When you were pushing the one lead into the ground your body was acting as an antenna and you were measuring the potential of you as an antenna. The car is acting as a shield with you inside as an antenna, so you shouldn't see much. When you are standing outside and you are grounded the EMF that is hitting your body is being shunted to the ground over your skin ( another way to phrase it is that electrons from the earth are neutralizing the EMF potential). When you are standing outside and not grounded the EMF is penetrating your body and any electrons in your body that would normally be used by your body are used to neutralize the EMF potential. One other point, did you scrape the coating off of the clothes hanger? If not you weren't getting a good ground if any.

To get a really good ground you need to go down 6-8 ft. If you take two hangers and place them in the ground say 10 ft apart and then use some extended leads with alligator clips to your meter, you probably will see some very small voltage, this is why you need to go down 6-8 ft to get a clean ground. This is why Dr. K says the best place to ground is in the ocean in about 3ft of water. My guess is that going barefoot in Central Park is no way as beneficial because of the immense amount of EMF in NYC as compared to going barefoot in one our national forests.

### caroline — 2013-03-11T08:33:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80030>

I guess you won't be inviting anyone to come over and "get grounded" just yet???

### Huck — 2013-03-11T13:59:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80038>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=80030)

> I guess you won't be inviting anyone to come over and "get grounded" just yet???

I guess not. Took the darn thing off until I can figure out why I am getting a voltage from the ground.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-11T17:21:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80051>

Huck, I'd like to try to replicate your findings. Which setting on the voltmeter do you use? Do you use just one of the two probes?

### Huck — 2013-03-11T18:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80057>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=80051)
>
> Huck, I'd like to try to replicate your findings. Which setting on the voltmeter do you use? Do you use just one of the two probes?

Volts AC. Held one probe in one hand and touched other probe to foil on sheet.

For outside, I held 1 probe in hand and pushed other probe into the ground.

### shilohman — 2013-03-11T18:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80060>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=80030)
>
> I guess you won't be inviting anyone to come over and "get grounded" just yet???

That is a hoot, LOL.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-11T19:00:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80066>

If you hold one probe in your hand and touch the other to an electrical plug ground, you will be able to read a voltage of some sort. Then, keeping these probes in this position, touch your earthing sheet with your other hand, and the AC voltage that your body is picking up should go way down, hopefully to zero.

It's a bit difficult to do, because the probe attached to the electrical outlet ground will fall out. A power strip makes it easier.

### Meg — 2013-03-11T21:41:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80083>

Huck- and everryone else.........So, I'm guessing that having grounding sheets on your bed unattached is a BAD thing????

### nonchalant — 2013-03-11T21:56:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80084>

Meg - yes. It would attract more EMF to your body.
Instead of the grounding sheet pulling the voltage from your body and sending it to ground, it would just give you more surface area to gather more voltage from your surroundings.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-12T00:45:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80111>

Is there a problem if the sheets are plugged into an outlet, but the breaker is off for that outlet, so no "dirty electricity" is coming through?? Any benefit from that? Wouldn't it be similar to using a grounding rod?

### shilohman — 2013-03-12T03:16:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80135>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=80111)
>
> Is there a problem if the sheets are plugged into an outlet, but the breaker is off for that outlet, so no "dirty electricity" is coming through?? Any benefit from that? Wouldn't it be similar to using a grounding rod?

Good question, seems like many on this forum have bought or made grounding sheets.
1) a grounding sheet is an antenna that is grounded. If the sheet is underneath the body, then any EMF coming in is going through your
body before any of it hits the sheet and is shunted to ground. If the grounding sheet is over the body, then it is protecting your body and
any EMF/RF will be shunted to ground and you are protected.
2) Turning off the breaker is not going to prevent stray voltages on the ground wire. What you need to do is to have an electrician install an
isolated ground at your circuit breaker box and then to attach the grounds for your bedroom wall sockets to to the isolated ground.

Some things you might consider doing:
1) attach a ground wire to the box springs and plug that into your isolated ground
2) if your mattress is one with springs, get an air mattress or memory foam mattress
3) ground the bed frame as in number 1 above
4) use a ground strap around your wrist or waist or leg or whatever instead of a grounding sheet. Why, because your body acts as an
antenna and if it is grounded then the EMF will not enter your body, but go over your skin to ground.

Also note that even if you dirty electricity or more accurately stray voltage on your ground, that any grounding straps you buy have an
resistor build into the strap so you have some protection from stray voltage i.e. being grounded to dirty electricity is better than no ground at all.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-12T03:39:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80139>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=80135)
>
> .
> 1) a grounding sheet is an antenna that is grounded. If the sheet is underneath the body, then any EMF coming in is going through your
> body before any of it hits the sheet and is shunted to ground. If the grounding sheet is over the body, then it is protecting your body and
> any EMF/RF will be shunted to ground and you are protected.

I tried a grounding sheet on top of me (actually it was below me and folded over on top as well). It got too hot. It has a fairly dense weave. I'd like something on top, so I can get the belly at the same time as the back. But when my back hurts during the day, I have to give it priority time on the sheet. This evening my back feels pretty good, so it's tummy time tonight!

### BJK77 — 2013-03-12T16:10:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80184>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=80135)
>
> Good question, seems like many on this forum have bought or made grounding sheets.
> 1) a grounding sheet is an antenna that is grounded. If the sheet is underneath the body, then any EMF coming in is going through your
> body before any of it hits the sheet and is shunted to ground. If the grounding sheet is over the body, then it is protecting your body and
> any EMF/RF will be shunted to ground and you are protected.
> 2) Turning off the breaker is not going to prevent stray voltages on the ground wire. What you need to do is to have an electrician install an
> isolated ground at your circuit breaker box and then to attach the grounds for your bedroom wall sockets to to the isolated ground.
>
> Some things you might consider doing:
> 1) attach a ground wire to the box springs and plug that into your isolated ground
> 2) if your mattress is one with springs, get an air mattress or memory foam mattress
> 3) ground the bed frame as in number 1 above
> 4) use a ground strap around your wrist or waist or leg or whatever instead of a grounding sheet. Why, because your body acts as an
> antenna and if it is grounded then the EMF will not enter your body, but go over your skin to ground.
>
> Also note that even if you dirty electricity or more accurately stray voltage on your ground, that any grounding straps you buy have an
> resistor build into the strap so you have some protection from stray voltage i.e. being grounded to dirty electricity is better than no ground at all.

Thanks so much shilohman. Sounds like my best, easiest option is just to somehow figure out how to get a copper rod 6-8' in my rocky, mountain soil!! Now that the ground is finally starting to de-freeze that will be my plan.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-12T20:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80219>

DH used a T-post (fencing) driver to pound our grounding rod into the soil. Ours was homemade decades ago, but I think you can rent them. We luckily have clay soil. We used a sledge hammer for the last couple feet.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-14T00:36:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-sheets-atennas.6271/post-80426>

I read about some things that bleed excess charge off into the air such as carbon brushes worn as ornaments. This made me consider that one of the purposes of hair might be to bleed off excess charge when it is damp. Charge is transferred to the atmosphere through increased humidity. This got me to wondering about spraying the bedsheets with colloidal/nanoparticle silver. Wouldn't evaporating body moisture have enhanced effects if the bedsheets were sprayed with colloidal/nanoparticle silver? I saw a list of materials that like to donate + or - charges and human skin was in the middle of the list. Going by that list, it prefers to remain neutral. Does this mean the lungs are used to get rid of excess charge through water in our breath? Are we grounding whenever we empty our bladder in the outdoors.

# Thread: Don't feel satisfied with coconut oil....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2013-03-11T02:07:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80008>

Has anyone else experienced this?

When I first started the LR I was extremely satisfied, I ate lots of grass-fed ghee, bacon, and lots of fatty meat. I went into ketosis very quickly and was never hungry.

Recently I switched my ghee for coconut oil and I started craving fat like crazy! The CO just never satisfied me. No matter how much fatty protein I ate I was dreaming about butter and ghee all day. I know that coconut oil doesn't have any cholesterol in it, maybe I just need that extra cholesterol? I switched back to ghee and feel so much better. I am of both northern and eastern european descent, maybe CO just doesn't cut it for me genetically...

I was thinking of going more epi-paleo but am fearful of the decreased fat content...I have been having wild salmon every day but have been having it with ghee....

Any thoughts, experiences?

Thanks!

Chrissy

### nonchalant — 2013-03-11T02:33:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80010>

Hi Chrissy, welcome!
Is it Winter where you are? I find it difficult to rely on CO in winter. Currently I use bacon fat, mostly. But with Spring I'll have to start cooking more with CO.

### cmegan — 2013-03-12T15:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80178>

Hi Nonchalant!

Thank you, I have been very focused on getting enough MCT for a recent concussion that I was not taking winter into account...After reading your post it made me remember that back in December when I first tried the LR I was eating primarily bacon fat (more so than ghee or butter) with all of my meals and was never happier! I find it even more satisfying than butter. I'm going back to bacon for a while! Thank you!

### caroline — 2013-03-12T23:52:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80247>

Please tell me more - a lot of fat/MCToil is good for a concussion? After a fall -I am wondering what I should most be incorporating. I am thinking Lamb heart would be great.....

### cmegan — 2013-03-14T17:21:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80504>

Hi Caroline,

I asked Dr. K. about treatment for a concussion and he replied to eat lots of MCT's in coconut oil....
I fell and suffered a concussion in the beginning of November...I was just starting with to experiment with the LR several weeks later. Around Christmas I totally went off and had Christmas treats, alcohol, etc., and my concussion symptoms were off the chart. I then went back into ketosis and the symptoms subsided. Remembering that western medicine uses ketotic diets for epilepsy I did a search and found that some neurologists were recommending it for concussions (I can't remember any sites, but I remember one medical center was in the process of doing a clinical trial with this.)...anyway, staying in ketosis and eating lots of fat has really helped, along with a lot of fatty fish. I remember that after I fell all I wanted to eat was bacon all day long...

### TheKid — 2013-03-14T17:28:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80506>

If I remember correctly, your body either cannot or finds it difficult to store MCTs. MCTs are taken up by the portal vein directly to the liver, I think. So you are bypassing the metabolic processes relate to other fats. So my understanding is that you'd use the MCTs for energy (ketones) or you'd lose them as heat. This may contribute somehow to how you feel.

I hope I got that right.

### cmegan — 2013-03-14T18:06:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80509>

yes, I think the process you describe is correct....

Unfortunately I did not discover Dr. Kruse's RX for concussions until several months after my fall when I asked him a question on the forum....I wish I had that info from day one, I would have healed much faster!

### TheKid — 2013-03-14T18:44:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80515>

I too have found that coconut oil does not fully satisfy me. I rather use it a bridge when I want to avoid snacking. I find that a tablespoon will take the edge off.

I cannot eat too much though, or I get some gastro problems. When I was drinking bulletproof coffee I found that the butter somehow made things go down more easily.

The key is probably to examine your morning breakfast. If it is large enough, your should be fully satisfied. And you can surely add CO into that morning meal in large amounts.

### cmegan — 2013-03-14T19:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dont-feel-satisfied-with-coconut-oil.6281/post-80521>

yes, I did go back to eating lots of bacon with my lamb burger instead of coconut oil, and what a HUGE difference!

# Thread: EMF & wifi N=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-wifi-n-1.6285/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-03-11T15:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-wifi-n-1.6285/post-80041>

Ok.. so saturday I had a consult w/Jack which ment the wifi was on. I did sit outside during the consult and grounded myself. the rest of the day I worked on our taxes (oh Joy!) and the wifi was on - unfortunately it got too windy to sit outside with all the paperwork. I did take several 30 min breaks during the day - and sat out side on the grass in just a pair of shorts and a tank top (free electrons)

finally got taxes done and filed byt 5pm. I didn't feel all that well..

I went to sleep w/the kids. eventually I moved to our bedroom - then I woke up and felt like crap around 130. got up to find DH had the downstairs TV on (which is right below our bed).... I asked him to turn it off... which he did.

Woke up on sunday and just felt off - not b/c of the time change - but b/c of the crappy sleep.... and within in 45min of waking I had a migraine.....

it continued to just get worse as the day went on....

so, unless I'm doing a consult w/Jack and I'm outside - wifi is gone in the house. buh bye. haven't told DH yet - by cable tv is going too...

when my cousin goes back to Africa in the next week or 2 we're also going to start turning off the breaker in the bedrooms....

### Hope — 2013-03-11T16:15:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-wifi-n-1.6285/post-80045>

Similar stuff happened to me....the weekend before this. Though in my case, I'm slow so it took me two days and I didn't sit outside.....it took me a couple days to feel normal again, that is "my" normal.....still pretty tired, but not so tired I feel like a walking zombie as I did those two days. Crazy stuff.

### Huck — 2013-03-11T18:21:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-wifi-n-1.6285/post-80059>

Tree pollen is also high these days. I started having bad congestion at night a couple of weeks ago.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-recommendations-for-kids-tweens-teens.6289/post-80069>

My 12-year old daughter just finished The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari (TMWSHF) and loved it. She has also decided she wants to learn more about the food industry, earthing, the Schumann resonance, etc, etc. I think reading TMWSHF is actually pushing her to embrace this newfound knowledge (instead of thinking mom's a quack). I want to encourage this while she is showing interest, but so far all of the books I'm reviewing are much too advanced for her.

Any suggestions?

\*She had a lesson in biology the other day on GMO's and came home absolutely livid! No doubt Monsanto is paying a small fee to schools to preach their worthiness. The class had to watch a video explaining why GMO's are so wonderful and will "save the world" . . . or at least third-world countries. My daughter's response was that they're not saving anyone, they're killing them!

# Thread: Help with estrogen results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-estrogen-results.6295/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-03-12T13:53:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-estrogen-results.6295/post-80173>

I had my hormone labs drawn last month on CD 20. I thought the lab had made a mistake because my total estrogens were less than my estradiol. They let me redo them this month. I tested on CD21 and the same thing happened, but much higher results. Anyone have any ideas on what could be going on? I did increase DHEA and testosterone a bit, could that be contributing to the increase? I still don't understand how my total estrogen can be less than my estradiol.

Feb 2013
Estradiol 103.5 pg/ml (43.8-211.0)
Total estrogen 73 pg/ml (156-350)

March 2013
Estradiol 373.5 pg/ml (43.8-211.0)
Total estrogens 286 pg/ml (156-350)

I had a short cycle this month at 23 days and I have been having night sweats the past few nights.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-12T18:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-estrogen-results.6295/post-80200>

The DHEA could be converting to estrogen in you which is highly likely. Maybe you are also an estrogen collector. You may want to try to drop the DHEA and test again and see what happens. Something in your hormones is obviously messed up if you are having night sweats and estrogen that high. Have you done Pg testing? Looking at the Pg/E2 ratio maybe helpful too.

### [email redacted] — 2013-03-13T19:18:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-estrogen-results.6295/post-80382>

I'm using the 7keto DHEA, so I didn't think that would convert to estrogen. My PG is very low so I know my ratio stinks, but it has always been low. I can't figure out why all of a sudden my E is climbing but yet I am having the night sweats.

# Thread: Ladies: Has CT affected your cycle length?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-03-12T15:16:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80180>

I was wondering if any of you have seen any cycle changes due to CT...especially in terms of it length/shortness?

I upped my CT this month, did more than I ever have (though I only started last fall, too) and.....my cycle is the shortest it's ever been this month: 23 days which I have only had one other time....in my life.

I will read through some threads again but wanted to ask here too......

Thanks!

### diane — 2013-03-12T18:15:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80188>

Mine was short for 1 month, then very long and way heavier than usual. I haven't done CT so regularly - but back on it now. This time the Metformin seems to have shortened by TOM, although my cycle was still longer. I just had my labs done at the end of Feb, and my progesterone is very low, which is why my cycle was long and heavy -estrogen dominant. Now to up the CT to see if that brings the progesterone back (along with supplementation which I'm starting this cycle). So yup, lots of changes!

### Hope — 2013-03-12T18:19:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80189>

Thanks for sharing, Diane. Great to hear CT could have this impact (because I don't know why else I have a big change this month). I am so confused about the cycles etc.....in terms of length and which HERmone determines cycle length....because one of my books says, short cycle = PG is LOW.....ugh. But clearly that is wrong.

May I ask, what do you consider a long cycle?? realizing your n=1

### diane — 2013-03-12T18:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80191>

I'm up between 36 and 39 days, where I was at around 30-31 before September (and August was when i started implementing a lot of things). My TOM would last 4-5 days, but I had one in October that lasted 14 days! I almost lost my mind lol. Then it went to 9 days. So my testing then made sense as to what i was seeing.

I'm reading the book Sex Lies and Menopause by Wiley that Darleen recommended. I'm only on the 3rd chapter, but she does an amazing job explaining the TOM and why/when/how, etc. So enlightening!

### freesia — 2013-03-12T18:39:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80199>

21 days shifted to 28 days after 2 - 3 months of CT. PMS gone gone gone.

Interesting to see the different effects on different peeps!

### Hope — 2013-03-12T18:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80202>

Oh, just saw this after reading one of your other posts on CT!  Messaged you too....thanks so much for sharing!! THis is what I need to hear!!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-12T20:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80220>

When I first was doing CT, which consisted mostly of cold showers, lots of swimming, and air-drying off after swimming, I noticed no cycle changes. When I really amped up my winter CT in December, my first cycle, from November to December, was typical, and right on time for me at 28 days. From December to January, I had no PMS symptoms at all, except for what I call my wicked irritability day on cycle day 20. From January to February, however things were WAY different. Severe breast pain on cycle day 19 (very unusual for me), bloating, weight gain of almost 5 pounds, and then I started my TOM on cycle day 25. Four days later I had severe hives, which I am attributing to a hormonal dump. Nothing was unusual to cause an allergic reaction. From February to the present, I am now on cycle day 25, I've had zero PMS symptoms, except I had moderate irritability on day 20. I've been doing grounding CT in a pool from natural springs two days a week, plus freezing cold showers after 45 minutes of swimming 2 days a week. I'm very curious to see how the next month goes.

### diane — 2013-03-12T20:29:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80221>

I forgot to also mention that I had pretty bad PMS as well. Now all of the cramps are gone - just the cravings remain. LOL.

### Hope — 2013-03-12T21:00:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80227>

Chrysanthemama and diane, wow, that is so fascinating!! Thanks so much for sharing. and wow - a pool from natural springs. I wanna live in NM too  Love your blog pictures....whew!

### diane — 2013-03-12T21:07:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80229>

Hope, for what it's worth, I have been complaining to various doctors/naturopaths/wholistic nutritionists - whoever would listen - about the escalating cramps for a few years. After my consult with Dr. K, it's the first time they were gone. And that was the very next month.

### Hope — 2013-03-12T22:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ladies-has-ct-affected-your-cycle-length.6296/post-80236>

That's awesome Diane!

# Thread: It has been One year............
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-has-been-one-year.6301/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-03-12T22:58:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-has-been-one-year.6301/post-80238>

This forum went live one year ago today. What an amazing year it has been .....so much to learn and incorporate into my life. I have loved every single second. I wish I understood the science better but I keep plugging away!

I know Dr. K. that you don't want thanks.....it has been such a great year for me and I hope you have achieved at least some of the things that you wanted to ...in spite of the many frustrations.

Thankyou to everyone for all the help and guidance and kindness and friendships and the amazing amount of caring that goes on here ..... I hope it continues .......

Maybe one day we will be able to raise a glass of red and salute each other and say Job well done - what a fantastic journey we have had!

Thanks Dr. Kruse and everyone......

### nonchalant — 2013-03-12T23:11:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-has-been-one-year.6301/post-80242>

Yes, thanks so much Dr. Kruse. To think I found this website, looking to lose weight, and ended up with mental clarity, velvety soft skin, feet that could actually work without shoes to brace them, good memory, eyes that could see colors better and sharper and see clearer in the dark, back pain relief, sharper sense of smell, wonderful sleep, no more hot flashes, taut skin, disappearing cellulite, great endurance, relief from headaches, and fabulous muscles.

From the forum I have found encouragement, better understanding of Dr. K's concepts, ideas of how to implement the concepts, and friendship. Thanks everyone for sharing all your information. Knowledge is power, and it is so nice to be able to make sense out of the chaos of bad health, and bad advice from others. It has been such a great year.

### caroline — 2013-03-12T23:24:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-has-been-one-year.6301/post-80244>

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=80242)
>
> Yes, thanks so much Dr. Kruse. To think I found this website, looking to lose weight, and ended up with mental clarity, velvety soft skin, feet that could actually work without shoes to brace them, good memory, eyes that could see colors better and sharper and see clearer in the dark, back pain relief, sharper sense of smell, wonderful sleep, no more hot flashes, taut skin, disappearing cellulite, great endurance, relief from headaches, and fabulous muscles.
>
> From the forum I have found encouragement, better understanding of Dr. K's concepts, ideas of how to implement the concepts, and friendship. Thanks everyone for sharing all your information. Knowledge is power, and it is so nice to be able to make sense out of the chaos of bad health, and bad advice from others. It has been such a great year.

I wonder how many of us wandered over here - looking to lose weight and look at what we found instead ..... simply amazing!

### diane — 2013-03-13T01:34:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/it-has-been-one-year.6301/post-80284>

I'm definitely glad I found my way here! I can't believe how much I've learned, and how my understanding of how things work has increased. It's been so much fun to get to know everyone here, and I love how people just want to help each other! Thanks to Dr. Kruse for sharing your knowledge - it's sure made a difference in my life.

Although we have battled a spectrum-like disorder, thanks to autoimmune/Epi-Paleo/ketogenic eating, she now has all of her faculties intact and mostly good days. She just saw me reading Dr. Kruse's site and said, "Reading Jack Kruse helps us stay healthy. I am pretending to be Jack Kruse: 'eat your oysters, take curcumin, get good Earthing outside, get good electrons from the sun, and drink LOTS of spring water! Wireless is BAD NEWS!'" ### SCRN2007 — 2013-03-13T02:05:32+0200

Yes, she has always amazed us! When the trouble started, after a round of vaccines and a subsequent month of diarrhea, at 18 months, we knew right away that something was wrong. The first sign was that she was so hyper and busy stimming and tantruming that she stopped reading her beloved books, and then came the absence seizures. No doctors took us seriously, since "18-month-olds are not supposed to be able to read aloud." I noticed that the new spectrum-type behaviors were all worse immediately following meals. That is when mommy went rogue and found GAPS and later this website. GAPS got us a symptom free year, but then the autoimmune foods caught up with her and the absence seizures returned, along with horrific palsy-like speech, stimming and hyperactivity. That went on for a month before I made the autoimmune foods connection via a careful food diary and reading this site. Three days clean of dairy, green beans, and eggs and the seizures and palsy-like speech were gone. The stimming and hyperactivity disappeared when we took away fruit and she entered ketosis. She is mostly fine as long as we do not allow her to go a day without a bowel movement (in that case she has hyperactivity, toe-walking, and mild stims). She turned out to be practically devoid of immunoglobulins when I demanded that she be tested at 18 months old. We just recently found out that she has many SNPs. We still have a lot to learn to get her to optimal. Thank God above for this site and for doctors like Dr. Kruse!

# Thread: Why FGlucose goes up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-fglucose-goes-up.6309/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3

### JanSz — 2013-03-13T18:22:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-fglucose-goes-up.6309/post-80371>

> [pmgamer18 said:](/goto/post?id=0)
>
> Read this link in this it talks about dumping your sodium.
> [http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal\_Glands.html](http://www.tuberose.com/Adrenal_Glands.html)

This section caught my eye.

> Actions of Cortisol
> Blood Sugar
>
> Cortisol is essential for maintaining blood sugar (glucose) levels in the proper balance. A drop in blood sugar triggers the adrenals to make more cortisol. The cortisol increases blood sugar levels by converting fats and proteins into energy in a process called gluconeogenesis. In this energy production process first fats are broken down into fatty acids and proteins into peptides and then these are converted into the needed blood glucose. This process is vital to keeping the blood glucose levels relatively constant throughout the day. Your body depends upon glucose as its most consistent form of energy. Cortisol works in tandem with insulin from the pancreas to provide adequate glucose to the cells where it is burned for energy. Cortisol ensures adequate levels of glucose in the blood while insulin unlocks the cell membranes to let glucose into the cells. When your body is under stress from any source, there are more demands placed upon its various tissues and organs, requiring more available glucose to fuel more energy production in the cell.

I think I am rather successful in using MLM pregnenolone.
Raises my preg
Raises my prog
Raises my cortisol
TCortisol, serum 8AM(18.1)(6.2-19.4)ug/dL

wait
my fasting glucose is going up 93, 106, 115 WTF

but Spectracell is saying that my Glucose-Insulin interaction is high into good area of their chart =54 (>38)
could use tiny bit more chromium

And I do not remember when last time I ate any grains or potatoes.
I got rid of 1500mg/day Metformin after my 23andMe told me that may Metformin benefits may be less that those of average person.

I am back on GlucoCare, looking for Bitter Melon and other similar stuff.
But possibly I am making too much cortisol because I take too much pregnenolone (scratching head)???

ideas???
...................

however - it doesn't mean you can't leverage metformin to reduce the leakiness at cyctochrome 1, which inturn drives down insulin levels. check out this thread - there's some gold in there [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6237-best-sleep-EVER](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6237-best-sleep-EVER)!
