### caroline — 2013-03-24T06:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81599>

Colleen Coble is the expert in all things HCG. As far as I am concerned she is a fountain of information. I don't know if she still comes on this forum or not. Maybe Check the HCG forum.

I did hhcg and then went directly into the reset - but you have to be very vigilant. I monitored my weight every day and then if I needed to - I would do a steak day. I never let it get away from me. In my opinion - I think we are pretty unstable after HCG so we really have to be on top of it for at least six months.

I found the BAB difficult but all cravings went away within a week or two which was huge for me and I went to two meals a day pretty quickly. I eat dinner around 3-4:30pm and am not hungry until the next morning. I used to be really bad at raiding the fridge/pantry in the middle of the night so that was huge too.

The only cravings really that I had left was apples/fruit and once I gave up fruit I am now rock solid. I do have some once in a long while and I am fine.

Now I seem to be eating a lot and my weight is drifting downward. I think the spring water is part of it and all the seafood and raw meat. My weight is now 56 K - probably haven't been that weight since I was 12.

In my opinion you need to follow Dr. Simeon's protocols exactly except delete the bread and fruit and add a little more protein. Also follow the leptin Rex exactly. Shift down to two meals when you can and absolutely no snacking.

The other important thing for me was to add more fat and take away some protein which made me feel much better and not so overstuffed.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-24T14:24:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81603>

"In my opinion you need to follow Dr. Simeon's protocols exactly except delete the bread and fruit and add a little more protein."

Caroline, I delete the bread & one fruit and also add some extra protein. This works best for me too. My extra protein is usually some of the ground beef jerky I make. Nibble on it during my hour long commute from work.

I am by know means perfect with the protocol, but it works for me. Love the inch loss and am already wearing some things that haven't been worn in quite some time. Keeping myself well-hydrated with spring water and grounding daily.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T23:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81650>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=81603)
>
> "In my opinion you need to follow Dr. Simeon's protocols exactly except delete the bread and fruit and add a little more protein."
>
> Caroline, I delete the bread & one fruit and also add some extra protein. This works best for me too. My extra protein is usually some of the ground beef jerky I make. Nibble on it during my hour long commute from work.
>
> I am by know means perfect with the protocol, but it works for me. Love the inch loss and am already wearing some things that haven't been worn in quite some time. Keeping myself well-hydrated with spring water and grounding daily.

In my view - I see so many people tinker with dr. Simeon's protocols and for the life of me I don't understand why. It works beautifully.......but so many people try to add in other things like tuna etc. I, too, had 1 apple a day but if I ever need to do it again I would delete all fruit since I think it is a problem for me re - sugar cravings.

Fitness - how will you stabilize after you are done? I think that is wha Dr. K is saying......HCG does work but you really have to work at it afterwards to maintain until you stabilize. It actually isn't that hard but you have to be present and really think about what you are doing. It isn't magic - if you want the weight gone - it is hard work but doable.

I see the same thing with the leptin r/x ..... If you keep falling off the wagon you keep resetting yourself back to the beginning and not getting anywhere. For me - this all had to be priority#1 if I wanted to achieve results. What is it that JK says? Something like ....food shouldn't be your hobby. There is more but I can't remember the exact quote - it really struck home with me.

How do you make ground beef jerky? I would love that! I guess you have a dehydrator.

Isn't it fantastic when clothes start fitting nicely? I love it when they Just hang off you. It gives you so much more confidence - it makes all the difference .....I guess that is a woman thing?

### fitness@home — 2013-03-25T00:32:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-on-the-fence-about-trying-hcg-again.6411/post-81652>

Caroline, I will send you a message tomorrow with the base recipe. It is on my computer at work. I have some ground chicken "jerky" going in the oven right now. Lowest heat setting for about 7-8 hours.

I stabilize in P3 by eating higher protein/fat ( sound familiar?) You just have to be very strict for the first three weeks and I kind of limit my choices...don't introduce a bunch of thing all at once. I stay away from pretty much all carbs, including any fruit during this period.

After that, it is just my normal way of eating. Seafood, meat, fats - ghee, coconut oil, and rendered fats. Add in some veggies and fruit at times. I am not perfect in this...there are times I will eat something not on the above list like dairy or occasionally eggs on the weekend in the form of breakfast tacos. The problems start when you indulge too often.

This round is helping me clean house so to speak. It is getting my eating habits back on track. Two of my friends have recently started rounds because they've seen how it has worked for me (and they know I research _everything_ thoroughly first).

I can tell you that I am missing my cocoa crack a lot right now . Can't wait to add that back to my diet after completing this round...

### caroline — 2013-03-25T00:50:21+0200

OMG .... I have forgotten about cocoa crack! I love it - but have a hard time staying away from it.

I am certainly not perfect either - by a long shot - but I weigh everyday to keep on top of it. Lately I seem to have more leeway. I eat a fair bit of Stilton cheese and goat cheese now. I seem to crave anything full fat.

Nowadays - a cheat day is an apple or some Stilton and red wine and I would probably have sushi if I was out with a friend.
That certailey is light years away from where I used to be.....so grateful....and I love it as well - so satisfying.

**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 17

### endless — 2013-03-20T22:12:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81217>

Anyone have a good mayo recipe? I can't find anything without canola or soybean oil in the stores. I was toying with the idea of using MCT oil....anyone tried that?

### cantweight — 2013-03-20T22:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81218>

[http://nomnompaleo.com/post/3440774534/paleo-mayonnaise](http://nomnompaleo.com/post/3440774534/paleo-mayonnaise)

### diane — 2013-03-20T22:23:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81220>

This one's really good. With olive oil.

[http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/08/19/mayo-how-to-video/](http://www.theclothesmakethegirl.com/2012/08/19/mayo-how-to-video/)

### Priscilla — 2013-03-21T00:39:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81233>

I make mine similar to the nomnompaleo link (above). I use avocado oil, and cider vinegar (with the mother) instead of white vinegar. I also do it with an immersion blender. Put all the ingredients except the oil in the container, then add oil on top, then place the immersion blender on the bottom. The oil incorporates quickly. slowly raise the blender to finish incorporating it all.

This is so simple. But.. when you are done, _yank the cord from the outlet first_! Not doing this brought me an emergency room visit and 10 stitches on the tip of my finger!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-21T01:06:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81239>

Priscilla, I'm going to try your method. I used to make mayo a lot but got lazy and stopped several years ago. Tried to make it last year and twice it flopped due to me not dropping the oil in slowly enough. I love the immersion blender idea! Thanks!

### endless — 2013-03-21T18:53:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81332>

Avocado oil! Duh.....why didn't I think of that, it's perfect! Thanks everyone!

### CarolMorris — 2013-03-21T18:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81333>

With the immersion blender it's like...magic

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-21T19:38:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81340>

I can't the mayo to set for the life of me... ends up very runny - even when I follow directions to a T as far as measurements... doesn't matter if I use an emersion blender, my vitamix, kitchen aid hand mixer, or whipping by hand... total failure...what am I doing wrong????

### Rachelle — 2013-03-21T21:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81349>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=81332)
>
> Avocado oil! Duh.....why didn't I think of that, it's perfect! Thanks everyone!

I also use avocado oil and MCT together. You can play around with the amounts.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-21T21:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-81350>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=81340)
>
> I can't the mayo to set for the life of me... ends up very runny - even when I follow directions to a T as far as measurements... doesn't matter if I use an emersion blender, my vitamix, kitchen aid hand mixer, or whipping by hand... total failure...what am I doing wrong????

Same here at Shijin, until I used my food processor. It made the difference for me. Just put in egg yoks, vinegar and salt and then start the food processor to make yolks to incorporate with vinegar and salt for a few seconds. Then slowly pour in oil while keeping the food processor running. Works every time.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-30T04:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-82243>

MCT would probably work fine. You could add some olive, avocado, or macadamia oil in there to vary the flavor some. MCT is a very "runny" oil. I plan to make some homemade mayo soon to use with a salmon, chopped clam, onion, and cucumber salad similar to a tuna salad. Maybe I will add some chopped boiled egg. It probably won't be pretty to look at.

Start with slow bursts of the stick blender at the bottom of the jar. Don't raise the stick blender higher until obvious mayo is beginning to form at the bottom. Egg yolk has the lecithin that emulsifies the fat so use enough egg yolk for the job.

### Glamazon — 2013-03-30T07:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-82253>

I have used half olive and half MCT oil. I think with the new batch I am going all MCT oil. I use a little cider vinegar. You can also making it with bacon grease. Haven't done that yet. You can also use lemon juice instead of cider vinegar. Gives it a very bright, light taste. I have also added some chopped garlic and chives.

### diane — 2013-03-30T15:28:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-82274>

Yes, I use lemon juice. I've tried with fresh lemon juice, but read that you get better results with bottled. Which I've found helps.

### Martha Ray — 2023-03-18T06:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-320045>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=81217)
>
> Anyone have a good mayo recipe? I can't find anything without canola or soybean oil in the stores. I was toying with the idea of using MCT oil....anyone tried that?

Been using this retail one lately
[Chosen Foods](https://www.vitacost.com/chosen-foods) Classic Mayo 100% Avocado Oil Based -- 24 fl oz
[https://www.vitacost.com/chosen-foods-classic-mayo-100-avocado-oil-based?CSRC=BPA-815074020096-bing\_pla\_gen\_pro\_food+&+beverages-&network=s&keywordname=&device=c&adid=92700074207528873&matchtype=e&gclick=&ds\_agid=58700008164553767&targetid=&utm\_source=bing&utm\_medium=cpc&utm\_campaign=bing\_pla\_gen\_pro\_food & beverages&utm\_term=4580496736182091&utm\_content=bing\_gen\_pro\_food & beverages\_condiments&gclsrc=ds&gclsrc=ds](https://www.vitacost.com/chosen-foods-classic-mayo-100-avocado-oil-based?CSRC=BPA-815074020096-bing_pla_gen_pro_food+%26+beverages-&network=s&keywordname=&device=c&adid=92700074207528873&matchtype=e&gclick=&ds_agid=58700008164553767&targetid=&utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=bing_pla_gen_pro_food%20%26%20beverages&utm_term=4580496736182091&utm_content=bing_gen_pro_food%20%26%20beverages_condiments&gclsrc=ds&gclsrc=ds)

I plan on buying an immersion blender soon and want to make my own Mayo.

### Marko Pollo — 2023-03-19T18:01:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-320079>

After roasting and eating a duck or chicken, simmer till broth is how you like and let it cool off. Skim, strain and separate the fat. It will be soft at room temp. Make mayo with this. Doesn't last long but is real food, not some virtue signaling processed garbage claiming the health benefits of processed oils. This will also make amazing blue cheese dressing if you like that. We have store bought mayo in the fridge, but it is not in the same league in terms of flavor or nutritional density. If the consistency doesn't perfectly mimic the commercial stuff, remember the food chemists can and do use any substance to make the product appear more appetizing and shelf stable.

### Annemarie — 2023-05-05T19:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-320912>

You can make mayonaise with all kinds of fat, with butter, or baconaise with bacon fat. Or if it has to be very neutral I guess you could use the flavourless coconut oill as well.

### ab70bandit — 2023-05-31T22:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mayonnaise.6413/post-321933>

The problem with alot of homemade butter mayo recipes is that after it's been in the fridge the mayo is not that spreadable. Chris cooking Nashville solve that issue with this recipe....
**Spreadable** butter mayo recipe: 1 large egg (separated) 1-2 tsp apple cider vinegar (optional) 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional) 1 pinch salt 1 stick butter (melted; 140-145F) Place egg yolk, vinegar, mustard, and pinch of salt in narrow container. Use stick blender on medium speed to combine well (10-15 seconds). Add melted butter 1-2 Tbsp at a time, blending well 10-15 seconds each time with stick blender in between each addition. This will create a thick mayonnaise. Add additional salt to taste if desired. Add egg white on top of thick mayo. Blend 5-10 seconds to combine. Blend an additional 10 seconds on high speed. Mayo will be a little frothy and somewhat runny. Refrigerate and use after it sets up. Mayo should be soft and spreadable straight out of the fridge.

I purchased some seaweeds and hoping to eventually get off t3 medication. I've purchased Kelp and Bladderwrack capsules from Mountain Herbs , Each capsule of Organic Kelp contains 595mg of kelp and the bladderwrack has 575 mg of organic bladderwrack along with this message: This product contains chemicals known to the state of California to cause cancer or birth defects or other reproductive harm.

I also have wakame and irish moss but they are loose and not capsules. I was going to sprinkle some in water and just drink. Again, I have no idea how much to take?

I remember Dr K mentioning in a webinar that as you use sea veggies, the diet etc, you might get some heart racing/faster beats, etc, which he said might tell you you're optimizing T3 levels and showing you you can back off. THat's what happened to me (including a 10 lb weight loss!), the only thing is...it also made my TSH go really low and I had to back off and I still haven't found the right dose. BUT that's my N=1, hopefully it will be much easier/smoothly for you!

Good luck.....

# Thread: Water and metabolism (from EMF Webinar)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-and-metabolism-from-emf-webinar.6425/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-03-21T22:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-and-metabolism-from-emf-webinar.6425/post-81361>

From the German study on water and it increasing metabolism....couldn't help googling this. It's right out of Berlin, my favorite German city (LOL)

[http://jcem.endojournals.org/content/88/12/6015.short](http://jcem.endojournals.org/content/88/12/6015.short)

Water-Induced Thermogenesis
Michael Boschmann, Jochen Steiniger, Uta Hille, Jens Tank, Frauke Adams, Arya M. Sharma, Susanne Klaus, Friedrich C. Luft and Jens Jordan

Abstract

Drinking lots of water is commonly espoused in weight loss regimens and is regarded as healthy; however, few systematic studies address this notion. In 14 healthy, normal-weight subjects (seven men and seven women), we assessed the effect of drinking 500 ml of water on energy expenditure and substrate oxidation rates by using whole-room indirect calorimetry. The effect of water drinking on adipose tissue metabolism was assessed with the microdialysis technique. Drinking 500 ml of water increased metabolic rate by 30%. The increase occurred within 10 min and reached a maximum after 30â€“40 min. The total thermogenic response was about 100 kJ. About 40% of the thermogenic effect originated from warming the water from 22 to 37 C. In men, lipids mainly fueled the increase in metabolic rate. In contrast, in women carbohydrates were mainly used as the energy source. The increase in energy expenditure with water was diminished with systemic β-adrenoreceptor blockade. Thus, drinking 2 liters of water per day would augment energy expenditure by approximately 400 kJ. Therefore, the thermogenic effect of water should be considered when estimating energy expenditure, particularly during weight loss programs.

No, but I have used Ex-Static cloth, and also Staticot cloth, on that same fabric page. I don't like the ExStatic cloth, it isn't conductive enough. Resistance is 10^5 Ohms. I like the Staticot much better, and use it for sleeping and on my chair. It has a resistance of only 1000 Ohms. It could be softer, for use at night, and I have ordered some SaniSilver, at less than 1 Ohm of resistance. But it is on backorder for another week.

Staticot would be great to use for clothing. Feels like a heavy cotton/poly blend. Maybe a lightweight khaki. You can't tell that it is a conductive fabric by sight or feel.

### Destiny — 2013-03-22T13:38:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-hack-this.6431/post-81422>

This is interesting .... [http://www.peaktestosterone.com/Free\_Testosterone\_SHBG.aspx](http://www.peaktestosterone.com/Free_Testosterone_SHBG.aspx)

It turns out that your levels of SHBG are actually very important, because, of course, SHBG is what binds to testosterone and can take it "out of commission." SHBG is controlled and produced in the liver primarily by androgens (and thyroxine). Normal aging, as many of us know all too painfully, leads to substantial drops in androgens such as testosterone and a corresponding rise in SHBG. Think how ugly this is: decreasing testosterone leads to increasing SHBG which leads to decreased free testosterone. No wonder some of you feel like crap!

Let me give you some of the most important ways to control SHBG and Free Testosterone:

1) Estrogen. You must keep your estrogen levels in check. Research shows that normal aging as well as an increase in adipose - that's a nice way of saying fat - tissue leads to ever increasing levels of estrogen. And here's the key: increasing estrogen leads to increasing SHBG. Please read the important link on How to Control Estrogen.

2) Testosterone. Low testosterone levels will tend to raise your SHBG as well. Yes, that's called a vicious circle. Read this link on How to Raise Testosterone Naturally if you have not already.
Your doctor won't give you testosterone? Show him What Low T Does. Combine supplements for max erectile strength? Go with This Approach.

3) Medications and Alcohol. Many medications can effect SHBG. Remember that SHBG is made in the liver and most medications effect the liver. Common drugs that often raise SHBG are sedatives, antihypertensiaves, tranquilizers as well as your old friends beer and booze.

4) Stinging Nettle. Another way to lower SHBG is through an herb called Stinging Nettle. Stringing Nettle is widely prescribed in Europe for BPH ( Benign Prostate Hypertrophy). Several studies have documented Stinging Nettle's SHBG-lowering powers \[2\] and, so far, Stinging Nettle has an excellent safety profile. However, its side effect profile is worth mentioning: Stinging Nettle also blocks the action of the enzyme that converts testosterone to DHT. Some would argue that this is good since DHT is associated with things like hair loss and prostate problems. However, sometimes, in sensitive individuals, side effects include erectile dysfuntion and a decreased amount of semen. Plus, DHT is a critical androgen for males and very important to libido and so Stinging Nettle is questionable in my opinion because of it. I should point out that there are no studies, as far as I know, that actually show Stinging Nettle increasing free testosterone: more research needs to be done.

5) Insulin Resistance. Insulin resistance will lower your SHBG \[1\] and, therefore, in the short term probably raise free testosterone. So let's all get insulin resistant and live happily ever after, right? Wrong! Insulin resistance is a death warrant. (Read this link on Metabolic Syndrome for more details.)

6) Sugar and Corn Syrup. These bad boys can lower SHBG. Unfortunately, these can lead to insulin resistance, inflammation, advanced glycation end products and many other nasty things. How does it do all this? Researchers have discovered that sugar will also lower SHBG, because it increases fat synthesis in the liver, which in turn shuts off the gene involved in SHBG synthesis. Bottom line: stay away from sugar and corn syrup even though in the short term they may raise your free testosterone.

7) Green Tea. Green Tea does actually raise SHBG.

8) Vitamin D. Vitamin D was found in a 2009 study of about 2,300 adult males to lower SHBG and increase both free and total testosterone. \[3\]

9) Boron (Sodium Tetraborate or Borax). One study showed that boron can not only increase free testosterone but lower estradiol as well. See my link on Boron and Testosterone for more details.

10) Protein-to-Carbohydrate Ratio. The protein/carbohydrate ratio has an interesting effect on free testosterone and SHBG. Several studies have shown that increasing carbs versus protein boosts total testosterone. However, before you start pounding granola bars, one study found that increasing the protein/carb ratio not only increased total testosterone but also SHBG. \[4\] This means that free testosterone may not have been increased significantly because of SHBG's opposing effects. (Increasing the protein to carbohydrate ratio also increased cortisol, a fact I cover elsewhere on this site.)

### Destiny — 2013-03-24T14:23:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-hack-this.6431/post-81602>

Patty already mentioned the effect of thyroxine so I won't repeat that.

I found more info on what can increase SHBG:

Any therapy that either increases SHBG levels (eg, estrogens or weight loss), reduces bioactivity of androgens (eg, androgen receptor antagonists, alpha-reductase inhibitors), or reduces insulin resistance (eg, weight loss, metformin, peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor \[PPAR\] gamma agonists), can be effective. Improvement is usually associated with a rise in SHBG levels, but bioavailable or free testosterone levels should also be monitored.

Patients with anorexia nervosa have high SHBG levels. With successful treatment, levels start to fall as nutritional status improves. Normalization of SHBG precedes, and may be predictive of, future normalization of reproductive function.

SHBG also has profound effects on the balance between bioavailable androgens and estrogens. Increased SHBG levels may be associated with symptoms and signs of hypogonadism in men, while decreased levels can result in androgenization in women.

Insulin resistance, even without obesity, results in lower SHBG levels. This is associated with increased intra-abdominal fat deposition and an unfavorable cardiovascular risk profile. In postmenopausal women, it may also predict the future development of type 2 diabetes mellitus. Androgens and norethisterone-related synthetic progesterones also decrease SHBG in women. In patients with known insulin resistance, "metabolic syndrome," or high risk of type 2 diabetes (eg, women with a history of gestational diabetes), low SHBG levels may predict progressive insulin resistance, cardiovascular complications, and progression to type 2 diabetes. An increase in SHBG levels may indicate successful therapeutic intervention.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-22T16:19:25+0200

I love following the links that come up on the forum....my latest searching took me from the Gerald Pollack interview with Dr. Mercola to the two links that were posted about lectures he did for a conference on the Electric Universe...anyway....I looked up the production company and got into the site for the Thunderbolts Project. There are tons of lectures and podcasts on the science of electricity titles like: A New Picture of Space: See how electricity trumps gravity, Our Electric Sun: Learn how the sun really works, Electricity of Life: Discover the electro-magnetic structures of life, The Human Story: Explore our many connections to the Electric Universe. Plus there seem to be some the early history of the earth.

They all seem to be in a paradigm shift position regarding Newtonian science....

Wow! Now I've just got to figure out how to listen to all this without too many emfs....I think I'll listen from across the room

### nonchalant — 2013-03-22T18:23:40+0200

### Arthurian Legend — 2013-11-22T14:28:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-thunderbolts-project.6435/post-101340>

I've recently stumbled across the Thunderbolts Project, after investigating structured water and the power of the vortex. Totally blown away by the theories and implications. This is a real game changer. I'm no expert in QED…but their models seem to tie the micro and macro together in a way that stumped Einstein! It's all about charged particles and plasma dynamics. Seems that the universe is essentially an electrical circuit (like us?!). Very interesting that images of neural networks in the brain look eerily similar to galactic nebulae...

The way I understand it, is that gravity is such a weak force that the equations cannot explain the mass of the universe and what's going on out there. According to the Electric Universe Model, the Big Bang did not happen. Black holes do not exist. Stars are not fusion reactors, but electrical discharge phenomena between 'filaments' (which explains why our sun has a cooler surface than the corona, why sunspots are black, and why the solar wind accelerates over distance).

Highly recommend checking out their YouTube vids as a starting point.

# Thread: Am I Leptin Sensitive yet (sorry can't post in leptin rx)?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-leptin-sensitive-yet-sorry-cant-post-in-leptin-rx.6444/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### razzy — 2013-03-23T07:38:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-leptin-sensitive-yet-sorry-cant-post-in-leptin-rx.6444/post-81515>

**Leptin**

Firstly, I apologise for posting in here, the forum is not letting me post in Leptin Rx for some reason.

I am trying to work out if I am leptin sensitive for the purpose of starting heavy lifting.

I Began reset:
21 Dec 12, Female,
5'4, 115.5lbs- small but not lean for the training I did and strength I had, body fat about 22-23% (not dexa)
Cortisol at this time: normal am, day, afternoon BUT HIGH AT NIGHT.
Already grain free 2 years (80% of time), already dairy free-strict 10months.
Sugar free since 1 Dec 2012
Cut exercise 8 Jan 2013

Total reset time: 3 months
Total reset without exercise: 2 months, Adrenal Rx: 2 months, Leaky Gut rx 1-2months.

CURRENT:Symptoms/ changes:
Weight 117lb (increased), body fat 21% (decreased about 1-2%), lost a lot of muscle with no training but weight gone up.
Increased sweating
Increased thirst
Sleep well, wake refreshed (sleeping-in for adrenal rx), still sometimes 1hr to fall asleep
Sleeping with light covers (but always have and have bad eczema so hot/itchy anyway)

Generally feel good (meditating also)
Already had new hair growth and changes in nail strength for 3 months prior to reset
Vit D is 142
CRP <1 (hs-crp not avail)

Still need three meals, if don't eat enough at breakfast still get hungry.
No crazy carbs cravings, but still 'really want' macadamias (have not cut yet)

I'm really not sure. I keep re-reading the blog and comments over and over to work it out!!!
Because of Adrenals and cortisol, and eczema hard to know if leptin fixed.

Any thoughts would help a lot. Really missing lifting!

Arclite: I know that Dr. K believes that it is far better to get our DHA and iodine in the form of it's whole food package. ### kathylu — 2013-03-25T17:50:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yikes-shop-for-your-seafood-carefully.6449/post-81709>

Buy your fish whole. That way you can look it over for problems. Have the store fillet it for you, give you back the bones, skin and head. Eat half the fillets raw, as sashimi or ceviche, lightly cook the rest. Make broth from the head and bones. Fry the skin in bacon fat until crispy and enjoy. Learn to fish and catch your own.

Then pray that we start to do a better job husbanding our fisheries. Actually call your politicians and demand that this issue be addressed.

### NittDan — 2013-03-25T17:53:21+0200

# Thread: Newbie to CT, but experiencing Adverse Effect after 3 weeks
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-to-ct-but-experiencing-adverse-effect-after-3-weeks.6452/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### miggity — 2013-03-23T22:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-to-ct-but-experiencing-adverse-effect-after-3-weeks.6452/post-81573>

Guys, I'm new here and new to CT. I have been eating Paleo for at least the last 6 months and started CT about 3 weeks ago. I am doing 6-7 minute cold showers at ~54 degrees twice a day -- morning and night. The benefits to date have been subtle, but one thing is for sure: i feel great after them and more alert throughout the day. Which is why i'm upset about a recent development: many of my toes have become red and swollen and somewhat painful. They look fluid filled. This has gone on for about a week (i.e., starting week 2 of CT) and seems now to be spreading to my fingers. I did a quick google search and found out about something called Chilblains that looks a lot like what i'm experiencing: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chilblains](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chilblains)

I spoke to an RN friend of mine who acted pretty alarmed that my cold showers had done this and he mentioned that the vasoconstriction caused by the cold has let some fluid escape into something he called "third space" -- that i'm "third spacing" -- he said this was not good and that if the cold could lead to a blood clot.

I'm really having a hard time with this because i want to continue, but in my case it is seeming that the risk is outweighing the benefits. i wanted to solicit the feedback of everyone -- i was prepared for a lot of benefits of doing CT, but not at all prepared for it putting my health at risk.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-25T02:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81660>

[http://shop.mercola.com/product/30w-compact-full-spectrum-light-bulb-120v-12-bulbs,772,87.htm](http://shop.mercola.com/product/30w-compact-full-spectrum-light-bulb-120v-12-bulbs,772,87.htm)

119.70 for 12 or 9.98 a bulb with limited time free shipping.

Anyone tried these or other full spectrum bulbs? I'm curious if they are actually helpful at all. I know I should get more real sunlight, but right now I'm still very much in my house. If theres something I can do to improve living in my house that would be great.

The only article I've found on it so far is that full spectrum bulbs may help for those that don't get much natural sun. They mentioned a possible placebo effect...if there are any studies on this anyone can link that would be great.

### IceBarbie — 2013-03-26T06:08:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81818>

We bought a bunch of these Mercola bulbs a couple of years ago and have mostly used them in our well-lit garage. A few have bit the dust--probably from being frozen solid most of the winter (the garage is unheated in Wisconsin) and a few got banged with a ladder, but overall, we've liked them. A few were replaced under warranty. I hate fluorescent lights and have hated them for years so this was one of the few alternatives we could find a few years back. There is one of these bulbs in my floor lamp that I often use too. It doesn't dry out my eyes like the fluorescent bulb that was in that lamp for awhile. It is bright and I don't shine it directly into my eyes or use it everyday.

### Inger — 2013-03-26T10:29:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81830>

Raiken why are you still very much in your house?
I have this great book about the sun, it says NOTHING really can replace the sun.. so I would do all what is in my power to just stay outside more. Sunlight is a huge healer. You need to be as nude as you can too and let it on your skin. You should read my book.. it is amazing what the sun is capable of, and how much damage no sunlight / much fake light does too.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-26T17:51:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81854>

I'll try to get out more. I know its good for me and I know I need to get out more. I'll work on it. Thanks for the encouragement.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-26T18:29:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81860>

Raiken, my plants love those lights.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-26T18:57:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81865>

> [Rachelle said:](/goto/post?id=81860)
>
> Raiken, my plants love those lights.

I was thinking that didn't matter to me...since I'm not a plant but considering they are living things I guess that's a good sign for me. Full spectrum lights or Mercola ones? I'm hoping Mercola's are good. They cost 9.98 a bulb...its not that bad, but I'd like to get the most out of my money.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-26T19:01:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-full-spectrum-bulbs.6461/post-81867>

It would be nice if they get around to producing affordable LED bulbs that you can change color temp based on the time of day. Tracking with what the light is outside. Late at night would be an option for minimal blue light. Would be great.

Inside light will never be as good as being outside in nature, but it would be nice to have as optimal a situation as possible inside.

### HeidiHen — 2013-03-25T15:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-melatonin-and-cortisol-testing-how-to.6463/post-81688>

I think I took my salivary melatonin test incorrectly this morning. Can anyone tell me if I should rinse out the collection vial and retest tomorrow? Here's what I did...

The melatonin instructions said to put the vial by the bed and not to turn on any lights. So, I woke this morning at the usual 5:30-ish AM, fumbled around in the dark and collected the cortisol vial. Then, I laid in bed, in the dark, with my head under the blanket until 7 am when I collected the melatonin vial. In re-reading the instructions again this morning, I'm wondering if I should have collect cortisol at 5:30, eaten my BAB, then collected my melatonin two hours later, sometime between 7-9, and not worried about staying in the dark. Does the instruction to stay in the dark only apply to the middle-of-the-night 2:30 am collection??? Should I retest melatonin tomorrow morning after being up and about? Thanks for your help!!!

Heidi

**Posts:** 4

### Rachelle — 2013-03-25T17:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweed-foot-soaks.6466/post-81701>

Since I am not near an ocean, what do you think of seaweed foot soaks or for a bath soak? Maybe add some sea salt for exfoliation. And of course an essential oil to smell good.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-25T17:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweed-foot-soaks.6466/post-81707>

Absolutely, Rachelle. I use epsom salts, sea salt, seaweed.. It's cheaper to add all the ingredients into a foot soak. I usually just add some epsom salts to the bath. But DH doesn't like too much salt in the sewer pipe. Corrosion, he says. Anti-inflammation, I say. So I compromise with more foot soaks.

### kathylu — 2013-03-25T17:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweed-foot-soaks.6466/post-81710>

### nonchalant — 2013-05-14T06:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seaweed-foot-soaks.6466/post-86002>

I recommend soaking your feet in RO or spring water, along with the salts. After just a couple soaks, I think I feel a difference in my feet. They seem tougher, and they tolerate barefooting longer. Previously I used (fluoridated) tapwater in my foot soaks, and I never noticed much of a benefit.

# Thread: Anyone own rental property?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-03-25T19:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/post-81727>

Bizarre question maybe, but it really does connect to this forum  DH and I are considering purchasing a place at the beach, probably a condo since that's all we can afford. We would definitely have to rent it out to help cover the costs. My thoughts are ranging from believing this is the BEST solution for me because:

a) We'd be about a 3-hr drive and I'd get to the beach a lot more . . . ocean, here I come; not to mention the photoelectric effect!
b) I'd have access to tons more fresh, local seafood
c) There's also the whole super low interest rate thing going on right now, which would make this a great investment.

Then I flip and think:

a) Condo = tons of wifi when I'm supposed to be helping myself by getting in the ocean, soaking up the son and binging on seafood. Does the wifi negate the other benefits?!
b) We have to rent it out to pay for the ridiculous HOA and condo fees. Will that be more stress than it's worth . . . who knows?!

Have you ever owned or thought about purchasing a vacation rental property? How did it go?

### JanSz — 2013-03-25T19:30:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/post-81736>

That are very good questions.

In the mean time I have booked Dec 15/Jan 15 condo at Breakers.
Snow Bird rates make it more accessible.

[http://www.breakers.com/?nck=8665860079&cmpid=2010vgh](http://www.breakers.com/?nck=8665860079&cmpid=2010vgh)

It is right on the nice beach, hardly many peoples this time of the year.
At least when I am on a beach (Myrtle Beach) I know I am in the right place. (Dr Kruse, is there anything wrong with Myrtle Beach, the actual beach?)
In the condo different story, likely more EMF than I have now in my house in Parsippany NJ.

....
Few miles south of Myrtle Beach, in Garden City, my friends owe a condo. For the last about 15-20 years.
There is hardly any problem with ownership. Rental agency take care of it all.
As long as the numbers work, there is a little problems with condo ownership.

.....

Watch out about insuring it.

My daugther and her husband owe condo to live in 365days/year.
One day in August few years ago bolt started fire, burned condo.
There is not much problems with that, nobody got hurt, we were watching air balloons elsewhere at that time.
Whole building, 12 condos got burned (not really, but that is how it was declared)
No problems, insurance pays for rebuilding condo.
But
mortgage must be paid every month
new expenses, they have to rent place and pay for it.
Insurance covered 2-3 months of living expenses, rebuilding lasted 3 years.
9 people out of 12 went bankrupt.
My daughter, son in law, two grand kids, German Shepperd moved into our house (for the three years).

Theoretically there should be no problem with rebuilding condo within 3 months.
Specially that the actual relatively minor fire damage was to only one condo on the top floor.
But, condo association is made up out of brothers, uncles and close relatives.
They got royal opportunity to milk the situation.

....

### kathylu — 2013-03-25T20:47:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/post-81745>

I am looking for a beach condo too. As long as the numbers work, it should be no problem, just list with a reputable rental agency. Only buy ocean front however, off the beach rentals are very inconsistent. I have owned rentals for 30 years, have made a ton of money from them and overall it is relatively little work.

Oh yeah...remember to build a Faraday cage in one bedroom. So when you stay there, you have some protection!

### JanSz — 2013-03-25T21:19:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/post-81751>

> [kathylu said:](/goto/post?id=81746)

> Oh yeah...remember to build a Faraday cage in one bedroom. So when you stay there, you have some protection!

Qualifications about Faraday Cage.

It is beneficial when inside the Faraday Cage and there are no sources of EMF inside the Cage. It protect us from EMF.

But, when there is a source of EMF (inside of the cage) it becomes Microwave oven with us being cooked.

I have newer seen it clarified, but it seems that when one builds Faraday Cage he/she plans on newer using there computer, cellphone, TV, or any other source of EMF.
With no way to escape, EMF will multiply xx fold.

.........

### Huck — 2013-03-25T21:24:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/post-81753>

I used to own a small 3 bedroom beach house on the NC Outer Banks. I owned it for 5 years and broke even (after taxes). The benefit I got was I usually used it about 5 times a year for free.

I had a real estate company rent it out and clean it. I always planned on a week in the Spring and another in the fall. I usually didn't reserve it in the summer because I needed the rent money to pay the mortgage. If it didn't get rented out for a particular week, I would usually go down for a 3 day week-end. I had the flexibility to take a day or 2 off on short notice, so this worked out great for me.

If money is an issue, you might want to run the numbers for a condo, townhouse, and detached house. While a detached house probably has a higher buy price, it will probably also have a higher rental rate.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-25T21:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-own-rental-property.6469/post-81756>

You wouldn't want to build a Faraday Cage on a 2nd or 3rd story apartment.

# Thread: My Future with Cold Thermogenesis & Epi Paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CTforlife — 2013-03-25T19:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-81734>

Hi guys, I just recently signed up on the forum, but I have been watching posts around here for a while. I have rosacea on my face, and eczema on my arm and legs. For this post to have any sort of logical flow I am going to have to share a part of my life with you guys. I grew up in California, where I lived for my first 15 years. At age 15 (freshmen going to sophomore year of high school summer) I moved to Nevada. At age 16, my gallbladder was acting up. I had the worst pain in my life, I couldn't bear it. My family knew nothing of alternatives because everyone is pretty much gone to cancer. I got my gallbladder removed, which was the worst decision of my life. I wouldn't wish it on people who sell whole grains to children. When I got my gallbladder removed, I went from a kid who had mild eczema on his arms, to having full blown eczema all over my body and out of control rosacea on my face. This happened gradually over a year. Before I got my gallbladder removed, I looked normal, besides my eczema, which at the time was very mild and didn't appear in summer (vit d much LOL). Now all of a sudden I was a ticking time bomb. This post is going to be long, but I promise everything connects, keep reading! In my junior year of high school my chem teacher asked me if I was limping, I said no. He said I was. At first I thought nothing of it, but then people slowly started commenting to me that my "gait" was slightly off. This made the fact I had horrible rosacea even worse. I went to a spine doctor of some sort, they checked for scoliosis, and said that wasn't the cause. They x rayed my lower legs. I had an leg length discrepancy. I forgot the term, but my leg was actually shorter (the bone) not a muscularity problem. If I sound uneducated about my own conditions I definitely am. They sized me up and ordered a shoe lift which I used to finish my high school career. Whats funny is the shoe lift didn't hurt my hip or my legs bad at all, which I hear most people complain about. Now at age 19, my right leg I would estimate is around 1 inch shorter, but heres the catch. I no longer where that lift.I now where vibrams. One day I had an epiphany. My body wasn't going to grow my right leg to compensate for it being shorter if I wore a lift each and everyday that signaled that in fact it didn't need to grow. I am a huge believer in epigenetics like most of you. I don't believe this is my fate. So long story longer (clever), I went primal my senior year of high school. Right after my 18th birthday when I looked in the mirror, saw something I didn't like (rosacea and dark bags under my eyes), and I weighed a bit too much. Weight loss was never a motivation for me, being healthy always was. I went from 236 pounds on the day of my birthday to 171 pounds on the day of my graduation. I still had rosacea, but my cheeks were only slightly pink, it was way under control compared to previous years, but my eczema was still bad. I practiced walking with my lift in my shoe soo much that I could walk normally so people couldn't tell I had a leg length discrepancy. The summer of my senior year till 3 weeks ago I ate like crap. Pizza, cereal, ice cream, you name it, I had it, and I had a ton of it. I grew tired of my rosacea worsening and my eczema spreading, and I realized I never met my goal of figuring out how to grow my leg back. Again, I believe in epigenetics more than most, because I feel like my situation, with a removed gallbladder, stripped my body of k2, (didnt take vit d), and I ate like crap from the time my gallbladder was out till my senior year of high school (till my birthday). I literally had Mcdonalds, arbies, tacobell, or subway every single day of my life from my sophomore year when I moved to Nevada, till I went primal on my birthday. I know I treated my body like garbage, I fed it garbage, we all know here that you are what you eat, more than most actually. Long story hopefully getting shorter, now I am on a plan to get optimal. If I could afford a talk with Jack I totally would, but for right now I am going to strictly ad hear to his protocols and see how that goes. I currently trying to eat as epi paleo as I can, shifting toward more omega 3 laden foods, like seafood. I have to admit staying on this diet isn't the least bit hard for me. I love the way it makes me feel, and better yet I love cold thermogensis, or I think I do. So earlier on in this post I said I have been following his protocols for 3 weeks (the leptin stuff). I thought cold thermogenesis could be my night in shining armor for my health, and hopefully my leg. To make sure I was't going to waste $24 on amazon, for the thermometer jack recommends, I tried cold showers. I love cold showers, I live in Nevada remember, this morning was 20 degrees, my running water is COLD. I know this isn't the same as real CT, but I needed to try it to make sure I had the balls to do it. After two weeks of cold showers and sometimes nightly showers, I have decided to bite the bullet and really do this thing. I ordered my fish oils (I know I know, but seafood is too costly for me atm), vitamin d, k2, nac with vitamin c, a thermometer, and I drink raw milk (we will get to this later, only half a gallon per week), and a multivitamin. Previously when I went primal my senior year, I didn't use any of these things, now I am, to come back stronger, fitter, leaner, happier, and excited about my future than ever. So this Wednesday my stuff comes. I am beyond excited! I just wanted to share my challenges and goals with all of you who care to listen, or that matter read this far. I am going to try to follow his protocols by the book, to ensure my time and energy is well spent.

My goals with CT and Epi Paleo - Cure my leaky gut, which in turns heals my rosacea and eczema, and grow this leg back to correct length. Some people may think I'm crazy, but I was feeding my body soooo much crap from since I was a baby till my 18th birthday, then I went primal till I graduated (4-5 months), then I ate like crap again till 3 weeks ago (now I'm 19). I honestly and whole heartily believe some ct, some vit d, and some k2 will help me out. Along with the epi paleo diet. I went from 215 to 190 in 3 weeks, which was mostly water, but I honestly think those cold showers helped me lose a ton of fat, like I think by my first CT session (this thursday) I will already be pretty lean again. I also forgot to add, it takes me around one to two minutes in a cold shower, but then my body radiates heat like no other, I swear my water isn't cold anymore. I don't want to sound ignorant, but I might of provoked cold adaption really well from eating very ketogenic lately.

I know this post was extremely hard to follow, but I had a very limited amount of time to write and edit it. I always free write before drafting. I will update my post when I get the time tonight. Thanks for making it this far, and most of all I want to thank Jack, for giving me the inspiration and information I needed to make a serious change in my life that matters. No more sub-optimal, I will be Optimal, or god damn I will die trying.

### CTforlife — 2013-03-26T18:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-81866>

Ok I want to make sure my diet/supplementation is on a path toward Optimal!

I am gonna take Vit D : 10000 UI a day, too much?
K2 : Not sure, need recommendations, got the life extension brand recommend by Jack.
A good multivitamin: Now Foods Adam or something, just the suggested serving size, should I increase?
EPA/DHA Fish Oil (Now Foods) : 4 Pills a day, I think they have 1000mg in each pill. Actually its 500 EPA, and 250 DHA, confused how it still says 1000mg total, but only has 750mg of EPA/DHA. I think the rest is filler or something.
NAC with Vit C: I think Jacks recommend amount is 600mg 3x a day with NAC and those are accompanied by 1000 MG of Vit C.

Also in my post I said that I am consuming raw milk. Heres the deal: I heard about the healing properties of raw milk and its potential to help fix leaky gut before I decided going epi paleo and signed onto a 6 month cow sharing agreement where I receive a gallon a week. This is split between my brother and myself. So I drink a half gallon each weak, or around a cup or so with every meal. Will this likely hold me back? I know I had horrible gut flora so I wanted good probiotics along with the healing properties of the milk (grassfed). I know you cannot give me any definite answers without some solid lab work to any of my questions dosing questions or about the milk, but your feedback will help guide me. I figured that I should go epi paleo for a month or two with CT and then get my stuff checked because I don't have the financials or the insurance to get this done at a reasonable price. Thanks for the feedback thus far, tomorrow is when all this stuff comes and CT really starts, ok time to take a cold shower and get off to class!

One more thing I forgot to mention. I take 2-3 tablespoons of Braggs apple cider vinegar 5-10 minutes before each meal to stimulate stomach acid and bile. Do you think this is a good idea. When I was primal back in my senior year of high school I used to take HCL before meals and super enzymes after (now foods). Do you think I should do that again? I think the vinegar is a great replacement for the HCL pills, but I don't have one for the enzymes. I would love to not take the enzymes because thats one more thing I need to buy. The other thing is should I do the vinegar with some ox bile pills? I know without lab results its hard to tell if I'm digesting fats properly without a gallbladder, but I think I might be because I have been primal before now and it might of trained my liver, but who knows. Thanks!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-27T04:01:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-81933>

Milk from Jersey cows or other older breeds has fewer of the opiates and other problems of milk from holsteins and other newer breeds. I was extremely reluctant to stop drinking raw jersey dairy (I would let it separate and drink the cream.) I felt very anxious about doing without it, actually. But when I started eating seafood daily, I found that I was willing to let the dairy go. I think my body had found a new source of the nutrition it needed.

### CTforlife — 2013-03-27T06:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-81937>

I am going to up my seafood intake dramatically here soon! Tomorrow is D-day, but I'm super excited! I'm not sure if I should keep hands in or not, because they turn purple with red spots in cold showers, but the rest of my body doesn't. I think its due to a bad omega 3 to 6 ratio or something. Whats weird is they don't hurt at all and when I get out of the shower it takes like an hour for normal colorization to set in. Well I'm off to bed, this has been one hell of a day.

### CTforlife — 2013-03-28T17:29:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82055>

Ok I lied, I got busy yesterday and put my first day of CT off. Today is my first day, I will be doing a 30 minute minimum water and ice bath hoping to go as long as an hour, but its my first time so I doubt that. I was wondering do I need compression gear for a water and ice bath or only for straight ice placed on my body? And should I leave my hands out of the water? If anyone sees this I would love a reply!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-28T17:41:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82059>

I think you should definitely leave your hands out of the water. Sounds like Reynaud's syndrome. And you might, after sitting down, lift your feet out as well, at least in the beginning.
My first ice bath was about 7 minutes. The next (a couple days later) was about 15, I think. Then 20, 25, and then 30 I believe.
Some people are more ambitious, but that first time was the hardest by far. Subsequent CT's were easier, as my body started adapting.
I used compression gear, but I don't think it is necessary if you don't have ice directly on the skin.

### CTforlife — 2013-04-02T00:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82531>

Ok sorry for the long period without updates, but I have been extremely busy. So I did my first CT on thursday, and I loved it. I loved it soo much I did it twice. The first time I did it was for 45 minutes keeping my skin at 55 degrees F and I didn't feel cold at all. Then, later that same day I stepped it up and bought more ice and did 32 minutes of CT in a bigger size tub so it would be easier to submerge my entire body. I felt good, but when I got out I was really cold. I think I did too much CT the first day, and pushed myself too hard too fast. I am looking forward to an hour tomorrow and another hour (hopefully longer) on Thursday. I have been eating way more seafood and getting my diet where it needs to be, also I cut out all dairy for right now and I am gonna cut out eggs starting today to see if that helps with my eczema and rosacea. Did any of you feel cold your first time? I mean the 45 minute length CT at 55 degrees F I felt cold for a half hour and then warmed up, but the later day one at 50 degrees F left me cold and using a heater to heat myself back up. Thanks for the continued support, it keeps me motivated to stay on track to Optimal. Also if anyone has advice on oysters please let me know, I bought a two pack thing of oysters which are already out of the shell and I have been cooking a few each morning along with my eggs, but now since I am no longer eating eggs the amount of seafood I am eating will skyrocket. I haven't tried them raw yet, mainly because I would rather taste my food than just eat them raw and not chew. If anyone knows if raw are better than cooked please let me know! Ok thats all for today, I'll let you know how tomorrows CT goes!

### nonchalant — 2013-04-02T00:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82536>

Wow! I'm impressed! Sounds like great results! How did your hands tolerate the CT?

Yes, later in the day your cortisol is probably lower and it takes longer to warm up.
Raw oysters are better, but any oysters are better than none. I always chew.

### CTforlife — 2013-04-02T07:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82573>

I put my hands and feet in, and to my surprise I had no abnormal discoloration and my hands didn't hurt. They turned pinkish and went to there normal color after I warmed up. I am going to man up and eat some raw oysters for breakfast tomorrow, wish me luck! The slime factor is going to be the hardest part, and if I just eat them I'm gonna have to eat a ton.

### CheshireCat — 2013-04-02T09:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82580>

Really interesting post CTforlife! I'm here trying to improve my families health - including my daughters eczema so I will follow your story with interest!

Good luck, your positivity and dedication is a real asset!

### CTforlife — 2013-04-03T23:05:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-82724>

So I did CT yesterday and it went, well, let me explain. I over bought ice and I was short for time. I only had an hour to CT, but I ended up only going for 30 minutes. I was an idiot and didn't check the water temp before I got in, I soon checked it after I realized that this water was much colder than I had even been in before. It was 36 degrees F, I got in, sucked it up and tried to survive. I managed this temp for around 10 minutes before I heated it up to around 45 degrees F, and then I finished the remainder of my CT at that temp. The thing is that I turned bright pink all over when it was 36 degrees F, and I didn't go pail. I didn't want to jump down that low that fast, but it happened and I am kinda happy about it. I only lasted 30 minutes because of the beginning temp because I had planned 45-50 degrees F for an hour. It only took me around 15 minutes to warm up in the sun which was starting to set. So I think it went alright, but next time I want 45 degrees F for an hour, not any lower until I progress. The reason I think I handled the 36 degrees F well is because I sun bathed for an hour before, 30 minutes upward, 30 minutes downward (laying on my stomach). I did initially feel really weird when I got in, like my body was tingling and kinda buzzing, very hard to describe, but this went away after I started to adapt. I ate a whole pound of raw oysters for breakfast yesterday, but not without a fight. I almost gagged for the first 1/2 pound or so. Then I got used to the texture and sensation. I cut out all dairy, including butter for almost a week now and I haven't noticed anything quite yet. I also decided to cut out eggs yesterday, which saddened me deeply because they are the most awesome breakfast! I love my onion, cilantro, and mushroom omelets with sausage or bacon. Looks like raw oysters for breakfast now, but I'll have to eat like 1/2 pound for breakfast daily because I can't afford to eat more. I'll add avocados and spoonfuls of coconut oil to increase my fat. My digestion is changing, but I can't tell for the better or the worst, the raw oysters came out, well, like a D bomb. I think this will clear up. @CheshireCat I haven't seen my eczema improve too much yet, but its just the beginning and I am definitely trying to get more sun so it will go away. Thats it for today folks, see you later! PS: Next CT is thursday, will update!

### chicho — 2013-06-27T04:13:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-89029>

Please help me understand as I am confused on the cold shower theory. I've read that cold showers increase body heat as the body reacts to try and warm up. This seems like it would have the opposite desired effect if you're trying to achieve CT?

What am I missing? Thank you in advance folks. I appreciate any insight.

### CTforlife — 2013-06-27T08:05:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-89043>

@chicho Forget the "theory" part. Cold water cools your body more efficiently than air. 50-55 degrees F is optimal, if your shower can reach these temperatures, prolonged showers will produce results. I personally like grounding with CT, but showers can work.
The idea of CT is to light that pilot light, burn fat as free heat in the Optimal temperatures, ideally at sunrise or sunset. I like a meal before morning CT, and I feel once a day is great because my morning CT is long (1-2 hours). Unfortunately, my local river is way too warm for what I like. I have to use a tub for the time being. The cold preserves life and allows to it thrive. Do this outdoors or in the ocean and experience colder temperatures as time goes on. Winter is life saving for those inflicted with neolithic diseases, we ARE our environment. Set into place your old mammalian ultimate survival epigentic programs and let your wings soar.

### CTforlife — 2013-06-29T07:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-future-with-cold-thermogenesis-epi-paleo.6471/post-89178>

I'm buying a magnetico sleep pad tomorrow. I can only afford the classic sleep pad, but it's better than nothing. I think the pad along with Epi-paleo & CT is a recipe for success. I'm feeling great and leptin sensitive, and I decided it was time to "push the weights". I will be purchasing a gym membership in the near future for HIIT weights. Bone growth has everything to do semiconductors and leptin, two of which were entirely screwed by my "up bringing". My rosacea is completely gone, as well my adrenal fatigue. At their core they were both inflammation due to circadian mismatches which created cellular chaos inducing inflammation and autoimmunity. My gut healed with a strict Epi-paleo diet coupled with CT and following of circadian rhythms. I can't think of a better addition to my "environment" than a magnetico sleep pad. My whole family is in the process of making their own changes and making their own decisions. My brother eats more paleo (less seafood/more fruit & starch) than me and doesn't follow leptin meal timing and has had great results. He was also at a different starting point than me. He has a gallbladder, something of value to a human. He saw me one day after I CT'ed, and what I did intrigued him into trying it. CT will bring you health and performance, its the perfect double edge sword. Grounding has had such a profound effect on my life. I think its the key to accomplishing my goal, along with CT'ing as much as possible. I will try to provide an update after I get the pad. I'm hoping over time my leg starts to grow, as I haven't had it measured, but it is WAY stronger now and I have been grounding a ton to direct current. I did a 10 mile hike the other day and my hip hurt, but after a few hours of grounding I no longer felt soar. I am in the process of restoring a normal "rhymth" to my life, which will in the end produce the result I'm looking for. If the quilt has taught me one thing its this: Everything is interconnected to your environment. Live the way your were designed and reap the benefits. We can make the conscious decision to change our life if we choose to. No other animal can modify its environment like us. Maybe its time to live in congruence, and the weird neolithic diseases will go away. What if we were designed to live way past what me might imagine?

### Hope — 2013-03-28T21:46:21+0200

### beach bunny — 2013-04-01T16:40:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-labs-should-i-get.6481/post-82461>

From Dr. Kruse's Blog:

LAB PANELS:
1\. VO2 Max assessment (cardio pulmonary findings lead to a work up if needed.)
2\. Initial visit DEXA with body comp. Optimization a DEXA scan to include body composition scores.
F/U visits I use BIA assessments to monitor progress and limit radiation exposure.
3\. Neuro cognitive battery of tests to assess processing speed, response speed, memory, learning, and attention repeated annually.
4\. Chemistry 20
5\. Liver function testing
6\. Serum Iron, TIBC, Serum Ferritin and Iron % Saturation
7\. Lipid panel: VAP and/or NMR analysis
8\. Homocysteine level
9\. Complete CBC with differential and platelet count (sometimes an Ivy bleeding time)
10\. Hormone panel includes (gender specific)……serum and salivary cortisol, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, PSA, IGF-1, Consider based upon history testing levels of progesterone, pregnenolone, melatonin, serum and urine Osmolarity, Prolactin levels
11\. Complete thyroid panel with antibody screening (TPO etc)
12\. Complete Urinalysis (women get beta HCG if they menstruate)
13\. Highly Sensitive CRP (Not a regular CRP)
14\. Complete Vitamin D panel
15\. Omega 6/3 serum test (for severe findings consider tissue assay)
16\. Fasting serum insulin and HbA1c assessment
The next set of labs are considered add ons if the patients’ history or physical require it being looked at.
Cancer history of colon, breast, ovary:
CEA for colon cancer
CA 125 for ovarian cancer
CA 27.29 for breast cancer
AFP blood test
CA125, 15-3, 27.29, 19.9,
Previous heart disease:
Apo A-1 and B, B natriurietic factor, LpA status, Lp-PLA2, CIMT, calcium index score
Bone pathology:
Osteocalcin frozen blood analysis, Se blood test, RBC blood testing for Mg, Deoxypyridinoline (DPD) Cross Link Urine Test, Parathyroid Hormone assay
Inflammatory/autoimmune panels:
EBV panel, ANA panel, complete cytokine assay, candida antibodies, helicobacter IgG panel, Hep A, B and C titers, breakout of cytokine panel, ESR, RA factor, SLE screen, T Lymphocyte helper suppressor assay, IL 8, IL 1 B or TNF alpha analysis

### MeghanK — 2013-04-02T16:30:36+0300

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-02T17:51:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-labs-should-i-get.6481/post-82613>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=82603)

n=1 is the most important thing...

Personally, I try to drink most of my water in the mornings. I figure when I wake up I have been without water for a long time, so I need to rehydrate fast.
The salt sounds fine, the more salt in our diets the better. Just don't put in so much that you don't want to drink the water.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-28T16:01:46+0200

I do the same. I generally have 48 oz of water by 8 AM every morning. It helps that my BAB is usually a HUGE bowl of soup with 16 oz of broth. I do add about 1/2 tsp sea salt to some of my glasses of water as well. I just find that it goes down more smoothly, especially first thing in the AM.

### Martha Ray — 2021-11-23T22:46:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-quench-thirst-any-ideas.6485/post-306578>

Over the past 12 years I have 'felt' dehydrated but not thirsty. I think I've been drinking water wrong... guzzling a lot at a time at least 1 gallon mostly RO Water from our home unit...but not too consistent except to avoid 30 min before eating and 1-2 hours after...thought perhaps I was flushing out electrolytes drinking this way so I added mineral drops (I use Trace Mineral drops), lemon or lime, ACV, and salt at times... stopped ACV as it seemed to cause a histamine reaction and a few years ago stopped adding salt to water because Dr. Kruse mentioned not to and also watching YT video [The Importance of Salt & Water, by Barbara O'Neill](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fa3nJpX5QFs) **[](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fa3nJpX5QFs)...** I liked what she said at about 11:40 minutes into the presentation about taking a small grain nugget or Celtic salt and chasing it with a full glass of water ...Barbara O'Neill believes in self healing and nutrition. I do that 1 or two times per day. Upon waking I drink 1 to 2 liters of water and I've also begun to fill 6 shot glasses with water about my home and sip more often instead of gulping. I use Dr. Ben Lynch's _Seeking Health_ brand **_[Optimal Electrolyte](https://www.seekinghealth.com/collections/electrolytes/products/optimal-electrolyte?variant=22331143028796)_ powder in the '**_**Seltzer**_' option about 1-2 X per week and feel like these have helped me. I tried a lot of different 'electrolyte' brands and I like this one for taste and electrolyte composition the best. I pack the **[Optimal Electrolyte - Sticks](https://www.seekinghealth.com/collections/electrolytes/products/optimal-electrolyte-sticks-30-servings?variant=21856331137084) when I travel as travel really dehydrates me!**
Diet wise I used to green juice some but not lately but have added iceberg lettuce wedges for some fiber and for it's higher water content ...I like that I can get it nice and cold and for ease of prep. I am thankful for and pray over my water as well as my food.

I think Dr. Kruse drinks hard water I guess from a well... I know there is calcium and magnesium in hard water and have heard it is better for cardiovascular health.. You'd think that would be discussed more. Wonder what range of alkalinity best ? All I find online is promotion of _softening_ water I guess for taste? or for not eroding pipes and household equipment?
**Water is a big question mark for me ?**
t just came in yesterday but I've begun reading the book, **[Quench: Beat Fatigue, Drop Weight, and Heal Your Body Through the New Science of Optimum Hydration](https://www.amazon.com/Quench-Fatigue-Through-Science-Hydration/dp/0316515663/ref=tmm_hrd_swatch_0?_encoding=UTF8&qid=1637690805&sr=8-5)** which at first review appears to stress movement as important for hydration. _Hope to find out more soon._

_**Where can learn more and get hard water to drink?**_

### JanSz — 2021-11-24T00:08:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-quench-thirst-any-ideas.6485/post-306579>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=306578)
>
> I think I've been drinking water wrong... guzzling a lot at a time at least 1 gallon
>
> Upon waking I drink 1 to 2 liters of water
> .......
> _**Where can learn more..........?**_

[https://www.labcorp.com/tests/046557/antidiuretic-hormone-adh-profile](https://www.labcorp.com/tests/046557/antidiuretic-hormone-adh-profile)

**Do this test every two weeks** until you understand how your body react, and how much water you need to drink.
Adjust your drinking habits in such a way that on above test you get ADH=2 or slightly higher.

**Synonyms**

-   ADH
-   Arginine Vasopressin
-   AVP
-   Vasopressin

Reference Interval
• ADH: 0.0−4.7 pg/mL
