# Thread: Why Does It Take So Long to Ground?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-03-26T16:35:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81848>

When I walk outside barefooted, why doesn't my electrical charge immediately normalize with the ground?

Or does it?

Is the recommended 30 minutes because it takes about 30 minutes or is it to provide a healthy electrical environment for 30 minutes?

### shilohman — 2013-03-26T17:21:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81850>

Because when you ground you are getting micro/milli amps of electrons, which is very small. I don't remember how many cells are in the human body, but it is at least in the millions. If you consider each cell as a rechargeable battery, then it takes time to recharge your cells. This is a very over simplification of what is happening and there are other factors that effect this, for example where you ground can influence what type of time is required. If you ground in Central Park in New York city where there must be a horrendous amount of EMF hitting the ground you probably will not get as good a ground as standing three feet in water in the ocean.

### Huck — 2013-03-26T17:29:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81852>

So that implies that laying on the ground naked is better than just being barefooted. Maybe faster would be more accurate than better. Or does the size of the contact area really make a difference? Maybe something else is acting as a regulator?

### shilohman — 2013-03-26T18:40:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81862>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=81852)
>
> So that implies that laying on the ground naked is better than just being barefooted. Maybe faster would be more accurate than better. Or does the size of the contact area really make a difference? Maybe something else is acting as a regulator?

I don't really know, but my guess is that it is no faster. The current flow is so small that I don't think it would have any effect. The size of the contact area should only matter when you are dealing with high amperages.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-26T19:11:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81868>

Great analogy CindyB!

Huck - I think when Jack gets to why we became bi-pedial first before we grew big brains - there's going to be something to the reason why feet and conductivity are very important

if you look at acupuncture - the feet have a plethora of nerves that can relate back to specific organs etc. think about your feet as your 7th sense. when walking barefoot it provides a completely different form of input to your brain for evaluation of the conditions the body is in, which I think could be very important....

### David — 2013-03-26T22:31:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81889>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=81850)
>
> Because when you ground you are getting micro/milli amps of electrons, which is very small. I don't remember how many cells are in the human body, but it is at least in the millions. If you consider each cell as a rechargeable battery, then it takes time to recharge your cells.

This is a very good observation. And the number of cells in the body is actually even larger than you indicate.

ScienceBlogs sez: [http://scienceblogs.com/gregladen/2011/11/28/how-many-cells-are-there-in-th/](http://scienceblogs.com/gregladen/2011/11/28/how-many-cells-are-there-in-th/)

"According to various sources, the following is more or less true:

Adult people (for our present purposes) weigh between 60 and 90 kg. (Iâ€™m ignoring small populations here because this is mainly for American Audiences.) The amount of bone in a body â€¦ the non-cellular part â€¦ is between 14 and 20 percent. The amount of blood that is not cellular (i.e, that is water) is about 5% of the total body mass. The mass of a typical body cell is about one gram times ten to the negative nine, or one nanogram.

â€¦ do the math â€¦

I get about 46 to 68 trillion."

### shilohman — 2013-03-27T00:04:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81895>

> [David said:](/goto/post?id=81889)
>
> This is a very good observation. And the number of cells in the body is actually even larger than you indicate.
>
> ScienceBlogs sez: [http://scienceblogs.com/gregladen/2011/11/28/how-many-cells-are-there-in-th/](http://scienceblogs.com/gregladen/2011/11/28/how-many-cells-are-there-in-th/)
>
> "According to various sources, the following is more or less true:
>
> Adult people (for our present purposes) weigh between 60 and 90 kg. (Iâ€™m ignoring small populations here because this is mainly for American Audiences.) The amount of bone in a body â€¦ the non-cellular part â€¦ is between 14 and 20 percent. The amount of blood that is not cellular (i.e, that is water) is about 5% of the total body mass. The mass of a typical body cell is about one gram times ten to the negative nine, or one nanogram.
>
> â€¦ do the math â€¦
>
> I get about 46 to 68 trillion."

Doh, I sure got that wrong, thanks David.

### Destiny — 2013-03-27T00:39:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-81898>

> [shilohman said:](/goto/post?id=81850)
>
> Because when you ground you are getting micro/milli amps of electrons, which is very small. I don't remember how many cells are in the human body, but it is at least in the millions. If you consider each cell as a rechargeable battery, then it takes time to recharge your cells. This is a very over simplification of what is happening and there are other factors that effect this, for example where you ground can influence what type of time is required. If you ground in Central Park in New York city where there must be a horrendous amount of EMF hitting the ground you probably will not get as good a ground as standing three feet in water in the ocean.

I think there are 20 trillions of cells in the human body, if I remember correctly.

### David — 2013-03-29T00:37:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-82098>

The electrical scheme appears to be quite involved, and not so easily analyzed. The numbers are rather impressive, though. "Power, Sex, Suicide, Chapter 5: Proton Power" indicates that it is believed that the electrical potential across the mitochondrial membrane is about 150 millivolts or 30 million volts per meter. Large numbers that strain credibility. And I imagine that the electron flow in grounding involves much more than an instantaneous exchange of charge. There are obviously a number of chemical and physical changes that meter the electron flow to produce nearly imperceptive results, perhaps a form of chemical or biological inertia that is slow to respond. (This is way over my head, I am speculating.)

### Glamazon — 2013-03-29T04:55:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-it-take-so-long-to-ground.6487/post-82125>

It is much easier and quicker to ground, sometimes almost immediately based on my experience, if you are adequately hydrated.

# Thread: PhosphatidylSerine and Low Cortisol/ Methylation Issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2013-03-26T19:29:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/post-81870>

I am working on my low cortisol, have been for well over a year now. I have not been able to do a recent salivary test, but I was low for the first three and low normal on the last time frame. In addition, I have identified some methylation issues and was following a protocol of supplements to benefit this - a special multi vitamin featuring TMG, intrinsi folate and and b12 combo, another b12 suitable for methylation troubles, and lastly phosphatidyl serine. So I just ordered the last supp, and now am reading it is only beneficial to those who have high cortisol.

Can we discuss phosphatidyl serine and the benefits you have seen? Or precautions?

I haven't opened it yet and am considering just returning it. While I haven't done the adrenal panel recently, I know I am not healed yet. I feel good for the most part, but major stressors still take me out. If I get overly upset about something, I am ready to sleep immediately upon calming down. This makes me feel as though my body is still working too hard to produce cortisol.

I have trouble keeping my protocols straight and really need to formulate a special chart to not try something that won't be good for me!

### janagram — 2013-04-18T05:23:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/post-84369>

I am wondering the same thing....trying PSerine for sleep but don't know if that's a good idea.....

### MeghanK — 2013-04-18T05:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/post-84370>

I have been using it for a month... but also use 5mg of melatonin and 2 xanax for sleep (I have anxiety disorder/panic attacks...) so not sure what is doing what... but I have been sleeping a full 7-8 hours without getting up the in the middle of the night with racing thoughts the last month...

### tweety — 2013-04-18T17:54:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/post-84409>

I decided to return the supp because it is indicated for people with high cortisol. Not me.

As far as improving sleep, my husband and I listened to a guided meditation for deep rest last night (right before sleep) and we both slept so deeply. The best sleep in a long time for me!

### beach bunny — 2013-04-19T15:50:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/post-84477>

> [tweety said:](/goto/post?id=81870)
>
> I am working on my low cortisol, have been for well over a year now. I have not been able to do a recent salivary test, but I was low for the first three and low normal on the last time frame. In addition, I have identified some methylation issues and was following a protocol of supplements to benefit this - a special multi vitamin featuring TMG, intrinsi folate and and b12 combo, another b12 suitable for methylation troubles, and lastly phosphatidyl serine. So I just ordered the last supp, and now am reading it is only beneficial to those who have high cortisol.
>
> Can we discuss phosphatidyl serine and the benefits you have seen? Or precautions?

Hi Tweety, not sure if I will be any help because I still don't have my Salivary Cortisol levels.
I was recommended the **Phosphatidyl Serine Complex** from Holistic Heal (Yasko's formulation, Dr. Mullan uses it too) to help with the "shortcut" of the methylation cycle.
Another Doc then recommended I try **Sunflower Lecithin by Now**. It is cheap, does not contain any Soy or GMO and is supposed to be quite effective.

Unfortunately, I do not have any personal experience with either product and have not yet researched them further. Just wanted to share this info in case it helps.

### tweety — 2013-04-19T16:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/phosphatidylserine-and-low-cortisol-methylation-issues.6488/post-84480>

I had gotten the Phosphatidyl serine complex as part of the Yasko methylation protocol too, and did manage to get soy free. But the fact that it lowers cortisol was concerning to me. I liked Yasko's ideas in general, but alot of the supplements recommended contained ingredients that should not be included. I really had to do a lot of digging to adapt the guidelines to my needs.

# Thread: evidence on microwaves
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evidence-on-microwaves.6490/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-03-26T21:51:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evidence-on-microwaves.6490/post-81884>

Hello
Does anyone have any links to studies of the effects of microwaves on food itself or of their effects on people standing next to the machine when in use. I've searched the forum and I can't find any info. Also, can someone post about Jack's two steaks in the microwave experiment.

Thanks

### shilohman — 2013-03-27T00:18:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evidence-on-microwaves.6490/post-81897>

> [agatha said:](/goto/post?id=81884)
>
> Hello
> Does anyone have any links to studies of the effects of microwaves on food itself or of their effects on people standing next to the machine when in use. I've searched the forum and I can't find any info. Also, can someone post about Jack's two steaks in the microwave experiment.
>
> Thanks

Can't comment on Jack's two steak experiment. You can do your own N=1 which is what I did. I cooked a sweet potato in the micro wave and I cooked another one in a little toaster oven. Then did a taste test. The one from the microwave had quite a different taste. The one from the toaster oven was very flavorful while the microwave one wasn't. I also tested the microwave with a TriField meter. With the microwave not on the reading was very high. With the microwave on the reading pegged the needle. Had to get 10 feet away before the reading dropped to a low reading. That is when I unplugged my microwave and haven't used it sense.

### endless — 2013-03-27T01:30:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evidence-on-microwaves.6490/post-81905>

Here's an article...not sure if ther4e are links to the actual studies [http://www.health-science.com/microwave\_hazards.html](http://www.health-science.com/microwave_hazards.html)

# Thread: 14 Spectacular Basalt Formations

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-27T13:45:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-spectacular-basalt-formations.6494/post-81949>

[http://www.theworldgeography.com/2013/01/basalt-formations.html](http://www.theworldgeography.com/2013/01/basalt-formations.html)

Basalt is a common volcanic rock formed from the rapid cooling of basaltic lava exposed at or very near the surface of our planet. Jointed basalt columns, as the most famous and most beautiful basalt formations, exists on many places on earth. These unusual columns are predominantly hexagonal in cross-section, but basalt polygons with three to twelve or more sides can be observed.

### fitness@home — 2013-03-27T14:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-spectacular-basalt-formations.6494/post-81950>

# Thread: What Time Is It and Where Am I?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-is-it-and-where-am-i.6499/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-03-27T21:48:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-is-it-and-where-am-i.6499/post-81982>

My internal clock used to be near perfect. I could wake up in the middle of the night and know what time it was before checking the clock. If I had to get up early, say 5 am, I would automatically wake up about 10 minutes to 5.

For years now, I had no clue when I woke up at night what time it was. These days it is getting a little better. Often I am within 10 minutes of the correct time, but there are still times I wake up at 1 am and think it is 4 am.

_Has anyone had their internal clock completely sync up again?_

Same thing with sense of direction. I used to explore the woods and go for long drives, both without using a map. I instinctively knew where the compass points were without having to think about it. I always felt like I knew where I was and always made it home without having to ask for directions.

I noticed several years ago that I was starting to have trouble with direction. When I moved to this location about 5 years ago, my sense of direction was completely lost. My brain tells me that North is South and East is West. I have gotten lost more times than I care to admit. I have not seen any improvement at all in my internal compass.

_Comments? Anyone think their sense of direction is improving?_

I have always had a fear of heights. I remember the 1st time I noticed it. I got into an glass elevator that ran on the outside of the building. By the time it got to the 4th floor, I had my back tight against the wall. No matter how hard I tried to move closer to the glass, my back stayed glued to the wall.

For years, I never had another experience. Then I had a panic attack while driving in the mountains when the passenger side was a sheer cliff dropping hundreds of feet to the bottom of the valley. I kept going slower and slower and felt like the car was being pulled toward the cliff. As soon as the cliff was no longer visible, the panic went away.

A few years later, I was driving across a very steep bridge and the same thing happened. I had to slow down to 35 mph in a 55 mph zone to make it across the bridge. This only happens going up, not down. Flying doesn't bother me at all.

_The reason I mention this is I wonder if this is related to our internal clock and compass. Thoughts?_

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-27T22:06:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-time-is-it-and-where-am-i.6499/post-81988>

Huck

I used to be able to tell exactly where I was, and what direction I was facing... then I lost it when I was put on depakote - I totally lost my sense of direction. that has come back slowly - but my sense of time is still off.

your comment about heights - triggered a thought for me... in the past 8yrs I've developed motion sickness - I have to be driving - or I don't feel well at all. this started about 2003/2004 - I'm guessing its a side effect of EMF

I always do better when I'm in Idaho....

### tweety — 2013-03-27T21:54:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances.6500/post-81983>

Can we talk through some food intolerance issues? Like why new intolerances show up for seemingly no good reason?

I had been eating home-fermented sauerkraut for a couple of years. In the fall, we just ran out of room in the fridge and my family got tired of it... I stopped making it. So a few weeks ago I picked up a cabbage, made a new batch and was all excited to get going again. But every time I have had it, I get horrible stomach upset. Massive bloating, indigestion... I used to eat this stuff by the heaps. I have taken probiotics until recently when I ran out. I did buy a new one but stopped taking it when I realized it had dairy, which I cannot tolerate.

But all of these things were things I happily ate with no issues, until they found there way out of my daily diet for one reason or another. And now, when I try to add them again, I can't. Now almonds and other nuts are causing me trouble.

I know my hormones have gone from all different extremes... can that wreak havoc on gut flora? I know there's a connection somewhere, just don't remember which direction or if its both? And if so, then once my hormones get more balanced will these food inolerances potentially calm down?

### Joann — 2013-03-28T00:40:09+0200

I'm waited for my usual probiotic to arrive. hopefully it will help. The gut is the boss as far as I can see.

I seem to be more more sensitive to things ..... I need to eat very simply and mostly raw....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances.6500/post-82083>

Thats what I am afraid of- eating more simply means cutting more things... and I am not sure I can add them back in. I cannot convince my family to eat raw, and my husband seems to be increasingly more squeamish the more raw I eat my food! He used to be the one that accused me of overcooking my food, and now he is constantly taking things back to the kitchen to finish them. At least my kids are coming around. I can't stand an overcooked steak anymore!

The farther along I am in this journey ...... I am in a much different head space ..... and food is only a chance to load up on electrons/ brain specific nutrients.

I thoroughly enjoy it and don't get sick of it at all - not one bit and it just makes me glow and I feel so darn good!

Dr. Kruse has said many times - just concentrate on getting yourself well first and hopefully your family will follow along when they see the changes in you.
Maybe , for now, just make food that you need for you and add in extra things for your family the way they like them .... not ideal I know.

# Thread: After Weight-Loss Surgery, New Gut Bacteria Keep Obesity Away
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-03-28T14:04:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82040>

> A study of lab mice, published on Wednesday, begs to differ. It concludes that one of the most common and effective forms of bariatric surgery, called Roux-en-Y gastric bypass, melts away pounds not - or not only - by re-routing the digestive tract, as long thought, but by changing the bacteria in the gut.
>
> Or, in non-scientific terms, **the surgery somehow replaces fattening microbes with slimming ones.**

> The researchers then **transferred gut microbiota from the Roux-en-Y mice to microbe-free obese mice. Result: the recipient mice lost weight and fat - no surgery required**. Crucially, obese mice that received gut bugs from mice that had received sham Roux-en-Y, not the real thing, did not slim down.

> In a study published on Tuesday in the online edition of the Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, researchers at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center in Los Angeles report that **people whose breath has high concentrations of both hydrogen and methane gases are more likely to have a higher body mass index and higher percentage of body fat**.
>
> Methane is associated with bacteria called **Methanobrevibacter smithii, which in overabundance may cause weight gain by extracting calories from food super-efficiently**, Cedars' Ruchi Mathur, who led the study, said: "It could allow a person to harvest more calories from their food."
>
> **The breath test could provide a warning that someone is at risk of obesity because he harbors fattening microbiota.**

[http://www.reuters.com/article/2013/03/27/us-obesity-surgery-bacteria-idUSBRE92Q0ZQ20130327](http://www.reuters.com/article/2013/03/27/us-obesity-surgery-bacteria-idUSBRE92Q0ZQ20130327)

I found labs that do the methane (hydrogen) lab. The question is, how do I get rid of the fattening microbiota and get the skinny ones? And where do I get the skinny ones? (Fecal transplant is not at the top of my list of options, although, ...)

### Joann — 2013-03-28T23:49:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82090>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methanobrevibacter\_smithii](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Methanobrevibacter_smithii)

Interesting, M.smithii breaks down complex polysaccharides maybe there is a way to tweak without transplant?

### Glamazon — 2013-03-29T04:53:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82123>

Many RNY pts regain a significant amount of weight back after surgery. You might check the statistics. They go on to have more significant restrictive revisions. So who knows what the long term results are from these bacteria. Mice aren't people. Also people don't gain weight from calories. According to Dr. K obesity is a brain inflammation problem.

### Huck — 2013-03-29T14:07:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82150>

> [Glamazon said:](/goto/post?id=82123)
>
> Many RNY pts regain a significant amount of weight back after surgery. You might check the statistics. They go on to have more significant restrictive revisions. So who knows what the long term results are from these bacteria. Mice aren't people. Also people don't gain weight from calories. According to Dr. K obesity is a brain inflammation problem.

I think he said that if they are leptin sensitive, they don't gain weight from calories.

And in EMF-4, he says

> ...you can't access the fat burning pathway when your molecular sense of timing is off. When your molecular clock is off you can never benefit from true fat burning.

I think the point is we need to do everything we can to loose weight until (if) we "turn on" the Pentose Phosphate Pathway (PPP).

### shilohman — 2013-03-29T16:54:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82167>

Eat a high fat, medium protein and very, very low carb diet. Make sure that your carbs are fermented. Also, drink the juices from your fermented carbs and drink kombucha. If your gut flora is so badly messed up your only solution may be a fecal transplant, but I would try the above first.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-30T05:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82245>

Dr. K says epipaleo/very low carb diet greatly simplifies the gut microbiome so this way of eating may be the answer for getting rid of the aforementioned methane bacteria. Wouldn't complex polysaccharides be found in a diet high in plants, legumes, and starches?

### Joann — 2013-03-30T07:25:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-weight-loss-surgery-new-gut-bacteria-keep-obesity-away.6509/post-82250>

# Thread: 15lb baby born -  fetal macrosomia continues to rise

**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-03-28T14:45:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15lb-baby-born-fetal-macrosomia-continues-to-rise.6510/post-82043>

[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/...d=maing-grid7|main5|dl1|sec3\_lnk2&pLid=290254](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/03/27/baby-george-king_n_2967517.html?icid=maing-grid7%7Cmain5%7Cdl1%7Csec3_lnk2%26pLid%3D290254)

"Dr. Shieva Ghofrany, an OBGYN with Stamford Hospital told The Huffington Post that fetal macrosomia -- defined as a birth weight of more than 8.8 pounds -â€“ can be directly attributed to maternal obesity and diabetes. But she clarified that those are not the only factors, pointing out that in the last five to six years, "babies have definitely been coming out bigger." Other factors contributing to rise of heavy newborns could be similar to the reasons posited for why girls are going through puberty earlier -â€“ contaminants in food or hormones in meats, for example."

Looks like they maybe missing the impact of EMF on birth. I wonder if other mammals - particularly those in captivity are seeing an increase in birth weights of their offspring? I know this year there have been some pretty big foals born in the throughbred -horse racing community....

### Hope — 2013-03-29T20:48:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/15lb-baby-born-fetal-macrosomia-continues-to-rise.6510/post-82184>

Yeah...I wonder how many years it will take for 'them' to get the impact of EMF. Hope we'll see it in our lifetime....

In a way, it's amazing not more people are having issues getting pregnant....but I wonder how the kids of those born today will do.....

# Thread: RO water sources
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-sources.6526/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2013-03-29T15:30:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ro-water-sources.6526/post-82160>

I have seen people asking where they can get RO water. Pepsi makes RO water called Aquafina. I once had a tour of the local pepsi plant and part of the tour was showing and boasting how Pepsi took city water and put it through the reverse osmosis process. The claim is their water is better than city water. Huge room dedicated to that process. Coke makes Dasani which is also RO.

I think you just have to be a label reader like you do with food to find out what is RO.

# Thread: EMF Webinar Series - sign up by April 1!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-series-sign-up-by-april-1.6531/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Team Kruse — 2013-03-29T20:25:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-webinar-series-sign-up-by-april-1.6531/post-82179>

Our first webinar is being recorded TODAY, and will be sent to members who sign up for the EMF Bootcamp on Monday, April 1!

This first webinar is going to review the Top 11 Areas for Reducing EMF:

Wifi
Cordless Phones
Cell Phones
Bedroom
Office
Kitchen
Lights / electrical wiring in house
TV Room / Gaming
Autos
Grounding
Meters & Testing

Michael Neuert (mechanical engineer and EMF expert from the EMF Center in Santa Rosa, CA) is going to give you the low-down on what you can do RIGHT NOW to begin reducing your EMF exposure, even before you buy a meter and start measuring.

Our first live call will be a Q&A on Thursday, April 4 at 7 pm CST. You must review the webinar before the call so that your questions do not repeat content already delivered in the webinar. We will have up to 90 minutes with Michael to answer your questions.

Then, we will have four more webinars, all on Thursdays at 7 pm CST:

April 11: Magnetic Fields (Power lines, wiring)
April 18: Low Frequency electric fields (electricity, body voltage)
Apri 25: Radiowaves (cell phones, towers)
May 2: Rewiring and new constructions, faraday cages, Final Q&A

For each of these webinars, we are going to drill into the ins-and-outs of EMF in all its forms, how to test for it, what to do with high readings, and so much more. This webinar series is going to offer you a MASSIVE education on EMFs! The weekly webinars will include lots of Q&A to help us unpack the different kinds of fields we'll encounter and what to do about them.

We will provide access to a recording of the calls after the series is over to everyone who signs up.

PS - Click [here](https://jackkruse.myquickcheckout.com/EMF-bronze) to get registered for the EMF Bootcamp!

# Thread: Sunflower Greens and Bacon
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-30T05:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/post-82247>

Anybody tried growing sunflower greens? Can sunflower greens be steamed or should they be eaten raw?
Apparently safflower greens can be grown the same way.

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FFebyipOhTQ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FFebyipOhTQ)

How about the Sunday Bacon by Applegate Farms? Supposed to have no hormones/antibiotics and no artificial ingredients or preservatives. The product locator says Super 1 Foods and Brookshires carries their products. Gonna look tomorrow for the bacon.

### janagram — 2013-03-30T07:57:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/post-82256>

that bacon is good....we like
pederson bacon also.

### Inger — 2013-03-30T08:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/post-82258>

I think it is not too hard, you just need to make sure the seeds are raw or they will not sprout. You need to soak them first too. I think I made them once many years ago. I always ate the sprouts raw, when I got some. They are delish and crispy!
I never cook anything if not necessary but I think you know that. I am quite sure they lose in taste when cooked. I have not had some for years tho... I prefer wild greens now. Way easier and cheaper.. Greens needs sunlight to get really healthy so if you sprout some try to put them out in the sun/daylight after they have sprouted, to grow and get green. I bet it makes them healthier!

### endless — 2013-03-30T08:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/post-82260>

Use them raw...they are great in salads!

### Jonathin — 2013-03-30T20:40:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/post-82311>

I've grown sunflower greens for years during the winter. Soak the unshelled seeds overnight then grow them on about an inch of composted soil on some sort of tray or dish. Keep them out of direct light until the cotyledons are somewhat open then give them a day or two of sunshine. Harvest and store in ziplock bag. Tasty as a finger food with a hardy meal.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-31T08:46:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunflower-greens-and-bacon.6542/post-82339>

Thanks for the tips, everybody.

# Thread: U.S. to reexamine health risks of cellphone radiation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/u-s-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation.6544/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-30T15:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/u-s-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation.6544/post-82275>

[http://vitals.nbcnews.com/\_news/201...reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation](http://vitals.nbcnews.com/_news/2013/03/29/17520884-us-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation)

U.S. regulators are looking into how radio frequencies emitted by cellphones and other wireless devices affect people amid lingering concerns about the risks of cellphone radiation.

The Federal Communications Commission said on Friday it is seeking comment from other agencies and health experts on whether it should update its standards limiting exposure to phones' electromagnetic fields, as they apply to children in particular.

The FCC last reviewed those standards in 1996, before the ubiquitous use of mobile devices. But the agency's officials say they have no reason to believe the current standards are inadequate and called the proceeding, which was announced in documents posted online on Friday, a routine review.

Scientists have been unable to determine whether radio waves emitted by mobile devices pose threats to the brain or other parts of the human body but studies continue as the number of mobile devices Americans own, already in the hundreds of millions, continues to grow.

### Rachelle — 2013-03-30T15:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/u-s-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation.6544/post-82276>

Whatever happens it will be a cost to the consumer. They made the problem but we will pay.

### Dali Dula — 2013-03-30T16:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/u-s-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation.6544/post-82279>

Whatever happens it will be a cost to the consumer. They made the problem but we will pay.

They did not create the problem. They created potentially harmful technology. WE bought it because we love toys and convenience.( I include myself in the WE.)
i'm not defending govt or the corporations, but it is us the consumer that creates these problems. the consumer has all the power. Consume or don't. The choice is yours. disconnect your wi fi. You pay for it every month. Throw away your cell phone you pay for it every month. Consumers can control markets by consuming or not. Is it hard? yes. Is it inconvenient? yes. If enough people stop paying for stuff you will quickly get the attention of the producers and the regulators. If the consumer effectively demands safe products the magic market will provide. Is this practical for the average person? No, but I have found AI disease to not be practical. Unfortunately , Buyer beware, is really the way to be. Due diligence sucks, but it works.

### Hope — 2013-03-30T16:57:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/u-s-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation.6544/post-82285>

I am not sure when the government will be willing to say 'this' is bad......at least in part because of the lobby of all the companies benefitting from all this. Just like what happened with Monsanto this week and GMO labeling. Just look at this sentence:

Scientists have been unable to determine whether radio waves emitted by mobile devices pose threats to the brain or other parts of the human body but studies continue as the number of mobile devices Americans own, already in the hundreds of millions, continues to grow.

Really?????? I think not! There is a lot of evidence out there. I guess there is no longer term study because it's all so new, in some ways. But all I have learned from Dr K is enough to have me totally convinced. I was sort of convinced before but I didn't fully get it (and still don't), but I am not waiting for anyone anymore......

As far as consumerism causing all this - of course. People, forgive me, are stupid. I was too. Unless people are or get really sick, they won't want to change. If you're sick, you're willing to look deeper. If not, it's SO easy to declare us believers crazy.....people really have to get it and there is very little real education....this place here excepted. It stinks but I know time will bear out the truth......

### Huck — 2013-03-30T17:49:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/u-s-to-reexamine-health-risks-of-cellphone-radiation.6544/post-82295>

> [Dali Dula said:](/goto/post?id=82279)
>
> Whatever happens it will be a cost to the consumer. They made the problem but we will pay.
>
> They did not create the problem. They created potentially harmful technology. WE bought it because we love toys and convenience.( I include myself in the WE.)
> i'm not defending govt or the corporations, but it is us the consumer that creates these problems. the consumer has all the power. Consume or don't. The choice is yours. disconnect your wi fi. You pay for it every month. Throw away your cell phone you pay for it every month. Consumers can control markets by consuming or not. Is it hard? yes. Is it inconvenient? yes. If enough people stop paying for stuff you will quickly get the attention of the producers and the regulators. If the consumer effectively demands safe products the magic market will provide. Is this practical for the average person? No, but I have found AI disease to not be practical. Unfortunately , Buyer beware, is really the way to be. Due diligence sucks, but it works.

Once the government took on the role of regulator, they also took on the role of safety. Unfortunately, the government can usually be counted on to get safety wrong and focus on regulation.

I take Citrate mainly, and the advice on the bottle says to take preferably with a meal.

But reading one of the reviews here:

[http://www.amazon.co.uk/The-Magnesium-Miracle-Carolyn-Dean/dp/034549458X/ref=tag\_dpp\_lp\_edpp\_ttl\_in](http://www.amazon.co.uk/The-Magnesium-Miracle-Carolyn-Dean/dp/034549458X/ref=tag_dpp_lp_edpp_ttl_in)

The author recommends taken Magnesium between meals as you need the stomach acid to help absorb it.

Many thanks

# Thread: Live on Saturday night, with my Trifield meter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-on-saturday-night-with-my-trifield-meter.6551/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-03-31T02:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-on-saturday-night-with-my-trifield-meter.6551/post-82323>

So my Trifield meter came. Couldn't wait to do some readings......turns out:

in the magnetic field 0-3 sensitivity, worst offenders are

\- when turned on, the area in front of the dashboard in the car is off the charts (good thing I don't sit there, but wow)
\- laptop on top of keyboard and in front of screen is off charts, gets better with more distance
\- the Dell laptop we have, the box that's part of the cable also jumps off the charts when you get close to it
\- my 2002 small boom box with radio in it when just plugged in is off the charts.

In other observations, I have noticed that there is always a 1.1 or 1.2 reading, at least, wherever I am in the house and even in the backyard. In the basement area, there are places where it goes up from that a bit, though I am not near actual appliances. Too weird.

I was surprised to see that our refrigerator (not energy smart, it's older) has the same reading as the bedroom night table lamp. Huh.

In the electric range, the cellphone (iPhone 3GS) goes off the charts when turned on the 3G network, not even wifi. Ugh.

But here is the most amazing (in a bad way) thing......when I test the laptop with the cable to charge it IN, it jumps off the electric range chart. When I unplug the laptop from it, it goes down to nearly zero (I bet because this meter isn't sensitive enough). So when I read somewhere it's a good idea to only use the laptop with the charger OUT, they weren't kidding.

So from now on, no using the laptop with the cable IN.

Can't wait for the webinars.

### Inger — 2013-03-31T08:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-on-saturday-night-with-my-trifield-meter.6551/post-82334>

Intresting!
Hope, we have 0,2 / close to zero almost everywhere in the house not close to any electrical stuff. In the basement too. That is too weird about your basement!
Our modern espresso / coffee brewer that makes fresh coffee from whole beans is totally bad.. even if only plugged in and switched off! How good I stopped using it a year ago because I thought there might be a mold issue (I was reading about it in a newspaper actually). Now I have a simple "french press coffee brewer" without electricity. Loving it. I unplug so much stuff today it is funny..

### Hope — 2013-03-31T22:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/live-on-saturday-night-with-my-trifield-meter.6551/post-82371>

Hi Inger,

oh, that is so so awesome!!!!! So you and your family are in good shape there. I read that in the instructions, that's a good level if you're rural. I wanna be too!!

And way to go for switching coffee brewers

I retested the basement and still the same thing.

I cannot get a reading anywhere below 0.8 (Sometimes, not every time) and thankfully that is above the bed. Not even outside!! I know we have to move.....our landlord will likely sell this year anywhere since the economy is getting better and I'm OK with that. Just a question of how far out I can convince DH to move to.

It's 100% clear to me that.....we must do all we can to reduce EMF. I can't wait to be able to do just that.

I have decided that starting today, I must sit outside with bare feet on the grass for 30 minutes every single day, first thing in the morning, or do some weeding in bare feet - no matter the temperature outside. It's just a necessity. It will be like a little experiment.

I like it so far, but wish it were more sensitive. I am not sure about cell towers - there are electric fields, magnetic and then RF as well (with RF being the least sensitive)......but I know radiation from power lines shows up, I tested the magnetic field so far.

### beach bunny — 2013-03-31T06:08:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-from-heavy-metals-in-seafood.6552/post-82330>

Hi guys,

According to both my Hair Analyses and DMPS Challenge I have very high Heavy Metal toxicity in my body (Mercury, Aluminum, etc, even Uranium yikes!).
The whole thing scares me to be honest.

I'm wondering if the body is not detoxifying the metals (like mine) what is the **best/safest** way to help **detoxify/eliminate** them... especially since:

1\. I want to continue to enjoy the vast benefits seafood adds to my healthy diet.
2\. I do not want the heavy metals issue to get more out of hand or cause degenerative conditions.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-03-31T08:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-from-heavy-metals-in-seafood.6552/post-82336>

I was under the impression that seaweeds contain compounds which help with heavy metals and radioactive toxins. The phycocolloids in the kelp trap metallic ions and they pass out of the body through the bowel. You may find the following helpful:
vitamin C, lipoic acid, methylcobalamin (Methyl B-12), B-6, magnesium, selenium, melatonin, methionine, glutathione, turmeric, milk thistle.

Glutathione is synthesized from three amino acids - cystein, glutamate and glycine. In wikipedia it states "Once oxidized, glutathione can be reduced back by glutathione reductase, using NADPH as an electron donor."

### Inger — 2013-03-31T08:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-from-heavy-metals-in-seafood.6552/post-82337>

Tanning.
I read in my book about the sun, tanning helps detoxifying heavy metals too! Amazing. I would tan as much nude as I could. You need to tan the whole body.

Eat coriander, put it on your food, goes great with fish! Or eat it fresh! It tastes great!

Maybe take some edible clay? Every morning in a glass of clean water, fasted, right when you wake up. Clay helps to detoxify too, in a very safe way. I love French edible clay.. tastes like creamy coffee, almost! You need to stir it with a wooden spoon, no metal!

I would stop eating any canned food.. and try to choose the seafoods with less contamination. You can read it up I am sure. There are seafood that is worse than others.

I am sure you already drink lots and lots of good water...

### paleo-bunny — 2013-03-31T23:50:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/detoxing-from-heavy-metals-in-seafood.6552/post-82389>

Fresh coriander (cliantro) as indicated above. Also drink green tea in between meals, i.e. not within 3 hours of consuming a meal, because as well as helping eliminate mercury, it inhibits absorption of dietary magnesium and iron.

Avoid high histamine and histamine-promoting foods that exacerbate under-methylatin issues. e.g. hard cheeses, cured meats, smoked products, tomato, citrus, spinach, red wine, and fermented products in general.

I found freshly cooked Wild alaskan salmon to be the optimal fish re: low mercury, low histamine, micronutrient content and high omega 3. That's pretty much the only fish I ate for about a year, along with occasional fresh trout, while detoxing mercury and getting my histamine levels under control.

Now I can enjoy eating a wider variety of fish and seafood inc.shrimps, scallops, mussels and smoked mackerel. Am still wary of tuna re: mercury but can eat in small amounts.

Also be aware that body fat stores are used as a dumping ground for mercury and other heavy metals in addition to other toxins ... so when you lose fat quickly, i.e. more than 2 lbs a month, those heavy metal poisoning symptoms that you'd hoped you'd eliminated for good may well reappear.

I had to detox after getting my mercury amalgam fillings replaced and having been a tuna addict. I'm sure that the fillings were the biggest culprit.

Fascinated by the tanning recommendation... another facet to sunshine being good for me

### Tanager — 2013-08-07T23:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/juil-grounding-sandals-50-discount-code.6557/post-91757>

I've been looking for grounding shoes. I think Juil shoes are beautiful, but I could never go walking in slip-on shoes -- they just don't stay on my feet.

I was looking for some grounding shoes (too many dogs in my neighborhood to go barefoot), and I found this link to Soft Star shoes. You can get leather soles that work for grounding. They've been tested for grounding and for running by a barefoot runner-blogger, and there are youtube videos of them. They are handmade to order, so you can also get different widths. By the way, I'm not associated with this company, but I am from Oregon where they are located!

Here's the link to their earthing shoes page: [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/a...&utm\_content=artTest\_A2&utm\_campaign=20130807](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2099/12/31/superbugs-antibiotic-overuse.aspx?e_cid=20130807_DNL_artTest_A2&utm_source=dnl&utm_medium=email&utm_content=artTest_A2&utm_campaign=20130807)

Here's a video: [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0uKwd6ewDcc](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0uKwd6ewDcc)

I'm thinking about ordering some. For now, though, I think I'll stick with my Hopi boot mocassins with rawhide soles I picked up on Arizona.

These "Teepee Boot Moccasins" also look interesting -- don't know if the "cushion insole" is removable, but they look comfy and very attractive. Too small for me, though. [http://www.indianvillagemall.com/footskin/teepeeboots.html](http://www.indianvillagemall.com/footskin/teepeeboots.html)

# Thread: Drier skin from CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drier-skin-from-ct.6560/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-03-31T23:41:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drier-skin-from-ct.6560/post-82388>

So I have been doing more CT and also taking more cold showers....and it appears that although my hands were fine all winter, all of a sudden I am getting drier skin on hands and face. Could it be from more water exposure, cooler? May be a silly thing, but I wonder....

Can't wait to get my labs back....to see what else may be changing?

### shilohman — 2013-04-01T02:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drier-skin-from-ct.6560/post-82396>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=82388)
>
> So I have been doing more CT and also taking more cold showers....and it appears that although my hands were fine all winter, all of a sudden I am getting drier skin on hands and face. Could it be from more water exposure, cooler? May be a silly thing, but I wonder....
>
> Can't wait to get my labs back....to see what else may be changing?

From Dr. K:
1) It means your D level may not be good and your B12 could be low too. Also, omega 6/3 may be high.
2) Arame is often associated with soft wrinkle-free skin turgor and tends to enhances hair growth and make your hair have an incredible sheen. The reason is because this veggie increase your free T3 to a dramatic degree when it is used regularly in your diet.
3) DHEA can stimulate pro-collagen production, and may have beneficial effects on aging skin.

### Hope — 2013-04-01T03:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drier-skin-from-ct.6560/post-82404>

Thanks, Shilohman! Fascinating bc my Vit D was 89 just three weeks ago. B12 could be it though. Oh, and I did eat some almond butter lately...and I am on DHEA. Hmmm. Thanks a ton!

So glad we have Dr K subs here for when he disappears

### Inger — 2013-04-01T07:34:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drier-skin-from-ct.6560/post-82428>

If you have hard water it might dry the skin out... at least it does it to me. When / if I shower warm (and we have very hard well water) I practically never use soap and I still get dry skin.. but not if I only dip in the river. That has very soft water though. But the shower does it to me. I then use lots of EVOO after the shower on my still wet skin and it helps. Coconut oil is great too!

### Hope — 2013-04-01T16:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/drier-skin-from-ct.6560/post-82455>

Oh Inger that must be part of my issue. Thanks so much!!!!! I am sure we have hard water....I can't wait to get to swim in the ocean.....

# Thread: Good Brands of RO Water??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-brands-of-ro-water.6561/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-04-01T00:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-brands-of-ro-water.6561/post-82390>

Has anyone found a good brand of RO water that does not have fluoride? My local store only carries Nestle, but since it starts with city water I'm concerned it's still going to contain fluoride.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-01T03:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-brands-of-ro-water.6561/post-82400>

Good question. I think most of the RO water available will be from city water. RO is supposed to be able to remove the fluoride. But I think Dr. K. has mentioned other things in city water that need to be avoided. I'm not sure RO will remove them.

That said, most of the water I drink is "purified" by RO. I drink some bottled spring water daily, but the rest of it is RO with minerals added back in.

Also, it's difficult to find about fluoride levels. I think that advertising "no added fluoride" might put a target on a brand.

### Hope — 2013-04-01T03:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-brands-of-ro-water.6561/post-82402>

I am not sure if I remember right, but didn't Dr K mention BJ's has some good RO water?

### beach bunny — 2013-04-01T05:34:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-brands-of-ro-water.6561/post-82418>

I have had an RO filter system put in so that all I have to worry about is changing the filter every 6 months. It's the only water I drink and cook with. When I had the water tested it was fantastic. Maybe if you check around for local companies it may be worth the investment.

# Thread: Finding a tanning booth?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-04-01T03:57:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82409>

Anyone know how to find a good tanning booth? Maybe go in with a Trifield and see what happens? I am tempted to try this...I don't think I could ever tan nude where I live....we are in a TH development with no privacy.

### endless — 2013-04-01T04:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82414>

Dr. K said that testing them is really the only way to know, so yes, I would take the Trifield in and test....obviously making sure they use electronic ballasts and not magnetic would be the first step.

### Inger — 2013-04-01T07:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82422>

Look for one with high UVB. You want to have it as close to the sun as it gets and the high UVA's are not...
Do at lowest power always - never more. Do longer instead.
And yes, test with a meter if high EMF too.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-01T15:17:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82445>

I agree w/ Inger on looking for UVB tanning beds. The ironic thing is they are considered "base" models, and thus have the least expensive sessions.

### Hope — 2013-04-01T16:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82454>

OK guys, will start calling around for that this week!!!!!!!! And definitely bring the TF.

Thanks so much for the great input!

### Hope — 2013-04-01T19:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82486>

So I called a local place and they said exactly what you predicted, ATL paleo. I am tempted to head over there asap!!

### Hope — 2013-04-01T19:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82488>

One more question....what would be a good way to start? Like how much would be a good amount. I realize n=1 but....I tend to overdo things, LOL. Also, for those of you who use tanning beds, how long do you before/after you CT? Do you do it every day? Every other?

My Vitamin D is currently 89 but I used to always burn fast in the past. Reddish hair, fair skin, the works.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-01T19:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82490>

On a base bed, 20 minutes is the max. Your Vit D is very good, so tanning bed should be okay. Ask your place ... given your skin type and being a newbie, I bet they suggest 8 or 10 minutes the first time. Monitor your results. Then you can gradually increase by several minutes upto the max in several weeks .... provided your n=1 allows it.

I use the tanning bed about once a week for 20 minutes ... but I get natural sunlight 3 to 5 times / week; weather and schedule permitting.

### Hope — 2013-04-01T20:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82496>

Thanks a ton, ATL Paleo!!! How much natural light do you get on the days you do? An hour? And how much body do you expose?

Can't wait to try this and see what happens. My mom always complains "you're so pale", and my DH loves my white skin LOL but it's all about the health.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-01T22:10:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82510>

Hope, I normally get 20 to 30 minutes per day. That gets my skin a bit pink, but I don't burn. Swim suit is required in my big city suburb.

### Hope — 2013-04-01T22:27:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-tanning-booth.6565/post-82512>

OK great...awesome!! Thanks

# Thread: Latest labs . . . looking for input :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-labs-looking-for-input.6571/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4

### BJK77 — 2013-04-01T17:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-labs-looking-for-input.6571/post-82467>

Iâ€™d love to get some input on my latest test results. These were done on what should have been day 20 of my cycle. I ended up only spotting a very tiny amount on what should have been days 1 and 2 of my new cycle and then absolutely nothing.

I had these tests done mid-afternoon, so I wasnâ€™t fasting. For all my previous tests Iâ€™ve always fasted for everything . . . Iâ€™m wondering if that alone could have made a difference at all. Just for reference, at the time of the test I was taking:
5 mcg T3
1 gr Naturethroid
200 mg Compounded Progesterone (I did start 100 mg Prometrium vaginally 3 days before testing, but I donâ€™t think that would have really impacted the results yet, right?)
65 mg DHEA (Iâ€™m now decreasing that to 50 mg since my testosterone was high and Iâ€™m assuming the DHEA is converting to T; thinking about switching to 7-keto??)
25 mg Pregnenolone (Iâ€™m now increasing to 50 mg)
12,000 IU Vitamin D (going to increase this to 50,000 IU/day and hit tanning hard)

Current - 3/18 in RED
Previous - 12/28 in BLUE
Range in BLACK

**TSH**
NOW: 3.03
PREVIOUS: 2.04
0.5 - 5.0

**Free T3**
NOW: 3.7
PREVIOUS: 3
2.3 - 4.2

**Free T4**
NOW: 0.5 low
PREVIOUS: 0.4 low
.6 - 1.6

**RT3**
NOW: 8
PREVIOUS: 14
7 - 24

**RT3 Ratio**
NOW: 46.3
PREVIOUS: 21.4
20+

**DHEA**
NOW: 595
PREVIOUS: 226
35 - 430

**Pregnenolone**
NOW: 24
PREVIOUS: 54
7 - 188

**Estradiol**
NOW: 63.2
PREVIOUS: 331
35 - 400

**Progesterone**
NOW: 16.9
PREVIOUS: 2.69
5.16 - 18.56

**PG:E2**
NOW: 231
PREVIOUS: 7

**Total Testosterone**
NOW: 59
PREVIOUS: 52
< 73

**Free Testosterone**
NOW: 9.4 high
PREVIOUS: 6.2
1 - 8.5

Vitamin D
NOW: 55
PREVIOUS: 44

Hereâ€™s the strange thing â€“ I see a lot of improvements in my labs (FT3, RT3, DHEA, PG:E2) and I had felt better in many ways too, but my sleep started royally sucking a few days after I had these labs done and it still sucks. On what should have been day 1 of my cycle, I also found my mouth started to feel constantly dry and parched even though I drink close to a gallon of water/day. We started sleeping on a 10 gauss Magnetico pad a week before my sleep tanked . . . could it have taken that long for a â€œdetoxâ€ effect to kick in and I need to just ride this out?

Thoughts on decreasing DHEA or switching to 7-keto to see if it lowers my free T while maintaining my DHEA? Or maybe just increasing my pregnenolone will help maintain DHEA while decreasing free T?
Iâ€™m going to get a lipid panel, glucose, CMP and hsCRP done soon . . . I was just hoping to have a good nightâ€™s sleep for it first and that hasnâ€™t happened for a good two weeks now 

### Hope — 2013-04-01T19:36:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-labs-looking-for-input.6571/post-82484>

BJK, how much tanning have you done so far? I would love to hear....and way to go on the improvements...

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-01T20:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-labs-looking-for-input.6571/post-82494>

Good news you've cleared any RT3 issues you may have had. your Free T4 is really low, which explains your High TSH - I think you probably need to increase your Naturethroid & drop the T3.

DHEA is great - I think you want to keep it there - especially if your in a HIGH EMF environment so I wouldn't worry about changing the DHEA to 7Keto - just keep an eye on it. Your Testosterone is pretty good, not too high - remember you need T - to help you shred body fat. you definitely want to bring your pregnenonlone up. your Pg:E2 ratio is 231.6 better than most here on the forum -

The thirst could be related to low Pg - as well as bad sleep - I've found I need more Pg at the beginning/end of my cycle that most doc's think... I should have.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-01T23:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/latest-labs-looking-for-input.6571/post-82519>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=82484)
>
> BJK, how much tanning have you done so far? I would love to hear....and way to go on the improvements...

Hi Hope,

I started tanning about 3 weeks before I had these labs done and I was going 3 times a week. I started out at only 7 minutes per session and increased to 12.

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=82494)
>
> Good news you've cleared any RT3 issues you may have had. your Free T4 is really low, which explains your High TSH - I think you probably need to increase your Naturethroid & drop the T3.
>
> DHEA is great - I think you want to keep it there - especially if your in a HIGH EMF environment so I wouldn't worry about changing the DHEA to 7Keto - just keep an eye on it. Your Testosterone is pretty good, not too high - remember you need T - to help you shred body fat. you definitely want to bring your pregnenonlone up. your Pg:E2 ratio is 231.6 better than most here on the forum -
>
> The thirst could be related to low Pg - as well as bad sleep - I've found I need more Pg at the beginning/end of my cycle that most doc's think... I should have.

Thanks Gretchen! I was wondering about low Pg leading to the dry mouth. The thing is I'm now at what should be day 10 of my cycle - no sign of it though - and my mouth feels like sandpaper - rough and dry. I asked about it in the last Q&A and Jack even mentioned it could be high T, which is why I was thinking I needed to cut back a little on the DHEA since it's a bit over the range in my labs. You're right though - I definitely don't want to lose the improvements I've made in my DHEA . . . hmmmm.

Back to the Pg though, maybe I'll try increasing to 300 mg oral tonight to see if that helps the sleep and thirst. I'm running out of my prescription faster than I should since I'm already doubling what my current doc will give me though. I'm terrified of what I'll do when I can't keep squeezing more out of him. I do see a new doc tomorrow, so fingers crossed he'll see things my way and won't be so scared of high dose Pg!!

I was thrilled with my PG:E2 ratio since it was 7 in December. I never expected it to jump that much, but I'll certainly take it

Have you heard the news?

Jack will not be doing consults after **_April 21st_**. This means that you want to be sure to book any outstanding consultations, **right away**!

Lost the link to book your consult? No problem - go to the member dashboard to have the link emailed to you: [http://www.jackkruse.com/member-dashboard/](http://www.jackkruse.com/member-dashboard/)

I have a disc bulge (c6/7) (dx'ed by neurosurgeon) and first had weakness, numbness/tingling starting Oct 2012, but it has improved significantly by itself. I saw a neurosurgeon in March 2013 and he said to take it easy, never flex my neck, use cervical traction, etc. i'm doing better, but my arm is still weak (it's about 80% back) and some dullness at tip of index finger...feel like it's getting better, but slowly. any suggestions on what to do to cure myself of disc bulge/DDD/straight neck and prevent this from happening again? thank you so much!

# Thread: How to replace wireless cable modem with wired cable modem
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-replace-wireless-cable-modem-with-wired-cable-modem.6588/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shilohman — 2013-04-02T17:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-replace-wireless-cable-modem-with-wired-cable-modem.6588/post-82608>

The only cable modem that you can get from the cable company where I live (time warner) is a WiFi modem.
What I was:
1) Go on Amazon and search for 'cable modem' you will find several. Get name and model numbers of several. You only want
one that has DOCSIS 3.0. You will also want to buy one that has several ethernet ports, otherwise if the one you buy has
only one ethernet port, then you will have to buy in addition a wired ethernet switch. Also, make sure it supports is 1 Gig
speed (which all should but make sure). You will also need some ethernet cables for each printer/computer/laptop you will
hook up, buy CAT-6 cables.
2) Call your cable tech support and ask them if they support your first choice modem. If not, then give them you second
choice.
3) Buy the cable modem and when you get it, disconnect the old modem and plug your new one in.
4) Call your cable tech support and tell the make and model of the modem you have. They will configure
everything in the modem for you from their end.
5) Turn the old modem into your cable company and you will get your rental fee knocked off of your monthly
bill.

An added bonus that you may get, depending on how your cable provider does it, is that you may get an instant upgrade in speed at no extra cost.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-02T17:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-replace-wireless-cable-modem-with-wired-cable-modem.6588/post-82610>

I've always just bought a hardline cable modum - then bought a wireless router - which was retired about a month ago. we now have a multiple hard line router and and I'm looking at options for running drops around the entire house.... wondering if we could do it ourselves - or if I'd have to have a professional come in and drop all the lines...

I think I've spent in total - maybe $45 getting everything hardlined... still have to teach dh how to bring the internet up on his system via the hard line LAN...

### cinnamon — 2013-04-02T18:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-replace-wireless-cable-modem-with-wired-cable-modem.6588/post-82620>

I was watching this video about why wi-fi should not be in schools:
[http://youtu.be/6v75sKAUFdc?t=21m56s](http://youtu.be/6v75sKAUFdc?t=21m56s)

The video is done by Dr. Magda Havas. She's a university professor and is an anti-EMF expert here in Canada. In the above link, the video starts at 21:56 so you can see what she gives as wired alternatives. (The whole video is excellent, though, so you might want to watch from the start as well).

She suggests using "Powerline Adapters". These allow you to use your existing electrical wiring to extend your wired network. Here's the D-Link version, for example:
[http://www.dlink.com/ca/en/home-solutions/connect/powerline](http://www.dlink.com/ca/en/home-solutions/connect/powerline)

I had never heard of this and I wonder if it might be a good solution. Dr. Havas is on board with mitigating EMF and she appears to takes a scientific approach. I find it hard to believe she would recommend this if it wasn't a decent way to go.

Has anyone tried this?

### shilohman — 2013-04-03T18:28:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-replace-wireless-cable-modem-with-wired-cable-modem.6588/post-82712>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=82620)
>
> I was watching this video about why wi-fi should not be in schools:
> [http://youtu.be/6v75sKAUFdc?t=21m56s](http://youtu.be/6v75sKAUFdc?t=21m56s)
>
> The video is done by Dr. Magda Havas. She's a university professor and is an anti-EMF expert here in Canada. In the above link, the video starts at 21:56 so you can see what she gives as wired alternatives. (The whole video is excellent, though, so you might want to watch from the start as well).
>
> She suggests using "Powerline Adapters". These allow you to use your existing electrical wiring to extend your wired network. Here's the D-Link version, for example:
> [http://www.dlink.com/ca/en/home-solutions/connect/powerline](http://www.dlink.com/ca/en/home-solutions/connect/powerline)
>
> I had never heard of this and I wonder if it might be a good solution. Dr. Havas is on board with mitigating EMF and she appears to takes a scientific approach. I find it hard to believe she would recommend this if it wasn't a decent way to go.
>
> Has anyone tried this?

Haven't tried it, but I have installed a security system in my mom's house which works on the house wiring. It works, but it has problems, like false alarms. The one downside to using this for internet access is you are limited to the speed that it works with. Also, from what I understand this would create an electromagnetic field on the house wiring which would emit EMF from the house wiring, but compared to the EMF emitted by wireless routers it would be a great lesser of two evils.

# Thread: Got magnetico - advice?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-04-03T02:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-magnetico-advice.6595/post-82660>

So we got the magnetico today. DH asked three times do I really want this? Ha.

Question is can one guy and a woman get all the pads upstairs?? No idea.....

How did you guys do it - more than 2 people if you got the super?

### shilohman — 2013-04-03T03:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-magnetico-advice.6595/post-82663>

Pick up one of the boxes so it is standing on one end, then walk it over to the stairs, lay it down and the stairs, get a long piece of rope/strapping - the goal here is to push/pull the box up the stairs with the rope/strap around the end of the box with one pulling both
ends of the rope/strap while the other pushes the lower end. Or get a neighbor to help carry it up.

The hardest thing was lining up the pads...oh my gosh!!!!! but we did it. hope we did it right. can't wait to see if i can get better.....

### caroline — 2013-04-03T12:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-seasons.6597/post-82686>

Does anyone have any suggestions for managing changing seasons? I am really crashing going into fall here in Oz. My sleep is just terrible and consequently energy levels are dropping. I was so cold today and hungry all day as well.
I have never noticed this so acutely before - i suppose because I have felt so great all summer. Maybe this tells me i have to move to north Queensland! I have never realised I loved the hot summer so much before....maybe this is my n=1 ....
Maybe i have to think about getting a T3 med - cytomel. I think Dr. K. was thinking I had a shot at managing it and reducing my inflammation without it but my rt3 hasn't come down much.

I understand that the light cycles are yoked but if I am avoiding artificial light.... but I guess it is all about EMF exposure in my case anyway.

Does taking cytomel help with sleep issues? My sleep had been so good ......now it is definitely crap and I have to go to the bathroom multiple times during the night. I don't understand how that is connected or is that a separate issue?

Did anyone hear the answer to my question on the Q&A on this topic?
That is the first time I have ever asked a question and couldn't hear the answer - my reception was terrible for that answer only! How does that happen??

Does anyone notice changes going into spring?

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-03T15:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-seasons.6597/post-82695>

This past fall - I crashed hard - about 3weeks after the 1st of Sept. migraines, got worse. discovered in the Fall my Thryoid drops like a stone, as does my Pg.

for me - as thryoid levels fall, so do steroid hormones fall as well. which makes sense - as you need Vitamin A and T3 to covert LDL to pregnenolone and the remaining hormone cascade.

figuring out how to manage your hormones during the winter, when your sitting in EMF is definitely a challenge - as I discovered. Get your labs check if you can... I bet you'll see a big swing on what your body is doing... this is why Jack says its important to run labs quarterly.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-03T15:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-seasons.6597/post-82699>

Sounds like the winter is a good time to get out of town. Vacation out to the seashore or the boonies in winter, not summer.

### Rachelle — 2013-04-03T19:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-seasons.6597/post-82713>

Depression, confusion and being tired sets in both early fall and early spring and I have been that way ever since I can remember. I am working with my homeopath on that. If he gives me any good information I will pass it along to you Caroline.

### freesia — 2013-04-03T21:33:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-seasons.6597/post-82717>

Last winter was the first time I didn't crash. I usually felt really terrible through April and May with six to ten weeks of swollen glands/spleen, blue fingernails as I tried to adjust to the cold, low mood and poor sleep. I feel for you, it's awful!

You probably know what I'm going to say...
In my case CT really, **really** helped. (FWIW, April I was bathing in mid 60's, May mid 50's, June - November mid 40's.)
I would double check that you're getting sunlight in your eyes first thing in the am when possible too.

I think you're CTing already Caroline so that ^ may not help....just adding my experience anyway.

Hope things improve for you soon.

Audrey what helped me was to stop fighting against it coming winter.. but instead flow with it.. and enjoy the ride. I think it is a mental thing too.. that one chooses maybe? To get used to the cold.. what works best for me is doing it gently, I always push only so hard that I still feel quite comfortable. But always at the edge of almost too cold, like room temp and so. Ice hole is seriously cold for sure but that I do only shortly. I do not shiver after. I have the feeling I need to do it gently, but often and in many ways, like always having it pretty cool in my apartment (around 15 degree C), doing the ice dip every day, sleeping with window wide open and no heating.. etc. That way I feel just great! You will do it Audrey, soon you will start to love the cold, I promise! Remember, it is the sigle most effective way to keep inflammation low.  AND balancing out your hormones too!

If it all gets too hard for you come here and stay with me the summer! here it is just perfect temps all summer long, not too hot, and we can CT together and tan.. you can live in the other apartment upstairs and you have a west balcony to tan on - no luxury but I promise, wifi free!

# Thread: increasing vit. D levels with Green Pastures FCLO
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-vit-d-levels-with-green-pastures-fclo.6605/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2013-04-03T21:11:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-vit-d-levels-with-green-pastures-fclo.6605/post-82715>

Hello!

I would like to find out if anyone has been able to increase their vitamin D levels by supplementing with Green Pastures Royal Blue Ice Fermented High Vitamin/Cod Liver oil?

I have been using this product intermittently throughout the years, but have never taken it consistently in conjunction with having labs drawn. Currently my vitamin D is 38, and I am concerned that I might be wasting my time trying to increase my levels with this product.

Thank you in advance!

Christine

### Ruthie — 2013-04-03T21:25:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-vit-d-levels-with-green-pastures-fclo.6605/post-82716>

I have had to supplement with D3 in addition to FCLO to get my levels up. I believe the reason is that FCLO is lacking in D3 and high in D2.

### cmegan — 2013-04-03T22:07:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-vit-d-levels-with-green-pastures-fclo.6605/post-82721>

I am having a hard time justifying the cost since I have moved mostly to epi-paleo!

### Dextery — 2013-04-04T01:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/increasing-vit-d-levels-with-green-pastures-fclo.6605/post-82748>

Green Pastures seems to not have much benefit for D3 and too expensive. I get my D3 from iherb.com 5000IU Country Life. Free shipping if order over $40 I think. You can use DEX002 to get a first time order discount. I keep my d3 level around 100ng/ml with 10000IU daily.

### cmegan — 2013-04-04T19:01:43+0300
