# Thread: school
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MrPinkies — 2013-04-06T00:19:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83024>

What do you pack in your kids lunch box???

~Kate

### caroline — 2013-04-06T01:59:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83040>

I don't have young kids anymore but I am sure you will get lots of ideas here. Have you gone to the website ...Nom Nom paleo? She has great recipes and two little boys....

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-06T17:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83120>

We homeschool, but often pack up food to take with us wherever we go. Our staples include things like cut up veggies like carrots, bell pepper, celery, cucumbers. We also often grill veggies like bell peppers, stuffed jalapenos and mini peppers wrapped in bacon, zucchini, eggplant and mushrooms, and they love the grilled veggies. Zucchini "latkes" is another favorite.

Meats like salami, leftover grass-fed burgers, meatloaf, sausage, or meatballs; salmon, sardines (or salmon, or tuna) mixed with Bubbie's relish, mustard, and hard boiled eggs, crab cakes,; soups and stews in a thermos like green chile stew, clam or seafood chowder, texas-style chili, etc. I also make a variety of casserole type dishes like red chile enchiladas made with grass-fed beef and eggplant for the "tortillas," butternut squash lasagna, frittatas, etc.

Thankfully my kids are not picky at all, so they happily eat lots of veggies. We also always keep frozen blueberries and rasberries on hand, too. I don't bake a lot, but if I did, I can think of a whole lot of other ideas. Some of the paleo cookbooks have good ideas for make-ahead food items that can be frozen and then used later.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-06T17:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83123>

I have a middle schooler and one in elementary school and we do a variety of the following for packed lunches:

\- meatballs
\- salmon spread with cut up veggies (smoked salmon/cream cheese mix)
\- roast beef
\- burgers
\- lettuce wraps (with ham, bacon, etc)
\- salads with bacon
\- beef stew / chili in a thermos

For sides they'll often take carrots or cut up peppers with ranch dip, muffins (grain free, of course!), homemade 'larabars,' berries, raw cheese and yogurt. It's definitely more difficult with my middle schooler because she tends to get made fun of for having "different" lunches. My 8-year old son couldn't care less what people say, so he's pretty easy!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-07T02:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83157>

Oh Yes! Lettuce wraps for sure. Another favorite, for those not opposed to deli meats (we buy the best quality we can find) is using ham or roast beef as the wrap material, and stuffing with cukes, peppers, avocado, jicama, etc.

A good friend of mine has a daughter who has life-threatening allergies to nuts, dairy, and soy, and she has become very creative with her food ideas. I like this list of ideas, which is where I got the meat-wrap idea.

[http://www.consciouseatery.com/2012/08/whats-for-lunch.html](http://www.consciouseatery.com/2012/08/whats-for-lunch.html)

### cantweight — 2013-04-11T21:13:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83781>

I have an 8, 10, and 12 year old. Lunch is a tricky one for us. My 8 yr old especially is concerned about not having a sandwich.....he really wants a damn sandwich..lol

A lot of what I do I know is still not optimal. It is grain free and organic and I am trying!!

Apples and nut butter
Larabars
Kefir
organic lunch meat rolled up
cheese sticks
shrimp
leftover meats...especially beef and chicken kebab style
chicken salad made with organic mayo, almonds and grapes
seaweed....they love seaweed!
sometimes I make mac and cheese with corn pasta...probably a big no no but they loooove it!

For his birthday my 8 yr old kept saying all he wanted was a lunchable! what the hell! This kid does not have any memory of junk food like my older ones do...it is so strange.
He also tells me that as soon as he learns to drive he is driving to mc donalds. SMH.

### kathylu — 2013-04-12T02:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/school.6654/post-83844>

My son demands bread too. It's a constant struggle, occasionally I give in but only gluten free breads. He likes "bread-like" items made from coconut flour, so I will make "paleo" pancakes and muffins once a week. He also eats more fruit than I would like. However, his diet is light years better than any other child I know.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-11T01:12:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-niacin-harmful-or-helpful.6665/post-83676>

I can't speak to the niacin, but several varieties of medicinal mushrooms might be more effective than niacin without any potential side effects. Maitake (Grifola frondosa) and Enokitake (Flammulina velutipes, which can be found in most grocery stores), as well as Shiitake are known to lower LDL. Shiitake can lower both HDL and LDL, though, so it might be safer to use Maitake. Maitake are fabulously wonderful mushrooms with a whole host of benefits.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11520942](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11520942)

# Thread: DHEA and Pregnenolone Dosing Question?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-and-pregnenolone-dosing-question.6668/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-07T04:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dhea-and-pregnenolone-dosing-question.6668/post-83176>

Hi,

Based upon my most recent ASI and DHEA labs, I think I need to raise my pregnenolone and DHEA to help the AM cortisol but I am not sure exactly how much (don't want to overshoot and screw up my sleep which has been slowly improving  I was actually surprised at how pitiful my DHEA still is on my last ASI given that I am taking 25 mg DHEA and 25 mg 7-keto DHEA. From what I've read, that seems like a high dose for a woman (I am 34 if that is relevant).

My current dosage schedule is:

25mg DHEA (12.5 first thing upon rising, 12.5 at noon )
25mg 7-Keto DHEA (first thing upon rising)
50 mg Pregnenolone (25 first thing upon rising, 25 at noon )

The DHEA and Pregnenolone are currently sublingual formulations by Douglas Labs. I am, however, considering either switching entirely or adding some of the micronized lipid matrix DHEA and pregnenolone JanSz recommended because the brand I'm taking clearly isn't getting the job done (or I'm just not taking enough). I realize that everybody is different and this isn't an exact science, but I would appreciate it if some people experienced with tweaking DHEA and pregnenolone to optimize their cortisol could suggest a ballpark adjustment based upon my current labs and dosing schedule.

NOTE to JanSz: In my other post you suggested starting with 2 of the 150mg MLM tabs. Does that still seem right to you based on my labs (I don't think I had posted my labs when you replied last time). Also, what would u reccommend for DHEA if I switch over to the MLM version of the DHEA?

[http://www.amazon.com/Douglas-Labs-...=1365295872&sr=8-2&keywords=douglas+labs+dhea](http://www.amazon.com/Douglas-Labs-Sublingual-Health-Beauty/dp/B00380WKEI/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1365295872&sr=8-2&keywords=douglas+labs+dhea)

# Thread: Savvy Analysis of this Recent Article?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-08T01:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/post-83286>

All kinds of alarms go off when I read it...anyone care to analyze, I've been doing too much yard work today to give it a shot.

First read about it here: [http://www.forbes.com/sites/larryhu...w-pathway-linking-heart-disease-to-carnitine/](http://www.forbes.com/sites/larryhusten/2013/04/07/researchers-find-new-pathway-linking-heart-disease-to-carnitine/)

And here is the research article:

[http://www.nature.com/nm/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nm.3145.html](http://www.nature.com/nm/journal/vaop/ncurrent/full/nm.3145.html)

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T01:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/post-83294>

quick glance at the actual study - says its how the gut microbes convert the choline, phosphatydle choline, and caratine from meat links to heart disease - since I don't have access to the full article - I infer the following: if your gut microbes are based upon SAD - then yes there might be a link - but if you've got proper gut microbes - say those you get from eating epi-paleo (especially raw) then it shouldn't be an issue

this is all about the microbes - and whether you have the right ones or the wrong ones...

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-08T05:36:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/savvy-analysis-of-this-recent-article.6680/post-83328>

I read the full article. The study is based on the correlation of trimethylamine-N-oxide (TMAO) levels and atherosclorosis. The authors state may not fully know mechanism for this. This is not the point of their study. this study is about TMAO production by specific intestinal microbiota in the presence of carnitine. When they wiped out the gut bacteria of human subjects for two weeks then fed them meat they produced no TMAO. Vegans and Vegetarians also produce little TMAO when fed l-carnitine. there is no discussion about how the diets in the TMAO-artherosclerosi studies were controlled for. I think the title implies a link that may not be fully established.
The best part of the article is the following:
The high level of meat consumption in the developed world is linked to CVD risk, presumably owing to the large content of saturated fats and cholesterol in meat1, 2. However, a recent meta-analysis of prospective cohort studies showed no association between dietary saturated fat intake and CVD, prompting the suggestion that other environmental exposures linked to increased meat consumption are responsible3. In fact, the suspicion that the cholesterol and saturated fat content of red meat may not be sufficiently high enough to account for the observed association between CVD and meat consumption has stimulated investigation of alternative disease-promoting exposures that accompany dietary meat ingestion, such as high salt content or heterocyclic compounds generated during cooking

Finally it is starting to be accepted that the fat-cholesterol CVD connection doesn't hold up.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Can I have some help understanding thyroid?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tweety — 2013-04-08T02:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-83301>

I have been on NDT/Armour successfully since last summer. I cruised through the summer at 90-120mg and did very well. In Nov my numbers looked great, (really low TSH - .1something, which is where it was recommended I aim) though I can't remember the rest of them now. In Dec hypothyroid symptoms showed up and we bumped up to 180mg of Armour. I did really well. In March, I started to get heart palps and extreme hunger, so I backed off to 150mg. That worked well, for a week. Hyperthyroid showed up again, palps and hunger, so down to 120mg. Continuing this pattern, now I am down to 60mg of Armour. I realize it is April in the NE USA and light cycles are lengthening.

I still feel really warm- much warmer than everyone else around me. We keep our house at 55-60 degrees. I have not CTed in a while, but I did start walking my dog outside barefoot in early March, even in the snow. We're talking 1 minute or less several times a day. Not 30-40min of grounding but still a connection. I also had to quit my progesterone after 15mos- weight gain around the middle and depression during period, swollen feet and ankles. I have felt wonderful the last month, very happy and just feel good in my bones. I know quitting P and lowering thyroid was what I needed, though my sleep is still iffy and today I had a killer migraine like I haven't had in years. Therefore I think i will need to try a different form of progesterone and maybe a combo of E and T in the near future.

What I am struggling to understand is the thyroid. Did I go hyper and now cannot tolerate any thyroid supplementation? I still find myself ridiculously hungry and able to eat much more than I should. Or am I healing and my body is trying to take over on its own? I am 34 and not really overweight (but at my heaviest and worst body composition in the past 12yrs) so its possible that I wasn't as far off track as I felt. I still have much healing to do. Leaky gut symptoms seem to be increasing after never really having any. I can't tolerate alot of foods that I used to (almonds, avocados, nuts, coconut meat...). They all cause bloating.

I have read and reread so much here and just when I start to connect things I lose sight of it for a while and have to try again. I am not able to do labs for a while, but intend to redo my ASI/hormone panel in a month or two. Last time I had low cortisol for all four readings. My energy is excellent, but I do not tolerate any caffeine (mild headaches in the morning) which makes me feel my adrenals still need addressing. Sorry for rambling, trying to include the main points.

Any thoughts thyroid gurus?

Thanks

### MeghanK — 2013-04-08T02:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-83315>

Have you read Stop the Thyroid Madness? Check out that book and website.....

### tweety — 2013-04-08T03:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-83321>

yes, but I should revisit it I guess

### Grizz — 2014-01-18T23:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-106148>

This changes everything:
The Thyroid-Gut Connection ✰ ✰ ✰

**Healing the gut-thyroid axis**

> ....All of these connections make it clear that you can’t have a healthy gut without a healthy thyroid, and you can’t have a healthy thyroid without a healthy gut. To restore proper function of the gut-thyroid axis, both must be addressed simultaneously.
>
> Healing the gut is a huge topic that can’t be covered adequately in a few short sentences. But I will say this: the first step is always to figure out what’s causing the gut dysfunction. As we’ve reviewed in this article, low thyroid is one possible cause, but often hypochlorhydria, infections, dysbiosis, food intolerances (especially gluten), stress and other factors play an even more significant role. The second step is to address these factors and remove any potential triggers. The third step is to restore the integrity of the gut barrier. My preferred approach for this last step is the GAPS diet....

### Albert83BCN — 2014-01-19T00:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-have-some-help-understanding-thyroid.6681/post-106153>

These resources pointed by Grizz and MeghanK are great ones. You might be interested as well in getting Dr Kharrazian book 'Why Do I Still Have Thyroid Symptoms?' IMHO is a really great book, it will give you a lot of insight and references so you can keep digging. Of course you'll find a lot of gold in the blogposts by JK as well.

You're not alone, I feel the same when I start understanding something just to realize there's a ton more of things that I missed completely. ### tweety — 2013-04-08T03:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83322>

I listened to a podcast interviewing Sara Gottfried. She said she raised hers using a protocol called sprint 8. I am looking into it, but some of the other things she said she recommends concerned me, so I am not all in. I do think sprints help, but you have to have decent cortisol first.

### Inger — 2013-04-08T12:52:52+0300

### tweety — 2013-04-08T16:21:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83354>

CT- yes. I have a love/hate relationship with CT. Sometimes I just crave it, and get really going with it. Then out of nowhere I don't want it. I try to follow my body's intuitions, and most of the time it leads me well. But this one I have to lead my body more than the others.

Colostrum- I keep forgetting about that one. We keep it on hand to boost immune systems when kids/we get sick. Good thought.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T16:28:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83355>

> [tweety said:](/goto/post?id=83354)
>
> CT- yes. I have a love/hate relationship with CT. Sometimes I just crave it, and get really going with it. Then out of nowhere I don't want it. I try to follow my body's intuitions, and most of the time it leads me well. But this one I have to lead my body more than the others.
>
> Colostrum- I keep forgetting about that one. We keep it on hand to boost immune systems when kids/we get sick. Good thought.

Tweety - that love hate relationship - I'd bet good money - your love of it coincides w/your PG being high, and your hate of it is when Pg is low and E is high.

colostrum is great.. but don't forget your Oxytocin therapy...

### HoneyChild — 2013-04-09T11:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83451>

How's your vitamin D levels? It appears vitamin D has an effect on IGF-1...

**Serum IGF1 concentration is positively associated with 25-hydroxyvitamin D level and increases upon treatment with vitamin D**

[http://www.endocrine-abstracts.org/ea/0029/ea0029p198.htm](http://www.endocrine-abstracts.org/ea/0029/ea0029p198.htm)

### tweety — 2013-04-09T18:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83500>

Interesting tie in with P and E levels. When I was in a pattern over the winter I noticed I would easily ho pin the tub the closer I got to my period. Right after my period (days 7-15 ish) was when I wasn't interested. That would be a time of lower P, which would mean higher E. I remember several women commenting that they did not like to CT around the time of their period...

I made myself do CT last night, and I just got out of a cold bath a few minutes ago. Of course its 70 deg and sunny, and the tap is not as cold, but it will do. I love CT in the summer too, especially after working outside in the garden.

HoneyChild- my D levels were in the 70s last I checked. And I still take a decent amount daily. Maybe I should up it...

### caroline — 2013-04-10T00:34:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83532>

My D levels are very high - so I guess more C/T.... and more oxy......

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-10T00:39:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83534>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=83532)
>
> My D levels are very high - so I guess more C/T.... and more oxy......

I've got to do this too caroline... don't forget to turn off the wifi and get out into nature

### caroline — 2013-04-10T00:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83537>

I am outside for hours and hours every day ...and the wifi is OFF.....

### MeghanK — 2013-04-10T03:21:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83562>

Is IGF1 a blood test you can get? Is it part of a spectrum or an individual test?

### caroline — 2013-04-10T04:17:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83571>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=83545)
>
> how's your DHEA?

Bottom of the range - did come up a little last ASI .....just waiting on results of new ASI. I think I should have been supplementing with more - I have been taking 50mg and cream but I have taken 100mg with no bad effects.

I don't understand my Pregnenolone tho - it is quite high. I don't get why it doesn't get shunted to Pg and my am cortisol is low too. To me it seems like it is just sitting there - not doing anything! not converting - just hangin out!

I will know in a couple of days if I have made any progress...

### HoneyChild — 2013-04-10T11:53:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naturally-increasing-igf1.6682/post-83587>

# Thread: Is any one doing smoothies?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-04-08T09:05:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83331>

I am wondering if anyone has tried Dave Aspreys protein powder and if so - what do you think?
If you are doing smoothies once in a while - what are you using? And how are you making them?

### Inger — 2013-04-08T11:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83334>

I do.. MHS.  Sometimes I have done raw colostrum milk with frozen lingonberries.. my ice cream, lol

I have done wakame seaweed smoothies witch chocolate too.. But IDK I would personally not use protein powder ever. I get way too much protein anyways with my foods, huh. I do not believe in such unnatural foods anyway, if there are better options.

I sometimes make raw egg smoothies for hubs, he loves those! Raw Colostrum milk, raw eggs, raw honey, vanilla beans and - ready. They are yummy!

In summer I do smoothies from nettles and other wild edibles - pur with water. Very nice and makes me feel great! You could do them with fruits too - like nettles + papaya. Those are good too. But I am not a smoothie fan really.

### cmegan — 2013-04-08T14:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83342>

Caroline, I have been using Tera's Whey from goat milk...I do not know about Dave Asprey's protein powder but will look into it...

I got back into doing smoothies several weeks ago after contracting a stomach flu. I couldn't bear to look at meat or seafood for at least a week, I was eating mostly broths with vegetables and then started using smoothies to get some protein. It was the only protein I could digest at the time. I have to admit now that I am feeling better and the weather is getting warmer in NY I am having a difficult time eating red meat (I eat lamb burgers) in the morning. I have just been mixing the goat whey with water and coconut oil in a blender. I am allergic to casein but have had no problems with this whey and my morning glucose has been steady...

### cmegan — 2013-04-08T15:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83345>

I just checked the Tera's whey website, it does not say that the goat's are grass fed...I'll have to call them, this is a fairly new product for me. I use the plain, un-sweetened one. I used to use Mt. Capra Fermented Goat Milk protein, their goats are 100% grass fed...

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T15:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83346>

I've used Dave's bullet proof whey - Its a great product. however for me - its one of those products that makes me gain weight. so Instead I just stick to using heavy cream, or coconut milk blended with cacao powder, maca and some d-ribose when I want a shake.

### David — 2013-04-08T15:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83348>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=83346)
>
> I've used Dave's bullet proof whey - Its a great product. however for me - its one of those products that makes me gain weight. so Instead I just stick to using heavy cream, or coconut milk blended with cacao powder, maca and some d-ribose when I want a shake.

Sounds like a winner! In my occasional shake, I add some spinach, cocoa, coconut milk, coconut oil, cinnamon, d-Ribose, Brazil nuts, a couple of scoops of LEF vitamin mix and a few drops of Lugal's to the whey.

I would like to know, though: what is the benefit of maca?

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T16:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83353>

maca helps women w/PCOS - If I remember correctly it helps support Pg production, and blanaces Testosterone/Estrogen and it also supports Adrenals...

### cmegan — 2013-04-08T17:53:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83362>

shijin13, why do you think it the bulletproof whey makes you gain weight? Because of the theory of whey affecting insulin levels?

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-08T18:08:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83363>

when I drink coconut milk or heavy cream by itself - I loose weight. when I combine either coconut milk or cream w/the whey I gain just my n=1. I think in a pinch it works for me. but I've found I do better on higher fat...

### caroline — 2013-04-09T00:58:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-any-one-doing-smoothies.6684/post-83393>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=83346)
>
> I've used Dave's bullet proof whey - Its a great product. however for me - its one of those products that makes me gain weight. so Instead I just stick to using heavy cream, or coconut milk blended with cacao powder, maca and some d-ribose when I want a shake.

So you would notice that your weight would be up the next day? When you have Dave's protein powder - you just mix with water? or do you add eggs etc?
Does anyone remember JK's recipe?

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7

### MeghanK — 2013-04-09T03:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/post-83413>

Just wondering anyone and especially Jack's thoughts on this since he is mentioned in the article.....[http://180degreehealth.com/2013/04/hot-water-vs-cold-water-thermogenesis?utm\_source=feedburner&utm\_medium=feed&utm\_campaign=Feed%3A+180degreehealth%2Frss+%28180+Degree+Health%29&utm\_content=Google+Feedfetcher](http://180degreehealth.com/2013/04/hot-water-vs-cold-water-thermogenesis?utm_source=feedburner&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+180degreehealth%2Frss+%28180+Degree+Health%29&utm_content=Google+Feedfetcher)

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-10T00:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/post-83538>

I'm not familiar with Matt Stone, but based ona cursory view of his website, he appears to be one of those people trying to make money by being on the anti-paleo side of things, the "I'm going to help fix you when Paleo breaks you," mentality. Clearly, he is orders of magnitude removed from all the information Jack has posted these past several months.

One statement in the blog post:

"Cold temperatures in winter are not conducive to better health and a higher metabolic rate either - or at least not greater leanness. Winter is fattening and exacerbates most health conditions, whereas the hot temperatures of summer or the tropics tend to favor greater leanness and lessen most health conditions. Most on thyroid meds have to up their dosage in the winter time just to keep hypothyroid symptoms at bay."

I don't think he did any actual historical research to verify the first statement. Many traditional cultures have a history of cold water plunges during winter, and often cite increased immunity as a reason for doing them. Secondly, the blog post is limited in what it is presenting as the PURPOSE of cold water immersions, and he appears saying that increased metabolism and weight loss are the only reasons people are doing CT.

His next statement about winter being fattening and exacerbating most health conditions is especially interesting, given all that I have learned these past several months. If he spent any time at all learning about what Jack has been presenting since the beginning of this winter, I think he might have a different perspective. I wish I could summarize it all in a snap, but I'd have to go re-read several blogs and forum posts to sum it up into a concise statement, but anyone who has been paying attention can see the flaw in his logic here, I assume.

"None of this of course takes into account the unrealistic idea that one is going to submerge themselves into icy water, intentionally, with any kind of regularity. Talk about going against instincts â€“ especially if you have a subpar metabolism and already have a major aversion to cold"

I don't know if this guy Matt has tried CT or not. I am doubtful that he's experienced CT outside where one gets the benefit of grounding, CT, and possibly the photoelectric effect, all combined into a session of ultimate awesomeness. He thinks it's an unrealistic idea to expect someone to submerge oneself in cold water regularly. Especially in winter, I presume. I have done more CT this past winter than I did last summer, and I found myself abandoning other enjoyable activities so that I could go hike up into a canyon and CT in my snowy, mountain environment. In fact, I still wish I had more time weekly to pack in all the things I like to do so that I could still ensure regular, weekly CT sessions outside. It's a juggle and I'm still trying to figure it all out. But very worth it to me.

### SCRN2007 — 2013-04-10T02:10:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-thermomgenesis-valid.6695/post-83546>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=83413)

Matt Stone has no qualifications whatsoever. When he first came on the scene I googled him to see if he had any education whatsoever regarding physiology . . . nope. Listen to him at your own peril. He recommends eating Snickers bars and such to "recover" the body from dieting. As Jack has stated, carbs in the short term might seem to help your thyroid from the possible pitfalls of traditional low-carb paleo, but the price you will pay in the end is HUGE. Hopefully Jack will chime in here if I am incorrect, but the preceding is my understanding of the situation. My take on Matt Stone is that he is profiting from telling carb-craving paleo blogger women with crappy hormone panels that it is okay to eat carbs to "recover" their basal body temps. I think it is a line of crap, as Jack's statements regarding "safe carbs" point out.

# Thread: Ear ringing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-04-09T17:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83481>

Does anyone have ear ringing? It is a most annoying issue. I've asked my naturopath, doctor, dentist, anti-aging doctor, osteo, chiro, holistic allergist (holy cow I've been to a lot of people LOL). The ONLY thing that's helped has been the K2 that Dr. K recommended during my consult. It doesn't make it go away, but I just ran out of K2 (actually gave the last 2 capsules to the dog for her arthritis and knee issues lol). And today, holy cow my ears are so loud! I definitely notice a difference taking K2 and not for the ear ringing.

Then in one of the articles about EMF someone had posted last week (I think it was a UK paper), one of the people interviewed said that tinnitus was the first sign of EMF issues. I had always thought it was linked to my inflammation and sinus allergies (and constant congestion). But maybe not.

Does anyone have any thoughts on it? I've had it for about 6 years. Actually got it for the first time coming home from NY - I got a cold while I was there, my ears got plugged on the plane home, and they've been ringing ever since. Sometimes it's so loud, I can barely hear (that's when it's really bad, but hasn't been that bad in a couple of years). I've actually heard of people committing suicide from it - to me it's not that bad, but maybe there's other stuff going on along with it?

Thought I'd throw it out there to see if anyone had any ideas!

### lioness7 — 2013-04-09T17:45:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83487>

My husband has had ringing in his ears for seven years. It started the same day (or next day) after he walked into a low tree branch and snapped his neck back. I am not sure if this is coincidental or not. He did chiropractics and some vitamin therapies. Some days it is better, but most days it stays the same. I think that his cell phone usage may be playing a big part in it not going away. I would love to know of others' experiences with this. I will give him more k2 to see if it makes a difference.

### cantweight — 2013-04-09T17:46:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83488>

I always thought there was a strong magnesium link for tinnitus and hyperacusis. I remember a lot of people in the Lyme community having issues with this and recommending mag, B vitamins, especially B12, and zinc.

The answer is probably fish and photons...lol But this is my take, hope it helps

### diane — 2013-04-09T17:49:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83491>

I take quite a bit of mag, B12, and zinc (my zinc was low on my last labs). Take iodine, eat lots of fish - but really need to work on the photons. Would love to do a grounding/photon experiment to see if it helps.

### cantweight — 2013-04-09T17:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83492>

I wonder if boosting the zinc and perhaps adding a different mag? Do you have any malate currently? I've had great response to that, so have my kiddos

I was just kidding about the fish and photons  Channeling my inner Jack...lol

### diane — 2013-04-09T19:22:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83506>

cantweight - you may be right about the fish and protons!

I do take malate - but have ordered orotate and threonate. Just waiting for the order to cross the border!

### Joann — 2013-04-09T21:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83512>

Ginko Biloba has been touted to help ringing in ears. I have at times more of a "hissing" which started after I had my neck extended back looking up at computer. sometimes I try to help myself by consciously relaxing my shoulders and opening my mouth and slowly closing my mouth then putting my tongue tip to roof of mouth.....lips together, teeth apart
It helps or pinch nose while mouth closed and blow. all my coping strategies

### Sistagrok — 2013-04-09T23:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83522>

I've had tinnitus for ever it seems. Before I came to realize what it was, I always just thought I had super sensitive hearing or something and could hear electrical fields..lol. I have it all day long , and have had it for as long as I can remember. Nothing I have been using has helped. I do think it is quieter than it used to be, until I start to listen to see if it's there, then it's loud and clear.  I do use K2 also.

### DarleenMB — 2013-04-10T00:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83530>

Mine is tied to seasonal allergies. Medication to help the allergies makes it worse. What a catch-22.

I would NOT pinch my nose with my mouth closed and blow. That's probably a good way to force bacteria into the inner ear.

Can't speak to whether yours is caused by emf issues since I'm not sure I believe emf is that big an issue. (yeah yeah I'm waiting for the slap upside the head now) As I said mine is definitely allergy related. that's when I know Spring is here, when the ringing starts up.

### diane — 2013-04-10T00:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83539>

> [Sistagrok said:](/goto/post?id=83522)
>
> I've had tinnitus for ever it seems. Before I came to realize what it was, I always just thought I had super sensitive hearing or something and could hear electrical fields..lol. I have it all day long , and have had it for as long as I can remember. Nothing I have been using has helped. I do think it is quieter than it used to be, until I start to listen to see if it's there, then it's loud and clear.  I do use K2 also.

Haha - exactly. Sometimes I think it's gone - then I listen and it's still there. LOL. I also at some points think I may be hearing magnetic fields, but then I think it's the vibration of the eardrum. No?

Interesting - it's way more common than I thought! When I tell people they look at me strangely - like they can't imagine it. My mom has it too. Darleen - I wonder what in the medication makes it worse? I have mine 24/7/365. I'm just lucky!

### cinnamon — 2013-04-10T03:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83558>

My father had tinnitus about 20 years ago. His was not a ringing sound, but rather a clicking. It just about drove him mad! After seeing your question this morning, Diane, I called him today to ask if he still had it, or if he didn't, what did he find helped.

He told me he saw several specialists and had tests done because they thought there could possibly be some cause. When they didn't find anything, the chief ENT told him that the diagnosis was tinnitus and that unfortunately there wasn't much he could do. He then told him that some people (including the ENT's own mother!) had found help from accupuncture treatments. In desperation, Dad decided to give it a try. He says after about 5 or 10 treatments, the clicking stopped! He went back every couple of months for a year or two after that and the clicking has never come back.

Maybe it is a disruption in energy flow that causes tinnitus?

### diane — 2013-04-10T04:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83572>

That's really interesting cinnamon. THanks for the input!

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-10T08:40:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83579>

I don't notice the ringing in my ears anymore unless I consciously listen for it.

### Sistagrok — 2013-04-10T16:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83606>

>
> Interesting - it's way more common than I thought! When I tell people they look at me strangely - like they can't imagine it. My mom has it too. Darleen - I wonder what in the medication makes it worse? I have mine 24/7/365. I'm just lucky!

Diane, yours sounds a lot like mine. I can see how it could drive a person mad. I heard or read somewhere, that a woman once did go mad, as she had tinnitus, and then went deaf. Therefore not being able to drown out any of the tinnitus with other sound. That scares me ! I hope that never happens to me. Here is an example of the sounds of a few different types of tinnitus. Mine is a mix between the first 2. Heck , even the squiggly lines they are depicting at the back of the head remind me of mine.
[http://neuromonics.com/about-tinnitus/what-tinnitus-sounds-like/](http://neuromonics.com/about-tinnitus/what-tinnitus-sounds-like/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-ringing.6701/post-83611>

I'm definitely number 2. Interesting that it says that one of the treatments is cognitive behaviour therapy. So if tinnitus is a disease of the brain - probably from inflammation - I'm guessing there's other links and causes that we don't realize.

> Thanks for saying that! I was sure he had mentioned shoes ...Misty made me feel like I was losing my mind! What he said gave me pause too. If you are walking dogs etc. in a park I think they would be great ..but not on pavement.

I recall him introducing the caveat that this was beyond his expertise ... that's what I meant to convey. He was hypothesizing, but that's it.

For context - Jack has been replacing his entire shoe collection with leather-soled shoes and boots, which is essentially the same "outcome" as the Juil's. Per the webinar today, if you want to know whether something is actually "working" as far as you're grounded while using the product, you would use a body voltage meter and the meter would say "0" while you're using the product. If it showed a charge above 0 at all, that means you're not grounded while using the product. I think that's the best way for to test this for yourself.

# Thread: Bug repelling?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-04-10T02:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/post-83555>

So it is in the 70s now and I can feel the mosquitoes coming, LOL...anyone have a good/effective way to repel bugs?? these things usually love me.

### fitness@home — 2013-04-10T03:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/post-83559>

Hope,

I used this product last year:

[http://www.goodcleanlivin.com/id73.html](http://www.goodcleanlivin.com/id73.html)

Soon it will be too hot at lunchtime for me to go the park and I will switch to spending 30 minutes there before work (watch the sky get light and enjoy nature). We have plenty of mosquitoes down here and they left me alone every morning last summer.

Read the ingredient list...love this stuff and it smells good too.

We have also used Cedarcide with good success also (cedar oil). They also have a website.

### BJK77 — 2013-04-10T14:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/post-83594>

I found increasing my B vitamins really helped prevent bites last year. Before that, I used to get eaten alive!

### Destiny — 2013-04-10T14:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/post-83597>

Would the Skeeter B Gones help with ticks as well?

### fitness@home — 2013-04-10T17:18:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/post-83613>

Not sure about repelling ticks. I just read a good tip for removing ticks without breaking the heads off.

Apply liquid soap to that area liberally and gently rub it around. Wait a couple of minutes the tick will remove itself. I have never been bitten by a tick (yet) and have not tested this method.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-10T18:08:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bug-repelling.6707/post-83619>

I think that mosquitoes always go after the person with the sweetest blood.

But for a repellent, if you have any American Beautyberry bushes nearby, their crushed leaves, rubbed on the skin, have an oil that repels them.

[http://www.countrysideaustin.com/2010/05/american-beautyberry/](http://www.countrysideaustin.com/2010/05/american-beautyberry/)

# Thread: My latest lab results and I need help please...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-latest-lab-results-and-i-need-help-please.6710/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Justfourme — 2013-04-10T04:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-latest-lab-results-and-i-need-help-please.6710/post-83570>

Please help with some lab results. Doc put me on 5 mg sub lingual PG after getting these results. Will increase to 10mg gradually.
Age 53 - peri menopausal - had been taking an estrogen sup but had been off of them 2 weeks prior to testing. Doc says estrogen is high.
Individual saliva tests: results estrone >50, normal range <16.0 pg/ml;
results estradiol >50, normal range 0.2 - 5.2 pg/ml;
Results estriol >50, normal range 2.0 pg/ml
Results progesterone, saliva <1, normal range 80-270 pg/ml
Results DHEA <0.01, normal range 0.10-1.30 pg/ml
Results testosterone, saliva 10, normal range 4-33 pg/ml
Results melatonin, am <0.5, normal range 0.1-25.0 pg/ml
Results 17-OH progesterone, saliva 4.1, normal range 15.4-60.5 pg/ml
Results androstenedione, saliva 30, normal range 6-69
PE ratio she has down as zero? Normal range 45-450
Results DHT, saliva <10, normal range <10-60.0 pg/ml
Results cortisol AM 3.99, normal range 1.8-3.8 ng/ml
Cortisol noon 1.65, normal range 0.3-1.4 ng/ml
Cortisol evening 0.74, normal range 0.1-1.1 ng/ml
Cortisol night 0.15, normal range 0.1-0.8 ng/ml

So cortisol is too high in the AM and she is recommending L Theanine and Phosphatidylserine in the AM.
I discontinued any estrogen supplements.

Am I on the right path?

Doc also said "cycle day" didn't matter as I had menses late aug 2012, then again late feb 2013 after I had started some PG cream on my own. Tests were taken late jan 2013. Quickly approaching menopause.

### Destiny — 2013-04-10T14:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-latest-lab-results-and-i-need-help-please.6710/post-83593>

> [Justfourme said:](/goto/post?id=83570)
>
> Please help with some lab results. Doc put me on 5 mg sub lingual PG after getting these results. Will increase to 10mg gradually.
> Age 53 - peri menopausal - had been taking an estrogen sup but had been off of them 2 weeks prior to testing. Doc says estrogen is high.
> Individual saliva tests: results estrone >50, normal range <16.0 pg/ml;
> results estradiol >50, normal range 0.2 - 5.2 pg/ml;
> Results estriol >50, normal range 2.0 pg/ml
> Results progesterone, saliva <1, normal range 80-270 pg/ml
> Results DHEA <0.01, normal range 0.10-1.30 pg/ml
> Results testosterone, saliva 10, normal range 4-33 pg/ml
> Results melatonin, am <0.5, normal range 0.1-25.0 pg/ml
> Results 17-OH progesterone, saliva 4.1, normal range 15.4-60.5 pg/ml
> Results androstenedione, saliva 30, normal range 6-69
> PE ratio she has down as zero? Normal range 45-450
> Results DHT, saliva <10, normal range <10-60.0 pg/ml
> Results cortisol AM 3.99, normal range 1.8-3.8 ng/ml
> Cortisol noon 1.65, normal range 0.3-1.4 ng/ml
> Cortisol evening 0.74, normal range 0.1-1.1 ng/ml
> Cortisol night 0.15, normal range 0.1-0.8 ng/ml
>
> So cortisol is too high in the AM and she is recommending L Theanine and Phosphatidylserine in the AM.
> I discontinued any estrogen supplements.
>
> Am I on the right path?
>
> Doc also said "cycle day" didn't matter as I had menses late aug 2012, then again late feb 2013 after I had started some PG cream on my own. Tests were taken late jan 2013. Quickly approaching menopause.

You need to oppose your estrogen with progesterone. Did your doctor tell you anything about your very low progesterone, DHEA and melatonin? I believe that is what you need to address that. Also look into taking DIM. Your cortisol is a bit high in the morning but the curve is descending as it should. Your evening and night cortisol is excellent. No adrenal fatigue here!

# Thread: Grounding question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-04-10T15:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83604>

Hi there,

Before I waste any time during the next webinar I was wondering if I got it right: you should only ground w/a dedicated wire that is dropped outside of your window and connected to a copper cord that in the ground. That said, you still may not get any good use out of it because the wire itself might be picking up some EMF and delivering them to you. So, do we have a solid solution or not? Can the wire be shielded? Thanks, Olga

### MeghanK — 2013-04-11T18:09:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83755>

Is there absolutely no benefit from getting grounded through the wall (I have a earthing mat and the "tester" it came with says my wall unit is "grounded")?

### nonchalant — 2013-04-11T18:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83760>

If you are talking about being in a location of high EMF, I would say any grounding is good grounding. Using a body voltage meter, you can see that your body voltage can get quite high near some items like a microwave, computer, cell phone, fluorescent light, dimmer switch, etc. And if you were to to touch your grounding pad, plugged into a grounded outlet, you will see that your body voltage drops to zero. That would be a very good thing, imo. It would get your body out of the EMF storm around you. I am not saying that it would be perfect, however. And I don't think a pure ground to earth in that situation would be perfect either. Shielding plus grounding would be better. Or just getting away from it all.

If you are talking about being in a room with little EMF, and plugging in that grounding pad into a wall outlet and putting your feet on, or laying down on that pad, that is where I would agree that the grounding would be unbeneficial, or even counter productive.

### endless — 2013-04-11T19:44:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83769>

I believe Michael mentioned in the webinar that lowering EMF should be done before grounding, maybe that is what reduces your chances of picking up stray current or dirty electricity? Either way the body voltage is probably the way to figure it out.

### Martin — 2013-04-11T21:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83779>

I agree.

Test.

I'm getting my body voltage meter this week and the first thing I'm going to check is how my grounding sheet was working when I had the ground rod driven in the soil near an underground power feed to the barn. We stopped using the sheet a while back and get more data on them.

Test. When you know better...

### Ruthie — 2013-04-11T22:17:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83786>

I will be interested to see the results of those who are testing their grounding sheets with body voltage meters. Michael got me concerned enough to stop using our grounding sheet, and I'm not totally clear on how to exactly figure out the cost/benefit of it in my own environment.

### agatha — 2013-04-11T23:38:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83804>

I have been using a grounding mat and sheet plugged into the plug socket for about a week now so it's early days. I have been testing each day with a body voltage meter and each time i test my body voltage reduces from between 2 to 6 volts ungrounded to 0.2v or less with the grounding equipment (depending on how damp my skin is). I live in a leafy suburban location in the UK so not too bad for EMF. Shocking to find my bed was the worst location in the house! Since starting grounding my sleep has been lovely and a large hard corn on my foot (had it for months) has reduced in size and softened - it will be interesting to see whether it disappears.

### MeghanK — 2013-04-12T00:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83822>

What body voltage meter do you use... can someone link?

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-12T02:07:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83837>

[http://www.amazon.com/Gardner-Bender-GDT-3200-Function-Multimeter/dp/B000N90JFO](http://www.amazon.com/Gardner-Bender-GDT-3200-Function-Multimeter/dp/B000N90JFO)

This is the voltage meter that Michael from EMF Center suggested to me. I bought it and I have found the results very helpful.

Directions on how to make your own body voltage meter. [http://www.emfinfo.com/make-bv-meter.html](http://www.emfinfo.com/make-bv-meter.html)

### tellmisty — 2013-04-12T06:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-question.6713/post-83862>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=83760)
>
> If you are talking about being in a location of high EMF, I would say any grounding is good grounding. Using a body voltage meter, you can see that your body voltage can get quite high near some items like a microwave, computer, cell phone, fluorescent light, dimmer switch, etc. And if you were to to touch your grounding pad, plugged into a grounded outlet, you will see that your body voltage drops to zero. That would be a very good thing, imo. It would get your body out of the EMF storm around you. I am not saying that it would be perfect, however. And I don't think a pure ground to earth in that situation would be perfect either. Shielding plus grounding would be better. Or just getting away from it all.
>
> If you are talking about being in a room with little EMF, and plugging in that grounding pad into a wall outlet and putting your feet on, or laying down on that pad, that is where I would agree that the grounding would be unbeneficial, or even counter productive.

Yes, as someone else has said, you don't want to try to ground in hi EMF environments ... you want to reduce the EMF. And yes, for grounding equipment, you test by using the body voltage meter, and as I understand it, you want the meter to show "0" to confirm that you are truly grounded.

> [Ruthie said:](/goto/post?id=83786)
>
> I will be interested to see the results of those who are testing their grounding sheets with body voltage meters. Michael got me concerned enough to stop using our grounding sheet, and I'm not totally clear on how to exactly figure out the cost/benefit of it in my own environment.

Explained above.

# Thread: Thyroid not getting anywhere...or maybe it's the pituitary
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-not-getting-anywhere-or-maybe-its-the-pituitary.6719/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-04-10T23:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-not-getting-anywhere-or-maybe-its-the-pituitary.6719/post-83669>

I posted this in Ask Jack and I am hoping he'll be willing to answer but also would love to hear from some of you amazing folks on this.....I will have more labs ready soon which I'll post after my trip....

here goes.....

So my labs after one month OFF any thyroid meds (to test what Id do), my TSH has gone up from 0.02 to 4, as expected, and T3 and T4 (free) are the same, i.e. low normal (2.6 and 1.1 respectively). So it appears none of the thyroid meds I have tried help nothing but the TSH. LDL is good, A low normal.

NO matter what I have tried to move FT3/4, nothing is happening. In four years of trying different things to make them come up.

MD wants me to try compounded slow release T3 (wilson protocol). He also thinks it isn't just EMF but lyme's that's affecting me bc my tongue when stuck out is not straight but deviates.

Any thoughts on this?

A move is coming up for sure...but until then, what?

### BJK77 — 2013-04-11T01:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-not-getting-anywhere-or-maybe-its-the-pituitary.6719/post-83678>

Hi Hope!

I just posted in your journal too, but I'm wondering if your diet could be adversely affecting your thyroid. Have you had your antibodies tested? What have you tried for increasing your T3/T4?

### Hope — 2013-04-11T02:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-not-getting-anywhere-or-maybe-its-the-pituitary.6719/post-83683>

Thanks....will check your other post too.

AB have always been super low - at least since 1998. Last tested in Feb, will get results for April soon....

What have I tried? iodine, up to 50 mg now for a few months. Since 2009 specifically, I have tried different thyroid supporting supplements (too many to list), and then since 2011, my MD had me trying several dosages of Armour Thyroid and NatureThroid, which is similar, getting on and off and nothing really making a difference. Then, in December I got on 50mg Levoxyl.....but had to stop that in Feb as I got heart palps. TSH went up in March so I got back on....but no matter what I do, I can't get it higher, the T3. That's why I am now to try the slow release T3 only, compounded....I've also been on something called T-100.....essentially, just sea veggies.

I am not being very detailed here. I am so tired tonight. I feel bad for not being able to write more right now. Have a headache....ugh. I am just so so frustrated. Nothing is or has worked.....

OF course EMF is HUGE for me.....we must move asap....but I just want to know if there is anything I don't know. MD thinks it's Lyme's so I am also doing support for that....without the expensive blood test. Just to see if that would do anything at all. Anyway....thanks for caring.

Diet not sure how. I eat EP basically. I have been cheating with some almond butter with cocoa powder - this week every day, but not in March.....UGH. But otherwise, strict. OH, actually - now that I think, the week before the test, I DID have some 85% chocolate (dark) that had sugar in it...not much, but a little every day. That didn't help but I am not sure how much a week-10 days of this would do anything. I was extremely strict with diet, and am mostly now too........and it's so so hard not to see changes......I don't snack. I do the BAB though not with 50g....I can't eat that much and Dr K said that was OK. I eat about 50-75g protein every day....I weigh 146 at 68 inches.

Anyway thanks..not sure this makes sense.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-11T03:50:21+0300

# Thread: Decided to try cycloset and have a few questions...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decided-to-try-cycloset-and-have-a-few-questions.6720/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-11T01:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decided-to-try-cycloset-and-have-a-few-questions.6720/post-83680>

Hi All,

I have decided to try the cycloset to increase my AM cortisol (so I can get off cortef once and for all) and improve fasting blood sugar. After doing a bit more research, I think I have a better understanding of how it works and of the importance of leptin. I found the following document pretty interesting (note bromocriptine is the same drug as cycloset...cycloset is just a quick release formulation).

[https://docs.google.com/viewer?a=v&...v\_su1g&sig=AHIEtbSJbR-JJr\_VqpZRisB37WhLBDBOjg](https://docs.google.com/viewer?a=v&q=cache:AbN89aL1w9gJ:am4wuhz3zifexz5u.onion.to/Library/English/fit/lifting/Lyle_McDonald/Lyle%2520McDonald%2520-%2520Bromocriptine.pdf+&hl=en&gl=us&pid=bl&srcid=ADGEESh5AQu42ql9Ay-6BXjyC1ow1FztnlYbyMSlBCYqzv77jGr0AlPNodEoS46qHxYGsDzskzIFkwdQwP1erW4h92-JkqM4eeYi06UgFxY5qsTa9JSFB0k7910SP-J6iXa-o0v_su1g&sig=AHIEtbSJbR-JJr_VqpZRisB37WhLBDBOjg)

So today is my 5th day on it and I am currently taking .8mg first thing in the AM. Although the effect is subtle, I have noticed that my brain seems to feel a little more clear and alert in the AM after I take it. This effect is more mental than physical. In other words, although I feel a little more alert mentally, I have not noticed any increase in physical energy. I also have not yet seen any changes in my blood sugar although I suspect that might take longer than 5 days  My most recent fasting reading was 107.

So I have a few questions for those that have used cycloset successfully...

1) What dose should I shoot for? The literature I've seen suggests somewhere between 2.5 and 5 mg. Although my fasting blood sugar is not great, I am not diabetic. I am, therefore, thinking maybe I should aim for the low end of that range (2.5 mg). I realize its necessary to work up slowly though because of the nausea side effect. Even at .8 I've experienced some very slight nausea.

2) For those that have taken it, how long did you take it before you began to see an effect on AM cortisol levels and improved blood sugar?

3) How long is it necessary to continue taking it? If you stop taking it after improving cortisol rhythm and fasting blood sugar, do those positive effects persist or do they only remain as long as you continue to take the drug?

Thanks,
Trinity

### Dextery — 2013-04-11T19:36:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bun-creatinine-ratio.6727/post-83767>

And then we have this from Livestrong.com [http://www.livestrong.com/article/323550-what-is-the-bun-and-creatinine-ratio/](http://www.livestrong.com/article/323550-what-is-the-bun-and-creatinine-ratio/)

_A normal BUN:creatinine ratio is 15:1, according to "Renal Pathophysiology." When the ratio approaches 20:1, it suggests a problem with bloodflow to the kidney; this may be caused by conditions such as heart failure or shock. Alternatively, a BUN:creatinine ratio of 10:1 suggests an intrinsic renal disease, such as glomerulonephritis or acute tubular necrosis.
_

On my last Quest Lab the ratio they suggest is 10.0 - 28.0 range. I was 13.6

I distinctly also remember Dr K on the last Q & A to get it closer to 10.

I found this in Top Ten EMF FAQs If you have a high **BUN/creatine ratio** and drink tap water potassium Iodide or iodine (200-300 mcgs) every other day if you eat seafood once a week. If you eat it more frequently you likely donâ€™t need any supplementation. Using seaweed in your broths is a great way to get this critical nutrient too. This is just another small reason eating an Epi-paleo template makes a lot more sense in our modern world.

This in Quantum Biology 2 So if youâ€™re E-dominate, which a PCOS Woman is, then youâ€™re also dehydrated! You can check this with your **BUN/creatinie ratio**, low CO2 level, or a high LDL cholesterol level. Add that together with Low Progesterone you canâ€™t enter the PPP! However, when you CT it helps neutralize the E-dominance by activating and increasing Progesterone. So ladies, when youâ€™re adverse to getting into the CT tank your progesterone is being dominated by Estradiol (E2) and your thyroid symptoms of being cold and craving heat goes up. When in reality you really need the cold to ramp up the Progesterone and bring down the E2.

Women's progesterone requirements in first half of cycle are same as men.
That is 1.5ng/mL

The 1.5 ng/mL is normal naturally expected value.
With my normal estradiol about 20pg/mL that gives my P/E ratio about 60
Women in day 3-4 should have E2 about 40pg/mL that would give P/E ratio about 30

Think how much you need to raise progesterone
when
dr Lam wants P/E~200
dr Kruse wants P/E~500

Post
hormone's names
what was tested, serum, saliva, blood spot, spot urine, 24hr urine, else
when tested, cycle day # or state that menopausal
was all list from one specimen or the values are from different specimens, different dates different laboratories
units
laboratory range
specify details of any hormonal supplementation, what, what form, how much, where, how often

\---------------------------------------------
When supplementing with pregnenolone, barring negative symptoms, you may want to use high dose at least until "empty buckets fill up". At the beginning, using frequent blood testing as a guide, for number of months, I was using 900mg/day pregnenolone-MLM
and
400mg/day DHEA

When evaluating progesterone, always against laboratory ranges, and second pass, against estradiol and total estrogens

In view of dr Lam's goal of P/E2~200
and
dr Kruse's P/E2~500
close to 100% people will be short on progesterone (men and women)
so just saying that you are low on progesterone does not tell much

singular cortisol-AM value that is low, is already bad news
must be investigated from number of angles
but, if you put good faith effort into Steroid Hormone Optimization, it may just fix itself in the process

Reading your journal, you are doing many things right and last time you had menstrual flow was some time ago, or more, regardless of people suspecting you of being pregnant.

Here, I wrote:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)
if menapausal aim at
progesterone, serum (15-30)ng/mL
estradiol, serum (40-60) pg/mL

I suggest, heavy duty:
Steroid Hormone Panel Optimization
Micronutrient Analysis and Optimization
Fatty Acid Analysis and optimization

No fish or krill oils supplementation

I would tend to (put honest effort to) minimize direct progesterone and hydrocortisone supplementation
in favor of
pregnenolone-MLM
and
DHEA-MLM

\----------------
You may want to do Visual Contrast Testing - VCT (ask Martin about it)(I think it is available over computer), just not sure how that depends on monitor type
possibly detox
and
followup supplementation
if
you fail Visual Contrast Test
I suggest that you buy this book (directly from dr Patricia Kane, so you can get latest revision)

[http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct304.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storeproduct304.aspx)

I am not sure, but your numb legs, may get better with heavy phospholipid supplementation,
and any specific detox as suggested by results of VCT and Fatty Acid Analysis.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bun-creatinine-ratio.6727/post-321119>

> [ATL\_Paleo said:](/goto/post?id=83840)
>
> 25.2 is a bad ratio, and yes represents dehydration. Are you still training? If so, stopping for awhile is the most economical way to help this ratio. Recently I started drinking spring water to get away from fluoridation. At the warehouse club I buy spring water for about 85 cents per gallon. Look around ... you probably can find an economical source of spring or RO water.

All a learning process.

I have not had Ratio testing yet but feel dehydrated. Conventional doc used a urine test strip (testing specific gravity - i think?) and said based on that I was not dehydrated.. I'm drinking AquaSana brand home RO filered water from kitchen tap and some expensive Icelandic water in heavier plastic bottles. I hate plastic but shipping rates make that unreasonable now. And the cheap water at the clubs normally come that that paper thin plastic bottles-- that can't be good too much BPA, and if 'BPA- free' the worst subtitutes BPS or BPF are most likely what was used. Now that I think about it that Extremely expensive DDW Dr. Kruse has recommended comes in that paper thin plastic bottles as well.
I try to avoid even 1 drop of tap water but I bathe and shower in tap. I do use a shower filter but it does not take flouride out. I heard adding powdered Vit C to bath would off set fluorider... but I'm not sure that is true. '

Find a Spring' can help locate springs all over the country. One could then run water through a Berkey while not ideal it might work for some. Where I am in central VA my organic grocer (a weight lifter) had to take is two strong sons to help him retrieve water in big buckets using a very long rope from a deep well at the spring closest to us.

Maybe rainwater or seawater?.

Going to rebound and Keep Moving.
All back to seeking the truth and how to apply it.

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2013-04-12T01:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenager-17-y-o-who-has-eaten-only-ramen-since-she-was-5.6737/post-83823>

[http://shine.yahoo.com/healthy-living/teen-s-strange-ramen-addiction--165713286.html](http://shine.yahoo.com/healthy-living/teen-s-strange-ramen-addiction--165713286.html)

"Readman could not be reached for comment but according to her doctors, she is malnourished and has the health of an 80 year old.

"That sounds like an accurate assessment," says Lisa Kaufman, a pediatrician at Village Pediatrics who has not treated Readman. "A diet of instant noodles has likely wreaked incredible amounts of havoc on her organs. The bodyâ€”especially one that's still developingâ€”needs protein, minerals, and nutrients to grow; that's just basic common sense. Without it, this girl has probably suffered stunted growth and IQ, osteoporosis, heart and kidney damage, and high blood pressure. Her lifespan has likely been shortened as well."

### Dextery — 2013-04-12T06:15:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenager-17-y-o-who-has-eaten-only-ramen-since-she-was-5.6737/post-83864>

Reminds me of this vegan story of a 12 year old vegan who has the bones of an unhealthy 80 year old.

[http://www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com/12-year-old-vegan-has-the-degenerating-bones-of-80-year-old/](http://www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com/12-year-old-vegan-has-the-degenerating-bones-of-80-year-old/)

A 12 year old girl raised on a strict vegan diet was admitted to the Royal Hospital for Sick Children in Glasgow, Scotland, suffering from a severe form of rickets. The girl had already experienced multiple fractures and been diagnosed with a degenerated spine comparable to that of an unhealthy 80 year old.

# Thread: Adrenal Fatigue? Suggestions?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-04-12T17:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-83899>

Cortisol
13.5 8:45am (7.0-10.0)
4.5 1:45pm (3.0-6.0)
3.8 6:15pm (2.0-4.0)
3.5 8:45 pm (<1.5)
DHEA Saliva 246.4 8:45am (57-615)

GABA uMol/gCR 4.8 (2.4-12.7)
Taurine <40.0 (52-1025)
Glycine 198.5 (182-2225)
Creatinine 38.6mg/dl (28-259)

These are from January... how long does it take to turn this around? How often should one test?

Thanks!

### Vicster — 2013-04-12T18:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-83907>

Meghan, for everyone it is different, but as a former marathoner and triathlete I know for a fact you can't have it both ways. With the way you are training you will not get rid of adrenal fatigue. It was hard for me to give up the training and especially the competition, but it was becoming a struggle. It has taken me over a year to start feeling better and rested and I would not trade that for another chance to race. You have to decide what is more important to you, feeling good and having a long life or overtraining to a shorter life and a medal that will just collect dust in your home.

Giving up all the hours of training and racing has given me the chance to spend more quality time with family and friends, relaxing and enjoying life. It really is so much better!

### MeghanK — 2013-04-12T20:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-83923>

Vicster,
Thank you so much for sharing your experience... it is SO HARD.... I am caught between the addiction to the competition and runners high and true health... I built my identity around being a coach and endurance athlete so its so challenging to let go... but my body is telling me I have too....
What stage of adrenal fatigue were you in? What stage do these #s represent? not sure my dr. understands it... need to find a new dr....

### MeghanK — 2013-04-12T20:45:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-83924>

Would love to hear other endurance athlete junkies stories....
I don't know how Barry does it.... I think he is truly an N=1

### jenaf — 2013-04-12T21:00:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-83927>

hi Meghan,

I'm a strength coach and was training for the RKC when I fried my adrenal hormone axis... after 6 months of being frustrated with not knowing why i was so tired and super dizzy when ever i trained I finally had my hormones tested - everything was flatlined - I was in adrenal exhaustion, or stage 4 fatigue. I've been on the max dose of isocort withthe help of my naturopath since novemeber and slowly i've implemented a lot of these rx into my life. It hasn't been until the last 6 weeks when I shut off the breaker to my bedroom and the wifi at night as well as really been strict with epi-paleo/leptin rx (i can't do ANY sugar/carbs at all) that I've started to feel really good. I recently flew to europe and I had no jetlag because i ct'd/got electrons by standing in the ocean for 45 mins as soon as i got home. I drink 2-4 liters of spring water a day and wear my amber glasses at night. It has made such an amazing difference. for the first time this morning I went on a hour long hike and felt great, no dizziness or tiredness
The lack of training has been very very frustrating, but the sooner you embrace it and focus on sleeping and eating right, no emf etc the sooner you'll feel better. I still have to implement CT more. My goal now is to do it everyday. I hear that it can really help as well but its something I'm working on implementing more habitually.
I'm now going to train - lightly (20 mins hiit style) for a kettlebell course in july. I'll keep you up dated.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-83933>

Hey Jena,
Thanks so much for sharing! I currently live with the parents.. I'm 31, I know it's sad... hoping to move out in about a month so turning the breaker and wifi off might freak them out... they already think I'm crazy... I definitely have to get the spring water.... I didn't realize my PUR filter wasn't doing anything.... keep me updated...

I'm 29  Live with my mom, sister, and a brother who all don't care to try to eat Epi-Paleo or any of the other stuff. Its not easy for sure. I've been on a binge/eat well cycle for a few weeks now. My lowest weight was 239, but I've never had a ton of energy. I obviously have multiple problems to sort out and its certainly not easy in this environment.

There is no way I could support myself with a job at Walgreens paying 10.23 an hour though. I could perhaps if I worked another job, but moving out and eating better while doing more work seems like it would be a negative.

Have you read The Iodine Crisis, Iodine by David Brownstein and/or Stop The Thyroid Madness yet? All deal with the Thyroid/Adrenals and more. I've read the last two and they were great and am currently in the process of reading The Iodine Crisis which has the forward written by Brownstein. From what I've heard and with that endorsement by Brownstein I'm sure its great. Stopthethyroidmadness.com has a wealth of information for anyone that may not want to buy the book at this moment. They discuss the temperature tests you can do to see if you have Thyroid/Adrenal issues and many other important things. Very good information.

Also if you haven't already looked at all the information on Grizz's Iodine references you should check it out.

[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

Included is a wealth of information on Iodine supplementation including details on possible detox or negative reactions, co-supplements, why it may be important to supplement etc...You probably have read some of that since I noticed you commented in that thread...just not sure to what extent and someone that may not have might stumble on this thread so I figured I'd include it.

If you get plenty of Iodine along with the required co-supplements which are all found in the Epi-Paleo diet it should help your body heal the Thyroid/Adrenal issues and deal with the issue of water that isn't ideal if you can't drink good water all the time for some reason.
