### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-14T18:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-suggestions.6743/post-84068>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=84065)
>
> I have had one done..... in Feb or March forget exact date.... but it was 58 .... range 34-523 (which seems to me to be a HUGE range)... but not sure if that is still deficient even thought I'm in range....

That sounds like a one time value. There is a urine spot test which is a one time test and a 24 hour urine loading test which I would think is given with just one value considering its a test where they test urine samples over a 24 hour period which would be multiple ones.

I've never gotten one but it sounds like one you got was a single time urine spot which isn't the best way to determine sufficiency at least that's what David Brownstein was saying if I understood correctly.

If that is a normal urine spot test I've noticed in most tests with a wide range that a good level is usually somewhere in the middle though I'm not sure on this one. Brownstein may talk about this in the book...I'll see if I can find that.

The 24 hour urine loading test I linked to is supposed to get urine for a full 24 hours and I would think the result would mention how much of a percentage Iodine was excreted. That's what Dr. Brownstein says over and over is that the amount of Urine excreted with the 24 hour collection should end up being 90% of the dose of Iodoral that was taken. I think it was also mentioned in his book and perhaps STTM that you should stop Iodine for some amount of time before doing a test. I'm sure you did that with whatever test it is you got just pointing it out.

I think he details why a one time urine spot test isn't as good as a 24 hour, but I can't remember specifically why though it would make sense that its partly more data makes it clearer how deficient you are. Basically a one urine spot test isn't enough information to determine how deficient someone is. I'll reread it see if I can quote the important stuff.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Earthing research
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-research.6745/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2013-04-12T17:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-research.6745/post-83901>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22291721](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22291721)

[J Environ Public Health.](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22291721#) 2012;2012:291541. doi: 10.1155/2012/291541. Epub 2012 Jan 12.
\[h=1\]Earthing: health implications of reconnecting the human body to the Earth's surface electrons.\[/h\][Chevalier G](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Chevalier%20G%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22291721), [Sinatra ST](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Sinatra%20ST%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22291721), [Oschman JL](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Oschman%20JL%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22291721), [Sokal K](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Sokal%20K%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22291721), [Sokal P](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed?term=Sokal%20P%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=22291721).
\[h=3\]Source\[/h\]Developmental and Cell Biology Department, University of California at Irvine, Irvine, CA 92697, USA. [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

\[h=3\]Abstract\[/h\]Environmental medicine generally addresses environmental factors with a negative impact on human health. However, emerging scientific research has revealed a surprisingly positive and overlooked environmental factor on health: direct physical contact with the vast supply of electrons on the surface of the Earth. Modern lifestyle separates humans from such contact. The research suggests that this disconnect may be a major contributor to physiological dysfunction and unwellness. Reconnection with the Earth's electrons has been found to promote intriguing physiological changes and subjective reports of well-being. Earthing (or grounding) refers to the discovery of benefits-including better sleep and reduced pain-from walking barefoot outside or sitting, working, or sleeping indoors connected to conductive systems that transfer the Earth's electrons from the ground into the body. This paper reviews the earthing research and the potential of earthing as a simple and easily accessed global modality of significant clinical importance.

### Team Kruse — 2013-04-12T18:31:50+0300

### nonchalant — 2013-04-12T21:07:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-83932>

Thanks for posting! I have done this, and it definitely works. Makes me wonder why laptops aren't already grounded.

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-06T16:39:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85514>

I would like to try this, but surely this is for laptops only and when they are not plugged in at the wall socket?
Otherwise with 2 sources of power I would fry my computer?
Also all our plugs have 3 prongs one being the "earth" (we also have 220v)
As you notice I am not technical but really would like to ground my computer as I spend so much time behind it.
Please those of you who did it, explain this to me in more detail.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-06T17:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85519>

I ground my laptop while it is also plugged into the wall socket. My laptop, however, only uses 2 prongs for power, no ground.
The grounding cord Michael showed has three prongs, but only the ground is attached to the wire. The other 2 prongs go nowhere. They are used only to anchor the plug in the socket.

I used my Trimeter during the initial process. I had quite high electrical and magnetic readings before grounding. Now it is very low.

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-07T12:23:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85561>

Thanks for the reply, but I am still not sure if this means that having a 3 prong plug I do not need to go through the process and that I am safe for excessive EMF?

### caroline — 2013-05-08T03:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85609>

Bugsy - I have 3 prong plugs as well - so the third one is the ground ...but when I asked one of the owners here they told me that they aren't actually hooked up and I would need to hire an electrician - just something to think about....

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-08T11:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85622>

The houses here are all wired with a third wire the "earth" so I presume I am OK.
I phoned a computer supplier and spoke to a "technician" and would you believe, he could not answer me and did not know about EMF radiation!!!
That tells you a lot about the collective knowledge in SA!!!

### beach bunny — 2013-05-08T16:41:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85633>

Bugsy, during the EMF webinar Michael explained that even though our outlets have the 3 prongs (so ideally would be grounded) sometimes we cannot be sure without testing each outlet. Unfortunately, this is the result of poor wiring in homes which contributes to "dirty electricity".

If I recall correctly Michael also recommends unplugging your laptop's regular power chord while you're working on your lap top.
So having the **grounding cable** plugged in while you're working to reduce the stuff it may be conducting onto your skin.
And then plugging in the regular power cable when you're not next to it (to charge it).

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-08T17:21:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85639>

Bugsy, if I understand you correctly, you're using a desktop computer and not a laptop. So unless your cabling is faulty, no further grounding should be necessary.

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-08T18:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85646>

Thank you both, I am using my laptop plugged in the regular power which I presume is earthed, I asked hubby what the inside of our wall plugs looked like and he said everything in our house has 3 cord wiring. So if I understand correctly the earthing of the laptop is only necessary when using it wireless? In that case I assume I am protected when it is plugged in at the wall.

### Optimalbound — 2013-05-09T14:05:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85725>

Your "brick" laptop power supply is grounded, but if you look at the cable going from the power supply to your laptop, you will see that it only has 2 wires in it. So the laptop itself is _NOT_ grounded in your current configuration, as I understand it.

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-11T12:28:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85835>

So no household appliances are grounded if I understand you? they all have only two wires in their cords. Then can someone explain to me the function of the earth wire in the wall plugs of your house, if nothing is actually grounded?
Sorry to be so insistant but I need to get to the bottom of all this, at this stage I simply don't understand.'

### Martin — 2013-05-11T15:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-85836>

The wall outlets let you plug in both types of appliances - grounded and un-grounded. The difference in those are that a while back manufacturer's figured out ways to build a device that is very insulated against having a human touch any part that would shock them. So they could build it simpler and cheaper and still provide you with protection. You have many of these it appears. We all do. I don't have a gauss meter so I can't give you comparisons on grounded vs. un, but that's great question. You home wiring has provisions for both.

### BJK77 — 2013-08-29T17:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-94173>

I bought the alligator clip and stripped a USB plug so I can ground my laptop. I measured using my trifield both before and after grounding it and I don't see any change at all. Has anyone found this to actually help? I did test the outlet and it's grounded.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-29T18:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-94192>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=94173)
>
> I bought the alligator clip and stripped a USB plug so I can ground my laptop. I measured using my trifield both before and after grounding it and I don't see any change at all. Has anyone found this to actually help? I did test the outlet and it's grounded.

Yes, my trifield showed a dramatic decrease after attaching the ground to the USB port. Your results are surprising. I wonder if your USB port is active? Not sure if it would make a difference.
Perhaps plug in a USB mouse or whatever, pull it out slightly, and touch a grounded wire to it, making sure the USB mouse works??
Have you tried grounding other things with your grounding wire? Does your body voltage drop when you touch the wire?

### nonchalant — 2013-10-03T18:20:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-96800>

I'm having second thoughts about my grounding pad that I use while on my USB-grounded laptop computer. I've had some inflammation the past couple weeks in my elbow which is usually in close proximity to the laptop. Ice made it better, of course, but it still persisted. It was getting worse and worse, until I finally guessed that it may be EMF. Sorry Dr. K, I'm still slow about that. Though my laptop is grounded through the usb port, could electricity still be jumping to that elbow? The laptop is grounded thru my house ground, and my grounding pad (that I keep my feet on) is grounded to the soil. The house ground has a longer distance to go before it gets to ground....

I moved the computer to a different place yesterday, and my elbow seems better already. I swear, I avoided touching my elbow to the laptop at all times! Maybe I should attach a usb hub so that I can have a keyboard as well as a mouse attached. How to ditch technology and still keep up with Dr. K.... hmmm...

### caroline — 2013-10-04T01:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-96834>

exactly right .......how do we keep up with Dr. K. and stay off the computer.....

I don't know much about this .....but dirty electricity is a big problem ...is it not?

### James Lech — 2018-07-05T16:33:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-your-computer-with-michael-neuert.6747/post-245018>

Hi nonChalant

The same application would apply to your other appliances such as your TV. For example, I got a new android TV recently. The purpose of the Android was so that I could install the app so that the picture is changed to a red instead of blue.

Anyways, The wiring in our place is really bad and the developer is refusing to fix it, hence legal action on them and us moving soon. On the downside, the TV being a US plug design, doesn't also have a built in ground. As a result, the electric field on the meter extended over 2 meters. Once I installed the USB ground to the earth plug, the field drastically dropped.

Regarding your laptop, also what can occur:
1\. Check that your AC cable is unplugged from the wall.
2\. Sometimes if your battery is old and faulty or if graphics card faults due to excessive heat, there is an internal issue that results in the unstable electric field.

# Thread: Need Advice - Looking for a new bed...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-looking-for-a-new-bed.6765/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-13T15:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-advice-looking-for-a-new-bed.6765/post-83985>

Hi guys,

I am going to be in the market for a new bed soon. I currently have a Sterns and Foster inner spring mattress. It is only 2 years old, but has major body impressions that are causing me a lot of pain in my hips. When I recently I started waking up with numbness in my hip, pelvis, and inner thigh I finally decided that enough was enough!

I suspect that the problem is compounded by the fact that I'm obese (5'5" @ ~200 pounds) and I carry a lot of weight in my hips and belly. As a result, the bed tends to cave in and not provide support where its needed most. I realize that getting an extra firm mattress might mitigate that issue somewhat, but it causes other problems. I actually tried a firmer mattress prior to getting my current one, but had to return it because I was in so much pain. It provided support, but I woke up every morning feeling like I'd been beaten up.

...So I think I am done with traditional innerspring mattresses. Basically, I am looking for an alternative that is is capable of relieving pressure on joints but also has adequate support in the belly / hip region.

As far as I can tell my options are:

1) memory foam - Seems like it could have the same type of problem (i.e. caving in and breaking down in the region where more pressure is applied)... I have a foam topper that definitely had this problem, but I've never had an entirely foam bed. Also, there is the issue of chemical off-gassing. EMF should, however, be minimal since there isn't any metal in the bed.

2) air bed - I like the idea of adjust-ability and I think they do make air beds with multiple chambers so you could independently adjust the hip region to be a bit firmer without compromising the comfort of the entire bed. I am, however, concerned because the padding layers might still break down and form body impressions.

3) water bed - Again, it seems like it would have more adjust-ability because you can add and remove water to change the level of support. Water is also good at conforming to the body and relieving pressure and water beds tend to have less padding layers to break down and form impressions. Some of the wave-less models look like they might be decent options. I realize that EMF could be an issue if I used a heater, but I don't plan to heat the bed.

I would really appreciate any advice or recommendations. I realize that there may not be a perfectly optimal solution, but hopefully I can get close

Thanks,
Trinity


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Trinity — 2013-04-13T15:29:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-search-of-an-optimal-bed.6766/post-83987>

Hi guys,

I am going to be in the market for a new bed soon. I currently have a Sterns and Foster inner spring mattress. It is only 2 years old, but has major body impressions that are causing me a lot of pain in my hips. When I recently I started waking up with numbness in my hip, pelvis, and inner thigh I finally decided that enough was enough!

I suspect that the problem is compounded by the fact that I'm obese (5'5" @ ~200 pounds) and I carry a lot of weight in my hips and belly. As a result, the bed tends to cave in and not provide support where its needed most. I realize that getting an extra firm mattress might mitigate that issue somewhat, but it causes other problems. I actually tried a firmer mattress prior to getting my current one, but had to return it because I was in so much pain. It provided support, but I woke up every morning feeling like I'd been beaten up.

...So I think I am done with traditional innerspring mattresses. Basically, I am looking for an alternative that is is capable of relieving pressure on joints but also has adequate support in the belly / hip region.

As far as I can tell my options are:

1) memory foam - Seems like it could have the same type of problem (i.e. caving in and breaking down in the region where more pressure is applied)... I have a foam topper that definitely had this problem, but I've never had an entirely foam bed. Also, there is the issue of chemical off-gassing. EMF should, however, be minimal since there isn't any metal in the bed.

2) air bed - I like the idea of adjust-ability and I think they do make air beds with multiple chambers so you could independently adjust the hip region to be a bit firmer without compromising the comfort of the entire bed. I am, however, concerned because the padding layers might still break down and form body impressions.

3) water bed - Again, it seems like it would have more adjust-ability because you can add and remove water to change the level of support. Water is also good at conforming to the body and relieving pressure and water beds tend to have less padding layers to break down and form impressions. Some of the wave-less models look like they might be decent options. I realize that EMF could be an issue if I used a heater, but I don't plan to heat the bed.

I would really appreciate any advice or recommendations. I realize that there may not be a perfectly optimal solution, but hopefully I can get close

Thanks,
Trinity

P.S. About half of my attempts to post never seem to show up on the forum, even days later. I don't know whats going on so if this gets posted twice, that's why.

Gretchen,
I was researching Iodine and I stumbled upon this.. I thought it might be interesting for you? IDK if you already do the Iodine protocol?
This man tested it and he's allergies and asthma disappeared! I think it was super intresting
Here is the link;

[http://betweenthetemples.com/2011/08/01/iodine-supplementation/](http://betweenthetemples.com/2011/08/01/iodine-supplementation/)

That is what unexpected benefits he got;

> While using Iodoral, I noticed the following unexpected benefits above and beyond what I found in my research:
>
> Allergies subsided – I had horrendous allergies, sinus infections exacerbated by certain foods, working outside, and changes in the season. Imagine my surprise after being on iodine for a few weeks when I went outside to mow and finished without a single sneeze. Further my eyes were not red or watering, and I was not congested. No medications or other treatments did this.
>
> Asthma disappeared – Shortly after beginning my iodine supplementation, asthma attacks associated with yard work or exercise and even foods vanished. I did not find anyone mentioning this when I first did the research on iodine. Since then I have found some interesting tid-bits.
>
> Able to tan – This one is hard to explain. Previous to taking iodine I could not tan easily and could not do so without burning. While taking iodine, about my third month I started to be able to go outside for extended periods of time without sun block and tan very nicely without burning. I cannot find anything on iodine that would explain this. I was not taking any other substances that might cause other effects. I even pushed the envelope I spent the day on a beach in Corpus Christi, TX during the month of July without sunblock without ill effect, just a darker tan. My wife who was taking Iodoral with me also had the same effect. Please do not assume that this will work for you, this is what I noted. I don’t know if iodine works like an internal sunblock and cannot prove otherwise beyond my personal experiences. If y

(I just started that protocol 2 days ago myself just to test.. I started quite heavy with 6 drops 7% Lugol's.. lets see what will happend! I have yellow breasts too now.. LOL I painted them today morning, I thought it cannot hurt..)

I'm not certain its that clear cut, but I don't think Fluoride sticks around when there is plenty of Iodine. Dr. Brownstein mentions several times in his book Iodine that Fluoride excretion levels in addition to bromide go up substantially when supplementing Iodine. I'm not sure if that is just Fluoride/Bromide that are being taken it at the same time or if it makes you get rid of the stuff this is already there as well.

My guess is it aids in doing both, but I am not absolutely certain. If someone has some good articles on this or can clarify that would be great. He talks about Iodine being used in all the cells of the body and if that's the case than if there is enough Iodine to replace the Fluoride than I think Iodine might displace all the Fluoride and remove it given enough Iodine and time.

# Thread: Blood sugar clarifications
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-clarifications.6776/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-14T22:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-clarifications.6776/post-84085>

Can anyone point to a good article or page detailing blood sugar spikes and what is acceptable? I've read numerous times different criteria, but can't remember ...my memory could use some help...anyway something I can bookmark for later would be great.

My blood sugar went up 18 points in an hour after drinking Coffee with Grass-fed butter, coconut oil, cocoa butter, cocoa powder, and one tablespoon of honey. I'm thinking at this point I probably shouldn't do the honey, but I'm not sure if it 18 points in an hour is too much.

I should probably also test the cocoa powder without the honey as it doesn't have any sugar on the label, but says it has 18 grams of carbohydrates with 7 grams of fiber. That would mean there are 11 grams of starch? Starch could spike blood sugar. I'm thinking the cocoa powder should be fine though as I'm not using an ounce which is the serving size. I used about 2 teaspoons. I guess that would be about 3.63 grams of starches.

Was doing BPC coffee basically with Upgraded Coffee, Cocoa Powder, and Coco Butter. Tropical Traditions Coconut Oil instead of MCT because I don't have any of that and Humboldt grass-fed butter since the last two times I went to Sprouts they were out of unsalted Kerrygold...I think they have a supply issue with Kerrygold.

### Dextery — 2013-04-15T03:42:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-clarifications.6776/post-84121>

Take a look at this one. [http://www.bloodsugar101.com/](http://www.bloodsugar101.com/)

Testing 2 hours after eating a food should bring the BS down to within 10 to 15 of the reading prior to eating.

### Dextery — 2013-04-19T02:06:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-sugar-clarifications.6776/post-84456>

In a follow up on controling blood sugar, this video outlines how cinnamon can be used. One problem, there are two types of cinnamon: cassia and ceylon. The cassia can control blood sugar somewhat but contains coumarin an active ingredient that can be toxic to the liver if too much is ingested. The ceylon cinnamon does not contain coumarin but it also is ineffective in blood sugar control.

What to do? Perhaps small of amounts of the cassia cinnamon is helpful. Know your cinnamon. Me asks...what's in those cinnamon pills we see in the stores?

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=BhZrco86qNs](http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=BhZrco86qNs)

>
> I should probably also test the cocoa powder without the honey as it doesn't have any sugar on the label, but says it has 18 grams of carbohydrates with 7 grams of fiber. That would mean there are 11 grams of starch? Starch could spike blood sugar. I'm thinking the cocoa powder should be fine though as I'm not using an ounce which is the serving size. I used about 2 teaspoons. I guess that would be about 3.63 grams of starches.
>
> Was doing BPC coffee basically with Upgraded Coffee, Cocoa Powder, and Coco Butter. Tropical Traditions Coconut Oil instead of MCT because I don't have any of that and Humboldt grass-fed butter since the last two times I went to Sprouts they were out of unsalted Kerrygold...I think they have a supply issue with Kerrygold.

See, I don't think honey can really put up your blood sugar level by 18 points, as a diabetic patient I used to avoid sugar products to eat but my family doctor itself recommended me to use "honey as sweetener" in the food, since I love to eat sweet products even if I'm having diabetes and you won't believe I'm using honey since 1 year and I haven't found any kind of problem yet

# Thread: Inexpensive EMF meters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inexpensive-emf-meters.6778/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JedEye — 2013-04-14T22:44:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inexpensive-emf-meters.6778/post-84090>

Are there reasonably sufficient EMF meters for 30-50 bucks?

### endless — 2013-04-14T23:57:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inexpensive-emf-meters.6778/post-84095>

According to Michael Neuert who is doing the EMF bootcamp series, no. That being said, I know some here have bought cheap meters. My fear is that you would not be able to trust them.

### Martin — 2013-04-15T00:37:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inexpensive-emf-meters.6778/post-84103>

No way in that price range. Not nearly sensitive enough.

It seems to me that the expense of an accurate meter comes from the quality of the parts inside, of course, but it has to be able to differentiate the many fields present sufficiently to give you an accurate reading.

That accuracy is calibrated at the factory by using all kinds of very specialized and expensive equipment and that drives the price up.

I looked at an ebay 3 axis meter at around half the price of what I've seen rec'd, but the accuracy was no where near the more expensive ones.

### HeidiHen — 2013-04-15T20:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inexpensive-emf-meters.6778/post-84171>

Can anyone post a link for a good quality, yet reasonably priced, EMF meter? What is everyone using?

### nonchalant — 2013-04-16T00:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inexpensive-emf-meters.6778/post-84177>

I found my Trifield used, on the Amazon site. Still expensive (for me) but it is in very good shape. Don't know if it is accurate, but it tells me things like my home thermostat and a dimmer switch are going to have to go.

# Thread: Newbie here with bloodwork - please help me!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justmisunderstood — 2013-04-15T03:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-84120>

Hello!
I am a 33-year-old female, 5'5", 170 pounds. I have a horrible quality of life that I want to improve. My main motivation is that I want to get pregnant as soon as possible, but not before obtaining optimal health. I need advice! Please help me!

Issues:
(For the last 8 years): muscle/joint pain; pinched nerves; extreme stiffness upon waking; muscle weakness; complete lack of stamina; run out of energy quickly; constant fatigue; always freezing cold hands & feet; intolerance to cold; periodic night sweats; periodic insomnia; bad acid reflux; constipation or diarrhea; excessive thirst/dry mouth; excessive phlegm; acne; irregular cycles (31-47 days); dry itchy skin; never feel rested (sleeping 11-12 hrs a night without an alarm). Waking up with less than 9-10 hours of sleep feels exactly like trying to come out of anesthesia, I will be nauseated for several hours, and I will be sleepy all day and need a nap at some point. Some days I awake to find my body feeling like I was hit by a truck.

(Since going Paleo - 3 months): always hungry, even after eating; blurry vision/headaches/shaking between meals; foggy brain; feeling like I'm going to faint; nausea; dizziness when standing; tinnitus; rollercoaster moods; mid to late-cycle spotting/bleeding; migraines for 3 days before period; crawling sensations on my skin; weight-gain; energy is better but still too tired to exercise; still struggling with the energy/stamina to prepare meals

My recent bloodwork:
Cardio CRP: 0.6 (Ref <1.0)
Hemoglobin A1C: 5.1 (Ref <5.7)
Vitamin D: 33 (Ref 30-100) \*low (this is with 8000 IU/day for 3 months)
TSH: 6.34 (Ref .40-4.50) \*high
Free T4: 1.1 (Ref 0.8-1.8)
Free T3: 3.0 (Ref 2.3-4.2)
Reverse T3: 20 (Ref 7-24)
DHEA: 1193 (Ref 102-1185) \*high
FSH: 4.9 (Ref 1.9-9.1)
LH: 5.2 (Ref 0.5-16.9)
Fasting Insulin: 11 (Ref <10) \*high
Fasting AM Cortisol: 28.4 (Ref 4.0-22.0) \*high
LDL-C: 136 (Ref <100) \*high
Triglycerides: 156 (Ref <100) \*high
HDL-C: 50 (Ref 60-80) \*low
(Heavy Metals Test: normal)

What's going on here? The Paleo diet alone is apparently not enough to "cure" me.

\>I need to lose weight, but I'm still too tired to exercise, and even mild exertion will result in extreme pain and stiffness the next day. It takes a long time to recover. If I eat very little food, I do not lose weight, but if I eat 3 "normal" meals, I gain weight.
\>I don't understand why my cortisol is still so high, since I've eliminated most of my stress.
\>I can't seem to get my blood sugar normalized, no matter what I eat. Before going Paleo, I could go all day without eating. Now it seems I can't go 2 hours before getting uncomfortable symptoms.
\>I need to get my adrenal, thyroid, and sex hormones normalized, but I don't know what to do.

Here's some background, in case it helps...

When I was about 8 years old, I fell from a high sliding board and broke both of my wrists; concussion was suspected but never confirmed. (I never considered this relevant until I read one of Jack's articles about head trauma...)

At some point around puberty, I started sleeping a lot. I was always sleepy, no matter how long I slept. I woke up tired; I went to bed tired. I could fall asleep in the middle of a loud party. I became known as the girl who was "always tired" or "always sleeping in the corner". With the earlier and earlier school times, it got worse, and I was late every morning because I could not be woken up. I would sleep right through alarms. My mother would pour cold water in my face, and drag me onto the floor, only to find me asleep again. I became grumpy and angry all the time because I was so tired. All I wanted to do was sleep. My parents thought I was just lazy, and gaining too much weight, so they made me exercise after school for a few years. Although I love exercise, it only made me more tired, I never lost any weight, and I would often get home and collapse into bed until the next morning.

I also developed bad acne, and was prescribed antibiotics for several years (which did nothing).

Around age 16, I caught mononucleosis and was told I would experience extreme fatigue. I remember thinking, "this is how I always feel". It was then that I realized something was wrong with me. I spent the next several years going to traditional and "alternative" doctors, only to be laughed at or told there was "nothing wrong" with me, or that I was "just depressed". I tried Prozac for a year, and it did nothing for the fatigue, so I stopped. I completely lost faith in the medical system and doctors in general. I started working full-time at 18, and survived on (very strong) willpower and up to 10 cups of coffee a day, right up until bed, and had no trouble sleeping.

(I now believe that I have narcolepsy, as it runs in my family, and I have enough symptoms that I have been "diagnosed" verbally by doctors. An "official" diagnosis requires a 2-day sleep study, which I have attempted, but was too worked-up to sleep.)

Around age 25, at my highest weight of 180, I entered a competition to lose weight. I spent 6 months dieting and exercising and lost 35 pounds.

From about 26-29, I experienced an intense bout of stress at work, which slowly eroded my health even further. Every 3-6 months I would need my glasses increased in strength. I started having panic attacks, and (what I would later discover as) gallstone attacks. My menstrual cycles became irregular, ranging from 30 to 50 days long. By the end, I would shake uncontrollably at any sign of conflict, cry constantly, have headaches daily, and lost my libido. I developed a large rash across my back for several weeks, treated with prednisone (didn't work), which eventually went away on it's own. I gained back most of the weight I had lost.

I am now 33, and I have not worked since, because I am afraid for my health. I (regrettably) had my gallbladder removed. I've had a dozen mercury fillings removed. I've eliminated most stress from my life, and sleep 12 hours a night. I've tried dozens of supplements. I believed that my body would "fix" itself eventually, but it hasn't. I've spent the last 3 years researching, reading, and documenting everything I can find on "health". I'm overwhelmed, and feeling like no one really has the answers. I'm tired of being a guinea pig. I'm tired of trying things that don't work. I'm tired of putting my life on hold "until I feel better". At this point, I'd be happy with enough energy to feed myself 3 times a day and keep my house clean.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-16T01:32:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-84183>

I would suggest making sure you are doing the Leptin Rx Epi-Paleo style. Get that BAB in within 30 min of waking. It took me almost 9 months of this program before I started to feel more energy. CT will help with energy as well.

It appears you have some evidence of a thyroid problem. Your TSH is too high and your FT3 and FT4 are low. Plug your number into this calculator and see where your rT3 ratio is. Best is above 20....if below you have a problem. This could be a contributor to your energy issues.

Vit D is way too low. I had to take 20,000 just to get my level up to normal. It looks like 8,000 is not enough for you. Bump that up ASAP to a level that you are comfortable with.

You need to get an ASI with melatonin saliva test done. This will tell you if you are experiencing some adrenal challenges as well. Other testing you could consider: hormones,

Read, read, read the blogs here. There is a lot of info (don't forget the comments too) and you need to educate yourself so you can slowly start to put the pieces together and make the changes to help you feel better. This is just the beginning of a fun journey! Remember it took 33 years for you to get to this place and it is going to take some time to get healthy again. I commend you on your decision to make your life better!

### judy32 — 2013-07-03T08:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-89428>

Hello all, I am Judy and I am new here. I have recently join this community to get in touch with the latest information and make discussion on issues related to different topics. I suggest you to Study, read, look at the weblogs here. There is a lot of details (don't ignore content too) and you need to inform yourself so you can gradually begin to put the items together and create the changes to help you experience better.

### caroline — 2013-07-03T10:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-with-bloodwork-please-help-me.6780/post-89434>

Welcome justmisunderstood ......Patty Cakes has great advise for you. I would also suggest you consider getting Dr. Kruse's book ....THE EPI-PALEO RX. \[from Amazon\] It is an easy way to understand all of this quickly and where Dr.K. is coming from. Also - if you can - listen to all the webinars - they are great.

There is a lot of reading for you to do - just start slowly and ask questions .... we are here to help....

Can we call you by your name? It is much nicer to have a positive image........

# Thread: Explore how human body reacts and adapts to cold
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explore-how-human-body-reacts-and-adapts-to-cold.6781/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2013-04-15T07:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/explore-how-human-body-reacts-and-adapts-to-cold.6781/post-84127>

Valery Malkov made the headlines when he fell out of a train in Siberia in -40 degrees Celsius wearing practically nothing, ran for 30 minutes to the next station and survived.
The man said that he had gone to the trainâ€™s vestibule for a smoke. As he was coming back to his carriage, he missed his door and opened another one to a non-functioning vestibule. He stepped into the dark â€“ and then found himself in taiga forest, on the railroad, all alone.
We explore how human body reacts and adapts to cold and meet enthusiasts who subject themselves to extreme temperatures.
Cold weather has a dramatic effect on human health. According to a University of California, Berkeley economist, deaths related to cold reduce the average life expectancy of Americans by a decade, if not more.
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=eAm3PA7x\_ek](http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_embedded&v=eAm3PA7x_ek)

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2013-04-16T00:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flooring-materials-and-grounding-in-a-home-addition.6787/post-84176>

putting travertine on concrete... should be conductive for a room floor. But what about the adhesives? Are there some that are conductive and others would be insulating?

edit: actually i probably just need cement based adhesive

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-16T02:37:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flooring-materials-and-grounding-in-a-home-addition.6787/post-84191>

We used Thin Set as the adhesive/leveler for our travertine floor. Usually a layer is put either on top the concrete (I think that is where) like a linoleum. This helps with movement so the travertine doesn't crack. We did ours w/o the linoleum. There are some spider cracks in a few tiles which probably wouldn't be there if we have the linoleum. I'll take the spider cracks in the few tiles in exchange for the grounding. Hope that helps!

# Thread: Berberine...is anyone taking it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cmegan — 2013-04-16T16:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84228>

Hi!

Has anyone taken (or is taking) berberine, and if so, what has your experience been?

Thank you!

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-17T22:05:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84356>

I take Glycosolve which has Berberine. Are you talking about Berberine in some other formulation or Glycosolve? I'm not surprised if there are others, but Glycosolve is the only supplement I personally know about with it.

Jimmy Moore has used to and mentions it working very well to help stabilize his blood sugars.

[http://livinlavidalowcarb.com/blog/n1](http://livinlavidalowcarb.com/blog/n1)

Read through the N1 Nutritional Ketosis articles they are a good read. Glycosolve/Berberine is also supposed to lower cholesterol. I'm not sure if that is a detriment or not as I know we need cholesterol. I don't remember if it lowers all cholesterol or LDL or what though.

I've been using it and it certainly works for the blood sugar for me as it did for Jimmy. I haven't taken any cholesterol tests to confirm what it does it regards to that, but my best guess is that it isn't a problem in regards to cholesterol.

There are known side effects of taking Metformin which either aren't an issue with Glycosolve/Berberine or perhaps because the Berberine's effect isn't as strong. I've heard Metformin works in a similar way but more so.

The side effects like stomach upset and some other issues might be related to that.

It seems like its a good supplement, but I'm not aware of possible negatives besides the side effects that might happen. They didn't happen with me or Jimmy, but that doesn't mean they don't with everyone.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-18T02:59:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84360>

As I understand it, the liver makes cholesterol on demand. If one has normal or lower cholesterol levels then perhaps the liver is receiving fewer requests to make it. Also I heard that only 10% of dietary cholesterol gets absorbed though I can't quote any studies on that. Wouldn't changing metabolic and hormonal demands alter cholesterol signaling?

### cmegan — 2013-04-18T18:31:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84415>

thank you both for your replies...I bought the glycosolve and am going to start again this week. I recently read Jimmy Moore's experiment on it which made me decide to take a chance. I took this last fall but I went off of it very quickly because of the constipation, and now thinking that a little bit of Mg+ will do the trick!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-19T04:19:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-is-anyone-taking-it.6793/post-84460>

Interesting. I hadn't heard of glycosolve. I am an herbalist, and I am familiar with the many different berberine-containing medicinal herbs out there. Thank you for the link to Jimmy Moore's N1. I look forward to reading through that information and seeing how people are using the extracted berberine as opposed to the traditional herbal method of utilizing a whole plant for its many biochemical properties. Here is a nice read on one popular berberine containing herb, Oregon Grape Root:

[http://www.methowvalleyherbs.com/2012/11/oregon-grape-root-it-could-save-world.html](http://www.methowvalleyherbs.com/2012/11/oregon-grape-root-it-could-save-world.html)

2- WHERE is the best place for ME to order them

TSH 3.69....... 0.30-5.60
T3 Total RIA 1.9...... 1.3-2.5
T4 free 11.1..... 7.2-21.0
Free T3 4.2....... 2.9-6.0
Vitamin B12 478...... >133
Ferritin 51...... 11-145
Cortisol AM 401..... 185-624

Vit D 82..... 75-200

Free
Testosterone 9.6...... 0-14.2
DHEA Sulphate 5.6....... <11.0
Homocysteine 4.9...... 5.1-15.4
IGF-1 273....... 117-329
hs CRP <0.2..... <1.0 Low Risk

>

>
> TSH 3.69....... 0.30-5.60 (aim at (0.5-1.5) using natural, pig, thyroid)
> T3 Total RIA 1.9...... 1.3-2.5
> T4 free 11.1..... 7.2-21.0
> Free T3 4.2....... 2.9-6.0
> Vitamin B12 478...... >133 (get it to a 1000)
> Ferritin 51...... 11-145 (ok)
> Cortisol AM 401..... 185-624 (get to the top range, 10% over), work on Circadian Rhythm, (work on preg & prog, should help with cortisol), progesterone should be very high to achieve prog/E~500 that dr K wants, or even 200 that dr Lam recommends)
> PTH 3.2..... 1.3-8.2
> Vit D 82..... 75-200 (get it to 100 or 150) but first double check your units, the 200 in your range is a flag
> Testosterone 1.6..... 0.4-2.6 (get DHEAs high, that should also raise testosterone)
> Free
> Testosterone 9.6...... 0-14.2
> DHEA Sulphate 5.6....... <11.0
> Homocysteine 4.9...... 5.1-15.4
> IGF-1 273....... 117-329
> hs CRP <0.2..... <1.0 Low Risk
> Thyroglobulin AB Negative..... Negative
> Microsomal AB Negative..... Negative
> Anti-Thyroid Peroxidas 15..... 5
> Cholesterol 7.15...... Below 4.20
> Triglycerides 0.51..... Target <1.71
> HDL 1.62...... Target>1.29
> LDL 5.30....... Moderate/High <2.0 or reduce by 50%
> CHOL:HDL 4.41
> **Absolute Lymph 3.4....... 1.1-1.3**
>
>

# Thread: Coconut oil Nutiva or Tropical Traditions...hmm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-17T21:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84353>

So I can get a 5 gallon of Nutiva or Coconut oil for maximum price savings. If I factor in shipping Tropical Traditions is 7 dollars cheaper. 199.80 for 5 gallons with 24 dollar shipping. Nutiva is 259 with a 10% discount and free shipping on amounts over 50 dollars. That comes out to 231.10.

Nutiva says its not fermented its organic and Jack has recommended it before. I've read that Tropical Traditions is fermented. Is this kind of fermentation good for coconut oil? Does that make it more healthy or less healthy.

Nutiva or Tropical Traditions..hmmm

Thanks for any and all input but please hurry sale ends tomorrow at least on the TT

### SeaJane — 2013-04-17T23:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84358>

Nutiva is by far my favorite coconut oil based on taste. I can't tell you if the fermenting is better for you or not.

### endless — 2013-04-18T04:58:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84366>

Tropical Traditions has a very good website that will tell you in great detail about how their different grades are processed....why not just go there and read up on it? I've always used TT and I think it's great, but I have no information or opinion on the fermenting thing....

### Dextery — 2013-04-18T05:13:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84367>

I get Vitacost.com private label coconut oil 54 oz for $21.49 each and freight prepaid with an order of $50 for any of their products they sell. I suspect Vitacost has Nutiva private lebel their product as I cannot taste the difference. Your delivered cost of 5 gal at 231.10 is .36 per oz and mine is .40 oz. My wife and I use 2 of the 54 oz containers in 6-7 weeks.

### RGP — 2013-04-18T14:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84374>

Can't beat Tropical Traditions for taste.

### kathylu — 2013-04-18T17:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84394>

Put yourself on the Tropical Traditions email list. They have a free shipping day the first week of every month. That's when I order from them.

Personally, I don't like the coconut taste of either Nutiva or TT. I use TT organic expeller pressed coconut oil. No smell or taste!

I do like Nutiva coconut manna though...very yummy.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-04-21T19:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84580>

### kathylu — 2013-04-22T18:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-nutiva-or-tropical-traditions-hmm.6805/post-84629>

I'm sure it's not as good as using totally unrefined oil, but I won't eat unrefined because I find the taste very off-putting. I guess we all have to decide what are compromises will be. I'm comfortable as long as the oil is not subjected to high temps or chemical extraction of any kind.

# Thread: Another Reason to Avoid Wi-Fi ... Top WiFi Routers Easy to Hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-17T23:02:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/post-84357>

[http://news.cnet.com/8301-1009\_3-57579981-83/top-wi-fi-routers-easy-to-hack-says-study/](http://news.cnet.com/8301-1009_3-57579981-83/top-wi-fi-routers-easy-to-hack-says-study/)

The Wi-Fi router you use to broadcast a private wireless Internet signal in your home or office is not only easy to hack, says a report released today, but the best way to protect yourself is out of your hands.

The report notes that all 14 of the devices had critical security vulnerabilities that could be exploited by a "remote adversary" and could lead to unauthorized remote control of the router.

Before you dismiss router hacks as exceptionally rare, it's important to note that they've been a small but growing segment of computer security threats.

Ted Harrington further explained why router hacking could turn into a big problem. "What's notable about this is that if you compromise the router, then you're inside the firewall. You can pick credit card numbers out of e-mails, confidential documents, passwords, photos, just about anything," he said.

### Martin — 2013-04-18T14:45:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/post-84376>

Good stuff, ATL. Hackers - they're out there all the time workin' it.

Gone wired myself.

### Optimalbound — 2013-04-18T17:26:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-reason-to-avoid-wi-fi-top-wifi-routers-easy-to-hack.6807/post-84399>

That's exactly why I've always used wired networking at home. I do have a wi-fi access point that I sometimes switch on when I have guests, but it sits _outside_ my firewall. And it runs on 25% signal strength.

# Thread: Feet swelling

### diane — 2013-04-18T15:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84379>

Hey all! I've been in NYC this week - the first time here in 6 years. It's a business trip so they are keeping us busy and lots of walking of course.

For the very first time my feet and ankles are so swollen they don't even look like my feet. I put them on the metal on the airplane as much as I could during the flight, drank a boatload of water. I haven't drank as much water since I've been here. Is it dehydration? Maybe related to takin the thyroid and progesterone? I'm sure it's a sign of bring disconnected but I have never experienced anything like this.

It's also been difficult on the diet, as all of our breakfasts and lunches are brought in. Which means wheat at pretty much every meal - and so hard to avoid.

Hope everyone is having a great week.

### Dali Dula — 2013-04-18T15:23:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84384>

Stomping around on concrete all day can be brutal and exhausting. When I do city stuff I have found that clogs work very well, but they may not be fashionable

### diane — 2013-04-18T15:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84386>

I actually found a pair of flats in the hotel shop with leather soles - regular $143 for $36!! I scooped them up!

Didn't pack any clogs lol - but yes the footwear hasn't been the most comfortable.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T15:33:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84388>

Diane

my n=1 for me says dehydration... push the CoQ10, drink, drink drink, and ground as much as possible - given you're in the middle of EMF soup. it can be done as I somehow manage to do it daily - it may not be ideal but I pull it off.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-04-18T16:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84391>

Assuming you are normally grain free, I would be thinking wheat, wheat, wheat. Can you eat more fat and protein and avoid the wheat??

My n=1 was that I lost a ton of inflammation all over my body when I dropped grains.

### cantweight — 2013-04-18T18:14:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84410>

I have been dealing with swollen feet and hands myself. I was thinking it was directly related to the heat for me.

### Rachelle — 2013-04-18T18:27:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84414>

For me it is always food like wheat and potatoes that give me swollen feet and hands. Since being off them totally I have no problem at all and I live also in an EMF sesspool.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-18T21:03:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84427>

Yep, it could be the EMF smog. Also, a close older relative tells me she tends to lose excess fluid when she takes the Iodoral (iodine) I gave her. Are you getting enough iodine and getting 8 or 9 hours sleep each night? You might try elevating the feet above the head to let your body reprocess the fluid that is collecting in the lower legs. Avoid flouridated and chlorinated drinking water and drink plenty of chilled spring/distilled/RO water. If you are magnesium deficient, you could supplement with Magnesium Glycinate. Are you getting enough vitamin D3? I don't trust the D2 (ergosterol).

### caroline — 2013-04-18T21:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84431>

Hi Diane! I love NYC......I have had the same problem the last few years when travelling. It has been really, really bad. In retrospect I think too ......wheat, wheat and wheat and MSG!!!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-18T22:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84436>

Micro-brewed beer is my absolute favorite beverage, and I succumb to indulging in it on occasion, after which my ankles always swell. I presume it's the carbs and the grains.

### diane — 2013-04-20T19:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84550>

Thanks everyone! Yes, I was taking Lugols before I left, but didn't take it with me. I get D3, and take mag malate each night (that I did take with me).

I first thought it was the flight - which really concerned me. But I got home yesterday, and could actually see the veins in my feet this morning. Which then rules out flying because I flew yesterday to get here. My ankles are still a little swollen - but holy cow I had cankles! And was wearing short pants - god why did I take those, if only I had known - so my big fat ankles were all out in the open.

Maybe it did have something to do with walking in improper shoes? Or maybe elevation (my hotel room was on the 47th floor, although the office was only on 22). Perhaps the alcohol, but I mainly drank red wine (other than the first night where I had a most delicious jalapeno margarita lol) - but I drink red wine at home occasionally too and don't get swelling.

It's so weird - but thanks for all your input! And I'm pretty relieved to see it going down today.

### Inger — 2013-04-20T21:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84555>

Oh.. Diane.. I am sorry you had big fat ankle issues! Sucks. I did not got swelling from flights so far me thinks. But I have never traveled more than 2 hours at once with a airplane so that might be why. Next time maybe try to drink lots of good water and take Q10 in larger amounts too and then some Iodine like you did. Maybe using compression socks would help too? And to try to hold your feet elevated on the flight if possible. I always try to do that and it works somehow, I use my bag or the chair in front of me to get my feet a bit higher.

47th floor...??? We do not have any buildings even close to that high here.. in the whole country! wow!

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-04-20T21:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84558>

I always swell a lot when I go back to Florida bc of the humidity. ESP when we go to the beach. The salty air makes my fingers fat but overall it feels amazing. I miss the beach!

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD

### diane — 2013-04-20T22:27:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-84559>

The flight is just over an hour - so not very long. i go to BC every year, which is a 5 hour flight, and I've never had that problem. It's so weird! But yes to the CoQ10 - I'm out so need to stock up (funny - thought I would be able to get some supplements in NY but had absolutely no time!).

### diane — 2013-05-04T21:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-85450>

So, this week has been pretty bad - not just my feet and ankles were swollen, but my calves were rock hard (no definition, couldn't see my calf muscles). My calves are kinda thick to begin with, but holy cow my legs were like stumps! Had some in the upper leg (above the knee) too. Funny - TOM started this week, so now I wonder if it's related to progesterone increasing then falling. My guess is that it's a clear sign I'm dehydrated.

I sent a message to my anti-aging doctor - she thinks it's the DHEA. I've been taking DHEA since last August with no issues. I started taking BHRT progesterone in April - I think it's related to that. But what do I know lol (ie. possibly more than my doctor??).

So I started hitting the CoQ10 and d-ribose harder. Then it struck me that a cold bath may help as well - so today it was into the bath. Definitely didn't feel as cold as it did in the winter! The good news is that my ankles are almost back to normal (little swollen) and my calves are pretty much back. Today I've been peeing like a race horse (my apologies for the visual) - have also been drinking water with lemon.

What I'm thinking is that this is an indication that my mitochondria are not functioning properly, so I need to still work on that while I take the BHRT. I don't know any of this for sure - just trying to tie some of the pieces together - the timing, what I've been doing - and it seems to correlate.

### diane — 2013-05-07T14:28:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-85562>

It's been 4 days and my feet are almost back to normal. Now I'm really wondering if it's related to my hormones and cycle, as they started swelling on Day 14, and then pretty much went down once TOM was over.

I'm pretty sure the cold bath helped - man I was peeing about every 15 minutes on Saturday! Think that was it all coming out. LOL. Will see what happens this cycle.

It's funny because when I looked down at my feet when they were swollen, I've seen that exact look before in many women. Also, wondering if people who are having weight gain with progesterone are really experiencing water retention from dehydration.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-07T16:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/feet-swelling.6811/post-85568>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=85562)
>
> It's been 4 days and my feet are almost back to normal. Now I'm really wondering if it's related to my hormones and cycle, as they started swelling on Day 14, and then pretty much went down once TOM was over.
>
> I'm pretty sure the cold bath helped - man I was peeing about every 15 minutes on Saturday! Think that was it all coming out. LOL. Will see what happens this cycle.
>
> It's funny because when I looked down at my feet when they were swollen, I've seen that exact look before in many women. **Also, wondering if people who are having weight gain with progesterone are really experiencing water retention from dehydration.**

Diane - I think you've just picked up on something very important here! I think hydration is key to one's ability to leverage Pg well. I'm currently re-reading Dr. Reiss's hormone book and in the progesterone chapter he mentioned that there are some women who can't tolerate much Pg, even though they're deficient. His clinical experience shows that usually this problem is related to candida overgrowth. and once its been eradicated the women don't have problems w/Pg any longer. I'm wondering if this might also be a similar reaction for those ladies here on the board who have Lymes and diseases/co-infections associated with Lymes?

In his book, he does state that even after clearing the candida, there is a small percentage that can't tolerate traditional dosing of Pg. This highlights for me how important your own journaling, and documenting is in helping you figure out what you need, but its also helpful for your doctor as well.

> Diane - I think you've just picked up on something very important here! I think hydration is key to one's ability to leverage Pg well. I'm currently re-reading Dr. Reiss's hormone book and in the progesterone chapter he mentioned that there are some women who can't tolerate much Pg, even though they're deficient. His clinical experience shows that usually this problem is related to candida overgrowth. and once its been eradicated the women don't have problems w/Pg any longer. I'm wondering if this might also be a similar reaction for those ladies here on the board who have Lymes and diseases/co-infections associated with Lymes?
>
> In his book, he does state that even after clearing the candida, there is a small percentage that can't tolerate traditional dosing of Pg. This highlights for me how important your own journaling, and documenting is in helping you figure out what you need, but its also helpful for your doctor as well.

Thats an excellent point. i do recall reading that part and making note of it. I dont think candida is my problem - has never come up for me as an issue, but its just another "hmmm...wondering about lyme and co-infections" to add to the arsenal. I am bummed bc I got the stomach flu something feirce last week (threw up in excess of 7 or so times, fatigued, headaches, weakness, the works) and had to miss my much anticipated lyme appt. I put off cancelling as long as I could but finally at 11:30 pm the night before i called the office and left a message telling them what was going on.

### agatha — 2013-04-18T20:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84422>

Hello all you cold water people
I'm just a feeling a little wobbly right now. I went across to Ray Cronise's blog to see what was going on over there and happened to mention in the most recent thread that I hadn't done well on an all plants diet. He went for me big time! Why are all these vegans so aggressive? I had a similarly unpleasant response from Colin Campbell when I emailed him to ask about why the plants only thing had made me ill.

I don't know wny I'm upset - perhaps it's the being accused of lying which hurts. I'm eating bacon to comfort myself.

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-18T20:27:16+0300

### Lava — 2013-04-18T20:37:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84425>

Agatha, I had the same problem on an all plant diet..Thank goodness I got over that and got my brain and life back. It's hard to change the dogma of others...Bacon is good comfort food!!!!!!! Hang tough and don't let them bug you...

### David — 2013-04-18T20:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84426>

While I am fascinated by Ray Cronise's "potato diet" and the concept of starch consumption as a path to great health (Healthy starch! Healthy whole grains!), I think Epi-paleo is much better for one's well being.

You have obviously scratched some sort of wound by pointing out your problems with the vegan diet.

Perhaps vegans run on a principal based on the "news media factor", ie "if everybody thinks something is true, which makes it a fact." So, you have experienced something that runs counter to these "facts", and expressing your contrary opinion to these folks means to them that you must be evil, misguided or crazy. I wouldn't sweat it.

(Along these same lines, I think I am beginning to see why Dr Kruse is sometimes disheartened by the reception he gets from people he believes should know better...)

### caroline — 2013-04-18T21:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84432>

Bacon always, always works for me!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-04-18T22:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84434>

I didn't realize he had a blog, so curiosity got the better of me and I headed over there to read through it. Whew! That was exhausting.
He told you that he could see the reason why you didn't feel good on your diet, but then declines to actually give an answer. Probably because he doesn't have one.

This is the second time this week I've encountered people who claim to be nutritionally enlightened who are recommending low fat, but for the life of me I haven't been able to find what their rationale is. Is Ray saying it's strictly a matter of calories?

I found his blog fascinating given all that I've been learning here. It's like he's talking a good talk, but I am not sure I buy it.

Regarding diet and cold soaking and weight loss, I'm in the midst of my own observations.

My weight loss stalled in November, for which I think several things are a factor...winter being the primary one. When I upped my cold soaking and was eating very small epi-paleo meals with no hunger at all for November, December, January, February, and March, I had zero weight loss, but my clothes fit differently and my skin felt completely different. Now that I'm moving through Spring, I feel like things are shifting in my body, yet nothing has happened yet. Hard to describe what I mean here, but it seems like the change of season is impacting hormones and a desire to be outside moving more, even though I did quite a bit of outside movement all winter (big skier, here!). I went paleo last April, nearly one year ago exactly. I lost 35 pounds in 4 months, and then fluxuated + or - 5 pounds between August and November. Then weight completely stable since then. I'm just wondering if I will start going into weight loss mode as spring heads to summer.

My own N=1.

Working on my will power.

### sjoshua — 2013-04-18T22:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84438>

Hmm... I have read quite a bit of Ray's stuff, and although he supports the "potato diet" (which in my opinion he came up with randomly in hopes to counter both keto and paleo diets, since it is not fat nor commonly accepted as a "safe starch" due to being white) and has documented successes using it, I am surprised to hear he went after you... The blog posts I have read seem to lean towards veggie/vegan, but he always seems to go back to leaving the choice up to the reader. (Mind sharing a link to the post/reply? Curiousity )

My impression is that Ray's approach is targeting shortcuts that primarily promote fat loss, and in that sense I believe he is doing some awesome work with his calorimeter and CT/Activity experimenting. Although I myself enjoy my primal diet and have worked towards keeping it even more keto since discovering Jack's work, I think if Ray's method is effective for an individual it could be utilized to trim some fat or even bypass a fat loss stall in the short term.

On the other hand, Jack's primary aim seems to be increasing overall health, longevity, and optimizing our own true potentials far beyond diet. Along the way, you will likely sculpt a beautiful physique and drop excess fat, but this is more of a secondary benefit due to living and feeding optimally. Jack's principles are derived from a much more scientific and logical analysis, and supported by his background as a neuro Dr.

### caroline — 2013-04-18T22:23:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84439>

Mama - what I noticed first - I went down 2 sizes but my weight didn't change or maybe went up a Kilo or 2. Nothing much changed until I upped the fat and cut down the protein - I have drifted down about 4 Kilos now and my weight is very stable - no wild fluctuations like I use to have. I know that being healthy and in control is absolutely our first priority and that is what Jack is all about .....but it sure does feel good to rock a pair of skinny jeans!!!

### agatha — 2013-04-18T23:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ouch-ray-cronise-savaged-me.6814/post-84445>

Thanks to you all for your encouragement - miuch appreciated. To satisfy your curiosity sjoshua here is the link to the whole (long) thread.

[http://hypothermics.com/2013/04/muscling-your-metabolism-part-2/#more-1895](http://hypothermics.com/2013/04/muscling-your-metabolism-part-2/#more-1895)

My part is near the end - here below are the final posts in a back and forth discussion. You'll notice he isn't able to produce an answer to my question of what was wrong with my vegan diet - he relies on accusing me of lying.

Me:
So, can you see where the problem was in my plant based diet? For two years, I ate daily:
At least 8-10 portions of colourful veggies including crucifers,
some starch each day (barley, oats, potatoes, sweet potatos), some legumes each day, herbs, spices, a couple of fruits daily, some nuts, seeds and avocados, very small amounts of olive oil. No sugar or processed foods. All food cooked at home from fresh. I didn’t lose weight so I know I was eating an appropriate amount of food.
I agree I have some health problems – this is why I tried eating a plant diet (after reading The China Study) to see if it could help me. Instead I felt much worse after a while. I don’t think the plants caused the deterioration (I still eat 8 portions of plants per day) but I think the diet lacked some things which I need. I started out with mild MS, I ended up with a host of other problems on top and these new sympoms resolved within a few weeks of adding butter and eggs into my diet. Just saying, some people might need more than just plants. I now feel at my best eating lots of plants and some high quality animal foods (fish, eggs, butter offal). Others may do well on plant based and that’s great.

Reply

Cody Fyler says:

April 18, 2013 at 7:00 am

Well, the fact that you felt better eating eggs and butter is telling to me. Maybe you were deficient in choline? Egg yolks are a good source of choline. Liver is, too. Butter is a great source of vitamin A, vitamin K2, and a little D. Not to mention, some folks (Ray Peat) believe that saturated fat is anti-inflammatory. Butter is a mix of saturated and monounsaturated fats, If memory serves…

Reply

admin says:

April 18, 2013 at 7:27 am

Actually it doesn’t mean that at all. If you are a smoker and quite – one “feels better” when they resume. Alcohol addicts have tons of symptoms when they quit. Feeling better is important, but it doesn’t “tell you” anything.

This is exactly how we end up with all of the anecdotal, summarized wrong information. This is an example of nutritionism at its finest. People discussing technical sounding words with “placeholder” notions of what they mean – 125 years ago it was protein, carbohydrate and fat. That predates vitamins and to this very day most people don’t even understand those three – even people that should know better.

I’m not interested in going there here. If that causes a few people not to come back, its okay. But it’s a case of blind leading the blind and I don’t want to participate.

Elle, there is a way for you to figure out what is going on. Butter an eggs are likely not the only or even best solution. If you care enough to talk about it publicly then care enough to figure out how to make it work – out destiny is no in the hands of extract another mammals lactate or ovum.

Challenge status quo a bit and figure out exactly what is happening. So many other variables are in the mix. There are already too many holes in the story that I’m not sure I’m getting the real facts. I’ve personally coached people that have told me “I ate perfect” when in fact they have not.

There are many other issues associated with the solution space you’re working in, but that’s for another time.

Thanks for input. Lets get back to metabolism.

Ray

### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-20T08:46:47+0300

For my birthday I bought a quart of goat milk for lunch. Its the only dairy I've had in a year except for butter. I showed it to an older lady at work who had both knees replaced and asked her if she wanted a swig cause it was good stuff. She declined and replied that her Dr. Pepper tasted good. I quipped back that cocaine tastes good to a drug addict, too.

I've been giving a quiz at work to a few people. I ask them how much white sucrose sugar the average person ate in the year 1900 (5 pounds). Then I ask them how much sucrose sugar the average person eats today (150 pounds).

This thread reminds me of something my mother told me about from her childhood. She and the neighborhood kids would run behind the city pest control vehicle in the street as it was spraying a fog of DDT to control mosquitos.

OMG! about the DDT..huh

I wanted to put some mussels in the freezer yesterday.. and then it was so full of stuff my stuff almost did not fit in, something my mom had filled it with a few hours before coming from a shopping tour.. it was filled with those Finnish traditional Easter porriges that you eat with sugar and cream/milk. It is made of whole rye and sugar basically, baked in the oven. 100% grain and sugar. Poison to me. I love the taste for sure but I stopped eating it many many ears ago. I said, mom! What is this poison filling up the fridge...?! (I said **poison** a bit louder) She said, ohh it is Memma, it tastes so good! I said, yes mom, many things taste good, sure. But are they good for us, no! My mom got so silent and looked ashamed. She is the one that have the hardest time staying away from sweet stuff BTW. Even if she sees her own kids suffer lots and lots of health issues.

It is so funny her own mum died from a massive heart attack way too early, she seemed healthy until then.. and her dad suffered years and years bedridden in hospital because plenty of infarcts he got in he's brain. I always say to my mom, do you want to end like that? Remember you have lots of grand kids that needs your love!

To me it seems to stay way from grains (and sweets) seems to be harder to do than a drug addict to stay away from he's drugs....
I do love my parents for sure. And I try to keep my mouth shut most of the time..lol But sometimes it is hard to understand what choices people make for sure!

### agatha — 2013-04-20T20:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/people-think-were-loopy-loo.6820/post-84554>

> [Chrysanthemama said:](/goto/post?id=84512)
>
> I'm leading an earthing class in June, and I'm doing an experiment with it. This is my first "tester" class. I live in a community with an abnormally high number of PhDs (one of the highest in the nation, in fact). So, for this first class, my description is more generic and about getting in touch with nature, although I do mention grounding. For the next offering, I'm going to change the description to being more true to what I'm really gearing up for, earthing, grounding, CT, mindfulness...
>
> I'm very curious to see how it will change who signs up, and the number of participants.

Do post an let us know what kind of reactions you get when you do the class.
