# Thread: Regaining LS - Starting at 51% body fat and need advice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-04-21T15:49:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84571>

Hi All,

I have really cleaned up my diet over the past 3-6 months and have been doing CT for 30 minutes nearly every day for the past 3 months, but I have not done the official leptin reset. I recently realized that I am probably \*severely\* leptin resistant and need to get even more serious about my diet and meal timings. My doctor recently drew a leptin level and it was almost off the chart, even using the obese reference range. At 5'5" and 205 pounds, it goes without saying that I'm obese... But the icing on the cake was when I did the DEXA body composition scan. My body fat was measured at 51%. That makes me \*super\* obese (worse than morbidly obese)! I knew it was bad, but I had no idea it was \*that\* bad. Like most obese people I have tried to loose weight, but found it next to impossible. Willpower was never the biggest issue... It was the fact that I have always felt \*horrible\* a few weeks into a diet with crashing mood, energy, and sleep... And even if I kept up with the diet (which I did for up to 6 months several times in spite of feeling like crap), I could never loose more than 5 pounds even after 6 months of diet and exercise. For the past year or so, I have given up dieting and decided that I'd rather be fat and feel good (or at least as good as possible since I don't actually feel \*good\* most of the time) than thin and feeling like crap. That is not to say I have done any bee-lines to the fast food joint or junk food isle. I haven't eaten that crap in years, but I somehow still managed to put on another 15 pounds over the last year eating a reasonably healthy (albeit not paleo until recently) low sugar diet. After reading a lot of Dr. K's leptin articles, a lot of things started to make sense and I am starting to realize that at 51% fat I will never really feel good or be healthy. And in fact, my obesity may be contributing to the chronic cortisol problems I've been battling for several years now.

Recently I started taking cycloset. I dislike taking drugs, but after reading Dr. K's articles and learning more about cycloset I realized that it might make a big difference for me where nothing else has worked. I've only been taking it for about 2 weeks, but I'm already noticing a decrease in appetite and am down about 2.5lbs on the scale. My fasting sugar has also dropped from the 100-110 range down to high 90's. I have now decided to support the cycloset by also doing the leptin reset protocol. I am wondering if anyone at my level of obesity has had success becoming leptin sensitive? In the Leptin Reset protocol, Dr. K mentions that it can take 6-8 weeks. Due to my obesity I expect it to take longer, but I am also wondering if it is possible to be obese and leptin sensitive. I suspect that it is not, but I also realize that it is probably going to literally take \*years\* for me to get my bodyfat down to a non obese level. Based on my past history, I do not expect a quick and dramatic weight loss. Does that mean I have to do the Leptin Reset for years before I even have a chance to become leptin sensitive? If so, that is pretty darn discouraging.... Not that I ever plan to go back to the SAD diet, but I have not done well with ketogenic diets in the past as they have led to especially severe mood, energy, and sleep disturbances. Right now I would estimate I'm at around 100 grams of low glycemic carbs per day. I realize I probably need to drop that for the Leptin Reset. I am wondering if dropping to around 50grams per day would be more sustainable for me if I have to do this for years as opposed to going all the way ketogenic and if I could still get benefits from the reset at 50grams of carbs per day. I would love to hear from anyone who has been obese and had success with the Leptin Reset.

Thanks,
Trinity

### Jude — 2013-04-22T07:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84603>

Hi Trinity, Have been following JK for almost 2yrs and have a few absolutes that I pass on to those interested.
Before you get stuck into epi paleo, try to
1\. Ensure you only drink pure water,approx 3L per day..(reverse osmosis, spring..no fluoride.)
2\. Try to reduce the EMF's in your home and especially in your bedroom at night.
3\. No artificial lights at night.

I won't comment about the foood side ...lots of folk here better at that than me.
All the best.

### agatha — 2013-04-22T23:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84636>

Hi Trinity
I don't have answers to your questions but my heart goes out to you when I hear of your struggles. I really hope the reset goes well for you - keep us posted as you progress.

### Trinity — 2013-04-23T00:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84638>

Thanks guys,

I drink a decent amount of water and try to stick with bottled water rather than tap water. I have also been wearing blue blockers at night and have installed yellow light bulbs in my bedroom lamps (got them from lowbluelights.com). I haven't really investigated the EMF thing much yet, but I am around computers at work pretty much all day. There is so much to research  Currently, my focus is upon leptin rx and CT.

\--Trinity

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-23T00:33:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/regaining-ls-starting-at-51-body-fat-and-need-advice.6834/post-84639>

Hi Trinity and welcome!

There are others here working a similar story - Quelsen is one - he doesn't post often...but occasionally he stops by! The Leptin Reset will help you bring things together. the Leptin Rest helps you focus on circadian cycles with meal timing. as far as how long it will take it depends on where you're at today...

ask questions, there are lots of great people here who will help you get on track.. I will say - consider trying getting in to ketosis again - but read this first [http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/)

good luck and let us here help you on your way to optimal health!

I never knew that scallops had carbs until I started epi-paleo!

# Thread: The Road to Optimal and my Health Problems
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-road-to-optimal-and-my-health-problems.6862/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-04-24T18:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-road-to-optimal-and-my-health-problems.6862/post-84744>

Hey all! I'm new to these boards and I wanted to receive some feedback from everyone here. Firstly I wanted to tell everyone that I can tell you all are very dedicated, wise, and intelligent, as some of the threads I've been reading through are nothing short of amazing. I have been dwelling over at the Bulletproof Exec Forums for the past few weeks, and I've now landed myself here, and although I agree with most everything Dave Asprey presents, I believe the community over here (in the grand scale) has a bit more knowledge.

So, to make a long story short, I had some questions on starting my path to Optimal. I originally landed into the paleosphere through PaleoHacks, as I asked questions pertaining to my health/(possible)psychological problems, as well as that of my brother. My post on myself can be seen here: [http://paleohacks.com/questions/175...he-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ROLhaPzh](http://paleohacks.com/questions/175553/last-question-on-the-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ROLhaPzh) and my brother here: [http://paleohacks.com/questions/170915/help-me-cure-aid-my-younger-brother-please#axzz2ROLhaPzh;](http://paleohacks.com/questions/170915/help-me-cure-aid-my-younger-brother-please#axzz2ROLhaPzh;) as you can see Dr. K commented on my post (on myself) and told me to read EMF2, then another member of PaleoHacks and these forums commented.

For the past couple months (I'd estimate), I have been following a primarily **Bulletproof Diet**, all while IFing for 16-18, sometimes 20 hours a day (sometimes less, though). I know I wouldn't have been considered Bulletproof as I was and am still lacking variety in my diet, in terms of food choices, veggies, herbs, etc. and I still drink filthy water (tap and water bottles). Here's my caveat, when I study something, I go through with it like Jack and Dave, I dig DEEP. So far I have made my way through the first 5 or so posts in the Leptin Prescription by Jack, I have annotated them all, annotated all of the comments, formed this into a large word file, and wrote summaries based off of that information. I plan on doing the same with his Epi-Paleo book, as well as the Better Baby Book by Dave Asprey. Just yesterday I stocked up on a bunch of canned wild caught seafood at Trader Joe's because I'm starting to believe that a seafood (primarily shellfish) based diet might work even better than the typical Bulletproof diet (as it seems meat heavy). I've been thinking about incorporating the best of both - heavy intake of seafood/shellfish, raw when possible/tolerable (I can do this with clams quite easily), eating everything (organs and bones/cartilage of animals and fish) while incorporating Bulletproof Coffee/Tea, Upgraded Chocolate/Hot Chocolate, and cyclical ketogenic dieting in the longer light cycles rather than eating carbs everyday (CKD is part of the Bulletproof diet).

My questions pertain to my health/psychological problems, and what I should start doing now. Everything is stated in that PaleoHacks post, but the gist of it is I **accidentally ingested what I believed to be PCP** at a concert, which screwed with me big time mentally and physically - this fortunately lead me to make many wise decisions regarding health and spirituality. I developed migraines with intense auras, extreme anxiety, **visual distortions** that would occur everyday, **pressure build up in my head** at odd times, depressed feelings, less energy, and much more. This lead me to screw up one of my classes at my local college as I had great grades in all of my classes, then had a terrible night of sleep, woke up to my parents fighting violently about finances, which created massive anxiety and a terrible migraine, and I missed my final. I have been improving drastically since implementing the Bulletproof diet and mindfulness as a practice, **I now do Jiu-Jitsu** and hold a job and have much less anxiety and better overall energy, but many of the odd symptoms persist. Visual auras persist, my visual snow (which I have since childhood) persist, my eye floaters have been slightly more noticeable, my visual distortions persist (things get wavy/morph slightly - this has cooled down a lot but still is present), and some other odd sensations. I believe this is because **PCP is an anticholingeric**, which basically means I damaged and important part of my brain/processing for a long time, hence why I felt so terrible shortly after, persisting for almost 15 months now, although it has SLOWLY gotten better. By the way, before these past few months of Bulletproof/Paleo dieting, I was a vegetarian for probably 3 months, and \*almost\* vegan for a month after that, all while trying to chronically lose weight I didn't really need to lose. I'm not trying to get up to about 165lbs or more with ideal body comp (I'm about 5'9 and 140ish, I would estimate 12-15% body fat, I feel as though I'm still growing, though, as I have gotten a bit taller and things about me are changing more).

Should I just do the **Leptin Reset** I've been reading about, coupled with **CT**, then jump into the **Post-LeptinRx**? I was thinking that since I can IF very easily and go 16hrs without caring to eat, and have been doing a cyclical ketogenic diet for the past few weeks (and have been in ketosis before that for months on end), and since I have been following a Bulletproof Style diet, maybe I don't need the reset, but should jump into the Post-LeptinRx and stop fasting as maybe that is just stressing my adrenals. Maybe fast for shorter periods of time starting at night (10-14hrs), or a few days a week rather than 6-7. I have begun something called Foundation Training to enhance my flexibility for BJJ and aid in resetting my back (I have a disc bulge), and for the past few days I have been doing it out in the open field by my house, barefoot, to ground, while remaining in minimal clothing to receive all the sunlight and nice breeze. So **(1)** should I engage in the Leptin Reset for 4-6 or 8 weeks? Start on the Post Leptin Reset Rx? **(2)** On a VERY TIGHT budget, **should I switch to eating primarily shellfish over grass-fed beef**? Is it fine if it is canned as long as there are no additives and it is wild caught? Clams, Oyesters, and Canned Salmon are all cheaper than a pound of New Zealand Grass-Fed Ground Beef at Trader Joe's, I'm just frightened of the **BPA** and other **xenoestrogens**. (3) Should I just focus on diet and grounding before I start supplementing heavy? I've been thinking that supplementing is unwise outside of performance enhancement (whether sports or sex or anything), deficiency, or nootropics - I've just been taking **600mg of chelated magnesium glycinate** before bed the past couple weeks... **I stopped Vitamin D** after reading a lot of literature that showed it may be rather detrimental to Nuclear Receptors in the long term and a whole lot of other aspects of signalling. I was thinking about maybe taking Potassium with the Magnesium as Dave Asprey recommends that, but I heard it is worthless and calcium in fact enhances the absorption of Magnesium.

Well, I apologize for this lengthy post, but I just wanted to introduce and establish myself on these boards. I will definitely be seen around frequently haha. For anyone that has any information for me, I thank you in advance, believe me, it will be greatly appreciated and will be utilized, thanks again!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Body Voltage Meter experiences
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-04-25T15:57:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84779>

I got my multimeter that is sensitive enough to read millivolts and set to work measuring my house, a Klein MM1000 at Home Depot for $60. A few parts from Radio Shack and some parts I had in my shop, and I was set.

First was my computer. I have a MacBook laptop and when I tested it with it's two-prong power adapter, it read 4.27 volts when I touched the case. Kinda high, to say the least.

I then converted the computer's power adapter to a 3-prong (has a ground prong) and when I touched the case using the same fingers and amount of skin surface area, it went down to .53. Interesting.

I then remembered that using the battery was the best way to use one's computer and yanked out the power cord from the side of the case and the voltage shot up to 7.34! WTF?

I've been reading but haven't found out why. Maybe it's the magenetic field that improves on battery power? I thought that went up and down with the electrical fields.

I'm trying different configurations to see if I can get myself more familiar with the parameters, but if someone can offer some suggestions...

### nonchalant — 2013-04-25T16:06:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84780>

Interesting, Martin. Computers vary, so perhaps that explains it. My laptop battery isn't strong enough to last more than a few minutes, so I have to keep mine attached.
How did you convert your adapter to a grounded one? Buy a new brick?

I grounded mine through the USB port, that works well also, according to my Trimeter.

### Martin — 2013-04-25T17:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84787>

Apple provides a grounded cord that slips onto the adapter when you remove the 2-prong module from that adapter. I'm setting up the grounded USB thingy today.

I measured BV around my ancient breaker panel and while there is voltage there (can't remember how much, I suspect EMFs), I expected it to be higher than it was.

I read 1.83 volts sitting in the middle of my studio (14 ft by 14 ft) and I need to check other places where I spend a lot of time. My side of the bed was nearly 2 volts while D-Dub's lower even though she's closer to the breaker panel on the other side of the wall.

I am getting a sickening feeling that I live in an EMF nightmare. The house wiring sucks ass and so do all my outbuildings. I will need to check them for specifics, but I know how some wiring looks because of the many remodels I've done here. Too damn bad I didn't know this info when I had the walls open. I have a sub-panel I know was done wrong, and it powers most of my studio. And the three-way switches have an independent traveller wire so I can't wait to see how effed up some of the spaces around them are.

Nearly fourteen years of my blood and tears making this farm ours, and many, many retirement dollars spent on a sow's ass? This place is beautiful with it's rolling hills, organic garden, orchard, spring water, and far from cell towers. But I really went downhill after moving here. Co-inky-dinky? Doubtful, after reading the research on the effects. There are no codes in the this county for building, and while the locals laud that as "freedom" from the gub-ment, the dumbasses don't know that some hick can wire their house with the best of intentions and they end up with a horror story.

More later. I'm going to stick my feet in the mud and hug my chickens.

We lost him to something, perhaps old age, but he fed them and protected them like a guy a hundred times his size. Come to think of it, kinda like me!

### Martin — 2013-04-27T23:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-84959>

More BVM adventures. I loves me some testing equipment!

I made a 30 foot ground wire and went around my house measuring BV and was shocked(!) to find that my studio was so high. Over at my music/recording equipment, it was at 4.8 volts (can you say, "Michael Jackson"?) and when I turned off the power strip (I'm starting to have bad thoughts about those handy things) it dropped down to 2.7. Still lousy, but I think I know why. Our recent remodel had a sub-panel installed and when I heard Neuert say that they are often wired incorrectly, I opened the panel door and sure enough, wrong. Not sure why, as there is no provision for the configuration that Neuert speaks of - separate neutral and ground bus bars (connection strips of metal to anchor the ends of the wires), only one connection bar with all the grounds and neutral connected together. It's a new box, not sure why it didn't have one, but I suspect it can be bought and bolted in and the wires separated.

Anyway, as I sat at my seat at the dining table, I read 3.6 volts.

Who knew?!

This area is fed by the aforementioned sub-panel, as is my studio, and kitchen, too. A magnetic gauss meter would prolly explode in flames if it came anywhere near this part of the house!

Some odd things came up, though. The readings would get lower as I approached the sub-panel box and, the main box, for that matter. Certain "hot spots" would show up, like right in the middle of the dining area, and in a bath closet. I suspect lots of wiring behind walls and under floors.

Next, I turned off the main breaker and was astonished to find around a 0.10 reading all over the house. I don't think it's power lines to the house because they are far away from where I got readings? What's that all about?

Is it time for a mud hut?

### caroline — 2013-04-28T00:51:04+0300

You are a very talented guy....and it sounds like you have a new lease on life!

How is the C/T going??????

LOL about mud huts... and Audrey.. I love that you are so happy now! It certainly shines through your posts....

### RayB — 2013-05-07T16:20:50+0300

I am shocked and dismayed by some body voltage readings I have seen around my home. I have a Far Infrared Sauna, that fortunately I rarely use, I had a BV reading of 24volts when sitting inside with the unit on. Yikes!!! Sitting on my toaster would probably be far more beneficial.

### RayB — 2013-05-07T16:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85570>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=84959)
>
> More BVM adventures. I loves me some testing equipment!
>
> I made a 30 foot ground wire and went around my house measuring BV and was shocked(!) to find that my studio was so high. Over at my music/recording equipment, it was at 4.8 volts (can you say, "Michael Jackson"?) and when I turned off the power strip (I'm starting to have bad thoughts about those handy things) it dropped down to 2.7. Still lousy, but I think I know why. Our recent remodel had a sub-panel installed and when I heard Neuert say that they are often wired incorrectly, I opened the panel door and sure enough, wrong. Not sure why, as there is no provision for the configuration that Neuert speaks of - separate neutral and ground bus bars (connection strips of metal to anchor the ends of the wires), only one connection bar with all the grounds and neutral connected together. It's a new box, not sure why it didn't have one, but I suspect it can be bought and bolted in and the wires separated.
>
>
> Is it time for a mud hut?

FWIW...bus bars can be purchased and installed. A neutral bus bar is insulated (no continuity) from the metal box. A ground bus bar has continuity with the panel's housing.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-07T16:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85572>

RayB, I wonder if your sauna can be grounded. My laptop was giving me higher BV readings than that, but grounding fixed it.
I have removed a dimmer switch, and lately I have been eyeing my house thermostat. And it might be a minor thing, but when using my debit card, I've started using the stylus and buttons more, instead of tapping on the screen.

### RayB — 2013-05-07T19:52:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85575>

nonchalant, From info that I have gathered, grounding will lower or completely remove body voltage 'cuz grounding puts your body at the same potential as the ground. Since we are using the ground as the reference voltage for all of our BV measurements, when our potential becomes equal with the reference voltage we seemingly fix things. In essence what we have done is taken a static skin voltage and converted it to current. Which is more damaging? IMHO, it's anybodies guess. According to Michael N., remediating the source of the BV is the first and best option then, when all else fails, ground.

BTW, the potential of ground is always assumed to be zero but in reality, it is not. But we have to use something as a reference and it is as good as anything. If it were zero, how could we source free electrons from it or where would lightning get it's electrons from or send it's electrons to?

> [RayB said:](/goto/post?id=85575)

> nonchalant, From info that I have gathered, grounding will lower or completely remove body voltage 'cuz grounding puts your body at the same potential as the ground. Since we are using the ground as the reference voltage for all of our BV measurements, when our potential becomes equal with the reference voltage we seemingly fix things. In essence what we have done is taken a static skin voltage and converted it to current. Which is more damaging? IMHO, it's anybodies guess. According to Michael N., remediating the source of the BV is the first and best option then, when all else fails, ground.
>
> BTW, the potential of ground is always assumed to be zero but in reality, it is not. But we have to use something as a reference and it is as good as anything. If it were zero, how could we source free electrons from it or where would lightning get it's electrons from or send it's electrons to?

RayB, it sounds like nonchalant is referring to grounding the sauna structure itself? Neuert shows us how grounding a computer's case shunts the voltage to ground so it won't use our body as a conduit, so if the sauna can be grounded, it would do the same?

### RayB — 2013-05-08T02:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-experiences.6868/post-85605>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=85583)
>
> RayB, it sounds like nonchalant is referring to grounding the sauna structure itself? Neuert shows us how grounding a computer's case shunts the voltage to ground so it won't use our body as a conduit, so if the sauna can be grounded, it would do the same?

You're probably right...sometimes I go off on a tangent.

The sauna itself can't be grounded...it's a wooden cabinet.

I would imagine that the effectiveness of grounding a computer's case would vary depending on the design of the case. An all metal case with very few openings/penetrations seems like it would act as shielding when grounded. Plastic case...not so much. Typically laptops are not directly connected to line current. They use a transformer. If they did use line current a metal case would be grounded already in accordance with UL rating.

# Thread: EMF Magnetic Field Home Testing - Feedback Please.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-magnetic-field-home-testing-feedback-please.6869/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul S — 2013-04-25T18:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-magnetic-field-home-testing-feedback-please.6869/post-84794>

I've listened in on the EMF webinar's thus far, and recently rented 3 test devices. Initial testing of the magnetic fields with the gauss meter, throughout my home, indicate levels between 0.9 - 1.5 milligauss. My situation/observation that I am hoping someone might confirm or clarify for me is:

All the walls facing the street are where I get the higher readings (1.4 - 1.5 milligauss). When I open my garage it edges up slightly more and in my garage is near 2.0. As I walk to the driveway and toward the street, levels approach 8.0 milligauss at the end of the driveway (which also happens to be near utility boxes). I've turned off the main breaker to the home and the measurements don't materially change (lower), except in a few places and, even then, no readings really much lower than 0.9 milligauss in the home, including the bedrooms (which are on the second story of the home).

From what I have gathered from the EMF webinars is that this situation appears to signify that (i) my magnetic fields are primarily driven by my neighborhood's issues more so than specific issues within my home and (ii) the levels (of 0.9 - 1.5 milligauss) within the home are such that it is entirely possible could be causing/contributing to sub-optimal health and labs?

If that is the case and I am interpreting the readings correclty - moving is probably the only way to "fix" the issue.....? Please offer any thoughts or clarification or others' experiences.

### cantweight — 2013-04-25T19:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-magnetic-field-home-testing-feedback-please.6869/post-84798>

I am no expert, but based on the fact you experience very little change when breakers are cut seems to strongly indicate your area is to blame. I lived in a very technologically rich neighborhood where illness abounded, I moved last summer and based my move on where I felt I breathed easier. We just tested the home we moved to and throughout the entire house the readings were .03-.04

I will make an effort to visit my old neighborhood to get a reading and repot back. But it definitely sounds like your problem is your area.

# Thread: Elevated liver enzymes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elevated-liver-enzymes.6872/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-04-25T19:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elevated-liver-enzymes.6872/post-84799>

We had our annual blood draw at work this month and I was pleased to see my CO2 and BUN levels have improved since I have not been drinking flouridated water. However, my liver enzymes, ALT & AST, are really elevated. They went from being in the 30s to being in the 50s.

Any thoughts as to where I should look for causes? I've been doing heavy metal chelation therapy for about 3 months and started taking Milk Thistle about 2 or 3 weeks prior to the draw. Could either one of those have contributed? Seems like those things would improve my numbers, though. BTW, I'm not overweight and my glucose and cholesterol numbers are good, so I'm not thinking fatty liver.

Any insights would be great.

### cantweight — 2013-04-25T21:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elevated-liver-enzymes.6872/post-84806>

From my experience with the lovely liver I would suspect both the chelation and milk thistle could elevate your liver numbers. Anything toxic your body is releasing is definitely going to make for an unhappy liver. While your numbers are elevated they are not crazy high. I would continue to cleanse by adding lemon to water and throw in a stronger binder, something like activated charcoal.

Take the charcoal away from other supplements and be sure bowels are moving splendidly!

I have also found coffee enemas to be helpful with major detox as well.

As the liver settles from dealing with the onslaught the numbers should go back down

# Thread: Anyone have input on a dairy craving?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-04-25T20:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84802>

Like clockwork, day 25 of cycle I have the most intense craving for milk.

I think milk is pretty nasty, I don't like the idea of drinking another species milk, I loathe the pasteurization process, yet my body is relentless in its yearning for some damn milk.

For years I have evaluated my food cravings and figured out what part of me needed something from that food and treated it with great success. This one baffles me.

We have cow milk in the house about 3 times a year, just so happened right now we had some. Today is day 25, I drank about 6 ozs and it felt like elixir of life!

Is this just another sugar craving masquerading as something else?

### Hope — 2013-04-26T00:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84827>

Wow. WHat an amazing observation! Could it be calcium you're wanting to help relax as you're approaching the end of a cycle? Though sugar would go along with that as well.....hmmmm

### cantweight — 2013-04-26T00:23:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84828>

Calcium...maybe that's it.

Was thinking maybe hormones too?

I always thought calcium in milk was not easily assimilated. Thinking a calcium craving would beg greens or something like that.

No clue!

### Hope — 2013-04-26T02:49:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84840>

Have you ever craved greens? I think milk is often a comfort food...so maybe it's more ice-cream than milk, but it's a thought....I think for me I would usually prefer to crave sugary things.

I used to always crave chocolate while PMSing bc I ''needed" the magnesium in it.....and other stuff...and of course there are way healthier options to get Mg from

### fitness@home — 2013-04-26T03:07:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84842>

### Hope — 2013-04-26T03:16:24+0300

### Hope — 2013-04-27T03:07:29+0300

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-27T03:31:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-have-input-on-a-dairy-craving.6873/post-84921>

Maybe it's a sugar craving and the milk sugar satisfies it.
